question: A farm has 4000 apple trees and each tree produces 25 apples. Half the apples are sold and the rest are used for apple juice. How many apples are used for apple juice?
Jake can load a truck in 30 minutes and unload it in 45 minutes. If he spends 4 hours loading trucks and 3 hours unloading, how many trucks did he work on?
A farmer has 5 apple trees. Each tree produces 120 apples. The farmer sells 6 apples for $1. If he sold all the apples, how much money did he earn?
A farmer grew 300kg of potatoes. 60% are good for market, and 35% of the remainder are used for seeds, the rest is thrown out. How many kilos are thrown out?
In a maths test, Lisa scored 85 out of 100. In the next test, she scored 5 points less. What was her score in the second test?
Susie baked 24 cookies. If she gave 8 cookies to her friend, how many cookies does she have left?
The Jones family is planning a road trip that is 400 miles each way. Their car gets 25 miles per gallon. Gas is $3.20 a gallon. How much will the Jones family spend on gas for the trip?

Jane is ordering a pizza party. Pizzas costs $5, beverages cost $1.5, garlic bread costs $2 and cupcakes cost $2.5 each. She orders two pizzas, two beverages, three garlic breads, and a cupcake. How much total amount she spent?
Bill bought a car for $8000. He had to replace the engine which cost 1/4 of the car's original price. What is the total he spent?

David rides his bicycle on the road. On a normal road, it can ride at a speed of 20 miles per hour. In downhill, it can ride 8 miles per hour faster than on a normal road. But when riding on an uphill, it travels at a speed 10 miles per hour slower than when it is on normal road. If David rides his cycle one-fourth of the time on normal, one-fourth on uphill and half of the time on downhill, what is his average speed in miles per hour?
In a chess club, there are 30 players and half of them are under the age of 18. If 2/3 of the adult players are professionals, then how many people are in the club?
Luke is 5 years old. He is 1/5 as old as his father and his father is 3/4 as old as his grandmother. How old is his grandmother?

Connie has a jar full of coins - quarters, dimes, and pennies. She has twice as many quarters as she has dimes and four times as many pennies as she has dimes. If she has 15 dimes, how many coins does she have in total?
In a parking garage, there are 480 cars. The garage’s security team inspected 30% of the cars for any irregularities. How many cars did the security team inspect?
Melanie is making bracelets for her friends. She uses 15 beads for each bracelet and she makes 6 bracelets in total. How many beads does Melanie use?
Tina is making bracelets. Each bracelet requires 15 beads. Tina has 150 beads. How many bracelets can she make?
Anan has a toy collection with 8 boxes containing 15 identical toys in each box. Due to damage, one box becomes unusable. Considering all other factors, how many total toys does Anan have now?
A restaurant has 150 tables. Each table seats 4 people. The restaurant's maximum occupancy is 500 people. How many more tables would they need to reach maximum occupancy if each table seats 4 people?
Jane worked 15 hours this week and gets paid $13 per hour. John worked 20 hours this week and gets paid $15 per hour. How much money did John and Jane make together this week?
Megan has 125 MB left to download a movie. If her internet connection speeds up to 5 MB/minute, how many more minutes will it take her to download the movie?
Jenny has 5 red apples and 10 green apples. She ate 3 of the red apples and gave 2 green apples to her friend. How many apples does Jenny have left?
Nina has 100 apples. She sells them for $2 each. If she works 5 days a week, how much does she make a week?
Peter bought a car for $18,000. He sold it after a year for 15% less than the purchase price. How much money did he get for the car?
A baker baked 34 loaves of bread. She has 6 containers to place them in. How many loaves of bread will each container get if one of the containers is smaller and can only hold 4 loaves?
Charles agreed to pay 60% of the cost of his son's education, which costs $150 a month. How much will Charles pay each year?
David has three cats. Each one eats 3 cans of cat food per day. How many cans of cat food does he need for a week?
In a school, there are three times as many girls as boys. If there are 200 girls, how many students are there in total?

A classroom contains 45 students. The teacher divided the students into groups of 3 for a project. Then, the teacher realized she needed one more group for a special project. How many groups are there in total?
Abby has 30 apples. She wants to divide them equally among her 5 friends. After dividing, 2 friends gave their apples to Abby's little brother. How many apples does Abby's little brother have?
An animal shelter has 16 dogs and 24 cats. Each dog costs $120 to adopt and each cat costs $80. If someone adopted all the animals, how much would it cost?

Mike receives food assistance as he's unemployed. If he gets a job that pays $1/hour and he works 35 hours a week, how much more will he actually earn a week if his food assistance is reduced by $70/month?
James sees 5 episodes of a 30-minute show and then watches a 20-minute documentary. How much time does he spend watching TV?
Marco had 12 pens. He gave some pens to his friends at school and only left with half of his pens. How many pens did Marco give to his friends?

A train is traveling at a speed of 50 mph. Another train is ahead of it traveling at a speed of 30 mph. If the distance between them is 40 miles, how many hours will it take for the faster train to catch up to the slower train?
John is saving for a new car. He puts $200 into his savings account each month. After 7 months, he receives a bonus of $500. How much money does he have after a full year of saving?
A bakery produces 100 cookies in the morning and 150 cookies in the afternoon. If a box contains 6 cookies, how many boxes can be filled in a day?
Alice shared 5 large pizza for a sleepover with 6 friends. If each pizza costs $10 and Alice paid for the pizzas, how much did each friend have to pay if they decided to share the cost?
Susie gets a paper cut everytime she re-arranges her bookshelf. If she re-arranges her bookshelf 5 times a month, how many paper cuts will she have after 3 months?
Micheal buys a new digital camera for $700 and additional photography accessories costing 2/5ths as much. How much does he spend in total?

Five friends decide to buy a birthday gift for their favorite teacher. They originally planned to divide the cost of the gift equally. However, one of the friends cannot contribute. If each of the remaining friends now have to pay an additional $5, what is the total cost of the gift?

Samantha needs to drive her car for daily chores. She needs to drive 5 miles to the supermarket, 3 miles to drop her children at school, 10 miles to take her kids to ballet practice, and thrice the distance from the school to ballet practice to get back home. If Samantha's car gets 30 miles per gallon, and gas costs $3.00, how much will Samantha spend on gas for her tour?
Ryan has a box of cookies. He sells 3 packs of cookies each day. Each pack contains 10 cookies. After 5 days, how many cookies did he sell?
Jackie and Ryan are saving money. Jackie saves $10 per week and Ryan saves $15 per week. How much will they have saved together in a year?

A group of friends went out for a paintball match. They bought paintballs at a rate of $8.75 for a packet of 100 paintballs. There were 12 friends and each one bought 3 packets. How much total money did they spend?
Hannah made 18 muffins for her bake sale. She sold 75% of the muffins at the bake sale. How many muffins were left unsold?

A restaurant served 100 burgers on Monday. On Tuesday, they served twice as many as they had on Monday, and on Wednesday they served half as many as on Monday. How many total burgers were served from Monday through Wednesday?
If Mike gives Dan $10, Mike would have the same amount of money as Dan. If Mike originally had $50, how much money did Dan have initially?
An online store sells books at a discount of 20% on their original price. If the original price of a book was $25, how much is discounted off?
A group of students is preparing a theater play. They sold 120 tickets at $8 each. How much money did they collect?

There are 1,500 students in a school. If 1/3 of the students join a science club, and 1/5 of the students in the science club join a robotics project, how many students are part of the robotics project?

A bakery makes 200 cupcakes every day. The cupcakes are divided equally into four flavors: chocolate, vanilla, strawberry and lemons. If on one day, all the vanilla cupcakes were sold and 3/4 of the chocolate cupcakes were sold, how many cupcakes were left?

Tom plays the video game, Space Survivor, his mission to protect the spaceship requires that he activate his shields on average every 20 seconds.  And each time he activates his shield, his shield remains on for 10 seconds.  On average, how many seconds per minute does Tom's shield stay active ?

Tom has a garden. He decides to plant 500 trees. If he plants 1/5 of the trees on the first day, 1/4 of the trees on the second day, and the remaining trees on the third day, how many trees does Tom plant on the last day?

Stacy owns 5 scarves, each has two stripes. She has four scarves with three stripes each, three scarves with no stripes, and another six scarves with single stripe each. What is the total number of stripes Stacy has on all her scarves?
Rosa has a bolt of fabric that is 200 square meters large. She plans on cutting the fabric into 4 equal parts. She keeps one and donates the other three. How much fabric does she donate?
At a bakery, 10 customers bought bagels at $3 each and 8 customers bought muffins at $2 each. How much money did the bakery make from the sale of bagels and muffins?
A library has a total of 90 books. Half of the books are fiction and the rest are non-fiction. If Paul borrowed 10 fiction books and 15 non-fiction books, how many books are left in the library?
A stationery store has 400 pens that it plans to pack into boxes. If each box can hold 20 pens, how many boxes does the store need?
An apple pie needs 5 apples and an orange pie needs 7 oranges. If Lisa needs to bake 3 apple pies and 2 orange pies, how much fruits does she need?

A bakery has 150 loaves of bread in the morning. Throughout the day, 90 loaves are sold, and 20 damaged loaves are thrown away. Another 35 freshly baked loaves are added. Then 25 more loaves are sold. How many loaves are left at the end of the day?
Benny is 3 years older than thrice the age of Sarah. If Sarah is 8 years old, how old is Benny?

Pete was 7 years old when he had four times as many fish as bikes. Ten years later, after losing some of his fish and buying 260 more bikes, the number of bikes became three times the number of fish. How many fish does Pete have now if he had 120 fish when he was seven years old?
Emily bought 4 cakes. Each cake has 8 slices. While serving, she accidentally dropped a cake and 3 slices from another cake. How many slices were left?

Kate works four days a week at a bakery and earns a different amount of tips every day. The past three days, she earned $35, $28, and $30. How much does she need to earn on her last day if she wants to make an average of $40 in tips per day?
A basketball team scored 150 points in a tournament, 30% of which were 3-point shots. How many points did the team score from 2-point shots?

Mrs. Jones is a teacher who can get a 10% discount on $300 worth of school supplies at Office Depot. She can also get a 15% off on $400 worth of school supplies at Staples. How much money would she save by choosing the cheapest option?
Tom is training for a marathon and each day he runs twice as many miles as the previous day. If on the first day he ran 2 miles, how many miles will he run in total during the first 5 days?

In an aquarium, there are 100 fishes. 40% of these fishes are Clownfish, and the remaining number are split evenly between Blue Tangs and Starfish. If a Clownfish has 5 fins, a Blue Tang has 3 fins and a Starfish has 11 fins, how many fins are there in the aquarium?
George and Harry washed their cars. George has 5 five-seater cars, each with 5 doors, and Harry has 8 four-seater cars, each with 3 doors. How many car parts did they wash?

The local football team, the Tigers, scored 3 goals in the first half, 2 goals in the second half, and 1 goal in overtime. Their opponents, the Lions, scored 2 goals in the first half, 1 goal in the second half, and they didn't score any goals in overtime. How many more goals did the Tigers score than the Lions?
Lily and her 3 friends decide to split the cost of a $200 concert ticket equally. How much does each person need to contribute?
Mary and Bob are cooking for a party. Mary has $200 and Bob has $180. Together they buy ten bottles of wine for $20 per bottle. How much money do they have left?
A supermarket buys a box of oranges that contains 100 oranges for $15. If the supermarket wants to make a profit of $10 after selling all the oranges, how much should it sell each orange for?
Sarah has 25 birds in her aviary, 40% of the birds are sparrows. How many sparrows does Sarah have?
Alex sold 15 tickets to a charity event and each ticket costs $25. If she donates 10% of her total earnings, how much will she donate?
A software engineer spent one-third of his day coding. If his working day is 9 hours, how many hours did he spend coding?
A bus can hold 40 people. If the school is taking 320 students and teachers on a field trip, how many buses will the school need?

Robert is baking a huge pie, and the recipe requires pounds of chocolate instead of telling him the amount. He needs 8 pounds in total. He looks up on the internet that 1 ounce of chocolate weighs 1/16 of a pound. If he needs 8 pounds of chocolate, how many ounces should he buy?
Sam has 15 candies. He gave 3 candies to his friend Peter and 2 more than Peter to his sister Jane. How many candies would Sam have left if he gave away 3 more candies?
In a basketball game, there are twice as many assists as blocks. If the number of blocks is 30, how many assists and blocks are in total in the game?

Adam has a jar of jellybeans that he wants to share with his friends. He gives 4 to his sister, 6 to his friend Mike, and 3 to his friend Kevin. He then equally distributes the remaining jellybeans among his 3 schoolmates. If he initially had 34 jellybeans in the jar, how many jellybeans did each schoolmate receive?
Mia picks all the apples from 20 trees that have 10 apples each. If she sells two-thirds of the apples and eats a quarter of the remainder, how many apples are left?
Harry has 4 blue pens for every 5 green pens that he has. How many green pens does Harry have if he has 20 blue pens?

Andy needs to fill his rectangular fish tank with water. The fish tank is 2 feet wide, 3 feet long, and 1.5 feet deep. If water costs $0.5 per cubic foot, how much it will cost to fill the whole tank with water?
Jennifer bought a box of chocolates which contained 30 pieces. She ate 2 pieces every day until she only had 1/5 of the chocolates left. How many days did it take her to eat the chocolates?

Charlie is building a tower of blocks. He has 20 sets of blocks that contain 60 blocks each. If each level of his tower has the same exact number of blocks, how many blocks are there per level if his tower is 40 levels tall?
An academy has 500 students. 60 percent are juniors or seniors. 1/4 of juniors and seniors have a bike. How many juniors and seniors do not have a bike?
A box of crayons contains 24 pieces. If Alice lost half of her crayons and then her friend gave her 5 more crayons, how many crayons does Alice have now?

David has a farm with 12 apple trees, 15 pear trees, and 18 banana trees. Each apple tree bears 100 apples, each pear tree bears 150 pears, and each banana tree bears 200 bananas. How many pieces of fruit does David's farm produce?
A car travels 15 miles per gallon. If the car's gas tank has 10 gallons, how far in miles can the car travel on a full tank?
Danielle has 50 seashells in her collection. She found 12 more on her next trip to the beach and gives away 20 to her friends. How many seashells does Danielle have now?

Ashley runs a bakery and she prepares 150 cupcakes daily. Out of the 150, 20 cupcakes were not sold. If Ashley will bake cupcakes daily for a whole month (30 days), how many unsold cupcakes will she have by the end of the month?

A theater has 30 rows of seats, with 20 seats in each row. In one screening, all the seats are filled. The following day, only half of the seats are occupied. How many people visited the theater for these two screenings?
Emily will make $600 more in a year than Alex. If Alex makes $2,000 in a year, how much will Emily and Alex make combined in a year?
A fruit vendor sold 150 apples in the morning, and double the amount in the afternoon. If each apple was sold for $2, how much did the vendor earn in total for the day?

Emma is a fan of vintage literature. She decided to buy 3 books by 'Dickens', 2 books by 'Austen', and 4 mysterious romantic adventures from the 18th century. The price of the 'Austen' book is $20 and it is two-thirds of the price of one book by 'Dickens'. The 4 mysterious romantic adventures cost 50% of the total cost of all other books Emma is going to buy. How much will Emma have to pay for all the books?

Grace wants to purchase 3 sets of games for her nephews and 2 sets for her niece. The game sets for the nephews are $20.00 each and the niece's set is $30.00 each. The store is currently offering 15% off, so how much will Grace spend on the games sets?
Emma baked 5 cookies for each member of her family. Her family consists of 7 members. If she already baked 30 cookies, how many more cookies does she need to bake?

In a charity marathon, participants earned $6 per kilometer last year. This year, they earn $4 per kilometer. If last year's champion earned $48, how many more kilometers does this year's participant have to run to earn the same amount of money?
In a fruit salad, there are 2 times as many strawberries as blueberries and 3 times as many oranges as strawberries. If there are 172 fruits in total, how many oranges are there?
Chris sells orange juice at $2.50 per cup, while John sells iced coffee at $3.00. If Chris sold 7 cups and John sold 5 cups, how much more money (in dollars) did Chris earn than John?

Mike is moving to another country for further studies. He needs to ship several items to his new place. He knows each of his books weighs 13 ounces. The courier service has a limit of 15 pounds for each package. Mike puts 30 books in a box but the package is too heavy. Therefore, he starts removing the books one by one. Upon removing enough books, the package becomes acceptable for shipping. How many books did Mike need to remove from the package?

A car rental company rents three types of vehicles: cars, vans, and buses. In a week, they rent a total of 200 vehicles. Cars make up 45% of the rentals, buses 30%. How many vans were rented that week?
In 10 years, Lucy will be twice as old as her sister. If Lucy is 12 years old today, how old is her sister today?

Sarah opens a coffee shop. She needs to pay rent of $800 a month and an additional 10% of rent for utilities. She has 2 employees working 8 hours a day for 6 days a week, and pays each employee $15 an hour. What are her monthly expenses to run the coffee shop?
A tree planting campaign took place in the town. Each volunteer planted 75 trees for each of the 5 days the campaign went on. If there were 20 volunteers, how many trees were planted in total?
A bus travels at an average speed of 40 miles per hour. The journey is 200 miles. How many hours does the journey take?
In a fruit market, an apple costs $1.2, a pear costs $1.5, and an orange costs $0.8. Tom bought 5 apples, 4 pears, and 6 oranges. How much did Tom spend in total?
Kevin has to read a 300 page book for his literature class. He reads at a rate of 20 pages per hour. How many hours will it take him to finish reading the book?
Lucas finds 4 beetles near the tree. Eliza sees 15 ants crawling on the ground. Jack discovers 10 butterflies on the flowers, but 3 of them fly away. How many insects are remaining?

Bobby is baking a cake for his mom's birthday. He needs to bake two layers of cake each measuring 9 inches by 13 inches. If one box of cake mix can result in a 6-inch by 9-inch layer, how many boxes does Bobby need to buy?

Lynn has a garden with flowers. She has 15 tulips and three times less roses. She can sell all the flowers for $8 each. How much money would she earn if she would sell all the flowers from her garden?
A grocery store sells oranges for $2 each. If Jake bought 6 oranges and paid with a $20 bill, how much change will Jake get?
A group of friends are sharing a packet of sweets. There are 120 sweets and 8 friends. If each friend gets the same number of sweets, how many sweets does each friend get?
Ann went shopping and bought 5 shirts for $25 each, 3 skirts for $45 each, and 4 dresses at $75 each. How much did Ann spend in total?
A store sells 5 mangos for $10. How much would it cost to buy 20 mangos?
Kelly can finish 6 books per week. She got a speed reading course which improved her reading speed by Twenty five percent per week. How many books can Kelly finish in a month?
A group of otters is called a family. There are 10 families of otters on a natural reserve. Each family has an average of 8 newborns per season. How many newborns are expected this season?
Emily wants to buy a dress. The dress costs $80. She has saved $45 by doing chores. How much more money does Emily need to buy the dress?
Throughout a year, Scott was trying to save money. He put away 5% of his salary each month. If Scott earns $2400 a month, how much would he save in a year?
In a farm, there are 40 chickens and 20 cows. If the owner sold half of the chickens and a quarter of the cows, how many animals are left in the farm?

Paul goes to the fair and buys tickets for rides. He spends $30 on tickets, with each ticket costing $3. Later, he wins 5 more tickets at a game stall. After going on several rides, he realizes he has 7 tickets left. How many rides has he been on?

At her job, Lisa earns 1 day of vacation for every 8 days worked. This year, Lisa has worked 240 days and took 3 days off in July and twice as many in November. How many more vacation days can Lisa still take?
One set of game cards contains 35 cards of four suits - hearts, diamonds, clubs, and spades. If each suit has the same number of cards, how many cards are there per suit?

Jasmine and Aladdin went to the market with a total of $100. Jasmine bought a lamp for $12, spices for $8, and some fabrics for $20. Aladdin spent the remaining money on rugs. If one rug costs $5, how many rugs can Aladdin buy?

A greenhouse can water 15 five gallon pots (each pot filled two thirds of its capacity) in two hours. If each pot is filled to full capacity instead, how long, in hours, will it take to water 20 pots?

A group of 4 friends is going to a concert. The ticket for each friend costs $35. Additionally, they will spend an average of $20 each for food and drinks. How much will they all need to spend in total?

In a basketball game, Player A scores 15 points, Player B scores double the points Player A scores, and Player C scores triple the points that Player A scores. What is the total number of points scored by the team?

Sarah has saved $8000 for a trip around the world. A plane ticket, with all taxes included, costs $600 per destination. If Sarah needs to keep $200 for a visa and double that amount for accommodation per destination, how many destinations can Sarah visit?

James goes to the grocery store and buys four cartons of milk costing $3 each, two packets of chips costing $2 each and three packs of soda costing $4 each. He also treats himself to a box of cookies for $5. How much does James spend at the grocery store?
If Jim works 6 hours a day for 5 days a week and earns $12 per hour, how much will he earn in 2 weeks?
In a farm, there are 27 cows. Each cow produces about 2.5 gallons of milk each day. If the farmer has already collected 15 gallons today, how many more gallons can he collect before the day ends?
Emma's laptop has an 8 gb memory chip. Her memory is four times this amount. If Emma replaces her memory with an additional 16 gb chip, what will be the total memory of Emma's laptop in gigabytes?
Alexis can bake 15 cupcakes in an hour. If she bakes for 4 hours, how many cupcakes can she make?
Alice baked 30 cupcakes for a party. At the party, 4 guests ate 2 cupcakes each and Alice ate 3 cupcakes. How many cupcakes were left?
A bee collects nectar from 5 flowers in an hour. If a bee collects nectar for 4 hours every day, how many flowers does it visit in a day?
James has 65 roses for sale. He sold 40% of the roses in the morning, and then in the afternoon he sold 25% more of the remaining. How many roses does James have left?
A baker made 50 cupcakes for a bake sale. 1/5 was chocolate flavor, 1/4 was vanilla flavor, and the rest was strawberry flavor. How many strawberry cupcakes did the baker make?

During a school trip, the school bus traveled 45 km, 52 km, and 40 km on three consecutive days. The round trip distance to the school was 280 km. How much shorter was the school bus ride compared to the driving distance to and from school?
Sophia raised 5 more chickens than Jimmy. Jimmy raised 3 chickens. Those 3 chickens each laid 2 eggs. How many chickens does Sophia have?
A school has 5 classes with 25 students each. One day, 3 students from each class are absent. How many students are present at school that day?
A shop sells pencils for $2 each and pens for $3 each. If Jane bought 7 pencils and 3 pens from the shop, how much money did she spend in total?
A recipe requires 150 grams of sugar to make a cake. If John has 2 kilograms of sugar, how many cakes can he make?

An employee makes 30 dollars an hour for the first 40 hours in the workweek and an additional 50% for every hour above 40 in the week.  If he works 6 hours for the first 3 days in the workweek and twice as many hours per day for the remaining 2 days, how much money did he make?
Julie had 50 balloons. 30% of them are pink, 40% are blue and the rest are yellow. How many yellow balloons does she have?

Jacob's brother had 10 comic books in his collection. Last month he bought triple the number of comic books he had and then gifted half of what he had. How many comic books does Jacob's brother have now?

Tom and Jerry run a relay marathon. Tom runs at a speed of 5 miles/hour and Jerry at a speed of 6 miles/hour. If the marathon is 66 miles long, how long will it take them to complete the marathon if they run alternately each hour?
In her garden, Anna has a total of 67 tomatoes. 22 of these are green, and the rest are ripe. If each ripe tomato weighs about 150 grams, what is the weight of all ripe tomatoes together?

Jenna divides her weekly allowance between food, transport and savings. She spends a third of her money on food, half of the remaining money on transport, and she saves the rest. If Jenna saves $15 a week, how much is her weekly allowance?
A meal and a drink at a restaurant cost $18. The meal costs three times as much as the drink. How much does the drink cost?

A group of friends decided to bake a cake. Cindy and Susan were the ones who prepared the ingredients that cost $15 and $20 respectively. Meanwhile, Mark and Jill prepared the baking tools that cost $18 and $22 respectively. After baking, they sold the cake for $100. How much profit did they make?
In Martha's garden, 9 red roses, 7 pink roses, and 12 yellow roses bloom. She picks 8 roses for a bouquet. How many roses are left in Martha's garden?

Simon is picking blueberries to make blueberry pies. He picks 100 blueberries from his own bushes and another 200 blueberries from blueberry bushes growing nearby. If each pie needs 100 blueberries, how many blueberry pies can Simon make?
There are 50 cookies in a jar. Jenny eats 20% of the cookies and then shares the remaining cookies equally with her brother. How many cookies does Jenny's brother get?
Two zombies have unleashed an apocalypse in the city, population 5000. On the first day, each of them infected 8 people, turning them into zombies. How many zombies were there after Day 1?
Adam is organizing a book club and wants to buy 5 copies of a book for the club members. The book costs $12 per copy. If Adam already has $20, how much more money does he need to buy the books?

Julie is a fitness trainer who works five hours a day. For every hour, half of the time is spent on weight training and the rest on aerobic exercises. How many hours each day does Julie spend on weight training?
There are 25 boys and 15 girls in the fourth grade and twice that number in the fifth grade. How many students are in grades 4 and 5?
Bella earns $50,000 a year working full-time. She wants to save up 25% of her income each year. Her dream vacation costs 10% of her yearly income. How long would it take her to save for her vacation?
If a gallon of paint covers 400 square feet, and a room is 1200 square feet, how many gallons of paint are needed to cover the room?

A bag of half a dozen mangoes costs $18 and Lucy has already spent $16 on apples and twice that much on oranges. What's the maximum number of mangoes Lucy can buy if she left her house with only $100 and needs to pay the $5 bus fare each way?
Eric needs to read 500 pages for school. He read 120 pages in the first week and 150 in the second week. How many pages does he still need to read?
A movie theater has 10 rows with 8 seats in each row. If the movie had a full house with all seats occupied, but 3 people left during the movie, how many people were still watching the movie?
Robin baked 4 trays of muffins for a bake sale. Each tray holds 12 chocolate muffins and 10 vanilla muffins. If he sold all the muffins at $2 each, how much money did he make?

A supermarket has 6000 canned goods in inventory at the start of the week. On Monday 3000 were sold, on Tuesday 1000 were sold, and 100 cans each day for the remainder of the week were sold (from Wednesday until Sunday). On Friday, the supplier delivers an order for 800 cans. How many canned goods does the supermarket have at the end of the week?

Lily has 4 different types of flowers in her garden: tulips, roses, sunflowers, and daisies. She has twice as many tulips as she does roses, and triple the amount of sunflowers as daisies. If she has 100 flowers in total and the same amount of daisies as roses, how many tulips does she have?
Lara, Sara, and Tara are collecting seashells on the beach. Lara finds 12 seashells, Sara finds 17 seashells, and Tara finds 14 seashells. How many seashells did they find on average?
A coffee shop sells a small coffee for $3 and a large coffee for $5. Linda buys 4 small coffees and 3 large coffees. If she gives the cashier a $50 bill, how much change will she receive?

Ron can type 48 words in a minute. Sally can type twice as many words per minute as Ron can. Bob can type half as many words per minute as Ron can. If all of them types for 10 minutes, what is the total numbers of words they have typed?

If a park has 12 benches and each bench can seat 3 people, how many people can the park seat at a time? Now if half of the benches are not available due to maintenance, how many people can the park seat?
A train travels at a speed of 60 miles per hour for 2 hours. Then the speed was increased by 50% for the next 2 hours. How far did the train travel in total?
Ahmed purchased books for himself and his two friends from a bookstore. Each book cost $25. On top of that, he paid a $15 delivery charge. How much did Ahmed pay in total?
Sally is packing gift boxes for a charity. Each gift box contains 3 toys and 2 books. If Sally has packed 120 toys and 80 books, how many gift boxes has she packed?

A coach is stocking water bottles for a marathon. Each runner needs 3 water bottles, and will be part of a team with 10 other runners. There are a total of 20 teams. How many water bottles will the coach need to make sure every runner has enough?
Bobby has 20 friends on his basketball team. His school team is 50% larger. How many people are there in total?

Mary bakes 3 batches of cookies each day for 5 days before her party. Each batch contains 12 cookies. On the day of the party, she bakes an additional 2 batches. How many cookies did she bake in total?

Samuel has a book collection. One Monday he bought 5 books. On Tuesday, he bought twice as many books as he did on Monday. On Wednesday, he bought 3 books less than he did on Tuesday. How many total books did Samuel buy from Monday to Wednesday?
There are 80 students in a school. Half of them play baseball and 1/4 of the students who play baseball also play soccer. How many students play both baseball and soccer?
Grant's catering service made 320 sandwiches for a corporate event. If 60% of these were vegetarian, with the rest being meat sandwiches, how many meat sandwiches were there?

In a kindergarten classroom, there are 12 children. Each child has a box of 24 crayons. The teacher asks the children to share all their crayons equally among themselves. How many crayons does each child end up with?
Velma visited an amusement park where she can play unlimited rides with an entrance fee of $50. After visiting the park 6 times in a month, how much did she spend in total?
Tiffany is saving up to buy a new video game console that costs $500. She saves $30 each week. How many weeks will it take for her to save up enough money?
Tim has 60 toy cars. He gave one fourth of the cars to his younger brother, and he still had twice as many cars as his friend John. How many toy cars does John have?

A baseball stadium sells tickets for $25 each. On a particular game day, 3200 tickets are sold. If the stadium has to pay $8000 in taxes and $2000 in staff wages, how much profit does the stadium make?
Jerry has 5 apples, Julia gives him 3 more and then Rick give him twice the number of apples that Julia gave him. How many apples does Jerry have now?
Tom's toy car factory produces 200,000 cars every month. If he continued producing at this rate, how many cars has Tom's toy car factory produced in two years?
A fruit stand sold 59 oranges. Twenty customers bought two oranges, five customers bought three oranges, and the rest bought one orange. How many customers bought one orange?
A pack of pens costs $10 and each pack contains 5 pens. Emily bought 3 packs and gave 2 pens to each of her 5 friends. How many pens does Emily have left?
A library has 4 shelves of books. Each shelf has 35 books. If 20% of these books are borrowed, how many books are left?

John has a green apple tree and a red apple tree in his garden. The green apple tree produced 30 apples, the red one produced double that. If John sells each apple for $1, how much money will he make?
Christina baked cookies for her friends. She made 15 chocolate chip cookies and half as many peanut butter cookies. How many cookies did she make in total?

Henry has decided to repaint his barn. He has a barn that's 60 feet long, 30 feet wide, and 20 feet high. The paint costs $25 per gallon, and a gallon of paint covers 400 square feet. How much will Henry spend on paint if he's painting the barn's four walls (two of them are half-size because of the sloped roof)?

The school canteen has 100 donuts and 80 muffins. The price of a donut is $1.25 and a muffin is $0.75. By the end of the day, there are only 20 donuts and 10 muffins left. How much money did the canteen make from selling donuts and muffins?
A theater has 150 seats and the ticket price for a movie is $8. If the theater is filled to 75% capacity, how much does the movie theater make?
Lisa planted a tree that grows at a rate of 20 cm per year. If the tree was 25 cm tall when it was planted, how tall will the tree be in 4 years?

Alice decides to build a swimming pool in her backyard. She wants the width of the pool to be about a seventh of the width of her backyard, and the length to be a fifth of the length of her backyard. Her backyard is 700 yards wide and 500 yards long. How many square yards is her swimming pool going to be?
Mr. Ray's house is 15 meters wide and 18 meters long. His garden is square shaped, the same width as the house. What is the difference in area between the house and the garden in square meters?
A classroom has 6 tables and 4 chairs per table. If 3 chairs are broken and not usable, how many students can the classroom hold?

Jacob is arranging his bookshelf. He has 15 books about sports, 25 books about history, and 10 books about science. He wants to create another section for cooking books. In order to have the same number of books in each section, how many cooking books does he need to buy?

400 people were invited to a concert. Around 60% of the invited people showed up. Given that 30% of the people who showed up had forged tickets and were not allowed into the event, how many invited people attended the concert?

In a soccer tournament, the total number of teams in 2017 doubled in 2018. In 2019, 3/4 of the total number of teams in 2018 came to participate. If the total number of teams in 2019 was 24, how many teams participated in the tournament in 2017?

David played a video game which ran for 2.5 hours. In this duration, there were 4 intervals, each 15 minutes long, for rest and refreshments. How long (in hours) was his gameplay excluding the rest intervals?

There are 60 workers in a factory. 25% of the workers are in charge of quality control and the rest are machine operators. Each machine operator can produce 20 pieces per day. How many pieces can all the machine operators produce in a day?
Cheryl wants to put a fence around her rectangular yard that measures 60 feet by 40 feet. If each fence panel is 10 feet long and costs $15, how much will she spend on fence panels?
A podcast episode is 40 minutes long and it takes 25% longer to record each episode. If they release 3 episodes each week, how many hours will it take to record 2 weeks of episodes?
70% of Lily's friends like chocolate. If she has 50 friends and the rest prefer vanilla, how many of her friends prefer vanilla?
A toy store sold 60% of its toys during the Christmas season. If there were 5000 toys, how many were sold during the Christmas season?
Ian had $45. He spent $18 on a book, $5 on a snack, and the rest on a shirt. How much did the shirt cost?
A bookstore had 80 books. It sold 25% of these books in the morning and another 50% in the afternoon. How many books does it have left?

Sarah has a bookstore. She sells each book for $15. If she sells 500 books in a week, and uses 2/5 of the sales to restock her bookstore. How much money does Sarah remain with after setting aside the money for bookstore restocking?
After a shift at the restaurant, Connor received 50 more tips than Aidan. If Aidan received $200, how much did the restaurant give the boys in tips total?
Paul spent $75 on 5 shirts. The day after, he bought 3 pairs of shoes, each pair cost him twice as much as a shirt. How much did Paul spend altogether?

In a basketball training session, James makes 5 free throws on Monday, doubles this count on Tuesday, and on Wednesday, he makes 4 less than on Tuesday. On Thursday, he makes 5 more than on Monday. How many free throws did James make in total?
Sara buys a pack of stickers for $15. She uses it 5 times a week for 3 weeks. How much did she spend on each use?
Peter earns $12 an hour working in a bookstore. He works 7 hours a day. If he works all weekdays for a month which is 20 days, calculate his monthly salary.
In a zoo there are 12 lions and 3 times more elephants than lions. How many elephants are in the zoo?

Joyce has a fruit stand where she sells apples and oranges. She bought 30 apples and 20 oranges from a local farm. She sells one apple at $0.50 and one orange at $0.40. If she was able to sell all her fruits, how much money did she make from her fruit stand?
There are 48 cats in a shelter and there are 70% more dogs than cats. How many animals are there in the shelter?
Four neighborhood gyms have 820 members. The first three have 100, 200 and 300 members. How many members does the fourth gym have?
Gary and Linda are selling lemonade for 50 cents a glass. If they sell 30 glasses, how much money will they make?

Oliver has to deliver three types of newspapers. He delivers 50 copies of the local newspaper, 30 copies of the national newspaper, and 20 copies of the international newspaper every day. How many newspapers does he deliver in a week?
Jenny bought a car worth $12000. Each year, the car's value depreciates by 10%. How much will the car be worth after 3 years?
Elaine is reading a four-chapter novel with 80 pages. Each chapter has five pages more than the previous one. What is the number of pages in the first chapter?
Joseph bought a bundle of 4 games online for $80. Later, Joseph realized he was overcharged because each game was actually priced at $15. How much did the online store refund Joseph?
Kat has a 10-hour flight. She wants to watch movies every 2 hours to relax. She also needs 3 additional breaks for meals. If each break takes 30 minutes, how many hours will her flight become?
A car consumes gasoline at a rate of 5 liters per 100 kilometers. If Sally drives 50 kilometers to work and back every day, how much gasoline is she consuming in a week?

Debby uses an app to keep track of her time spent on completing a particular task. Last week she finished the task in 45 hours. This week, she managed to finish it in 30% less time. How many hours did Debby spend this week completing the task?

A local bakery offers 24 different types of pastries. The bakery incorporates cherries into 3 pastries, uses blackberries in a quarter of the pastries, and blueberries in 1 pastry. Jeremy, who does not prefer berries, is open to trying a pastry where he can remove the berries himself from one pastry. How many pastry options does Jeremy have?
Jill's garden produces 4 pounds of tomatoes each week. Jack's garden produces twice the quantity of Jill's garden. If they combine their weekly produce, how many pounds of tomatoes do they have?
Carmen has an apartment that she rents out for $750 a month. After a year, she decides to increase the rent by 15%. How much will the rent be after the increase?
Sam purchased 10 pounds of apples and 5 pounds of grapes. If the price of apples is $2 per pound and the grapes are $3 more expensive per pound than the apples, what did Sam spend in total?
A box contains 6 packs of candies. Each pack contains 10 pieces of candies. If a child ate 4 candies, how many candies are left in the box?
A garden has an area of 600 square feet, and is planted with tulips in a grid, each one taking up 2 square feet. How many tulips are planted in the garden?

Mike runs an ice cream shop that sells 1 scoop of ice cream for $3. He recently decided to offer a discount and sold 3 scoops of ice cream for $7. If a customer buys 3 scoops of ice cream, how much does the customer save now?
A school has 12 teachers with an average age of 40 years and 8 students with an average age of 20 years. What is the average age of all 20 individuals in the school?
Alan saves $15.00 a week for 3 weeks. Then he saves $20.00 a week for the next 3 weeks. Then he saves $30.00 a week for the next 3 weeks. How much money has he saved over 9 weeks?

Jessica saved $652.00 in the bank from her profits in selling baked goods. She used $145.00 to buy baking ingredients and $29.00 for the utility bills. How much money left does Jessica have in the bank after paying for these expenses?
A store is selling candies in packs. Each pack contains 12 candies. Tommy wants to buy 72 candies. How many packs does Tommy need to buy?
A can of paint covers an area of 9 square meters. If a wall is 8 meters long and 6 meters high, how many cans of paint will be needed to paint the wall?
James wants to buy pencils and rulers. A pencil costs $1 and a ruler costs $3. If he buys 5 pencils and 3 rulers, how much will he pay in total?
Alex cuts 4 pieces from a 20m rope. If each piece is the same length, how long is each piece?

Billy can finish painting 10 square meters in 20 minutes. Kim can paint 7 square meters in 20 minutes and Jo can paint 12 square meters in 20 minutes. If they have been painting for 80 minutes now, how many square meters have they painted in total?
Cindy paints a mural and charges $25 per hour. If the mural takes 6 hours to paint and she also charges an additional $50 for materials, how much does Cindy make from the mural?
The town city park has an area of 300 square meters. Of this, 40% is grass, and the rest is a playground. What is the area of the playground in square meters?

Karen’s work tote bag is twice the weight of her husband Kevin’s briefcase when the briefcase is empty. When Kevin puts his laptop and work papers in his briefcase, it is twice the weight of Karen’s tote. Kevin’s work papers are a sixth of the weight of the contents of his full briefcase. If Karen’s tote weighs 8 pounds, how many more pounds does Kevin’s laptop weigh than Karen’s tote?
Jack has a grove with 4 trees by 5 trees.  It takes 6 minutes to clean each tree.  He gets help to clean the trees so it only takes half as long.  How many hours did he spend cleaning trees?

A zoo has 5 giraffes, 6 lions, 9 monkeys and 2 hippos. If the zookeeper needs to feed each giraffe 4 bunches of leaves, each lion 3 pieces of meat, and each monkey 2 bananas while a hippo needs 7 watermelons, how much food does the zookeeper need in total?
Emma bought 5 kg of fruits. 20% of the fruits were apple, 50% were oranges and the rest were pears. How many kgs of pears did she buy?

Amanda baked some cupcakes for a charity sale. She sold half of them in the morning and 15 more in the afternoon. If at the end of the day she was left with 5 cupcakes, how many did she bake at first?

There are 300 students at a small school. 20% of them enter a mathematics competition. The competition takes place in 6 rounds and, in each round, half of the contestants are eliminated. After completing all 6 rounds, how many contestants remain?
David has 45 stickers. His sister gives him some more, and now he has 135 stickers. How many stickers did his sister give him?
Alice makes 5 mistakes on the test, while Andy only got 3 errors. If the test is made up of 40 questions, how many more correct answers does Andy have than Alice?

James is running a marathon training program. In the first week, he runs 5 miles a day. Every week after that, James increases his daily run distance by an additional 2 miles. How many miles will James be running per day at the end of 4 weeks?
John played in a city chess tournament. If the tournament had 200 games and 18% of them resulted in him as a winner, how many games did John win?

Melissa wanted to save money for her college. For the first year, she saved $200 every month. For the next 2 years, due to her part-time job, she managed to save $300 every month. How much did Melissa save in total for her college studies?
A movie theater has 10 rows and each row has 15 seats. One day there was a popular movie showing and 75% of the seats were filled. How many seats were occupied?
At the town fair, there are 25 fireflies caught in a jar. If 3 fireflies escape every hour, how many hours before there will be only 10 fireflies left in the jar?

A group of friends decided to split the bill for a birthday dinner. The total cost of the meal was $200, and the birthday girl did not have to pay. Among the remaining 5 friends, two of them agreed to pay 30% of the bill each, and the other three agreed to split the remaining bill equally. How much did each of the three friends pay?
Lucas works at a toy factory packing toys. He can package 3 boxes of toys in 4 minutes. Each box has a dozen toys. How many individual toys can he package in 3 hours if each hour has 60 minutes?
If Sarah buys a book and pays a 10% sales tax on a purchase price of $75. If she gives a bookseller a $100 bill, how much change should she receive?
Lucas collects stamps. He bought 25 stamps from different countries for $3 each. He also bought 10 rare stamps for $5 each. How much did Lucas spend on his stamps collection?
There are 45 chocolates in a box. If John eats 2/5 of the chocolate, how many chocolates are left?
A restaurant sold pizzas for $20 each. If they sold 85 pizzas in a day and used 75% of the money earned to pay the employees, how much money did they have left?
A fruit shop sells apples at $1 each and oranges at $2 each. Ben bought 15 apples and 7 oranges. How much did he spend in total?

Lucy filled up her new 30 foot rectangular pool in her backyard using three hoses. One hose releases water at a rate of 3 gallons per minute, the other two each release water at a rate of 2 gallons per minute. The pool has a volume of 18,000 gallons. How many hours will it take to fill up the pool?
Sam works at a video rental store. Renting each video costs $4. If a customer rented 3 action movies, 4 comedies and 5 dramas, then how much did the customer pay in total?
Joan bought 3 notebooks for $4 each and 5 pens for $2 each. How much did she spend in total?
Alex made 6 trays of cookies, with 10 cookies in each tray. He gave away 1/2 of the cookies to his friends, then ate 1/6 of the remaining and put the rest away. How many cookies did Alex put away?

Michael runs for 4 kilometers every day for his morning exercise. He wants to increase his distance by 2 kilometers each week for ten weeks. How many kilometers will Michael be running each day at the end of ten weeks?
Dasia has 4 packs of 50 crayons. She uses these packs to fill up pencil boxes. Each pencil box can hold 25 crayons. How many pencil boxes does she fill?
There are 20 apples on a tree. Johnny picks 5 apples and Jenny picks twice as many as Johnny. How many apples are left on the tree?
A farm harvested 20 apple trees. Each tree yields 15 apples. If each apple basket can hold 30 apples, how many apple baskets did the farm fill?
Chris has $20 and he saw a shirt he liked which cost $15. He also saw a hat worth $7. How much more money does he need to buy both the shirt and the hat?
If an apple tree produces 18 apples every day, how many apples does it produce in a year of 365 days?

Laura wants to send her mom a bouquet of roses and lilies for her birthday. The online store sells roses for $6 each and lilies for $8 each. She buys 5 roses and decides to spend the remaining $20 in her budget on lilies. How many lilies can Laura buy?
A grocery store has 1250 apples, 65% of which are green and the rest are red. How many red apples does the grocery store have?

Julie drives at three times the speed of her brother, John. If it takes Julie 25 minutes to travel from Richmond to Charlottesville, how long, in minutes, will it take John to travel the same distance?
Bill spent 3 hours reading on Saturday and twice that time on Sunday. What was his total reading time on the weekend?
Kim's dinner at a restaurant costs $15.25, but she has a discount code for $5.75. If she also gets an additional 10% student discount, how much does she pay for her dinner in total?
Tom wants to treat his friends to ice cream cones that cost $0.5 each. He has $4. How many friends can he treat to ice cream?

Ted works in a restaurant and earns $9 per hour plus 20% commission of his total service charge. Last week, he worked for 32 hours and his total service charge is $1500. Deductions for taxes and other benefits is 10% of his total weekly earning. How much is left from his income after the deductions?

A bag can hold 30 kilograms of potatoes. Peter has 4 bags which he bought for $200. He wants to sell the potatoes for $3 per 1 kilogram but 5 kilograms of potatoes were rotten. How much will be Peter's profit from selling those potatoes?
A bakery produces 1200 muffins during the night, double that amount during the morning and three times as the night during the afternoon. How many muffins does the bakery produce a day?
Jon planted 2 apple trees and 4 mango trees in his garden. Each apple tree has 40 apples, while each mango tree has 60 mangoes. How many fruits does Jon have in total from all the trees?
There are 120 students in a school. If each student in the school has 4 textbooks and a teacher has five times the number of textbooks a student has. How many textbooks does 1 teacher have?
Leonardo has $200. He visited a bookstore and bought 5 books for $18 each. He also decided to get 4 notebooks for $10 each. How much money, in dollars, does he have left?
Tom is twice as old as Jerry. In 5 years, Tom will be 30 years old. How old is Jerry now?
A group of friends went on a road trip. They decided to evenly split the cost of gas. The total cost of gas was $120 and there were 4 friends. How much did each friend pay?
Ron ate pizza with his friends the other day. If they ordered a 12-slice pizza and each of them ate 4 slices, how many friends were there with Ron?
A bottle can hold 600ml of liquid. A pump fills the bottle with water at a rate of 20ml per second. How many seconds will it take to fill the bottle?
Emily reads 15 pages of a book every day. After 7 days, she has read 105 pages. How many more pages does she have to read if the book is 250 pages in total?

Sandra makes 72 bracelets every week. She sells them at a local market each week. Mary sells 20 less bracelets than Sandra and Helen sells half of what Mary sells. How many bracelets do they sell in total every week?
Luke works at a bookstore. If the bookstore is open 6 days a week and he worked 5 shifts a week for 3 weeks, how many shifts did Luke work?
Kate’s mother gave her $500 for doing well in school. Kate decided to share it with her three friends, so she gave each of them 1/5 of $500. How many dollars are left for Kate?

Samantha has a collection of vintage postcards. She bought 25 new postcards on her trip to Europe and gave 8 to her friend Emily. Now she has 78 postcards. How many postcards did Samantha have before her trip?
A football player made 15 field goals in the regular season. Each field goal is worth 3 points. How many total points did he score off the field goals?
Peter saves $2 more each day than Maria. If Maria saves $3 each day, how much would they save together in a week?
Amy has a book and a notebook. She bought the notebook at 2/3 the price of the book. The book cost $9. How much did Amy pay for both the book and the notebook?
Mary has $500 in her savings account. She withdraws $20 each week for 6 weeks and then deposits $100 at the end of the 6 weeks. How much money does Mary have left in her account after 6 weeks?

Katrina saw an ad that said if she signed up for her local recycling program, she could earn $5.00.  When she signed up, they told her for every friend that she referred, the friend would receive $5.00 and she would receive another $5.00 per friend.  That day, she had 5 friends sign up and another 7 friends by the end of the week.  How much money in total did she and her friends make?
A pond contains 90 gallons of water. Each day, evaporation causes it to lose 3 gallons of water. After a week (7 days), how much water remains?

Each of Bill's pigs weighs 50 pounds. Every 20 pounds the pigs weigh, they need 1 pound of pig food per day. His five pigs include two that weigh twice the standard weight. How much pig food does he need each day?
Ali bought a TV, which was on a 25% discount. If the original price was $1,200, how much did he pay?
A hockey team won 70 % of their first 40 games. In their next 60 games, they won 60% of matches. How many total games did they win?
Amy is helping her mom buy gifts for her younger siblings. The Teddy bear costs $10, the puzzle costs $15, and two books cost $5 each. If Amy chips in $20, how much will her mom have to pay?

Alex starts a new fitness plan which includes 10 push-ups a day, but each day he adds 5 push-ups from the day before for the course of two weeks. On his first day, he does the initial 10. How many push-ups has Alex done after the to weeks?
Brianna and Anna went to a candy store. Brianna bought 5 candies and Anna bought double the amount Brianna bought. How many candies they got in total?
Tommy hires a bike for $5 per hour. He hires 2 bikes for 5 hours and pays with a $100 bill. How much change will he get?
A water tank can hold 150 gallons of water. If the tank is filled at a rate of 10 gallons per minute, how long will it take to fill the water tank?
A library contains 40 books. 20% of the books are non-fiction. How many fiction books are in the library?
Peter's weekly grocery shopping includes 4 cartons of milk for $3 each, 2 lbs of chicken for $7 each and 5 loaves of bread for $2 each. How much does he pay for his groceries that week?
In a town, there are 4 blocks of buildings that each have 5 buildings, and each building has 10 apartments. How many apartments are there in total in the town?

Luke's lemonade stand sells a glass of lemonade for $2.50. In a day, he can sell up to 150 glasses of lemonade. However, there was a rainy day where he only sold 60% of his usual quantity. How much money did Luke make that day?

Eve and Clara decide to paint their rooms. Each can of paint costs $20. Eve buys 3 cans of paint and Clara buys 2. They also decide to buy a painting each, which costs them $50 each. How much did they both spend in total?

In a dog show, the German Shepherds category recorded 5 dogs, Dalmatians category was twice as many, while the Spike category was decreased by 2 from the Dalmatians. How many dogs were there in all the categories?
In a pizza parlor, one pan pizza costs $15 and a roll costs $5. If Mike goes to the parlor with $150 and buys 5 pan pizzas and 10 rolls, calculate the money he will receive back.
David saw a rug that costs $250.  The rug had a 15% discount and he had a coupon for another 25% off. How much did he pay for the rug after the discounts?
A bakery sells donuts for $1 each and muffins for $2 each. If John buys 7 donuts and 5 muffins, how much does he spend?
A school decides to purchase chairs for 8 classrooms. Each classroom requires 25 chairs. Given that each chair costs 20 dollars, how much money will the school spend on purchasing chairs?
A carpenter can make 5 chairs in a day. If he works 5 days a week and takes 2 weeks to complete a order, how many chairs can he make?

Daniel is a basketball player. He scored 3 points in the first quarter of the game. In the second quarter, he scored twice as many points as in the first quarter. In the third quarter, he scored 5 points less than in the second quarter. At the end of the game after the fourth quarter, he had scored a total of 18 points. How many points did Daniel score in the fourth quarter?
In Tim's art class, there are 4 more girls than boys. If the total number of students in his class is 24, how many boys are there?
Mike was given $100 by his father to buy clothes for himself. He bought 1 jacket that costs $40 and 2 pairs of trousers that cost $20 each. How much money should Mike return to his father?

In his candy box, Tom removed 5 green candies from the first box, 7 from the second box, 4 from the third and fourth boxes, and 6 from the fifth box. What is the average number of green candies in a candy box?
Henry works as a pizza maker. Every day, he makes three times as many pizzas as he did the day before. On the fourth day, he makes 81 pizzas. How many pizzas did Henry make on the first day?

Melissa wants to cover a wall in her room with wallpaper. The wall is 8 feet wide and 10 feet high. If wallpaper is sold by the roll, and each roll covers 30 square feet, how many rolls does she need to buy?
Jack bought a secondhand car for $5000. Later, he sold the car for 20% more than the amount he paid for it. How much did Jack sell the car for?
Petra read 20 more than four times the pages that John read. If John read 500 pages, how many pages did Petra read?
There are 50 rows in a movie theater. Each row has 22 seats. If the movie theater is full, how many people are there in the movie theater?
Todd has a collection of 45 comic books. He gives 15 to his brother and then buys 25 more at a comic book store. How many comic books does Todd have now?

Mickey and Donald are saving up for a trip. Mickey saves $305 every month, while Donald saves $180 every month. After 5 months, they decided to equally split their savings. How much does each person receive?
John has twice as many stamps as Linda. Linda has 15 more stamps than Benjamin, who has a collection of 25 stamps. How many stamps do John and Linda have combined?
Jessica wants to tip 15% on a $300 bill. Her husband wants to contribute $20. What additional amount should Jessica add to the tip?
Kate had 32 bunnies and 25 guinea pigs before she sold 5 of the bunnies. How many more bunnies than Guinea pigs does Kate have now?
One suit requires 6 yards of fabric. Emily needs to make 6 suits for a play and she has 18 feet of fabric. How many more feet of fabric does Emily need?
In a bakery, 5 donuts can be made from 1 cup of flour. If the bakery uses 60 cups of flour, how many donuts will the bakery be able to make?

A restaurant has a special promotion. On Mondays, pizzas are sold at half the usual price. A customer orders 4 pizzas on Monday and pays $48. What's the total cost of ordering the same number of pizzas on any other day of the week?
Ella has 135 stickers. She gives 5 to each of her 18 classmates. How many stickers does she have left?

Sarah has a rectangular garden that she wants to fence. The length of the garden is 20 meters, and the width is 15 meters. If Sarah fences all around the garden, how many meters of fence will she need?

Brian has a large collection of comic books. He counted and found that he had 2,500 comic books. His friend, John, gave him 23 more comic books for his birthday. How many comic books does Brian have now?
Samantha wants to buy 1 and one-third dozens of notebooks. How many notebooks will she have?

The total distance to school and back home is 12 kilometers. If Maria walks to school at the speed of 4 km per hour and rides her bicycle back home at a speed of 12 km per hour, how long is her total travel time in minutes?
A classroom has 20 students. If there are 5 rows in the classroom and every row has the same number of students, how many students are in each row?

In a pizza party, each person has to contribute $15. After the party, it was noticed that the total expenses were $200 more than the total contribution. If there were 50 people at the party, how much money was used for the party expenses?

Lucien is a freelance graphic designer. This month, he bought $100 worth of design software, paid $50 for his internet, and his other miscellaneous expenses amounted to a quarter of his total bill. How much is Lucien's overall expenditure this month?
A restaurant serves 25 customers for lunch and double that for dinner. If the restaurant is open seven days a week, how many customers does it serve in a week?
Sarah collects 5 seashells at the beach every day. If she started with 15 seashells, how many seashells will she have after 12 days?
In a parking lot, there are 28 cars. Each car has 4 wheels. How many total wheels are there in the parking lot?
Elsa builds 7 snowmen each day. After 5 days, she finds that some snowmen have melted. If she is now left with 29 snowmen in total, how many snowmen have melted?

A basketball coach promises to treat his team to a pizza party if they can make a total of 300 successful shots within a week. Each successful shot earns 3 points. If there are 12 members in the team, how many successful shots does each member need to make within a week to get the pizza party?
A bag contains 60 marbles, 50% are blue, 20% are red. How many are not blue or red?
A company ordered 25 pieces of chairs for every 5 offices. How many chairs does each office receive?
In a baseball field, there are 4 bases. If the distance between each base is 90 feet, what is the total distance a baseball player needs to run to make a complete round?
If water flows from a faucet at a rate of 2 gallons per minute, how long will it take to fill a 30 gallon container?

Jack spent 5 hours playing video games last week. He spent three times as long drawing as he did playing video games, and he spent half as much time reading as he did drawing. How many hours did Jack spend reading last week?
The price of a bag of almonds costs $8 and a bag of walnuts costs $10. Harry plans to buy 60 bags of each type of nut and he has a 5% store discount. How much would he pay in total?
Bob wants to drink at least 8 glasses of water every day for a week. On Monday, he drank 2 glasses. Every day after, he added an extra glass of water. How many glasses did he drink on Sunday?

Mary is packing up box lunches for a picnic. She puts 4 sandwiches in each box and adds 2 apples. When she runs out of sandwich bread, she has only prepared half of the boxes. After finishing the rest of the boxes with just apples, Mary has used a total of 90 apples. How many box lunches did she prepare?
A baker makes cupcakes in batches. Each batch requires 200g of flour and 150g of sugar. If she wants to make 5 batches, how much flour and sugar does she need in total?
John runs a distance of 5 km every day for a week. How many kilometers will he run in the entire week?
A bookstore had 500 books on its shelves. They received a new shipment of books, which was 50% more than the existing number. How many books are there in the bookstore now?
A clothing store cut the prices of its jeans by $10 due to a change in fashion trends. If the original price was $70 and Nina bought 15 of such jeans, calculate the total price she paid.

Benjamin and Isaac went foot racing on a 1-mile training track. Benjamin managed to circle the track 14 times in one hour, but Isaac was tired, and he only moved at an average speed half of what Benjamin did.  What speed did Isaac run, in miles per hour?
Liam starts reading a book on Monday that has 360 pages. He reads 3/8 of the book on Monday and 1/4 of the book on Tuesday. How many pages does he have left to read on Wednesday?
John and his friends went to a pizza place that sells an 8-slice pizza for $8. If John wants to buy 3 pizzas and his friend wants to buy 2 pizzas, how much will they have to pay in total?
Mark needs 12 liters of paint to make a mural on a 20 feet wall. How many liters of paint does Mark need to make a mural on a 100 feet wall?
Alice buys a dress and a bag. The bag costs $25 more than the dress. If the total bill is $155, how much does the dress cost?
A liter of chlorine costs $10 and is now sold at 20% off. A box of soap that costs $16 is now sold at 25% off. How much will you save if you buy 3 liters of chlorine and 5 boxes of soap?
Laura read 15 pages of a book for each of 5 days. Erica read 18 pages for each of 6 days. How many more pages did Erica read than Laura?

At a live concert, tickets cost $100 each. The venue can hold a maximum of 5000 people. If the concert is sold out and 70% of the audience plans to buy a band t-shirt for $20, how much revenue will be made from ticket and t-shirt sales?

Samantha has 1500 candies in her candy shop. Every day, she sells a constant rate of 30 candies. In addition, Samantha has a younger sister named Sam, who loves to eat candies. For the first 15 days, Sam ate 5 candies per day. How many days did it take for all of the candies to be all gone from the candy shop?

A theatre group has 12 actors. Each actor has 2 costumes, and each costume has 3 accessories. The group decides to buy one more accessory for each costume. How many accessories will the theatre group have in total?

A picnic basket consists of 5 sandwiches, 6 bananas, 10 apples, 3 bottles of juice, and 2 bags of chips. One sandwich costs $4. A banana costs $0.50. An apple costs $1. A bottle of juice costs $2, and a bag of chips costs $3. What is the total cost of the picnic basket?
Thomas is training for a marathon. He runs 5 miles on Monday, 4 on Tuesday, 7 on Wednesday, 8 on Thursday, and 6 on Friday. How many miles does he run during the week?
Susan ordered a total of 4 textbooks for her college classes. Each textbook costed her $50. How much total did Susan spent for the textbooks?
Marry is saving money for a laptop. The laptop costs $500. If she saves $20 every week, how many weeks will it take her to save enough money for the laptop?
Ron prepares 48 burgers for the family picnic. He prepares 1/6 of the burgers for vegetarian guests. How many burgers did he prepare for non-vegetarian guests?
Every day Jenny drinks 3 16-oz glasses of lemonade along with 48 ounces of water. How many ounces of fluid does she drink in a week?
Jenny earned money from babysitting. She spent two-thirds of the money she made. If she worked 10 hours and on average she's able to earn $15 per hour babysitting, how much money does she have left?
James spends $65 on a new shirt and $90 on a pair of shoes. He is given a 10% discount at the counter. How much does he pay in total?
Mary is hosting a party at her house. She needs to bake 5 cakes for the party. So far, she has baked 2 chocolate cakes and 1 strawberry cake. How many more cakes does Mary need to bake?

Kelly runs a kitten shelter and recently three cats gave birth with 7 kittens each. She managed to find homes for a third of the kittens. Then, half of the remaining kittens were adopted by a local pet store. How many kittens does Kelly have left?
An aluminium bar's weight is three times a bar of silver. If an aluminium bar weighs 25 kgs more than a gold bar, and a gold bar weighs 50 kgs, calculate the total weight of 15 bars of each metal.
A group of 5 friends ordered 1 pizza each for $15. They also ordered 2 liters of soda for $5. What was the total cost?
Shelly's mom promised her $10 for every A she gets on her report card and $5 for every B. If she gets five A's and three B's, how much will she receive?

Jeremy plays basketball with his friends every Saturday.  3 of his friends play forward, 2 play guard and 1 plays center. On Sundays, he plays soccer with 8 other friends. How many friends does Jeremy play sports with during the weekend?

Beth purchased 9 boxes of hand sanitizers at $11 each. Each box contains 100 hand sanitizers. She sold 4 boxes by repackaging them into sets of 20 selling for $5 each. She sold the remaining 500 hand sanitizers in groups of 10 for $6 each. How much profit did she make?
In a marathon event, Jenna runs 15 miles each day for 6 days. If Sophie runs twice as much as Jenna on a daily basis, how many miles will they both have run after 6 days?

Mark has two hours to complete driving test. He is done with 30 out of 100 course. He has used 40 minutes of his time. If he continues with this speed, how many minutes will be left when he completes the test?
Mark took a test with 60 questions. He answered 80% of them correctly. How many questions did he miss?
Hannah has 4 bills of 10 dollars and 3 bills of 5 dollars. How much money does she have in total?

Sam drove from his hometown to a vacation spot 430 miles away. His car consumes gas at a rate of 25 miles per gallon, and it has a 20-gallon tank that he had just filled before the trip. Calculate the remaining mileage he can drive without refilling his tank after reaching his destination.
Jane has 25 daisies. She picks 15 more daisies than she already has. Alex has twice as many daisies as Jane picked. How many daisies do they have combined?

A bakery is making cupcakes for a party. They throw a cupcake away if it has a crack in it. They make 200 cupcakes, and throw away 30. They pack the rest in boxes of 20. How many boxes of cupcakes did they pack?

Adam can jog 5 miles an hour. His friend Hugo can jog 7 miles an hour. If they start in the same place and jog in straight lines in opposite directions for 4 hours, how many miles apart do they end up?
A pack of jelly beans costs $2. If Dave buys 3 packs of jelly beans and gives $10 to the cashier, how much change should he receive?

Emma wants to bake cupcakes for her birthday party. She estimates that each guest will eat 3 cupcakes and she is inviting 15 friends. Each bake batch can make 12 cupcakes and takes 20 minutes to prepare. How long will it take for Emma to prepare all the cupcakes?
Erica bakes 120 cupcakes for a party. Each cupcake needs 3 tablespoons of sugar. If sugar is sold in 60-tablespoon packets, how many packets will Erica need to buy?

Emily pays $25 per month for unlimited internet on her phone. Alternatively, a pay-as-you-go plan charges $.50 per hour of internet usage. Emily uses the internet for 50 hours a month. How much would she save on the pay-as-you-go plan?
Steve bakes 250 cookies and sells them for $4 each. He spends 1/2 of his earnings to buy ingredients for the next batch of cookies. How much money is Steve left with after buying the ingredients?
Susan bought a pack of 24 pencils. She gave 5 pencils to her friend, used 2 for her drawings and lost 1. How many pencils does Susan have now?
A group of 15 friends went to watch a movie. Each ticket costs $10. They also bought 5 buckets of popcorn for $8 each. How much did the friends spend on the movie altogether?
Kira bought a piano and decided to take lessons. If each lesson costs $20 and she takes lessons twice a week, how much does she spend on piano lessons in 2 weeks?
Mike has a toy car collection. He has 4 times as many cars as his best friend. His best friend has 7 cars. If Mike gives his friend 3 cars, how many will Mike have and his friend?
Rachel bought a dress for $200 and she used a 10% off coupon. Then she bought a pair of shoes that cost $80. How much did Rachel spend in total?
Ian bought a video game for $45 and a book for 60% of the video game's cost. How much money did Ian spend in total?
Jenny has $50 less than thrice the money Luna has. If Luna has $100, how much money do they have together?
Melinda prepared a punch that is 1 part grape juice, 2 parts orange juice, and 3 parts apple juice. If she uses 5 cups of apple juice, how many cups does the punch contain in total?
Sam saved $80 every month for 3 months. However, he had to spend $50 on textbooks. How much does Sam have now?
Sara owns 5 bunnies. She adopted 6 more bunnies. How many carrots should she buy if each bunny needs 3 carrots?
Leo saves $200 each month. After saving for 6 months, he bought a guitar worth $650 and continued saving for another 4 months. How much money does he have now?

A small cafe uses 2 kilograms of flour to make pies each day. They sold all pies and made $500, which was $250 less than they would have made selling the same number of pies without a discount. How many more dollars does a pie cost when not on discount?
Sam has a pizza shop. He sells each slice for $3. One day, he sold 15 slices in the morning, 25 slices in the afternoon, and 20 slices in the evening. How much did Sam earn in one day?
Mrs. Green bought 7 bags of apples. Each bag has 15 apples. If her family ate 45 apples in the first week, how many apples are left?
Linda has 24 pencils. She lost one third of them, so she bought a new pack of 10 pencils. How many pencils in total does Linda have after the purchase?
Jason runs 1 mile every day for a week, then 2 miles a day for the next 2 weeks. How many total miles did he run in 3 weeks?

Suzy decides to make cupcakes for a school fair. Her recipe makes 12 cupcakes, and she plans to make 8 batches. If she sells each cupcake for $3 and wants to earn $240, how many cupcakes does she need to sell?
A group of friends bought a pizza with 16 slices. If 4 of them ate equally, how many slices did each one of them have?

Emily can complete 60 squats in 5 minutes. Sarah can do 45 squats in 5 minutes and Lisa can do 50 squats in 5 minutes. If they have been doing squats for 30 minutes now, how many squats have they completed in total?
Liam bakes and decorates 30 cookies and 15 cupcakes for a charity sale. He manages to finish 17 of the cookies and 9 of the cupcakes. How many more baked goods does Liam have to decorate?
Sam has 15 books. He reads 2 books per day. How many books will he have left after 4 days?

Bob sells lemonades at a local fair to raise funds for his school. On the first day, he sells three lemonades and sells two more lemonades than the previous day each day afterward. If the fair opens five days a week and each lemonade costs 50 cents, how much will he earn in a week in dollars?

Bella loves peanut butter sandwiches. She made 150 sandwiches for the school bake sale. Each sandwich is cut in eight pieces. Half of the pieces are double stacked and the other half are regular. How many peanut butter sandwich pieces does Bella have?

There were 150 chocolate bars in a box to be distributed at a school event. Out of the 60 students taking part in the event, 70% were allowed to get chocolate bars from the box. Each student got 1 chocolate bar. How many chocolate bars remained in the box after the students took their share?

A store is selling a book for $23. If a customer buys 4 or more books, they get a discount of 15%. If a study group of five students each want a copy of the book, how much would they save if they all buy their books together rather than individually?

John's family loves pancakes. They require 4 ounces of maple syrup for each pancake. Each bottle of maple syrup John buys contains 24 ounces and costs $6. If his family eats 20 pancakes, how much do they spend on maple syrup?

Concert tickets cost $10 each on a Tuesday, three times as much on a Thursday, and four times as much as Tuesday on a Sunday. If Mary goes to the concerts on Thursday and Sunday, how much does she spend?
Liam has decided he wants to save money. He puts $2 in his piggy bank every day. How much money will he have saved after 2 months, assuming a month is 30 days?
Carol baked 5 pies for a dinner party. Each pie can be cut into 8 slices. If each dinner guest ate 2 slices of pie, how many guests can Carol feed with her pies?
Mary got a job where she earns $15 an hour and her sister Emma earns double as much as her. If they both worked for 5 hours, how much did they earn in total?
A dress costs $70. If it is sold at a 30% discount, how much does the dress cost now?
A toy store has 50 dolls in stock. Each day, they sell 3 dolls. After 10 days, how many dolls are left in the store?
Sue owns a fabric store. She has 425 yards of ribbon, and each roll of ribbon is 25 yards. How many rolls of ribbon does Sue have?

There are 5 different types of cookies in a bakery. There are twice as many chocolate chip cookies as ginger cookies and three times as many oatmeal cookies as ginger cookies. If ginger cookies amount to 10, how many cookies are in the bakery in total?
John worked 9 hours each day for 5 days. If he is being paid $15 per hour, how much will he earn in total?
Jake sells lemonade for $1.50 per cup. If he is able to sell 24 cups in one day and saves half of the profits, how many dollars did he gain at the end of the day?
There are 15 kids in the soccer team. John has 5 ping pong balls, and he buys two times as many footballs. The football team has 12 players. How many balls and players are there in total?
Anne purchased some rare coins for her collection. Initially, she had a third of the coins she bought. If she bought 600 coins, how many coins does Anne have in total after the purchase?
