Jane and Mark went to a bookstore where there was a 15% discount on all books. Jane bought 2 novels for $20 each, and Mark bought 3 memoirs for $15 each. How much did they pay in total?
The school sells pens for $4 and notebooks for $7. They sold $46 in stationary. If they sold 6 pens, how many notebooks did they sell?
Jordan has a total of 12 candies, and he gives 4 candies to his friend. If he continues to give 4 candies a day, how many days will his candies last?
In a garden, there are 4 apple trees. Each tree produces 30 apples. Ethan picked 20 apples from each tree. How many apples are left in the garden?

Ben has 150 candies - some are chocolate, some are mint, and the rest are strawberry. He has twice as many mint candies as chocolate candies. He has 20 fewer strawberry candies than mint candies. How many chocolate candies does Ben have?
Bob earns $15 an hour working in a coffee shop. He worked for 75 minutes yesterday. How much did he earn?
Jeff has to read a book that is 480 pages long. He has already read 140 pages. If he reads 20 pages each day, how many days will it take him to finish the book?
Liam's father gives him $5 every day for a week. After the week, Liam buys a toy for $26. How much money does he have left?

Bob checked how many chairs the classroom has. It has 3 circular tables and 1 rectangular table. Each circular table has 5 chairs, and the rectangular table has 10 chairs. How many chairs are there in all?
A school has 10 classrooms and each classroom can accommodate 30 students. If the school is at maximum capacity, how many students are there in the school?
Johnny has 20 ducks on his farm. Each duck laid 10 eggs. If 5% of the total eggs does not hatch, how many ducks will he have in total?
A bakery needed to pack 2 dozen egg tarts. Each box can fit 5 tarts. If there are 24 pieces in 2 dozen tarts, how many boxes would the bakery need?

Peter the playful parrot, Mandy the magnificent monkey, and Oliver the overjoyed otter are performers at Green Meadows Zoo. In one act, Peter balances on a ball for 8 minutes. Then Mandy balances on a ball for four times as long as Peter. Finally, Oliver balances on a ball for half as long as Mandy. What is the total duration, in minutes, the three performers balance on the ball?
A farmer has a harvest of 500 apples, and he plans to put 20 apples in each basket. After packing all of the apples, how many apple baskets does the farmer have?
Rachel needs to bake 500 cakes for a charity event. She can bake 10 per hour. If she works 8 hours a day, how many days will it take her to bake all the cakes?
Peter is packing boxes for a move. He packs 10 boxes with books, 8 boxes with clothes and 3 boxes with kitchen items. Each box weighs an average of 20kg. How much do all the boxes weigh together?
As a teacher, Jill earns $60 a day. How much would she earn in 45 working days?
John plays soccer and needs new balls for his team. A soccer ball costs $25, and he has a discount coupon for $10 off the total. If he needs to buy 8 balls, how much will he pay?
A baker makes 8 loaves of bread each day for 5 days. How many loaves of bread are made in total?
Grace is a freelance artist who charges $150 a day. Every day, she saves two-thirds of her earnings and spends the rest. After working for 15 days, how much did Grace save?

Marie wants to buy new outfits for her doll collection. Marie's doll collection consists of 15 dolls, each doll needs 3 hats, twice as many shirts as hats, and half as many skirts as shirts. Marie wants to buy double the hats her dolls currently have, half the shirts her dolls currently have, and the same amount of skirts her dolls currently need. What is the total number of outfits Marie wants to buy?
Liam paints 3 walls an hour and he needs to paint 24 walls in total. If he paints for 6 hours straight, how many more hours does he need to finish painting?
Mark volunteers at his uncle's farm. He gets paid 7 cents for every apple he picks. How many apples must he pick an hour if he wants to earn $14 an hour?

Mrs. Silva told her students that if the class average score on the midterm exam is above 80%, they will get a field trip. There are 30 students in the class. One student was sick and took the exam later. The average score of the students who took the exam was 82%. What is the lowest score the sick student can get for the class to still go on the field trip?
A group of 4 friends went to a carnival. They played 3 games each costing $2.50. How much did each person spend on games?
A movie theater has 18 rows of seats, each row has 24 seats. If each ticket costs $12, how much will the theater earn if they sold out all of their seats?
Liam has a box with 25 candies. He sent 10 candies to his friend Noah. After that, he bought another 15 candies. How many candies does Liam have in total?
Sally goes to the bookstore every week and buys 3 books for her collection. If she continues this habit for 12 weeks, how many books will she have collected?
There are 20 cats in a park.  Quarter of the cats are sitting on the bench and the rest are running around the park. How many cat paws are moving?
Alex wants to install a fence around his rectangular garden. The garden is 35 feet long and 20 feet wide. If fencing costs $10 per foot, how much will he spend?
Lisa has twice as many kittens as Megan. If Megan has 9 kittens, how many legs are there in total for Lisa's kittens?
A library has 8,000 books, and they are adding 1,200 new books. Among the new books, 1/5 are science books. How many science books are being added to the library?

Lydia baked a three-layer cake and two dozen cookies for a school bake sale. Each layer of cake requires the same amount of flour as a dozen cookies. Lydia used 1200 grams of flour in total. How many grams of flour are in one cookie?
A toy car costs $3. If John has $12, how many toy cars can he buy?

A school had a budget of $500 for a field trip. The cost of transportation was $200 and they also planned to spend twice that amount on entry fees to a park. Will they have any money left in their budget after paying for transportation and entry fees? If yes, how much?
A toy factory produces 200 teddy bears every hour. If the factory operates for 10 hours a day, how many teddy bears are produced in a day?
Alice bought a dress on sale for 20% off the original price of $50. If she gave the cashier a $100 bill, how much change did she receive?

Anna and Bella run a race where Anna has a 160 yard head start, but Bella runs 20 yards a second, while Anna runs 10 yards per second. How many seconds does Anna have to get to the finish line before Bella catches up?

Kareem is planning a road trip that is 240 miles long. He expects to travel at a speed of 60 miles per hour. Due to traffic, he can only drive at this speed for 2 hours each day. How many days will it take Kareem to complete his trip?
Brad needs 30 more dollars to have three times as much money as Stacy, while Jack has 5 times as much money as Stacy. If Jack has $100, how much more money does Jack have than Brad?
Madison and Carter are going to a movie theater. The ticket price is $10 per person. Cab fare is $3 per person both ways. If they bring $50, how much money will they have left for popcorn and drinks?

Charlie decides to drive to Las Vegas, which is 420 miles away. He drives at a speed of 70 mph. He stops for 2 hours during the trip to rest and eat. How much total time does Charlie spend on his trip to Las Vegas?

There are 50 houses in a neighborhood. On Halloween night, only 20 of these houses gave out candy. If each house gave out a candy bag per trick or treater and each bag had 15 candies, how many candies were given out in total?
Zoe has 25 pencils. She gave 5 to her brother and 3 to her friend. She found 7 more pencils in her bag. How many pencils does she have now?
Evelyn enjoys running in marathons. She usually runs a marathon, which is 26.2 miles, in 4 hours and 20 minutes. If Evelyn decides to run for 6 hours at the same pace, how many miles can she cover?

John is planning a birthday party. He invited 15 friends. The cost of the cake is $40, snacks cost $30 and drinks cost $20. Each friend also gets a goody bag which costs $10. What is the total cost of the party?
Martin buys two books at $25 each and a pen at $5. How much does he spend in total?
A group of 5 friends want to split a $50 pizza bill evenly. How much does each friend pay?

Bob drives to the office every day. If he takes the highway, it will take him 1 hour, but if he takes local roads, it will take him 2.5 hours. How much time could he save per week (5 days) if he takes the highway?
The age difference between Sarah and Jessica is 25. If Jessica is 18 years old, what is the total of their ages 30 years from now?

Anna makes a toy block tower. Each block is one inch tall. Sometimes Anna adds one block to the top of the tower, and sometimes she removes one. At the beginning of the day, the tower is 3 blocks tall. She adds one block seven times and removes one block nine times. How tall is the block tower now?
A bakery made 625 cupcakes for a party. 1/5 of the cupcakes were chocolate and the rest were vanilla. How many vanilla cupcakes were made for the party?
In an office, employee A is said to type 3 times as fast as employee B. Together, they can type 80 pages per hour. How many pages can employee B type in an hour?
A theatre has 20 rows with 15 seats each. A group of school children come for a show and each child occupies one seat. If 4 rows of seats are empty, how many children came for the show?

A group of friends plans to travel to a campsite that is 134 miles away. One friend can drive the group at a speed of 67 miles per hour. During the travel, the group decides to take a 15-minute break every hour. How many hours will the travel time take including the break time?
Jackson bought 3 shirts where each shirt cost him $45. If he paid using a 100-dollar bill, how much was his change?
John bought 5 math books and 2 science books. He also bought 4 magazines. Each book cost $12 and each magazine cost $5. How much did John spend in total?
A tailor sews 10 dresses each day from Monday through Friday. If each dress takes 30 minutes to sew, how much time does the tailor spend sewing dresses in a week?
A gardener planted 6 rows of carrots. Each row has 15 plants. If 1/3 of them did not grow well and had to be removed, how many carrot plants are left in the garden?
Peter has 500 plants in his nursery. If 15% of the plants are roses and 25% are tulips, how many plants do not belong to either category?

Alex wants to print a 50-page document. His current ink cartridge allows him to print only 5 pages. If each new cartridge costs $3 and allows him to print 10 pages, how much will he spend on new cartridges?
Peter has 8 boxes of pens. Each box contains 10 pens. If Peter used 20 pens and gave 2 boxes to Mike, how many pens does he have left?
Linda had 150 daisies. She sold 25% of the daisies and gave 10% of the remaining daisies to her friend. How many daisies does she have left?
Laura studies 20 hours a week. She spends 3 hours per day practicing violin. She spends 7 hours per day sleeping. She works part-time 30 hours a week. How many hours does Laura have left in a week?
Mike can fill a pool in 9 hours. Sarah can fill the same pool in one fourth of the time it takes Mike. How long will it take Sarah to fill the pool?

Jackie sells boxes of cookies for her school. On Tuesday, she sold 50 boxes. On Wednesday, she sold 20 more than twice that number. And on Thursday, she sold half as many as Wednesday. How many boxes did she sell over the three days?

Madeline and her co-worker Neil are planning to pitch in for a $3,000 office furniture set for their new workspace. The cost is divided by the percentage of workspace use. Neil plans to use the workspace for 5 days a week whereas Madeline will use it for the rest of the days. How much does Neil have to contribute?
In 2022, Daniel is 25 years old. His older brother Alan is 7 years older than him and their youngest brother Fred is 5 years younger than Daniel. What year was Fred born in?
Parker saw 7 birds on a tree. Three more birds flew in and joined them. Later in the day, two birds flew away. How many birds were left on the tree?
Monica decorated 3 wreaths with 15 ornaments each. She divided the ornaments equally among five gift boxes. How many ornaments are in each box?
There are 8 books totalling to $72. Four of them are each worth $7. If the remaining books are equally valuable, how much is one of the remaining books?

Mona runs a clothing store. In the first week, she sells 60 dresses. The next week, the sales drop to half of the first week's sales. If she buys new dresses at 30% of the total sold, how many new dresses did she buy?
Sally spends 30% of her $500 paycheck on groceries. How much money does she spend on groceries?
Tom collects stamps. In his collection, he has 12 stamps from the USA, 18 from Canada, and twice as many from Spain as he has from Canada. What is the total number of stamps is Tom's collection?
Sandy reads half as fast as Lily. If it takes Lily 60 minutes to read a chapter, how long, in minutes, will it take Sandy to read 3 chapters?
A store orders 120 cartons of milk each week. Each carton holds 6 liters. If the store sold 90% of the milk this week, how many liters of milk were left?
The Smith's adopted some cats from a shelter. Laura adopted 8 more cats than Peter did. Anne adopted 5 cats fewer than Peter. If Anne adopted 7 cats, how many more cats did Laura adopt than Anne?
Bob's monthly gym membership is $50 per month. His monthly membership increased by twenty percent when he upgraded to premium. How much is his total monthly membership fee after the upgrade?
Sam has a rectangular garden that is 10 feet long and 6 feet wide. He decides to increase the length by 4 feet and decrease the width by 2 feet. What is the new area, in square feet, of Sam's garden?
A bicycle wheel has a diameter of 60 cm. If John bicycles 1000 rotations, how many meters does John travel?
Alexa has $500 in her savings account. She withdraws $50 every month for her phone bill. How much will she have in her account after 6 months?
Jessie has 5 times as many books as Lisa. Lisa has 3 fewer books than their friend Frank. If Frank has 20 books, how many books does Jessie have?

A young boy pours 12 red beads and 10 white beads into a bowl. He divides the total into 2 equal parts, removes some beads from each part, and triples the rest to have 12 beads in each part now. How many beads were removed from each part?
Paul bought 6 packs of juice boxes. Each pack contains 10 juice boxes. He drank 5 juice boxes a day. After 8 days, how many juice boxes were left?
Lisa saved $200 in the first month, $250 in the second month and $150 in the third month. How much money did she have in total after three months?

Becky has 4 containers of cookies and 3 loose cookies. Each container has 22 cookies. She wants to store all of the loose cookies into smaller containers that have a capacity of 6 cookies each. How many smaller containers does she need?

A bike courier delivers parcels and is paid $.20 per mile. The bike can cover 15 miles per gallon of fuel. The fuel costs $1.5 per gallon and the courier rides at a speed of 20 miles per hour. If he works for 8 hours, how much money does he make after paying for fuel?
Daniel needs 16 ounces of flour to bake two pizzas. If he wants to bake 6 pizzas, how much flour will he need?
Michael bought a car for $15000. He wants to sell it for a profit which is one-fourth of the initial investment. What will be the selling price of the car including the profit?

Linda owns a bookstore. In the morning, her inventory shows she has 8 Stephen King books and 15 J.K. Rowling books. During the day, they sold 4 Stephen King books and 7 J.K. Rowling books. Also, they found out that 2 Stephen King books and 1 J.K. Rowling book had missing pages. How many books were sold in total?

Jane and her friends are having dinner at a seafood restaurant. They each order Lobster that costs $15 and Shrimp that costs $10. They also share a Seafood Platter that costs $25. They spend 90% of their combined money. If Jane brought $30, how much did her friend bring?
In Harry's school, there are 500 students. 20% of them are in first grade and 30% of them are in the second grade. The rest are in other grades. How many students are in other grades?
David earns $20 per day, while Maria earns $24 per day. If they both work 5 days a week, what is the difference between their weekly incomes?
Cindy has 72 ounces of candied popcorn. If each bag holds 8 ounces, how many whole bags can Cindy fill?
Peter reads 5 chapters a day. If he reads a quarter of the chapters from a book Tom just finished, how many chapters will Tom's book have if Peter finished reading in 8 days?
Rachael bought 5 pairs of socks and 2 shirts at a clothing store. Each pair of socks cost $10. The total cost of Rachael's items was $70. If each shirt is the same price, what is the cost of 1 shirt?

Purchasing a television comes with a warranty that costs 10% of the MSRP, plus an additional state tax of 7% after the warranty calculation. Daniel decides to buy a television that costs $750 at MSRP. How much will he pay in total?

John sells paintings for $150 each. He sells half of his paintings to a local gallery and then sells another quarter of his paintings at an art fair. He has 8 paintings left. How many paintings did he have to start with?
Sue baked 600 cookies for a charity event. Each box can hold 20 cookies. After the event, 9 full boxes remained. How many cookies were sold?
In a basketball match, Team A scored 65 points and Team B scored 75 points. What percentage of total points did Team A score?
A single card is worth 500 points. A full house is worth 7 times that much. John scored 5 singles and 3 full houses. How many points did he score?

After they finish eating, they decide to play video games and realize they need $45 to rent a selection of games. If they only have $80, how much more money do they need to afford the video games?

In a class of 180 students, 1/2 are boys and 1/3 are girls. How many are the remaining number of students?

Lucy wants to purchase a laptop worth $1000. She has already saved $300. If working part time helps her save $90 a week, how many weeks will it take for her to buy the laptop?

Paul, Mark and Samantha liked to collect stamps. Paul found 7 rare and 10 common stamps. Mark found 15 rare and 16 common stamps. If Samantha found thrice as many rare stamps as Paul and Mark and twice as much common stamps as Mark, how many stamps did Samantha have?
A pet shop sells mice for $8 each and fish for $20 each. If Kara went in and bought 3 mice and 2 fish, how much money did she spend?

Leo has 5 pennies, 2 dimes, and 6 quarters in his piggy bank. His father gives him 3 dimes. How much money, in dollars, does Leo have now?

Ann owns a bakery business. She has 24 loaves of bread and found out that 4 loaves were overbaked. She sold the rest to customer X who bought 2 loaves, customer Y who bought some loaves, and customer Z who bought 5 loaves. All non-overbaked loaves were sold. How many loaves were sold to Customer Y?

Jennifer plans to split her time between 60 books of Romance and Crime genres. If she wants to read more Romance than Crime, and wishes to read at least 25 books of Romance genre, how many Crime novels will she be left with?
John and Roy went lobstering. Roy caught 15 more lobsters than John. If John caught 30 lobsters, how many lobsters did they catch in total?
An apple tree produces 150 apples in a season. Each apple weighs an average of 0.25 kilograms. If a farmer sells the apples at $2 per kilogram, how much will he earn in total?
70 beads are required to make a bracelet. Maria has already made 25 beads, but she needs to make 3 bracelets. How many more beads must Maria make?

Mary and her colleagues are planning a team building activity. Mary plans two more activities than Micheal, who plans 3 activities more than Mark. If Mark planned 5 activities, how many activities do they plan altogether?
Nat can run 10 miles per hour. Zio can run half the speed of Nat, and Tian can run 5 times the speed of Zio. If Marco runs 3/4 as fast as Tian, how fast can Marco run?

Rick went grocery shopping and bought rice, milk, eggs, and vegetables. The rice is thrice as expensive as the milk and the price of the vegetables is 60% of the price of the eggs. If the milk costs $20 and the eggs cost $25, how much did Rick spend for his groceries?
A school has 15 teachers and 25 students. If 3 teachers and 4 students leave the school, how many teachers and students are left?
Henry bought a notebook for 7.5£ and a pen for 2.5£. He pays with 20£ bill. How much change should he get back?
Emily wants to sell 360 tickets for a fundraiser event. With 30 days until the event, how many tickets does she need to sell per day to reach her goal?

Jill is a web designer who charges $50 per hour for her services. She spent 30 hours creating a website for a client. The client requested an additional feature that took Jill 8 hours to complete. Jill offers a discount of $100 for loyal customers. How much does the client owe Jill?
Each apple costs 75 cents and each orange costs 50 cents. If you buy 4 apples and 5 oranges, how much would you spend?
Paul has $100 in his savings. He spends 1/5 of his money on a video game. How much money, in dollars, does Paul have left?
In art class, Benny painted 7 art pieces each selling for $25, while Lenny painted 9 art pieces each selling for $20. How much more did Benny earn than Lenny?
A bookstore has 500 books. On Wednesday, they sold 200 books and on Thursday, they received a delivery of 50 new books. How many books are there now?
Jeffrey stocked up his supplies store with 10 boxes of pens with 50 pens each. He sold 35 pens during the first day and another 100 pens the next day. How many pens does he have left?

Sarah decided to start cycling around a hexagon park. Each side of the park is 10 miles. Sarah can cycle at thrice the speed she can walk. It takes her 30 minutes to walk 1 mile. How long, in hours, does it take to cycle the park?
Andy loves reading books. He has already read 5 books this month. If he reads 2 books every week for the next 4 weeks, how many books will Andy read in total this month?
Suzy is baking cookies that require 300g of flour. She measures out 250g, 235g and 270g on three different trials. How much more flour does she need to reach her target on average?
A bus can carry a maximum of 50 passengers. If 30 people are already on the bus and 25 more people want to get on, how many people can't get on the bus?
To save for a holiday, Jenny decided to save $15 each week for a year. However, every month Jenny also earns $150 from babysitting. In total, how much money will Jenny save by the end of the year?
Mike's room is 12 feet wide and 16 feet long. How many square feet is Mike's room?
A store sells video games for $50 each and a console for $200. Mark buys three video games and two consoles. How much does he spend in total if he has a coupon for $100 off?
Paul spent 25% of his money on a book and then 20% of the remaining money on lunch. If he originally had $200, how much money does he have left?
If Mike is currently 35 years old and his son is currently 11, how many years will it be until Mike is twice as old as his son?
Anna's farm has 50 chickens that lay 2 eggs each per day. She sells the eggs at $1 each. How much will Anna make in a week if all the eggs are sold?
Justin picked 12 apples and gave 1/3 of them to his friend. How many apples were left with him?
In a fruit store, a kilogram of apples cost $4 and a kilogram of oranges cost $5. If John buys 3 kilograms of apples and 2 kilograms of oranges, how much will he pay in total?
If Grace tutors math for $30 per hour and tutors 3 students for 2 hours each today, how much has she earned?
Peter has $150. He wants to buy books that cost $10 each. How many books can Peter buy?
Bella brings 9 loaves of bread to a charity event. Each loaf is sliced into 10 pieces. If 6 people share the loaves equally, how many slices does each person get?

A road has to be built from one city to another, spanning a distance of 700 miles. On the first day, 100 miles were completed, and two times this amount was constructed on the second day. How many miles are left to finish the road?
A movie theater has 20 rows with 15 seats in each row. If 3 rows are reserved for VIPs, how many seats are available to the general audience?
Lisa organizes a field trip for her students. She rents 3 buses, each having a seating capacity of 50 students. If only 80% of the seats were filled, how many students went on the field trip?
Anna needs to read a book that has 250 pages for a book report. If she reads 25 pages each day, how many days will it take her to finish the book?
Peter bought 150 toast bread packets for $5 per packet. He then sold 2/3 of the packets for $10 each. What is his profit?
In Lisa’s vegetable garden, there are 24 tomatoes, thrice the number of cucumbers as tomatoes, and twice the number of beans as cucumbers.  How many vegetables are there in total in Lisa's garden?
Julie finds 3 shells for every four minutes she spends on the beach. She spends 20 minutes on the beach every day. If she's collecting shells for 5 days, how many shells does she collect?
Joe has 50 trees and each tree has 8 fruits. If 1/5 of the trees have no fruits, how many fruits does Joe have?
Mike, Benny and Grant split a pizza into 16 equal slices. If Benny ate 25% of the pizza, and Mike and Grant shared the remaining slices equally, how many slices did Grant have?
Emma's bookstore is running a promotion where for every 5 books purchased, the buyer gets a 20% discount. If a book is priced at $15, how much do they save when they buy 5 books?
A cashier has 15 quarters in her cash register. Her manager gives her 4 rolls of quarters, each containing 40 quarters. How many quarters does the cashier now have?

Matthew drives a racing car on a circular track.  Under optimal conditions, driving at full throttle he completes a lap in 5 minutes, whereas under the same conditions, driving at half throttle he completes a lap in 10 minutes.  If he drives for 3 hours at full throttle and then for another 3 hours at half throttle, how many laps will he complete?
Lilah consumes 5 apples on Monday and 3 apples on Tuesday.  She aims to eat a total of 12 apples from Monday to Wednesday. How many apples does Lilah need to eat on Wednesday?
A train has 3 cars and each car can seat 24 people. If all the seats on the train are full, how many people can the train carry?
Paul bought a laptop for $1200, a phone for $800 and a watch for $200. However, the watch was on sale and he got it for half price. What is the total amount that Paul spent?
Mike works 9 hours each day and earns $18 per hour. How much does Mike earn in 5 days?
A bottle has 1 liter of milk. The bottle is emptied by 25% each hour. How many liters will be left after 3 hours?
Samantha collected shells at the beach. She has 25 white shells and a third as many pink shells. How many shells does she have in all?
Jason read a book in two weeks, reading the same number of pages every day. If the book had 420 pages, how many pages did Jason read per day?
In a college basketball game, each team has 5 players on the court. If there are 8 teams in the tournament, how many players will be on the court in total during the tournament?

James is travelling to two different cities. He has to travel 120 miles to city A, then 150 miles from city A to city B. If his car uses 30 miles per gallon and the gas price is $3 per gallon, how much will James spend on the gas?
Billy needs to buy lumber for a construction project. Each piece is 6 feet long and costs 20 dollars. His project requires 90 feet of lumber in total. How much money would he need to spend?
Kristen has a bag of marbles. She has 10 red ones and 20 blue ones. If she loses 5 red ones and 10 blue ones, how many marbles does she have left?
Elizabeth loves to read. She spends 2 hours reading every day. Each book takes her 4 days to finish. How many books does she read in a month?
In a tropical beach, a resort offered 6x more coconut cocktails than pineapple cocktails. If the resort had 20 pineapple cocktails, how many coconut cocktails does the resort have?
Sam works 40 hours a week and $25 per hour. He has worked for 52 weeks. How much money did Sam make from his job?
A boutique provides a 15% discount for purchases beyond $3000. Jane buys 10 designer dresses that each cost $350. What is her total cost after the discount?
Jenny buys a pack of 72 post-it notes for her college work. She uses 3 post-it notes per day for each of her 4 different subjects. How many days will the post-it notes last?
If the product of two numbers is 50 and the first number is 5, what is the second number?
Mr. Thompson plants 12 trees in his orchard each year. After five years, he removes 10 trees that have died. How many trees are in his orchard?
Greta initially had 72 apples, but then she ate 10. Ron has 1/6 more than what Greta currently has, plus 5. How many apples does Ron have?
A school is having a pizza party. They ordered 10 pizzas and each pizza is divided into 8 slices. If there are 40 students, how many slices will each student receive?

Tommy works in a delivery company. He has a van with a load capacity of 2 tons (where a ton is 2000 pounds). He has to deliver 120 boxes and each box weighs 30 lbs. How much more weight can the van carry?
In a class, 75% of students passed maths test and the rest failed. If there were 28 students who failed the test, what was the total number of students in the class?
Lucas has 60 marbles. He lost 15 marbles and found 25 more marbles later that evening. The next day, he lost half of all his marbles. How many marbles does he have left?
A bicycle factory produced 150 bikes last week. This week, they produced three times the number of bikes than last week. How many bikes did the factory produce in total?
In a preparatory school, for every 8 boys in Martin’s class, there are 5 girls. If the number of girls in the class is 55, how many boy are there in his class?
Bob has a bakery. He bakes 120 muffins in the morning, and by 10AM, he sells 75%. By noon, he sells another 10 muffins. How many muffins are left?

In a 400-meter swimming relay race, four swimmers take turns swimming. The first swimmer completed her part in 110 seconds, the second in 90 seconds, and the third swimmer in 100 seconds. How much time does the last swimmer have left to complete his part if the team wants to finish the race in less than six minutes?
John wants to build a rectangular garden that measures 15 feet by 20 feet. How much fencing will he need if he plans to have a gate that is 3 feet wide?

Tom's cat loves to eat fishes. Every day the cat eats 3 fishes. Tom catches fishes from his backyard pond. In the morning, he catches 9 fishes, and in the evening he catches 8 more. But when he removes the fishes from his fishing net, one escapes. Tom wants to gather the whole week's food for his cat. How many more fishes does he need?
A box contains five bananas, seven pears, and ten guavas. Oliver eats two bananas from the box, three times as many pears as bananas, and half the number of guavas. How many fruits remain in the box?
James has 72 comic books in his collection. He sold half of them at the comic-con and gave away a quarter of the remaining amount to his friend. How many does he have left?

Marilyn bought a pack of stickers for $5.50. She used two stickers for her project and gifted the remaining 8 stickers to her friends. How much did each sticker cost if we consider the price equally for each?

Evan manages a small grocery store and noticed that he sold 350 canned goods in the first week. For the next two weeks, he sold 20 percent more canned goods per week than what was sold during the first week. How many canned goods were sold in total after three weeks?
Emily reads 80 pages of a book in two days. If she reads 12 more pages each day in the next two days, calculate the average number of pages she reads in four days.
A plane travels 600 miles in 2 hours. What is the average speed of the plane in miles per hour?

Mr. Green bought 300 pounds of apples to make apple cider. He used 100 pounds of apples in his first batch. The second batch luckily was four times as much as the first. Mr. Green had to throw away 12% of his remaining apples due to spoilage. How much apples in pounds does Mr. Green have left?
A movie theater has 360 seats split evenly into 9 rows. How many seats are in each row?
Mike is selling lemonade. Each lemonade costs $0.50. If Mike makes 10 liters of lemonade, and each lemonade portion is 250 milliliters, how much money can he make if he sells all the lemonade?
Zoe has 9 more than 3 times the number of pens that Li has. If there are 36 pens in total, how many does Zoe have?
Tommy built several model airplanes, each taking 2.5 hours to build. If Tommy spent 40 hours building model airplanes, how many airplanes did he build?
Eddie is a car salesman who earns a base salary of $800 a month plus $200 for every car he sells. This month, Eddie earned $1800. How many cars did he sell this month?
Mike goes to the gym and burns 1000 calories every day except for Sunday, when he burns 500 calories. He consumes 1500 calories every day. What is his weekly caloric surplus?

It takes John 13 seconds to run 100 meters.  He only goes 4 meters in the first second.  He then maintains the same speed for the rest of the race.  James is 2 m/s faster at the top speed and he can run the first 10 meters in 2 seconds.  How long does it take James to run 100 meters?
Joe saves $15 a week. If he wants to buy a bike that costs $350, how many weeks will it take Joe to save up enough money?
Elena ran 5 miles every day for a week. How many miles did she run in total over the week?

Olivia has a library that contains 120 books. Forty percent of her books are fiction, thirty percent are horror, and the rest are science books. How many of Olivia's books are either horror or science?

Jimmy has 3 jars of pearls. One contains 15 pearls, one contains 10 pearls, one contains 20 pearls. He gives the 10-pearl jar to his friend and keeps the other two jars for himself. How many pearls does Jimmy have now?
Lisa starts working at a bakery for $8/hour. After the first year, she got a 10% raise, and in the second year, she got another 10% raise. How much does Lisa make now?
Reba found 7 shells at the beach on Saturday and twice as many on Sunday. How many shells did she find in total?

Betty goes for shopping every week. She buys three dresses every week at the local fashion store. One dress costs her $50. In an online store, there is a discount going on during which each dress costs only $40. How much would Betty save in three weeks, if she would buy her dresses from this online store instead of her local store?
James has a fish tank with 20 fish. Unfortunately, 3 fish die. If he buys twice as many fish as there are dead to replace them, how many fish will he have in the tank?
Lily loves reading and can read 60 words per minute. The book she picked has 150 words per page and the book is 300 pages long. She plans to read for 10 hours. How many books should she get?
Cloe has two fewer stickers per sticker book than Jen, who has 7 stickers per book. They each have 3 sticker books. In total, how many stickers do they have between them?

In a school, 4 classes took a math exam. In the first class 18 students passed the exam, in the second class 20 students passed the exam, in the third class 15 students passed the exam, and in the fourth class 22 students passed the exam. How many students passed the math exam in total?

John runs a fruit shop. On Monday, he sold 36 apples. On Tuesday, he sold 15 more apples than he did on Monday. On Wednesday, he sold 5 less than twice the apples that were sold on Tuesday. If John sold a total of 295 apples for 4 days, how many apples did he sell on Thursday?
A library has 5,000 books and each book has an average of 250 pages. If one page weighs approximately 10 grams, what is the total weight of all books in the library?
Leo needs to bake 150 cookies for the school fair. He realizes that he only has enough sugar for 200 grams per batch, which is enough for only 30 cookies. How many more grams of sugar does he need?
Ella reads 100 pages of a book per day. The book has 2000 pages. If she increases her pages read per day by 25%, how many days will it take for her to finish the book?
John has a habit of collecting rare coins. He buys 2 new coins every week, and each coin costs $15. After 7 weeks, how much has John spent on his coin collection?
Carl had 80 dollars and spent a third of his money on a video game. How much money does he have left?
For breakfast, Sophie eats 2 eggs each morning. For dinner she eats 3 eggs. If there are 7 days in a week, how many eggs does Sophie eat in a week?
After geometry class, Bill wants to count how many lines he drew. For each shape, he drew one line per side. If he drew 12 triangles, 8 squares, and 4 pentagons, how many lines did he draw?
A tennis player scored a total of 80 points in a game. If each successful stroke is worth 4 points, how many successful strokes did the player make?

Lily has an important conference to prepare for. She decides to prepare one day for 3 hours. The next day she triples this amount, and the day after that she prepares two hours less than the previous day. How many minutes does Lily prepare over the 3 days?
Erika needs 40 red paper, 30 blue paper, and twice as many yellow paper as red paper to create a project. How many papers does she need to make 5 projects?

Katie went to a book sale. The books were separated by price into 2 sections. In the first section, she found 15 books that she wanted. These were $3.00 apiece. The second section marked $6 each and she found 4 books she wanted. What was the average price of each of the books she bought?
Amy loves to read and buys books in bunches. She spends 3 times as much on books as her friend Mark. If Mark spends $60 on books, how much do they spend on books together?
The regular price of a shoes is $80. If the shop offers a 15% discount, how much will the shoes cost now?
Catherine has 16 jars of homemade jam. She gives half of them to her neighbor. Then she makes 5 more jars of jam. How many jars does she have left?
Mary is knitting scarves. She can finish one scarf in 2 days. She's made it a routine to take a day off after finishing each scarf. How many scarves can she make in one month of 30 days?
A movie theater has 25 rows of seats. Each row has 60 seats. How many seats are there in total?
If a laptop costs $1000 and an additional 9% sales tax is applied, how much is the total cost of the laptop?
25% of doctors in a city recommend Vitamin X. 35% recommend Vitamin Y. If the city has 800 doctors, how many more doctors recommend Vitamin Y than Vitamin X?

Linda wants to bake a cake for her son's birthday. She bought a pack of 3 eggs for $1.50, a flour bag for $3.20, a carton of milk for $2.30 and a chocolate bar for $1.80. She also needs a pack of sugar that costs $0.90. How much money did she spend in total?
Yesterday, a coffee shop sold three times as many cappuccinos as espressos. The total coffee sold were 40 cups. How many cappuccinos did the shop sell?
Jacob buys a new laptop for $800 and gets a 20% discount. How much did he pay?

Peter brings a tray of cookies to a party. He gives 5 cookies to each of his two friends and keeps thrice this amount for himself. If he had originally made 50 cookies, how many cookies does he have left to give to his other friends?

A factory produces boxes of toys. Each box contains 5 teddy bears and 3 boats. If the factory produces 200 boxes and they are to be donated to 10 children's homes equally, how many toys will each home receive?
In his book collection, Peter had 30 thriller books, 40 mystery books and some romance books. Overall, Peter had 100 books. How many romance books did Peter have?
Jacob is saving up money to buy a new phone. He already has $150 and needs $500 in total. If he saves $50 every week, how many weeks will it take him to save the remaining amount?
In a class of 60 students, girls form 40% of the total students. Tom wants to give toffees to all the boys in the class. Toffee pack has 10 toffees. How many packs should Tom buy?

Joe and 8 of his friends decide to have a sushi dinner together. Each person buys sushi for $35 and some snacks for $5, and half of the people also pay $15 extra for drinks. If the dinner gets canceled due to a power cut, and everyone gets a 75% refund. How much total amount did the group lose?
Sarah planted 5 apple trees and 2 oranges trees in her garden. Each tree occupies 3 square feet of area. What is the total area occupied by the trees in her garden?

Four colleges organized a music contest and invited students from all the colleges to perform before a panel of judges selected by all the colleges competing. There were 65 students from Beethoven College at the contest, 3/5 times as many students from Symphony College as Beethoven College, and 2.5 times as many from Harmony College as the combined number students from the first two colleges and twice that number from Musical Genius College. Calculate the total number of students at the contest?

At Pro-Phone, a family bundle costs $200 per month for the first four lines, and each additional line costs $25. At Expert-Phone, the family package costs $195 per month for the first four lines, and each additional line costs $20. For a family requiring 7 cellular lines, how much cheaper is Expert-Phone than Pro-Phone?

Patrick saved money for a trip. He saved $500 from his salary, sold a bike for $300 and received $200 as a birthday gift. Later, he paid $400 for the flight ticket and $100 for hotel booking. How much money is left after these expenses?
A group of 3 friends decides to share the $54 cost of a video game. How much does each friend pay?

Lucas buys 2 bottles of juice, 3 cupcakes, and 2 bagels.  Each cupcake cost as much as the combined cost of 2 juices.  Each bagel costs twice as much as a cupcake.  If the juice cost $2 how much did everything cost altogether?

A bus travels a distance of 900 km from town A to town B. It stops in town C which is 500 km away from town A. The bus then proceeds to town B. How many kilometers would the bus have traveled in total when it returns to town A?
Stacy scored 5 goals worth 1 point and 10 goals worth 2 points in a soccer competition. How many points did she score in total?

Emily makes $1,000,000 per year. If she has 10 employees who make $20,000 per year, how much would her salary be if she took part of her salary to make sure all of her employees make $35,000 per year.
Emily has 120 pages to read of her novel for her literature class. If she reads 15 pages a day, how many days will it take for her to finish reading?
Emma has $200 in her bank account. She wants to buy a new camera which costs $350. How much more money does she need to save to buy the camera?

In a bakery, 60% of the pies cost $8 each, and the remaining pies cost $6 each. If Ashley bought half the pies priced at $8, and all of the pies priced at $6, and the total number of pies in the bakery was 300, how much money did Ashley spend buying the pies?

Benny decided to start food blogging. He cooks and photographs 8 dishes. It takes him 3 hours to cook each dish and 40% less time to photograph each dish. How much time does he spend cooking and photographing all dishes?
Joe has six stacks of comic books with twelve books in each stack. His father gave him four stacks with nine comic books. If he gave 60 comics to his friends, how many comic books does he have now?

Billy has a total of 80 marbles, some of them are in 4 filled bags and 10 marbles are not in a bag. His neighbor gives him 20 marbles. If all marbles must be placed in a bag, how many extra bags does he need?
If there are 5 horses and each horse eats an average of 8 buckets of food a day, and each bucket costs $3. How much money do you spend on buckets of food in a month?
A football field is 100 yards long. Peter runs the whole length of the field 5 times a day. How many yards does Peter run in a week?
In the football stadium, there were 2000 spectators. At halftime, 300 spectators left the stadium but 150 more spectators came in than who left. How many spectators are in the stadium now?

June made a design with 20 equal tiles. Three tiles are yellow and the number of blue tiles is one more than the number of yellow tiles. Six tiles are purple and the remaining tiles are white.  How many white tiles are there?

If five more hamsters are added to the ten in the habitat, the number of hamsters in the habitat will be 1/4 the number of hamsters Chloe saw at the pet store today. How many hamsters did Chloe see at the pet store today?

A jewelry store makes half of its profit on Monday, a fifth of its profit on Tuesday and the remainder on Wednesday. The store makes a total profit of $2,000. How much profit, in dollars, was made on Wednesday?
In a bicycle race, Hannah biked 18 miles in two hours. If she maintained the same speed, how many miles will she bike in 5 hours?
Laura and Sophie are buying books. Each book costs $10. Laura buys 5 books and Sophie buys twice as many books as Laura. How much money do they spend in total?
John's mom gave him $10 to buy candies. If one candy costs $2, how many candies can John buy?
There are 32 students in a class. If the teacher made 8 teams for a competition, how many students are there in each team?
Liam reads a book series composed of 7 books with 450 pages each. If he finishes reading all books in one straight week with no breaks, how many pages does he read per day?
Rachael has a bag with 25 different colored marbles. She gives 5 marbles to each of her five friends. How many marbles does she have left?

Mark has a wardrobe full of t-shirts. If half of his t-shirts are blue, and one third of the blue t-shirts have stripes. If 60 of his t-shirts have stripes, how many t-shirts are there in total in Mark's wardrobe?
A bus can travel 50 miles with 5 gallons of gas. The bus driver forgets to refill the gas at last stop and only has 20 gallons of gas left. How far can the bus travel before it runs out of fuel?
Jake runs at a constant speed of 6 miles per hour. If he started running at 8 am and stopped at 10 am, how far did he run?
A music label has 7 bands. Each band has 5 members. If every member gets two VIP tickets to their own shows, how many VIP tickets does the label give out in total?

Marcus raises turtles, and his adult turtles recently had two sets of 10 each. He gave a fifth of the baby turtles to his brother Alex to keep as pets. He sold twice the number of babies he gave Alex to a pet store. One-third of the remaining turtles were sold to others as pets. How many baby turtles did Marcus have left?
Ben is 10 years old. His older brother, Mike, is five years older than he is. His cousin, Jane, is 3 years younger than Mike. How old is Jane?

Penny can type 4 pages in an hour, Mike can type 1 more page in an hour than Penny and Lisa can type twice as many pages as Mike. Jack can type 2 fewer pages than Lisa can in an hour. How many pages can Penny, Mike, Lisa and Jack type in 2 hours?
A fruit stand has 80 apples and 120 oranges. If each apple cost $2 and each orange costs $1.5, how much is the total value of the fruits in the stand?

A school is planning a field trip and need 5 buses to carry all the students. Each bus can only hold 20 students. If only 70% of the students show up for the field trip, how many students are there in the school?
The cost of a video game that was priced at $60 was increased by 20%. What is the new price?
George had 30 pages left to read in a book. He read 15 pages before lunch and then 9 pages after lunch. How many pages does he have left to read?
Katie loves to read and visits the library every week. She can borrow 5 books at a time. If there are 3 weeks in a month, how many books does she borrow in 2 months?
Alex rented a bike for $60 plus 10 cents for each mile. He drove 400 miles on Tuesday and another 350 miles on Wednesday. How much did he spend?

In a school, 2/3rd of students play football and 1/4th of these football-playing students also play basketball. If there are 8 students who play both football and basketball, how many students are in the school?
A zoo has 4 elephants and twice as many monkeys as elephants, and half as many giraffes as monkeys. How many animals are there total in the zoo?

A cocktail recipe requires 4 parts vodka and 3 parts orange juice. If a bartender wants to make 42 servings of the cocktail, what is the difference between the number of vodka and orange juice parts used?

Sarah is preparing jars of cookies for a bake sale. Each jar can contain 24 cookies, and she can only pack 50% of each jar at once. If she needs to prepare 15 jars, how many cookies does she need in total?
Peter decides to play 3 songs on his guitar every time he goes to the park. He goes to the park 2 times a day every day. How many songs does he play in a week?
George is a plumber. He charges $45 for each visit and $25 per hour for labor. If he was called out for a job where he had to work for 3 hours, how much was the client charged?
Linda has 3 cats. Each cat eats 2 cans of food a day. How many cans of food does Linda need for her cats in 14 days?

Steven decided to start a savings plan. In the first week, he saved $15. He continued to increase his savings by $5 each week, so in the second week he saved $20, and in the third week $25. How much did he save in total after 3 weeks?
If a train travels 10 miles in 2 min, how long would it take to travel 180 miles?
Samuel has some new pencils. Maria has 2 more new pencils than Samuel. Paul has seven more new pencils than Maria. If Paul has 14 new pencils, how many new pencils does Samuel have?
There are nine more green apples in a basket than red apples. If the number of red apples is 30, and after an hour, a third of the apples are eaten, calculate the total number of apples remaining.
A pencil costs 15 cents, a pen costs 25 cents and an eraser costs 30 cents. If Lisa buys 4 pencils, 3 pens and 2 erasers, how much will she pay in dollars?

Ian has a jar full of 80 candies. 25% are mint flavored and the rest are orange flavored. His friend will give him 3 orange flavored candies for every mint flavored one. If Ian keeps 2 mint flavored candies, how many total candies does he have after trading with his friend?
In a computer retail company with 200 computers, 40% of the computers are gaming computers and 30% of the gaming computers are without a mouse. How many of the gaming computers come with a mouse?

John has 2 fewer tins of paint than cans of spray paint. He has 6 tins of paint and each tin contains 3 litres. Each can of spray paint contains 4 litres. How much paint in litres does John have in total?
George is building 3 5 ft long by 4 ft wide bookshelves and 2 7ft long by 3 ft wide bookshelves. What is the total sq ft of storage space that he will have?
If a school has 20 classrooms and each classroom has 24 students, how many students are there in total in the school?
David has $200 to spend on furniture for his new room. He buys a desk for $60 and a chair for $40. How much money does David have left?
Larry collects rare coins and has 4 times as many coins as his friend Bill. If Bill has 15 coins, and Larry finds 3 more coins at an antique shop, how many coins does Larry have?
Ron is participating in a book reading challenge. In the first week he read 2 books. Every week after that, he read 3 books more than the previous week. After 4 weeks, how many books did Ron read?
Stephanie baked 10 pizzas for her daughter's birthday party. If each pizza was sliced into 8 equal pieces and there were 2 pizza slices left after the party, how many pizza slices were eaten?

Tim borrowed a book from the library and it was 400 pages long. He decided to read 100 pages every day. After the third day, his friend gave him another book of 200 pages. How many pages does Tim have to read now?
Kara baked 5 pies to sell at a local market. Each pie was cut into 10 slices. If she sold each slice for $2.50, how much money did Kara make if she sold all the slices?
Maria has 12 toy cars. She gave 3 to her friend. How many toy cars does Maria have left?
Richard wants to buy a game console for $210. He has already saved $85. If he can save $25 a week, how many weeks will it take to get enough money for the console?

Joseph runs a small candy shop. He bought 15 lollipop packs, a chocolate pack costing twice the lollipop pack, and a gummy bear pack costing thrice the chocolate pack. If a lollipop pack costs $3, how much did he spend on candy packs?
Sally bought 3 boxes of pens. Each box has 12 pens. Sally gives 5 pens to Richard and another 10 pens to Tania. How many pens does Sally have now?
Lisa has three times as many stickers as Jenny. Bob has 30 fewer than twice the number of stickers Jenny has. If Jenny has 40 stickers, how many more stickers does Lisa have than Bob?
Lucas practices piano for 30 minutes every day. He practices 4 times more on the weekends. How much total time does Lucas spend practicing piano in a week?
Lucy can complete a puzzle in 10 minutes. If she has 30 puzzles to finish and she works for 1 hour, how many puzzles does she still need to complete?
A pastry shop sells cupcakes for $10 each. Billy bought 4 cupcakes and gave 2 cupcakes to his sister. Afterwards, he bought 3 more cupcakes. How much money did Billy spend in total?
A package holds 3 dozen cookies. If a party ate 10 cookies, how many cookies are left?
Sarah has 18 cupcakes. She wants to place them equally in 3 boxes. How many cupcakes will be in each box?
Every morning, Mr. Chandler buys 5 newspapers for $1 each. He also buys a cup of coffee for $3. How much does he spend on newspapers and coffee in a week?
An art teacher gives her students 2 paint brushes every week. If she has 20 students and teaches them for 15 weeks, how many paint brushes does she give to her students in total?
John has 60 candies. He gives away 10% of them to his friend Mike, and eats 15% of the remaining candies. How many candies does John have left?

Jennifer is making a mural. She applies a base coat that takes 4 minutes to dry, three color coats that take 5 minutes each to dry, and a clear top coat that takes 10 minutes to dry. How many minutes total does Jennifer spend waiting for her mural to dry?
Paul has $25 and bought 5 boxes of cookies. Each box cost the same amount. How much did each box of cookies cost?
Jenny baked 15 cupcakes for a party. 6 of them were chocolate flavored, 4 were vanilla, and the rest were strawberry. How many strawberry cupcakes did she bake?
Tom has saved $400 in his bank account. His mother gave him $150 for his birthday and his father gave him twice as much as his mother. How much money does Tom have now?

Amin has several empty jars of different sizes, holding either 2, 4, or 5 kg of rice. He fills the 4-kg jar and tips as much as he can into the 2-kg jar. Then, he tips the remainder into the 5-kg jar. How much more rice, in kg, can he put into the 5-kg jar, without overflowing?
A cruise ship can hold 1200 passengers. If 500 passengers boarded the first day and 600 passengers boarded the second day, how many more passengers can the ship take?
Lisa has 5 more toys than twice the amount that Hannah has. Hannah has 3 less toys than Jack. If Jack has 7 toys, calculate how many toys all three of them have in total?

John mows lawns during his free time. He usually spends 2 minutes trimming the edges, 10 minutes mowing the lawn, 3 minutes raking the clippings, and 7 minutes cleaning the equipment. One afternoon, he mowed 3 small lawns and a large park, which took three times as long as a small lawn. How many minutes did John spent mowing all the places?

Eva swims 15 minutes every day. She swam for a total of 285 minutes in a given period. John also swims, but he swam less than Eva by 165 minutes in the same period. How many minutes does John swim each day if he swims the same number of days as Eva?
Paul and Mary split a lottery prize such that Paul gets 30% and Mary gets the rest. If Mary's share is $7,000, how much was the total lottery prize?
Emily is making pasta for dinner. The recipe calls for 5 cups of flour. If Emily only has 4 cups of flour, how many more cups does she need?
Larry has thrice as many cows as Paul on his farm. Paul has 6 more cows than John does. John has twice as many cows as David does. David has 15 cows. How many cows do all 4 people have combined?
A delivery truck carries 112 cans of paint. The driver delivers a third of the cans to the first site, and half of the remaining cans to the second site. How many cans of paint are left in the truck?
Lisa buys 2 bags of cat food and gives $100 and gets $10 in change. How much did each bag of cat food cost?
Sam and his friends are having a burger party and decide to share the bill. They bought 100 burgers and each costs $1.5. If there are 5 of them how much would each have to pay?
In a board game, each player starts with 500 points. In his first turn, John gains 37 points but loses 48 points in his second turn. How many points does John have left?
A baker uses 3 eggs to make a chocolate cake and 4 eggs to make a vanilla cake. If he made 10 chocolate cakes and 15 vanilla cakes in a day, how many eggs did he use?

A pizza shop sells pizza in three sizes: small, medium, and large. A small pizza has 8 slices, a medium pizza has 12 slices and a large pizza has 15 slices. If a group of friends ordered 2 large pizzas, 3 medium pizzas, and 4 small pizzas, how many slices of pizza do they have?
Mr. Smith planted 75 apple trees in his orchard. The number of peach trees he planted was 5 less than twice the number of apple trees. How many peach trees did Mr. Smith plant?

Mr. Johnson is grading 150 midterm essays from his Literature class. On Thursday, he grades 50% of the essays. On Friday, he grades 70% of the remaining essays. On Saturday, how many more essays does he need to grade?

A pizza parlor sells a medium pizza for $15 with each topping costing $2. If John buys a medium pizza with four toppings, and the shop has a discount of 20% off the total price. How much did John pay for his pizza?
A high school football team won 75% of its games during the season. If the team won 36 games, how many games did the team play during the season?
Billy has a basket filled with apples and oranges. He counted and he has twice as many oranges as apples. The total fruit count is 36. How many apples does Billy have?
Marco drives 120 miles in 3 hours. How far will he travel in 20 minutes?
A farm holds 4000 chickens. Every week 10% of the chickens lay eggs. Among them, 30% don't produce sellable eggs. Calculate the number of chickens that produce sellable eggs every week.

Alice's dad can restore an old classic car in 10 hours. He has a workshop where he can restore up to 3 cars at the same time. If he works 8 hours per day, how many days will it take him to restore 6 classic cars?

Nathan's garden has 120 roses. One day he noticed that 25% of his roses have wilted. He designs a new watering system which repairs 80% of the wilted roses. How many roses are wilted after using the new watering system?
In a town, 35% of the houses are painted white, the rest are painted blue. If there are 200 houses in total in the town, how many houses are painted blue?

In a classroom, there are 32 students. Half of them brought lunch from home. The rest decided to buy lunch from the school's canteen which costs $3 each. How much money was spent by the students who bought lunch from the canteen?
Tom spends 45 minutes on math homework and 30 minutes on science homework every night. If he follows this routine from Monday to Friday, how many hours does he spend on these homework in total?
Julia buys one pizza for $15 and three bottles of coke for half of the price of the pizza. How much does she pay in total?
Danny went shopping and spent $72. He bought a jacket for $48 and 2 pairs of socks. Each pair of socks cost the same amount. How much did one pair of socks cost?
Jerry and Linda and their 4 children went to the cinema along with Peter and Susan and their 2 children. If there are 28 fingers at the popcorn stand, how many people are not at the popcorn stand?
Bella has 12 cartons of juice. Each carton contains 1 liter of juice. Bella pours the juice into glasses that can hold 200 milliliters each. How many glasses of juice does Bella have?
Sam participates in a charity walk where each participant must walk 10 miles. If he started walking with his 4 friends, how many miles did they walk in total?
Each day, Samantha reads 7 pages of a book. If the book has 126 pages in total, how many days will it take for her to finish reading the book?
Karen has three times as many dresses in her closet as jackets. She has 4 more jackets than blouses and currently has 7 blouses. How many dresses does Karen have?
Sasha is a miner. He can get 2 diamonds for every boulder he mines. If Sasha mines 3 boulders every day, how many diamonds does he get after 4 days?

Joshua is saving money for a new bike that costs $120. He saves $15 every week. After 5 weeks, his grandparents decided to help out and gave him an extra $25. How much more does he need to save to buy the bike?
Jimmy saved up $800 from his summer job and he wants to buy video games that cost $50 each. If he also wants to save $100 for the upcoming school year, how many video games can Jimmy buy?

Sally wants to prepare a garden in her backyard, she goes to a nursery with $150. She buys 30 different seed packets for $2 each, bags of topsoil for $18 each and intends to use the rest of the money to buy gardening tools. If a tool set is $20, how many tool sets can Sally buy?
A bookshop has a 5% discount on all books on Thursdays. If a book costs $150, how much will it cost on a discount day?

A classroom is planning a trip to a museum. Each adult ticket costs $15, while each student ticket costs $7. The total cost for all the tickets is $220. If there are 4 adults going, how many student tickets were purchased?
Joan and her husband bought a 750 square feet condo. The width of condo is 25 feet. How many feet is its length?
Bryan saved $100 every month for a year. His brother David saved $80 monthly, but started 3 months later. How much money did they both save together at the end of the year?
Jimmy just bought patch stickers and customizing his skateboard cost him $40. If the patch stickers cost $5 each and Jimmy bought two of them, how much does his skateboard cost?

In the classroom, there are 36 students. Three-fourths of them are boys and the rest are girls. If 60% of the boys and 80% of the girls need new uniforms, how many uniforms in total need to be ordered?
Alex sold 5 video games for $20 each and 3 board games for $15 each. If he paid $10 of his earnings to cover shipping costs, how much money did he make in total?
James wants to jog 100 miles in a month. If he jogs 5 miles a day, for how many days does he need to jog?
A tank of gas allows a car to drive 300 miles. The car is currently halfway empty. If the car has already driven 100 miles, how far more can it go with the remaining gas?
Danny owns a bookstore. Each book costs him $4. He sells each book for $10. This month Danny sold 350 books. How much profit did he make this month?
Sam and Alex bought some video games. Sam bought 10 games less than Alex did. Sadly, Sam lost 2 games. If Alex bought 45 games, how many games do they have combined?
Alice paid $30 for 4 one-pound bags of cherries and 2 bunches of blueberries. Bertha paid $15 for 2 one-pound bags of cherries and 1 bunch of blueberries. How much does a bunch of blueberries cost?
In the city of Braavos, a blacksmith can forge 10 swords per day. If he has orders for 155 swords, how many days will he need to fulfill the order?
Tom owns a restaurant. He rents a place that costs $500 per month. If Tom earns $125 per day, what is his total profit every month if there are 30 days?
John wants to paint his house. He has already painted 60% of it. If his house surface is divided into 600 equal portions, how many more portions does John have to paint?
Miss Johnson had 50 pens in her office drawer. She buys new packs of pens that have 10 pens in each pack. Now, she has 120 pens. How many new packs did Miss Johnson buy?

Zoe runs a small bakery shop where she sells cupcakes for $3 each. One day, she decided to have a promotional offer of 'Buy 3, get 1 free'. Paul, a regular customer bought some cupcakes under the promotional offer and paid $27 in total. How many cupcakes did Paul receive?
If a printer prints 20 pages per minute and I have to print 240 pages, how long will it take?

Nancy makes $9 per hour babysitting. One Friday, she babysat for 4 hours in the morning and 2 hours in the afternoon. She babysat for the same number of hours that Saturday. What is the total amount of money that Nancy earns over the weekend?
Jeremy's total weekly income consists of $325 from his day job and an additional 25% of this income from his part-time job. How much money does Jeremy earn in a week?
Liam has fifty bookmarks. He gives twenty bookmarks to his sister, five bookmarks to his best friend, and keeps the rest. How many bookmarks does Liam keep?
Ethan has a collection of 150 postage stamps. Every week, he gives away 12 stamps to his friends and buys 7 new ones. How many stamps will Ethan have in 6 weeks?
A plumber installs a new water heater that uses 20% less energy than the old one. If the old heater used 500 kWh per month, how much energy will the new one use?
Bob owns a stationary shop and sells pens for $2 each. If he offers a bundle of 10 pens for $15, how much money will Jim save if he buys 30 pens?
Maria bought a dress at a 20% discount and paid the sale price of $80. What was the original price of the dress?
Sara needs 5 pounds of flour to make 25 cupcakes. How many pounds of flour does Sara need if she wants to make 120 cupcakes?
Alex is selling chocolate bars for a fundraiser. He starts with 200 bars. In the morning, he sells 2 dozen bars. In the evening, he sells 58 bars. How many bars does Alex have left?

Mandy has a skincare routine that she does every night. She puts on 4 products with 3 minutes interval in-between each product. She further includes a 10-minute mask session into her routine. How long does Mandy's skincare routine take?
Mr. Smith is a farmer who planted 12 rows of corn with 24 plants in each row. It took him 6 hours to plant. How many corn plants did he plant each hour?
Joe has 7 pencils and 5 pens. He gives 3 of his pencils to his friend and then buys 2 more pens. How many writing instruments does he have now?
A chicken lays 7 eggs in a week. How many eggs are laid by 13 chickens in a week?
A basketball coach makes $90,000 a year. He just received a 10% raise. How much will he make next year?

Sarah is planning a birthday party for her son. The park she rented charges $10 per guest. She wants to invite 15 classmates from her son's class, 10 family friends, and 5 cousins. She also bought a $50 cake and $30 decorations. How much does Sarah spend in total for her son's birthday party?
Adam bought 6 new action figures last week. This week he bought three times as many so that he has 60 action figures now. How many action figures did Adam have originally?

In a salad recipe, Jenny needs to get 3 types of vegetables. She needs 2 heads of lettuce which cost $1.50 each, 4 tomatoes which cost $0.75 each, and 5 cucumbers which cost $1 each. How much will Jenny need to pay for all these vegetables?

Rob has a coin box which he divided into 4 parts. He put $20 in one part and the rest of the parts he filled equally. If after spending $10 from the coin box he had $70 left, how much money did he put in the coin box to begin with?
Samantha is baking a cake. She needs 3 cups of flour and twice as many cups of sugar. If she divides the batter into molds that can hold 2 cups each, how many molds does she need?

Jessica is organizing books in the library. 80% of the books are fiction, and 1/4th that number are reference books. If there are 2000 books in the library, how many books are not fiction or reference?
In an orchard, there are 120 apple trees, 150 orange trees, and 80 peach trees. If a disease affects 15% of all the trees in the orchard, how many trees get affected?
In a class of 50 students, 20% of the students prefer vanilla ice cream, and the rest prefer chocolate. How many students prefer chocolate ice cream?
In a marathon, participants run 42.195 kilometers. If a runner completes 25% of the marathon, how many kilometers has he run?
Jill baked some cookies for a school fundraiser. She sold each cookie for $2 and was able to sell 50 cookies. How much money did Jill earn?

Paula sells handmade jewelry. She has 200 necklaces and 150 bracelets. She sold half of her necklaces and 1/3 of her bracelets. Each piece of jewelry is sold for $15. How much has Paula earned from the pieces she has sold?

Robert has a 20-liter tank that he needs to fill with water. However, the water jug he uses can only hold 1.5 liters. How many times does he need to fill the jug and pour it into the tank to fill up the tank?
Emma baked 60 cookies. She gave half of them to her sister and one-third of the leftover to her friend. How many cookies does Emma have now?
Tim grows 300 tomatoes in his garden. He gives 30% of them to his neighbors. How many tomatoes does Tim keep?

Angie is baking cookies for her friends. She has 3 different cookie cutters. It takes her 5 minutes to cut out a batch using each cookie cutter. If she wants to use each cookie cutter 5 times, how long will it take her to cut out all the cookies?
In a marathon, your average pace is 9 minutes per mile. If you are going to run a half marathon (13.1 miles), how long will it take you to finish?
Meg has four times as many marbles as Tom. Jack has three times as many marbles as Tom. If Meg has 28 marbles, how many does Jack have?
There are 15 cars and each has 5 seats. If all cars are full and each passenger brought 2 bottles of water, how many bottles of water did they bring altogether?
Peter can bench press 150 pounds. Jake can bench press twice as much as Peter. If Jake lifts his max three times, how many pounds did he lift in total?
Fred buys a bag of candy for $3. In the bag are 30 candies. Fred sells each candy for $0.15. How much profit does he make after all the candy has been sold?
A bus can seat 50 passengers. If there are already 20 passengers on the bus, how many more passengers is the bus able to carry?
Jane has twice as many stickers as Bob. Bob has 3 times as many stickers as Charlie. If Charlie has 10 stickers, how many stickers do they have in total?
Sarah has 12 candies. If she gives 3 candies to each of her brothers and she has 3 brothers, how many candies will she have left?
Jane has 200 stamps. She gave away a quarter of her stamps to her friend and the rest she put into 4 albums evenly. How many stamps are in each album?
Liam has 3 boxes of oranges and 2 boxes of apples. Each box of oranges costs $5 and each box of apples costs $4. How much did all the boxes cost?
A pizza shop sold half of its pizzas in the morning and a quarter of what's left in the afternoon. If the pizza shop originally had 40 pizzas, how many pizzas did they sell in the afternoon?

Emily needs $15 for school supplies. She collected 3 quarters, 5 dimes, 2 nickels and 7 pennies. Each quarter is 25 cents, each dime is 10 cents, each nickel is 5 cents, and each penny is 1 cent. How much more does she need for her school supplies?
In a zoo, there are 25 flamingos and each flamingo eats 3 fish a day. If each box of fish contains 20 fishes, how many boxes of fish does the zoo need for a week?

Lucia secured a school building project that included the construction of four buildings with several classrooms. The first building was planned to have 5000 classrooms, the second building half as many classrooms as the first building, and the third building was to have 25% more classrooms than the second building. How many classrooms were Lucia contracted to build?
Peter plans to build a rectangular dog house that is five times longer than it is wide. If the perimeter of the dog house is 72 feet, what is the width of the dog house in feet?
Sam runs at a speed of 9 km per hour. If he runs continuously for 2 hours, then takes a break, how much distance would he cover in his entire morning run?
The restaurant has 10 indoor tables and 8 outdoor tables. Each indoor table has 4 chairs and each outdoor table has 2 chairs. How many chairs are there in total?
A farmer cultivated 20 apple trees in his orchard. Each tree yields 150 apples. Unfortunately, birds ate 30 apples from each tree. How many apples does the farmer have in total?
Marco reads 2 books every week. How many books will he read in a year?
Jenny has 20 apples. She wants to divide them equally into baskets. If she has 5 baskets, how many apples will be there in each basket?
Lisa bought a dress for $90. She also bought a pair of shoes that cost twice as much as the dress. How much did she spend in total?

Mike started a new routine. He started doing 10 push-ups daily. After a week, he started doing twice as many push-ups daily as he did before. How many push-ups does he do in a week after he changed his routine?
Maria has 5 books in her bag and she just bought another 2 from the store. If each book costs $12, how much did she pay for all the books?
Lisa is a part-time teacher who teaches 3 classes a day. In each class, there are 20 students. Each student buys two $15 books from her. How much does she earn in a five-day work week?

Bianca's garden is divided into three sections. The first section has 20 rose plants, the second one has 25 tulip plants, and the third one has 30 daisy plants. If each plant needs 2 liters of water per week, how many liters of water does Bianca need to feed her plants in one week?
Angela and her 3 friends decide to sell lemonades over the weekend. If each cup of lemonade sells for $1.50 and they sell a combined total of 120 cups over the weekend, how much money did they make?
Lucas buys a jar of peanut butter. Each serving is 1 ounce. He uses 2 servings every day. If the container is 3 ounces less than 1 gallon, how many days will it last?
There are 25 people on a basketball team. Only two-thirds of the team attended practice. How many players did not come to practice?

John saw a shop that sells antique watches for $50 and vintage sunglasses for $30. He planned to buy 2 watches and 2 sunglasses for  $300. If he uses all his money, how much will he have after buying all?
A tree has 75 apples on it. Each day 5 apples fall off. By the end of the week, how many apples are left on the tree?
A train traveled 400 miles in 4 hours. What is the average speed (in miles per hour) of the train?
James buys 10 shirts and half as many pants.  The shirts cost $6 each and the pants cost $8 each.  How much did everything cost?
In 2030, Max is 45 years old. His sister Alice is 2 years younger than him and their brother Jeff is 4 years older than Alice. What year was Jeff born in?

A forest lodge has 100 liters of drinking water. It gets delivered 20 liters of water each day. If the consumption of water per day is 30 liters, how many days would it take to completely run out of water?
A concert ticket costs $50 more than a movie ticket. If a movie ticket costs $20, find the total cost of both tickets.
Mike has a reading goal of 8 books per month. By the third week, he has read 5 books already. How many more books does he need to read to reach his goal?
Jaden purchased a second hand video game console for $180 and wants to resell it. If he wants to make a profit of $50, for how much should he sell the video game console?
Laura baked 30 cookies. 2/3 of them are chocolate chip cookies and the rest are oatmeal cookies. If each oatmeal cookie sells for $3, how much will she earn from selling all the oatmeal cookies?

Carl wants to buy two types of notebooks for his school. The scientific notebooks are priced at $6.50 each and the basic notebooks are priced at $4.00 each. If he wants to buy 3 scientific notebooks and 5 basic notebooks, how much will he spend in total?
Peter is a software developer who can write 40 lines of code every hour. If he worked for 8 hours, took a 1 hour break, and worked for another 4 hours, how many lines of code did he write?
A school bus can hold 25 students. If there are 128 students going on a field trip, how many buses will the school need?
Alex travels 60 miles to work each day. If his car gets 20 miles per gallon and gas costs $2.50 a gallon, how much does Alex spend on gas to commute to and from work every day?
A movie theater has 50 rows of seats. Each row has 15 seats on the left, 20 seats in the center and 15 seats on the right. How many seats are there in total in the theater?
Matt walks at a speed of 4 km/h. How many kilometers will Matt have walked after 2 hours and 30 minutes?
In a one-week Vacation, the Turner family spent $450 for lodging, four times that amount on meals, and quarter of that meals money on entertainment. What was their total spent on vacation?
6 birds collected 360 worms. If each bird needs 70 worms to get through the month, how many more worms does each bird need to collect?

Lila's candy shop received three boxes of candies. Box A has 200 candies, Box B has 400 candies, and Box C has 300 candies. The shop sells candies at the rate of 10 candies per day. How many candies does the candy shop have after 20 days?

Lucas can run faster than anyone in his school. In fact, he can run 2 times faster than the average speed of the next three fastest runners. If the three next fastest runners can run at 15 mph, 20 mph, and 25 mph, how fast can Lucas run?

John runs a bakery. He baked 220kg of bread on Monday. On Tuesday he baked 1.5 times that. On Wednesday he only baked 150kg. If he initially planned to bake 500kg of bread for the week, by how much did he exceed his target?
Julia uses 5 eggs in a recipe for a cake. She makes three cakes per week. If eggs are sold in packs of 10, how many packs of eggs does Julia need to buy for one month (4 weeks)?
The middle school sold 6 more than two times the number of fair tickets as it did tickets to the baseball game. If 25 fair tickets were sold, how many baseball game tickets did the school sell?

Samantha is making homemade cards for her 12 friends. She has 3 rolls of stickers and each roll has 8 stickers. How many stickers will Samantha have left after making the cards if she uses 2 stickers per card?
Abby's dress costs 25% less than her shoes. Her shoes cost $100. How much was Abby's outfit?
Jenny has 20 stamps. Jack has 3 times as many stamps as Jenny, but 5 less than Judy. How many stamps do they all have together?
Emma runs 2 miles every day. On the weekend she runs an extra mile. How many miles does Emma run in a week?

Freddie Hawkins can read at a speed of 180 words per minute.  Due to poor lighting, his reading speed drops by 30 words per minute. If he is supposed to read a chapter with 4200 words, how many minutes will it take him to finish reading the chapter?
