If Alice travels 75 miles to visit her grandmother and the trip takes 1.5 hours, what is her average speed in miles per hour?
Anna has a collection of 36 cars and planes. She has 2 times as many cars as she has planes. How many planes does Anna have?
Ellen mows lawns for extra money. She charges $50 for each lawn. One summer, she mowed 10 lawns a week for 8 weeks. How much money did she make?
Greg bought an apartment for $200,000. He was able to pay 10% down, and his uncle paid off an additional 20% of the remaining balance. How much money does Greg still owe on his apartment?

Olivia and Jacob are athletes practicing long jumps. Olivia did twenty long jumps during practice. Jacob jumped half the distance that Olivia did. If each of Olivia's long jumps were 3 meters, how many meters did Jacob jump in total?
Maria goes to a bookstore and buys 5 mystery books and 6 romance novels. Each mystery book costs $8 and each romance novel costs $10. How much did Maria spend in total?
Bob purchased 15 horses, 6 pigs, and a number of cows. He will be moving all these animals to a new ranch in 3 equal groups of 37 animals each. How many cows does he have?
Lily has 4 more novels than Sara. Sara has a third of the number of novels Ron has. If Ron has 30 novels, how many novels do they have in total?
There are 20 kids in a class and each kid needs to be given 5 books. If each book costs $3, what is the total cost of the books?
A restaurant manager ordered 15 sacks of potatoes in June and 20 sacks in July. There are 50 potatoes per sack. How many potatoes did the manager order in June and July?
Helen was 12 years old 5 years ago. How old will she be 7 years from now?
Cindy runs 3 miles each day. If she continues this daily routine, how many miles will she run in 2 weeks?
Billy has 7 stickers while Dina has three times fewer stickers than Billy's. How many fewer stickers does Dina have than Billy?

Anna has 5 startup companies. For each company, she invested $20000, except for one which she invested $30000. If she decided to profitably sell all her companies for a total of $200000, how much will Anna get from selling all her companies?
A school has 600 students. Each student needs 2 pencils for school supplies. The pencils are sold in packs of 100. How many packs does the school need to buy?
A bus can fit 50 passengers. If 3/5 of the seats are occupied, how many seats are still available?
Samson is going to another town which is 140 km away. He will use his car that uses ten liters of gasoline for a distance of 70 km. How many liters of gasoline will Samson need for a one-way trip?
If a bakery uses 20 cups of flour for every 8 loaves of bread and Caroline used 60 cups of flour, how many loaves of bread did she make?
In San Diego Zoo, the lion consumes 25 kilograms of meat, and the tiger consumes 20 kilograms of meat per day. If they have 90 kilograms of meat, how many days will the meats last?
Each pound of paper would recycle into seven newspapers. Jim's old papers they want to recycle weight 20 pounds. How many newspapers can Jim make by recycling all his old papers?
Alice exchanged three CDs each worth $9 for 1 CD worth $30. What is the profit Alice made in this transaction?
Molly has a collection of 35 rare coins. She gives 7 of her coins to her sister and 12 more to her best friend. How many coins does Molly have left in her collection?
Jack brushes his teeth twice a day and showers once a day. How many total times does he perform these activities in a year, if we take it to be 365 days?

An earthquake caused four buildings to collapse. Experts predicted that each following earthquake would have double the number of collapsing buildings as the previous one, since each one would make the foundations less stable. After three more earthquakes, how many buildings had collapsed including those from the first earthquake?
One kilogram of potatoes costs $3. If Peter buys 2.5 kilograms, how much does he have to pay?
Henrik can burn 100 calories by running 1 kilometer. If Henrik burns 700 calories, how many kilometers did he run?
At the end of a parade, there are 18 dogs. Half of the dogs are sitting while the other half are standing. How many dog legs are on the ground?

A group of squirrels discovered a batch of acorns in a hollow tree. Each squirrel could carry a certain number of acorns. The first squirrel carried off 5 acorns. The second squirrel carried off 2 more acorns than the first. The third squirrel carried off double what the second squirrel carried. The fourth squirrel couldn't find any more acorns. How many acorns were in the original batch?
A movie theater has 14 rows seats with 18 seats in each row. If the theater is full, how many people were watching the movie?

John's gym membership is $20 per month. He pays an 5% increase for every month he doesn't use the gym and $2 per month for every late payment. If he wasn't at the gym for 2 months and he delayed his payment for 3 months, what's his new gym membership fee?

Last month, Sarah worked 25 hours for 4 days at $15 per hour. This month, she will receive an increase of $1 per hour if she can work for 30 hours. Suppose she can work for 30 hours this month, how much will Sarah receive for working two months?
In a hotel, each floor has 8 rooms and 2 amenities rooms. The hotel has 20 floors. If only 120 rooms are occupied, how many rooms are unoccupied?

Greg wants to buy a new laptop which costs $650.  He agreed to mow the lawn for his neighbor to earn money at a rate of $20 per hour.  If he works for 6 hours every weekend for 10 weekends, how much money will be left over after he purchases the laptop?
Paul used 25 gallons of water for his garden this week. Last week he used 15% more. How much water did he use in total for the two weeks?
Lucy has a rectangular garden in her backyard. The garden is 8 feet wide and 10 feet long. She plans to build a fence around it. Each foot of fence costs $12. What is the total cost she needs to pay?

Lily's dog Ella got sick. She spent $350 on a vet visit, and her pet insurance covered 70%. She then had to buy medication costing $60. Insurance didn't cover any of the medication cost. How much did she pay in total?

Amy and Mia decide to practice singing.  Amy sang for 2 hours and then another 7 hours after taking a 2-hour break.  Mia sang the whole time Amy was singing and resting, and then another 1/4 times more hours.  What was their combined singing time without including Amy's break time?

Alex has 25 books on his shelves. He plans to split them between his two friends, Isabelle and Philip. Isabelle got 7 books from Alex, and Philip got 3 more than Isabelle. How many books will Alex have left?
Steve gets 1 apple from his mom everyday. How many apples will he have after 2 weeks?
Carrie is arranging a pizza party for her 5 friends. Each person eats 2 slices of pizza and a pizza has 8 slices. How many pizzas should Carrie order?
A turtle moves at a speed of 2 meters per minute. How far will it move in a week?

A chemistry lab has 40 liters of a solution. The lab uses 12 liters of the solution for an experiment, and then replaces it by adding 18 liters. The lab then uses 15 liters for another experiment. How many liters of the solution are left in the lab now?
Gina has 30 candy bars. She gave 5 to each of her siblings, Isaac, Ryan, and Cecilia. If she buys another pack of 5 candy bars, how many candy bars will Gina have?
Maeva makes 16 cupcakes. She and her brother ate 4 cupcakes and she gave another 6 cupcakes to her best friend. How many cupcakes does Maeva have left?
John can paint a house in 6 hours. Steve is twice as fast as John. If they decide to work together, in how much time can they finish painting a house?
A train travels at an average speed of 60 km/hour. The distance to be covered is 480 km. How many hours will the trip take?
Sandra collects stamps. She has 9 albums, each album has 15 pages, and each page can hold 5 stamps. How many stamps can she collect in total if all albums are filled?
Reese is an illustrator who receives client commissions daily. She gets 8 commission requests every day and completes 5 of them each day. How many pending commissions would she have after 7 days.
In a library, there are 5 sections, each containing 1200 books. If 20% of these books are borrowed, how many books are still available in the library?
Mr. Thompson bought twice as many candies for his younger daughter as he bought for his elder daughter. If the elder daughter received 40 candies, how many candies did Mr Thompson buy?
A factory produces 80 bicycles everyday. If in a month (i.e., 30 days) the factory did not work for 5 days, how many bicycles were produced in that month?
Zoom just bought 2/5 times as many cats as James, who has 9 more cats than Ricardo. If Ricardo has 15 cats, how many tails do Zoom's cats have?
Sarah is packaging candies in bags. Each bag holds 20 candies. If she has 600 candies, how many bags will Sarah need?
Jack has to get a blood test and a general check-up. The blood test is $150 and the check-up is double that cost. Insurance covers 70%. How much did he have to pay?
Claire loves to bake. She baked 16 chocolate cupcakes and ate 4. She made an equal number of vanilla and red velvet cupcakes, 3 each. How many cupcakes did she bake in total?
Carrie worked for 2 hours a day for 4 days to make and decorate a wedding cake. She was paid $22 an hour. The cost for supplies to make the cake was $54. How much profit did Carrie earn on the cake?
Ava has a jar of marbles. There are 15 red, 10 blue, and 5 green marbles. If she removes 2 red, 3 blue, and 1 green marble, how many marbles are left in the jar?
Nancy baked 15 less cookies today than she did yesterday. If she baked 27 cookies yesterday, how many cookies did she bake in total for two days?
Isabel spent $12 on each of her 5 magazines and later bought a book for 50% more than the price of the magazines. How much did Isabel spend on magazines and the book in total?
A farm produces 300 liters of milk every day. The production decreases by 50 liters per day during summer. How much milk does the farm produce in 7 days of summer?

A train leaves from Los Angeles to Phoenix which are 400 miles apart. The train travels at 50mph. Once it arrives, it stays in Phoenix for 2 hours before returning to Los Angeles. How many total hours does the trip take?
A king gets a crown made that costs $20,000.  He tips the person 10%.  How much did the king pay after the tip?

During the music festival, 500 spectators attended. 60% of the spectators were regular ticket holders. If 30% of those regular ticket holders received free merchandise, how many regular ticket holders received free merchandise?

George is planning a camping trip with his friends. He needs to fill a 5-liter water bottle for each member. The group consists of George, Bob, Kate, and Lisa. If each liter of water costs $1.20, how much will George spend on water?
Daniel is 4 inches taller than his sister Claire. Claire is 2 inches shorter than their friend Max. If Max is 44 inches tall, how tall is Daniel?
Alice has three times as many cats as dogs. If she currently has two dogs, how many pets does she have?
In a cinema, there are 15 rows of seats and each row has 12 seats. If all the seats are occupied, how many people are there in the cinema?
Paul played basketball for 90 minutes each day for 10 days. Rita also played basketball. Together they played basketball for 1350 minutes. How many minutes did Rita play basketball?

Jessica bakes cupcakes for a party. She invites 10 friends and each will want three cupcakes. Each batch makes a dozen cupcakes. If she bakes 3 batches, how many cupcakes will be left if she eats two cupcakes herself?
Mom bakes 6 cupcakes in a batch. If she bakes 3 batches but the kids eat 5 cupcakes, how many cupcakes are left?
Luke made some cupcakes for a fundraiser. Vanilla cupcakes were 2/5 of the cupcakes and the rest were chocolate. If there were 30 cupcakes, how many were chocolate?

Maria gets a monthly paycheck. Although her salary is $1000 monthly, her actual paycheck is reduced by some taxes and fees. If the government charges 20% income tax and 10% state tax, and she has a freeway toll of $100 per month, then what is the final amount left in Maria's paycheck?
Jenna uses 6 pieces of white, red, and blue construction paper to make a flag. Each color has the same number of pieces. If she make 25 flags, how many pieces of construction paper does she need?

Sam went out for a road trip. Usually, he would travel 100 miles in 2 hours. But today, due to some road work, he had to slow down and took 3 hours to travel the same distance. How much longer would it take him to travel 500 miles?
Peter mixed cups of flour and water in the ratio of 2:3 to bake some bread. If he used 60 cups in total, how many cups of flour did he use?

Ella works as a waitress at a local restaurant. She gets $10 per hour and an additional $6 per table that she serves. One day, she worked for 7 hours and served 16 tables. How much money does Ella make in a day?
A supermarket sells chocolate bars for $2 each. If Mike buys 15 bars and gets a 10% discount on his total amount. How much does he pay for the chocolates?
Brandon is making sandwiches for his class picnic. He needs 4 slices of bread per sandwich. If he has to make 25 sandwiches, how many slices of bread will he need?
At the fruit stand, Melanie has 8 crates of apples, each filled with 12 apples. If she sells 30% of the apples, how many apples will she still have?
A farmer noticed that his apple trees bore 25% more fruit this year than the previous year. If the farmer collected 5000 apples this year, how many apples were there last year?
Carlos and Jim have a combined total of 80 Pokemon cards. Carlos has 30 cards. If Carlos decided to give away half of his Pokemon cards to a local charity, how many cards would they have left?
On a farm, there are 16 cows, and each cow produces 30 liters of milk every day. The farmer puts 5 liters of milk into one bottle. How many bottles can he fill in a day?
Stuart has 50 comic books. Fifteen percent of his comic books are Marvel, 30% are DC, and the rest are unbranded. How many unbranded comic books does Stuart have?
John gives away 2 apples every day from his apple tree to his friend. If John's apple tree produces 120 apples in a season, how many days will the apples from John's tree last for his friend?
Sally wants to buy a new laptop that costs $800. She has already saved $300. If she saves $50 every week, how many weeks will it take for her to be able to buy the laptop?
It takes a gardener 12 hours to plant 48 trees. If two gardeners of equal skill are working, how long will it take them to plant 48 trees?
A bakery makes 500 cookies every day. For a special event, they decide to double the production for 3 days. How many cookies will they make during these 3 days?
Stella's uncle gave her $150 to buy books for school. She bought 3 books that cost $15 each and a calculator that cost $45. How much money does Stella have left?
Bob wants to build a 6-foot fence around his rectangular garden. The garden is 12 feet long. How wide is the garden if he has exactly enough fencing material?
Marie is a baker. She is baking a large batch of cookies for a charity event. Each batch she makes yields 24 cookies. If she wants to have 120 cookies, how many batches does she need to make?
Lucas buys a box of chocolates containing 25 pieces. He eats 6 pieces and then shares the remaining chocolates equally with his 3 friends. How many chocolates does each friend receive?
A box contains 30 apples. Each apple weighs 150 grams. If 5 apples were eaten and the rest were made into juice, how many grams will the juice weigh?

Andrew bakes cupcakes to sell at the local market. Each batch he bakes makes 24 cupcakes. He calculates that each cupcake costs him $0.50 to make. If Andrew sells each cupcake for $1.50, how much profit does he make after selling 4 batches of cupcakes?

A square dance hall can hold 100 couples before reaching max capacity. One night, the hall is booked for an event, but only 70% of the tickets are sold. Of the couples who bought tickets, 5% never show up to the event. How many couples are at the event?
If a shirt costs $25 and it goes on sale for 20% off, how much would the shirt cost after the discount?

A charity event was organized where each ticket was sold for $125. The organizers sold 350 tickets for the seated section and 500 for the standing section. They also received donations amounting to $6500. How much total money was collected in the event?

David has a collection of 150 stamps. He decided to add an extra number of stamps to his collection that totals twice the current amount. After getting his new additions, David sold 35% of his total collection. How many stamps does David still own?

Mike went to the bookstore and bought 7 fiction books, 5 non-fiction books, and 4 magazines. One fiction book costs $10, one non-fiction book costs $12, and one magazine costs $4. How much did Mike spend?
A tree's height is one-fourth of its shadow length. If the tree's shadow is 20 feet long, calculate the height of the tree.

Lisa collected 80 stamps in different sizes, colors, and shapes. A quarter of them are square, and 30 of them are circular. There are 25% fewer trapezoidal stamps than circular stamps. How many marbles are of other shapes?
Michael is saving for a new bike that costs $320. He has already saved $80. If he saves $40 a week, how many more weeks will he need to save for the bike?

Alice baked 3 types of cakes for her son's birthday: chocolate, strawberry, and caramel. She made twice as many caramel cakes as strawberry cakes and thrice as many chocolate cakes as caramel cakes. If there were 42 cakes in total, how many caramel cakes did Alice bake?
Paul has read 3 different books. He spends 4 hours reading the first book and twice as long reading each of the other two books. How much time does he spend reading?
Paula read 4 books in January, 5 books in February and twice as many books as that in March. How many books did she read in total?
Tim has $120. He bought 5 new books and 2 pens. Each book costs $20 and the total cost for pens is $10. How much money does Tim have left?
Tina invites 3 friends to a movie, it costs $11 each. She decides to add 2 more friends but gets a group discount of $2 on each ticket. How much does she spend on movie tickets for her friends?
A 25% increase in efficiency in the paper mill this year was due to upgrading the machines. If the machines were producing 200 sheets per hour, how many sheets are they producing after the upgrade?
Christine is making candy for a party. Each batch of candy uses 2 cups of sugar. She has 20 cups of sugar. How many batches of candies can Christine make before running out of sugar?
Peter has 40 books, and Simon has 70 books. If they bought the books at $5 each at the bookshop, how much more money did Simon pay than Peter for his books?
Steve has 3 more than twice the quantity of apples that Bill has. If Steve has 9 apples, how many apples does Bill have?

For her end of the year project, Emily performed a survey in her school. Each class population was approximately 30 students, and there were 10 classes in the small school. If each student was given a 2-page survey, how many pages would she need to print for the entire school?

Jack went shopping for a new hat. He tried on 6 hats in the first store and 4 more hats at the second store than he did at the first. At the third store, he tried twice as many hats as he did at the first and second stores combined. How many hats did Jack try on in total?
Daryl has 5 less robots than Kurt. Glenn has 2 more robots than Tony. Tony has twice as many robots as Kurt. If Kurt has 7 robots, how many robots do they all have together?

Kyle left home and traveled at an average speed of 50 mph towards the city. An hour later Sarah left from the same home and traveled in the same direction at 70 mph. How many hours after Kyle's departure did Sarah catch up with him?
Lisa reads 5 chapters of a book on weekdays and twice as many on weekends. If she reads every day in August (31 days), with 4 weekend days, how many chapters does she read during that month?

Kelly is creating a costume for her role in a Shakespearean play. She needs to design a dress and two cloaks. Each cloak uses a rectangular piece of fabric that measures 14 feet by 5 feet. She also needs a bodice that uses 3 square feet of fabric and 4 square feet of fabric for each of the sleeves. If the fabric she uses costs $2 per square foot, how much does she spend on the fabric in total?

At a parking garage, it costs $4 per hour to park. If Diana parked her car for 2 hours but then found out that she had a discount coupon that reduced the cost of parking by $1 per hour, how much did Diana have to pay for parking?
Zara makes a monthly deposit of $100 into her savings account. After a year, how much money will she have in her account?
Jenna is planning a trip to a vintage bookstore that sells each book for $15. She currently possesses $40. If she wants 3 books how much more money does she needs to get the books?
Annie has 36 fruit baskets. She gives 4 baskets each to her 3 friends. How many fruit baskets does Annie have left?

There are 20 rows of 10 desks in a classroom. The first two rows are reserved for the honor students, the third and fourth rows are for the average students and the last three rows are then for the students with special needs. If 2/3 of the desks reserved for students with special needs are occupied, how many vacant desks for students with special needs can be given to the average students?
Stacey has 50 seashells. Her grandmother gives her one-fifth of her 75 seashells. If each seashell has 3 ridges, how many ridges does Stacey have in total?
A garden center is selling plants for $3 each. Bella has $150 while Katie has $50. If they want to buy plants, how many plants can Bella and Katie buy altogether?
Mark bought 3 shirts with a price of $25 each. He also bought 2 pairs of shoes, each pair costing $40. If he paid using a coupon that takes $20 off, how much did Mark pay in total?
Lisa made 80 cookies for a bake sale. 75% of the cookies were sold. How many cookies are left unsold?

Five friends joined a 24-hour relay race. The first friend ran for 100 minutes, the second friend ran 20 minutes less than the first, the third friend ran 30 minutes more than the second, the fourth friend ran 10 minutes less than the third, and the last friend ran as long as the first. How long, in minutes, it took for them to finish the relay race?
Jonathan ran 7.5 kilometers. Mercedes ran twice that distance and Davonte ran 2 kilometers farther than Mercedes. How many kilometers did Mercedes and Davonte run in total?

Lana is planning a food drive in her neighborhood. There are 10 apartment buildings, each housing 20 families. She wants to donate 5 cans of food to each family. How many cans of food does Lana donate?
Sam can carry 15 kg. His younger brother, Dan can carry 50% less than Sam and their sister, Ann can carry 25% more than Sam. How much can they carry altogether?
Lara bought 12 novel books for $5 each and 5 cookbooks for $10 each. She also bought a gift card that costs her $25. How much money did Lara spend in total?
If a restaurant needs 4 lemons to make a cup of lemonade and they have just received a total of 800 lemons, how many cups of lemonade can they make?

A restaurant produces 4 batches of rolls in a day. Each batch makes 56 rolls. After the first batch, 40 are sold. After the second batch, 60 are sold. After the third batch, 50 are sold, and after the fourth batch, 45 are sold. How many rolls are left?
Sam weighs 70 pounds. His cousin, Bob, is twice as heavy as he is, and their common friend, Ella, is half as heavy as Bob. How much does Ella weigh?
Linda bought 6 dresses from a sale. The first one cost $30. Each subsequent dress cost $10 more than the previous one. How much did Linda spend in total?
Emma watched a movie that lasted for 2 hours and then watched another movie that was 3 times longer. How many total minutes did Emma spend watching movies?
Bobby saved $90 for the annual school fair. He spent $7 on the entrance ticket, $15 on games and $9 on food. How much money does Bobby have left?
There are 500 students in a school. If one-fifth of the students are in the 5th grade, how many students are not in the 5th grade?

A health clinic decides to hire a private doctor who was previously earning $80,000 per year. They offer her a 30% increase in her salary. If there are 8 partners in the clinic, how much does each partner have to pay for the doctor's salary?

Oliver is baking cookies for his friends. He has 3 trays; the first can hold 12 cookies, the second can hold 15 cookies, and the third can hold 10 cookies. If he fills each tray with cookies but the third tray is only half full, how many cookies has Oliver baked?
A bakery has 150 loaves of bread. Each day, it sells 20 loaves. How many days will it take for the bakery to run out of bread?

Peter loves to fish and decides to go deep-sea fishing. He catches 3 fish in the first hour, twice as many in the second hour and half the amount in the third hour. In the fourth hour, he doesn't catch any. How many fish does he catch in total?
Billy owns a cafe. A cup of coffee costs $2.5 and a sandwich costs $4. If he sells 100 cups of coffee and 50 sandwiches in a day, how much revenue does he earn?
The school is holding a fundraiser. They need $500 to reach their goal. If each student brings in $5, how many students need to participate to reach the school's goal?
Billy orders 4 hamburgers that cost $7 each. He pays with a $50 bill. How much does he get in change?
If you read 35 pages of a book in one day, and the book has 245 pages in total, how many days will it take to finish the book?

Samantha, Tim, and Bob are collecting stones for their garden. Samantha collected 7 stones. Tim collected 2 more stones than Samantha. Bob collected 3 fewer stones than Tim. If each stone weighs 4 pounds, how many pounds of stones did they collect in total?

During a summer baseball league, Paul hit 25 home runs in 50 games. If Paul played the same number of games in the fall league, how many more home runs did he need to hit in fall to double the number of summer home runs?
Joe's test scores are 70, 85, and 90. What score does he need to get on a fourth test so that his average score for the four tests is exactly 80?
A bakery produced 240 cookies in the morning. During the day, they sold 7/8 of the cookies. How many cookies are left unsold?

Codger is a three-footed sloth.  He has a challenging time buying shoes because the stores only sell the shoes in pairs. If he already owns the 3-piece set of shoes he is wearing, how many pairs of shoes does he need to buy to have 5 complete 3-piece sets of shoes?
Laura and John play a game of darts. Laura scores 85 points and John scores 95 points. How many more points did John score than Laura?
A farmer has 4 bags of grain. Each bag can feed 9 chickens for a day. If the farmer has 12 chickens, how many days will the grain last?

When Emma bought her first laptop, it had a screen size of 15 inches and cost $750. Her new laptop has a screen size of 30 inches and costs $1300. How much more expensive, as measured by cost per square inch, was her first laptop compared to her newest laptop?
Three times Jeff’s age, minus 5, is 25. How old is Jeff?
During a 6 hour road trip, Martin stopped for breaks totaling 1 hour and 15 minutes. How many hours did Martin spend driving?

Every day of last week, Daniel tried out a new juice recipe. On Monday, he followed a juice recipe that called for 8 cups of water. Each day after that, he doubled the amount of water. How much water did he use for the juice on Friday?
In a marathon, Peter ran 10 miles more than John, while John ran 5 miles less than Kate. Kate ran 15 miles in total. How many miles did the three run altogether?
A farmer has 150 apples. He sells 50 at the market and gives 20 to his neighbor. How many apples does he have left?
Chris bought 2 identical pizzas. Each pizza was cut into 8 slices. He ate 3 slices and his friend ate 5 slices. How many slices were left?

A science teacher is organising a field trip for his class to visit a local museum. He has 30 students, 2 parent volunteers, and himself. He also decided to get 6 additional tickets in case of last-minute joiners. Each ticket cost $15. How much does he have to pay for all the tickets?
Liz, Alice and Peter were playing a board game. Liz scored 2 points more than Peter, and Peter scored 3 points less than Alice. How many points did everyone score together, if Alice scored 12 points?
Amy bought 20 oranges, which she distributed equally among her 4 friends. How many oranges did each friend get?
Luke is training for a marathon and runs 6 miles every day. For how many days should he train to run a total of 84 miles?
There are 20 oranges, 30 bananas and 50 apples in a fruit shop. How many pieces of fruit are in the shop in total?
Mia has 50 novels in her bookshelf. She reads 4 novels each week. After how many weeks will Mia finish reading all the novels in her bookshelf?
There are 30 students in a class, and each student reads 5 books. If each book has an average of 220 pages, what is the total number of pages read by the entire class?

John owns a farm where he sells the milk of his cows and eggs from his chicken. He sells a gallon of milk for $3 and a dozen eggs for $2. If Peter bought 15 gallons of milk and 24 dozens of eggs from John, how much money did Peter pay to John?
Sandra has a saving plan where she saves $2 a day. She already saved $18. How much money will she have saved after 2 weeks?

A class of 26 students went on a field trip. Each student brought $10 for lunch. The teacher paid $5.25 per student for lunch. How much money do the students have left combined after paying for lunch?
There are 360 bottles of juice in a carton. They are packed into 3 large crates. If each crate contains 4 cartons, how many bottles of juice do the 3 crates contain in total?
Peter and John picked 180 oranges. Peter picked 5 times more oranges than John. How many oranges did John pick?
A tree has 20 branches. Each branch supports 10 smaller twigs and each twig has 5 leaves. Additionally, each branch bears 2 fruits. How many leaves and fruits are there in total on the tree?

A factory produces 150 bolt nuts per hour. After quality control, 10% of these will be classified as defective and will be unusable. How many bolt nuts will be considered usable after 8 hours of operation?
Joyce is constructing a fence for her garden and needs 10 lengths of iron rods that measure 3 feet and 4 lengths of iron rods that measure 5 feet. How many feet of iron rods does Joyce need to buy?
A cupcake store sells cupcakes for $.75 each. Maria has $3 and leaves the store with $1.50. How many cupcakes did she buy?

A school has 2000 students and each student pays a tuition of $5000 per term. If 1/4 of the students are on scholarship and do not pay tuition, how much money does the school collect in tuition fees per term?

Jenny and her friends are participating in a marathon relay. Each of them has to run 5km. If there are 4 members in their team, and Jenny wants to run two legs of the relay, what will be Jenny's total running distance?
Carlos made a huge pudding weighing 100 lbs. Each day the pudding reduces by half. How many lbs will the pudding weigh on the third day after he made the pudding?
A local ice-cream shop sells a scoop of ice-cream for $2.50. Timmy has $15. How many scoops of ice-cream can Timmy buy?
The black rope is 100 meters long. The white rope is 10 meters more than twice the length of the black rope. What is the total length for the 2 ropes in meters?
Jonathan is three times as old as his younger brother James. The sum of their ages is 52. How old is Jonathan?

Sarah's mom bought a blender, a toaster, and a coffee machine. The cost of the blender and the coffee machine was $100. The toaster cost 4 times less than the coffee machine. If the toaster costs $20, how much was the coffee machine?
John's car can run 40 miles on a gallon of gas. If he has to travel 240 miles, how many gallons of gas will he need?

There are 10 7-year-olds, 15 8-year-olds, 20 9-year-olds, and 12 ten-year-olds at a particular music school. If the 7 and 8-year-olds are in one class and the 9 and 10-year-olds are in another class, what is the average class size?
Carlos ordered 4 pizzas for his party. Each pizza has 8 slices. If each of his 16 guests ate 2 slices, how many slices of pizza would be left?
A bakery sold 130 cupcakes in a day. 35% of these cupcakes were chocolate flavored, and the remaining cupcakes were red velvet flavored. How many red velvet cupcakes did the bakery sell in a day?
Kate has 4 stickers. Jean has 3 more stickers than Kate. Liz has twice as many stickers as Jean. How many stickers do they have in total?
Tim's school bus has 45 seats. In the morning, 80% of the seats are occupied. After school, only 50% of the seats are occupied. How many students in total does the bus transport?
In a softball game, 13 balls are used in the first inning. The game has 9 innings in total. If 2 balls are lost each inning, how many balls must the team have at the beginning of the game?
Aunt Betsy wants to order 4 personalized coffee mugs for her children. The mugs are 40% off of $10.00 and having their photos printed on them will cost $5.00 each. How much will she spend in total?
In Carmen's farm, she has 25 chickens. Each chicken lays 2 eggs daily. If half of the eggs hatched after 3 days, how many chickens does Carmen have now?
In a bike race, Mark covered the distance twice as fast as James, who took 3 hours. How long did it take Mark to complete the race?
Peter had 5 packs of cards. Each pack contains 52 cards. He lost some cards while playing and now he is left with only 4 packs. How many cards does Peter have now?
Samantha sells necklaces at a market. Each necklace costs her $5 to make, and she sells them for $15 each. Last week she sold 20 necklaces. How much profit did Samantha make last week?
A library contains 50% fiction books, 35% non-fiction books, and the rest are reference books. If the library contains 8000 books, how many of them are reference books?
Helen has a small flower garden. She plants 10 roses and 8 tulips each month. After 12 months, how many flowers has she planted?

Jessica decided to paint all the rooms in her house over the weekend. Each room took about 3 gallons of paint to finish. The house has 4 bedrooms, a kitchen, a living room and a dining room. Moreover, Jessica accidentally spilled half a gallon of paint in the kitchen and had to buy an extra. At the end of the painting project, Jessica had 2 gallons of paint left. How much paint did Jessica buy?
A school ordered 5 cartons of milk. Each carton contains 250 milliliters of milk. How many liters of milk did the school order in total?
David ordered 4 pizzas for a party. Each pizza is cut into 8 slices. If there are 5 people at the party and each person eats 3 slices, how many slices are left?

Maria went shopping for school supplies. She bought three notebooks, two pens, and a geometry set. The geometry set was three times as expensive as a notebook, and a pen was 50% less than the cost of a notebook. If a notebook cost $5, how much did Maria pay for all the supplies?
David gets 30% more pocket money than his friend who gets $100. How much pocket money does David get?

The Craft store sells origami sets for $200 each. A certain club bought 3 sets for their workshop and also bought thrice as many for their annual competition. Since the club bought more than 10 sets, they were given a 10% discount for each set. How much did the club pay in total?
Anita reads 50 pages of a book per day. However, she also gets 10 additional pages to read each day. How many days would it take for her to read a 500-page book?
Jenny wants to buy a bicycle. To save money, Jenny sold lemonade for $2 a cup and cookies for $1 each. She sold 50 cups of lemonade and 30 cookies. How much money did she make?
Lisa started her journey at 5:00 p.m. and reached her destination at 9:00 p.m. It took her 1 hour to rest on the way. How many hours did Lisa drive?
Amy is a kindergarten teacher who wishes to distribute crayons to her students. If she has 24 students and plans to give each student 5 crayons, how many crayons does she need in total?
Laura runs a fruit stand. Each apple is sold for $1 and each banana is sold for $2. If she sells 15 apples and 10 bananas, how much does she make in total?
Over a week, on each of 5 Wednesdays, it snowed 3 cm. On each of 6 Thursdays, it snowed 2 cm. How many more centimeters did it snow on Wednesdays than on Thursdays?
A bookshelf has 5 shelves. Each shelf can hold 18 books. How many books can the bookshelf hold in total?
A cake recipe calls for 6 eggs. How many eggs would you need if you tripled the recipe?
Sally baked 10 cookies. Each of her 5 friends wants to eat 3 cookies. How many more cookies does Sally need to bake?
A library has four shelves with 150 books on each shelf. If there are 120 books checked out, how many books are still at the library?
Mike jogs 4 kilometers in the first hour. If he doubles the distance every subsequent hour, how much will he jog if he continues for 3 hours?
Jack’s soup recipe makes 12 quarts and needs 3 pounds of meat. He wants to make a smaller batch of 3 quarts. How much meat will he need?
Steve runs 5 kilometers every morning and 3 kilometers every evening. If Steve runs for 6 days a week, how many kilometers does he run in 2 weeks?

Two painters are hired to paint a building. One painter can paint the building by himself in 4 days less than the other painter. If they work together, they can paint the building in 6 days. How many days would it take for the slower painter to paint the building by himself?
Louise has 42 candies. She decided to keep 12 candies for herself and she gave the rest to her 6 friends equally. How many candies did each friend receive?
Amy is a painter, she can paint 2 walls in one hour. How many walls can Amy paint in 4.5 hours?
Samantha weighs 10 pounds more than her sister Amanda now. Samantha also weighs 15 pounds more than Amanda did 1 year ago. How much did Amanda weigh 1 year ago if she currently weighs 150 pounds?
Sara baked 12 cookies and she wants to distribute them equally into 4 tins. How many cookies will be in each tin?

A school is organizing a math competition. If there are 34 students taking part in the competition and each participant is given a participation trophy that costs $4, how much will be spent on trophies?
Jessica and George ran a marathon together. The marathon is 26 miles, and George ran two-thirds of the distance Jessica ran. How many miles did Jessica run?
A shopping bag can hold 15 items. If Sally has 80 items she wants to put into the shopping bags, how many shopping bags does Sally need?

Molly has a cake business. She sells cupcakes for $2 each, cookies for $1 each, and cake slices for $4 each. If Molly's goal is to make $100 and she has already made $50 selling cupcakes, how many cake slices does she need to sell to reach her goal?

Dave is preparing a beach party on a sunny day, where there will be a total of 20 people. He will need 3 liters of water per person to account for the heat. Water bottles are sold for $1.50 for a pack of 6. One bottle has 0.5 liter of water. How much will he spend on water?
Jane bakes cookies and uses 300g of flour for each batch. If she has 5kg of flour, how many batches of cookies can she make?
Ashley's garden has 40 fruit trees. Each tree gives her 25 fruits in 7 days. Ashley decided to buy 10 more trees. How many fruits will all the trees give her in 14 days?
Sarah baked 5 apple pies and each pie cost her $5 in ingredients. She also bought 3 pumpkins for $3 each. How much money did Sarah spend on baking and buying pumpkins?
Emma bakes 5 trays of muffins. Each tray holds 6 muffins. If each muffin is sold for $1.50, how much money would Emma make if she sells all the muffins?
In a box of fruits, 35% of the fruits are rotten. If the box has 30 rotten fruits, how many fruits are there totally in the box?
There are 500 seats in a concert hall. 1/4 of the total seats are reserved for VIPs. 1/10 of the total seats are not occupied. How many seats are left for regular ticket buyers?

Annabel has decided to bake a large batch of cookies to distribute to her neighbors. She starts with 25 cookies and then bakes another 75 cookies. If she decides to keep 35 cookies for her family, how many cookies does she have now?

Sally has five 2-liter bottles of root beer and three 2-liter bottles of cream soda. Her friend Lucy has two 2-liter bottles of root beer and four 2-liter bottles of cream soda. How many more liters of soda does Sally have?
Alice received a gift of $100 from her dad. She wanted to buy a watch which costs $75 and a perfume which costs $40. How much more money does she need to buy both the items?

John buys 3 comic books priced at $10 each and 2 video games priced at $50 each. His younger brother, Sam, buys toys that are worth half the cost of the comic books John bought but buys twice as many video games as John. How much do they both spend?
There are 50 children in a class. 30% of them have brown hair, and 20% of them have blonde hair. How many children have hair of other colors?
John put 15 candies in each party bag for a birthday party. Mike put 3 times the amount of candies in each bag compared to John. If there are 25 bags, how many total candies did Mike put in the bags?
Tony and Henry are painting a house. Tony paints 80 square meters and then Henry paints 2/5 as many as Tony. If they paint an additional 30 square meters each, how many have they painted on average?

John plans to run a marathon and needs to prepare. In the first week of training, John runs 30 miles. Each new week, he plans to add 5 miles to the distance. How many miles will he run in the third week?
Sue baked 10 cupcakes for a bake sale. Each cupcake is sold for $5.00. She also baked 3 cakes and sold each for $20.00. How much money did Sue make from the bake sale?
A teacher bought 8 boxes of chalk, each containing 50 pieces. After using some in classes, he still had 270 pieces left. How many pieces of chalk had he used?
Sara is a shoe salesperson, and she receives 10% commission from every shoe she sells. If she sold shoes worth $200 to one customer and $150 to another customer how much commission did she earn?
Sandra is baking cookies for a party. She has 5 batches of cookies with 12 cookies per batch. If she eats 4 cookies and gives 10 cookies to her neighbor, how many cookies does she have left?

Sophia has 4 flower pots. Each pot holds 6 flowers. She bought a pack of flower seeds that contains 100 seeds for $20. If each seed grows into a flower, how much does she need to spend if she wants to fill all her flower pots?
Lenny picked 24 apples in the apple orchard. He dropped and lost 8 apples on the way home. How many apples does Lenny have left?
Tina and Tim are doing a running challenge. Tina runs for 25 minutes every day and Tim runs 10 minutes more than Tina. How long, in minutes, do they run together in a week?
In a school, there are 7 classes each containing 30 students. If every student needs 3 notebooks for the academic year, how many notebooks does the school need to order?

John surveyed 500 people in his city asking them which pet they preferred - dogs, cats, birds, or fish. 150 respondents said they preferred dogs, 200 respondents said they preferred cats, and 75 respondents said they preferred birds. How many respondents picked fish as their favorite pet?
Tim needs to write an essay of 500 words. He can write 100 words in 10 minutes. If he has already written 50 words, how many more minutes will he need to finish the essay?
Kim and Hannah decided to split their lottery winnings so that Kim gets 30% and Hannah gets the rest. If Hannah's share is $7,000, how much did they win in total?
Leo's dog catches 2 squirrels and 5 birds. Lilly's dog catches 4 less than three times as many animals as Leo's dog. How many animals does Lilly's dog catch?
Emma drives 45 miles to work every day. If fuel costs $2.5 per gallon and her car gives an average of 15 miles per gallon, how much does Emma spend on fuel in a month (considering 20 working days)?
Emily runs a poultry farm with chicken and turkeys. Each chicken lays 5 eggs daily and each turkey lays 3 eggs. Emily collects 100 eggs daily. If she has 15 chickens, how many turkeys does she have?

Lisa runs a small bakery where she sells homemade cookies. She makes 50 chocolate chip cookies and only half as many oatmeal cookies each day. If each cookie is sold for $2, how much does she earn from selling all her cookies in one day?

A garden has four types of roses planted: red, white, yellow, and pink. Half of them are yellow, and only 5 are red. Most of them are pink. There are 64 roses in the garden. How many have pink petals?
Maya wants to buy her and her friends a candy bar and a soda. A candy bar costs $1.00 while a soda costs $0.50. If Maya has $15.00, how many of her friends can get a candy bar and a soda?
A box of chocolates cost $10 and a bouquet of flowers cost $15. How much would 3 boxes of chocolates and 2 bouquets of flowers cost?

Emily is planning a party and wants to fill 4 punch bowls with apple juice. Each punch bowl can hold 10 cups of juice. Juice comes in packets that can make 8 cups of juice and cost $4 each. If Emily spends $32 on juice, how many punch bowls can she fill?
A group of students collected cans for a recycling drive. If they collected 35 cans each day for a week, how many cans did they collect in total?
Sean made 3 more than four times as many painted rocks as Cody. Eric made twice as many as Sean. Cody made 7 rocks. How many did Eric make?

There are 10 students in a drama club and each needs 3 costumes for their play. However, the director decided that each student needs to have one extra costume for emergencies. How many costumes does the drama club need?

Melody needs to read 20 pages for her English class, 16 pages for her Science class, 8 pages for her Civics, and 12 pages for Chinese class. She decided to read one-fourth of the number of pages for each class tomorrow. How many pages will she read tomorrow?

Sam flies a kite which goes up at a rate of 70 feet per minute. The kite goes down at a rate of 20 feet per minute when Sam gives it some slack. During his kite flying session, Sam had the kite going up for 20 minutes, gave it slack for 10 minutes, then had the kite going up again for 10 minutes, before pulling it back down. What was the highest point reached by the kite?
Juanita baked 3 dozen cookies for a school fundraiser. Each dozen cost her $2 in ingredients. She sold each cookie for $0.75. How much profit did Juanita make from selling all the cookies?
Tom has saved $80 in January. In February, he saved twice as much as he saved in January. How much did Tom save in total for these two months?
Bonnie bought a skirt worth $70 and a blouse worth $35. If she used a 20% off coupon, how much did she pay in total?
Peter works for 300 minutes. He takes a coffee break every 30 minutes and a lunch break every 150 minutes. How many more coffee breaks does he take than lunch breaks?

Stella breeds beagles, and her adult beagles recently had five litters of 6 each. She sold half of the puppies to a pet store. She sold the remaining beagles at $100 per pet. How much money did Stella earn from selling all her beagles?
A company prints business cards. The company charges $12 for every pack of 50 cards. If a customer orders 600 cards, how much money does the customer have to pay?
In a orchard, there are 200 apple trees. There are twice as many orange trees as apple trees. How many trees are there in total in the orchard?
George turns 42 today. 4 years ago, he was thrice as old as James will be in 5 years. If James' older sister is 3 years older than James, how old is James' older sister?
Joel spends $70 on groceries and $85 on clothes. He also repays his friend $25 he borrowed. If he had $200 to start with, how much money does he have left?

Claire runs a bakery shop. She bakes 150 cookies every day. However, Claire realized that 1/5 of the freshly baked cookies were burned and hence, not sellable. How many cookies did Claire have left for sale?

Joshua, Martha, and Kayla went to buy notebooks for school. Joshua bought twice as many notebooks as Martha. Martha bought 5 notebooks. Kayla bought the same number of notebooks as Joshua. If one notebook costs $2.50, how much money did the three friends spend in total on notebooks?
Bryan is 40 years old and his son Tom is 5 years away from being 1/3 the age of Bryan. How old is Tom?

Rachel has 30 paperback books and 15 hardcover books. Her friend Lauren has half as many paperback books as Rachel and the same number of hardcover books. If they combine all their books together, how many do they have in total?

A toy store has a special sale on stuffed animals. They sold 40 stuffed animals and made $320. This was $80 less than they would have made if they sold it without the special sale. How much more does a stuffed animal cost when not on sale?
A concert hall has 200 seats. An adult ticket is priced at $10, and a child ticket is $6. If the concert hall was filled with 120 adults, what's the total ticket revenue for the concert?
Ella drank 6 glasses of water every day for a week. If a glass holds 200 milliliters, how many liters of water in total did Ella consume in a week?

A toy shop has 20 Star Wars action figures and twice as many Batman figures. If they decide to bring in 3 times the current total number of action figures next month, how many Star Wars and Batman action figures will they have in total?
At a picnic, 3/4 of the 120 attendees were children. Every child at the picnic was given 3 balloons, and every adult was given 1 balloon. How many balloons were given out at the picnic?
A bookstore sells 500 books and 250 magazines per day. How many items does it sell in one year if it's open every day?
In a store, 30 notebooks are sold in a day. After a week, how many notebooks are sold?
A man deposits $500 into a savings account that earns 3% interest annually. How much money will he have in the account after 5 years?

The distance from Matthew's home to the post office is 1.5 miles, and the distance to the grocery store is 2 miles. He makes the round trip to the post office once every day for 5 days a week. Then he makes a round trip to the grocery store twice during weekends. What is his car's mileage for a typical week?
In a store sale, you get a $2 discount for every $20 that you spend. If you buy items worth $60 before discounts, how much will you end up paying?
A farm has 5000 apples. If each box can store 20 apples and each truck can load 50 boxes, how many trucks are needed to transport all the apples?

Tim is on a flight from Paris to Tokyo which takes 14 hours. He watches a series of 8 short films, each taking 30 minutes. He then sleeps for 6 hours. After that, he reads a book for 2.5 hours. If there were no breaks between activities, how many more hours are left before Tim lands in Tokyo?
Peter has two children, Andrew and Mary. Their ages add up to 28. Mary is two years older than Andrew. If Peter is five times as old as Andrew, how old is Peter?
Liza can read 100 more pages this month compared to last month, which is twice as much as what she could read last month. How many pages can Liza read in total this month?

John worked 3 hours every morning delivering newspapers in his neighborhood for $15.00 an hour. 4 days a week, after school, he worked at a bookstore for 3 hours and made $13.50 an hour. On the weekends, he usually made $15.00 an hour babysitting and was guaranteed 5 hours every Sunday from his neighbor. How much money did he make over 7 days?

John bought some food for his picnic: a bottle of soda, a pizza, and a bottle of iced tea. The pizza was for $10, and the soda was three times more expensive. The bottle of iced tea cost was 50% of the total cost of the pizza and soda. How much did John pay for his food?
Donna wanted to buy a new dress that costs $80. However, the store was offering a 30% discount on the dress. How much did Donna pay for the dress?
Dani's pet gerbil Jerry takes 5 seconds to finish 10 sunflower seeds every day. How long would it take Jerry to finish 500 sunflower seeds?
A factory makes 50 tires per hour throughout an 8-hour workday. How many tires are made if the factory operates 5 days a week?
If a company produced 1000 units of a product in January and doubles the production every month, what would be the total production in May?
Sarah worked 7 hours a day every day from the 2nd to the 7th, including the 2nd and not including the 7th. How many hours did she work?

Finn and Jake passed through the magic forest and the candy kingdom. In the magic forest, Finn saw 5 magical creatures and Jake saw 10 while in the candy kingdom, Finn saw 3 different kinds of candies and Jake saw 6. They each passed through the magic forest 2 times and the candy kingdom 3 times. How much magical experiences did Finn and Jake had?
A boy earns $10 per hour for washing cars. He has already earned $50. If he works for another 4 hours, how much will he have earned in total?

Lucy sells handmade bracelets at a flea market. She charges $14 for each bracelet. One weekend, she sold 4 bracelets on Saturday and 7 bracelets on Sunday. How much money, in dollars, did she make over the weekend?
If 4 girls each make 14 cookies for a school event, and 3 other boys are making 15 cookies each, how many total cookies will be available?

Eva has collected 90 liters of water to use for her plants. She uses 8 liters to water her indoor plants and uses half as much to water her outdoor herbs. Then, she uses a third of the remaining water to wash her car. How many liters of water does Eva use to wash her car?

Jane bakes cookies for the school bake sale. She bakes 3 dozen chocolate chip cookies, 4 dozen sugar cookies, and 2 dozen peanut butter cookies. If each dozen weighs 1.5 pounds, how many pounds of cookies did she bake in total?
Sam weighs 60 kg. He intends to lose 20% of his weight in the next 3 months. How many kilograms does he plan to lose?
On beautiful Island, there are 80 flamingos. This is 20 more than 3 times as many flamingos on Lonely Island. How many flamingos are there on Lonely Island?
John ran 5 miles yesterday. Sal ran 2 miles less than John did. How many total miles did John and Sal run yesterday?
Arnold has 3 plant pots. Each pot needs 7 liters of water every week and he changes the water every week. How many liters of water will he need in three weeks?
A tower has 30 floors. Each floor is 4 meters high, except for the top three floors. The top three floors are each 0.75 meters higher. How tall is the tower?
Tyler caught 5 fish at the lake. His brother Dylan caught twice as much as Tyler did. If they released 3 fish back into the lake, how many fish do they have now?

Mandy uses a box of baking soda to bake cookies. Each box can make four batches of cookies, and each cookie requires 3 tablespoons of baking soda. If a box of baking soda has 72 tablespoons, how many cookies can she make from one box?
Lisa buys 4 dresses that cost $30 each. She also buys $80 worth of accessories. How much does she spend?
Pizza Palace makes 50 large pizzas each day. Half of the pizzas are pepperoni, one-third are cheese, and the rest are veggie pizzas. How many veggie pizzas does the Pizza Palace make every day?
An ultramarathon runner ran for 18 hours straight. If he ran at an average of 5 mph, how many miles did he cover?
Toronto is 500 kilometers east of Montreal. Halifax is 200 kilometers west of Montreal. If you travel at 100 kilometers per hour, how long will it take to get from Toronto to Halifax?

A school cafeteria has 200 students and 6 long tables for them to eat at. Each long table seats 20 students. How many additional circular tables, with each seating 10 students, should the school bring so that all the students can sit to eat?
Adam is 70 kilograms. Going with his weight loss regime, he would lose 1 kilogram per week. If 1 kilogram equals 2.2 pounds, how many pounds will he weigh after losing weight for 12 weeks?
Leo bought a bouquet of 60 roses for $30. If he decides to sell each rose for 50 cents, how many roses will he have left by the time he recovers his expenditure on the roses?

Benjamin, who is a twitch streamer, streams for 8 hours each day. He started streaming halfway through January, maintained that pace, and then tripled the number of stream hours on the remaining days. What's the total number of stream hours by the end of the month?

Isabella runs a gardening shop. She charges $45 for plants, $35 for fertilizer, and $75 for garden tools, but she has to buy a set of seeds for $15 for each plant. Today, she has sold three plants, two sets of fertilizers and three garden tools. If she makes $25 in tips, how much money will she have in dollars at the end of the day?

A television was originally priced at $1200. After a week, the price was reduced by 10%. If the television was still not sold a month later and the selling price was reduced again by 5%, how much was the total reduction from the original selling price?

Joe had 200 data points on his dataset. He then added 20% more data points to the dataset. If he reduced the data points by 1/4 of the total data points, calculate the total number of data points the dataset contains.

Michael has 30 comic books. He gives half of his books to his cousin. From his remaining books, he sells one-fourth of his books at a garage sale. Then he buys 4 new comic books. How many comic books does Michael have now?
Kate earned $500 from her part-time job. If she saved 1/4 of it and spent the rest for her expenses, how much is left in her savings?
At a movie theater, 80 people watched the first show, 95 people watched the second show, and 120 people watched the third show. How many people altogether watched shows at this theater?

At a soccer tournament, 5 teams are participating. Each team consists of 11 players, and each player needs a pair of shoes and shin guards to play. The tournament organizers provide each player with 3 socks per pair of shoes. How many socks were provided?
Alex is planning a party. He needs to buy 6 bags of chips at $3.00 each, and 8 six-packs of soda at $4.00 each, if he has $60.00. How much money will Alex have left?

Ryan bought 3 comic books at the store on Monday. On Tuesday, he went back and bought 6 more. Wednesday morning, he saw that the store was having a sale and he bought thrice the number of comics he did on Monday. Later that day he discovered that his brother had borrowed 2 of the comics. How many comics does he have now?
A book club reads 5 books a year. If there are 12 members in the club, how many books in total does the club read in 2 years?

In a breakfast cafe, 40% of the menu are egg dishes and the rest are other combinations. Luke ordered half of the egg dishes and a third of the other combinations. If the total number of dishes on the menu was 250 dishes, how many did Luke order?

A teacher prepared a spelling bee for her students. She splits the class equally into 4 groups. If the class has 28 students in total and each group gets 10 minutes for spelling words, then how long, in minutes, does it take each group to finish?

Susan wants to pack some gift boxes for the children in her local community. She bought 6 packs of chocolates, 4 boxes of crayons, and 3 teddy bears. The chocolates cost $3 each, the crayons cost $2, and the teddy bears cost $4. She packed everything into 4 boxes. If some boxes were lost during delivery, how much did the value of the lost boxes cost if it turned out that only 2 boxes were delivered?

Sara is arranging flowers. She has 2 vases to fill. In the first vase, she places 10 roses and 5 lilies. In the second vase, she places 15 roses and 7 lilies. She still has 8 lilies left to arrange. How many flowers did she start with?
Laura baked 48 cupcakes for her daughter's birthday party. She wants to pack them in boxes, with 6 cupcakes in each box. How many boxes does Laura need?
Pete has 4 times as many comic books as Sam. Sam has half as many comic books as Jack. If Jack has 30 comic books, how many comic books does Pete have?
In a school, there are 600 students. If one in six students plays a musical instrument, how many students play a musical instrument?
Joshua had 160 DVDs in his collection. Each month, he bought 15% more DVDs. How many DVDs did he have in his collection after 3 months?
Cindy bought five dresses at $150 each, a purse for $300, and two pairs of shoes at $75 each. If the tax was 8% of the total amount, how much did she pay including the tax?

Each cow needs to eat 45 apples per day to live. Each wolf needs to eat 2 cows per day to live. Each lion needs to eat 8 wolves a day to live. How many apples get eaten every day in this forest if it has 5 lions?
Sue has saved $20 every month for 1 year. How much money does she have now?
Maggie baked 4 trays of cookies, each tray with 12 cookies. She gave half of the cookies to her neighbour. How many cookies does Maggie have left?

Gabriel bought 12 packs of chocolate, each pack contains 5 bars. Gabriel and his friend ate 3 bars each. If Gabriel wants to share remaining bars with his 2 nephews equitably, how many chocolate bars would each nephew receive?
If a bookstore has 11 copies of a book and decides to order 4 times as many books as they currently have in stock to meet demand, how many total books will the store have?
A factory produces 2000 widgets per hour. If the factory operates 8 hours a day, how many widgets does it produce in a work week of 5 days?
Howard has 20 balloons, 25% of which are blue. The rest are red. How many red balloons does Howard have?
A bus ride costs $2.5 per ride. If Peter buys a $20 bus card, how many bus rides can he take?
A bookshelf has 3 rows. Each row has 6 books. How many books are there on the shelf?

At a marathon, Peter finished the race 9 minutes after John. John finished the race 1/3 of the time that George finished. George finished the race in 45 minutes. How long did it take for Peter to finish the race?

A running club took part in a marathon. The club members took 4 buses, 8 cars and 3 vans. There were 20 people in each bus, 5 people in each car and 7 people in each van. How many people from the club took part in the marathon?

Shelly and Katie went to the farm to pick fruits. Shelly picked 10 buckets of peaches and 7 buckets of strawberries. Katie picked 8 buckets of peaches more than Shelly, and 3 fewer buckets of strawberries than Shelly. How many buckets of fruits did Katie pick, in total?
Matthew works 5 days a week and earns $80 per day. How much does he earn in 7 weeks?
If a packet of vegetable seeds costs $1.20 and Alice needs to buy 15 packets, how much will all the seeds cost?
A baker has 9 ingredients to make a cake. If each cake requires 3 ingredients and all ingredients are used, how many different cakes can the baker prepare?
A school library has 500 books. 1/5 of the books are fiction, and 1/4 of the books are non-fiction. How many books are other types of books?
George's restaurant serves 5 breakfast meals for $10 each and 7 lunch meals for $20 each. How much did George's restaurant make from selling breakfast and lunch meals?
Mia and her sister went on a shopping spree. Mia spent $250 and her sister spent twice as much as Mia. How much did they spend altogether?
A square garden has a side length of 12 feet. What's the perimeter of the garden?

Bob works in a factory that manufactures tennis balls. In one day, the factory can produce 10,000 tennis balls. Each can of tennis balls holds 3 balls. How many days will it take to produce enough tennis balls to fill 5,000 cans?

A bookshop sells 5 types of books. The first type costs $4 each, the second type costs $6 each, the third type costs $8 each, the fourth type costs $10 each, and the fifth type cost $12 each. If a customer buys one of each type, how much does he have to pay in total?
Simone buys a pack of socks for $12. If the pack contains 6 pairs of socks, how much does each pair of socks cost?
A doughnut shop sold 15 doughnuts in the morning and twice as many in the afternoon. If doughnuts cost $2.50 each, how much money did the shop make on doughnut sales that day?
A certain recipe calls for 3 cups of flour. How many cups of flour will be needed if the recipe is doubled, then halved, then tripled?

Emma is baking cookies for her school's bake sale. She baked 4 dozen chocolate cookies, 5 dozen vanilla cookies, and 3 dozen oatmeal cookies. If they sell cookies for $1 each, how much will all the cookies cost?
Tom has 50 baseball cards. He wants to divide them equally among his friends. If each friend receives 5 baseball cards, how many friends does Tom have?
A gym is open from 9 am to 8 pm on weekdays and 11 am to 6 pm on weekends. How many hours is the gym open in a week?

In her garden, Cathy has 8 apple trees, 5 peach trees, and 4 pear trees. Each apple tree produces 10 kg of apples, each peach tree produces 12 kg of peaches, and each pear tree produces 15 kg of pears. How many kilograms of fruit does Cathy's garden produce in total?
Maria baked 12 pies for a community event. Each pie was divided into 8 equal slices. If 63 people each took a slice, how many slices were leftover?
Sarah went to the store and bought books for $30 and 3 CDs that cost the same amount of money. She spent a total of $90. How much did Sarah pay for each of the CDs?
Nina is planning a road trip that is 450 miles long. Her car gets 30 miles per gallon. If gas costs $3 per gallon, how much will Nina have to spend on gas for the whole trip?
A bus can carry 50 passengers. If 3 quarter full, how many passengers are on the bus?
If the printer produces 15 pages per minute and works for 2 hours, how many pages will it produce?
A bookshelf can hold 15 books in each row. Elaine managed to fill up 6 rows and Casey managed to fill up 4 rows more than Elaine. How many books do they have altogether?

Alexa is aiming to break the current school record for most goals in a football season. She is just 10 goals short of tying the record. By the end of the season, she surpasses the record by 4 goals. The old record was 73 goals. Alexa scored 2 penalty goals (worth one goal each), 6 regular goals, and a few hat-tricks (worth three goals each). Normally, she scores 1 hat-trick a game. How many more hat-tricks did she score in the final game compared to her normal amount?

Janet wants to buy a $500,000 house that her friend Mike is selling. She only has $10,000 in cash. She intends to raise the remaining amount by selling her old cars. She has 2 Ford cars, 3 BMW cars, and 5 Toyota cars. She will get $20,000 for each Ford car, $50,000 for a BMW car, and $40,000 per Toyota car. After paying Mike for the house, how much money will Janet have left?

Every month of the last quarter Sara tried a new pasta recipe. The first month, she used 18 boxes of pasta. Each month after that, is double the last month. How many boxes of pasta did she use in the third month?
Seven less than twice the number of cats plus the difference between the number of cats and the number of dogs is equal to 68. If the number of cats is 15, how many dogs are there?
Harold has a tank filled with 80 liters of water. He notices a leak in the tank, and it's leaking out 5 liters of water per hour. How many hours will it take for the tank to be emptied?
John received 15 packets of gum for his birthday and each packet has 9 pieces of gum. How many pieces of gum does John have in total?
Luke has 35 candy bars. He eats 2 candy bars each day for 7 days. Then his dad gives him 10 more candy bars. How many candy bars does Luke have left?
Marta has to complete a project in 6 hours. She starts at 9 AM, how many project tasks does she have to complete per hour if there are 54 tasks?
Lisa goes to a coffee shop and buys a coffee for $4.00 every weekday for a month. She also buys a muffin for $2.50 every Friday. How much does she spend at the coffee shop in a month of 30 days?
In a school, 1/4 of 1200 students are on the debate team. Among them, 50% also participate in the drama club. How many students are both on the debate team and in the drama club?
In a zoo, there are 150 species of animals. If half of them are mammals and the rest are birds, how many birds are there in the zoo?

Maria runs a bakery that sells cupcakes and doughnuts. Each box of cupcakes contains 6 pieces while each box of doughnuts contains 10 pieces. In a week, Maria sold 25 boxes of cupcakes and 15 boxes of doughnuts. How many total pieces of cupcakes and doughnuts did she sell?
A factory produces 250 car tires every hour. However, 10% of these tires are defective and are discarded. How many good tires does the factory produce in 8 hours?
Emilia had 4 $20 bills, 6 $10 bills, and 2 $2 coins. How much money did she have altogether?

A bracelet is made up of three types of gemstones. There are nine ruby stones and twice as many sapphire stones as rubies. The third type of gemstones are emeralds. How many emerald stones are in the bracelet if there's a total of 50 gemstones?
A bakery sells a dozen donuts for $12. Each day the bakery makes 100 dozen donuts. There is a daily waste of 10% donuts unsold. How much does the bakery earn daily?
A DVD store rents movies at $5 per DVD. If Johnny rents 4 DVDs on Monday and 3 DVDs on Tuesday, how much will he pay in total?

Ellen is a book author. In the past six months, she has written 600 pages of a novel. The novel releases a new part once every two months. The first and second parts have the same number of pages, but the third part is two pages longer than the others. How many pages was the first part of the novel?

Mike decides to buy a new camera.  He wants to buy a top-of-the-line camera but he decides to wait for the new model to come out.  The new model costs 30% more than the current model.  The old camera cost $4000.  He gets $200 off a $400 lens he bought.  How much did he pay for the camera and lens?

Sarah bought a smartphone for $650 and the protective case for it worth $25. She also bought a pair of headsets for $45 and a portable charger for $30. How much did Sarah spend in total for all of these?
A restaurant sells burgers for $5 each and hot dogs for $3 each. If Sally buys 4 burgers and 6 hot dogs for her family's picnic, how much did she spend?

Laura went to the grocery store and bought a loaf of bread, a pint of milk, and a dozen eggs. The bread cost $2, and the milk was twice as expensive. The eggs cost 80% of the total cost of the bread and milk. How much did Laura pay for her groceries?

Cindy plans to run a fundraiser bake sale. Each batch of cookies costs $5 to make and there are 10 cookies in each batch. If Cindy wants to charge $1 for each cookie, how much money will Cindy make after selling all of the cookies from 5 batches?
Sara has a collection of 20 books. She wants to divide them equally into 4 shelves. How many books will she put in each shelf?
A tree is planted each year around a park. If there were already 50 trees in 2010 and 70 trees in 2018, how many trees were planted between those years?

Emma has a rectangular backyard that is 60 meters long and 40 meters wide. She wants to build a fence around her backyard. If a meter of fencing costs $5, how much will it cost Emma to build the fence?
A quarter is worth 25 cents and a dime is worth 10 cents. How much money will Bob have if he exchanges his 5 quarters and 3 dimes?
Robert needs to buy books for his new semester. Each book costs $50 but if he buys more than 3 books at once, each book is discounted $10. If he needs 5 books, how much will he pay in total?
A worker with a monthly salary of $3000 obtained a raise of 5%. What is his new salary?

Sam spends three hours doing homework, two hours playing games, and the remaining waking hours of his day are equally divided between reading and studying. If he sleeps for 8 hours every day, how many hours does he spend studying each day?

A workshop is offered to a company for their employees. The regular workshop cost is $200 but they offer a 10% discount if they register 1 week before the scheduled workshop. The company registered 15 employees for the workshop a month before the schedule and it also released a $15 food allowance for each of the employees. How much did the company spend in all?
Tim is building a garden in his backyard. He plans to plant 4 different types of flowers in each row. If he has 12 rows, how many flowers will Tim plant?

Joe is growing tomatoes in his backyard. He collects them in buckets that can hold up to 15 tomatoes each. Each collection, half of the buckets are full, and the remaining buckets are one-quarter full. He gets an average of 10 buckets in each collection. How many tomatoes does Joe collect in an average collection?
Mike bought a house for $300,000. Over the years, the value of the house increased by 15%. Mike then sold the house. How much money did Mike get for the house?
In a class of 50 students, 5 students forgot to bring their books and half of the remaining students forgot their pens. How many students came prepared with both their books and pens?

A book series has been published over a span of 20 years. Out of those 20 years, 10 volumes had 10 chapters, 5 volumes had 15 chapters, and 5 volumes had 20 chapters. What is the average number of chapters per year?
A door has 5 wooden panels each. A building has 20 single doors on the ground floor and 30 double doors on the first floor. How many wooden panels are there in the entire building?
Samantha was watching a movie, which was aired for 2 hours. During this time, there were 4 trailers, which lasted 15 minutes each. How long (in hours) was the movie itself, not counting trailers?
Tara and Tim enjoy playing a game of chess. In one year, Tara beat Tim 45 times and Tim beat Tara 30 times. How many games in total did they play in one year?
Billy can build 2 doghouses a day, and Bob can build 3 doghouses a day. If they work together for 5 days, how many doghouses will they have built?
John and Sarah plan to paint their house. John paints two rooms in a day, while Sarah paints one room. If the house has 12 rooms, how many days will it take for them to finish painting?
Paula wants to serve 2 slices of pizza to each of her 100 party guests. Each pizza has 8 slices. How many pizzas will she need?

Jasmine has three jars of cookies. Each jar contains twice the amount of cookies as in Ana's cookie jar. If Ana's cookie jar has 22 cookies, how many cookies are there in total in Jasmine's three jars?

Heather has 3 1-hour meetings today. She spends the rest of her 10-hour workday processing loan applications. If she can process 20 loan applications an hour, how many applications can she process today?
Sue sleeps for 7 hours a night for 3 nights in a row. The next 2 nights she sleeps for 9 hours each night. How many hours of sleep does she get in total?
Paul saves $4 per day. His sister Barbara saves $2 per day. If Paul had initially $20 and Barbara had $10, after how many days will Paul and Barbara have the same amount of money?
A tailor sews 150 shirts in 5 days. If the tailor sews the same amount each day, how many shirts does the tailor sew in 10 days?

Mark leaves his house at 6:00 AM and arrives at his office by 8:00 AM. He stops at the gym for 30 minutes and at the grocery for 15 minutes during his commute. How many hours does Mark spend commuting?

Leon has nine coins in his wallet that are either quarters or dimes. A quarter worths 25 cents and a dime worths 10 cents. If he bought a $1.50 soda and still has $1, how many quarters did he initially have?
A group of children had a lemonade stand. They sold 25 glasses for $2 each and 15 glasses for $3 each. How much money did they make in total?
John collects stamps. He has 60 stamps from Asia and 3 times more stamps from Europe. If he gives away 25% of his European stamps as a gift, how many stamps would he have left in total?
A school cafeteria serves 120 students in total. 1/4 of them are vegetarian. Half of the non-vegetarian students prefer meat dishes. How many non-vegetarian students prefer meat dishes?
In a farm, there were 150 ducks and three times as many chickens. If a fox caught 25 ducks and 30 chickens, how many animals are left in the farm now?
There are 100 guests at a wedding. The dining table seats 10 people. How many times does the waitstaff need to reset the table to serve everyone?
The cake shop sells a total of 120 cupcakes and brownies in a day. If the number of cupcakes sold is four times the number of brownies sold, how many brownies did the shop sell?

A baseball team is selling tickets for their upcoming season. The tickets are priced at $25 each. They manage to sell 500 tickets. However, they could sell 750 tickets if the stadium was larger. How much potential profit does the team miss out on by not being able to sell all the tickets?
Alice collected 20 leaves from three different types of trees. She collected 8 leaves from the first tree and 5 leaves from the second tree. How many leaves did she collect from the third tree?

Dan earns $15 per day by doing chores. He is saving for a new video game console costing $300. He already has $50 saved. If he does chores 6 days a week, how many weeks should Dan work to get his new console?
Tom plants 14 apple trees that have 5 apples each. If he donates a third of the apples to a local food bank and sells half of what's left to a market, how many apples are left?
Lily and James were making bracelets. Lily made 20 bracelets and James made twice as many as Lily. They sold 7 bracelets at a fundraiser. How many bracelets do they have left?

Sharon and Dave are competing in a typing contest. Sharon can type 5,000 words an hour. Dave can type 100 words a minute. If they type for half an hour straight, how many words will they have totaled?
A printing company charges $10 for every 50 pages and $15 for every 100 pages. If a company ordered print outs of 150 pages how much will they pay in total?

Scarlett played a video game for 15 hours over the weekend. Assuming every hour of gameplay helps her advance 3 levels, how many levels would she advance if she played for 2/3 of the time she played over the weekend?

In a bake sale, there are five types of cookies: chocolate chip, peanut butter, sugar, oatmeal, and shortbread. For every 2 chocolate chip cookies, there are 3 peanut butter, 4 sugar, 6 oatmeal, and 7 shortbread cookies. If there are 10 chocolate chip cookies, how many cookies are there in total at the bake sale?
Rebecca bought 5 blue ribbons. Each blue ribbon costs $2 each. She also bought some red ribbons. The red ribbons cost $3 each and she spent $24 on them. How many red ribbons did Rebecca buy?

Sam made 2 different stacks of Lego blocks. The first stack was 6 blocks high, the second stack was 4 block(s) higher than the first. Sam’s brother knocked over the entire first tower, in the second tower he left 3 blocks standing. How many blocks in total fell down?

An art teacher ordered six boxes of modeling clay. Each box contained 12 packages, and each package contained 2 pounds of clay. If the clay costs $4 per pound, how much did the art teacher spend on modeling clay?
