Kevin plans a daily routine where he exercises for 30 minutes each day. If he wants to keep this up for one month (30 days), how many total hours will he have spent exercising by the end of the month?
Zoe wants to buy a pair of shoes priced at $75. During the Black Friday sale, the store offers 50% off on all items. How much does she have to pay for the shoes?
A farm had 50 pigs and 40 cows. At the end of the month, the farm was able to sell 3 pigs and 5 cows. How many animals were left at the farm?
Sara harvested 650 cherries for her entire family. There are 42 family members and each of them took 4 cherries. How many cherries are left?
Samantha bakes 10 cakes, each cake has 8 pieces. If Samantha sold 50 pieces, how many cakes does she have left?
If one fifth of the computers are nonfunctional, and there are 4 stacks of 50 computers each. How many computers are functional?
Sarah has 80 marbles. Jack has twice as many marbles as Sarah. If Jack gives Sarah 30 of his marbles, how many marbles does Sarah have now?
Maria bakes 40 muffins a day. Eva bakes 30 more muffins a day than Maria. How many muffins do they bake together in 5 days?
Liam bought a bicycle for $200. After a month, he decided to sell the bicycle at a 15% loss. How much did he sell the bicycle for?
John has $50. He spent $15 on a new game and $5 on a toy car. How much money does John have left?

A school is hosting a charity fair. They made 150 sandwiches to sell. During the first two hours, they sold 45% of the sandwiches. After another hour, they found out that all but 30 sandwiches were sold. How many sandwiches did they sell in the third hour?
Sarah baked 60 cookies and divided them equally among her 5 friends. How many cookies does each friend get?
Jerry is infamous for tipping only 2%. If average tippers tip around 15%, how much extra cents than what he usually tips should Jerry tip on a bill of $35 to maintain the average tipping rate?
Chris goes to the gym 4 times a week. On each visit, he spends 1.5 hours exercising, and 15 minutes showering. What percentage of his gym time does Chris spend exercising?
Jimmy has saved $300. He spends $45 on a video game and $25 on a book. How much money is left?
Alex earns $600 each month. He spends 20% of his paycheck on rent and 15% on groceries. How much money is Alex left with at the end of the month?
Out of a group of 100 people, 35% prefer coffee over tea. Out of those who prefer coffee, 60% drink it black. If this ratio holds true, how many people from a group of 500 would drink black coffee?

Jody and Frank are planning a trip for next summer. They both decide to work as dog walkers and save half of their earnings for their trip. If Jody makes $5 per day and Frank makes $10 per day, how much money will they both have saved for their trip in a year?
Lisa bought a book for $30 and she sold it for $45. Then, she bought another book for $40 and sold it for $60. How much profit did Lisa make?

Bryan drives 50 miles from his house to the beach. After spending the day at the beach, he drives to his friend’s house. The drive to his friend’s house takes 45 minutes going at 30 miles per hour. How many miles in total does Bryan travel?

Miss Adaeze is the head teacher of a class of 60 students. She wants to divide them into three groups by proficiencies. If a quarter of the class is beginner level, and half are intermediate level, how many students will be in the third group (advanced level)?
Paul has a box with 280 pieces of candy. He gives 80 pieces to his friends and eats half of the remaining pieces. How many pieces of candy does Paul have left?

Sally made fruit punch for her party. She used 9 16-oz cans of soda, 35 oz of crushed ice, and a 50 oz bottle of orange juice. If she wanted to serve 12 oz servings, how many servings of punch does she have?
Lisa is making cookies for a bake sale. She uses 2 tablespoons of sugar for each cookie. If she makes 20 cookies, how many tablespoons of sugar will she use in total?
A grocery store received 180 cans of soup. If 12 cans are packed in one box, how many boxes does the grocery store need?
Alex spends 3 hours studying Mathematics each day. If he studies 6 days a week, how many hours does he study Mathematics in one week?

Lisa went to the amusement park. She paid $25 for the ticket and decided to buy some popcorn. The popcorn were quarter the price of the ticket. How much did Lisa pay in total for the visit to the amusement park?
In a birthday party at Julie's home, a 12-slice pizza was served and everyone ate 3 slices each. If the total number of slices eaten was 12, how many friends were eating pizza with Julie?
George is having a party where he plans to serve pizzas. Each pizza can be divided into 8 slices. If he expects 32 guests and each guest will eat 2 slices of pizza, how many pizzas should he buy?

Lisa has been saving money for 4 months. In her second month, she saved $40 more than in her first month. In the fourth month, she saved three times as much as in her first month. If she has a total of $500, how much money did she save in the third month?
Tom has 4 jars, each jar contains 12 candies. He gives 2 candies to each of his 8 friends. How many candies does Tom have left?
A printer prints 12 pages in a minute. How many pages will it print in 3 hours?

Layla and John are saving up for a new bicycle that costs $200. John has already saved up $50, while Layla has saved $30. Each week, Layla saves $10 while John saves $15. How many weeks will it take for both of them to buy the bicycle together?
Tom is planting a vegetable garden. The rows are 180 feet long, and each vegetable needs its own space of 6 inches to its right. How many vegetables can Tom plant in each row?
Michael together with his seven buddies ordered 4 pizzas. Each pizza had 10 slices. If they all finished and ate the same amount of pizzas, how many slices did each person eat?
A library buys 20 new books each month. If each book costs $15, how much does the library spend on new books each year?

If Peter normally has an average heart rate of 70 beats per minute, but after drinking a caffeinated drink, his heart rate goes up by 15 beats per minute. How many beats per minute above the average maximum heart rate is Peter if the average maximum heart rate is 80 beats per minute?

A popular book "GREAT" costs $15 at the bookstore, while a less popular book "MED" costs $5. If the bookstore sold eight of "GREAT" and fifteen of "MED" last week, what is the total earnings from these two books?
Jason is reading a lengthy novel. The novel is 450 pages long and Jason reads 20 pages every day. How many days will it take for Jason to finish the novel?
Mary ran 5 miles per day for 3 days. On the fourth day, she ran twice as far. After that, she ran 4 miles for the rest of the week. How many miles did she run for one week (7 days)?

Selena baked some cookies. She packed 1/3 of them for her children's school snack. She then served 1/2 of the remaining cookies at a party. If she has 18 cookies left, how many cookies did she bake in the first place?
Mia spent 1/3 of her money and an additional $12 on clothes. She then spent 1/2 of the remaining money and an additional $5 on shoes. If she was left with $45, how much money did she have initially?

A bookstore has an inventory of 24 thrillers, 34 story books, 48 comic books, and 40 educational books. The number of horror books in the store is one-fourth of the sum of comic books and story books, and the number of biographies is three-fourths the sum of thrillers and educational books. How many more horror books than biographies are there?
Lauren can paint a house 3 times faster than Emma. Emma can paint a house in 15 hours. How many hours will it take for both Lauren and Emma to paint a house together?

A basketball team consists of three point guards and eleven forwards. The team also has half as many centers as forwards, and remaining are shooting guards. If the team has in total 30 players, how many shooting guards are in the team?

A basketball team had 15 players ready for a game. The first quarter saw 5 players start the game with 3 substitutions made. In the second quarter, the team made twice as many substitutions as it made in the first quarter. How many players on the team did not play that day?

Sophie has 96 centimeters of ribbon that she wants to use for decorating her room, but she needs to cut it into smaller pieces first. She cuts it into 16 equal length pieces, but then she realizes it's too short, so she ties two pieces together. The knots then make each piece 2 centimeters shorter. How long are her pieces of ribbon after all this?
Olivia is hosting a cookie swap and needs to bake 6 dozen cookies. If each batch makes 24 cookies, how many batches does she need to bake?
In a school, there are 6 classes with an equal number of students. If there are a total of 180 students, how many students are there in each class?
A car uses 8 gallons of gas to go 120 miles. How much gas would the car use to go 450 miles?
In a basketball team, there are 10 players and each player needs 2 pairs of socks. If each pair of socks costs $5, how much would it cost in total to buy socks for the entire team?
James goes to the gym 6 days a week, and each session lasts 1.5 hours. How many hours does James spend at the gym in a year?
Tom purchased 2 kg of grapes and 3 kg of peaches. The total cost of grapes was $20. The peaches were three times cheaper than the grapes. How much did Tom pay for 1 kg of peaches?
Sophie wants to install a floor-to-ceiling mirror in her room, leaving a 6" gap from the ceiling. If her room is 9 feet tall, how tall does the mirror need to be?

Sam's bank account has $3257. He decides to put a quarter of his money into a savings account and uses a third of the rest on a new laptop. He then spends $150 on groceries. How much money does he have left?

In a marathon, a runner covers a distance of 42 kilometers. Max decides to train for the marathon and runs at a speed of 8 kilometers per hour. If he trains 3 hours a day, how many days will it take him to cover the marathon distance in training?
Alex owns twice as many football cards as Mike does. Jake has three times as many cards as Alex does. If Jake has 90 cards, how many cards does Mike have?

In a quiz contest, each player scored 5 points for each correct answer and lost 2 points for each wrong answer. If Sophia answered 20 questions correctly and 12 questions incorrectly. What was her total score?

Libby is planning to knit a blanket. She can knit 5 rows per day. Each row requires 2 balls of yarn and each ball of yarn costs $3. How much will she spend on yarn if she plans to knit the blanket with 80 rows?
George has 5 times the amount of apples as Jack. If Jack has 10 apples, how many apples does George have?
Miggy's mom brought home 3 bags of birthday hats. Each bag has 15 hats. Miggy accidentally tore off 5 hats. During the party, only 25 hats were used. How many hats were unused?
A grocery store has 80 apples, 50 oranges, and 60 bananas. If half of each type of fruit is sold, how many fruits are left in the grocery store?
A tree has 500 leaves. If 20% fall off in the autumn, how many leaves are left on the tree?
Sophie has $250 saved up. She goes shopping and buys 2 dresses at $35 each, 4 pairs of shoes at $20 each, and 3 hats at $15 each. How much money does Sophie have left after her shopping?
A bus can hold 50 passengers. If the bus runs 8 trips a day, how many passengers can it carry in a day?
Jessica spent one fifth of her money on a book. Then she spent one fourth of the remaining amount on lunch. If she has $48 left, how much did she originally have?

Sam planted 10 tulip bulbs in his garden. After a few months, he observed that only half of those bulbs bloomed. He then planted twice as many new bulbs as he previously had. How many bulbs does Sam have in total now?
Paul has 10 toy cars. He gave 2 to his friend, then he was rewarded with 3 more toy cars by his mother for tidying his room. How many toy cars does Paul have now?
Anna bought 15 gallons of milk at $1.50/gallon. She needed to make more milkshakes for her cafe, so she bought another 12 gallons of milk at $2.00/gallon. How much did she spend on milk altogether?
Ron baked 16 cookies for his picnic. On the first day, he ate a quarter of the cookies he made. The next day he ate 3 cookies more. How many cookies does Ron have left after these two days?
A library bought 4 shelves of books, each shelf contains 30 books. However, 5 books on one of the shelves were borrowed. How many books does the library have now?

In a competition, in 2015 there were 200 participants. In 2016 the participants were 30 more than three times the number of participants in 2015. In 2017, the number of participants was 40 less than half the number of participants in 2016. How many more participants were there in 2016 than in 2017?

Arnold built 4 panels of fence for his backyard, but decided to change it. He took down the panels and built 5 new panels each of twice the length of the original. Then he decided to add 3 more panels of half the length of the new panels. If the original panels were 10 feet long, how long did his new fence become, in feet?
Sophie was 5 inches taller than Samuel. Samuel was 3 inches shorter than Stella. Stella is 70 inches tall. How many inches tall is Sophie?

John owns a bakery. He knows that in the morning he sells 15 muffins, in the afternoon he sells 20 cookies, and at night he sells a cake that sells for exactly twice as much as the muffins and cookies combined. If a muffin is $2, how much does a cookie cost if the cake costs $140?

Jamie wants to put a small flower pot at each window of his square-shaped house.  Each pot will have 2 daisies that cost $2.50 per flower, 3 tulips costing $3.00 per flower and 4 roses that cost $2.00 per flower.  How much will it cost to fill all the pots?
In 6 years, Sam will be twice Lucy's age. If Lucy is currently 7 years old, how old is Sam now?
Total Package Store has twice as many boxes as Complete Packaging Store. If Complete Packaging Store has 150 boxes, how many boxes do they have combined?
In Anna's bakery, a group of 8 adults and 4 children ordered pastries and coffee. If each pastry cost $3 and each coffee cost $2, how much was the total bill?
Ben started a small business with an initial investment of $5000. He had earnings of $200 per week for 8 weeks. If his monthly expenses were $450, how much money did he have after 2 months?
In a football team, there are 11 players on the field. If there are 4 substitutes waiting, how many players are there in total?
Archie ran 3 miles. Betty ran 760 yards and Jughead ran 1090 feet. How many combined feet did everyone run?

A hiker found a water source in the wilderness. He drank 60 ounces of water. His dog drank four times as much as he did. There are 128 ounces in a gallon. How many gallons of water did they drink altogether?

Sarah and her three friends throw a picnic and split the cost equally. They buy 20kg of food that costs $2 per kg. They also buy $40 worth of drinks. Sarah spends an additional $20 for cutlery. How much did Sarah spend in total?
Benjamin waters his garden for 2 hours every day. After 15 days, how many liters of water will he have used if he uses 30 liters each hour?
Mike borrowed $1500 at 5% interest per annum compounded annually. How much will he pay total if he returns the money after 3 years?
A train travels at a speed of 120 km/h. How far will it travel in 3.5 hours?
Amy has a stack of 150 books. She needs to distribute them to 25 libraries. After giving each library 6 books, she decided to take back 2 books from each. How many books does each library have now?
If a car travels at a speed of 60 miles per hour, how many miles will it travel in 4 hours?
Samuel has a collection of 16 red toy cars and 22 blue toy cars. He then bought 3 more red toy cars and 4 packets of toy cars, each containing 5 cars. How many toy cars does Samuel have overall?

A family decided to plant a garden. They divided the garden area into 3 zones. They sowed 2000 carrot seeds in each planting zone. If the success rate of sprouting is 75%, how many carrot plants do they expect to grow?

Ben works as a cake decorator in a bakery. He needs to decorate 60 cupcakes for a party, and he can decorate 5 cupcakes in 20 minutes. If Ben takes a 15 minute break after every hour of work, how many hours will it take him to finish decorating all the cupcakes?
Jenny and Mark decided to sell lemonade. Jenny made 12 cups and Mark made twice as many. How many cups of lemonade do they have in total?
John wants to buy a new laptop that costs $1200. He has saved $300 so far. Each week, he saves an additional $50. How many weeks he needs to save to afford the laptop?
Sarah has $50. She wants to buy a necklace that costs $15 each but she needs to save at least $20 for her next week's allowance. How many necklaces can Sarah afford to buy?
A baker used 5 bags of flour for making cookies. Each bag of flour can make 30 cookies. How many cookies did the baker make?

A dad ordered 120 pizzas for his son's birthday party. Two-thirds of them are vegetarian. There are 30 gluten-free pizzas and half of them are also vegetarian.  How many pizzas contain meat and gluten?
Liam and Noah are saving money to buy a gift. Liam saved $50, while Noah saved twice the amount Liam did. How much did they save in total?
In a gym, 10 people are lifting weights and twice as many are using the treadmills. If there's a limit of 5 people per group for a scheduled conditioning workout, how many groups can be formed?
Benjamin spent 3 hours reading a novel and 1.5 hours reading a newspaper. The novel has 300 pages and the newspaper has 50 pages. How long does it take for Benjamin to read one page?
Kevin plays his favorite video game for 2 hours every day. How many minutes has he played at the end of 1 week?
A bakery sold 55 muffins in the morning and 37 more muffins in the afternoon than in the morning. How many muffins did they sell in total?
Mike decides to start a vegetable garden. He pays $200 for a garden bed. He then hires a gardener for 10 lessons at $60 per lesson but gets a 50% discount. How much did the whole setup cost?
A movie theater ticket costs $12. If the price goes down 15% on Tuesdays, how much would a movie ticket cost on Tuesday?

Anna and Sarah went to the mall to buy dresses. They both spent the same amount. Anna bought two t-shirts for $30 each and a dress for $120. Sarah bought a jacket for $90 and three handbags. How much did one handbag cost?
In a class, there are 11 boys and 12 girls. The teacher wants to form groups of 3 students. If each group should have at least 1 boy, how many groups can be created?
Billy has a stack of 15 comic books. He buys 5 more, then reads and gives away 5. How many comic books does he have left?
Daisy went to a stationery store. She bought 8 notebooks costing $3 each, 3 pens costing $0.5 each, and 4 boxes of pencils, with each box costing $2. What is the total amount that Daisy paid?
The average score for 9th graders is 85, for the 10th graders is 3 more than the 9th graders and the 11th graders average score is 87.  What is the average score for the school?
Jane read 4 books a month. If each book has an average of 250 pages, how many pages does Jane read in two months?

In an auditorium of 60 people, one-third are wearing hats while the rest are not. If there are 10 more people not wearing hats than those wearing hats, how many more people are wearing hats than those not wearing hats?

Carlos spent half of his savings on a new laptop. He then spent half of what was left on a concert ticket. Finally, he spent half of what remained on a gift. If he has $25 left, how much was his total savings?

What is the average age of the oldest and youngest cat if the youngest cat is 2 years old, the 2nd youngest cat is 1 year older than the youngest cat, the middle cat is 3 years older than the 2nd youngest cat, the 2nd oldest cat is double the age of the middle cat, and the oldest cat is 4 years older than the 2nd oldest cat?

A car uses 5 gallons of gas for a 10 miles trip. Samantha needs to commute a total distance of 50 miles to work and back. How many gallons of gas will Samantha need for two weeks if she works five days a week?

Jane baked 240 gingerbread cookies for a charity event. She adds some icing decor to the cookies and finds that she can decorate 15% more cookies during the afternoon. How many cookies did she decorate in total?

Ms. Ellie is baking cupcakes for her son's birthday party. She makes 2 batches of cupcakes and each batch makes 24 cupcakes. In the first batch, 1/4 of the cupcakes are vanilla. In the second batch, 1/3 of the cupcakes are vanilla. How many vanilla cupcakes did Ms. Ellie bake total?
Jenny starts with 120 pages to read for her book club. She reads 30 pages in the morning, 20 pages in the afternoon, and 40 pages at night. How many pages does she have left to read?

Emma spends an average of $50 on food every week. Her rent is twice that amount and she also needs to account for her car maintenance which costs her $75 each month. If she gets paid every 4 weeks, how much does she spend on these three expenses?
Each package has 250 stickers. If Sarah puts these stickers into 5 massive albums, and each album needs 8 packages, how many stickers will these 5 albums hold?
An office consumes 5 packs of coffee per week. How many packs of coffee would the office consume in a year?

Allen buys 3 large pizzas for a party, each cut into 8 slices. Allen eats 2 slices, his friend Ryan eats a quarter of the pizzas, and their friend Olivia eats 3 slices. How many total slices are left over?

A young engineer wants to assemble a bicycle. He needs 3 pieces for the frame, 2 tires, 1 saddle, 2 handles, 1 bell, 1 pair of pedals and 2 brakes. If the pieces come in pairs, how many pairs does he need to buy?
Alice has $500 in her savings account. She buys a jacket that costs $90, a bracelet for $45 and a book that costs $35. How much money does Alice have left in her account?
Steve spent 1/5 of his money and now has $75 left. How much did he have in the beginning?

Jill has 260 books. She decided to only keep 20 books and then gave some books to Blake. Then she saw 10 friends and decided to give them 15 books each, and the remaining books were given equally to her 3 cousins. If each cousin got 4 books, how many books did Jill give to Blake?
I purchased a laptop for $950 excluding tax. What is the cost of the laptop when considering 10% of the sales tax?
Julia's dad is currenlty twice the age of Julia who is 22 years old. After how many years will Julia's dad be three times as old as Julia?

A group of friends decided to go to the cinema. John bought 3 adult tickets and 2 children's tickets for $46. Emma bought 2 adult tickets and 3 children's tickets for $48. If the price of an adult ticket is $10, how much is a child's ticket?
John has a bucket that can hold 5 liters of water. He uses it to fill up his 25 liter tank. How many times does he have to refill the bucket to fill up the tank?
From a total batch of 120 cupcakes, a bakery sells 70% of them in the morning. How many cupcakes are left over for sale in the afternoon?

A box of cookies has 10 cookies, with each cookie having 200 calories. A box of chocolate bars contains 8 chocolate bars, with each chocolate bar having 250 calories. How many more calories does the box of chocolate bars have?
Samantha is 4 years older than her sister Ella. If Samantha is currently 32 years old, how old will Ella be in 5 years?
Alex wants to buy 10 books. Each book costs $15 but since he is a member of the bookstore, he gets a 10% discount on his purchase. How much will Alex pay for the books?
Chris is driving to a city 300 miles away. He drives half the distance the first day, and the second day he covers the remaining distance. How many miles does Chris drive the second day?
Stella has twice as many posters as Emma. If Emma has 12 posters, and Stella buys additional 16 posters, how many posters will Stella have?

Sarah wants to fill her fish tank with saltwater. The tank is 5 feet tall and has a square base of 3 feet by 3 feet. A liter of saltwater costs $4. If a cubic foot of water is equal to 28 liters, how much will it cost to fill the tank?
The football team players raised $500 total from selling popcorn. They want to buy new equipment. If a football costs $20 each and they want to buy 15 footballs, what is the amount left?
Jenna has a tortoise pet. It walks 5 feet per minute. Jenna has an outside path that is 2.5 miles long. If the tortoise walked the entire path without stopping, how many hours would it take?

John bought a new house and decided to paint all rooms. He painted the living room with 5 cans of paint and the kitchen with double the amount of cans used in the living room. For the two bedrooms, he used three times the amount he used in the kitchen. How many cans of paint did John use in total?
A school has 80 students. Each student pays a school fee of $50. Half of this school fee will be used to buy books for students. How much will the school spend to buy books?
Ron borrowed $5000 to buy new furniture. He has to pay a 7% interest every year. How much does he have to pay back after 3 years?

A group of children decide to sell lemonade in the park to earn some money. They decide to sell a glass of lemonade for $0.45. On the first day, they sell 70 glasses. If they continue at this pace, how much will they earn after four days?

Sarah and John started biking at the same time for 20 miles. Then Sarah biked an extra 15 miles and John an extra 5 miles. If the total distance they have to bike is 100 miles, how many miles do Sarah and John have to bike to reach the end?
If Bob is five years older than Andy and Andy is eight years older than Mike, and the sum of their ages is 64. How old is Mike?
A car travels at a speed of 60 miles per hour. How far does the car travel in 2 and a half hours?
Johnny walked 5 kilometers on the first day, and every subsequent day, he walked 1.5 times further than the previous day. On which day did he walk more than 25 kilometers?
Emma plans to bake a cake for each of her 10 friends. Each cake requires 3/5 pound of flour. If a bag of flour costs $12 and weighs 5 pounds, how much will Emma spend on flour?
Tom has twice as many comic books as Jerry. If Jerry has 60 comic books, how many comic books do Tom and Jerry have in total?
Linda participates in a skipping competition. After rigorous practice, she triples her skipping rate which was originally 50 skips per minute. How many total skips does she make in 10 minutes?

A dance school charges $20 per lesson for ballet classes and $30 per session for hip-hop classes. If a student decides to take 5 classes of ballet and 3 sessions of hip-hop per week for the next 8 weeks, how much money will they spend?
Mr. Johnson purchased 4 books for $4 each. He also purchased a pencil case. In total, Mr. Johnson spent $22. How much did the pencil case cost?
David is three times as old as his nephew, Brian. In six more years, Brian will be 21 years old. How old will David be in six years?
An office has 6 rooms. Each room has 4 light bulbs and each bulb costs $2.50 to replace. How much would it cost to replace all the light bulbs in the office?

At an animal shelter, there are 9 puppies, 12 kittens, 7 bunnies and 4 hamsters. The next day 3 additional puppies, 2 additional kittens and 5 additional bunnies arrive at the shelter. How many total animals are there in the shelter now?
There are 5 houses on Bella's street and each house has 3 windows on the front. Bella's house, however, has twice as many windows. How many windows are there in total on Bella's street?
Emma invited 20 people for dinner. 15% of them didn't show up. 60% of those who attended chose fish and the rest chose meat. How many people chose meat?

In the Brown family, Tom uses 4 hand towels, their three kids use a total of 9 hand towels, and his wife uses 2 hand towels in one week. If the washing machine can hold 5 hand towels per load, how many loads of laundry will the Browns need to do for their hand towels?

In Ryan's collection of books, he has 4 more crime novels than sci-fi novels but three times as many sci-fi novels as romance novels. If he has 25 crime novels, how many romance novels are there in his collection?

Carla is awake for 3/4 of the day and spends 1/3 her time awake reading books and the rest on other activities. Her sister, Anna, is awake for 2/3 of the day and spends 1/4 of her day reading and the remainder on other activities. How much time, on average, do they spend reading?

Amy has a goal to read 45 chapters of a book in a month. So far, she has read 8 chapters in the first week and 11 chapters in the second week. How many more chapters does Amy need to read to reach her goal?
Natalie goes to a clothing store where there's a 60% off sale on all items. If she buys a dress that's originally priced at $75, how much would she pay for it?
Joey brought $50 to the fair. He spent $15 on ride tickets, $10 on food, and $18 on a game. How much money does he have left?

A caterer was hired to prepare meals for the 210 guests at a wedding. She was about to prepare 3 servings per person when she found out that there were 50 vegetarians amongst them. These vegetarians refused to eat meat, so the caterer decides to make 2 vegetarian servings for each vegetarian. How many servings should she prepare in total?

The number of rhinos at Preservation Zoo is 4 times as many as the number of rhinos at Grand Wildlife Park. If there are 80 rhinos at Grand Wildlife Park, calculate the total number of rhinos in the two parks.
Sarah went to a book store with $100. She bought 5 books for $12 each. Then she saw another book for $20 that she wanted to buy. How much money does Sarah have left after buying all the books?

Paul runs a bakery and he wants to prepare for a large order. He makes 4 loaves of bread and 5 dozen cookies per day. Each loaf of bread requires 2 pounds of flour and a dozen cookies needs 3 pounds. How many pounds of flour does Paul need in a week?
In a pet shop, there are two kinds of animals: cats and dogs. Each cat costs $20 and each dog costs $30. If the shop sells 7 dogs and 13 cats in a day, how much total money does the shop earn?
A comic book costs $5 each. Liam bought 8 comic books to be sold each at $6.50. What will be Liam's total profit from selling these?
Sam has 20 pictures on his wall, and his sister Lisa has 3 times as many. Their cousin Amy has 30 pictures less than Lisa. How many pictures do they have all together?

Dayna made a batch of cupcakes. She ate 2 of them and gave her brother 4. She then offered the cupcakes to her soccer teammates, with the first taking 2, the second taking 4, the third taking 6, and so on. By the time the last teammate took her cupcakes, the tray was empty. How many cupcakes did Dayna make initially?
An elevator is capable of carrying 15 people at once. There are total 60 people who want to use the elevator. How many trips will the elevator have to make to transport all 60 people?
A gardener planted 20 rows of sunflowers with 15 plants in each row. Each sunflower yields 30 seeds. How many seeds can the gardener get from all his planted sunflowers?
4 towns received a donation of books. Town A received 200 books, Town B 150, and Town C 100 books. The total donation was 500 books. How many books did Town D receive?

A school bought 5 packets of cookies for a bake sale. Each packet contains 30 cookies. After the first hour, 65 cookies were sold. After the second hour, only 25 cookies were left. How many cookies were sold in the second hour?
A football team needs 11 players to start a match. If 3 players are injured, how many substitutes does the coach need to have in order not to forfeit the game?
Karl drove an average of 450 miles for 5 days. Savannah drove a total of 3,500 miles in two weeks. How many miles farther did Savannah drive?
A bookshop had 140 books in stock. Today, it sold 15% of the books and received a shipment of 40 new books. How many books are now in stock at the bookshop?

Richard has 2 hours to finish his homework. His physics homework takes him 30 minutes. His geography homework takes 40 minutes. His chemistry homework takes 35 minutes. He also needs to review for an important test the next day. How much time does he have left for review?

Bob harvested 30 pounds of apples. He used 1/3 for apple pie, 1/6 for apple sauce, and kept the rest for fresh eating. If it takes 6 pounds of apples to make one apple pie and 2 pounds to make 1 pound of apple sauce. If Bob sells apple pie for $10/pie, apple sauce for $3/pound, and fresh apples for $1/pound, how much money does he make?
Jessica reads 4 books a month. If she decides to double the current number of books she reads per month, how many books will she have read by the end of the year?

During a baseball game, Team C scored 5 runs in the first inning while Team D scored double that amount. In the second inning, Team C scored the same amount they did in the first inning and Team D scored 3 less than in the first inning. How many runs did both teams score in total?
Liam has 100 new baseball cards. He sorts them out into shiny and regular ones. If he has 60 shiny ones and 30 regular ones, how many card does he have that are neither shiny nor regular?

A pet shop has 6 dogs that each requires 2 hours of care daily. The pet shop has 3 care assistants and they want to split the care hours equally. How many hours will each assistant spend caring for the dogs?
Linda has 30 apples. She gave 15% of the apples to her friend and she ate 25% of the remaining apples. How many apples does Linda have now?
Tim is baking a cupcakes and needs 4 eggs for the recipe. He currently has 13 eggs. How many more cupcakes can he bake with the eggs he has left?

Ben purchased 15 comic books and spent a total of 5 dollars. He decided to give three-fifths of the books to his brother, Tim, and Tim insisted on paying Ben for his share. How much did Tim pay to Ben?
If Meg has $45 more, she would have $120. If Bob had $20 less, he would have the same money as Meg has. How much money do Meg and Bob have altogether?
Laura buys 4 novels a month and each novel cost 5 dollars. How much does she spend a year on novels?
A movie theater has 12 rows of seats. Each row has 25 seats. If the theater is sold out, how many people are in the theater?
Benny has a jar of 50 marbles. He gives away 30% of his marbles to his friend Jamal and loses 25% of the remaining marbles. How many marbles does Benny have left?
Jenna read twice as many books this month as her brother Jake, who read 5 less than their sister Jane. If Jane read 10 books, how many books did Jenna read?
A pizza shop sells 10 pizzas per hour for 5 hours, 15 pizzas per hour for the next 3 hours and then 5 pizzas per hour for another 4 hours. How many pizzas in total does the shop sell in a day?

Janet is a very busy businesswoman who often works late into the night. She is counting how many cups of coffee she drinks in a week. If she has 3 cups of coffee per day from Monday to Friday and 1 cup on each of Saturday and Sunday, how many cups of coffee does Janet have in a week?

Linda needs to wash her dishes for 3 minutes each to get rid of food stains and 5 additional minutes for each oil stain. If her dishes have 4 food stains and 2 oil stains, how long does she need to wash them?

Samantha and her mother are painting the interior walls of the house. Samantha can paint 2 walls in 3 hours while her mother can paint 3 walls in 4 hours. How many walls can they paint together in 6 hours?

Georgia has a garden with 220 roses. She decided to expand her garden and plant 25% more roses. After three months, she sold half of the roses at $15 each. How much money does she make from selling roses?
In a 2-hour movie, Lily watched the first 30 minutes, then took a 15-minute break. How long did she spend watching the rest of the movie?

A quantity surveyor is figuring the construction costs for a couple that wishes to build a house. The costs are as follows: land costs $50 per square meter, bricks cost $100 per 1000 bricks and roof tiles cost $10 per roof tile. If the house they wish to build requires 2000 square meters, 10000 bricks, and 500 roof tiles, how much construction costs are required for this project?

Jane went to a boutique to buy a dress and a pair of shoes. The dress was priced at $120, and the shoes were priced at $85. If Jane received a 10% discount for the dress and a 15% discount for the shoes, how much did Jane pay in total?
Alex drops a stone off the edge of a 150-foot tall cliff. The stone returns to three quarters of the height from which it fell on each bounce. How high does it bounce on the third bounce?

Natalie is learning three new songs on the piano. She spends sixty minutes learning the first song. The second song is easier, and she learns it in half the time. The third song is more challenging, so it takes her as long as the other two songs to learn. How many minutes did she spend learning the three songs?
A baker needs 3 bags of flour to make a cake. He has to bake 50 cakes for a wedding. If one bag of flour costs $2, how much will he spend on flour?
In her recipe, May mixes 2/3rd of 15 ounces of milk with 3/4th of 12 ounces of cream. How many ounces of the mixture does she make?

Lucy and Jack went paddleboarding in the Stardust Lake, which is a half-mile circuit. Lucy managed to circle the lake 8 times in two-thirds of an hour, but Jack's paddleboard was leaking, and he only moved at an average speed two-thirds of what Lucy did.  What speed did Jack's paddleboard average, in miles per hour?
Brad has a garden for 5 plants by 6 plants. Each plant gives 7 tomatoes. He sells each tomato for $1. How much money does he make, in dollars?

Sarah buys jugs of almond milk that have 8 servings each. She drinks one serving a day. Almond milk is currently selling at $2.50 per jug. How much will it cost her to buy enough milk to last for a month (30 days)?
Annie bakes 7 cookies for each of her 12 friends. How many cookies does she bake in total?

Oliver wants to buy action figures that cost $6 each. He saved up $60 from his allowance. If he also wants to buy a comic book that costs $12, how many action figures can he buy with his remaining savings?

An artist uses an average of 15 brushes to complete a painting. She expects to complete 7 paintings for an upcoming exhibit. If she already has 32 brushes, how many more brushes does she need to buy?
A school building has 16 classrooms and a third of them are free. The occupied classrooms are all filled with students. If each classroom has 20 students, how many students are in the school?
Craig catches 18 fish during his fishing trip. His friend Tom catches 5 more than Craig. However, Tom accidentally let half of his fish escape back to the lake. How many fish does Tom have left?

Mary found 500 seashells at the beach. 60% of them are white, 35% are brown and the rest are pink. How many pink seashells did Mary find?
A farm has 80 chickens. If they lay 3 eggs each on average daily, how many eggs will they lay in a week?
If it takes 5 minutes for one printer to print 25 pages, how long would it take to print 125 pages?
A tire company produces 150 tires every hour. If they operate for 8 hours a day, how many tires do they produce in a day?
In a garden, there are twice as many roses as tulips, and half as many daisies as roses. If there are 300 flowers in total, how many daisies does the garden have?
Martha bought 3 cartons of milk. Each carton contained 2 liters of milk. How many liters of milk did Martha buy in total?

Frank picked 60 strawberries. He gave 5 to his brother, 10 to his sister, 3 to his mother, and 2 to his cat. Then, he divided the remaining strawberries equally among his 4 friends. How many strawberries did each friend get?
Susan bought a new wardrobe for $300 and a chair for $60. After this she saw that she only had $40 left. How much money did she initially have?

Ben purchased a new bicycle for $500 but received a 10% discount. With the bike, he participated in a race where he won a prize of $1000. His coach gave him 80% of the prize. How much money does Ben have from this event?

Madison has decided to ride her bike to work as an environment-friendly initiative. She calculated that by doing this, she would reduce her carbon emissions by 20 pounds per week. If she does this for a year, how much Carbon Dioxide emissions would she prevent?
John wants to buy 5 comic books that cost $15 each. He also wants to buy a toy that costs twice as much as all the comic books combined. How much would he need to buy everything?

Aileen has to produce 450 hand sewn buttons for the dressmaker by the end of the week. On Monday to Wednesday, Aileen produces 25 buttons per day. On Thursday, she doubles her production. On Friday, she triples it compared to Monday to Wednesday's rate. Finally, she produces the remaining needed buttons on Saturday. How many buttons does she produce on Saturday?
A 5-gallon bucket is filled with water. If a gallon of water weighs about 8.34 pounds, how many pounds does the full bucket of water weigh?

Peter spent 1/4 of his allowance on a movie ticket. The ticket cost him 15$. How much is his whole allowance?

A train travels 500 miles in 5 hours. What is the average speed of the train?

A storage facility charges $25 for the first day and $10 for each subsequent day. If John rented a storage unit for 7 days, how much did he pay int total?
Rose is planning a big party and wants to bake cookies. Each batch of cookies requires 1/4 pound of flour. If she has 5 pounds, how many batches can she bake?
Jerry has 100 marbles, he gives 15 to his friend, Alice. Jerry gives another 25 marbles to his another friend, Bob. How many marbles does Jerry have now?
Molly's stack of books is twice as tall as Robert's stack. The total height of Molly's books is 12, 8, 6, and 4 inches respectively. How tall is Robert's stack of books?

In a basketball match, Robertson scored 10 points, Westbrook scored thrice the points as Robertson, Durant scored 11, Harden scored half the points as Durant. How many points does the team have in total?

A team of photographers is tasked to cover an event. Each photographer can take 200 photos per hour. If the event is 12 hours long and the team has 5 photographers, how many photos are taken during the event?
Every day, Jennifer cycles 8 kilometers. How many kilometers does she cycle in 4 months when each month is assumed to have 30 days?
Sarah is creating a collection of doll clothes. It takes her 30 minutes to sew a dress and 90 minutes to sew a coat. If she sews 5 dresses and 3 coats, how long does she spend sewing in total?

Jeff found a treasure chest containing $150. His brother gave him $100 from his savings and his parents gifted him $250 for his birthday. If he saved 1/2 of this total money, how much money did he save?

As chefs, Tom, Jerry, and Bob need to bake a lot of pastries. Tom baked 15 pastries, Jerry baked 2 times more than Tom, and Bob baked half the number that Jerry was able to. How many pastries have they baked in total?

Karen has a monthly salary of $2500. She saves 20% of her salary every month. How much money will she save in a year?
Samantha can bake a batch of cookies in 1.5 hours and a pie in 2 hours. How long does it take for Samantha to bake 8 batches of cookies and 5 pies?

He had to use a different type of pasta, which makes the other ingredients half as potent. How many cherry tomatoes should Tim add to a pasta dish to get the same taste as before?

One day, Lisa sold 50 loaves of white bread, 30 loaves of wheat bread, and 20 loaves of rye bread. How much total income did Lisa make for that day?

Amy earns money every summer by cleaning houses. She wants to buy a new dress, which costs $120. Last summer she earned $70. If she earns $10 from cleaning a small house and $20 from a large house. She cleaned five houses this month. Assuming she cleaned equal numbers of both sizes, how much more does she need to save before she can buy the dress?
Sally had 14 fries at lunch. Her friend Mark gave her one-third of his 36 fries at lunch. How many fries did Sally have for lunch after that?
There are 15 boys and 10 girls in a class. If 1/3 of the boys and 1/2 of the girls are absent today, how many students are present?
A garden has both roses and tulips. The ratio of roses to tulips is 4:3. If there are 21 tulips in the garden, how many roses are there?
Jacob walks his dog twice a day. Each walk is 1.5 miles long. How many miles does Jacob walk in a week?
If Dan does 10 sit-ups a day, and he aims to reach 300 sit-ups total for the month, how many days must Dan perform his exercise?
Edna bought a box of chocolates for $15. She also bought roses for $25 and a gift for $45. If she received a discount of $10 on the total purchase, how much money did she pay in total?
Tom has an insect collection. He has 300 butterflies and 7 times as many beetles. How many insects does Tom have in total?
Tim needs to buy a new video game costing $60. He saved $10 each week from his allowance. In addition, his parents gave him $15. How many weeks will Tim need to save in order to buy the game?

Mathilda needs to buy fabric for her DIY project. She calculated that she needs 3 meters for curtains and 5 meters for throw pillows. If one meter of fabric costs $8, how much will she spend for the fabric she needs?

Harmony buys 4 birthday cakes. Each cake can be sliced into 16 pieces. The total cost of the cakes is $100. How much does 6 slices of cake cost?
In a high school of 800 students, 30% rated their cafeteria food as Great; 50% rated Good; the rest rated Poor. How many students rated the cafeteria food as Poor?
Ava needs to buy craft supplies for her art project. She buys 5 paint bottles for $3 each, 3 brushes for $2 each and 2 canvases for $10 each. How much does she spend in total?
A bag contains 45 green marbles and twice as many red marbles. If Tim removes one third of the red marbles from the bag, how many red marbles are left?
A high school band has 45 members. There are 12 flautists, 10 clarinet players, 6 drummers and the rest are trumpet players. How many trumpets players are there in the band?
Joe wants to build a rectangular garden in his backyard. He has 24 meters of fence to surround the garden. If the width of the garden is 6 meters, what will the length of the garden be?

Bernard runs a bakery. He bakes 100 loaves of bread in a day. At the end of the day, 30 loaves are left unsold. The next day, Bernard bakes half the number of remaining loaves. How many loaves does Bernard have now?
A coach needs to prepare 130 water bottles for his team's marathon practice. If he managed to prepare 17 bottles every hour, how many hours does it need to finish his task?

A boutique sold 120 ties in a day. The ratio of plastic ties to fabric ties sold was 5:3. How many were the fabric ties that the boutique sold?
Peter bought 7 comic books, each costing $45. He gave the cashier a $500 bill. How much change did Peter receive?

A group of kids owns a total of 150 candies. If we know that Joe has 10 candies less than Susan, and that Susan has 45 candies, how many candies does Joe have?

Jane usually bakes 4 apple pies, 6 cupcakes, and 10 chocolate cakes regularly for a week. For this week she was able to bake triple the number of apple pies, cupcakes, and chocolate cakes. How much more cakes was Jane able to bake for this week?

Phil and Dina are hiking a trail that is 240 miles long. Phil can hike 30 miles a day while Dina can hike 20 miles a day. They plan to take 4 rest days during the hike. How many days will they need to finish the trail?
Beth can read a book 80% faster than her sister. If her sister takes 10 hours to read a book, how long would it take Beth to read 2 books?
After Sarah’s piano recital, she received 1 dozen roses from her grandparents, and 3 roses from each of her 5 cousins.  How many roses did Sarah receive in total?
Emma, who weighs 45 pounds, wants to ride on the carousel with her cousin Lily, who weighs 30 pounds. How many 3-pound toys does Emma need to hold to make their weights equal?

Rosa wants to build a rectangular flower bed in her garden. The width of the flower bed will be 8 feet. She wants the length to be twice the width. How much fencing will she need to enclose the flower bed?
Sandy wants to buy a skirt which costs $45. She saves $5 every week. How many weeks does she need to save the money?
A team of 5 players scored 480 points in a game. The highest scorer scored twice as many as the lowest scorer. If the lowest scorer scored 60 points, how many points did the four other players score?
Anna takes 3 classes at university. Each class meets twice a week. If Anna doesn't miss any classes for 4 weeks, how many classes does she attend in total?
Adam is four times as old as Mary. Alice is half times as old as Mary. If Mary is 20 years old, what is the combined age of the three?
A karaoke bar charges $20 for the first hour and $5 for each additional 15 minutes. If John spent $50, how much time did he spend at the karaoke bar?
A factory produces bolts. Every bolt has a weight of 0.035 kg. If the factory produces 1500 bolts every day, how many kilograms of bolts does the factory produce in a month of 30 days?
Katya sold 8 glasses of lemonade and Ricky sold 9 glasses of lemonade. If Tina sold twice as many as Katya and Ricky's combined sales, how many more glasses of lemonade than Katya did Tina sell?
Kaia has 7 cats, and each cat has 4 kittens. If each kitten also has 2 toys, how many toys are there in total?
A third of all dogs in the city are brown. A half of the brown dogs in the city are small. If there are 600 dogs in the city, how many dogs are brown and small?

James loves reading books. He read 5 books in January, 3 books more in February than in January, and twice as many in March as in February. How many books did James read in the first quarter of the year?
Jim's age is 3 times the age of his son. If the son is 8 years old, how old is Jim?
A plumber charges $50 for the call-out and then $25 per hour of work. If a job takes 7 hours, how much will the total cost be?

Carol stacked 5 piles of books for library work. The first pile had 8 books. The second pile had the same amount as the first. The third pile had 3 books more than the first pile, the fourth pile had 7 books less than the first pile, and the fifth pile had 10 books more than the first pile. How many books are there in total?
Jake is preparing sandwiches for a picnic. Each sandwich requires 2 slices of cheese and serves 1 person. If 30 people are going to the picnic, how many slices of cheese does he need?

Steven is practicing the guitar to perform in a concert. He practiced for 4 hours every day for a month (30 days). If he continues to practice for the next 15 days, how many hours will he spend on practice in total?
A small business had total expenses of $150. If the total income from services offered was 1/2 of the total expenses, calculate the loss the business incurred.
Carlos wants to make bouquets for his friend's wedding. It will take 3 dozen roses to make 1 bouquet. If he wants to make 5 bouquets, how many roses must he pick from his garden?
Bill enjoys eating chocolate. He ate 7 chocolate bars on Wednesday, three times more on Thursday, and 25% less on Friday than on Thursday. How many chocolate bars did Bill eat over these three days?
Kelly went shopping and saw a dress that costs $120. The store offered 15% student discount. How much did Kelly pay for the dress?
Mitch has 7 antique coins from his grandfather. His friend Sarah has 4 coins fewer than him. If an antique coin is worth $1500, how much are all their coins worth?

Maria gathered 50 strawberries. She ate one-fourth of the strawberries and then gave three-fourths of what was left to her neighbor. If her neighbor made jam out of the strawberries, with 5 strawberries required for a jar of jam, how many jars of jam could her neighbor make?
Bobby was given $10 for mowing the grass. He decided to bank half his money and spend the rest on comic books. If each comic book cost $1.50, how many comic books was he able to buy?
A plane left the airport carrying 300 kg of cargo. After one hour, 3/5 of the cargo had been dropped off. How much cargo was remaining in the plane?
A zoo has 4 penguins per enclosure. There are 12 enclosures in total. How many more penguins are in the zoo with 15 enclosures than the zoo with 12 enclosures?
A baker makes 50 loaves of bread every day. Each loaf is sold for $2.00. If all the loaves are sold each day, how much does the baker make in 30 days?

Kyle has baked 3 batches of cookies. The first batch has 12 cookies, the second batch has 15 cookies and the third batch has 10 cookies. If he has given 2 cookies from each batch to his friends, how many cookies does he have left?

On the first day of the camping trip, Sam hiked half the distance to the campsite by walking for 10 hours at a speed of 5 miles per hour. Due to a sudden rainstorm, he had to stop and stayed in the same place. After the storm, Sam realized that he was now only one-fourth of the way to the campsite. How many miles had he been blown in the wrong direction by the storm?
Penelope has $180 to buy her art materials for the week. She spends a third of that on paints, and she spends half of what is left on brushes. What is the amount of money Penelope has left?

A bakery produces 1000 loaves of bread each day. Due to a malfunction, the bakery only produced 60% of its usual production for 3 days. If the bakery is now running normally, how many loaves were produced in 7 days?
Jessica and John collect stamps. Jessica has 100 stamps. John has 50 more stamps than Jessica. How many stamps do Jessica and John have together?
Sarah has 5 friends and each of them had 4 candies. Sarah also received 10 candies from her mom. How many candies does Sarah have?
Lisa and Mark are doing a 1000-piece jigsaw puzzle. Lisa has completed 45% of the puzzle and Mark has completed 20%. How many pieces are still not done?
A factory produced 100 tables. For every table, there were 3 chairs produced. How many pieces of furniture were produced by the factory?
Thomas drives 25 miles each way to work for 5 days a week. His car gets 20 miles per gallon. How many gallons of gas does he use a month considering he works 4 weeks in a month?
Josh can type 400 words in 5 minutes. He has a friend, Amanda, who types 4 times faster than he does. How many words can Amanda type in 15 minutes?
James has to pack 5 sets of gifts. Each set needs 2 yards of wrapping paper. If Sandra lets him use her 12-yard long roll of wrapping paper, how many yards of wrapping paper will be left?

A childcare center needs to prepare packed lunches for a trip. Each child requires 3 sandwiches, and will travel in a group with 4 other children. There are a total of 6 groups. How many total slices of bread will the childcare center need to make enough sandwiches for all the children?
Larry is four years younger than Paul. In three years, Paul will be 25 years old. How old is Larry now?
A shop sells pencils for $2 each. If Sam buys 3 pencils and a notebook for $5, how much does Sam spend on his purchases?

Mike is planning to travel to his vacation home for 10 days. Due to the distance, his car will consume 6 gallons of gas per day. If the price of gas is $4 per gallon, how much will Mike spend on gas during his trip?
In a park, there are 9 squirrels for every 3 birds. If there are 45 squirrels, how many birds are there in the park?
A company is organizing a conference. They have 8 speakers, each of whom talks for 30 minutes. They also have a 15-minute coffee break and a 1-hour lunch break. How long is the conference?
Nancy bought 12 packs of pens with each pack containing 10 pens. She gave 2 of her friends 5 pens each. How many pens does Nancy have left?
Sandra travels 500 miles in a week. Oil change is required after every 2000 miles. If oil change costs $30, and she gets a free oil change twice a year, how much does she pay in a year?
Mike has 45 stickers. He distributes them equally into 5 albums. He then gives one album to his sister. How many stickers does Mike have left?
An apple orchard produced 120 baskets of apples in September and 240 baskets of apples in October. Each basket contains 25 apples. How many apples did the orchard produce in September and October?
In a dance competition, Sierra and Thea practice 4 and 3 hours respectively each day. If they practiced for 5 days, how many hours in total did they spend practicing?
In a painting competition, Lara used 3 pots of blue paint, 5 pots of red paint and 2 pots of yellow paint. If each pot of paint costs $7, how much did she spend in total for the paint?
In a 200-question test, Mark answered 45% of the items correctly. Diana answered 30% more correct answers than Mark and Peter answered double Diana's score. What is Peter’s score?

Jasmine decides to bring cookies for her team. There are 50 team members, 20 of them want macadamia nut cookies and the remaining want the oatmeal raisin cookies. The macadamia nut cookies cost $1.50 each and the oatmeal raisin cookies cost $1 each. How much is the total cost for the cookies?
Lily has double the cats as Zoe. Zoe has one third of the cats Ella has. How many cats are there altogether if Ella has 18?
Benjie is five years older than Margo. If Benjie is 6 years old, how old will Margo be in three years?
A farmer has 200 apples. If he packs 20 apples in each box, how many boxes does he need?

A sandwich shop sells a ham sandwich for $5 and a turkey sandwich for $4. Yesterday, they sold 20 ham sandwiches and 25 turkey sandwiches. How much total income did the shop make from the sandwiches sold yesterday?
A pastry chef baked 15 pies, each containing 8 apples. However, only 10 pies were sold. How many apples did the chef use in total?
Hugo can eat 5 bags of fries from Monday to Wednesday. If Hugo ate three times the amount on Thursday, how many bags of fries did Hugo eat in all?
Sara divided her candy equally among her three friends. If she had 45 pieces of candy, how many candies did each friend receive?
Simon bought 4 packs of basketball cards. Each pack contains 15 cards. If he gave 10 cards to his friend, how many cards does he have now?
Mia has 25 dolls, and each year the number of dolls she has doubles. How many dolls will Mia have in three years?
A book has 280 pages. If Sarah reads 4 pages every day, how many days will it take her to finish reading the book?
Ella has 8 bracelets and she sells 25% of them. She then makes 5 more bracelets. How many bracelets does she have now?
A bookshelf has 7 shelves. Each shelf can hold 30 books. How many books can the entire bookshelf hold?
The teacher bakes 4 cakes and each of them has 6 slices. If everyone in a group of 10 students wants 2 slices, are there enough slices of cake for everyone?

Laura volunteers at a shelter and there are 60 cats divided equally into 3 rooms. On Wednesday, 5 cats were adopted from one room and 7 were brought into another room. How many cats are there now in each room?
Jordan is a baker who can bake 4 batches of bread rolls in an hour. If each batch contains 12 rolls, how many bread rolls can Jordan bake in 3 hours?
James stocks his shoe store with 200 pairs of shoes. He places 50 pairs on display and keeps the rest in storage. How many pairs of shoes are kept in storage?

Julia and Sandra are planning a birthday party for their friend. They plan to buy 10 balloons each costing $2, birthday cake for $25, and gifts for a total of $45. If they have a budget of $100, how much will they have left?

Benjamin wants to buy a bike. He needs 5000 points to get the bike. He has already earned 20 dancing dolls that are worth 200 points each. Each toy car he earns gives him 50 points. How many toy cars does he need to earn to get the bike?
Adam opens a new book and reads 10 pages the first day and triple the amount of pages the next day. How many pages did Adam read in total?

There were 480 people in the school gym for a concert. During the intermission, one-sixth of the boys and one-twelfth of the girls left early. How many people remained to see the end of the concert if there were 360 girls at the beginning of the concert?
A library contains 200 books. If 25% of these books are borrowed, how many books remain in the library?
A baker made 150 cookies. She sold 60% of them in the morning and 30% of the remainder in the afternoon. How many cookies are remaining?
A bookstore sells used books for $12 and new books for $25. James wants to buy a mix of used and new books. He ends up buying 10 used books and 5 new books. How much did James spend in total?

Jane has a small cake business. She sells small cakes for $5 each and large cakes for $10 each. On Tuesday, she made 5 large cakes and 10 small cakes. If she donates 25% of her earnings to a charity, how much money did she donate?
Luna bought a dress for $90 that was originally priced $120. By how much was the dress discounted?
In a year, Mark saved 50% of his annual income of $40,000 and then donated 10% of his savings to charity. How much money did he donate?
Teddy bought 50 pens. He gave away 10 pens to his classmates, and also lost 1/5 of the remaining pens because he is forgetful. How many pens does he currently have?

A coffee maker uses 5 cups of water to make a large pot of coffee, 3 cups for a regular pot, and 1 cup for a small pot. If a cleaning cycle is run, there is an extra large pot made to clean the system. If there are three large pots, two regular pots, and one small pot made in a day, and two of those days required a cleaning cycle, how many cups of water were used over those days?

Perry and Megan are studying for an exam. They plan to study 5 lessons each lasting 2 hours and 3 quizzes each of which will take 1 hour.  They plan to study 3 hours each day. If they take a 5-minute break every half hour and a 10-minute lunch break, how many days should they plan to study?

A colony of ants is carrying breadcrumbs back to their anthill. Each ant can carry 3 breadcrumbs and there are 75 ants in total. If 1/3 of the ants were not able to find any breadcrumbs, how many breadcrumbs did they manage to collect in total?
Katie hires a clown for a birthday party for $30 an hour. He works 4 hours every day for a week. How much does she pay the clown?
A pizza place sells their pizza slices for $2 each. One day they had a sale and sold their pizzas for 20% off. How much did the pizza slice cost during the sale?

A farmer harvests 9 acres of wheat, 7 acres of corn, and 11 acres of barley. If wheat takes 12 gallons of water per acre, corn requires 29 gallons of water per acre, and barley needs 50% more water than wheat, how much total water does the farmer use?
Sarah read 4 chapters of her book on Monday, 5 chapters on Tuesday and 3 chapters on Wednesday. How many chapters has she read in total?

Leanne is packing for a holiday. She has 8 dresses, 4 pairs of shoes, and 6 swimwear. Today, she decides to take off 50% of her dresses, 25% of her shoes, and 75% of her swimwear from her suitcase. How many items did she remove from her suitcase?
David purchased a laptop on sale. The original price of the laptop was $1200, but he got a 30% discount. How much did David pay for the laptop?

John has an apple farm. He collected 250 apples on Monday, 175 on Tuesday, and 200 on Wednesday. He sold the apples for 50 cents each on Thursday. How much money does John make on Thursday in dollars?

There are 50 children in a park, 35% of them are playing soccer while the rest are playing basketball. If more children who prefer soccer join them, the ratio of children playing basketball to those playing soccer becomes 5:8. How many children joined who prefer soccer?

To get to the hospital, Bob spends 15 minutes waiting for his taxi, 45 minutes riding in his taxi, and 2/3 of the combined wait and trip time on his second taxi ride. How many minutes does his second taxi ride take?

Peter is saving up to buy a new skateboard that costs $75. His weekly allowance is $7. He gets an extra $5 for cleaning the garage. His grandparents give him $10 for his birthday. He already has $30 saved. He'll receive his allowance next Monday and he's planning on cleaning the garage the same day. How much more does Peter need to save before he can buy the skateboard?
Mike spent 2 hours at the gym every day. If the gym charges $15 per hour, how much would he spend if he goes to the gym for 7 days?
Mary wants to buy 4 toys. One toy costs 15 dollars. If she paid with a 100 dollar bill, how much change will she receive?

Jake's family is going on a trip. Each of his 3 siblings is bringing 3 bags, and his parents are bringing 4 bags each. Jake decides to bring only 1 bag. How many bags is the entire family bringing on the trip?
On a bad day, Jane's father sells goods worth $500, which is half the amount he sells on a good day. What's the total amount of money he makes if he has 4 good weeks and 2 bad weeks?

Mike's family consumes an average of 8 gallons of water per day. Mike decided to get a water tank that can supply his family's water needs for 5 days. How many gallons of water should the water tank be able to hold?
A rectangular picture frame has a length of 10 inches and a width of 7 inches.  What is the perimeter of the picture frame?

Mary baked a large pizza and cut it into 16 slices. She had one for lunch every day for 4 days. Her son took one per day for 4 days to school. They shared 2 with their neighbors. How many pieces of pizza were left?

Vick decides to buy a motorcycle. It costs $13,000 but he gets a 15% discount. He sells it after a year for $14,500 but has to pay a 10% commission on the sale. How much money did he make from the motorcycle?
Alex bought some furniture for his bedroom. The bed cost $1500, the wardrobe cost $1000 and the rest of the items cost $500. He got a 20% discount on everything. How much did he spend?
Four friends decided to split the cost for a gift. The gift costs $200. If Alex contributed 25% of the total cost, how much did Alex contribute?
Ted had 30 table lamps with 4 light bulbs each. If 1/5 of them have 1 burnt-out light bulb each, how many light bulbs are still working?
Liam has 6 comic books. His best friend, Max, has twice as many comic books as Liam. How many comic books do they have altogether?
George bought 3 crates of apples. Each crate contains 24 apples. He plans to make apple pies and each pie requires 4 apples. How many pies can he make?
Tom has 12 pencils. He gives half of his pencils to Jerry. How many pencils are left with Tom?
In a school, there are 600 students. 30% of the students are female and the rest are male. How many male students are there in the school?
George has a total of 50 toys, consisting of 10 cars, 25 teddies, and the rest are balls. How many balls does he have?
Amy bakes 12 cookies, while Mary bakes twice as many. Later, they both decide to bake 3 times the number chocolate chip cookies they initially baked. How many cookies did they bake in total?
A farmer has 5 hens. Each hen lays 3 eggs per day. How many eggs will the hens lay in a week?
Maria loves to swim in the pool. The length of the pool is 60 meters. She swims 4 times back and forth in the morning and 6 times in the afternoon. How many meters does Maria swim in a day?

Victor plays basketball. He made 15 shots in one game and each shot is worth 2 points. In the next game, he made 12 shots which are worth 3 points each. How many points does Victor make in total from the two games?
Sam, a bookseller, has 120 books in his inventory. Now, he buys 15 more books every month. How many books will Sam have at the end of 8 months?
Billy had 80 comic books. He received 5 comic books for his birthday and then bought another 4 books at the store. How many comic books does he have now?
A movie theater made $18,500 in ticket sales and $12,500 in popcorn and soda sales. If the theater had to refund $3,500 due to a projector failure, how much did the theater make in total?

Chandler's lantern could be seen at night from a distance of 500 feet. Joey's lantern could be seen at night for a distance two times farther than Chandler's lantern, and Ross's lantern could be seen at night from a distance 1000 feet less than 4 times farther than Joey's lantern. If Chandler's and Ross's lantern were placed next to one another and turned on at night, how much farther, in feet, could Ross's lantern be seen compared to Chandler's?

A farmer has 8 apple trees and each tree yields 20 apples. The farmer managed to sell each apple for $2. He then realized he made a $24 profit. How much does it cost the farmer to cultivate each apple?

Oliver has a bee farm. He can harvest honey from his bees every month and can sell a jar for $12. He has 8 bee hives and each hive can produce 10 jars a month. How much money can Oliver earn in a year?
John has 5 books. He bought 2 more books than what he already had and received 3 books less than what he bought as a gift from his friend. How many books does John have now?

A bookstore has 500 books. Half of the books are fiction, one fifth are biographies, and the rest are textbooks. On a one-day sale, they sold 20% of the biography books and 10% of the textbooks. How many books are left after the sale?

Billy is taking care of his 36 chickens. He is planning to expand his chicken business next month by buying 2 additional chickens each week. In three weeks, how many chicks Billy will be having in his barn?
Peter's laptop uses 150 watts of electricity per hour. He uses it for 5 hours a day. If electricity cost 12 cents per kw/h how much does his laptop cost to run for a week?
Lisa borrowed $5000 from the bank. She has to pay 8% annual interest. How much interest does she have to pay after one year?
Paul has a pear tree in his garden. There are 7 pears hanging on the tree and 9 have fallen to the ground. If Paul's cat eats 2 of the pears off of the ground, how many pears are left?

The age gap between Tim and Natalie is a third of the total number of books Tim owns. Their ages when added together total 180, and Tim is 60 years old. If Natalie has 100 more books than Tim, calculate their combined total number of books.
A baker makes 18 loaves of bread each hour. If the bakery is open for 5 hours a day, how many loaves does the baker make in a week?
Andrew's family consumes a gallon of milk every 3 days. How many gallons of milk do they consume in a month of 30 days?
It takes 45 minutes to bake a cake and 30 minutes to cool it down. If Debra can make 4 cakes at once and she needs to bake 16 cakes, how many hours would it take for Debra to finish all the cakes?
When Laura is three times as old as Molly, Molly will be seven times as old as Donna. If Donna will be 9 years old, how old will Laura be?
Sarah wants to buy a pair of shoes that costs $60. She earns $12 per hour. How many hours does Sarah have to work to afford the shoes?
In a bakery, a single cake requires 40 minutes to bake. If the bakery has 3 ovens and operates for 8 hours a day, how many cakes can be baked in one day?

Daisy baked 60 cupcakes for a charity fundraiser. It cost her $6.50 for the ingredients. She decided to give 5 of her cupcakes to her neighbors. She sold all of the remaining cupcakes at the fundraiser for $0.50 each. How much profit did she make (money earned after paying initial costs) on the sale of her cupcakes?
A bakery sells cupcakes for $3 each and muffins for $2 each. Anna bought 7 cupcakes and 10 muffins. How much did she spend in total?
Peter needs 8 boxes to pack his collection of comic books. Each box can hold 50 comic books. If Peter has a total of 318 comic books, how many comic books will be in the last box?

Lilly loves the color pink and eats all the pink sweets. In her first pack, she eats 7 pink sweets. In the second pack, she eats 10. In her third and fourth pack, there are 6 pink sweets each. In her fifth pack, there are only 5 pink sweets. What's the average number of pink sweets in a pack?
In a marathon race, Mary runs at a speed of 5 miles per hour. If the race lasts for 4 hours, how many miles did Mary run in total?
There are twice as many girls as boys in a classroom. If the total number of students is 36, how many boys are there?

The total weight of 4 apples, 2 bananas, and 1 pineapple is 2 kilograms. If an apple weighs 100 grams, a banana weighs 150 grams, and the pineapple weighs twice as much as a banana, what is the weight, in grams, of the pineapple?
Sam has 3 boxes of chocolates. He ate 2 chocolates from each box and gave the rest to his friends. If each box initially had 18 chocolates, how many chocolates did Sam give to his friends?
John has twice as many oranges as Mary. If Mary has 15 oranges and John gives away 10, how many oranges does he have left?
A quarter of Elly's money was $12. She gave $5 to Ken and double that amount to Linda. How much does Elly have left?

The cash price of a car was $20,000. John wanted to buy the car but pay in installments. If he paid a deposit of $5000 and paid 50 equal monthly installments of $500 each, calculate how much money he would have saved by paying cash.
A basketball team has lost five times as many games as they have won. If they have won four games, and tied two games, how many total games have they played?
A marathon is a long distance running race with an official distance of 42.195 kilometers. If James has to run 1.85 times more than a marathon, how many kilometers will he run?

Jason wants to purchase a car that costs $20,000. He can save $200 a month. In addition to his savings, he can count on a $5000 gift from his parents. How many months will it take Jason to save enough money to purchase the car?
Lisa bought 12 books. Each book cost $8. She gave the cashier $100. How much change does Lisa get?
A book fair sold 100 books on the first day. On the following day, they sold 20% more than the first day. How many books did they sell in total?
A library has 5000 books. If 25% are fiction books and the rest are non-fiction books, how many non-fiction books are there in the library?

Carmen is a teacher who asked her class of 36 students to make origami stars for a project. Each student made 5 stars. However, some stars were not made properly. Each student has on average 1 improper origami star. How many proper origami stars did they make?
A toy shop had a sale where all teddy bears are sold for $4 less than their original price. If the original price was $15, and John bought 5 teddy bears, how much money did he save?
A bag contains 15 red marbles and 10 blue marbles. If Charlie randomly picks one marble from the bag, what is the probability that the marble he picks is red?
Jake pays 5 dimes for a toy car and 20 dimes for an action figure. How many dollars does he pay in total?

Angie is planning to visit her family living 80 city miles and 300 highway miles away. Her car has a mileage of 20 miles per gallon in cities and 50 miles per gallon on the highway. Given the cost of gas is $2.50 per gallon, how much will Angie have to pay for the trip?
Martin bought a bike for $800 and received a discount of 25%. How much did he pay for the bike?
Peter earns $10 each hour for babysitting. If he babysits for 8 hours on Saturday and 7 hours on Sunday, how much money does he earn over the weekend?
Cindy uses 6 strawberries to make a bowl of fruit salad. If she wants to make 25 bowls of fruit salad for a party, how many strawberries does she need?
Marvin earns $2800 and his friend Camden earns two-thirds of what Marvin earns. How much money did they both earn together?

Ashley is considering to buy a new house. The house costs $200,000 and she needs to get a loan from the bank. The bank has a rate of 10%. How much extra will Ashley have to pay if she loans the entire amount?

Paul, Ricky, and Susan each painted a room. Paul's room is 350 square feet, Ricky's room is 250 square feet, and Susan's room is 150 square feet. If each gallon of paint covers 200 square feet, how many gallons of paint did they use in total?

Michael has 4 different items he applies in the evening and he waits 10 minutes between each item. He also spends an additional 30 minutes brushing his teeth and showering. How long does his nighttime routine take?
Alex reads 12 pages of a book every day. If the book has 240 pages, how many days will it take to Alex to finish reading the book?
Sarah had $8 left after spending 1/4 of her money. How much is 3 quarters of her original amount of money?

John is planning to buy a car that costs $13500. He has already saved $8500. He takes some odd jobs which pay $30 each. If he needs to do these jobs to get enough money to buy the car, how many jobs does he need to take?

A bus driver spends $3 per gallon of gas. Her bus goes 15 miles per gallon. She drives at a rate of 60 miles per hour. If she is paid $.70 per mile, how much money does she make if she drives for 8 hours?
Emily is trying to save $5000 for a vacation. She has already saved $2000 from her birthday gifts. Each month she saves $200 from her salary. How many months will it take Emily to meet her target?

A group of friends decided to take a road trip. They realized that the total cost of fuel for the trip is $240. If they decided to share the fuel cost equally, and each person contributed $30. How many friends are on the road trip?

A school is holding a fundraiser and the students are selling baked goods. Each cookie is sold for $2, and each brownie is sold for $3. If a total of 50 items were sold, with 20 being cookies and the rest being brownies, how much money was raised from the sale of the cookies and the brownies?
A shirt costs $25. If one pants costs twice as much as 3 shirts, how much does 2 pants and 5 shirts cost?
Amy wants to bake cookies for her classmates. Each batch of cookies she bakes makes 12. If she has 36 classmates and she wants each of them to have 2 cookies, how many batches should she bake?

Liam started reading a 192-page book on Tuesday, and he decided he wanted to finish it by the end of Sunday. He read 32 pages on Tuesday, 48 pages on Wednesday, and 35 pages on Thursday. He knows he will have time to read twice as much on Sunday as he does on Saturday. How many pages does he have to read on Saturday to finish the book in time?
In a fruit basket, the number of apples is three times as many as oranges. The basket has 20 oranges. How many apples are in the basket?
Anthony bought a car for $15,000. He sold the car five years later for 60% of the price he bought it. How much did he sell the car for?
David loves reading and has 74 books. His brother, Ryan, has 46 books. However, David is planning to gift 12 of his books to Ryan. How many books will Ryan have after receiving the books?

Samantha is planning a party and bought 4 packs of soda, with 12 cans in each pack. She decides to return one pack because she bought too many. Her friend Jenny is bringing 10 cans of soda as well. How many cans of soda will they have for the party?
Natasha has 89 dollars, 15 Euros, and 4500 yen. If there are 3 dollars per Euro and 90 yen per dollar, how much does Natasha have in total in yen?
A fitness gym charges a monthly fee of $80 for a membership. If Mike decides to work out at the gym and buys a membership for 1 year, how much did Mike spend for the magazine for that year?
