Mindy needs 3/4 as many cups of sugar as she needs butter to make a cake. If she uses 16 cups of butter in her recipe, calculate the total number of cups of sugar and butter she uses all together.
Amanda has 12 beads in her left hand and 16 beads in her right hand. She then picks up 5 more beads with each hand. How many beads does Amanda have in total?

Samantha has a collection of 200 books. She gave 60% of her books to a library and the rest she sold in a garage sale. Among the sold books, 25% were bought by kids and the remaining were bought by adults. How many books were bought by adults?
A rose garden is 3 times as large as a tulip bed. If one fourth of the rose garden is 6 square meters. How large is the whole tulip bed in square meters?
Henry is selling tour packages. One package costs $450, and it costs him $150 to assemble. If he sold 50 packages this week, what is his total profit?
Anna is baking cupcakes and needs butter. The price of butter rose 25% from its previous price of $6. How much will Anna pay for the butter now?
Alma bought 6 bags of sugar and each bag has a weight of 5lb. Helen bought 5 bags of sugar that weighs 7lb each. How much more sugar does Helen has compared to Alma?
John owns a bakery and sells cupcakes in boxes of 3, 6 and 12. He sold 4 boxes of 3, 8 boxes of 6 and 10 boxes of 12 cupcakes yesterday. How many cupcakes did he sell in total yesterday?

Sophie made 80 cookies for her school's bake sale and Fiona baked 105 muffins for the same event. Fiona's muffins were sold for $1.50 each, while Sophie's cookies were sold for $1.00 each. How much money did they make in total?
John has $90. He spent 30% of it on new books and then 50% of the remaining money on school supplies. How much money does he have left?
Jasmine's flower bouquet has 10 roses, 8 tulips, and 12 daisies. She wanted to add more daisies so that half of her bouquet is made of daisies. How many more daisies should she add?

A small store has sold 25 toys in one day. The second day, it sold half as many as the first day. On the third day, it sold twice as many as the second day. How many toys in total have been sold in three days?
Tom opens an amusement park.  It cost $100,000 to open initially.  It also cost 1% of that to run per day.  He sells 150  tickets a day for $10 each.  How long will it take to make back his money?
Jim exercises for 90 minutes every weekday. On Tuesday, he exercised 30 minutes less but added 20 minutes on Friday. How many minutes did he exercise in total for the week?
Emily has a jar of gumballs. She has twice as many blue gumballs as red ones, and three times as many green gumballs as blue ones. If she has 5 red gumballs, how many gumballs does she have in total?
Marie walked 5 kilometers. Bob walked 1500 meters and Jules walked 3000 feet. How many combined feet did they walk?
Sarah can read 30 pages of a book in an hour. If she reads the book for 3 hours, then takes a break for 30 minutes, and reads for another 2 hours, how many pages will she have read?

A psychologist charges $100 for a one-hour session. If they see 8 clients a day for a week, how much does the psychologist make in a week? However, the psychologist decides to donate 10% of their income to a mental health charity. How much money will they donate?
Dwayne creates 7 bracelets out of one foot of twine. He needs four feet of twine to make a belt. How much twine does he need to make 5 belts and 14 bracelets?
Madison went to a bakery and bought 5 cupcakes. Each cupcake costs $3. She also bought a cake which costs two times the price of all cupcakes combined. How much did Madison spend in total?

Teddy received a video game as a gift which is valued at 60 dollars and a pair of sneakers worth 80 dollars. He also has 45 dollars in cash. If he decides to sell the video game and the sneakers at half their value, how much money will he have in total?
Lisa likes to jog around the park near her house. If the park is a perfect square and each side is 100m, how much distance does she cover by jogging around it once?
John works 5 days a week and spends 10 hours per day at his job. He spends 30% of this time in meetings. How many hours per week does John spend in meetings?
A movie theater sold 120 seats for a total of $840. They made $280 more with the normal price than with the discount. How much does a seat cost with the original price?
A grocery store has 200 apples. 20 of them were bought by a customer and 30 were not fresh so the store removed them. How many apples does the store now have?
Steve repairs defective gadgets. He repaired 390 gadgets this semester but 5 of the gadgets needed to be repaired a second time. How many gadgets did Steve actually work on?
A factory produces 200 cars a week. How many cars do they produce in a year?
Kim has a music subscription for $10 per month and watches movies online for $8 per month. She also has a gym membership for $20 per month. How much does Kim spend on these services in a year?
A toy factory produces 3 types of toys. They put 4 of each type in every box. The factory produces enough toys to fill 6 boxes per hour. How many toys does the factory produce in 5 hours?
John works for 300 minutes. He takes a coffee break every 30 minutes and a lunch break every 150 minutes. How many more coffee breaks does he take than lunch breaks?
Shane ordered two pasta dishes each costing $8.50. He also had a soft drink that cost $2.50. How much was his total bill?
Laura has $850 to buy a new phone. She spends two-thirds of that on her phone, and she spends a third of what is left on her phone case. What is the amount of money Laura has left?

James, the baker, makes 16 loaves of bread, each weighing 2 pounds. He then packs an equal number of the loaves in 4 different baskets. Unfortunately, one of the baskets falls and all the loaves in it get destroyed. What is the total weight, in pounds, of the remaining loaves of bread?

Olivia has a garden where she collects strawberries. For each pound of strawberries she collects, she makes $2. On Friday she collected 6 pounds and on Saturday she collected 4 pounds. How much money did she make from selling all the strawberries?
Mary is planning a party and she bought 15 balloons for $3 each and 3 party hats for $4 each. Then, she decided to buy a birthday cake for $30. How much total did Mary spend on the party?

Jenny is saving for a trip to Europe. She needs $3000 for the trip and she saves $150 every month. Her parents decided to give her a one-time gift of $500. How many months will it take Jenny to save for her trip?
A school library has 210 students enrolled. Out of that, 2/5 of the students prefer to borrow comic books while the rest prefer novels. How many students prefer to borrow novels?
Mary has a box of chocolates. She gives 2/5 of her 150 chocolates to her 3 friends equally. How many chocolates does each friend get?
Jenna has 5 pieces of candy. Her friend Ashley gave her 3 more. How many pieces of candy does Jenna have now?
A cafe owner buys coffee beans for $20 per pound. He used 15 pounds to make coffee in a week. Additionally, he had other expenses amounting to $500 for the week. How much did he spend in total?
Sara wants to make her famous blueberry muffins. Each batch she makes requires 3 cups of flour. If she wants to make 5 batches, how many cups of flour does she need?
Jonathan and Jake found $70. Jonathan’s share is four times as much as Jacob’s. How much is Jonathan's share?

A library has 4 shelves, each with 30 books. The librarian ordered some new books. Half of the books were added to the first shelf, while remaining half is equally divided between the other shelves. How many books are there now in total?
Julia read a book with 300 pages in total. She read for 5 days, every day the same number of pages. How many pages did she read per day?
Sam's kitten weighs 4 pounds at 6 weeks old. Its weight triples every 2 weeks. What will be the kitten's weight at 10 weeks old?
Liam starts his job with a monthly salary of $8000. After working for a year, his salary increases by 25%. How much is his total earnings after 2 years?

Emily wants to stock her garden with some new spring flowers. She buys 10 marigold plants, twice as many petunias, and 5 rose shrubs. If she still needs to buy 15 additional plants, how many plants were there in her garden initially?

A bus tour guide has 7 tourists in the morning and an additional 5 tourists in the afternoon. Each morning tourist pays $10 while each afternoon tourist pays $15. How much did the tour guide earn in a day?

A bakery sells 12 bread loaves every day. After four weeks, a new bakery policy dictated that half of the produced loaves must be donated every day. How many loaves does the bakery sells per day with the new policy?
If there are 25 students in a class and every student needs 3 pencils, how many pencils will the teacher need to provide?
In a field where there are 200 animals, there are 40 cows, 56 sheep and goats. How many goats are there?
There are 3 classes with 30 students each and 2 teachers for each class. How many teachers in total need to be hired if the school has 9 class in all?
A car can travel 500 kilometers on a full tank of gas. If gas costs $2 per liter and the car needs 50 liters to fill up the tank, how much will it cost to drive 1000 kilometers?
Samantha feeds her fish 2 times per day with 12 flakes each time. How many flakes will Samantha feed her fish in a week?
A toy store sells a board game for $14. Beth buys 3 board games and a toy car that costs $9 less than the board game. If she also buys 5 toy cars, how much will Beth pay in total?
A construction project requires 25 kg of nails. A single box of nails weighs 1.5 kg. If a box of nails costs $12, how much will the total cost be for the necessary amount of nails?

Martin bakes 40 cupcakes and 25 muffins. Martin eats 3 cupcakes and 2 muffins. Martin's friend eats 2 cupcakes and 1 muffin. If Martin sells a cupcake for $2 and a muffin for $3, how much money will Martin make if he sells all of his baked goods?

A water dispenser that serves five departments in an office holds 1500 liters of water and is filled to the top each week. If one department uses 200 liters of water in a week, the second department uses twice as much water as the first department in a week, and the third department uses fifty more liters of water than the second department in a week, how many liters are left for the remaining departments?
Mark has saved $20. His sister Tania saved three times what Mark saved. Mark's brother Damien saved twice what Tania and Mark saved combined. How much money have they saved in total?
The length of a rectangle is four times its width. If the width of the rectangle is 3 inches, what is the perimeter of the rectangle?

Danivan Drugstore has 4500 bottles of hand sanitizer gel in inventory at the beginning of the week. On Monday 2445 bottles were sold, on Tuesday 900 bottles were sold, and 50 bottles each day for the rest of the week were sold (from Wednesday until Sunday). On Saturday, the supplier delivers an order for 650 bottles. How many bottles of sanitizer gel does the Drugstore have at the end of the week?
A website charges $25 for an annual membership. If Jack signed up for 2 years and he has a 10% discount coupon, how much will he pay for the two years membership?
In a clothing store, a shirt costs $20. When Ray purchased 5 shirts, he was given a discount of $2 each. How much did Ray pay in total?
John had 3 apples. He ate one apple in the morning and bought 2 more apples in the afternoon. How many apples does he have now?
In the morning, there were 5 birds on a tree. In the afternoon, 3 more birds landed on the tree. But by evening, 2 birds flew away. How many birds are left on the tree?

In a bookstore, there are 8 shelves of fiction books and 5 shelves of non-fiction books. Each shelf holds 30 books. An order comes in with 60 new books: 36 fiction books and 24 non-fiction books. After keeping these books, how many books will be in the bookstore?
Five years ago, John bought a painting for $5000. The same painting will be five times as much in another five years. How much money will the painting have increased by?
Marie spends one third of her $150 savings on a book and some of the remaining amount on a doll. If Marie has $80 left, how much did the doll cost?
Liam bought a bicycle for $120 and sold it for $150. How much profit did he make?
Lily visited the library. She spent 10 minutes searching for a book, and triple that amount of time plus 25 minutes reading the book. How long did she spend in the library total?

Paul is making homemade candles and wants to sell them at a local arts fair. He spends $30 on wax and $20 on jars and other ingredients. He can make 50 candles from these materials. If he sells each candle for $5, how much profit will he make?

Libby is building an igloo in her backyard using bricks of snow. She builds her igloo in rows, using a total of 10 rows of bricks of snow. The bottom half of the igloo has 12 bricks of snow in each row while the top half of the igloo has 8 bricks of snow in each row. How many bricks of snow did Libby use for her igloo?
In Los Angeles, Eli bought 15 gallons of gas at $3.00/gallon. He paid $2.00 more per gallon in San Francisco where he bought another 15 gallons of gas. How much did he spend on gas?
A farmer has 50 chickens. Each chicken lays 2 eggs every day. If the farmer sells a dozen eggs for $3, how much will he earn in a week?

Jack can do 30 push-ups in one minute. Lisa can do three times as many push-ups per minute as Jack can. And Bob can do 4 more push-ups per minute than Lisa can do. If Jack does push-ups for 2 minutes, and Lisa does push-ups for three minutes, and Bob does push-ups for four minutes, what would be the combined total number of push-ups performed?

Randy wants to buy burgers for himself and his 10 friends. Each burger costs $7 and can feed 2 people. If Randy earns $6 a day by washing dishes, how many days will he have to work to afford the burgers?
The total number of fruits in a basket is 54. If there are 18 apples and 12 oranges, how many bananas are in the basket?
Mary has 3 more soft toys than 5 times the number of puzzles she has. If she has 23 soft toys, how many puzzles does she have?
Ted sold 47 ice creams on Monday and twice as many on Tuesday. If each ice cream sold for $2, how much did he make on these two days?
Anton has three times as many cards in his collection as Heike does. Ann has six times as many cards as Heike does. If Ann has 60 cards, how many more cards does Ann have more than Anton?

Samantha goes to an electronics store to buy 3 smartphones for her family members. Each smartphone costs $250.00 and the store is offering a 20% discount on each. How much will she need to pay after the discount?
John went to a fruit market and bought apples and oranges. If an apple costs $1, and an orange costs $2, and he paid a $100 bill for buying 55 apples and 20 oranges, how much change will he get?
In a classroom, there are 25 students. 12 of the students are female. Of the male students, 8 are freshman and the rest are seniors. How many male students are seniors?

Theo, Mason, and Roxy are siblings.  Theo drinks 8 cups of water every day. Mason drinks 7 cups of water.  Roxy drinks 9 cups of water every day.  In one week, how many cups of water do the siblings drink together?
John has read 180 pages of a book. This number is 60% of the total number of pages in the book. How many pages are in the book?
Jenny and her two friends are having a pizza party. They order 3 pizzas, each with 8 slices. If they each eat the same number of slices, how many slices does each person get?
A travel agency manages 15 buses. Each bus has 25 seats. The agency had a booking for a school trip and they used 2 buses for that. How many seats are available for the next booking?

Richard is working on a construction project. He needs 50 bricks for the wall, 25 bricks for the path, and another 15 bricks for the stairs. If the price per brick is $4, how much will he pay in total?
Jessica is raising funds for a charity. She received $500 and she hopes to triple this amount. How much money does she need to raise to reach her goal?
Tom sells cupcakes in packs and crates. Each pack has 5 cupcakes. Each crate has 20 cupcakes. How many fewer cupcakes are there in 7 packs than in 3 crates?
George sells lemonade for $2 a glass. He sold 10 glasses to his neighbors on Monday and 2 times more on Wednesday than on Monday. How much money did he make overall from Monday and Wednesday?
If a store has 10 apples and each apple costs $2, how much will the cost be if they sell 3 apples and the customer uses a $5 discount voucher?
Mary’s mom baked 80 cookies in the evening. The next morning she baked two times as many as she did in the evening. How many cookies did Mary’s mom bake in total?

Mary is preparing a salad for a family gathering. She bought 6 heads of lettuce, 5 pounds of tomatoes, and 2 pounds of cucumbers. If each head of lettuce weighs half a pound, how many pounds of ingredients does the salad have in total?

Michelle harvested 150 strawberries on first day. The next day, she harvested twice as much as the first day. On the third day, she harvested 90 strawberries. What is the total quantity of strawberries that she harvested?

For their wedding anniversary, John gifts his wife a diamond ring worth $5000, a vacation trip worth $7000, and a car worth three times the value of the diamond ring. What is the total value of all the gifts?

A group of friends are planning a road trip which is 680 miles and their car gets 34 miles per gallon. If they use premium gas, which costs $3.50 per gallon, how much will they spend on premium gas for the trip?
Every 3 months, the number of books in Peter's library doubles. Currently, he has 20 books in his library. How many books will there be in nine months?

In a garden, there are 3 types of trees. There are 15 more apple trees than orange trees and twice as many pear trees as apple trees. There are 90 trees in all. How many orange trees are in the garden?

Jack estimates the height of three trees. He estimates that the first tree is 70 feet tall, the second tree is 90% the height of the first tree and the third tree is 10 feet taller than the combined height of the first and second trees. What is the estimated total height of the three trees?
Martin has three $5 bills while Mary has four $10 bills. If both of them decide to exchange their bills for $1 bills, how many more $1 bills than Martin will Mary have?

Rebecca decided to repaint her room. She bought 3 cans of pink paint and 2 cans of white paint. Each can of pink paint covers 40 square feet, and each can of white paint covers 50 square feet. How many square feet can Rebecca paint in total?
Daniel bought 4 packets of pencils. Each packet contains 12 pencils. If he gives 2 pencils to each of his 13 classmates, how many pencils will he have left?
Alex is baking loaf of breads, each takes 3 hours to bake. If Alex wants to bake 10 loaves, how long will it take him?
Luke bred 30 dogs, and Lisa has 6 fewer dogs than Luke. After 4 months, Luke has three times as many dogs as before and Lisa gives one third of her dogs to Luke. How many dogs does Luke have?
Sarah runs a small bakery and decides to make donuts for a charity event. She can make 20 donuts for $10. If she decides to make 200 donuts, how much will it cost her?
Alex exercised in the gym for 90 minutes each day for 20 days. On the other hand, Maria also worked out. Together they exercised for 3000 minutes. How long did Maria exercise?

Steve has a stamp collection. Steve's collection contains 80 more stamps than Janet's collection. Janet has 120 stamps. If Steve's friend, Richard, gifts him 15 stamps, how many more stamps does Steve have than Janet?
A school club consists of 20 members. If each club member contributes $3 every Monday for their club activities, how much will they have collected in 3 weeks?

Andy has 12 books to read before the end of the summer. Each book has approximately 24 chapters. After the first week, Andy managed to read 4 books and 6 chapters from another book. How many chapters does Andy need to read to finish his reading list?
James purchased 2 bottles of juice. The first one contains 1.5 liters, and the second one contains 500 milliliters. How many liters of juice did James buy in total?
Jack has a collection of 500 stamps. Of them, one fifth are American stamps and the rest are from other countries. Half of the foreign stamps are from Europe. How many European stamps does he have?

John can sail his boat from the harbor to a point 10 miles away and back in 2 hours while his friend Mike can only sail to that spot and not back in the same time. If John makes the round trip in 2 hours, how fast does Mike's boat go in miles per hour?
Kim cooked 12 pies, while Jim cooked 20 pies. John cooked 12 pies more than Kim and Jim combined. How many pies did they cook altogether?
Liam collected 45 stamps, Kyle collected 25, and Olivia collected 15. They decided to equally distribute all the stamps among their 5 friends. How many stamps did each friend get?
In an arts and crafts store, there are 12 blue ribbons, 10 red ribbons, and 5 yellow ribbons. How many total ribbons are there in the store?

Sara works online as a freelancer. She works 10 hours a week and makes $20 an hour. She works 15 hours a week and makes $30 an hour. She works 2 hours a week and makes $50 an hour. She spends $700 a month on expenses. How much money does Sara have at the end of the month?
Tom is training for a marathon. He starts running 5 km per day. But every next day, he will run 1 km less than the previous day. Tom ran for 5 days. How many kilometers did he run in total?
Jake's Toolbox has 240 screws. He uses 60% of them to fix his furniture. How many screws does he have left?

Shawn, Valentina, and Sebastian participated in a pie-eating contest. Shawn ate 2 pies less than Valentina, and Valentina ate 3 pies more than Sebastian. If Valentina ate 7 pies, how many pies did all three of them eat in total?

Mrs. Lopez has 150 flowers in her garden where 25 are roses and the rest are tulips. Fifteen tulips do not bloom while the rest of the tulips do. How many blooming tulips does Mrs. Lopez have in her garden?
Sarah bought two dozen roses. She gave 1/4 of the roses to her mother and half of the remaining roses to her best friend. How many roses does Sarah have left?
Lauren is arranging flowers for a wedding. For each table, she arranges 3 roses, 2 tulips, and 1 lily. If there are 20 tables, how many flowers in total does she arrange?
The Evergreen park has three times as many trees as the Sunflower gardens. If there are 15 trees in Sunflower gardens, how many trees would remain in Evergreen park if 60% of the trees are cut?
A shipping company ships 1000 toys in one crate. How many toys can they ship in 27 crates?
Sarah has 15 markers in her pencil case. 7 markers are green, 3 are red and the rest are blue. How many blue markers does Sarah have?
Andy bought 5 candies for $1 each, 3 chocolates for $2 each and 2 milkshakes for $3 each. If he paid with a $20 bill, how much change does Andy get?
If Sara has 6 dolls and Emily has 5 times as many dolls as Sara, how many dolls do Sara and Emily have in total?

Stella loves eating chocolates. She received a box of chocolates containing 100 pieces for her birthday. She ate 25% of the chocolates on the first day and 20% of the remaining chocolates on the second day. How many chocolates are left?
Mr. Williams buys a tank for his new pet lizard. The tank is 4 feet longer than it is wide. It is 2 times as long as it is tall. If the tank’s width is 3 feet, what's the volume of the tank?

Nick's project was divided into four parts. The first part took him 10 minutes, and the second part took him three times as long. If he finished his project in 80 minutes, how many minutes did Nick spend on the last two parts?
Billy spends 15 hours every week reading. If he takes 5 hours to finish a book, how many books can he read in eight weeks?

A school has 3 classes for the 5th grade. The first class has 18 students, the second class has 20 students, and the third class has 22 students. If the school decides to divide the 5th grade into 4 classes instead, and if there are an equal number of students in each class, how many students will be in each class?
Alice has a tin of cookies. She ate 3 cookies today and 2 cookies the day before. There were originally 15 cookies in the tin. How many cookies are left?
Clara and her friends decide to have a book swapping event at school. For every 2 books a student brings, they can take 1 book home. If Clara brings 12 books, how many books can she take home?
The ratio of black to white buttons Sebastian has is 3:4. If Sebastian has 15 black buttons, calculate the total number of buttons Sebastian has.
Adam earns $25/hour at his job and works 35 hours per week. He gets a bonus each week of $100. How much does Adam make in a week?
Raven works five hours a day and earns $10 per hour. If she works 22 days a month, how much will she earn in three months of work?

Molly's grocery store sold 5 more than three times the number of apple boxes that Liam's grocery store sold. If Liam's grocery store sold 25 apple boxes, how many apple boxes did Molly's grocery store sell?

John and his friends are at a fair. John wins 10 tickets from a game. His friend, Mark, wins double the amount John did. Their other friend, Zoe, wins half the amount Mark did. How many tickets do they have in total?
Becca is sewing a blanket. She has 18 square pieces of fabric that she can sew to the blanket. Each time she sews three squares, she rests for an hour. How many hours of rest did she take?
Matt has a bag of 90 chocolates. He gives 1/3 of the chocolates to his sister and eats 15 chocolates. How many chocolates does Matt have left?

Paul is planning a road trip. He has $500 to spend. If hotel accommodations cost $120 per night and he needs to stay for 4 nights. Moreover, he needs to rent a car which costs $200. How much money will Paul have left for the rest of his expenses?

Nelson needs to buy a new bicycle, so his dad offers him extra allowance for doing chores. He earns $10 for each hour spent on chores. Nelson spends 3 hours mowing the lawn, and does this twice a week. He also spends 1 hour cleaning the garage, and twice as long washing cars. How much allowance does Nelson receive in a week?
The restaurant has 20 sushi chefs and 20 waiters. If 5 sushi chefs and 7 waiters quit, how many sushi chefs and waiters are left?
Mike likes to draw pictures. Each picture takes him 2 hours to draw. If he spends 3 hours doing homework and the rest of the 8 hours he has free drawing pictures, how many pictures can he draw?
In a class of 45 students, 60% of them are girls. If half of the girls and three-fourths of the boys went on a field trip, how many students went on the field trip?
A school purchased 50 notebooks for each of its 7 classrooms. Within a month, each classroom used up 20 notebooks. How many notebooks are left?
Eugene has $30.56 to spend at the bookstore. He bought 3 books that cost $3.75 each and a notebook that cost $5.80. How much money does Eugene have left to buy more books?
A group of friends want to split the cost of a pizza delivery. The pizza costs $20 and the delivery fee is $5. There are 5 friends. How much does each friend need to pay?
Johnny has 7 blue socks and 3 red socks for every blue one. How many red socks does Johnny have?

There were 9000 oranges in a certain box, and 8 people started to grab some. The first four people each took the same number of oranges as each of the last four people took. If the last four people each took 500 oranges, how many oranges remained in the box?

Jack spends 1 hour a day on reading, 2 hours a day on crafts, and 3 hours a day on studying. If there are 30 days in a month and Jack takes the first 3 days off, how many hours does he spend on these activities in a month?

Carlos is a beekeeper who sells honey for $10 per jar. This month, he sold three times the amount of honey than he sold in the previous month. If Carlos sold 15 jars of honey last month, how much money did he make in total for the two months?
Michael buys 7 comic books that cost $8 each and 5 action figures that cost $10 each. How much did Michael spend in total?

Ben loves to read.  He read one book that had 30 chapters, three books that had 20 chapters each, and one book that had half the chapters of the three previous books combined. How many chapters did Ben read?
There are 12 doughnuts in a box. Alex bought 7 boxes for a party and 4 friends each ate 4 doughnuts. How many doughnuts are left?
Billy buys 15 candy bars costing $2 each and sells 5 of them for $3 each. The rest of the candy bars he sells for double the purchase price. How much profit does he make?
A school provides 8 textbooks to each student for the entire year. If there are 200 students in a school, how many textbooks does the school provide in all?

During his term, Mayor Johnson attended twice as many public meetings as Mayor Williams. Mayor Smith attended fifteen more public meetings than Mayor Johnson during the same period. If Mayor Johnson attended 25 public meetings, how many public meetings did the three Mayors attend altogether?

A new employee at a software company is assigned 60 bug fixes on his first day. His manager asks him to resolve 2/3 of them by noon and the rest after lunch. However, right after the lunch, they discover that another 50% more bugs have popped up. How many bug fixes does the new employee have to resolve in total?
Lisa is saving money for a new laptop. She already saves $320. If she gets $20 for each hour of work, and she also finds $30 on the street. How many hours did Lisa work?

There are two classes -- Science and Math, each having an equal number of students after 3 students transferred from Science to Math. How many students were there in Math before students transferred, if both classes have a total of 60 students?

Lillian has 25 cookies and 15 brownies. She has guests coming over, so she gives them 5 cookies and 3 brownies each. After her guests leave, how many more cookies than brownies does Lillian have left?
Sara has been saving $20 each week for a year to buy a bicycle. The bicycle costs $950. What is the remainder of her savings after buying the bicycle?

In an aquarium, there are 1000 fish. There are twice as many goldfish as angelfish. If there 300 angelfish and the rest of the fish are clownfish, calculate the total number of clownfish in the aquarium.
Sophie had a bag of 100 marbles. She gave 1/5 of her marbles to her brother. How many marbles does Sophie have left?
John saves $5 each week in his piggy bank. If he already has $120 saved up and wants to buy a Lego set that costs $250, how many more weeks does he need to continue saving?
A bus travels at an average speed of 60 km/h. How far will it travel in 3 hours?
A farmer harvested 120 bushels of corn. He sold each bushel for $3. He used his earnings to purchase a new plow that cost $250. How much money does the farmer have left after purchasing the plow?
Rose baked 32 cupcakes for her son's birthday party. She gave 6 cupcakes to her neighbor and ate 2. How many cupcakes are left?
A shopkeeper has 60 boxes of pencils in stock. He sells off some boxes of pencils and restocks 80 boxes of pencils. How many boxes of pencils did he sell if he now has 100 boxes of pencils?
When Sam turns 25, he will be half the age of Jenny and thrice as old as Andy. Sam is 12 years old now. What is the sum of the ages of Jenny and Andy now?

Sandra was hired to plant 500 trees in a park. The park authorities will pay Sandra a $500 fee, plus $8 for every tree that is correctly planted. Sandra has to pay the park authorities $5 for every tree that dies. If 10 trees die, and the rest survive, how much should Sandra be paid?

A school has three grades. The first grade has 50 students, the second grade has twice as many students as the first, and the third grade has half as many students as the first. What is the total number of students in the school?

Celeste is purchasing items for a bake sale. She buys 5 pounds of flour, 8 pounds of sugar, and 3 times the amount of cocoa powder as flour. If her bag can carry a maximum of 35 pounds, how many more pounds of groceries can she still fit in her bag?

A group of musicians played at the park for 3 hours. They were paid $150 an hour. After the performance, they had to pay $50 for the equipment rental. How much money did the group earn after paying for the equipment?
An electric stove running for 10 hours would consume 12 kilowatts. How many kilowatts would the electric stove consume if it was used for 8 hours a day for 4 days?
In a school, every 1 in 5 students plays basketball and every 1 in 4 students plays soccer. If there are 100 students, how many students play neither basketball nor soccer?
In a school, there are 1200 students. 60% of the students are boys and the rest are girls. How many girls are there in the school?
Mary buys 3 packets of chocolate cookies for her teammates. She has 5 basketball teams and 12 players on each team. How many packs of cookies does she buy?

Jessica's task was divided into three parts. She finished the first part of the task in 30 minutes. It took her three times as long to finish the second part. If she managed to finish her task in 2.5 hours, how many minutes did Jessica spend on the third part of the task?
Sara bought a teddy bear for $15. She plans to sell it with a 50% profit. If one of her classmates wants to buy two teddy bears, how much should her classmate pay?
Anna's family is going on a trip. The total driving time is 6 hours. They decide to drive for 2 hours a day. How many days will it take them to complete the driving?
Sam has 5 boxes of marbles. Each box contains 20 marbles. If Sam loses 30 marbles, how many marbles does he have left?
Peter had 80 pieces of chocolate. He gave 1/4 of it to his friend John. How many pieces of chocolate has he left?
George can clean a car in 20 minutes and a bike in 10 minutes. If he spends 3 hours cleaning cars and 2 hours cleaning bikes, how many vehicles did he clean?

Michael reads 3 books per month. The first month he reads 5 books. The second and third month he reads 6 books. His fourth month he reads half as many as his first month. His fifth and sixth month he reads 7 books. How many books does Michael read in 6 months?

Emma is shopping for fruits. She buys 5 bananas, 6 oranges, 4 apples, and 3 watermelons. Each banana costs $0.5, each orange costs $0.6, each apple costs $0.7, and each watermelon costs $1. How much does Emma spend in total?
John earns $1000 if he works for 50 hours a week and gets an extra $50 for every hour of overtime. If he worked 60 hours last week, calculate his total income.
A toy car travels at the speed of 20 meters per minute. How far will it travel in 2 hours?

Sophia and Sam need to cycle 500 miles together. Sophia cycles at a speed of 25 miles an hour for 4 hours, and Sam cycles at a speed of 35 miles an hour for 3 hours. How many miles do they still have to cover?
Alice studies 4 hours a day for her exams. She plans to increase her study time by 1 hour a day until she reaches 8 hours a day. How many days will it take for her to reach her goal?
A 100-page book uses 10 pages for illustrations. Of the remaining pages, 2/5 is used for tasks and the rest is used for textual content. How many pages are used for the textual content?
Eva buys 9 shirts and 7 dresses for $300. If a shirt costs $20, how much does each dress cost?
Emma has 4 cats. David has twice as many cats as Emma and Kate has 10 fewer cats than David. How many cats do they have altogether?
Eva's backyard had 100 trees. Each year, the backyard grows ten more trees than the previous year. How many trees were in her backyard after 5 years?

For a charity program, Laura was promised that for each friend that she could get to donate, the friend would receive a free t-shirt and she would receive $10. If Laura managed to get 15 friends to donate by the end of the event, how much money did Laura make?

Teacher Stella bought 18 packs of chocolates. Each pack has 12 chocolates. She also bought 5 packs of candies each containing 30 candies. How many pieces of candies and chocolates did Teacher Stella buy in total?

I have 30 chickens in my farm. Some foxes invaded the farm and ate 5 chickens. If we sent out one fox every day from the farm, how many days will it take to send out all the foxes if the number of foxes equals the number of remaining chickens?
Adam's house is 7 feet taller than 3 times Toby’s house. If Adam’s house is 37 feet tall, how tall is Toby’s house?
In a bookstore, there are 90 books. Every day, the bookkeeper organizes 15% of the books. How many books can be organized in 3 days?

Jack is having a chocolate sale to raise money for his school's field trip. Each carton has 15 chocolate bars in it. He sells 7 cartons. He sells each chocolate bar for $2 and buys each bar for $1.5. How much profit does he make from these sales?
Lisa went shopping for clothes. She bought 2 shirts at $15 each, 3 pairs of jeans for $30 each, and a dress for $45. She also had a discount coupon for $10 off her total. How much did she spend?

Sarah collects the same percentage of white and black stamps in her collection of 200 stamps as she does of green and red stamps in another collection. She owns 80 green stamps and 40 red stamps. How many black stamps does Sarah have if she has fewer black stamps than white ones?
John has 30 stickers. There are 3 times as many red stickers as there are blue stickers and the rest are green. If he has 5 blue stickers, how many green stickers does he have?

Sara is involved in 4 projects. Each project has 7 stages. She uses 3 notepads per stage. Each notepad cost $8. After completing all projects, she sells half of the notepads for $6 each. How much money did she spend?
A box of laundry detergent contains 3.5 kilograms. If Robert uses 200 grams each time, how many times can he use the laundry detergent from the box?

Two rockets are traveling from earth to the moon. The first rocket makes the 238,900-mile journey in 12 hours, while the second rocket takes 14 hours. Assuming both rockets traveled at constant speed, what was the difference in speed, in miles per hour, between the first and the second rocket?
A factory produces 300 toys per day. If the factory operates 20 days a month, how many toys are produced monthly?
Mr. James sold products worth $150. If 1/3 of the total amount of sales he made were from online sales, and the rest were in-store sales, how much did he get from in-store sales?

Tom has 500 colored pens and 300 colored pencils in his art store. If each colored pen costs $2 and each colored pencil costs $1.50, how much is the total value of the colored pens and colored pencils in his store?
Sara runs her own bakery. She bakes 6 batches of bread every day and each batch has 12 loaves. How many loaves of bread does she bake in one week?
Five years ago, Sarah was four times the age of her brother Sam. Sam is currently 13 years old. How old is Sarah now?
A delivery company charges $60 per shipment, plus $2 per product. If a company wants to send 36 products how much will it cost altogether?
Mario's cat consumes 2 cans of cat food every day. If he bought a box containing 28 cans of cat food, how many days will the box of cat food last?

Steve celebrated his birthday on January 1st and his wife Lisa, who is 3 years older than him, celebrates hers on the same day. This year, they celebrated Steve turning 30 and their combined age was four times Steve's age when they got married. How old was Steve when they got married?

Jane has 6 apple trees in her orchard and each tree produces 300 apples. If 20% of the apples fall into Jane's neighbor's yard, and each apple weighs 6 ounces, how many ounces of apples does Jane's neighbor have?
Alice buys 20 packs of stickers for her crafts, each pack has 50 stickers. She uses some for a project and is left with 600 stickers. How many stickers did Alice use for her project?
Hank buys a bag of cat food for his cat. His cat eats 2 cups of cat food a day, and the bag contains 40 cups of cat food. How many days can he feed his cat with this bag?
Michael owns 30 pairs of shoes. He donates 5 pairs to charity. He then buys 10 more pairs and his brother gives him 4 pairs. How many pairs does he have in total now?
There are 250 employees in a company. If 45% of the employees are men and the rest are women, how many women work in the company?

Zoe has 12 cookies in her jar. She had 3 cookies for breakfast, 2 cookies for lunch and 4 cookies for dinner. She then made some cookies and added them to the jar, ending up with 18 cookies. How many cookies did she make?

Barry stands on his head for 10 minutes at a time, but then he must sit for 5 minutes before he can take another turn standing on his head.  How many turns can Barry take standing on his head during a single 2-hour period?
The local movie theatre has 20 rows with 30 seats each. One-fourth of the seats are reserved. How many seats are still available?
Out of the 200 people at the community event, 1/4 are adults while 3/5 are children. The rest are teenagers. How many teenagers came to the community event?
A building has 20 floors. Each floor is 3 meters high. The first floor starts at 4 meters above the ground. How high is the top of the building from the ground?
Jade is making bracelets to sell at a fair. Each bracelet uses 5 beads. Jade has 85 beads in total. How many bracelets can Jade make?
A factory can produce 160 cars in a week. In a month, the factory has to halt production for 3 days for maintenance. If each month has 30 days, how many cars can the factory produce in a month?

A brewery produces 500 bottles of beer each day. Due to increased demand, they have increased production to produce 3 times more beer per day. How many bottles of beer will they produce in a week following the increased production?
A bee collects 2 droplets of nectar from each flower. If the bee visits 15 flowers, how many droplets of nectar will it have collected?
A basketball coach planned a 2-hour training for his team. If the team needs to spend a third of the time for warm-up, how many minutes is the warm-up?
Alice can read 40 pages of a book each day. If she reads a book for 7 days, how many pages can Alice read in 7 days?

Sofia likes to knit scarves. She has 7 red scarves, 2 times more blue scarves, and a yellow scarf. Her grandma decided to give her three additional green scarves for every blue scarf she has. How many scarves does Sofia have in total?

Max bought 7.5 boxes of cereals each weighing 20 grams. Liam bought 5 boxes of cereals each weighing 30 grams. Max and Liam combined their cereals and then partitioned them into 5-gram packets. How many packets did they make?
Mr. Brown baked 150 cookies for a charity event. 75 were chocolate chip, 45 were sugar cookies. The rest were oatmeal raisin cookies. How many oatmeal raisin cookies did Mr. Brown make?

In an online community of 500 members, each person makes 35 posts per week. If the admin of the community decided to remove 50 members due to violations, how many posts would be made by the community in a month?

The half-hour newscast includes 12 minutes of national news, 5 minutes of international news, 5 minutes of sports, and 2 minutes of weather forecasts. The rest is advertisements. How many minutes of advertising are in the newscast?
Linda bought a new bicycle for $380, plus gear for 20% of the cost of the bicycle. How much money does Linda have left if, before buying these items, she had $800?
John started a small business with a capital of $1,500. He gained a profit $25 every day. How much money will he gain after 24 days?
Mia has a collection of stamps. She has 5 times as many stamps as her brother. If her brother has 12 stamps and their mother gives Mia 10 more stamps, how many stamps does Mia have in total?
Patricia has 2 dozen cartons of eggs. Each carton contains 12 eggs. How many eggs does Patricia have?
If there are twice the number of dogs as cats in the pet shop and there are 15 cats, how many dogs are there in the pet shop?

Leah has a box of chocolates with 90 pieces. She gives 10 pieces to her friend and shares the rest evenly with her three sisters. If her youngest sister ate 5 pieces from her share, how many pieces of chocolate did Leah's youngest sister initially have?

Leo has a sweet tooth and he is collecting candies. He started with a total of 100 candies. One day, he was feeling generous and decided to give his cousin 15% of his total candies. The next day, he ate 50% of the remaining candies. How many candies does Leo have left?

Mike is trying to manage the weed problem in his yard. Each rabbit he introduces eats 8 weeds, and each time he sprays weed killer, he reduces the total weed population to 70% of what it was previously. If the yard has 500 weeds to start, and Mike sprays once and introduces 15 rabbits, how many weeds are left?

A game developer plans to release a new game. He initially wants to create 1500 levels in the game. However, because of time constraints, he was only able to make 800 levels at the time of release. Every week, he adds 20 new levels. How many weeks will it take him to create the remaining levels?
Tommy has 12 comic books, which is 30% of Carrie's comic books. How many comic books does Carrie have?
Joe and David were building a model airplane. Each model airplane requires 15 parts. Joe made 120 parts while David made 30% less than Joe. How many model airplanes can they build all together?
The perimeter of a rectangular garden is 70 meters. If the length of the garden is twice as long as the width which is 10 meters, how many meters long is the length of the garden?
A greengrocer has 1000 kg of apples. He sells them for $3 per kg. But he had to buy them for $2 per kg. How much profit did he make?
A garden has 50 trees with fruits. Half of the trees have mangoes, the rest have apples. If 5 mango trees were cut down, how many fruit trees are left in the garden?

Greg is saving up for a new video game which cost $80. He already has $20 saved. He plans to sell his old comic books to add to his savings. If he sells 8 comic books and still has $10 left after buying the game, how much did he sale each comic book for?

On Monday, Daniel received $20 from his mom. The next day, his mom gave him twice as much as she did on Monday. On Wednesday, his mom gave him $5 more than what she gave him on Tuesday. How much more money did Daniel receive on Wednesday than Monday?

Alex has a basketball team. He has 10 times as many players as his baseball team. He has half as many players on his soccer team as his baseball team. He has 3 players on his baseball team. How many players does he have in total on all three teams?
Billy was 40 inches tall last year. If he grew 4 inches more than his sister who grew 2 inches last year, how tall is Billy now?
Amy finds 9 beetles in the garden, Sam sees 14 butterflies while tending, and Ann discovers 13 ants carrying food. Later, 4 of the beetles crawl away. How many insects remain in the garden?

At the beginning of the year, Tom had 80 dollars in his piggy bank. Each month, he saved 10 dollars more than he saved the previous month. How much money did he have in his piggy bank at the end of the year?
Hannah, Eva, and Mia collect postcards. Hannah has twice as many as Eva, and Eva has three times as many as Mia. If Mia has 10 postcards, how many postcards do they have in total?
George makes 15 cups of lemonade for a picnic. If his friends drink 80% of the lemonade, and George takes half of the remaining cups home, how much lemonade does George take home?
Lena works 6 days a week and she usually works less on Wednesday and Sunday, working just a quarter of a day. If her daily salary is $8, how much does she earn every week?
Jane eats a pound of potatoes and three times as much cabbage. If potatoes have 130 calories per pound and cabbage has half as many calories, how many total calories did she consume?

Sally picked up 20 mangos on Monday. On Tuesday she picked up twice the quantity she picked on Monday. On Wednesday she picked 4 times the quantity she picked on Tuesday. What is the total quantity Sally picked on these three days?
Peter collected 35 seashells at the beach on Monday, twice as many on Tuesday and half as many on Wednesday. How many seashells has Peter collected in total?
Sarah plans to send 50 roses to her friend. If a dozen roses cost $15, how much would she pay for the roses she wants to send?

Bill plays 3 football games a week. In his first 15 games, he scored an average of 2 goals. How many goals a game does he have to score in his remaining 12 games to bring his average for the season up to 4 goals a game?
If the price of a kilogram of apples is $3 and Jane buys 7 kilograms, and then gives her brother 2 kilograms, how much did Jane effectively pay per kilogram?
Paul has a bookshop. He purchased 10 boxes of novels. Each box contains 15 novels and each novel costs $25. How much did he spend?
George has a business that produces 150 units of a certain product per day at the cost of $20 each. He sells each unit for $45. How much profit does he make in 30 days?
In a class of 30 students, 5 students were absent today. If the teacher has taken attendance and counted 3 more students than there actually are, How many students did the teacher count?

Elizabeth bought some packs of stickers. Each pack contains 12 stickers. She needs 300 stickers to complete her sticker album. If she already has 45 stickers, how many more packs does she need to buy?
Alicia is reading a book with 300 pages. She read 60 pages on the first day and then reads one fifth of the remaining pages each day. How many pages will be left after the second day?

A Wizard has been creating potions for the last 3 days. On the first day, he made 5 potions. Each subsequent day he doubled the number of potions made from the previous day. How many potions did the wizard make in total?
Jen takes a reading challenge to read 7 books a week. Each book is 268 pages long. How many pages would Jen have read at the end of the week?
A basket has 12 apples. Bethany picks up 5 more and puts it in the basket, but she eats 3 from the basket. Then she gave 2 apples to her friend. How many apples are left in the basket?

Maria decorates houses for Christmas. She used 20 strings of lights for a small house and three times more for a large house. If she decorated 5 small houses and a large one, how many strings of lights did she use?
Hal swims at the community pool every weekend. The pool has distance markers every 25 frames. If Hal swims to the 8th marker and back twice, how many frames has he swum?
Julia found a box on her shelf that contains 30 bracelets. Half of them are made of silver. She bought 3 more bracelets, all made of gold. How many silver bracelets does Julia have in total?
Sebastian has 48 nickels in his money box. His younger sister Angela has 96 nickels in her money box. How much more money does Angela have?
On one day, Ann reads 2/3 of a book. The next day she reads the remaining pages of the book in 4 hours. If she reads at a constant rate, how many hours will it take her to read the entire book?
Billy and Fred have a combined total of 20 vintage postcards. Fred has 25% more postcards than Billy. How many postcards does Fred have?
Angie has a green thumb and loves to garden. She grows 20 plants a day. If she already has 250 plants, how many plants will she have after 3 days?

An alien army invades Earth and abducts 500 humans. They return 60% of the abducted humans. The remaining humans were divided equally between two different alien planets. How many humans were taken to each alien planet?
A school play sold 400 tickets. Twice as many adult tickets were sold than student tickets. On the first day, they sold 90 adult tickets. How many student tickets were sold on the first day?

Alex bought a new mobile phone for $700. After two years, he decided to sell the phone and he received $200. If he earned an additional $1000 from a part-time job during the two years, what is his total savings?
John saves 10 of his brownies to give Amy, who saves a quarter of her 12 cupcakes to give to John. How many desserts does Amy end up with?

Sophia plants 4 pots of daffodils, 8 pots of roses, and 5 pots of orchids. Each daffodil plant has 3 blooms, each rose plant has 20 blooms, and each orchid plant has 15 blooms. How many blooms are there total?

A mechanic has three different jobs to choose from to earn the most money.  The first job is repairing two cars and three bicycles.  The second involves repairing three bicycles and four motorcycles.  The third involves repairing one car, one motorcycle, and two bicycles.  If the mechanic charges $60 to repair a car, $30 to repair a bicycle, and $80 to repair a motorcycle, what is the most money the mechanic can earn if he can only choose one of these three jobs?

Miriam is trying to exercise more and figures if she counts her exercises it will be encouraging to see her numbers go up. On Monday she does 5 push-ups. On Tuesday she does 7 push-ups. On Wednesday she does twice as many push-ups as the day before. On Thursday she does half the number of total pushups she already did that week. Finally, on Friday she does as many pushups as the total number she did over the previous four days. How many pushups does Miriam do on Friday?
A cafe can serve 18 tables at a time and each table seats 4 people. If 2/3 of the tables are filled, how many empty seats are there?
Jonathan runs a lemonade stand. He usually sells 100 cups of lemonade in 5 days. If Jonathan got sick and wasn't able to sell lemonade for 2 days, how many cups of lemonade does he sell in a week?
A shopkeeper buys 20 hats at a cost of $10 each and sells each at $15. What is the total profit that he makes?

Winona needs 5 teaspoons of sugar to make one batch of her famous pancakes. Every sugar packet contains 10 teaspoons of sugar. If she plans to make 4 batches of pancakes, how many sugar packets does she need?
A farmer has 20 acres of farmland. He uses 1/4 of the land to grow corn and uses 1/5 of the land to grow wheat. How many acres of land does the farmer have left?
Alice found that she has 2 times as many comic books as Mike. If Mike has 16 comic books, how many pages does Alice's comic books have if each book has 25 pages?

Billy has a music playlist of 90 minutes. He has 20 songs in the playlist. The first 10 songs are each 4 minutes long. The rest of the songs in the playlist are all an equal length. How long is each of these songs?
Gabby is reading a book with 120 pages. She reads 15 pages each day. How many days will it take her to finish the book?

In an art gallery, 15 painting are of landscapes, 10 more paintings are portraits than landscapes, and 5 fewer paintings are abstract than portraits. How many paintings are in the art gallery in total?
In 3 years, Kim will be thrice his brother's age. If his brother is now 3, how old is Kim now?
A bakery uses 2 bags of flour for each loaf of bread and 4 bags for each cake. If the bakery made 50 loaves of bread and 30 cakes in a week, how many bags of flour did they use in total that week?

A store sells any hat for the same price and any pair of boots for the same price, but the price for hats and boots are different. If you bought 4 hats and 2 pairs of boots for $180 total, then realized you didn't need the boots. How much does a hat cost if when you returned the boots you received a 50% refund?

Tom, Anna, and Jerry are cyclists who train every Monday. Tom cycled 1/4 of a 48-kilometer track on one Monday. Anna cycled 3/4 of the distance of Tom, and Jerry cycled half of the distance of Anna. How many more kilometers should Jerry cycle to cover the same distance as Tom?
Ellen has 9 pink bows and three-fourths as many blue bows. Anna has 8 blue bows and two times as many pink bows. How many bows do they have together?
Julia walks 10 miles every day for 6 days. On the seventh day, she walks 3 miles more than the sixth day. How many total miles will she walk in 7 days?
Jenna had 75 colored pencils. She gave 1/3 of her colored pencils to her friend, Laura. How many colored pencils does Jenna have left?

Jenny started working on a 2000-piece jigsaw puzzle. The first day, she completed 20% of the puzzle. On the second day, she completed another 10% of the remaining pieces. On the third day, she completed 40% of the remaining pieces. How many pieces does she have left to complete after the third day?
Susan goes to the gym every 2 days. Each gym session takes her 1 hour. How much time does Susan spend at the gym in a year, considering the year has 365 days?
A local store sells pencils individually at $2 each. However, if customers buy 2, they receive a 30% discount for the 3rd pencil. How much money is saved if a customer decides to buy 3 pencils?

In a forest, there are three types of trees: oak, beech, and elm. Each oak tree yields 120 logs, each beech tree yields 95 logs and each elm tree yields 150 logs. If the forest has 5 oak trees, 4 beech trees, and 3 elm trees, how many logs can be harvested from this forest?
Billy is able to type 50 words per minute. If he needs to write a 5-page essay and each page requires 400 words, how long will Billy take to complete it?
A pizza shop sold 20 large pizzas at $15 each, 15 medium pizzas at $10 each and 10 small pizzas at $5 each. How much total revenue did they earn from selling pizzas that day?
In a marathon, Jen runs at a speed of 5 miles per hour and Karl runs at a speed of 7 miles per hour. If they both run for 4 hours, by how many miles does Karl run further than Jen?
I want to buy books for myself. I found 3 interesting books each priced at $15. I also want to buy a bookshelf which costs $50. How much total money do I need?
During a charity fundraiser, Lucy sells cakes. She sells 9 more cupcakes than muffins and 20 more brownies than cupcakes. In total she sells 128 pastries. How many cupcakes did Lucy sell?
Jake's scores on his first three math tests are 76, 85, and 89. What score does he need on his fourth test to have an average score of 90 for the four tests?
A jug contains 2.5 liters of water. If the jug is 80% full, what is the total capacity of the jug?
A book club meeting lasts for 2.5 hours. If they meet 4 times a month, how many hours does the book club meet in a month?
There are 36 horses in the race track. One-fifth of the horses are champion racers. Three-fourths of the remaining horses are experienced and the rest are rookies. How many horses are rookies?
Roger needs to drive 450 miles to reach his vacation destination. His car gets 30 miles per gallon of gasoline. How much gasoline does Roger need to reach his vacation destination?

John has $5000 for his shop expenses. He orders goods for his shop and pays $1500. A customer pays him $1000 for goods he had bought on credit. John then does some repairs in his shop and spends $2000. How much money does John have left?
Donna bought 6 boxes of cookies and 4 boxes of candies. Each box contains 24 pieces. How many more cookies than candies did Donna buy?

The parking lot has a total of 50 spaces. By morning, 15 cars parked in the lot. By afternoon, 10 more cars arrived. In the evening, one-fourth of the cars left the lot. How many cars were left in the lot by the end of the evening?
Dan, Sarah and Chloe are to share a box of 240 cookies. If Sarah takes 20%, and Dan and Chloe are to share the remainder in the ratio 3:4 respectively, how many cookies will Chloe get?
Melissa is repairing her shoes. For each shoe, it takes her 5 minutes to replace the buckle and 10 minutes to even out the heel. How many minutes does Melissa spend on this project total?
A jar contains 40 cookies. Each day, Jane eats 2 cookies and her brother eats 3. How many cookies will be left in the jar after 5 days?
Sam has 12 candies. He gave 2 to his friend John, 3 to his sister Kate, and saved the rest for later. How many candies does Sam have left?

Jessica's mom has saved up $2000 to be divided among her children. Jessica will receive 40 percent of the total since she's the youngest. Tim will get 45 percent of the remaining money, and the rest will go to Mike. How much money will Mike get?
John wants to distribute 5 bags of candy equally among his 8 friends. If each bag contains 24 candies, how many candies will each friend receive?
Mary is baking cookies. She made 20 cookies on Monday and three times as many on Tuesday. Then she sold half of these cookies and gave away 10. How many cookies does she have left?

Sara, a travel blogger decided to tour across Europe. She spent 3 days exploring France. After that, she spent twice as much time in Germany. After spending time in Germany, she went to Italy and spent half as much time she spent in France. How many days did she spend travelling across these three countries?
Sarah buys 4 pairs of shoes and gives $600 and gets $80 in change. How much did each pair of shoes cost?
Pamela is baking cakes for her family reunion. She needs to bake for 50 family members. If each person gets one slice and each cake yields 8 slices, how many cakes does she need to bake?
Lisa's phone uses 1000 watts of power per hour. She charges it for 3 hours a day. If electricity is 16 cents per kW/h, how many cents will charging her phone cost for a month?
Sue has two apple orchards. One orchard has 150 trees and the other has 200 trees. If each tree produces an average of 50 apples. How many apples does Sue have in total?
Amy’s monthly Netflix subscription costs $10 and she spends an additional $5 for every extra movie she rents. This month she spent a total of $60 on Netflix. How many extra movies did Amy rent?
Megan is organizing her bookshelf. She wants to place 5 books on each shelf and she has 4 shelves. Each book weighs 3 pounds. How much weight will there be in total on the bookshelf?
Lucy earns $20 an hour cleaning houses. She cleans 3 houses each day, 5 days a week. How much money does Lucy earn in a week?

A pizza restaurant sells their large pizzas for $15 each. If they sell twice as many medium pizzas, which are priced at $10 each, as their large pizzas in a day. How much do they make if they sell 10 large pizzas in a day?

Amy started saving for a trip almost 1 year ago. For the first half-year, she saved $30 every week. In the second half of the year, she got a job that pays $150 per week, so she managed to save whole of her salary. If the trip costs $4,500, how much more money does Amy need to buy the trip by the end of the 1 year?
Sophia has $30. She wants to buy a book for $15, a poster for $20, and a notepad for $7. How much more money does Sophia need to buy all those?
Samantha spent $9 on a movie ticket. The movie ticket was double the cost of her drink. If she tips the bartender 15% of her original drink cost, what is the total amount Samantha spent?

On Monday, Alex sold 1200 paperback books. On Tuesday, he sold twice the number he sold on Monday. On Wednesday, Alex sold 600 fewer than he did on Tuesday. How many paperback books did Alex sell from Monday to Wednesday?
A car consumes 1 gallon of gas every 20 miles. If a person has to travel 240 miles, how many gallons of gas will the car consume?

A string has been cut into three pieces. The first part is 20 cm and the second part is half as long as the first part. The third part is 10 cm less than the second part. How long is the string in total?

Michael takes a 60-minute train to school every day. His classmate Natalie takes 2/3 of this time to arrive by train at school. If Michael walks back home every day, 4 times slower than the train, and Natalie takes the train back home, calculate the total amount of time, in minutes, they spend traveling from school to home and vice versa each day.
James had 200 marbles. He gave half to his sister Clara. Clara gave half of hers to their friend Fred. Fred lost 15 marbles while playing. How many marbles does Fred have left?

Mary has a garden that measures 10 meters by 8 meters. She wants to plant roses that take up 1 square meter each. If she only wants to cover half of her garden with these roses, how many roses does she need to plant?
Sue has 420 flowers in her garden. She gave 150 flowers to her friend Ann, and 70 more flowers to her friend Betty than to Ann. How many flowers does Sue have left in her garden?

Molly has a collection of books. She has 20 romance novels, 30 sci-fi books, and 40 mystery novels. If the romance novels cost $3 each, the sci-fi books are $4 each, and the mystery novels are $5 each, how much did Molly spend on her book collection?

Kevin earns $4000 a month and spends $1500 on rent. His monthly groceries cost $300 and he spends $200 on public transportation. How much money does Kevin have left after paying for his monthly expenses?
Danny invests in stocks, he bought 30 shares for $25 each. If the value of each share goes up by 1/5, how much is his total investment worth now?
In a bookstore, every book costs $15. Jenny wants to buy books for her five friends. She buys three books per person. How much should Jenny prepare to pay for the books?
Jessica baked 10 dozen cookies for a festival. Each person at the festival eats 2 cookies. How many people can eat Jessica’s cookies?

If Johnny has a card collection of 500 cards, and he sells half of them. Later, he discovers a previously unknown stash of cards in his attic, which raises his collection by 25%. How many cards does Johnny currently have in his collection?
John runs 5 miles every day. If he decides to run 2 times more miles per day. How many miles will he run in a week?
John baked cookies and sold each for $3. If he earned a total of $81, how many cookies did he sell?

Shane has a collection of stamps. He starts with 100 stamps. He then gave 20 stamps to Alice. He also gave 15 stamps to Bob. If he sold a third of the remaining stamps, how many stamps did he have left?
Ellis runs 5 kilometers every day. In 2 weeks (14 days), how many kilometers does he run altogether?

Larry has 300 comic books. Ken collected thrice as many, and Ed collected half of Larry and Ken's combined total. If Ed gave comic books evenly to his 4 older brothers, how many comic books does Ed have left?
There were 15 hunters in the woods. If they caught 6000 quails in total, and 14 of them caught 350 quails each, calculate the number of quails the fifteenth hunter caught.
Timothy is combining paint to get his desired color. He combines 0.5 gallons of yellow paint, 1.5 gallons of blue paint, and 0.2 gallons of red paint. How many gallons of paint does he have in total?
Jenna and her friends went to a theme park where the entry fee was $45 per person. If Jenna paid for herself and 5 friends, how much did she spend in total?
A soccer match lasts for 90 minutes. If the first half of the match takes up 55% of the total time, how long does the first half last?
Jacob has 64 marbles. He gives 4 marbles to each of his 8 friends. Then, he buys 3 times as many marbles as he gave away. How many marbles does Jacob have now?
A group of friends decided to split the cost of a $500 hotel room. If 4 friends chipped in $75 each and one friend could only contribute $50, how much money is still needed to pay for the room?
Peter buys 7 shirts for $25 each. However, he realizes that two of the shirts are damaged and gets a refund for them. If the refund is 80% of the cost of each shirt, how much does he get back?

Marcus throws a party in his house and orders 5 pizzas for his guests. Each pizza has 8 slices. During the party, guests ate at a constant rate and after 2 hours only half of the pizzas remained. How many slices were eaten per hour?
In a community garage sale, Mike sold 35% of his items on the first day and 85 items on the second day which is 25% of the items. How many items did Mike have in total?
A car's top speed is 180 km/hour. This car travels twice as fast as a bicycle. How far will the car travel while the bicycle travels 45 km?

A bakery has 3 types of pies: apple, blueberry, and cherry. The apple pie weighs 2 pounds, the blueberry pie weighs 1.5 pounds, and the cherry pie weighs 1.7 pounds. In one day, the bakery makes 4 apple pies, 6 blueberry pies, and 3 cherry pies. What is the total weight of all the pies made in a day?

Christina walks 7km to school every day from Monday to Friday. She returns home covering the same distance. Last Friday her mother asked her to pass by her friend, which is another 2km away from the school in the opposite distance from home. How many kilometers did Christina cover that week?

During a one-week vacation to the beach, it was sunny for 4 days. The temperature on these days were 25, 30, 27, and 28 degrees Celsius. The average temperature during the same week at their home city was 20 degrees Celsius. What was the difference in average temperature between the beach and their city?
At a music store, each CD costs $12. If Mike buys 7 CDs, how much will he have to pay?

Mary adds 2 teaspoons of sugar per glass of lemonade. She usually has 3 glasses of lemonade each day. She buys her sugar in a 32-ounce bag. If there are 48 teaspoons of sugar per ounce, how many days will her bag of sugar last?
Emily baked 8 dozen cookies for her school bake sale. Each dozen has 12 cookies. She sold 2/3 of the cookies and ate 6. How many cookies does she have left?
Margo and Tom want to buy a $90 video game console. Margo has saved $25 and Tom has saved double that amount. How much more money do they need to buy the video game console?
A box contains 10 red balls, 15 blue balls, and 20 green balls. What percentage of the balls are not red?
In a classroom, there are 20 boys and 5 times as many girls. How many students are there in the room in total?

Rick went to the market and bought 3 kilograms of rice that costs $2; 1/2 kilogram of chicken costs $10 for 1 kilogram; three jars of pickles costs $3 per jar. Rick also wants to buy a $7 pizza for dinner.  How much money should he have with him to buy all those?
Jean earns $100 a day. If she doesn't hand in her assignment by the deadline, her pay will be reduced by 30%. If this happens, how much will she earn a day?

A bookstore has a collection of 150 children's books. If 10 people borrow 3 books each from the bookstore in one day, and 30 more books are borrowed the next day. How many books are remaining in the bookstore after these two days?

A bakery has three types of cakes, the first one has 10 slices per cake, the second one has 12 slices per cake, and the third one has 8 slices per cake. If the bakery sells 20 cakes of the first type, 30 cakes of the second type, and 40 cakes of the third type, how many slices of cake do they sell?

Sarah needs to cover a wall of her room with wallpaper. The wall is 12 feet wide and 9 feet high. The wallpaper is sold in rolls, each roll covers 30 square feet. How many rolls of wallpaper does Sarah need to buy?
The teacher divided 30 candies equally among 5 students. One of the students gave 2 of his candies to a friend. How many candies does he have left?
A bicycle dealer marks all his bicycles up by 80% of cost. If the bicycle costs him $200, find the selling price of the bicycle.

Aaron earns $15 for every hour he works at the supermarket. He also received $90 as tips this month. If Aaron managed to save $300 from his earnings this month, how many hours did he work at the supermarket?
Deborah runs at a pace of 6 mph for an hour each day. Andrew runs at a pace of 7 mph for 45 minutes each day. Over the course of a week, how many more miles does Deborah run than Andrew?
A rectangular playground has a length of 75 meters and a width of 35 meters. What is the area of the playground in square meters?

Elm Forest Park hosted 15,000 deer on Saturday night. The next morning, there was a 5-hour deer exodus out of the park, at a constant rate of 1,500 deer/hour. Over the next 8-hour period, new deer entered the park at a constant rate. If the final number of deer in the park was 16,500, at what rate did the new deer enter the park?

Sarah is sewing together three rectangular patches of fabric into a quilt. The first is 9 feet by 6 feet, the second is 7 feet by 8 feet, and the third patch is 8 feet by 5 feet. She plans her quilt to be 24 feet long. How tall will her quilt be?

A hotel had operations expenses totaling $100. If the hotel received clients whose total payments for services offered totaled 3/4 of the cost of the operations, calculate the loss the hotel incurred.
Joseph bought a laptop at a 10% discount. If he paid $900, how much was the original price of the laptop?
A carpenter is constructing a deck around a pool. The pool is 12 feet wide and 20 feet long and he wants to build a 4-foot-wide path around it. What is the area of the deck in square feet?
George can type 90 words in 5 minutes. How many words can he type in 30 minutes?

Linda's mother wants to lose 7 pounds so that her dress will fit better. She asks Linda to help her keep track of her calories. Linda's mother burns 3,000 calories of fat a day by doing moderate exercise. There are 3,500 calories in a pound of body fat. How many days will it take for Linda's mother to burn off 7 pounds if she does moderate exercise and consumes 2500 calories a day?
Sarah threatened to deduct 5% of Mark's bonus if he does not meet his target sales for the month. If Mark's bonus is $1000, how much will he receive if he does not meet his target?

An apple store sells iPhones for $800 each. For a special weekend sale, the store decreased the price by 25%. However, on the same weekend, they also charged a 10% tax on all purchases. How much would an iPhone cost after the discount and tax?
Mike has 8 boxes full of books. The first 3 boxes each contain 5 books. The next three boxes contain 10 books each. The last 2 boxes contain 15 books each. How many books does Mike have in total?
Alice had 125 apples. She gave 25 apples to Bob and 45 apples to Charlie. Then Tom gave her 20 more apples. How many apples, in total, does Alice have now?
Simon and his four siblings ordered 4 pizzas. Each pizza had 12 slices. If they all ate the same amount of pizzas, how many slices did each person eat?

In a running event, the race is 500 meters. This year, the route will be 3 times as long. The organizers need to set up 4 water stations for the runners. The distance between the stations will be the same and the last station will be at the finish line. What is the distance between water station 1 and station 3 in meters?

John runs a bakery shop. He bakes 100 chocolate chip cookies and 50 donuts daily. If a cookie is sold for $2 each and a donut for $5 each, how much does John make daily from selling both donuts and cookies?
In a garden, there are total 30 trees. 10 are oak trees, 5 are maple trees, and remaining trees are apple trees. How many apple trees are there in the garden?

A school bus can accommodate 60 students. If there are 325 students in a school who are going for a field trip and each student takes the school bus separately, how many school buses does the school need to arrange?
A sports club has three coaches and fifteen players. The club also has twice as many fans as players, and the rest of the people are staff. If the club has 90 people total, how many staff are there?

John wants to build a wooden fence around his backyard. The backyard is rectangular and measures 30 feet by 20 feet. If he builds the fence along the perimeter of the backyard, how much wood does he need?

The pizza store had a buy 1 take 1 promo on their regular size pizzas. Five customers bought pizzas. The first two bought 1 pizza each, the next two bought 2 pizzas each and the last customer only bought 1. How many pizzas did the store giveaway?
Mike planted 12 apple trees in his garden. Every tree gives 30 apples. How many apples does Mike have in total?
Samuel has started a small gardening workshop with a team of 4 co-workers. He needs to purchase 5 shovels for each of them. If a shovel costs $25.00, how much will he spend on shovels?
Peter has 60 toy cars. He gives away 10 cars to his friend and then buys 20 more cars. What percentage of his original number of cars did peter give away?
30% of Sam's books are fiction. The rest are non-fiction. If a non-fiction book costs $3 each, how much will Sam spend if he has 20 fiction books?

A record store bought a number of albums for 15 dollars each. If they sold each of them at 20 dollars and managed to sell a total of 40 albums in a week, how much profit did the record store make in that week?

In a month, Joy can earn 80 stars to unlock a special reward from playing her favorite game. If she got 5 stars for winning 8 times and earned 10 stars for a single in-app purchase, how many more stars can she earn this month to unlock her special reward?

David commutes to his workplace every day. It takes him 7 minutes to find a place to park and 10 minutes to walk to the office. 3 days of the week it takes him 20 minutes for the elevator and the other 2 days are less busy so he waits for the elevator only for 5 minutes. How much time does he spend commuting in a week with 5 work days?
George is doing a puzzle that has 300 pieces. He completed 90 pieces yesterday and 45 pieces today. How many more pieces does George need to finish?
Tracey is making a salad dressing. She reduces 2 cups of vinegar to 1/10 of its original volume, then adds 1 cup of oil. What is the final volume of the salad dressing in cups?

Jack sent 80 emails on Monday, 5 more emails on Tuesday than on Monday, 10 fewer on Wednesday than on Tuesday, and 15 more emails on Thursday than on Wednesday. How many emails did Jack send in total?
A farmer has 60 chickens. He sold half of them and then bought another 12. How many chickens does he have now?
Mike has 5 cats and each cat has 4 legs. Billy has 3 dogs and each dog has 4 legs. How many legs do the pets have in total?
A school library has 169 books. 35% of the books are about science. How many science books does the library have?

Laura is jogging to the gym which is 18 miles away, at a speed of 6 miles per hour. At the same time, her brother realized Laura forgot her water bottle and needed to catch up to her. He decided to cycle to her at a speed of 18 miles per hour. How long in minutes does Laura's brother spend traveling to reach her?
In a city, 75% of the population own a house, and 300 of them own an apartment. How many people are there in the city?
Amanda was given a 750ml bottle of water. She drinks 250ml every morning. How many mornings will the water last if Amanda drinks the same amount every day?
A lemonade stand sells 3 cups of lemonade for $5. Sue bought 2 cups, and John bought 4 cups. How much do they owe in total?
Jonathan bought 70 oranges. He gave twenty oranges to Arnold. He gave ten more oranges to Bella and kept the rest. How many oranges did he keep?
John bakes 5 cakes and each cake is cut into 6 pieces. How much would each piece cost if John sold all the cakes for $90?
A bakery shop sells buns for $2 each and cakes for $15 each. If a customer buys 4 buns and 2 cakes, how much does he need to pay in total?
Emma is baking cookies. The recipe calls for 4 cups of flour for 1 batch of cookies. However, Emma wants to make half a batch. How many cups of flour will she need?
