Emma is saving for a new laptop which costs $800. She already has $200 saved up and can save $100 from her part-time job each month. How many months will it take for her to have enough for the laptop?
400 people attended a concert. 70% of the attendees were adults and the rest were children. How many children attended the concert?
Alfred's skateboard business made $250 in one week by selling 25 skateboards. The next week, Alfred sold 35 skateboards. How much did Alfred's skateboard business make in the second week?

In a chemistry class, there are 24 students who are divided into 3 groups for a project. If 5 students of one group could not participate in the project and are transferred to another group, how many students are there in the largest group?

Lisa wants to make muffins for a bake sale. Each batch of muffins requires 2 cups of flour. She already has 8 cups of flour at home and decides to buy 12 more cups from the store. If she uses all the flour to bake muffins, how many batches of muffins can she make?

Frank wants to buy a new bike that costs $250. He saved $50 from his birthday and is saving $10 each week from his allowance. How many weeks does he need to save in order to have enough money to buy the bike?
Lucy bought 3 shirts and 2 pairs of pants for $85 total. Sally bought 1 shirt for $20. If every shirt and pair of pants costs the same price, how much does a pair of pants cost?

Cindy wishes to purchase a desk for $500. However, she only has $300 right now. She decides to save $50 a month to reach her goal. How many months will it take for Cindy to have enough money to buy the desk?
Jill has 4 peanut butter cups for every 2 snickers bars. If she has 16 peanut butter cups, how many snickers bars does she have?
Sue paints 4 paintings per week. If each painting sells for $120, how much money does Sue make in a 10 week period?

Sarah is planning to buy a skateboard that costs $150. She saved up $25 from her birthday, earns $20 per week for doing chores, and spends $5 per week on ice cream. How many weeks would it take for Sarah to save up enough money to buy the skateboard?
A construction company needs 4 bricks to build a wall. Each brick costs $2. If the company needs to build 3 walls, how much will it cost for the bricks?

Ann and Tom play a soccer match. Every goal scored by Ann is worth 3 points, and every goal scored by Tom is worth 2 points. If Ann scored 7 goals and Tom scored 14 goals, how many points in total did they score?
Emily has 10 roses. Every day, the number of roses doubles. After four days, how many roses does she have?

Tina and John are planting trees in a park. Tina plants 5 trees every hour and John plants 7 trees every two hours. They plan to plant trees for 5 hours. How many trees will they have planted altogether?
Sarah works 6 hours a day from Monday to Friday. If she earns $15 per hour, how much does she earn in a week?
Bob's car can go 350 miles on a full tank of fuel. If he wants to make a round trip that totals 800 miles, how many full tanks of fuel will he need?
Tom runs a fruit farm. He grows 20 apple trees, each tree produces 50 apples. He also grows 15 orange trees, each tree produces 70 oranges. How many pieces of fruit in total does Tom's farm produce?
John has 30 books on his shelf but he gives 5 of his books to his friend Bob. Then his sister gives him double the amount of books he gave to Bob. How many books does John have now?
Chris can climb a ladder twice as fast as Jenna. If it takes Jenna 3 minutes to climb the ladder, how long will it take Chris to climb?
A baker has 67 loaves of bread and divides them evenly into 5 baskets. After a few hours, he realizes that 2 baskets have been eaten by rats. How many loaves of bread does the baker have left?

Paul wants to save up to buy a new computer that costs $800. He can save $100 per week from his work salary. However, he has to pay $50 per week in various bills. How many weeks will it take Paul to save up for his new computer?
Lucas has 6 feet of string that he wants to use to make bracelets. If 1 foot of string is left after Lucas uses 0.5 feet of string for each bracelet, find out how many bracelets he made?
Isabel picked 35 lemons from her lemon tree. She used 15 lemons to make lemonade and she gave her friend 5 lemons. How many lemons does she have left?
Tim and Alex went to a bookstore. Tim bought 3 books for $15 each and Alex bought 2 books that cost the same as Tim's. How much did Alex spend on books?

Jean and Mark are writing their 1500-word group project. Jean wrote 350 words while Mark wrote 200 more words than Jean. After editing their project, they remove 30 words and add thrice as many words as they removed. How many more words should they add to reach the project word limit?

Joanna spent $320 on a new dress for her collaboration event. Emily spent $45 on shoes, $80 on a dress, and $15 on accessories for the same event. How many more dollars did Joanna spend for the event?

The basketball team went to a game in a different city. They took 3 SUVs and 4 buses. There were 5 people in each SUV and 8 people in each bus. Each SUV can hold a maximum of 7 people and each bus can hold a maximum of 20 people. How many more people could have ridden with the basketball team?

There are 30 bicycles parked at a park. Each of these bicycles has a bike rider and a passenger. At the park, each bike gains an extra passenger. How many people are in the bicycles by the time they leave the park?
Rob bought 7 packs of sweet candies and 3 packs of sour candies. Each pack contained 20 candies. How many more sweet candies than sour candies did Rob buy?

Lucas is baking cookies for his classmates. He needs $3 for flour, $2 for sugar, $4 for chocolate chips and $1 for baking soda. If there are 30 students in his class, how much is he going to spend on the cookies?

In a city park, in the morning there are twice as many joggers as there are bicycles. In the afternoon, there are three times as many bicycles as in the morning. If there are 25 bicycles in the morning, how many joggers and bicycles are there in the park in the afternoon?
Peter has 12 fish in his aquarium. He decides to add 3 more fish every month. How many fish does he have in the aquarium after 8 months?

A neighborhood has a total of 20 houses. Each house is either blue, red or green. The number of blue houses is 5 more than the number of red houses, and the number of green houses is twice as much as the number of blue houses. How many green houses are there in the neighborhood?
A toy train has 6 carriages, each carrying 5 teddy bears. If one of the carriages is empty, how many teddy bears are in the toy train?

Lily decides to start cycling around a circular park. The circumference of the park is 22 miles. Lily can cycle at three times the speed she can walk. It takes her 30 minutes to walk 1 mile. How long, in hours, does it take to cycle around the park?
A construction site works 5 days a week and uses 3 tons of gravel each day. If gravel costs $70 per ton, how much is the total cost of gravel for one week?

Penny is making chocolate chip cookies for her neighbors. Each batch of cookies requires 2 cups of chocolate chips. She plans on making 10 batches of cookies and then 3 additional batches the next day. Each bag of chocolate chips contains 4 cups. How many bags of chocolate chips does Penny need to buy?
Elaine plans to buy a dress that costs $55. The store offers her a discount of 15%. If she buys two dresses, how much will she need to pay?
Michelle's car has a fuel efficiency of 40 miles to the gallon. She drives 100 miles per day. If gas costs $3.50 per gallon, how much would she spend on gas in 15 days?
Elizabeth sells handmade jewelry. Each bracelet costs $15 and each necklace costs $25. How much income would Elizabeth make if she sold 6 bracelets and 3 necklaces?
The song, 120 MB in size, downloads at the rate of 20 MB per minute for its first 100 MB, and then 30 MB per minute thereafter. How long, in minutes, does it take to download entirely?
A zoo had 45 peacocks. Sadly, 5 of them flew away and 15 new baby peacocks were born. How many peacocks are there now in the zoo?
Mike has 12 oranges and divides them equally between his four friends. How many oranges does each friend get?
Jeffery has 6 pens in total. He lost 2 pens in the park and 1 pen in the school. His father bought him 5 new pens. How many pens does Jeffery have now?
Jessica purchases two rolls of fabric that are each 15 yards long. Each bow she makes requires 1.5 yards of fabric. How many bows can Jessica make?
In a library, there are 120 books. One-third of them are history books, the rest are science books. How many science books are there in the library?

Michael runs a car spare parts shop. For this month, one-third of their sales are tires, one-fourth are headlights, and the rest are exhausts. If Michael's shop was able to sell a total of 84 parts, how many of them are exhausts?
A farmer has 90 chickens. Every week, he sells 10% of his chickens. How many chickens does he have left after a week?
Jill has 15 books. Tom has three less than double the amount of books Jill has. How many books to they have in total?

Elmer has a pond that initially contained 2400 pollywogs, but these pollywogs matured into toads and left the pond at a constant rate of 50 pollywogs per day.  In addition, Elmer has a son named Melvin, who loves to catch pollywogs, remove them from the pond, and release them into a nearby stream.  For the first 20 days, Melvin caught 10 pollywogs per day and released them into the stream.  How many days did it take for all of the pollywogs to disappear from the pond?

Carla owns a restaurant and has an order for 4 pizza, 3 hamburgers and 5 pasta dishes. Each pizza recipe requires 2 pounds of cheese while each hamburger and pasta dish requires 1 pound of cheese. Cheese at the market is priced at $3.00 per pound. How much will Carla spend on cheese for this order?

Sarah is part of a global project team that comprises 50 members in total. 40% of them are from Europe, 10% from Asia and the remaining are from the United States. How many team members are from the United States?

Xander wants to organize a party. The venue costs $500 for rent, but he also has to pay 20% tax. Moreover, the food costs $600 and the band charges him $300. If Xander sells tickets for $50 each, how many tickets does he need to sell to break even?
A bookstore sold 45 books in the morning. Twice as many were sold in the afternoon. How many books were sold in total?
Mike bought a box of chocolates that contained 24 pieces. He ate 5 pieces and shared the rest equally among his 3 friends. How many chocolates did each friend get?
Julia pays for a $1.29 pack of gum with a $5 bill. If the cashier gives her smallest number of bills and coins as change, how many pieces of money will Julia get as change?
James earns $15 an hour. In one week, he works 40 hours. If he saves 20% of his income every week, how much money will he have saved after 6 months?
Anna has 300 apples. She sells 1/3 of them. How many apples does Anna have left?
Lulu is a baker. She bakes 60 cookies every day. However, on Sunday she bakes twice as much. How many cookies does she bake in a week?

Samantha's name contains 8 letters and her brother's name contains 6 letters. Their dad's name contains 4 letters and their mom's name contains 5 letters. What's the total number of letters in their names?
A potato farmer has a farm with 3000 plants. 50% of the plants are potatoes, 30% are tomatoes and the rest are onions. What percentage of the plants are onions?
A building was 30 meters tall at the end of 2010. It will grow 15% more than its previous height each year. How tall has the building grown from 2010 until the end of 2013?
Alex wants to save some money for his trip to Europe. He already has $1000 in his savings account. Every month he adds $200 to his savings. How much will he have after 6 months?
A jug contains 5 liters of milk. John pours 200ml of milk into a glass, 7 times a day. How many days will it take for John to empty the jug?
Emily plays basketball. In the last game, she scored 4 times more points than in the previous one. If she scored 20 points in the last game, how many points did she score in the previous game?

A bakery produces cookies every day. Their production rate is 500 cookies per hour. They work for 8 hours a day. If they triple their production rate overnight, how many cookies will they produce the next day?
A worker bee travels 12km a day to gather nectar. If the worker bee makes this trip for 30 days, what’s the total distance the worker bee has traveled?

In Mr. Johnson's 3rd grade class, there are 30 students registered but 5 students were absent. In his 4th grade class, there are 25 students registered but 3 students were absent. How many students were present in Mr. Johnson's classes?
Lucy walks to school, which is 2 miles away from home. She goes back home for lunch and then goes back to school. When she finally goes home, how many miles has she walked?

A group of students decide to sell cookies for a school fundraiser. They bake 7 batches of cookies, and each batch makes 20 cookies. After a day at the school fair, they realize they've sold 85 cookies. How many cookies do they still have left over?
Katie bought 3 books at $15 each, 5 notebooks at $10 each, and 2 pens at $2 each from a stationery shop. What is the total amount Katie paid?
A bakery makes 120 cupcakes a day. If a box can fit 15 cupcakes, how many boxes will the bakery need for one day?
Lucas has a collection of toy cars. He receives 5 toy cars for his birthday every year. If his collection started with 10 toy cars and this year is his 7th birthday, how many toy cars does he have?

Kimberly received a 10% salary increase at her job. Before the increase, she earned 70 dollars per hour. She works 6 hours per day for 5 days per week. Every week, her expenses amount to 800 dollars. How much does she save a week after the salary increase?
Sarah has a collection of coins in silver, bronze, gold and platinum. Four-fifths of them are silver, and 60 are bronze. The gold coins are 75% of the bronze ones. How many coins are platinum?

Joseph's lemonade stand sells 10 cups of lemonade per hour. After 5 hours, his sister Kate joins him and they manage to sell 20 cups of lemonade per hour together. How many cups of lemonade were sold in total after 8 hours?
John has collected $18 in quarters. How many quarters did John collect?
Emily has agreed to cover 60% of her son's tuition fees, which total $12000 per year. How much will Emily have to pay each year?
Ethan and Brandon are both saving money. Ethan has saved $50 and Brandon has saved three times as much. How much money have they saved all together?
It takes Steve 20 minutes to clean one window. If he cleans 10 windows in the house on Monday and Tuesday each. How many hours he spends cleaning windows?
Peter and Jack are collecting stamps. Peter has 50 stamps and Jack has 20 more stamps than Peter. How many stamps do they have altogether?
Jacob found 7 seashells at the beach and twice as many pebbles. How many items did Jacob find in total?

Paul has 2 accounts with 15,000 dollars in each. He withdrew 2.5 times from one account than he did from the other. If Paul withdrew 2000 dollars from one account, how much money does he have left in total?
A library has 24 shelves of books and each shelf contains 30 books. The library decided to donate 5 shelves of books. How many books does the library have left?
Alex buys a new gaming console. It cost 20% more than his old $200 console. How much did he pay for the new console?

Sarah bought five magazines on Monday and Peter bought eight magazines on Tuesday. If next week they plan to triple the number of magazines they bought, how many combined magazines will they have in total?
Gina buys 8 candies each for 3 of her friends and 12 candies for her little brother. If one candy costs $0.50, how much did Gina spend on candies?
Jennifer received 36 stickers as a gift. She plans to decorate her scrapbook using these stickers. If she uses 4 stickers per scrapbook page, how many scrapbook pages can she decorate?
There are 60 polling booths in a county, and 4/5 of the polling booths are fitted with digital voting machines. How many polling booths have digital voting machines?
Lisa cycles 10 miles every day. After 3 days, she increases her daily cycling distance by 50%. How far will she have cycled after a total of 6 days?

A movie theater charges $8 per person for a ticket. A family of 4 children and 2 adults visited the theater. If a child's ticket costs half the price of an adult's ticket, how much did the family spend on tickets?
At a birthday party, 5 balloon bundles are needed. Each bundle has 3 red, 4 green, and 2 blue balloons. How many total balloons are needed?

John's bicycle got a flat tire. He spent $20 from his savings to fix it. Before that, he also bought a new bell that costs three times the repair fee, and new handle grips, each costing the price of two bells. After that, he had $400 savings left. How much money did John have saved before?

Harry works as a painter for $12/hour. On Wednesday, he works for 8 hours, and he receives $22 in tips. On Thursday he works for 6 hours, and he receives $15 in tips. On Friday he works for 7 hours, and he receives $18 in tips. How much money does he earn in total from Wednesday to Friday?
Peter found 5 bags of marbles. Each bag has 12 marbles. Out of excitement, he lost 7 marbles. At his party, Peter and his friends used 30 marbles for a game. How many marbles were left unused?
At a bookstore, a novel costs $10 and a picture book costs $15. If Dan buys 3 novels and 5 picture books, how much did he spend?

Ben is recording a new album with 8 songs. He spends 6 hours in the studio, where he writes, records, and edits his songs. Each song takes 10 minutes to record, then it takes 40 minutes to edit all of his songs. How many minutes did it take Ben to write each song, if each song took the same amount of time to write?
John bought a book for $30 and sold it for $45. What is the percentage of profit John made?

Toby buys a box of chocolates that contains 6 packets, and each packet has 5 chocolates in it. He eats 2 packets of chocolates and shares the remaining chocolates with 4 of his friends equally. How many chocolates do each of his friends get?
Sarah is making cookies for her friends. Each friend receives 2 cookies. If she has made 18 cookies, for how many friends did Sarah bake?
Sue reads 30 pages every day. How long will she take to finish a book with 450 pages?
Maggie's dog weighs 60 pounds and gained 20% of his weight in the past month. How much does he weigh now?

Suzy currently has 100 pens. John buys 1/5 as many pens as Suzy. If John's age is 15 and Suzy is younger than John, calculate Suzy's age if their age difference is the same as the difference in number of pens they've each got.
Maria is selling her hand-crafted bracelets at a school fair, she sells 45 bracelets for $6.50 each and 25 necklaces for $10 each, how much does she make in total?
Emily had 3 times as many candies as Jessica. Emily gave Jessica 15 candies, and now they have an equal number. How many candies does Jessica have now?

A toy store has 1700 different toys. There are 700 toys that are cars. The remaining toys are either dolls or teddy bears. If the number of dolls and teddy bears are equal, how many teddy bears are there in the toy store?

Diane has a badminton club. She earned a total of $120 from 100 g shuttlecock and $90 from 200 g shuttlecock every week. If each piece of 100 g shuttlecock costs $6 and each piece of 200 g shuttlecock costs $3, how many pieces of shuttlecock does she sell every 2 months?

Lily and her brother Jake both leave their house to go to the park, which is 6 miles away. Lily bikes at a speed of 3 miles per hour, while Jake runs at a speed of 2 miles per hour. If Jake decides to run an extra 2 miles, how much more time does he spend outside than Lily?
Mike can paint a room in 5 hours. When his brother, John, helps him, they can finish in 2 hours. How long does it take for John to paint a room on his own?
Jacob has 15 chocolates. He gives 5 of them to his best friend, and then another 4 to his brother. How many chocolates does he have left?
In a school, 60% of the students are girls and the rest are boys. If the total number of students is 500, how many boys are there in the school?
Jane earns $20 for each dog she walks. She sees a dog every 20 minutes. Her shoe expenses are $100 a week. If she walks dogs for 6 hours a week, how much profit can Jane expect to make?
Jake baked 48 cupcakes for a school event. He wants to divide them into boxes that hold a dozen cupcakes each. How many boxes does he need?
Lila and her friends are collecting stickers. They started with 200 stickers. Each of them added 20 stickers to the collection. After 5 rounds, how many stickers did they have in total?
At a zoo, there are 200 animals. 45% of them are birds, and 30% of the birds are parrots. How many parrots are there in the zoo?
Bill's dog catches 5 squirrels and 10 birds. Jim's dog catches 5 less than four times as many animals as Bill's dog. How many animals does Jim's dog catch?

An amusement park charges $20 for the entrance ticket and $5 for each ride. Jenny and her three friends want to go to the amusement park and plan to ride each of the 10 rides once. How much will they have to spend in total?

Riley bought some packets of stickers to add to his collection. He gave one packet to his little sister and then shared half of the remaining packets with his friends. If Riley has 5 packets of stickers left, how many packets did he buy initially?

Tim has collected stamps from three different countries. He collected 7 stamps from Australia, 15 from Japan, and 9 from Canada. On his birthday, his friends gifted him another 25 stamps. How many stamps does Tim have in total now?

Charlie has saved $250 for a new video game console. However, the console costs $350. He decided to sell some old video games and made $75. After selling his old games, how much does he still need for the console?
A hiker climbed 6,280 feet in the morning and in the afternoon he climbed three times as much. By the end of the day, how many feet did the hiker climb?
A classroom contains 20 desks, each with 4 seats. On the first day of school, 30 students come. Ten more students arrive on the second day. How many empty seats are there after the second day?
In a sports store, there are 50 bundles of tennis balls, each having an equal number of 3 balls. How many bundles does a coach need to buy if he wants 120 balls?
Sarah has 8 larger cookies and 15 smaller cookies. The larger cookies weigh 3 ounces each and the smaller cookies weigh 1 ounce each. How much do all of the cookies weigh together in ounces?
In a classroom of 25 students, 15 students have pencils, and 10 students have pens. Each pencil costs $0.50, and each pen costs $0.75. How much is the total cost of all pencils and pens?

Layla bought a pretty throw pillow. It has red, black, and white patterns. There are four times as many black patterns as red ones, and three times as many white patterns as black ones. If the pillow has 2 red patterns, how many white patterns does it have?
A pack of paper contains 500 sheets. If Alice needs 3 sheets for a project and she does 6 projects a day, how many days will one pack of paper last?

Paul, Lily and John went to the apple orchard where each interested picker is charged $5 for entry. The standard price of a pound of apples is $15. After their picking, they paid $115 for their harvest. The generous owner deducted the entrance charge from the cost of the harvest. How many pounds of apple did they pick?
Each box of crayons contains 24 colors. If Jenny buys 3 boxes and Shelly buys 2 boxes, how many crayons do they have in total?
A flower bed starts with 20 tulips, and each year the number of tulips doubles. How many tulips will be in the flower bed after 8 years?
Tim reads 30 pages in an hour, and Jerry reads 25 pages in an hour. How many more pages does Tim read than Jerry in 2 hours?

A team of 4 programmers developed a software application in 12 weeks. If they have to develop a similar application but 3 times bigger, and they add 2 more programmers to the team. How many weeks will it take them to develop the new application?
Carl and Lisa decided to start a lemonade stand. Carl sold 45 cups of lemonade yesterday while Lisa sold 2 times more. How many cups of lemonade they sold yesterday?
Rebecca bought 4 boxes of cookies. Each box of cookies cost $7. Later, she returned 2 boxes and got her money back. How much money did Rebecca spend altogether?

Wynter went to her local town bike shop to buy her sister a bicycle as her birthday gift. While at the shop, Wynter counted 50 bicycles and 20 tricycles. How many wheels in total did the vehicles she saw have?

Linda has a basket of fruits. She picks 5 apples and 7 mangoes from the basket. Later, she added 3 more mangoes to the basket. The next day, she decides to make a fruit salad. She uses half of the fruits in the basket. How many fruits remain in the basket?

James has a garden with different kinds of vegetables. He has 30 tomatoes plants, 15 more bell pepper plants than tomato plants, and half as many cucumber plants as bell pepper plants. How many plants does he have in total?
A recipe requires 2 cups of flour, 1 cup of sugar, and 3 cups of milk. If Jamie doubles the recipe, how many cups in total will she require?
Susan bought 3 books and 2 magazines. If each book costs $12 and each magazine costs $6, and she used a discount coupon that reduced her total by 10%, how much did she pay?
Suzy is selling cookies to raise money for charity. Each box of cookies costs $3. She sells 7 boxes on Monday, 9 boxes on Tuesday and 5 boxes on Wednesday. How much money did Suzy raise in total?
Anna has 5 times as many books as Lucy. Lucy has twice as many books as Mike. If Mike has 7 books, how many books does Anna have?

Ben plans to wash cars all summer, and then use the profits to buy a skateboard and accessories. He charges $20 per car. The skateboard is $150. The accessories are $10 each. At the end of the summer he has washed 25 cars. He wants one skateboard, and then he will use the rest for accessories. How many accessories can he buy?
To fix her computer, Maria needs to add a new keyboard and two mouse pads. If each mouse pad costs $15 and each keyboard costs $50, how much does she pay total?

Erwan went on shopping. He purchased a pair of shoes at $200 but discounted 30%, and two shirts at $80 each. Upon checkout, the cashier said that there is an additional 5% discount. How much did he spend after all the discounts?
A shop sells peaches for $6 a kilogram. Sara bought 3 kilograms of peaches and 2 kilograms of oranges that cost $4 a kilogram. How much did Sara spend overall for peaches and oranges?

Lucy read 180 pages of a book in 4 days. If all the days she spent the same time reading, and now she could only spend half of that time reading each day, how many more days will she take to finish the rest of the book, which is 420 pages?
Lucy the dog has 5 ticks. The other dog, Daisy has 4 times the amount of ticks as Sally. The final dog, Sally has half the amount of ticks as Lucy. How many ticks are there?
Tom collects baseball cards. He currently has 200 cards. During a card convention, he buys 300 more cards. Sadly, he lost 50 cards on his way home. How many baseball cards does Tom have now?
10 barrels each contain 60 liters of oil. Each barrel is filled up to half of its capacity. How many liters of oil altogether are contained within the barrels?

At a marathon event, Mike ran 10 miles. After a brief rest, he ran 50 percent further. After another break, he ended up running three times the distance he ran for the first time. How many miles did Mike run in total?
Amy is 4 years older than Joe. In 6 years, Joe will be 20 years old. How old is Amy now?
Debbie has a collection of 340 books. She has 80 fiction, 90 nonfiction, and the remaining books are comics. How many comic books does she have?

Zoe is making cupcakes for her birthday party. The recipe for the cupcakes says that it can make 24 cupcakes. But Zoe wants to make 36 cupcakes for her party. By what factor should Zoe increase the recipe?

Lucy has a flower shop. She buys 5 bunches of flowers every day. Each bunch has 20 flowers. However, not all flowers are sold each day. 5 flowers from each bunch are left unsold and withered. After 10 days, how many flowers were left unsold?
Marvin has a collection of 60 stamps. He wants to put 70% of his stamps in a new album. How many stamps will Marvin put in the new album?

A party planner is organising a birthday party. There will be 30 guests and each guest will receive a party bag costing $5 each. She also needs to buy decorations costing $150 in total. How much will she spend in all?
George and his friends go fishing. George catches 4 fish and each of his three friends catch the same amount as him. How many fish do George and his friends catch altogether?

Emily starts a new job with a salary of $40,000 per year. After one year, she gets a raise of 10%. After another year, she gets another raise of 12% on her new salary. What is Emily's salary after the two raises?
Pearl needs to read a 300 page book for her assignment. She reads 20 pages each day. After reading for a week, how many more pages does she need to read?
A TV show runs for 30 minutes. Commercials take up 1/5 of the show time. How long in minutes is the actual show?
Alice reads 12 pages of a novel each day. If the novel has 300 pages, how many days will it take her to finish the novel?
In an apple orchard, there are 50 apple trees. Each tree can produce 30 apples. How many apples are there in total?

Three bees are collecting pollen for their hive. Each bee makes 7 round trips a day to a field that is located 300 miles from their hive. What is the total distance covered by the three bees in their trips in one day?
Jessica bought 5 apples and 2 oranges for 50 cents each. How much did she spend for the fruits?
Andy has 7 pet turtles. Each turtle needs 3 lettuce leaves per day. How many lettuce leaves would Andy need for his turtles every week?
Maddie saves half of her $80 weekly allowance. After 4 weeks, she withdraws $60 for a concert ticket. How much does she have left in her account?

Sophie decides to learn to play golf. She can hit a golf ball at 100 yards. She goes through rigorous training 5 times for 3 weeks each time and at the end of the last one she can hit a ball 15% farther. How much distance (in yards) did she gain per week, assuming she gained an equal amount of distance (in yards) each week?
Amy's horse weighs 350 pounds; it weighs 5 times as much as Jenny's horse. What is the combined weight of both horses?
Paul earns $150 a week mowing lawns. He uses $100 a week to pay his school fees. If he starts with $0, after how many weeks will he be able to buy a game console for $500?
John's car cost $10000. Mike's car is worth three times as much. What is the total price of their cars?

Jessie baked 240 cookies for a fundraising event. During the event, 70% of the cookies were sold and Jessie ended up giving away 10% of the remaining cookies to her friends. How many cookies does she have left?
Greg drinks coffee almost every week. Last week, he drank 7 cups on Monday, 5 cups on Wednesday, and on Friday, he drank double the number of cups he drank on Monday. How many cups does Greg drink?
In a zoo, there are 200 animals. Out of these, 60% are mammals. Half of the mammals are herbivores. Out of these herbivore mammals, one-third are giraffes. How many giraffes are in the zoo?

In the playground, Alice found 10 pebbles and twice as many acorns as John. If John found 3 acorns and 4 pebbles, and both Alice and John only found acorns and pebbles in the playground, how many more things did Alice find than John?
Eva is preparing lunch for a big family gathering. She cooks 5 pizzas, each containing 8 slices. How many pizza slices does Eva have in total?
Michael bikes at 30 mph for 3 hours. Louise bikes at 40 mph for 2 hours. How many fewer miles does Michael bike than Louise?
Tim harvested 25 pounds of apples from his tree. He used 1/5 of the apples to make apple pies, and half of the remaining apples to make apple juice. How many pounds of apples does Tim still have?
Nadine collected different colored pebbles. She has 20 white pebbles and half as many red pebbles. How many pebbles does she have in all?

Alice is selling bags of popcorn for her school's charity event. If she sells 75% more on Tuesday than she sold on Monday, then she'll have sold a total of 175 bags on the two days. How many bags did she sell on Monday?
Hannah ran 5 kilometers each day for two days. On the third day, she ran twice as far. How many kilometers in total did Hannah run?
A bookshelf containing 120 books is to be divided equally among 4 classrooms. Then 10 new books arrive, and they are also distributed equally. How many books does each classroom receive?
Peter reads 4 chapters of a book every weekend. How many chapters will he have read after a quarter of a year?

John and Peter started a lemonade stand.  John sold seven cups of lemonade at $1 each cup and five lollipops at $0.50 each. Peter sold eight cups of lemonade and an additional five lollipops. How much total money did they make?
Peter wants to buy a new car that costs $20,000. He plans to trade in his old car and was offered $4,000 for it. How much should Peter pay for the new car after the trade-in?

In a school, there are three classes. Class A has 25 students, class B has 5 less than twice the number of students in class A and class C has 5 more than twice the number of students in class A. How many students are there in the school?
The combined age of twin brothers Peter and Paul is 18. Since they are twins, their ages are equal. How old is Peter?

Stella has a part-time job at a bookstore where she earns $10 per hour for the first 30 hours, after which she gets paid 1.5 times that amount per hour. How much does Stella earn in a week if she works for 45 hours?

Lisa started a 250-page book on Sunday and wants to finish it by Wednesday. She read 35 pages on Sunday, 63 pages on Monday, and 75 pages on Tuesday. If she wants to finish the book on Wednesday, how many pages does she have to read?
Perry has 20 less books than Mary, and Brian has twice as many books as Perry. If Mary has 56 books, how many books does Brian have?
Viktor and Sylvie played 20 rounds of chess. Viktor won 7 more rounds than Sylvie. How many rounds did Sylvie win?

For her 7th birthday party, Alice received 14 packs of stickers. Each pack contained 36 stickers. At the end of her party, there were 5 packs left. How many stickers did Alice and her friends use during the birthday party?
Jenny charges $15.00 per hour for a photography job. For her loyal customers, she provides a 20% discount. Ross is a loyal client who requires a job for 5 hours. How much will Ross pay?
Angela and Luis are biking. Angela bikes 45 miles in 3 hours. Louis bikes 75 miles in 5 hours. What is the speed (defined as miles per hour) of the biker with the higher average speed?

A family of 6 goes to a theme park. Only 4 of them rode the roller coaster which cost $7 each. All of them tried the bumper cars (at $4 per person). Then they bought 3 bags of popcorn each (each bag cost $6). How much did they spend in total?

Olivia and Sophie have a lemonade stand. Olivia made 12 cups of lemonade and 8 cups of orange juice. Sophie made 4 cups of lemonade less than Olivia, and 6 more cups of orange juice than Olivia. How many cups of drinks did they make in total?
Eliza has $1200 in her bank account and withdraws $20 for expenses everyday. After how many days will she run out of money?
Bella is 4 times older than Katy. If Katy is 7 years old, how old is Bella?
A local supermarket has a 20% discount on the price of the apples Olivia wants to purchase. If the apples initially costs $50, how much will she pay for the apples?

At the end of his shift, Joe counts his register. It contains 3 $100 bills, 2 $50 bills, 4 $20 bills, 5 $10 bills, 6 $5 bills, 30 $1 bills, and some change in coins. If he needs to leave $350 in notes and the change, how much money does he turn in to the main office?
Jake can paint a room in 5 hours. His friend, Tim, can paint the same room in 4 hours. If they work together, how long will it take for them to paint the room?
A box contains 24 blocks. One third of the blocks are green, one fourth of them are blue, the rest are red. How many blocks are red?
Jamie bought 8 boxes of apples that were sold at $6 per box. Each box came with 20 pieces of apples. She sold half of these boxes for $5 per 10 pieces. How much money did she make?

Benjamin owns 2 coffee shops. His first coffee shop serves 130 cups of coffee per day, and his second coffee shop serves 100 cups of coffee per day. How many cups of coffee do his 2 coffee shops serve in total in a week?
A box contains 40 pieces of candies. Each piece has a total weight of 5 grams. If Bill ate 4 pieces of candies, how much does the remaining candies weigh in grams?

Every day, James goes out to pick apples from the orchard. Each box he uses can store 15 apples and each day he fills 6 boxes. If he starts his business with 5 days of picking and selling apples, how many apples does he sell?
In one week, Andy's cat eats 3 pounds of food for every one pound of food that Andy eats. How much food do Andy and his cat eat in a month of 30 days if Andy eats 15 pounds of food each day?
A bus transports 50 passengers from city A to city B in two trips. The first trip takes 2/5 of the total passengers and the second trip takes the rest. How many passengers were on the second trip?
Mark bought 20 bananas, 15 apples, and 11 oranges. Mark ate 2 of each. How many fruits in total does he have left?

Laura baked some cupcakes for a bake sale. She baked 25 cupcakes on Monday, half as many on Tuesday, and 15 more than twice the number she baked on Monday on Wednesday. How many cupcakes did Laura bake in total?

To celebrate his son's graduation, George took him to a fancy restaurant. He ordered two appetizers for $10 each, a mouth-watering lobster for main course at $40, two glasses of white wine with dinner for $4 each, and two scoops of ice cream for dessert for $5. He had a discount coupon for 25% off the total bill, but he generously tipped the waiter a full 15% of what the full cost of the meal would have been without the discount. How much, including the tip, did George spend on dinner?

Jackson has a savings of $150. He decided to start a small business that costs $50 per month to maintain. If he didn't add any more money to his savings, how long will it take for his savings to be used up?

A pizza shop offers a large pizza for $20.00. They have a weekend special offering a 15% discount on their regular price. John wants to get 3 pizzas for his party. How much will he spend on 3 large pizzas?
Kim has 5 bags each containing 10 apples. If each apple weighs 0.5 kg, what is the total weight of all the apples in kg?
James deposited $200 into his bank account. Each year he earns 5% interest on the money in the account. How much money will be in James' bank account after 3 years?
Jim sold 4 tickets for $25 each and 3 tickets for $15 each. How much did Jim make from selling tickets?
A garden has 12 flower beds. Each bed has a length of 10 feet and a width of 6 feet. What is the total area of all the flower beds?
Rory has 6 toy cars and each car has 4 wheels. 3 of his toys broke down and lost all their wheels. How many wheels does Rory have left?
Larry baked 5 dozen cookies for his friends. He gave 3 dozens to his neighbors. How many cookies does he have left?
Sarah has twice as many apples as Mary. If Mary has 15 less than half of Sarah's apples, how many apples does Sarah have?
Jonah got paid $500 for 20 hours of work. His friend Aidan received $100 more than Jonah. If Jonah saves half of his earnings and Aidan saves a third of his earnings, how much did they save in total?
A translator can translate 30 pages per day. If 1500 pages must be translated in a 5-day week, how many translators must be hired for that week?
Emily saw 8 puppies at the park. Each puppy weighs 2 pounds. If Emily picked up each puppy twice, how much weight in total did Emily pick up?

Jessica's family decides to have a picnic at the park. They order pizza which costs $20 per pizza, and they order 4 pizzas. They also get juice boxes which are $1.50 each and they get 10 juice boxes. Jessica's brother also decides to get chips which cost $3.00 per bag and he gets 2 bags. How much did they end up spending for the food and drinks for the picnic?

In a farmhouse there are 3 types of animals: cows, pigs and chickens. There is a total of 300 animals in the farmhouse. Cows make up 20% of all the animals, and pigs 30%. The number of chickens equal to the number of cows and pigs combined. How many chickens are there in the farmhouse?
Sam splits his birthday cake into 16 equal slices. His friends ate one-third of the cake and the rest were saved. How many slices of cake were saved?
Melanie receives 300 messages in her email daily, half of which are promotional. 1/5 of the remaining emails are spam. How many non-promotional, non-spam emails does she receive?

Emma is a seamstress. She has 100 meters of fabric. She uses 3/4 of the fabric to make dresses and 1/10 of the fabric to make skirts. The rest of the fabric will be used to make blouses. How many meters of fabric will she use for the blouses?
Tim packed 5 apples in his school lunch every day. After eating lunch at school for 4 days, he went on a hike and packed 12 apples for the trip. How many apples did he eat in total?
Jacob is 5 years old. He is 1/4 as old as his brother and his brother is 3/4 as old as their sister. How old is their sister?

Thomas wants to buy a car that costs $20,000. He already has saved $5,000 and plans to finance the rest. If the finance company offers a loan with a 3% interest per year, and Thomas plans to pay the loan in 5 years, how much would the total amount be including interest?
Fred has 80 candies. He gave away 25% to his little sister. How many candies did Fred give to his little sister?

A construction company has 4 teams working to complete a project. Each team has a front loader and a backhoe. A front loader can move twice as much dirt as a backhoe. If a backhoe can move 8 hectares of dirt each day, how much dirt can all four teams move in a day?

James has a spotify premium subscription for $10 per month. If he upgrades to the family plan, it will cost an additional $5 per month. Moreover, his internet costs $30 per month, which is $10 less per month than the music streaming service. If James upgrades the music streaming service and continues the internet, what will his total monthly cost be?
Bob charges $30 per repair session plus $10 per hour. The total amount paid to Bob for one repair session is $100. How many hours did Bob repair for?
Kevin has 200 apples and 3/4 as many oranges as apples. Joe and Sam, two of his friends, visit, and he gives each of them 1/4 of the fruits. How many fruits is Kevin left with in total?

100 parents participated in a survey about their children's after-school activities. 30% said their child participates in musical instruction, 20% said athletics, and 5% gave other options. The remaining parents said their child participates in arts and crafts. How many parents responded that their child participates in arts and crafts?
John buys 15 apples. He eats 3 apples and gives 1/3 of the remaining apples to his friend. How many apples does John have left?
Peter's car travels 20 miles per gallon of gas. He has 15 gallons of gas in his car. How many miles can he travel without stopping for gas?

When all his clothes are washed and in his drawer, Jason has 15 shirts, 9 pants, and 12 shorts.  Today in his hamper, he has 60% of his shirts, 40% of his pants, and 30% of his shorts.  How many pieces of clothing will he need to put in the washing machine?

Luke runs a startup software company. The company releases one product every half a year. Each product sale earns Luke 200 dollars. Luke received an order for 1200 dollars worth of product. If he has already sold 600 dollars worth of product, how many half years will it be before he can fulfill this order?

There are 80 fish in an aquarium.  Mrs. Green's biology class normally has 20 students, but 5 students were absent that day. The remaining students get to take home 3 fish each. How many fish are still left in the aquarium?
Four months ago, John earned 15 times more money than Sarah. If his salary has increased by 20 percent now, and Sarah's salary then was $800, calculate John's salary now.

Jane has two jobs. At her first job, she earns $8 an hour and at her second job she earns 2 times as much as her first job. If she works 5 hours at her first job and 7 hours at her second job one week, how much does she earn in total?

A strawberry field has 9 rows of strawberry plants. If farmer Bob picks 120 strawberries from each row and gives away 15 strawberries from every row to his neighbours, how many strawberries does he have left from the entire field?
Helen and Mary decided to plan a garden. They planted 16 roses, each in a separate area. If a row can have only 4 roses, how many rows did they make?
A theater has 250 seats. If 60% of the seats are filled, how many people are in the theater?
Robert started reading a new 3500-page book. He reads 500 pages on the weekends and 250 pages on weekdays. How many weeks does it take him to read the whole book?
A bakery uses 5 kg of flour to bake 12 cakes. If the bakery has 30 kg of flour, how many cakes can it bake?
David is 22 years old. His brother Steven is four years younger than him. How old is Steven going to be when David is 30 years old?
A bicycle has 2 wheels and each wheel has 36 spokes. If 10% of the spokes on each wheel are damaged and need to be replaced, how many spokes in total need replacement?
In December, the lake near the park is 6 feet deep. The lake's depth doubles every month. How deep is the lake in February?

Missy's band is performing in 9 different cities. In the first city they sold 10 t-shirts, and in each subsequent city, they sold 5 more t-shirts than in the previous city. How many t-shirts did they sell in total?
If the diameter of a circle is 14 cm, what is the radius of the circle?

Alex is preparing a salad. He is using 7 tomatoes for his salad. First he cuts each tomato into 10 large slices. Then he takes half of those slices and cuts them into 2 smaller pieces each. How many slices and pieces of tomatoes total is Alex going to add to his salad?
Julia and Caleb are donating books to a local library. Julia is donating 3 books and Caleb is donating twice as much as Julia. How many books they are donating altogether?
Tom bakes 4 batches of cookies, with each batch yielding 36 cookies. He wants to give half of the cookies to his friends. How many cookies will he give away?
David likes to bake muffins. He makes 5 batches each week, with each batch containing 3-dozen muffins. If he sells 2/3 of the muffins, how many muffins does he have remaining?
Daniel has a truck that can carry a maximum of 720 pounds of goods. If each box he delivers weighs 36 pounds, how many boxes can he deliver in one trip?
Maria collected 60 shells from the beach. She gave 12 shells to her sister and 18 shells to her brother. How many shells does Maria have left?
Each bag of oranges contains 10 oranges. Billy needs to buy enough oranges to make 60 servings of orange juice, and each serving requires 2 oranges. How many bags of oranges does he need to buy?

In a mathematics test having 50 questions, four points are given for each correct answer and two points are deducted for each incorrect answer. If a certain student gives all correct answers, how many points will he get?
A beach has four times as many seashells as pebbles. If there are 20 pebbles, what is the total number of pebbles and seashells together?
Stephanie has just as many candies as Bella, who has 3 more candies than Benny. After eating 2 candies, Benny has 5 candies left. How many candies does Stephanie have at the start?
Michael has 4 cats. They eat an average of 12 pounds of food a week. One cat eats 8 pounds a week. Another one eats double this. How much does each of the remaining two cats eat per week?
Michael reads 12 pages of a book every day. This week, he read 1.5 times more than last week. If one book consists of 15 pages, how many books did Michael read in two weeks?
Mila is planning a party for her friends. She is planning to order 12 pizzas, if each pizza costs $10 and she also order beverages at $40. How much total will she spend for the party?
Alan earns twice as much as Bob does. If Bob earns $10 per hour, how much does Alan earn in 6 days working 6 hours a day?

A fast food place sells pizza slices at $3, a burger at $5, a hot dog at $2, and a soft drink at $1 per can. Jimmy buys two pizza slices and a soft drink. Sam buys a burger and two soft drinks. How much do Jimmy and Sam spend together?
A car travels at a consistent speed of 60 mph. How far in miles will it travel in 8 hours?

John has a big fish tank with many fish. He feeds them every day. His guppies eat 6 pellets each, his tetras eat 3 pellets each, and his angelfish eat 15 pellets each. If he has 5 guppies, 10 tetras, and 2 angelfish, how many total pellets does John need in a day?
A book on a reading list has a total of 320 pages. Jack has read a quarter of the book. How many pages does he have left to read?
Ravi went on his bicycle to the hardware store. He pedaled 3 miles to the shop, 2 miles to his friend's house, and 1 less than five miles back home. How many miles did Ravi cycle?
Charlie and Sam are fishing. Charlie catches 5 fish and Sam catches double what Charlie catches. Afterwards they both release all their fish. How many fish did Charlie and Sam catch in total?

Jennifer has 120 marbles. She gave half of them to her friend. Then she realized she lost 10 marbles. From the remaining ones, she has to give a quarter to her cousin. How many marbles will Jennifer have left after giving to her cousin?
John has 3 apples. His friend Jake gives him 4 more apples. Then his friend Mary gives him twice as many apples as Jake did. How many apples does John have now?
There are 7 red apples and 10 green apples in the basket. Stella ate 3 red apples and 2 green apples. How many apples are left in the basket?
On his weekend trip, Peter drove 140 miles on Saturday and 200 miles on Sunday. How many more miles does he need to drive on Monday to reach a total of 450 miles for the trip?

Baxter gets 1/4 of a cat cookie in the morning and before bed.  He gets 1 whole cookie during the day.  Baxter’s cookies are packaged with 50 cookies per box.  How many boxes will he need to last him for 21 days?
A train travels at a speed of 80 km/h. If its speed is reduced by 20 km/h due to long-term wearing, how long will it take for the train to travel 240 km?
Joanna baked 100 cupcakes that she wants to sell at a school event. Each cupcake costs $1.50. If she sold all of them, how much money would she make?
A company produces 600 paper boxes each week. The production cost for each box is $7. If they sell each box at $20, how much profit does the company make each week?

Nick collects antique coins. His collection has 25 silver coins, 15 bronze, and 40 copper coins. The silver coins cost $5, the bronze are $2.50, and the copper are $1.00 each. How much did his collection cost?
Stacy goes to yoga class 4 times a week. Each class is 90 minutes and she burns 5 calories per minute. How many calories does she burn in a week?
John had 250 dollars. Mary gave him another $150. John then paid Alice $350 and kept the rest. If John has $300 left, how much money did John have at first?
Jasmine invested $600 in Bitcoin. In the first week, it increased by 10%. In the following week, it decreased by 20%. What is the value of her investment now?
Jenny has 3 more picture frames than Sarah. Sarah has 4 times as many picture frames as Ben. If Ben has 2 picture frames, how many does Jenny have?

David has a box of gloves with 30 fewer pairs than a box of ear plugs. He decides to put 50 more pairs of ear plugs into the box of ear plugs. If the box of gloves has 200 pairs of gloves, how many ear plugs are there?

A small bakery sells cupcakes for $3 each, pretzels for $2 each, and hot chocolate for $1 each. Mike buys three cupcakes and a pretzel, while Lola buys a cupcake and a hot chocolate. How much do Mike and Lola spend in total?
In a certain factory, each worker on the assembly line contributes to building 5% of a car. If there are 35 employees on the assembly line, how many cars are they able to build?

Dora is preparing lunch for her 15 friends. Each friend will eat 3 sandwiches. If each pack of bread has 10 slices, and every sandwich requires 2 slices of bread, how many packs of bread does she need to buy?
Sarah is studying for her exams and her reading speed is 40 pages per hour. If she is reading a book of 180 pages, how many hours will it take her to finish reading the entire book?
Sarah has 150g of sugar in a jar, 200g of sugar in a bag, and 250g of sugar in a box. If one cupcake requires 100g of sugar, how many cupcakes can Sarah bake?
Michael reads 50 pages of a book every day. If his book has 450 pages, how many days will it take him to finish the book?
Ellen has bought 70 square feet of wooden planks to build shelves. She knows that each shelf consumes 5 square feet of the wooden plank and 6 hours to build. How many hours will she have to work?
Ella has 5 cherry trees in her backyard. Each tree gives 48 cherries. If she eats 23 cherries and distributes the remaining cherries equally among 3 friends, how many cherries does each friend get?

A tailor had 200 meters of fabric. He used 50 meters for making sofa covers and 75 meters for making curtains. Out of the remaining fabric, he sold half of it. How much fabric is left with the tailor?
A user on a website can earn 25 points for writing a review, and 10 points for adding a photo. If Sam wants to earn 300 points, how many reviews does he have to write if he also adds 2 photos?

Adam baked 15 dozen cookies for a charity bake sale. Only half of the cookies were sold in the morning and Adam decided to lower the price for the remaining cookies. How many cookies are left for sale in the afternoon?
Bob wants to distribute apples among his 8 friends. If Bob has 3 apples and each apple can be divided into 5 equal parts, how many parts of apple will each friend get?
Linda has $56 in her savings. If Linda buys a toy for $6 and a book for $3. How much money does Linda have left?

Maria has a baking business. One week, she bakes 200 cupcakes for a wedding, 150 for a birthday party and plans to bake twice that amount for a big event. If she has baked some amount for her shop and her total count for the week is 800 cupcakes, how many did she bake for her shop?
Tom is building a wall with bricks. The wall has 20 bricks laid horizontally and 20 vertically. Tom has already placed 50% of the bricks. How many more bricks does he need to place?
Samantha sells handmade candles. Each candle costs her $2.25 to make and she sells them at $4.50 each. How much profit does she make if she sold 150 candles?
A math tutor gives 8 lessons daily and works 20 days every month. For each lesson, the tutor is paid $10. How much does the tutor earn in total after working for 3 months?

Linda is a garden decorator who charges $70/hr for her services. She was hired to work on a project that involved redesigning 5 gardens. She took 2 hours for each garden. If she was also paid an additional $30 as transportation charges, how much did she earn in total?

Lisa practiced ballet for 90 minutes. She spent 30 minutes on warm-up exercises and 25 minutes on practicing turns. She spent the rest of the time practicing her routine. How many minutes did she spend on her routine?
John made a rectangular garden plot that is 8 feet long and 12 feet wide. He wants to build a fence around the plot. If the fence costs $5 per foot, how much will it cost him to build the fence?
John bought 24 magic cards, each costing $3. He later sold them all, making a profit of $30. How much did he sell each card for?
John had 200 daffodils in his garden. He sold half of them at the local market and gave a quarter of the rest to his neighbor. How many daffodils does he have left?
A bookstore sells books, CDs, and DVDs. A book costs $5, a CD costs $10, and a DVD costs $20. If David buys 3 books, 2 CDs, and 4 DVDs, how much money does he spend?

Andy, Bob, and Charlie went on a bike trip that was 120 miles in total. Andy biked 30 miles more than twice the amount Bob biked, and Bob biked 4 times the amount Charlie biked. How many miles did Bob bike?

Kelly's bookstore sold 15 books every day. During the weekend, due to a sale, Kelly managed to sell twice as much as their daily average. The bookstore is closed on every Monday. How many books did Kelly sell in a week?
Bill and Jack are running in a race. Bill runs at a speed of 8 mph and Jack runs at a speed of 6 mph. If the race is 26 miles long, how much faster does Bill finish the race than Jack?
John collects stamps. He has 50 of them, which is half of the amount Ben has, and Ben has three times as many stamps as Sam. How many stamps does all of them have?

Emily reads 20 pages of a novel in 15 minutes. Her twin sister can read double the number of pages that Emily reads in the same time. Their little brother can read half the number of pages that Emily reads in 15 minutes. How many pages can their little brother read?
Lily has 5 more books than Sarah. Sarah has twice as many books as Tom. If Tom has 4 books, how many does Lily have?

If Paul's house is thrice as far from Bella's house as from Lily's house along a particular road and Paul's house is 6 miles away from Bella's house along this road, what is the total distance Paul will cover if he goes from his house to Bella's then Lily's and then back to Bella's before returning to his house?
A bus travels a total of 530 miles in 2 days. On the first day, it travelled 1.5 times more miles than on the second day. How many miles did the bus travel on the second day?

A video rental store charges $1 per day for every movie rented. In June, Sarah rented two movies to watch at home. If she returned one movie after 10 days, and the other one stayed at her house until the end of June, how much did she pay to the rental store for the movies?
Kate wants to buy a special pen as a gift for her friend. The pen costs $30, and Kate only has money for a third of that amount. How much more money does she need to buy the pen?
Maggie collects stamps as a hobby. She has 10 times more US stamps than international stamps. If she has 110 stamps in total, how many international stamps does she have?

John has 30 apple trees in his orchard. He has twice as many red apple trees as green apple trees. The rest of his trees are yellow apple tree. If John has 12 red apple trees, how many yellow apple trees does he have?
A pizza place sells one whole large pizza for $15. Sally bought 2 pizzas for her party, whereas Jenny bought twice as many pizzas as Sally did. How much did Jenny spend on pizzas?
At the beginning of the year, Timmy had 365 baseball cards. During the year, he bought 50 more and he sold 30. How many baseball cards does he have at the end of the year?
Bethany spends 5 minutes adjusting her canvas and 4 times that amount of time painting. How much time does she spend on these activities total?
Joe has a small business selling T-Shirts online. Each T-Shirt costs him $7 to make, and he sells them for $20 each. If he sold 150 T-Shirts last month, how much profit did he make?

Anne has 120 beads in three different colours. Each colour has the same number of beads. If Anne donates 10 red beads, twice as many green beads, and three times as many blue beads than the red ones, how many beads does she have left?
A cyclist is traveling at a speed of 12 miles per hour. If her destination is 36 miles away and she stops for a 15-minute break, how long will her journey take in hours?

An ice cream shop sells small-sized ice cream for $3 each, medium-sized for $5 and a jumbo-sized for $8. Bella bought 2 small-sized, 3 medium-sized, and 1 jumbo-sized ice cream. How much did Bella spend for the ice cream?
In a 5000 meters race, Mary was ahead of Lisa by 12%. If Lisa finished the race in 12 minutes, how long did Mary take to finish the race?
If one box of chalk contains 20 pieces and one package of markers contains 8 pieces, how many pieces of chalk and markers will you have if you buy 5 boxes of chalk and 12 packages of markers?

A class has 60 minutes for a math lesson. If the teacher spends 15 minutes explaining the new concept, 20 minutes for the students to do practice problems, and 10 minutes to check their work, how much free time do the students have?
Lisa has a jar filled with 20 candy pieces. Every day she eats 2 candy pieces. After a week, she decides to add 15 more candies to the jar. How many candies does she have in the jar after one week?
Mary baked 200 cookies for a bake sale. She sold 75% of the cookies by noon. How many cookies does she have left?
George makes lemonade in jugs. He uses 4 lemons for each jug. One day, George decided to make 15 jugs of lemonade. Unfortunately, he only has 45 lemons. How many jugs of lemonade can George make?
Fifteen years ago, Jessica was four times older than Vanessa is today. If Vanessa is currently 10, how old is Jessica now?
Lisa spends 15 minutes every morning and 20 minutes every evening on her skincare routine. How many hours does she spend on her skincare routine in a week?
Robin spent 35% of his salary on rent, 25% of it on groceries and saved the rest. If he saved $1800, what was his total salary?
An amusement park ticket costs $50. If a group of 5 friends decides to go and they all get a group discount of 10%, how much will they pay in total?
A baker makes 4 types of cakes. Each type requires 5 pounds of flour. If a bag of flour costs $3 and contains 2 pounds, how much does the baker spend on flour to make one of each type of cake?
Dante started reading a book that has 840 pages. He read 300 pages in the first week, and 120 pages in the second week. How many pages does he still have to read?
There are 4 packs of yogurt. Each pack contains 6 yogurts. If Kelly eats 3 yogurts every day, how many days will the yogurts last?
A bus can seat 40 people. If an elementary school needs to send 225 students on a field trip, how many buses will the school need to hire?

On Monday, Sarah collected 6 shells at the beach. On Tuesday, she collected twice as many as she did on Monday. On Wednesday, she collected 2 fewer shells than she did on Tuesday. How many more shells did Sarah collect on Wednesday than on Monday?
There are 50 puppies in a dog shelter. Four-fifths of them are poodles. How many poodles need to be adopted out to reduce this proportion to half?

Martha loves to read books. She reads 5 chapters a day. After a week, she decided to take her reading up a notch and started reading 2 additional chapters a day. After another week, how many chapters has she read in total?

John got twelve points for every right answer in the science quiz. He solved 7 problems correctly and got a bonus of 5 points for each problem solved within the time limit. The bonus applied to all the problems John solved correctly. How many points did John score in total?

Jack went to the gym and found 3 thirty pounds weights set up for weightlifting. He added one more weights to the setup and started his exercises. How many pounds of weights is Jack using for his exercises?
1 ounce of coffee can make 2 cups of coffee. Matt, Jake, Bella and Alice each want 3 cups of coffee for their study session. How many ounces of coffee will they need?
Three buckets can hold 16 liters of water each. If two buckets are filled to their brims and the last one is half full, how many liters of water are there in total?

An office building has two parking lots. One has 65 spaces, and the other has 35 spaces. If 40 cars are parked and 1/3 of the spaces in the smaller lot are filled, how many parking spaces are still free?
A farm has 150 cows. Each cow produces 20 litres of milk every day. How many litres of milk is produced in 30 days?
Carrie picked 12 marigolds from her garden. Each bouquet she makes has 3 marigolds. If she needs 4 additional marigolds from the store to make 5 bouquets, how many marigolds does Carrie have?

Andy spent 6 hours working on his school project. He drank 1 litre of water every hour and also used an additional 3 litres of water to mix his paints. How many litres of water did Andy drink/use in total?
A school library contains 100 books. 30% are science books. How many science books are in the library?

Oliver is saving money for a new video game which costs $60. Oliver has only half of the money he needs. His parents decided to give him $10 for that purpose, and his friends four times as much as his parents. How much more money does Oliver need to buy the game?

Martinez bought a new coffee table that can only hold up to 60 pounds of weight. Martinez has 50 magazines that each weigh 1 pound, 6 glasses that each weigh 2 pounds, and 4 books that each weigh 10 pounds. How many pounds over the coffee table's weight limit is this total collection of items?
Each box contains 24 bottles of soft drinks. If the store received a delivery of 500 boxes, how many bottles of soft drinks were delivered?
A school has 500 students. 20% of the students walk to school. Of the students who don't walk to school, 75% take a school bus. How many students take the school bus?
The canteen served 300 sandwiches. Only 30% of the sandwiches is chicken sandwich, the rest were ham and cheese. How many ham and cheese sandwiches did they serve?
A pizza delivery guy has to deliver 40 pizzas. He delivers them to 10 houses on a street. 3 of the houses have blue gates and 2 have green gates. How many pizzas will each of those houses get?

Billy works in a car dealership. He sold 8 cars in the first week and twice as many in the second week. If Billy's commission is $500 for each car sold, how much did he earn in commissions over these two weeks?
Sam organizes a film screening for 7 friends. Snacks cost $2 per packet. If he wants all his friends to have 3 packets each, how much will it cost?
When Zoe swims, she does 8 laps per minute. She swims for 15 minutes each session and does 3 sessions each week. How many kicks will Zoe do in 4 weeks?
Jane baked 4 dozen cupcakes for a charity event. Half of these cupcakes are chocolate flavored, a quarter are vanilla and the rest are strawberry. How many strawberry cupcakes did she bake?
Paul has twice the number of apples as Jenny. If Paul gives away 4 apples, he will have 12 apples left. How many apples does Jenny have?

A gardener is planning a flower bed consisting of roses, tulips, and daisies. She needs twice as many tulip bulbs as rose bushes and three times as many daisy seeds as tulip bulbs. If she needs 10 rose bushes, how many total plants does she need?

A lemonade stand collects $120 in revenue from selling lemonade at $3 a cup. If they decide to offer a promotional discount of 25% for the next 10 cups, how much revenue will they earn after selling those 10 cups?
Jessica wants to buy a new bicycle that costs $125. She has been saving $15 every month for 8 months. How much more does she need to save to buy the bicycle?

Tom is hosting a game night and is expecting 20 guests. He would like to ensure everyone, including himself, gets at least 3 cans of soda. Each can contains 10 ounces of soda. How many ounces of soda does he need to buy for the game night?
In Melissa's garden, there were initially 12 flowers. Every week, the number of flowers doubles. How many flowers will there be after 4 weeks?
A farmer has 540 acres of land. He dedicated one third of this land for corn growing. The rest is for wheat planting. How many acres of land are dedicated for wheat planting?

A company has 3 offices in different locations. The first office has 50 employees, the second office has 35 employees and the third office has 15 employees. Each employee is to be given a $20 bonus. How much does the company spend on bonuses?

Sally plants 5 seeds, each of which sprouts 3 flowers. She picks and sells a dozen flowers for $24, then uses half her profits to buy seeds that sprout twice as many flowers. How much does she earn from selling all the flowers sprouted from the seeds she bought?
Jenna baked 48 cupcakes for a bake sale. She sold 12 in the morning and twice as many in the afternoon. How many cupcakes does Jenna have left?
In a classroom, the ratio of girls to boys is 3:5. If the total number of students in the classroom is 64, how many girls are there in the classroom?
Alex bought 3 t-shirts for each of his 4 friends. If each t-shirt costs 15 dollars, how much did Alex spend in total?
In a train there are 100 seats. If 3/4 of the seats are occupied, how many seats are vacant?
Harry drives 30 miles to work every day. He also goes to the gym 5 miles away before driving back home. If he works 5 days a week, how many miles does he drive in a week?

Alan is building a wooden box that required 15 wooden planks. Each plank costs $8.5. He also needed to buy nails which cost $5.30 per pack. He decided to buy 3 packs of nails. How much will Alan spend in total?
In eight years, Jacob will be three times John's age then. If Jacob is 16 now, how old is John?
A school library has 5 bookshelves. Each bookshelf holds 30 adventure books, 20 science books, and 25 history books. How many total books are there in the library?

Susan is creating a necklace with beads, each bead is 10 cm long, the string is 5 times the length of two beads, and she will need 3 feet of extra string for knotting. If string costs $4/m, how much does Susan spend on string?
A box of pencils contains 12 pencils. If John has 5 boxes and he gives 2 boxes to his friends, how many pencils does John have left?
A taxi ride costs $2.50 for the first mile and $0.75 for each additional mile. If Jane took a taxi ride that cost her $6.75, how many miles did she travel?
A baker uses 3 eggs for one cake. If he has 60 eggs and uses them all for baking, how many cakes does he get?

Aiden stores his summer clothes in 3 boxes, each box contains 18 pieces of clothing. For the winter, he uses 2 boxes, each with 15 pieces of clothing. How many pieces of clothing does Aiden have in total?
Maggie's toy box has 100 plush toys. She gives 10% of her toys to her neighbor. Among the remaining toys, 15% are dinosaurs. How many dinosaur toys are left in Maggie's toy box?
Lucy has 50 roses and 30 daisies in her garden. She decides to give 5 roses and 2 daisies to each of her friends. If she has 10 friends, how many flowers does she have left?
If Joel can paint 5 rooms per day with his paint sprayer or decorate 7 rooms per day with the same tool, how many days would it take him to paint 30 rooms and decorate 21 rooms?

The cinema ticket price is $10 per person and a minimum of 5 people is allowed in a group. If Jack has paid $200 for the tickets, by how many people has Jack’s group exceed the minimum allowed people?
A tennis player wins $200 every time he wins a match. He lost 3 matches and won 7 matches. How much money did he make?

Ryan and Kyle are playing a card game. They must draw 3 cards and add the values of the 3 cards. Ryan draws cards with the following values: 3, 7, and 6. On Kyle's turn, he draws 2, 6, and X. How much value must Kyle's last card have so that he has more points than Ryan?
A market has four stalls, and each stall sells twice as many apples as the number of oranges in all stalls. If there are 40 oranges in each stall, what's the total number of fruits in all stalls?
Max is saving money to buy a new game that costs $60. He already has $15. He makes $10 each week doing chores. How many weeks does Max need to work to afford the game?

The movie Out of an Abyss is 15 minutes longer than the movie Under the Neon, which is 50 minutes shorter than the movie Arizona Drive. If Arizona Drive runs for 1 hour and 45 minutes, what is the running time of Out of an Abyss in minutes?
John has 2 baseball cards and his brother gives him 3 more. How many baseball cards does John have now?
There are 12 white roses, 3 times more pink roses than white roses, and 5 less yellow roses than double the pink roses. How many yellow roses are there?

Emily is buying supplies for a school science project. She purchases 5 poster boards at $2 each; 3 packs of markers for $1.50 each; 4 packs of stickers for $0.75 each, and; 2 science kits for $5 each. How much did Emily spend in total?
A car uses 1 gallon of gas for every 20 miles it drives. If the car has 16 gallons of gas left in the tank, how far can the car drive before it runs out of gas?

Alex's cliff has a height of 5,000 feet above sea level. Toby's cliff has a height that is 1,000 feet shorter than Alex's cliff. If Alex climbed his cliff 4 times, how many times would Toby need to climb his cliff to have climbed the same number of feet as Alex?

James went on a road trip. He traveled one quarter of the total distance on the first day, half of the remaining distance on the second day, and the rest on the third day. If the total distance was 800 miles, how many miles did he travel on the third day?
Emily bought 7 apples from the grocery store. Her brother Jack took 3 apples. How many apples does Emily have now?
A bakery sells cupcakes for $4 each and pies for $10 each. If a customer buys two cupcakes and one pie, how much will she pay?
Brian had read 3/5 of a book when he was interrupted. After he was able to continue reading, he read another 1/5 of the book. What fraction of the book has he read so far?
A store sells a comic book for $7 each. A student buys 5 comic books for his friends. How much does he spend in total?

A lorry carries 200 boxes each trip. It takes the lorry 5 trips to transport all the boxes. If an error occurred and an additional 2 fifths of the total number of boxes was added, how many boxes needed to be transported?
Mary spent $15 on a book and 4 times that amount on a purse. How much money does she spend in total?
A jelly factory produces 360 jars of jelly daily. One day, it produced 15% more jelly jars than usual due to a special order. How many jars of jelly were produced that day?
Andy draws a comic book that has 20 pages. If he draws for 4 hours each day, and it takes him 2 days to finish a page, how many days will it take him to finish the comic book?

Mr. Thompson teaches history in two grades in school. There are three times more students in Mr. Thompson's grade 4 class as compared to his grade 2 class. The number of students in grade 8 class is twice that in the grade 4 class. If the grade 2 class has 30 students, how many students does Mr. Thompson teach?
Andrea has 5 apples. Her brother Alex has 3 times as many apples as Andrea, but 2 fewer apples than their sister Alyssa. How many apples do they have altogether?
A museum had 300 visitors last Friday and 60 more visitors on Saturday than on Friday. Calculate the total number of people who went to the museum over the two days.
Michael buys a packet of 12 pencils, and then he buys another packet of 15 pencils. He gives 5 pencils to his friend. How many pencils does Michael have left?

A farm has 5 chickens. It has 2 times the number of cows than chickens and 3 times the number of sheep than cows. The number of pigs is a third of the number of cows and chickens combined, and there are 2 fewer horses than pigs. What is the difference in number between the horses and the sheep?
Dave spends $20 a week on transportation. If Dave continues this spending pattern, how much will he have spent on transportation after 10 weeks?
Daisy made 30 cookies. She gave Mike 10 cookies and gave 5 to Lisa. How many cookies does Daisy have left?
Alex's soccer team scored 315 points throughout the year. There are 7 players on the team. If 2 of them averaged 70 points each, how many points did the rest average?
Samantha organized a marathon with her friends. She ran 3 miles, Jack ran 1.5 times more than Samantha and Mike ran half the distance of Jack. How many miles did they run in total?
Joshua needs 50% more protein than he takes now. If he's currently taking 60 grams of protein each day, how much protein will he have to take every day?

At a farm, Bella gathers eggs from a henhouse. Each hen lays 4 eggs each day. Bella collects double the eggs from the hens on the first day. On the first day, Bella collects 8 eggs. How many eggs from the hen will Bella collect in two days?
A cinema has 10 rows of seats. There are 15 seats in each row. One ticket costs $8. If all the seats are full, how much does the cinema earn in one screening?
Lucy and Jack decided to start a small garden. They need to plant 200 seeds. Each packet of seeds costs $2 and contains 25 seeds. How many dollars will they spend on seeds?
A conservationist is tagging birds in a park. If she tags 15 birds every day for five days, how many birds have been tagged?
A stationery store sells pencils in sets of 10. Each set costs $3. A teacher needs 150 pencils for her class. How much will she need to pay?

Sarah is planning a road trip that is 780 miles long. She can travel for 520 miles with a full tank of gas. If it costs $45 to fill up her gas tank, how much will it cost her in gas for the whole trip?
John works at a pizza shop and makes $12 an hour. Additionally, he gets tips of approximately $5 each hour. How much does he earn in an 8-hour shift?

Karl runs a small cafe selling assorted sandwich bundles. He makes a 15% profit on each bundle he sells. This week, he sold $3000 worth of sandwich bundles. Seventy percent of his earnings will be used to pay for ingredients and the rest will go to his savings. How much will Karl save this week?

A car manufacturing company produces 3 types of vehicles every day: cars, minibuses and buses. Every day, they manufacture 7 cars, 3 minibuses and 1 bus. How many vehicles do they manufacture per week if they work 6 days a week?
John and Peter have $20 each. They both bought pencils that cost $3 each. How much money in total do they have left?
James has $50. He spends $20 on food, saves $10, and uses the rest of his money for transportation. How much money does he spend on transportation?

Out of 60 people in a theater, 15 are wearing hats. The rest of the people are wearing glasses and scarves. The number of people wearing scarves is 3 times as many as the people wearing hats. How many people are wearing glasses?

Alex is taking a business trip. He drives for three days, refueling his car each day when he needs it. He visits 3 different gas stations in total, with the price of gas being $2.80, $3.10, and $3.40 respectively at each. If Alex's car has a 15-gallon tank, how much does he spend on gas for his trip?
Lucas wants to buy a large pizza that costs $20. He invites 8 of his friends to share the cost. If everyone pays the same amount, how much will each person need to pay for their share of the pizza?
