A parking lot has 8 cars and three times as many trucks. The number of motorcycles is 4 less than the trucks and twice as many as the bikes. How many vehicles are there in the lot?
A quarter of the students in a classroom are boys, and the rest are girls. If there are 32 students in total, how many of them are girls?
Jenna has a jar of coins. There are 150 quarters and 200 dimes in the jar. Quarters are worth 25 cents and dimes are worth 10 cents. How much money in dollars is Jenna's jar worth?
Ali is about to bake a cake and he needs to add four cups of flour and two cups of sugar. If each cup of flour costs $3 and each cup of sugar costs $2, how much does he pay total?
Mrs. Riley bought twice as many cookies for her elder daughter as she bought for her younger son. If the younger son received 30 cookies, how many cookies did Mrs Riley buy?
Isabel has $204 in her piggy bank. She spent half the amount and bought a toy. She then spent half of the remaining money and bought her brother a book. How much money, in dollars, was left over?
Greg subscribes to a music streaming service which costs $10 per month. His brother decides to split the cost with him. How much will Greg have to pay in total for the service during a year?

In a basketball game, Olivia scored twice as many points as Lily. Meanwhile, Mandy scored 5 points less than Olivia and Lily combined. If the total points scored by all the three players was 65, how many points did Lily score in the game?
Oliver's computer was bought on the same day as Eve turned 6 years old. Eve is exactly 3 years older than Oliver. On Oliver's 7th birthday, how many years old was his computer?

A car travels on a straight road for 8 hours at an average speed of 70 kilometers per hour. Then it takes a 2-hour break before driving for another 4 hours at an average of 60 kilometers per hour. How far, in kilometers, has the car traveled in total?
Caleb bought 3 dozen bananas. He gave one dozen to his friend and sold half of the remainder to his neighborhood. If there are 12 bananas in one dozen, how many are left for Caleb?

Rick's vegetable garden has 30 squash plants, twice as many tomato plants as squash plants, and 20 fewer cucumber plants than tomato plants. How many plants does Rick have in his vegetable garden in total?

A diner has a lunch rush and needs to make sandwiches for a large table that has arrived. 4 local businesses have each ordered 12 turkey sandwiches and third of the table that has arrived have asked for 6 turkey sandwiches each. If the diner makes a total of 72 turkey sandwiches, how many customers are at the large table that arrived?
At a party, each person eats 3 slices of pizza. If there are 5 people at the party and each pizza has 8 slices, how many pizzas do they need?
Alyssa is packing for a two-week trip. She plans to pack 6 t-shirts, 4 pairs of shorts, and a number of dresses. If her suitcase can hold 22 articles of clothing, how many dresses can she pack?

Two trains, Train A and Train B, left from the same station at the same time and traveled in the same direction. Train A traveled at a speed of 60 miles per hour and Train B traveled at 80 miles per hour. After 3 hours, how far apart are the two trains?

If there were 150 students who passed a Maths course three years ago, and each subsequent year until the current one that number increased by 70% of the previous year's number, how many students will pass the course this year?
John has a candy store where he sells chocolates at $1.50 each, and lollipops at $0.75 each. He sold 30 chocolates and 40 lollipops in one day. How much money did he make?
A fruit vendor has 15 apples, 22 oranges, and 12 bananas. If he packs the fruit into baskets containing 10 pieces each, how many baskets will he be able to fill?
There are 120 eggs in a crate. If a restaurant uses an average of 15 eggs per day, how many days would it take for them to use all the eggs?
Lisa’s bamboo plant grows 4 new shoots 3 times a year. After 5 years, how many new shoots will the bamboo plant have produced?

Jared uses 230 stickers to decorate his scrapbook. There are 46 stickers on a sticker sheet and he has 3 sheets. If he purchased 2 additional sheets, each sheet costs $10, how much did he spend in total?
A tree has 50 apples on it. Half fall off in a windstorm. If Ken picks up a third of the remaining apples, how many are left on the tree?
Samantha owns a bakery. She sells two types of bread: rye and wheat, for $3.50 and $2 respectively. If all her 15 loaves of rye bread and 10 of wheat bread are sold, how much will Samantha earn?

Andy has three basketballs, two baseballs, and one golfball. Benny owns twice as many basketballs as Andy, the same amount of baseballs as Andy, and three times as many golfballs. What is the combined total number of items owned by Andy and Benny?
Adam is planning to take his patients on a trip. There are 72 from the adult ward and 36 from the children ward. Each bus can carry 40 people. How many buses will Adam need for the trip?
Jonathan has 50 candy bars. He decides to share them evenly with 4 of his friends. How many candy bars will each friend receive?
George gives piano lessons for 25 dollars an hour. He spends 4 hours per week giving piano lessons. If he saves half of what he earns for a vacation, how much money would he have saved after 8 weeks?
Smith turned 47 years old today. 2 years ago, he was thrice as old as Paul will be in 5 years. If Paul's brother is 3 years older than Paul, how old is Paul's brother?
In a highschool debate competition, teams from both School A and School B participate. School A sends 4 teams of 3 people and School B sends 5 teams of 3 people. How many students are there in total?

James, a farmer, plants 10000 square meters of eggplant every year. Each square meter can produce 15 kilograms of eggplant. However, about 0.2 of the total production was lost due to pests. How many kilograms of eggplant did James harvest?
Peter harvested all the apples from 20 trees that have 8 apples each. If he sells a quarter of all the apples and gives a fifth of the remaining ones to his neighbors, how many apples are left?
A restaurant has 8 tables, each seating 5 people. If there are 3 seatings each evening, Tuesday to Saturday, how many people can be served in a single week?

Alex just held auditions for the college choir. 70% of the 40 sopranos got in, a third the 60 altos got in, 1/4 of the 80 tenors got in, and 1/5th of the 40 basses got in. How many people are in the choir total?
A family plants 152 apple trees and twice as many orange trees. How many trees did they plant in total?
A highway is 360 miles long. It takes 8 hours to travel from one end to the other without stopping. How fast is the car moving in miles per hour?
If it takes 10 people 10 days to shovel 10,000 pounds of coal, how many days will it take half of these ten people to shovel 40,000 pounds of coal?
Linus has a farm with pigs and chickens. He has three times as many pigs as chickens. The total number of pigs and chickens combined is 80. How many chickens does he have?
A farmer harvested 120 pounds of tomatoes. He sold 2/3 of the tomatoes at the market and ate 1/4 of the remaining tomatoes. How many pounds of tomatoes does he have left?

Mike owns a coffee shop that opens 5 days per week. On average, the shop sells 75 cups of coffee per day, at a cost of $4 each. For each cup of coffee, the cost of the ingredients is 25% of the selling price. How much does Mike earn from selling coffee each week after subtracting the cost of the ingredients?

Stephanie is moving into a new apartment. She needs to figure out how many pieces of silverware she should purchase. She needs spoons, butter knives, steak knives, and forks. For herself she figures 5 of each would be sufficient. But in case she has guests, she wants to have 10 extra pieces of each type. Then she realizes that if everything is clean at the same time, that she will have too many of everything and not enough room to fit them in her kitchen silverware drawer. So she decides to purchase 4 fewer spoons, 4 fewer butter knives, 5 fewer steak knives, and 3 fewer forks. How many pieces total of all the silverware is Stephanie going to buy?
A builder uses 25% of the bricks in a pile to build a wall. He also used 75% of the remaining bricks in the pile to build a house. If there were 200 bricks in the pile, how many bricks are remaining?
A museum has 5 exhibits. Each exhibit takes 20 minutes to tour. If a group of people need an additional 30 minutes for a break, how many hours will the museum tour take?

John is a book collector. He has already collected 100 books in his library. Around 40 books are from the fantasy genre, and 25 are from the mystery genre. The remaining half of his books are from history. How many history books does John have in his collection?
A library contains 640 books. Each shelf holds 32 books. How many shelves are needed to store all the books?
John has 6 more stamps than Mary. If Mary has 12 stamps, how many stamps do they have together?

Emma needs to finish preparing dinner by 7 pm for a family gathering. She started preparing at 4 pm. Her daughter helped her for 60 minutes, then her son assisted her for another 45 minutes. Now her husband is helping her for the next 30 minutes. How much time will Emma have left to finish the dinner preparations to be on time?

Dilan, Martha, Phillip, and Veronica went to the park together to have some fun. They all had a different number of marbles: Dilan had 14 marbles, Martha had 20 marbles, Phillip had 19 marbles and finally, Veronica had only 7 marbles. They wanted to redistribute the marbles so they each had an equal number. How many marbles did each friend have at the end of the day?
Mary's family went on a picnic. They picked 250 strawberries. They ate some of them during the lunch and returned home with 160 strawberries. How many strawberries did they eat?

Grace takes an online course that requires her to watch 3 hours worth of videos everyday for 5 days. If she watches videos for 2 hours on the first day and 3 hours on the second day, how much time does she have to spend watching videos over the next three days?
Samantha had a garden with 120 flowers. She planted 80% of the flowers herself. The rest were planted by her son. How many flowers did her son plant?
Billy is 5 inches taller than Patrick. Patrick is half as tall as Max. If Max is 50 inches tall, how tall is Billy?

Marco completed a 120 page book in four sessions. Each session, he read twice as many pages as the previous session and in the first session, he read 5 pages. How many pages did he read in the last session?
Ben has three cupboards. Each cupboard can fit 1/3 as much as Emily's cupboard, which can fit 150 books.  How many books can all Ben's cupboards fit?

Emily is measuring the snowfall in January. On the first day of the month, 15 cm of snow fell. On the second day, another 20 cm fell. On the third day, it snowed 5 cm less than the second day. If the average snowfall for the first three days of January is usually 120 cm, how much less snow has fallen this year compared to the average?

The total number of deer in a forest is three times the number of bears in the same forest. The number of rabbits is one-fourth the combined number of deer and bears. Calculate the total population of the three animals in the forest if the number of deer is 300.

In a school, there are 200 students. 40% of them are boys and the rest are girls. After a charity event, they could collect 500 books. If each book is divided among the boys, how many books does each boy receive?
A packet of crisps weighs 50 grams. How many packets would you need to have 1 kilogram?
A school organizes a trip for 120 students. Each bus can fit 40 students. If the cost for each bus is $300, how much will the school spend on the buses?
Emma paints 6 canvases every week. Matthew paints half as many as Emma. How many canvases do they paint together in a month?
A gym charges a $30 registration fee and $25 per month for a membership. If John paid $205 in total, how many months of membership did he purchase?

A vegetable farm produced 490 kilograms of carrots, 100 kilograms less per cabbage than carrots, and 200 kilograms less per potatoes than cabbages. How many kilograms of vegetables did the farm produce?

James has two containers. One contains 40 liters of water and the other 25 liters of fizzy drink. He used 15 liters of water to cook and 10 liters of the fizzy drink for a party. How many liters does he have left?

Roberta went to the supermarket with $80. She bought 4 jars of peanut butter at $4 each, cheese for $20, bread for $5, and meat for $15. She wants to buy bananas which cost $1 each. How many bananas can she buy with the remaining money?
A jar contains 100 candies. John ate 20 candies and then shared the remaining candies equally among him and his two friends. How many candies will each of them get?
Samantha sells handmade bracelets for 5 dollars each. If she sold 15 bracelets yesterday and 10 more today than yesterday, how much money will she have made in total?

A motorbike has a tank capacity of 15 liters. At the start of a trip, the tank was three quarters full. After travelling some distance, the motorbike consumed 7 liters of fuel. How many liters of fuel were left?

Jordan is recording player attendance for his football club. He knows that 3 players are in the locker room, and two less than four times that number of players are missing. If there are 5 rows of player benches each 4/5 full, how many players does Jordan oversee, including the ones present and absent?

Billy went on a hiking trip for 7 days. He packed 56 protein bars. He plans to eat 2 for breakfast, 1 for lunch, and 2 for dinner each day. After his trip, he plans to give a third of the remaining protein bars to his hiking buddy. How many protein bars will he have left after doing that?
Ben made 250 piece of origami cranes. He gave 30% of the cranes to his friends and another 15% were donated to local museums. How many paper cranes does Ben have left?
Alex has 6 more books than double the amount of pencils he has. If Alex has 50 pencils, how many total items does he have?
Sarah had 24 cupcakes in her bakery. In the morning, she sold 1/3 of them. In the afternoon, she sold 2/5 of the remaining cupcakes. How many cupcakes does Sarah have left?
In a classroom, 35% of students play basketball, 25% play soccer, and the rest play table tennis. If there are 120 students in the classroom, how many students play table tennis?
An electrician charges $45 per hour for labor. If he works for 7 hours a day, how much will he earn in a 5-day workweek?

A mountaineer is observing different types of rocks in a mountain. There are 30 different layers of rocks. Seven of the layers are granite. One-fourth of the rest are limestone. Three of the remaining are quartz. One-third of the rest are shale. The rest are all different kinds of rock. How many types of rock make up only one layer in the mountain strata?
Paul baked 4 trays of chocolate cupcakes, 3 trays of vanilla cupcakes and 2 trays of strawberry cupcakes for a bake sale. Each tray holds 12 cupcakes. How many cupcakes did Paul bake in total?
A car traveled 62 miles on a road trip. The car used 1 gallon of gas every 14 miles. How much gas did the car use for the trip?
Kendra bought two T-shirts and a pair of pants. The T-shirt cost $15 each and the pants cost $60. She paid with a $100 bill. How much change does Kendra get?
Jeff wants to bake 5 loaves of bread. Three packages of yeast are required for baking a loaf. If 1 package of yeast is $2, how much does he pay for the yeast that is enough to bake 5 loaves?
Brad has 85 dollars. He spends 30 dollars on a video game and then decides to invest half of the remaining money. He then buys a shirt for 12 dollars. How much money does Brad have left?
Amara had 24 stickers. She gave a quarter of them to her friend, Sarah. Later, her mom bought her another 12 stickers. How many stickers does Amara have now?
John saves $20 every week from his salary. If he doubles his savings after first 10 weeks, How much will he save in 40 weeks time?
A school has 25 teachers and 500 students. If the staff-student ratio needs to be maintained as 1:20. The school plans to admit 300 more students, how many more teachers are required?
If an electrician charges $40 per hour of work and spent 6 hours fixing the electrical connections, how much money in dollars, did he earn in total?
Samantha needs to distribute 150 pamphlets. If she distributes 10 pamphlets every hour, how many hours will she take to distribute all the pamphlets?
Mary is in a bookstore. She bought 4 books, each cost $15. She also bought 2 pens, each cost $3. How much money did Mary pay in total?

Sam makes cupcakes for his party. On Tuesday, he makes 2 cupcakes. Each day for the rest of the week he makes two more cupcakes than he did the day before. If on Sunday he counts that there are 8 cupcakes left, how many did he originally make?
Lisa bakes 10 cupcakes for a party. Each cupcake uses 1/2 cup of sugar. If Lisa runs out of sugar after baking these cupcakes, how many cups of sugar did she initially have?
In a garden, the ratio of roses to tulips is 5:15. If there are 300 flowers in total, how many roses are in this garden?
Teacher gives Eliza 21 pages to read, which was 3 times more than what she gave John to read. How many pages did John get?
A factory makes 20 cars in an hour. How many cars does it make in a day?

Emma is designing herself a silk dress for a wedding. The silk fabric she selected was $30 per yard. She bought a pattern for $20, and three spools of gold thread for $4 each. If she spent $184 for the pattern, thread, and fabric, how many yards of fabric did she buy?
Elise bought 15 books for her library. Each book cost $7.50. She also spent $12.50 on a library lamp. How much did she spend in total?
Adam has 8 more computers than Emily who has one fourth the number of computers that Jack has. Jack has 16 computers. How many computers does Adam have?
Madeline pays $35 for her weekly piano lessons. If Madeline decides to take piano lessons for an entire year, how much money will she spend on the lessons?
Mike has $10, and he is saving to buy a book that costs $65. Each week, he saves $5 more. How many weeks will it take for Mike to have enough money to buy the book?
A pizza place offers a discount of $1 for every 3 pizzas bought. If each pizza costs $10 and Sam orders 9 pizzas, how much will he pay?
A pizza restaurant needs 2 pounds of cheese to make a large pizza. If the restaurant has 50 pounds of cheese, how many large pizzas can they make?

Lisa wants to buy balloons for her child's birthday. A small bag is sold for $5 that contains 60 balloons, a medium bag for $7 with 90 balloons, and a big bag for $10 with 150 balloons. With $35, what is the greatest number of balloons she can buy?
A rectangular garden plot is 10 feet long and 6 feet wide. If a path 1 foot wide is added all around the outside, what is the area of the plot plus the path?
Michael has 100 Instagram followers. 40% are male and 60% are female. Emily has thrice as many followers who are male and has 300 followers in total. What percentage of Emily’s followers are female?

In a contest, Rebecca scored 5 points for every correct answer and lost 3 points for every incorrect answer. She answered 50 questions correctly and 40 incorrectly. What was her total score at the end of the contest?

An interior designing company purchases wallpaper rolls in bulk. A room requires 12 rolls of wallpaper, 5 times as many rolls of wallpaper as it does cans of paint. For each room, they use 3 cans of paint. If the company designed 3 rooms one day, and 2 rooms the next, how many cans of paint are needed for the next day?
Samantha bought a dress at $80 and sold it for $100. Later, she bought a similar dress at $90 and sold it for $110. What was her total profit?

A bakery has 500 muffins. 90% of the muffins are blueberry, and the rest are chocolate chip. The muffins are in packs of 6. If 15 packs have already been sold, how many muffins are remaining in the bakery?
Each slice of apple pie contains 450 calories.  If there are a total of 3600 calories in every apple pie, and Mark ate 50% of the apple pie, how many slices of apple pie did Mark eat?
Susie baked 6 pies for a party. Each pie was cut into 8 slices. At the end of the party, there were 18 slices left. How many pies were completely eaten at the party?

A bakery shop sells two types of muffins: blueberry and chocolate. Each blueberry muffin is priced at $1.25 and each chocolate muffin is priced at $2. Jade wants to buy 10 blueberry muffins and 15 chocolate muffins. How much will she pay in total?

Paul consumes 64 ounces of water each day. To reduce plastic waste, he decides to buy a reusable glass water bottle. If the bottle holds 80 ounces of water, how many times will he need to fill it in a week?

A lemonade stand at a fair is selling small glasses for $1 and large glasses for $3. The first batch of lemonade produced 10 small glasses and 5 large glasses. The rest of the glasses made from the subsequent batches were sold as small glasses. If the stand made $35 from selling lemonade, how many small glasses were sold from the subsequent batches?
Maggie recently bought a new laptop using a 2 year loan with no interest. She paid a down payment of $1,000, and her monthly payment is $100. What is the total cost of the laptop?
Chris runs 6 miles each stage of a relay race. If there are 7 stages in total, how many miles will Chris run in the entire race?

Ken needs 15 bananas to eat until he is full. Bob needs thrice as many bananas as Ken to eat until he is full. If they gather 80 bananas from a local farm, how many bananas will be left after they've eaten to their satisfaction?
Lisa just started working at a coffee shop. She grinded 30 pounds of beans and 5 more pounds of medium roast beans than dark roast beans. How many pounds of beans did she grind?

Peter has 15 chocolate bars and 10 caramel candies. He gives his friend Harry 1/3 of his chocolate bars and 1/2 of his caramel candies. If Harry initially had 8 chocolate bars and 15 caramel candies, how many candies and chocolate bars does Harry have?

Tom teaches one science course per year. He taught Physics for 3 years, Chemistry for thrice as many years, and Biology for 4 times as long as he taught Chemistry. How many total years has Tom taught?
On a school trip, Harry bought 3 postcards for 2 dollars each and 2 magnets at 3 dollars each. Then, he had lunch for 10 dollars. How much did Harry spend on the trip?

Jessica loves to read books. She bought 9 mystery books, 6 romance novels, and 5 science-fiction books. The mystery books cost $7 each, romance novels cost $5 each, and the science-fiction books cost $6 each. How much money did she spend on books?
Joey collected 132 stamps over the course of 6 months. If he collected the stamps at a constant rate, how many stamps did Joey collect in one month?

Sam pays $150 a month for his car insurance. He started with $800 in his bank account this year. At the end of the year, he gets a bonus that is worth double what he has left in his bank account after the insurance payments. How much money does he have then?

Ian makes sculptures out of clay. A dragon sculpture requires 85 grams of clay and sells for $100. A unicorn sculpture requires three times as much clay and sells for $230. If Ian has 1360 grams of clay, how much more money will he make turning it all into unicorns instead of dragons?
A car uses 2.5 gallons of gas for every 25 miles. How many gallons will it need to travel 100 miles?

In a warehouse, there are 5 pallets. Each pallet is stacked with 7 boxes and each box contains 3 laptops. A technician removes 1 laptop from each box. There are no other changes. How many laptops were removed from the warehouse?

A kindergarten teacher bought a set of crayons for each student in the class. If a set of crayons cost $3 and there are 25 students, including her son who is also in the class, how much did she spend in total?
A store sells apples at $2 per pound. If you buy more than 10 pounds, you get a 10% discount on the apples purchased. How much would 12 pounds of apples cost?
Adam is playing basketball. He manages to score 30 points out of 40 attempted shots. How many points will he have if he doubles his shots while maintaining the same accuracy?

Carl was given five packets of sunflower seeds to plant in his garden. Each packet contains 50 seeds. If he plants all of the seeds over a weekend, one at a time, planted at equally spaced intervals, what is the duration, in hours, between planting each seed, if he works 8 hours each day?
John cycles 12 kilometers each hour. He cycles for two hours a day. How many kilometers will John cycle in a week?
A city has a yearly budget of $50 million. If a fifth of the budget goes towards infrastructure and $15 million goes towards healthcare. How much money is remaining for other municipal services?

Lily is a literature teacher who needs to buy books for her new literature class. She wants to buy 3 copies of a novel, 5 copies of a poetry book and 2 copies of an encyclopedia. If the novel costs $15, the poetry book $20 and the encyclopedia $30, how much will she spend in total?
It takes 3 hours to prepare a chicken dish and 1.5 hours to prepare a vegetable dish. What is the number of minutes it takes to prepare 4 chicken dishes and 2 vegetable dishes?
Maggie and Tom scored a total of 60 points in the basketball match. The difference in points scored between Maggie and Tom is 20. How many points did Maggie score if Tom scored more points?
Carlos has a watermelon farm and he harvests 3 watermelons per plant. If he has 25 plants, how many watermelons did Carlos harvest?

John plans to harvest his farmland. The first 3 days, he manages to harvest 25 acres each day. For next 4 days his 3 helpers assist him. They harvest 15% fewer acres per day compared to John. What is the total acreage harvested?
Sarah has a vegetable garden. She grows 300 tomatoes, 250 cucumbers, and the rest are carrots. If her garden has 1000 vegetables in total, what percentage of the vegetables are carrots?

Matt baked five times as many cookies as Kevin did for their school event. On the following day, Matt baked 30 fewer cookies than Kevin's batch. If Kevin baked 50 cookies on the first day, and 80 more cookies on the second day than the first day, calculate the total number of cookies they baked together over the two days.
Liam bought a car and has to make monthly payments of $525 for 4 years. If he put $3,000 down as a down payment, how much did the car cost in total?

Richard travels to his office daily. His office is 15 miles away from his home. He first travels to the grocery store which is half the distance to his office from home, then travels 2 times the total distance driven so far to get to his office. How many miles does Richard drive between the grocery store and the office?
Ella was asleep for 8 hours last night and dreamed for 1/4 of the time. How much of the night was she not dreaming?
Jeff wants to purchase 15 comic books. If each comic book costs $5, how much will he need in total?
Alice started a lemonade stand and on her first day, she made $20. Each day after that, she made $5 more than the day before. How much in total did Alice make in a week of 7 days?
In a basketball match, Jamie scored 20% more points than Alex. If Alex scored 50 points, how many points did Jamie score?
Sally reads 3 pages of a book every day. If the book has 90 pages, how many days will it take Sally to finish reading the book?

Richard is making a fruit juice. He adds 4 cups of orange juice, 1 cup of pineapple juice, 2 cups of mango juice, and three times as much apple juice as pineapple juice. What percentage of the juice is orange juice?
Mandy wants to buy a dress worth $120. She has already saved $30. If she saves $15 per week, how many weeks will it take for Mandy to buy the dress?

Kate is building a pyramid in her garden using bricks of stones. She builds her pyramid in layers, using a total of 6 layers of bricks of stones. The bottom half of the pyramid has 10 bricks of stones in each layer while the top half of the pyramid has 5 bricks of stones in each layer. How many bricks of stones did Kate use for her pyramid?
A class of 25 students takes a field trip to a museum. Each entry ticket is $8 and the bus ride costs $200 in total. How much does the trip cost in total?
Jenny bought a dress for $50 and shoes for $45. She also bought a necklace for $15 and a matching ring for $10. If Jenny paid with a $130 bill, how much change did she get back?

Lena works as a writer for her university's student newspaper, earning $12 per article. She writes 3 articles every day. For each extra article she writes, she gets an additional 25% of her usual rate. If she writes an extra article each day, how much does she earn in a week?

Every day, 6000 ants are born in an ant colony. If the colony loses 2000 ants every day due to predators, what would the total population of the ant colony be at the end of 5 days if initially the colony was comprised of 50000 ants?

A library has 500 books on its shelves. On Monday, the library donates 100 books and buys 75 new books. On Tuesday, the library buys 125 books and donates 50 books. How many books are on the library's shelves after Tuesday?

In a class, 3 students share a pack of 60 colored pencils equally. If one of the students needs 5 different colors to complete her art project, how many art projects can she complete with her share of colored pencils?
Karen scored 7 goals worth 3 points each and 12 goals worth 2 points each. If her basketball team scored a total of 90 points, what percentage of the team's total points did Karen score?

Sarah wanted to decorate her room with fairy lights. She bought 7 packets of fairy lights where each packet had 10 strings of lights in it. She used 3/4 of the fairy lights to decorate her room. How many strings of fairy lights are left?

A catering service charges $15 per person for a meal, and an additional $150 for setup and service charges. Allan is hosting 100 people for a party. How much, in dollars, did Allan pay for the catering?
Mike biked 30 miles and runs 15 miles to a friend's house. If he biked an average of 20 miles per hour and ran an average of 6 miles per hour, how long did his trip take in hours?
A water tank is filled at a rate of 5 liters per minute. If the tank capacity is 100 liters, how long will it take to fill the tank entirely?
David has 70 comic books in his collection. He decides to sell 20% of them because he no longer reads them. After selling them, he buys 5 more. How many comic books does he have now?
John has 4 shirts and he buys 2 more. Each shirt costs $15. How much did he spend?
Billy buys a car for $20,000. Each year, the value of the car depreciates by 15%. How much will the car be worth after 2 years?

Ryan's novel hits the market and sells 500,000 copies. He had an advance for 50,000 copies and earns $3 for each sell. His publisher takes 15% of that. How much money did Ryan earn excluding the advance sells?
Henry has 5 more cats than John does. John has triple the number of cats than Michael who only has 2 cats. How many cats does Henry have?
A store sells notebooks for $2 each. If a student buys 6 notebooks and has $10 left, how much money did the student have initially before buying the notebooks?
Emily runs an art class where every student uses 2 brushes and 3 paints each. If Emily has 50 students, how many brushes and paints does Emily need in total?

A pet shop has 3 parrots and 2 rabbits. Each parrot needs a 1kg bag of feed per week and each rabbit needs a 2kg bag of feed per week. How many kilograms of feed are needed per week for all the animals?
Tim is planning a road trip that is 500 kilometers long. He knows his car can travel 20 kilometers per liter of fuel. If fuel costs $1.50 per liter, how much will he spend on gas for his trip?
A group of friends decided to save money to attend a concert. They need a total of $250. Each of them saves $5 a day. If they are 4 friends, how many days will it take them to save $250?

Tom, Jerry, and Spike are working in a hospital, for 9 hours each day. They attend to 3 patients per hour. Unfortunately, one day, Spike had to leave earlier, so he worked only for 5 hours. How many patients did these three serve in total on that day?
Samantha runs a charity event for 10 hours, she generates $7000 every hour. For the remaining 6 hours, she generates 30% more per hour. How much total money does Samantha raise?
Jack wants to buy a laptop. The cost of the laptop is $1500. He already has 60% of the amount he needs. How much more money does Jack need?
A hotel has 15 rooms, each with 2 beds. Each bed is made with 3 pillows. One day, the hotel needed to replace all of its pillows. How many pillows did the hotel have to buy?

Sam gets a salary increase to $25 per hour and works 35 hours a week. His previous job paid $20 an hour for 30 hours per week. How much more money does he make per year in his new job than the old job if he works 50 weeks a year?
Lisa is packing boxes for moving. Each box weighs 10 pounds, and she has already packed 7 boxes. If her moving limit is 100 pounds, how many more boxes can she pack?
Joseph needs to bake 18 muffins for a school fair. He has only one six-cup muffin tray. How many times will he need to use the tray?

Stella baked 3 trays of brownies that were each cut into 20 squares. She served one tray at a party, kept half of another for herself, and gave a quarter of the third tray to her neighbors. How many brownie squares were left?
Amy spent $500 on a computer. She also bought a software package that was 25% of the cost of the computer, and a printer that was half the price of the software. How much did she spend in total?

John, Lucy, and Sam went to a funfair. John played twice as many games as Lucy, and Lucy played 3/4 as many games as Sam. If Sam played 16 games, calculate the total number of games played by the three.
Lily has 15 puppies and 25 kittens in her pet shop. A customer buys 1/3 of the puppies and 1/4 of the kittens. How many pets does Lily have left in her pet shop?

Scott decided to challenge himself in running. He ran 15 miles per week for the first 3 months of the year. For the rest of the year, he increased his mileage to 25 miles per week. What's the total miles he ran for a whole year?
Dan and Kyle were assigned 72 pages of homework. If Dan has finished 26 pages and Kyle has finished 31, how many pages are left for them to do?
Andy had some money. Jenny gave him another $150. Andy then gave Louise $850 and kept the rest. If Andy has $1150 left, how much money, in dollars, did Andy have at first?

Four friends decided to donate money to a charity. Paul donated $30, Emma donated $10 more than Paul, Tim donated $15 less than Emma and Jack donated $25 less than Paul. How much did they donate in total?
Sarah received 60 donuts from her mom. She gave 15 to her dad, 10 to her brother, and she gave 5 to her friend. How many donuts were left for her?
A truck can travel 60 miles in one hour. The truck drives for 4 hours before taking a break which lasts 1 hour. How many miles can the truck travel in 10 hours?
Tim earns $20 for every car he washes. If he washes 7 cars, how much would he earn?
Billy's pool can hold up to 5000 gallons of water. If 60% of the pool is filled, how much more water is needed to fill it up completely?
Sarah goes for a run every day. She runs 5 miles on weekdays and 10 miles on weekends. How many miles does she run in one week?
Sarah has a collection of 120 books. She donates 20 at a charity and buys 15 new books. How many books does Sarah have now?
Ethan and Tony went to watch a movie. Ethan had $45 and Tony had twice as much as Ethan. After seeing the movie, how much in total did they have?
Mike bought 12 chocolate bars. He gave 2 to his sister and 3 to his brother. Then he ate 4 of them. How many chocolate bars are left?
Laura loves baking. She's making donuts and each batch requires 3 cups of flour. If she has decided to bake 7 batches, how much flour will she need?
Emily bought a book and a pen. The pen cost $10. The book cost 120% as much as the pen. How much did she pay for everything?

A bee farmer has bees and honey jars in a ratio of 20:4. He decides to sell half of his bees for $10 each and 3/4 of the honey jars at $8 each. If the total number of bees and honey jars he has is 600, how much will he make from the sale?

A school club made 60 total buttons. Jessica made twice as many as Sarah, and Matt made 5 times as many as Jessica. If each student had to make 5 different types of buttons in equal proportion out of the total amount, how many buttons of each type did Matt make?
James is 4 times as old as Jane. Bert is twice as old as James. If Bert is currently 24, how old will they be in total in 5 years?
Tom needs to read a book for his literature class. The book has 320 pages. He read 38% of the book on the first day and 25% on the second day. How many pages does he still need to read?

Laura has 30 more pink candies in her candy jar than green candies. She eats 1/3 of the pink candies and 2/5 of the green candies. If she initially had 60 green candies, how many candies are remaining within the candy jar altogether?
A store received a shipment of 30 cases of soft drinks. Each case contains 12 cans. If half of the cases are sold within the first week, how many cans were sold?
Megan and her friends collected seashells at the beach. If Megan collected 20 seashells and her three friends each collected 15 seashells, how many seashells did they collect in total?
A chicken lays 2 eggs per day. How many eggs will 5 chickens lay in a week?
A group of friends ordered 3 large pizzas, each divided into 12 slices. They only ate two-thirds of all the pizza. How many slices of pizza were left?
Linda wants to buy 3 dresses and each dress costs $50. How much will she pay if she also buys 2 shoes that each costs $30?
A bakery sells 15 loaves of bread weighing 200 grams each every day. How many kilograms of bread does it sell daily?
A sculptor bought 300 pounds of clay to make various art pieces. If he uses 15 pounds of clay to make one statue, how many statues can he make?

A school library has 150 books. The number of science books is 5 times the number of math books and the number of geography books is 2 times the number of science books. If the number of math books is m, how many geography books are there in the library?
Eric wants to buy 4 books that cost $6 each. He already has $10. How much more does he need to buy the books?

Mark and his three friends decided to pool their money together to buy a video game. Mark contributed $20, Jack contributed twice as much as Mark, Jill contributed half as much as Jack, and Jane contributed $10 less than Jill. How much money did they have in total to buy the video game?
A baker made 300 cupcakes for a party. If 25% of the cupcakes were chocolate flavor, 35% were vanilla flavor, and the rest were strawberry flavor, how many strawberry cupcakes were there?
Eva has a flower garden. There are three times as many daffodils as tulips and two times as many roses as daffodils. If Eva has 10 tulips, how many flowers does she have in total?
Emma has a school fair. She agreed to bake 3 trays of cookies. Each tray holds 24 cookies. How many cookies will Emma bake?
Anita spends $20 on a new book, $30 on a new outfit, and saves $100. Then, her grandmother gives her $25 for her birthday. How much money does Anita have after purchasing the book and the outfit?
Peter wants to buy a car that costs $12000. He can save $300 per month. How many months will Peter need to save money to buy the car?

Emily bought some notebooks for school. She bought 6 spiral notebooks that cost $2 each, and some composition notebooks that cost $3 each. If she spent a total of $30, how many composition notebooks did Emily buy?
Jason has $15 more than twice the amount of money his brother Dylan has. If Dylan has $20, how much money do they have in total?

Alex wants to become a teacher. He attended college for 4 years to earn a bachelor's degree. After, he decided to get a master's degree which took an additional 3 years. Plus, he also took 2 years to complete his doctorate degree. How many total years did Alex spend in school?

There are 600 workers in a factory. 1/5 of them contract flu and are unable to work. 3/4 of the remaining workers were overworked, causing 1/3 of those overworked workers not to come in the following day. How many workers came to work the following day?
Mario’s house is 4 times farther from school than Jenna’s house. If Jenna’s house is 500 meters from school, how far is Mario’s house from school?
Emma has 6 new dresses. Lucy has 2 more new dresses than Emma. If Rose has five more new dresses than Lucy, how many new dresses does Rose have?

Jake builds 5 toys for a charity auction. 3 new charities also want Jake to build toys for them, so he builds for these charities as well. Each of these new charities receives the same amount of toys. For each toy, Jake needs 3 screws. For each charity, he needs another 4 screws for fixing up the toys. If Jake uses 70 screws on building and fixing his toys for all of the charities, how many toys are at each of the 3 new charities?
Amy and her classmates were making origami cranes. Amy made twice as many cranes as Lisa. Lisa made one third as many cranes as James. If James made 30 cranes, how many did Amy make?
Jake can read a 300-page book in 5 days. If he reads the same number of pages every day, how many pages can he read in 2 weeks?
David ran 3 kilometers every morning except on Wednesdays, when he rests, and Sundays, when he runs 10 kilometers. In one week, how many kilometers does David run?

Liz needs to buy a dress, a pair of shoes, and a purse. The dress costs half the price of the shoes. The shoes cost $80 more than the cost of the purse, on which Liz spent $50. How much did Liz spend that day on shopping?
Leo has been saving money to buy a new toy. He already has $15, and he earns $7 each week from doing chores. How many weeks will Leo need to work in order to buy the toy that costs $44?
A company made a profit of $5000 in the first year. In the second year, the profit increased by 10%. In the third year, the profit decreased by 5%. What was the profit in the third year?
Melissa went shopping for fruits. She bought 15 kg of apples at $3 per kg, 20 kg of oranges at $4 per kg, and 25 kg of bananas at $2 per kg. How much did Melissa spend on fruits?

There are 400 attendees at a conference. The conference organizer charges $150 per person to cover the cost of the conference facilities, food, and materials. After the conference, the organizer found that they made $3,000 more than the total expenses. How much were the total expenses for the conference?
A teacher divided her class of 30 students into groups of equal sizes of 6. At a rate of 1 group per hour, how many hours would it take to meet all the groups?
If Stacy fills up her car with diesel at $2.5 per gallon and her car's tank capacity is 15 gallons, how much does a full tank of diesel cost for Stacy's car?
Amy sews 12 dresses every week. After 5 weeks, how many dresses has Amy sewn?

Amy is baking cupcakes for a party. She can bake 20 cupcakes in the first 30 minutes. However, during the next 30 minutes, she increases the speed and can bake 30 cupcakes. How many cupcakes does she bake in 1 hour?
Samantha bought 10 rolls of film for her old camera. Each roll can take 24 photos. She uses 4 rolls for a trip. How many photos does she have left?
Abee owns a plant nursery. Abee has 15 mango trees and each tree needs 5 litres of water daily. If she added 10 more mango trees, how much water does Abee need for all the mango trees for 30 days?
Larry saves 10% of his salary every week. If his salary is $500 per week, how much money would he save in a year?
Julia bought a piece of steak that costs $75. If the price per pound is $15, how many pounds did Julia buy?

A multi-storey car park has 5 levels. The first level has 100 parking spaces. Each subsequent level has 15 more parking spaces than the previous level. If the car park is currently half full, how many more cars can the car park accommodate?
Pablo is 17 cm taller than his sister. Together, their heights sum to 148 cm. How tall is Pablo in cm?

A recording studio charges $20 per hour to use its facilities. A band wants to record their new album there for 5 days with 8 recording hours every day. What will the total cost of their studio time be?
Emily and Samuel are baking cookies. Emily baked 10 cookies and Samuel baked three times as many cookies. How many cookies did they bake altogether?
Katie buys 5 packs of cookies, each containing 12 cookies. She eats 2 cookies, then splits the rest equally among her 4 friends. How many cookies does each of her friends get?
Zara bought a dress for $150 and sold it for $200. What is her profit?
A group has 300 chairs and 3/4 times as many cushions as the number of chairs are available for each chair. What's the total number of cushions available for the group?
John earns $15 per hour at his job. One week he works 25 hours. After taxes, which are 30% of his earnings, how much money does he make in the week?

Sandy opened a lemonade stand. On the first day, she sold 25 cups of lemonade. On the second day, she sold twice as many cups as on the first day. On the third day, she sold half as many cups as on the second day. How many cups of lemonade did she sell in total?
If Kate read a book of 490 pages in 7 days, reading the same number of pages per day. How many pages did she read in day?
There were 60 birds in the tree. Suddenly, 1/3 of the birds flew away. After an hour, another 15 birds left. How many birds are left in the tree?

There are 2,500 students in school. If half of the students are going to math club,and 1/5 of the students going to math club are going to the math club in the morning, how many students are going to math club in the afternoon?
The Fortnite game on Timmy's console takes up 4GB of storage space. He has a 160GB hard drive on his console. If all the space on his hard drive is free, how many of the same game can he store?
Mrs. Smith passes her hat 1/5 of her total height. If her ears are also 1/10 of her total height, and she is 100 cm tall, calculate the length of the rest of her body.

Janet is early morning jogger. She runs  along the stretch of a park that's 700 feet long. If Janet has to take 5 rounds of the park, but decides to skip one round, how much did she run in one morning?
A school has 7 periods in a day. There are 56 minutes in each period. If lunch and passing times between classes take up 44 minutes, how long is the total school day?
John is raising chicks in his backyard. Each chick consumes 2 cups of feed per day. If John has 20 chicks in his backyard, how many cups of feed will be consumed for 15 days?
Mia has two bookshelves. Each shelf can hold 1/3 as much as Sophia's bookshelf, which can hold 180 books. How many books can both Mia's bookshelves hold?
Rebecca is three times as old as her daughter Emily. In 5 years, she will be 40. How old will Emily be?
John's rent is twelve times his age currently. After 4 years, John will be twice as old as Lily. If Lily is right now 8 years old, calculate John's monthly rent.
Hazel walked 2 kilometers in the first hour. If she walked twice as far in the second hour, how far did she walk for 2 hours?

An artist makes an earning of $45 per hour painting custom murals. He spends 1/4 of his earnings on painting supplies and $700 on his monthly utility bills. Calculate the artist's total income after his monthly expenditures if he works 35 hours a week.

Lucy climbs up 5 flights of stairs. Each flight is 6 feet. She then climbs a ladder that is double that height. Finally, she climbs a rope that is 6 feet shorter than the ladder. How high up did she go?
Melissa's garden has 15 pink flowers and twice as many yellow flowers. If each flower has 4 petals, how many petals are there in the garden in total?
Abby worked three 8-hour shifts at her job, where she makes $10.50 per hour. How much did she make in total?
Samantha works in a toy store. When the store opens, she puts 50 teddy bears, 30 toy cars, and 40 dolls on the shelves. By evening, she has sold 90 toys. How many toys does she have left?
Emma bought 5 large flower pots and 10 small flower pots for her garden. She spent $80 in total. If each large flower pot costs $8, how much does each small flower pot cost?

Jonas has a box full of marbles. In this box, he has twice as many red marbles as blue marbles, and half as many yellow marbles as red marbles. If Jonas has a total of 180 marbles in the box, how many yellow marbles does he have?

In a school, there are 36 students in 6th grade and twice as many in 7th grade. If 7th grade students make up 30% of all the students in the school, how many 8th graders are there if they make up 5% of all the students?
Sam bought 2 kg of apples. He ate 300 grams on the first day and 200 grams on the second day. How many grams of apples does Sam have left?

A book club needs 7 copies of a particular book for their next meeting. Each book costs $15 but they have a discount coupon that reduces the price by 20%. How much will the book club pay for the 7 books?
Mario starts work at 9:00 AM and finishes at 5:00 PM. He takes a lunch break for 45 minutes and two 15-minute breaks during the day. How many hours does Mario actually work in one day?
Grace's rabbit has 4 times as many spots as her hamster. The hamster has 5 fewer spots than the 10 spots the guinea pig has. How many spots does the rabbit have?
Charlie spends 12 dollars on comic books every month. How much does he spend in a year?
Michael bought 15 books at the bookstore for $6 each. He also bought a gift card for $50. How much did Michael spend in total?
In a school, there are three times as many girls as boys. If there are 50 boys in the school, how many students are there in total?
Jake can mow 3 lawns in an hour. He can water 5 gardens in an hour. If he spends 4 hours mowing lawns and 3 hours watering gardens, how many tasks has he completed?
Sara bought 3 books, each costing $12. She also bought a notebook for $5 and a pencil for $1. She gave the cashier a $50 bill. How much change did she receive back?

Joe steps 7 steps backward, then takes 15 steps forward, and then 5 steps backward, finally he takes thrice the amount he last took, steps forward. How many steps forward is Joe from his initial position?
Susie has 80 candies and she distributes an equal number to her 5 friends. How many candies does each friend get?
A group of 12 friends are ordering burgers. If each friend eats 2 burgers, and each box contains 4 burgers, how many boxes should they order?
If Tom ran 15 miles, Jerry ran half as many miles, and their friend, Bob, ran 10 miles more than Tom, how many miles did they run in total?
George exercises 6 days a week, burning 400 calories each time. In two weeks, how many calories will George burn?
John helps pay 60% of the cost for his son's tuition, which costs $500 a month. How much will John pay in a year?

Sarah is a wedding planner. This month she has organized 15 weddings. For 20% of them, she had to order special flowers and for 30% she had to hire a live band. For the rest of the weddings, she had just to organize the decorations. For how many weddings did Sarah just organize decorations?

Robert went to the gym for 2 hours. Lifting weights for every 15 minutes took him 5 minutes, but every 45 minutes he had to take a 10-minute break. How many minutes did Robert spend lifting weights during his visit to the gym?
In Colorado, Carrie bought 20 gallons of gas at $2.50/gallon. In Utah, the gas was $1.50 more per gallon and she bought another 20 gallons. How much did she spend on gas in total?

Jake has a budget of $500 per month for groceries. In the first week, he spent $120. In the second week, he spent twice the first week's amount. In the third week, he spent half the second week's amount. How much is left in his budget for the fourth week?

Jimmy's toy car is 8 inches long and 4 inches wide. It cost him $96. His new toy robot is 16 inches tall and 8 inches wide and costs $152. How much more—measured by cost per square inch—was his car compared to his toy robot?

Sarah makes 2 dozen muffins every Sunday and sells them at the local farmers market for $1.50 each. If she doesn't sell any the first Sunday, but sells all of them the second Sunday, how much money does she make?
Each row in a theater has 20 chairs and there are 15 rows. During a concert, 4 chairs in every row were not occupied. How many chairs were occupied?

A cafe uses 5 cups of milk to make a large cappuccino and 2 cups of milk to make a small cappuccino. If the cafe serves 100 large and 150 small cappuccinos in a day, how many cups of milk do they use?
Henry spends 9 hours working, 2 hours of exercise, and gets 7 hours of sleep per day. How much free time does Henry have in a day?
Mike is 10 years old and his sister is twice his age. In five years, how old will Mike's sister be?

A carpenter buys wooden boards of various sizes to craft tables. He requires 7 meters from one board and 3 meters from another board to make 1 table. On a trip to the market, he can only buy 100 meters of wood. How many tables can the carpenter make assuming he uses all the wood?

Charlie and his friends are planning to rent a beach house for the summer. The total rent for the house is $3000. If two of them will shoulder 15% of the rent each and Charlie will pay the rest, how much will Charlie pay?
Kevin starts reading a 480-page book. In the first 3 days, he reads 40 pages each day. He then increases his daily intake to 60 pages for the next 5 days. How many pages are left unread in the book?
Sandy made 35 bracelets in the morning, and in the afternoon she made 15 more than what she had done in the morning. How many bracelets did she make in total?

Sara bought a beautiful dress that cost $200 at full price. The dress was on sale for 30% off. She then used a coupon that provided another discount of $15. She paid using a credit card that gave her an additional 5% off the remaining price. How many dollars did she save compared to the original price of the dress?
Tim needs to travel to a town 40 miles away. If he has already travelled 18 miles, how many more miles does he need to travel?
Molly walks her dog 2 miles every day. How many miles will she walk her dog in a year?

Richard has a jar of 70 jelly beans. He eats 5 jelly beans every weekday (Monday through Friday) and 10 jelly beans every weekend (Saturday and Sunday). How many jelly beans does Richard have left after a week?

Peter has 30 chocolates to distribute among his five friends. He needs to buy six more chocolates so he can give an equal number of chocolates to his friends. How many chocolates will each of his friends get?
Four times a week, Sophia runs 20 kilometers each day. Her friend Aditi runs 10 more kilometers than Sophia's daily running distance. How many kilometers do they run in total in a week?
Lisa is buying school supplies for her two children. If she buys 10 notebooks for $1 each and 4 pens for $3 each, how much will she spend in total?
A group of friends go apple picking and collect 7 apples each. If they're 4 in the group, how many apples do they have overall?

Hannah makes necklaces using beads, wires, and pendants. For each necklace, she uses 3 wires, twice as many beads as wires, and two pendants. If she wants to make 5 necklaces, how many beads will she need?

A zoo has 3 lions, 5 elephants, 7 giraffes, and 2 zebras. Already in the morning, 9 tourists have visited the lions, and each of the rest of the species has been seen by 7 tourists. How many tourists have visited the zoo in the morning?

John's farm can only use 1500 gallons of water during August due to water rationing. John uses 200 gallons for drinking and cooking and 30 gallons per shower. He wants to fill his hot tub, which measures 5 feet by 5 feet by 4 feet. If each gallon of water fills 1 cubic foot of the hot tub, how many showers can John take in August?
A baker makes 7 types of cookies every day. Each type requires 15 minutes to prepare and 20 minutes to bake. How much time does it require to prepare and bake all types of cookies?

A bakery has 45 loaves of bread and 60 muffins initially. They sold one third of the bread and half the muffins, and then baked another 20 loaves of bread and another 40 muffins. How many loaves of bread and muffins are left in the bakery now?

A playground has 4 benches, each with 3 seats. There are also 12 swings and 3 slides. Each slide can hold 2 kids at the same time. If all seats and rides are occupied, how many kids are at the playground?
Lucas received 2 dollars as a reward for every book he reads. After reading 5 books, he spent half of his money on candy, which cost 1 dollar each. How many candies did he buy?
Jason collected 80 comic books. He gave 1/4 of his comic books to his brother and sold 1/4 of the remaining comic books. How many comic books does he have left?
A car dealer has a sale for car accessories. The whole set costs $300 and the dealer offers a 20% discount. How much does a customer should pay for the accessories after the discount?

Jason baked cakes for his sibling. Each cake contains 10 slices. His sibling took 2 slices from each cake. Jason made three cakes. How many slices are left if his sibling took 2 slices from each cake?
Alex is 15 years older than Blake. In 5 years, Alex will be 30 years old. What is the combined total of both their ages now?
Laura has twice as many dogs as Karen. Karen has 5 more dogs than Charlie, who has 3 dogs. How many dogs does Laura have?

Liam decided to try his luck at the local carnival's ring toss game. He paid $5 for three toss attempts. On his first try, he won a toy priced at $2, on his second try, a toy priced at $4, and on his third try, a toy priced at $3. How much in total value did Liam gain from the game?
Zoe is 3 years older than her sister Amy. If Amy is 15 years old now, how old will Zoe be in 4 years?
Mitchell has a lawn that is 20 feet by 30 feet. He rakes leaves off the lawn, bagging up 15 bags of leaves. Each bag can hold 2 cubic feet of leaves. How many inches deep were the leaves on the lawn?
There are 15 more orange marbles than blue marbles in a box. There are 20 blue marbles. How many marbles are there in the box?

5 siblings are sharing a chocolate bar. John eats twice as much as Peter, while Peter eats the same as Sarah, Tony, and Alice, each. If Sarah, Tony, and Alice each eat 10 grams of the chocolate, how many grams did John eat?
One gallon of paint can cover 400 square feet. John has to paint a wall with an area of 2800 square feet. How many gallons of paint does John need?

Kennedy grew tomatoes from three plants she had in her backyard.  The first tomato plant produced two dozen tomatoes.  The second plant produced 5 more than half as many tomatoes as the first plant.  And the third plant produced two more tomatoes than the second plant.  How many tomatoes did the three plants produce?
Lisa has a total of 350 stamps in her collection. She has 52 stamps from England, 78 from Australia, and the rest are from Canada. How many Canadian stamps does she have?
Tom is cycling to his friend's house, if he cycles at 10 miles per hour, he will reach in 50 minutes. How far is Tom's friend's house?
Rachel will be twice as old as Peter in 6 years. If Peter is currently 18 years old, how old is Rachel now?

Eva and Lily meet at a cafe for lunch. Eva orders a salad for $5.45 and a smoothie for $3.5. Lily orders a sandwich for $6 and a soda for $1.50. They also order a plate of nachos to share, which costs $4.55. If the tax on the bill is $.25 and they split the cost, how much does Eva have to pay if she started with $20?
Maggie bought 80 meters of ribbon and cut it into 4 equal parts. She then used 2 parts of the ribbon. How many meters of ribbon is not used?
A school principal invited his secretary, three teachers, and seven students to the meeting. Each teacher was told to invite two parents. How many people did the principal expect at the meeting?

Peter is purchasing dinner sets for his newly opened restaurant. Each dinner set includes 4 plates, 3 bowls, 5 glasses, and a set of spoons, forks, and knives. Each dinner set costs $45. Due to a sales event, there's a 15% discount on the dinner sets. If Peter needs to purchase 20 dinner sets, how much will he pay after discount?

Mrs. Rodriguez is baking for a school event. She prepares 50 muffins, 30 chocolates, and 40 cookies for the 30 students in her class. If she wishes to distribute an equal number of sweets to each student, how many sweets will each student receive?
Half of the students in a class are girls. One fifth of the boys have glasses. If there are 40 students in the class, how many students are boys without glasses?
Tom has twice the money that Harry has. If Harry has 40$ on him and they decide to donate half the money they have combined to charity. How much money did they donate?
Angela is planting a garden and needs 4 packs of tomato seeds and three packs of cucumber seeds. She only has a buy-2-get-1-free coupon. How many free packs will she get?
Donna spent $15 on soda and $10 on popcorn at the movie theater for her kids. Her tickets for the movie cost $7 each. If she bought 5 tickets, how much did she spend in total?
Daniel is learning how to play guitar. He practices for 1 hour everyday. How long will it take Daniel to reach 100 hours of practice?
Jordan swam 6 laps each day on Monday, Tuesday and Wednesday. He swam 4 laps on both Thursday and Friday. How many laps would Jordan swim each day if he swam the same number of laps each day?

A purse contains 4 dimes, 5 nickels, and 6 pennies. If the value of the dimes is twice that of the nickels, and the value of the nickels is five times that of the pennies, calculate the total value in the purse.
Buzz the bee collects 200 grains of pollen each day. If a single grain of pollen weighs approximately 0.000006 gram, how much pollen does Buzz collect in grams in 7 days?

A construction company purchases 30 tons of concrete for a new project. Each cubic meter of concrete weighs approximately 2.5 tons. In addition, they order 5 tons of steel reinforcement. How much total weight of building materials does the company have?
A bag holds 5 dozen cookies. If Tina's family ate 15 cookies, how many cookies are left?
Paul owns a bakery and sells muffins for $3.50 each. For each muffin, he has an operating cost of $2.00. If Paul sold 180 muffins today, how much profit did he make?
A bowling alley has 15 lanes. Each lane needs 10 pins to set up a game. If 6 lanes are already set up, how many more pins are needed to set up the rest of the lanes?

Steve and Bob need to transport 180 crates from a warehouse to their store. Steve can carry 6 crates at a time and Bob can carry 9 crates at a time. If they make trips together, each carrying their maximum amount of crates, how many trips would they need to make to transport all crates?

Amy has 75 water bottles. Each water bottle takes up 40 square inches of space before being compacted and 25% of that amount after being compacted. How much space do all the water bottles take up after being compacted?

Lucy wanted to buy a dress and a pair of shoes. The dress was on clearance at a 20% discount, and the pair of shoes was 30% off. If the original price of the dress is $60 and the shoes cost $40, how much would Lucy pay in the end for both the dress and shoes?
Tom has two times as many stickers as Jerry. If Jerry has 50 stickers, how many stickers does Tom have?
In a parking lot, there are 240 cars. 70 of the cars are blue. The rest are red. How many cars are red?
A gym has 150 regular members. There are twice as many women as men. After a 50% increase of saving, how many women are now registered?

Pauly is making ice cubes. He needs 10 pounds of cubes. He knows that 2 ounces of water make 1 cube and each cube weighs 1/16th of a pound. It takes him 1 hour to make 10 cubes. Every hour his ice maker run costs $1.50. Every ounce of water costs $0.10. How much will it cost to make all the ice?
There are 3 plums in a basket, and for each plum, there are 2 pears. If for each pair there is a peach, how many fruits are in the basket?

A railway station has 8 platforms and all of them are full. The remaining platforms are only at 30% capacity. If each platform can hold 6 trains and each train carries 300 people, how many people are at the station?
Linda owns a bakery. She sells bread for $3 each, and in one day she sold 40 breads. She also sold 30 cookies for $2 each. How much did Linda earn in a day?
Mark has 5 cars in his Hot Wheels collection. Each car cost him 12 dollars. If his friend Sam gives him another car, how much was the total cost of Mark's collection?
A delivery truck has 150 boxes. The driver delivers 30 boxes in the first stop and 45 boxes in the second stop. How many boxes are left in the truck after the second stop?
Linda bought a necklace for $250 and a bracelet for $120. She also bought an earring which cost twice as much as the bracelet. How much did Linda spend in total?
There are 200 cows on a farm. Each cow provides 3 gallons of milk a day. If a gallon of milk sells for $3, how much income is generated from the milk a day?
Kerry has $45. She spent $15 buying dinner. She spent a third of the money she had left on a dessert. How much money, in dollars, does Kerry have left?
At a bakery, each cake needs 5 eggs. If a single egg costs 20 cents and the bakery makes 60 cakes a day, how much do they spend on eggs each day?
Ben wants to buy a new guitar that costs $150. He mows lawns in his neighborhood to earn money and gets paid $15 for each lawn he mows. How many lawns will Ben need to mow to buy his new guitar?
Nick saves $15 a month to buy his mom a birthday present that costs $180, how many months will Nick have to save for?

John collects comic books. He has 10 comic books in his collection. Every week, he buys 3 more comics. After John buys his comics for the week, his younger brother borrows 2 comics each week. How many comic books will John have after 3 weeks?
Jim and Tom are building a fence around their garden. Each of them can build 2 feet of the fence per hour. If they work together for 4 hours, how long in feet is the fence they built?

A bus that seats 40 people arrives at a bus stop with 25 people waiting. As soon as it leaves another bus with 35 seats pulls up, and 10 more people arrive at the stop. How many people are still waiting after the second bus leaves?
Kyle runs 5 miles to the beach. After spending a couple of hours there, he runs back home. How many miles does he run in total?

Emily wants to buy a dress that costs $50 and a pair of shoes that costs twice as much as the dress. If Emily's mother gives her $25, how much more money does Emily need to buy the dress and the shoes?
Laura baked 24 cupcakes and wants to distribute them equally between her 6 friends. How many cupcakes will each friend receive?
Ken reads 20 pages of a book in 1 hour. If he consistently read at the same pace for the next 8 hours, how many pages will Ken read in total?

Out of 60 students in a library, 15 are reading comic books. The rest are reading either novels or textbooks. The number of students reading textbooks is 3 times as many as the ones reading comic books. How many students are reading novels?
John bought a 200 liter water tank. It already had 40 liters of water. If John decides to fill 20 liters every day, how many days will it take for the tank to be full?
Marie has 20 old books on her bookshelf. She sold half of them and then bought 10 more. How many books does Marie have now?
A bike and scooter rental service charges $5 per hour for a bike and $7 for a scooter. If John rented a bike for 6 hours and a scooter for 2 hours, how much did John pay for the rentals?
Gabby is saving money to buy a new makeup set. The makeup set costs $65 and she already has $35. Gabby’s mom gives her an additional $20. How much money does Gabby need to buy the set?
A school library has 45 story books and 35 reference books. Each of the story books has 120 pages and each reference books has 300 pages. What is the total number of pages in the library?
A tank is 10 feet in height, 9 feet in width, and 12 feet in length. A fish tank is 2 feet in height, 1 foot in width, and 3 feet in length. How many fish tanks can fit into the tank?

For several years, the number of lions in the Savannah has been thrice the number of elephants in the Jungle. Today, the park ranger says a quarter of the elephants have migrated to the Savannah. If there are now 75 lions in the Savannah, how many elephants remain in the Jungle?
Melissa bought 5 yards of fabric. She uses 2/3 yard of fabric to make one dress. How many dresses can she make?
If a movie theater can hold 200 people and 56 people left after the first hour, how many people are left in the movie theater?

John wants to ride the Ferris wheel 2 times, the rollercoaster 3 times and the carousel once. It costs 3 tickets to ride the Ferris wheel, 4 tickets to ride the rollercoaster and 2 tickets to ride the carousel. How many tickets does John need?
Clarissa has 12 dollars to buy trinkets. Each trinket costs $0.50. After buying as much trinkets as possible, how many pennies will she have left without any change?
A store has 34 bags of flour. Each bag weighs 10 kg. If 3 bags are sold, how many kilograms of flour does the store have?
For his math homework, Alex needs to solve 20 equations. Each equation takes him 15 minutes. After 2 hours of uninterrupted work, how many more equations does he need to solve?
A water tank can hold 200 liters of water. It is currently 60% full. How many liters of water are needed to fill up the tank?
John bought 100 cans of soda for his party. Each can contains 12 ounces of soda. If 780 ounces of soda were consumed at the party, how many full cans of soda are left?
Jane has 25 lemons. She uses 5 to make lemonade, 2 for cooking, and her sister takes twice as many as were used for cooking. How many lemons does she have left?
The distance between two towns is 150 miles. A motorist travels the distance back and forth with an average speed of 50 miles per hour. How much time did they spend driving?
If a bundle of wheat is $15 when the price of a bundle of barley is $20, how much would Susan pay for buying 40 bundles of each grain with a 20% discount?
Sam runs 2 laps around a park each day. The park is 3 kilometers in length. How many kilometers does Sam run in a week?
Charlie found 24 seashells at the beach. He wants to split them evenly between his 6 friends. If he lost 3 seashells on the way home, how many seashells will each of his friends receive?

Lisa the leopard has unique circular spots on her beautiful fur coat. Two-fifths of her spots are located on her head, one-fourth are located on her back, and the rest are on her tail. If Lisa has 20 spots on her head, how many spots does she have on her tail?
Each side of a square painting is 8 inches long. What is the area in square inches of the painting?
A pitcher is filled with 64 ounces of water. If each glass holds 8 ounces, how many glasses can be filled from the pitcher?

Oliver needs to bake 60 cookies to be divided among 15 of his friends. If Oliver decides to bake the same amount of cookies for each of his friends, how many cookies does Oliver need to bake for each of his friends?
Tom bought a pack of 18 cookies. He ate 3 cookies every day. How many days it will take to finish all cookies?
The school orchestra is planning a concert. They need to rent chairs for the audience. Each chair costs $8. They have a budget of $500. How many chairs can they rent with their budget?
Laura and Sarah split $70. Laura's share was four times as much as Sarah's. How much did Laura get?

A family of five has saved $1500 for their annual summer vacation. Their flight tickets cost them $500 and the hotel costs them $200 per night for 4 nights. They plan to spend the rest of the money on meals and tours. How much money do they have left for meals and tours?

Maria has three baskets with a total of 60 vegetables. There are 10 carrots in one basket, 20 tomatoes in another, and the remaining two have the same number of onions and cucumbers, respectively, how many onions are there?

Emily eats 4 meals a day. Breakfast is 400 calories. Her brunch contains 50% more calories than that. Her lunch is three times as many calories as the brunch and her dinner is the same amount of calories as breakfast. She also has 2 snacks that are each 200 calories. How many calories does she consume in a day?

In one amusement park, a roller coaster ride costs $7 for each person, and a Ferris wheel ride costs $5 each. If a group of 3 took the roller coaster, then all of them rode the Ferris wheel thereafter and then they all bought 2 $3 snacks each. How much did they spend in total?

Peter is growing sunflowers in his garden and has been recording their heights. One plant grew 30 cm one week, 45 cm taller the following week, and in the third week, it had grown 15 cm less than in the second week. The average growth for these types of sunflowers is usually 110 cm over three weeks. How much more or less did Peter's sunflower grow than the average?
Sophia wants to make apple pies for her friends. Each pie requires 6 apples. If she plans to make 7 pies and has 10 apples at home, how many more apples does she need to buy?
A crate contains 100 apples. If Alex eats five apples a day, how long will it take him to eat all the apples in the crate?
If a book costs $15 and is on sale for 20% off the original price, what is the final price of the book?
Anna has a box of 10 strawberries. She uses 2 strawberries to make a smoothie and gives 4 strawberries to her sister. She then eats half of what's left. How many strawberries does she have left?
The bakery has 100 breads, each weighing 800 grams. After baking, each bread lost 10% of their weight. Now, how much does each bread weigh?
In a library, there are 60 fictions and twice as many non-fictional books. How many books are there in total in the library?

John went to a supermarket to buy some kitchen items. He bought a pan for $5, a spatula for $2, and a pot for three times the price of the spatula. He had $20 for his shopping. How much money did John have left?
A car travels 500 miles in 8 hours. At this rate, how far would the car have driven in 5 days ? (Consider one day as 24 hours)
A baker uses 2 cups of flour to bake a loaf of bread. If he has 30 cups of flour, how many loaves of bread can he bake?

A grocery store ordered 50 boxes of apples and 60 boxes of oranges. Each box of apples costs $25 and each box of oranges costs $30. If the store sold all the boxes of fruits for $40 each, how much profit did the grocery store make?
A football field is 120 yards long. How many feet is the football field if 1 yard equals 3 feet?

There were 120 people at a winter celebration. There were 80 cups of hot chocolate, 70 mugs of tea, and 60 cups of coffee. One-fourth of the guests drank hot chocolate, one-fifth of the guests drank tea, and one-third of the guests drank coffee. How many total mugs and cups were consumed?

Sam bought a $200 bike on an installment plan at the local shop. He made a down payment of a quarter of the cost of the bike. The shop then offered him a plan to pay the balance within 5 months. What is the monthly payment, in dollars, to own the bike?

Jennifer is observing traffic from a cafe. She counted 64 vehicles so far today. She saw 14 trucks, 3 buses, three times as many taxis as buses, 4 motorcycles, and 32 cars. If the trucks held 2 people each, the buses held 12 people each, the taxis held 3 people each, the motorcycles held 1 person each, and the cars held 4 people each, how many passengers has Jennifer counted today?
Kate and Lily went to a cafe. They spent the same amount. Kate bought a coffee for $3 and two muffins for $4 each.  Lily ordered a tea for $2 and a piece of cake.  How much did Lily's cake cost?
At a pizza party, there are 10 people. Each person eats on average 3 slices of pizza. How many pizzas (with 8 slices each) are needed for the party?
Mario loves reading. He reads 4 books per month. If he buys a pack of books containing 10 books, how many months will it last him?
Fern goes to the park every day. The park is 2 miles away from her home. If she walks both ways from the park every day for a week, how many miles does she walk in a week?
Cameron, Kate, and Will play a game. Cameron scores in 80 seconds. Kate takes 15 seconds more than Cameron. If Will is twice as fast as Kate, how many seconds does it take for him to score?

John works 5 days a week and takes home $200 each day. His expenses every day are $50 for food, $25 for transportation, and $10 for miscellaneous items. How much total savings will John have after 2 months of working?

Samantha saves her money for six months. Every month she saves $20 more than she did the previous month. If she saved $100 in the first month, how much money will Samantha have at the end of six months?

A toy car travels at a speed of 3 mph and a toy train travels at a speed that is thrice as fast as the toy car. If they both start from the same point, how far does the toy train travel in the time the toy car takes to travel 9 miles?
Alyssa subscribes to a music streaming service. The first 50 songs cost $20 and the next 50 songs cost half that much. She splits the cost evenly with her friend. How much did she pay?

Maria has four times as many nickels as dimes in her piggy bank. She decides to keep 1/4 of the dimes for use later. If she has 800 nickels, calculate the combined number of dimes and nickels she has after keeping aside 1/4 of the dimes?
A school bus can hold 60 students. If it makes 5 trips to bring all the students to the school, how many students are in the school?

A school is planning a field trip to the zoo. 3 buses were arranged to take the students and teachers to the zoo. The first bus has 15 students, the second bus has twice the number of students as the first bus. The third bus has 5 fewer students than the second bus. How many students are going to the zoo in total?
Mike can read 10 pages of a book in an hour. But, if he reads 2 pages more each hour, how many pages will he read within 5 hours?
Sara goes to a gym 3 times a week. Each session lasts 2 hours. If a month has 4 weeks, how many hours does Sara spend in a gym in a month?
Mason is preparing for a marathon. He runs 12 miles every day. How far, in miles, does he run in three weeks?
Alice and John decide to save money for a new TV. They both save a third of what they earn. If Alice earns $9 daily and John earns $6 daily, how much money will they have saved after a year?
Liam has $70 more than Ava. If Liam has $200, and they decide to pool their money together, how much money do they have in total?

Jack and Jill went to the candy store. Jill bought twice as many candies as Jack. If Jill bought 36 candies and Jack bought some chocolate bars, how many candies and chocolate bars did Jack buy if he bought same quantity of both?
