A football pitch is 105 meters long. If a player runs the entire length of the pitch 20 times during a game, how many meters does he run in total?
A company spends $500 on advertising and $1,000 on production costs. If they make a profit of $600, how much did they earn?
Jack drives at a speed of 60 miles per hour. How long will it take to drive a distance of 300 miles?
A school cafeteria has 80 tables. During one lunch shift, 70% of the tables were occupied by students. If each table has 6 chairs, how many seats were still available?

Mike has 70 books in his library. 5 of the books are in bad condition so he decides to throw them away. Mike buys 10 new books the same week. He then decides to give 20 of his old books to his friends. How many books does he have now?
Each month John gets 2 pairs of three socks each as a gift for winning a sweepstakes. If he initially had 35 socks, calculate the number of socks he'll have in 4 months.
Mason and Noah are saving money to buy a new video game. Mason has $30 and Noah has $40. The new game costs $120. How much more money do they need to save together to buy the video game?
In a zoo, lions consume 250 kg of meat in a day. Tigers consume double the amount lions consume. If the zoo has food stock for 10 days, how many kgs of meat are there in the stock?

Jake hopes to raise $2000 to fund his innovative app idea. He has already raised $500 from friends and family. If the average individual investor contributes $50, how many more investors does Jake need to meet his goal?
Grace is baking cookies for a school bake sale. Each batch of cookies requires 2 cups of sugar. If she has 10 cups of sugar and wants to use it all, how many batches of cookies can she bake?
A box can fit 6 cupcakes. If Mary wants to transport 75 cupcakes using such boxes, how many boxes does she need?

A group of tourists decided to visit a zoo where admission fees are 10 dollars per adult and 5 dollars per child. The group consists of 15 adults and 10 children. After visiting the zoo, they decided to eat at a restaurant where the average meal cost is 20 dollars per person. How much did the group spend in total?
Jake travelled 10km north, 15km east, and 5km south. How many kilometers did jake travel in total?

Magda plants three types of flowers in her garden: roses, tulips, and daisies. She plants 15 rose bushes, 20 tulip bushes, and 25 daisy bushes. Roses need 2 liters of water per week, tulips need 3 liters of water, and daisies need 5 liters of water. For how many liters of water will Magda need in a week to water all the bushes in her garden?
Michael donates 15% of his $2000 income to a local charity every month. How much does he donate to the charity annually?
A fish tank has 8 fish. Three of them are yellow, four of them are blue and the rest are red. How many fish in the tank are red?

In a book club, 60% of the members read romance novels. There are 50 members in the book club. If each member who reads romance novels needs to buy a new novel for $15, how much do all the romance novel readers spend in total?

Isabel loves to run in the forest and she does it within a circuit that is 365 meters long. She runs the circuit 7 times in the morning and runs it 3 times in the afternoon. How many meters does Isabel run during a week?

Debbie sells lemonade for $2 per cup, iced tea for $3 per cup, and fruit punch for $4 per cup. During a sunny afternoon, she sold 10 cups of lemonade, 8 cups of iced tea, and 6 cups of the fruit punch. How much money did Debbie make?
A bus has 45 seats and each seat was occupied. At the first stop, 15 people got off the bus. At the second stop, 30 more people got off. How many people are left on the bus?
At a party, 10 people ordered pizza and 5 people ordered burgers. Each pizza costs $8 and each burger costs $6. How much is the total cost of all the food ordered?

Sam decides to put more shelves in his study room. He already has 5 shelves in one corner and decides to install twice as many shelves in the other three corners each. He then adds 3 more shelves. How many total shelves does he have?

Brian works 4 days a week as an artist and his goal is to paint 80 paintings in a month. He painted 20 paintings in the first week and 15 in the second week. How many paintings does he need to make per day for the remaining weeks?
Peter has a garden in which he planted 4 rows of carrots and each row has 6 plants. If Peter visits his garden 2 times per day, how many plants does he visit each day?

In a school presentation, twice as many students voted for Sally's project compared to Tim's project, and the number of votes for Leslie's project was 30 more than the number of votes for Tim's project. If 15 students voted for Tim's project, how many total votes were cast?
Charles reads 90 pages of a novel, which is 30% of the whole book. How many pages are in the book?

Joel has a job that pays him $20 an hour. He worked 4 hours on Monday, 6 hours on Tuesday, 5 hours on Wednesday, 3 hours on Thursday, and 8 hours on Friday of the same week. How much did Joel make that week?
Thomas has twice as many soccer balls as Adam. Nathan has thrice as many soccer balls as Thomas. How many soccer balls are there in total if Adam has 4?

Jacob, Max, and Lily are picking strawberries for a party. Jacob managed to pick 40 strawberries, which is twice as many as Lily. Max managed to pick 10 more strawberries than Lily. How many strawberries do they have in total?
Tom's dad is 3 times older than him. If Tom will be 50 in 30 years, how old is his dad now?
Emily has 50 apples. She gave away 30% of her apples to her friends. After that, she ate 20% of the remaining apples. How many apples were left?

A charity is hosting a run where each participant can choose between a 5km or 10km distance to run. 15 of the participants choose the 5km distance, and a total distance of 200km is covered. How many participants chose the 10km distance?
Susan has 15 more chickens than Michelle. If Michelle has 25 chickens, how many chickens do they have in total?

Jennifer has to review three books for literature class. Each book takes her 5 hours to review. If she earns $20 per hour of work, calculate the total amount of money she earns from reviewing all the books for literature class.

Peter decides to clean the neighborhood park during his holidays. The park is full of soda cans weighing 4 ounces each. He is able to clean up 140 cans a day for 5 weeks. If one ton of cans recycles for $30, how much did he make?
In Jane's garden, she has planted 60 trees. She planted 15 apple trees, twice as many pear trees, and the rest are orange trees. How many orange trees does Jane have?
John has 20 marbles. He gives 5 marbles to his friend and then he loses 4 marbles. His father gives him 10 more marbles. How many marbles does John have now?
Lola has 45 apples and wants to put them into baskets. Each basket can hold 7 apples. How many baskets does Lola need?
Jenny sells handmade bracelets for $10 each. If she made 15 bracelets and sold them all, how much money did she make?
John spends 70 minutes commuting to work, 10 minutes getting coffee, and 480 minutes working each day. How many hours does he spend on these activities in a five day work week?
A charity decided to plant 100 trees every week. How many trees would they plant in a year?

A Psychology student wants to find out the average weekly lunch budget of the college students. According to his survey, 3/4 of the students have an average of $5 lunch budget per day while the rest has an average of $3 a day. If he surveyed 80 students, what is the total amount of money those 80 students spend in a week (5 days)?

Steve runs a web design business. He charges $50 per hour and works 7 hours per day for 6 days per week. He spends $250 per week on bills and other expenses. How much does he earn per week after expenses?
Joey has 10 gallons of water. He uses 2 gallons of water every day for watering the plants. How many days will the water last?
If you buy 3 books at $15 each and 2 CDs at $10, how much would you pay in total?
Adam loves to read. He reads 4 books every week from his local library. If the library has a checkout limit of 20 books per patron, how many weeks will it take for Adam to reach the limit?
Jamie has fifteen $20 bills and three fewer $10 bills. How much money does Jane have in all?
Stan can type 100 words per minute. After a typing course, he increases his speed by 50 words per minute. How many words can he type in 10 minutes?
Paul is making a smoothie that requires 12 ounces of strawberries for every 8 ounces of milk. If he only has 24 ounces of strawberries, how many ounces of milk will he need?

Olivia is a pet sitter and is creating a schedule for all of her clients this week. On Monday, she needs to take care of 5 cats and 3 rabbits. On Tuesday, she takes care of the same amount of rabbits but isn't sure how many cats she should take care of. On Wednesday, she takes care of 6 hamsters. It takes 2 hours to take care of a cat, 1 hour to take care of a rabbit, and 3 hours to take care of a hamster. If she can work for a total of 40 hours this week, how many cats can Olivia take care of on Tuesday?
Zack borrowed a book from the library. The library charges a late fee of $0.25 per day. After 14 late days, how much does Zack have to pay the library for the late fee?

Liam had 3 separate jars of cookies. Each of his jars had 8 cookies left. His sibling, Mia, also had 3 jars of cookies. One of her jars had 15 cookies left while the other two each had 5. How many more cookies did Mia have?
Paula saves twice as much money as Sonia, who saves half as much as Kenneth. If Paula saves $300, how much does Kenneth save?
In a basketball game, Jordan scored 25 points in the first half. In the second half, he scored twice as many points as in the first half. How many points has Jordan scored in total?

A soccer team is selling tickets for their home games. Adult tickets cost $5 each and children tickets cost $2 each. If they sell 100 adult tickets and 125 children tickets, how much total revenue did the soccer club earn?
Riley paints a house in 5 days. If he paints 8 hours a day, how many hours in total does Riley paint the house?
In a football tournament, each team plays with each other twice. There are 5 teams participating. So how many matches were played?
Melanie baked 15 muffins for a bake sale. Each muffin cost her $1.20 to make. If she sells each muffin for $2.50, how much profit will she make?

Lucy can read 20 pages per hour. She reads for 2 hours each day, 6 days a week. After 2 weeks of doing this, she decides to read twice as many pages per hour. What was the total number of pages Lucy read after 4 weeks?

Jenny and John have a collection of 200 stamps that they want to arrange in their album. Jenny can arrange 5 stamps at a time, while John can arrange 10 stamps at a time. If they keep arranging the stamps together, how many times do they need to arrange all the stamps?
Jill received a discount on a skirt originally priced at $120. She paid 25% less for the skirt. How much did she end up paying?
Luke read 20 pages of a book each day for two weeks. If the book had 350 pages, how many pages does he have left to read?
Anna has 60 apples. She gave 10 apples to her friend and 8 apples to her brother. How many apples does she have left?

Tony has a farm where he has planted 1560 apple trees and 2100 pear trees. If each apple tree can produce 12 apples and each pear tree produces 15 pears, how many pieces of fruit can Tony potentially harvest?

In an art class, 4/5 of the students are drawing a picture and one-tenth of the students painting a picture are foreign students. If the class has 200 students, how many students are non-foreign students who are painting a picture?

Jane went to the bookstore. She bought 6 novels and 3 textbooks. She also bought 5 magazines. Each novel costs $6, each textbook costs $8 and each magazine costs $3. How much money did Jane spend in total?
A software installation package is 1200 MB large. After downloading 700 MB at a speed of 4 MB per minute, how long will it take to finish the download?
Mary bought a ribbon that is 50 meters long. She cut the ribbon into 5 equal pieces. How long is each piece?

A school has 25 teachers. One-fourth of the teachers are Math faculty. Half of the remaining teachers are English faculty. The rest of the teachers belong to the Science department. How many teachers belong to the Science department?
Sarah needs 120 pages to finish her book. If she reads 15 pages per hour, how many hours will it take her to finish her book?
Lisa is part of a reading club. Every member needs to read 5 books per month. There are 30 books in the library. How many members can the club have at maximum?
Lucy has a collection of 150 stamps. She wants to divide them across 5 albums. If she already filled one album, how many stamps does she have left for the remaining albums?
Sara earns $3000 per month and wants to save three-quarters of her monthly earnings to buy a car worth $18000. How many months of saving will it take her to buy the car?

Oliver has a jar of 90 marbles. He first gives 10 marbles to his little brother. Then he loses 30% of his remaining marbles while playing with his friends outside. After coming home, he discovers that his cat has knocked over the jar and 15% of the remaining marbles were lost. How many marbles does he have left now?
Vanessa has a box of 100 markers. If she gave 30% of the markers to her classmates, how many markers does she have left?

Lily received $50 from the Tooth Fairy for losing 10 of her baby teeth. Every time she lost a tooth, she put it under her pillow for the Tooth Fairy, except for two that she lost at school.  If the Tooth Fairy left her $10 for her first tooth, how much did Tooth Fairy leave her for each tooth after the first one?

Amy is measuring the height of some of her plants. The first plant is 12 cm tall and has 36 leaves, the second plant is 15 cm tall and has 45 leaves. On average, how many leaves do these plants have per cm?
A movie theater sells small popcorns for $10 each, which is 50% cheaper than the large popcorns. How much is the theatre charging for a large popcorn?
Emily made 10 cupcakes for a party. Each cupcake has 2 cherries on top. How many cherries did Emily use in total?

A hostel hosts 5 guests who need to stay for 4 nights each. One of the guests only needs to stay for 2 nights. Another guest needs 1 night more than that. How many nights would the hostel host the remaining guests?

Robert ran for 45 minutes every day in February, a total of 28 days. He decided he should run more and began running 1 hour everyday in March, which has 31 days. How many more hours did Robert run in March?

John had $47 and his brother Jack had twice as much money as John had. Their grandma gave each of them the same amount of money so that they had a total of $150. How much did their grandma give each of them?
There are 15 birds in a tree, 7 birds flew away and later 4 birds landed on the same tree. How many birds are in the tree now?
Two years ago, Patrick was 2 times the age of his son Bob. Bob is twice as old as Steve. If Steve is 10 now, how old is Patrick now?

Tim has a sack of 500 marbles. He hands his sister 25% of them, and then she gave 10% of her new marbles to her friend. However, her friend gave back 5% of his new marbles to Tim's sister. How many marbles does Tim's sister have now?
Mike has a collection of 50 comic books. He decides to sell 25% of his collection to his friend. How many comic books does he sell to his friend?

Anna loves orange juice. She drinks 10 ounces for breakfast. At lunch, she buys a 70-ounce bottle of juice and drinks 10 ounces. She drinks another 10 ounces at dinner. If she ends the day with 60 ounces of orange juice, how many ounces of orange juice did she start the day with?

Ellie gets a book from the library. She finishes the book in five days. She read 55 pages the first day. The next day, she read three times the number of pages that she'd read on day one. On the third day, she read 15 more pages than she read on the second day. If the book is 480 pages long, how many pages did she read on the last two days?
Lucy has a garden with 60 rose bushes. Each bush yields 8 roses on average. However, due to a disease, 1/5 of the bushes do not produce roses. How many roses will Lucy have?
Sally is planning to buy t-shirts for her charity event. She is ordering 120 pieces. Each t-shirt costs $6 but with a discount, each shirt now costs $3 less. How much will Sally spend?
Molly bought 10 storybooks at a book fair. She then found another 3 more at a garage sale. If she reads 4 books in a week, how many weeks will it take Molly to finish reading all the books?
Billy bought 7 pieces of candy for $2 each and 4 pieces for $3 each. How much did Billy spend in total on candies?
Rose has 7 books on her shelf. She borrowed 4 books from her friend and bought 3 books from the book store. What is the total number of books that Rose now has?

A fast food restaurant sells chicken nuggets in packs of 6, 9, and 12 pieces. A teenager bought 5 packs of 6-piece chicken nuggets, 3 packs of 9-piece chicken nuggets, and 2 packs of 12-piece chicken nuggets. How many pieces of chicken nuggets did the teenager buy?
Teddy sold his bicycle for $100, which was 25% less than what he bought it for. How much did Teddy buy his bicycle for?
John's book reading speed is 20 pages per hour. If he reads a book which has 400 pages, how long will it take him to complete the book?
A fidget spinner has 4 sections. Dave has 15 fidget spinners. How many sections do all of Dave's fidget spinners have in total?

Alice wants to paint her room. She needs 3 cans of paint for each wall. Her room has 4 walls. She also wants to paint her ceiling which will need 2 cans of paint. How many cans of paint does she need in total?

A botanist planted 150 tulips. Each tulip can produce 8 seeds. If a packet of tulip seeds sells for $1.20 and each packet can hold 20 seeds, how much can the botanist earn from all of his tulips' seeds?
Lilly uses 3 cups of flour for every 2 cakes she bakes. If Lilly wants to bake 10 cakes, how many cups of flour will she need?
George travelled 35 miles each day from Monday to Thursday. On Friday, he travelled three times as much as he did on a weekday. How many total miles did George travel in a week?
There are 30 apartments in a building. Each apartment has 4 rooms. How many rooms are there in the building?
Eva bought a bicycle for $250 and sold it for $320. She repeated the process and sold another bicycle for $380. How much profit did she make in total?
In a box, there are 3 times as many red marbles as there are blue marbles. If there are 20 blue marbles, how many red marbles are there?
John plans to read 2,400 pages this month. He knows that an average book consists of 300 pages. If he wants to read an equal amount of pages each day, how many pages should he read per day?
A baker plans to bake 8 pies for a local event. Each pie requires 3 cups of flour. If the baker has 10 cups of flour, how many more cups does he need?
Mary received $500 for her birthday gift. She decided to spend 1/4 of it on clothes and save the rest. How much did Mary save?

Charlotte picked 60 apples from her tree. Half of the apples were bruised and the other half were perfect. Charlotte kept 20 bruised apples and gave the rest to her neighbor Andrea, who made apple sauce. If it takes 5 apples to make a jar of apple sauce, how many jars of apple sauce can Andrea make?
Matthew is running a small business and sold shirts. He sold 20 shirts at $25 each and 30 hats at $15 each. How much total did he earn from selling the shirts and hats?

Jane's internet provider charges $25 a month with an additional $5 charge for every gigabyte of data she consumes over the 10Gb limit. If Jane is billed $60 this month, how many gigabytes did she consume over the limit?
Billy has 26 toys and decides to trade half of them away to his friend Tyler. How many toys does Billy have left?
A school has 750 students. 60% of them are boys and the rest are girls. How many girls are there in the school?
Ryan has a savings goal of $500. He already has $200 saved up. If he saves $50 each week from his part-time job, how many more weeks will it take for Ryan to reach his savings goal?

Louise made a gift package for her niece in a different state. Louise placed a parcel on a scale, and then she placed in enough toys to bring the weight to 3 pounds. She then added books that tripled the weight. Next, she added another 3 pounds of toys. And finally, she added dolls that doubled the weight. What was the final weight of the gift package, in pounds?
A store sells packs of pencils for $4 each. If Tom bought 5 packs of pencils and a notebook that costs $3, how much did Tom spend in total?
Henry runs 9km per day. After running for 3 weeks straight, how far has Henry run in kilometers assuming a week has 7 days?
Leo, Mary and Paul are to share $60. Mary will take 50% of the total amount while Leo and Paul will divide the remainder between themselves equally. How much more than Paul will Mary get?

Jane and John both decide to open a joint savings account for their upcoming wedding. Jane commits to saving $75 every week, while John is able to save $150 every week. After 3 months of saving, they decide to book a venue that costs half of the total saved amount. How much does the venue cost?
Liam is stacking books in his bookshelf. Each shelf can hold 6 books. If Liam has 45 books, how many shelves does he need?
A box of chocolates costs $15 and a bouquet of flowers costs twice as much. If John bought 3 boxes of chocolates and 2 bouquets of flowers, how much did he spend in total?
In a sandbox, there are 120 toys. 30% of them are trucks, 40% are shovels, and the rest are buckets. How many buckets are there in the sandbox?
Jamie was 30 years old when Amy was 11 years old. How old will Jamie be when Amy turns 21?
Alex goes to the gym 3 times a week and each gym session lasts 1.5 hours. He also runs for 30 minutes every morning. How many total hours does he exercise in one week?
Sarah wants to make an orange juice for her family.  If it takes 2 oranges to make 1 glass of orange juice and her family consists of 4 people, how many oranges does Sarah need to buy?
Abigail is buying some art supplies online. The shipping cost is equal to a flat $6.00 fee plus $1.00 per item. If she buys 7 items, how much is the shipping fee?
Emma baked 100 cookies and put them in 5 different boxes. Her brother secretly ate 4 cookies from each box. How many cookies are left in each box?
Ms. Lim spends $100 on groceries every week. She buys milk for $4 and bread for $2 everyday. How much more does Ms.Lim spend on groceries in a year than on milk and bread?

Lisa is planning to have a garden party. She needs to buy 5 flower bouquets, each for $8, and 10 paper lanterns, each for $2. If she wants to have $10 left in her budget, how much should she have initially?
Elaine is 35 years old now. Her sister Moira is two thirds Elaine's age. In 7 years how old will Moira be?
A pizza shop makes 400 pizzas every day. If 60% of the pizzas have pepperoni, and 50% of the pepperoni pizzas also have mushrooms, how many pepperoni and mushroom pizzas does the shop make each day?

In a marathon, John ran for 2 hours at a speed of 15 kilometers per hour. Then he took a 30-minute break. After the break, he ran for another hour at a speed of 10 kilometers per hour. How far did John run in total?
Beth has 2 drawers full of pencils. Each drawer contains 75 pencils. If she gives her friend, Emily, 1/3 of the pencils, how many pencils will she have left?

To bake cupcakes, Sarah needs 5 cups of sugar, 10 cups of flour, and twice the amount of tablespoons of butter as the cups of sugar. How many total cups of sugar, flour, and tablespoons of butter does she need for her cupcakes?

A toy manufacturer produces 300 dolls. The initial production resulted in 1/5 of the dolls having a defect, so they were discarded. If the manufacturer decides to package the dolls in sets of 6, how many full sets of dolls can be made?
Mrs. Novak baked 80 cookies. She divided them into two equal parts, one for her family and one for sell. She sold her portion at a price of $1.50 per cookie. How much did Mrs. Novak earn?
There are seven bananas in a bowl. There are three more apples than bananas.  There are twice as many oranges as apples, and there are 2 less grapes than oranges.  How many fruits are in the bowl?
A bakery sells pies consisting of 60% apple filling, 15% crust, and the rest is sugar. If one pie weighs 1 kilogram, how much does the sugar weigh?

John wants to go on the summer camp to Yellowstone Park with his class and the cost is $525. His parents gave him $300 toward his fees and he must earn the rest by selling cupcakes. He makes $1.50 for every cupcake he sells. How many cupcakes does John need to sell to earn the money for the trip?
Max went to a party and ate 7 slices of pizza. Each slice had 300 calories. He then played soccer for an hour and burned off 500 calories. How many calories did Max end up with?
A shop sells chocolates for $10 per box. If Tom bought 12 boxes and received a discount of 20% on the total price, how much did he have to pay?

Jacob is cooking sushi for a party. He plans to make fifteen salmon rolls, which cost $4 each to make, and eighteen tuna rolls, which cost $6 each to make. If he wants to earn a profit of $30 selling all the rolls, what should he charge for each roll if both kinds sell at the same price?
A bookstore has 300 books. Every minutes, 5 books are sold. If the bookstore continues to sell books at this rate, how many books will be remaining after 1 hour?
There were 150 people at a concert. If 2/5 of them were children, how many adults were there?
Sarah plans to bake cookies for her 5 friends. Each friend will receive 6 cookies. If a pack of sugar makes 18 cookies, how many packs of sugar does she need?
There are four times as many sheep as cows on a farm. The total number of sheep and cows is 600. How many cows are there on the farm?

DeShaun always wins the award in English class for reading the most books over the summer. This year is no different. Summer break is 80 days long. When he arrives, he notifies the teacher that he read 60 books over the summer. Each book averaged 320 pages long. The person closest to him read 75% as much as he did. How many pages did the second person read on average each day of break?
If Aiden can fill a water tank in 3 hours, and his friend Ashton who is twice as fast as Aiden helps him, how long will it take them to fill the water tank?

A group of friends decided to plant trees in their community. Each friend can plant 3 trees per day. If they plan to plant 150 trees, and there are 5 friends available, how many days will it take to finish planting all the trees?
Sarah studied for 4 hours daily from 10th to 15th of the month, including the 10th and not including the 15th. How many hours did she study in total?
Michael is a painter who sells his paintings online. This month he sold 40% of his total paintings and gave away 5 as gifts. He now has 55 paintings left. How many paintings did he originally have?

In his basketball career, Jordan played in 500 games. 60 percent of his games he scored more than 30 points, and 30 percent of those games he scored more than 40 points. How many games did Jordan score more than 40 points?
In a school, each class consists of 20 students and there are 10 classes. If the school plans to increase the number of students in each class by 3, how many students will be in the school then?

In a candy factory, there were three candy makers. The first candy maker made twice as many candies as the second one, and the second one made 50 more candies than the third one. If the third candy maker made 500 candies, how many candies did all of them make in total?

Linda wants to replace her old fences. She needs to replace 2 fences in her front yard, which are each 20 feet long, and 4 fences in her back yard, which are each 15 feet long. The fences all have same height of 6 feet. How many total square feet of fence does Linda need to replace?
If Rob has 6 times the number of books that John does, and John has 3 times the number of books Sara does, and Sara has 2 books. How many books does Rob have?

George is renovating his home. He bought 4 gallons of paint for $30 each for the living room, 3 gallons for $40 each for the kitchen, and 5 gallons for $35 each for the bedrooms. If he also hires a painter for $200 to do the painting, how much does the entire renovation cost?
Clare has 10 apple trees in her backyard. Each tree produces an average of 70 apples. Clare wants to put the apples into crates. If each crate can hold 50 apples, how many crates can Clare fill?

A movie theater sold 85 tickets for the Friday show and 25% more tickets for the Saturday show than the Friday show. If the price of each ticket was $10, how much more money did the movie theater make in ticket sales on Saturday compared to Friday?
In a bakery, a baker needs 5 cups of flour for each cake he bakes and 3 cups of flour for each batch of cookies. If he bakes 4 cakes and makes 3 batches of cookies, how much flour does he use?
We are planning a surprise party for Vera. It will consist of 2 of her friends who have two children each, her sister, and her parents. How many people does that add up to?

Alice is practicing piano and wants to play a piece without any mistakes. She starts playing at 3 PM, and she makes a mistake every 20 minutes. If she continues playing until 5 PM, how many times will she make a mistake?
Chloe earned $5000 from her part-time job. She decided to save 2/5 of her earnings and spent the rest on shopping. How much did Chloe spent on shopping?
Jill makes 15 cupcakes every hour. If she bakes for 5 hours, and each cupcake sells for $2, how much money will she have from selling the cupcakes?
Amy has a basket of fruits. There are 8 apples, 12 oranges, 10 bananas and the rest are strawberries. If the total count of fruits is 35, how many strawberries does she have?

Jake chooses what he and his brother will watch on Hulu twice as often as his brother does. If his brother watches a total of 36 shows on Hulu per month, and each show is 45 minutes long, how many minutes of Hulu does Jake get to choose?
Alex bought 7 packs of pencils. Each pack contains 12 pencils. He wants to distribute them equally among his 4 children. How many pencils will each child get?
Tom has $150 in the bank. He made a $30 withdrawal and then a $20 deposit. What is Tom's bank balance?

Ken's friends surprised him with a birthday party and 16 of them brought presents. Each gift had a value of around $10. If Ken received all the money instead of the gifts, how much money would he have?

Janice plans to read 100 books this year. She reads 10 books in January and 12 books in February. If she continues to read 2 more books each month than the previous month, how many books will she have read by the end of the year?
John wants to build a fence around his garden. The garden is rectangular and measures 20 feet by 30 feet. How many feet of fencing will John need?
Peter bought 7 comic books. Each comic book costs $20. If Peter paid for the books using a $200 bill, how much change did he receive?
Sarah bought 4 packs of note cards. Each pack has 12 cards. She used 15 cards to send thank you notes. How many cards does she have left?
Chris has four times as many stamps as Rose. Rose has 5 times as many stamps as Lily. If Lily has 8 stamps, how many does Chris have?
A park charges $10 for adults and $5 for children to enter. If a family of 2 adults and 3 children want to enter, what is the total cost?
Alice wants 1000 folded paper cranes. She folds half by herself, and a friend folds a fifth of the remaining paper cranes for her. How many paper cranes does Alice still need to fold?
Billy has 20 crayons. He gets 10 more crayons and then gives half of them to his best friend. How many crayons does Billy have left?

Amy has three siblings and each of them received 2 chocolate bars from their parents. If the chocolate bars that Amy received were doubled in number than her siblings got, how many chocolate bars in total did Amy and her siblings have?
Sarah is thrice as old as Ann, and Chris' age is a third of Ann's age. If Ann is 12 years old, what is the difference between Sarah and Chris age?
If Kayla reads 2 books every day and Jessie reads 3 books every day, how many books do they read in total in a week?
Brian has 30 apples and half that number of oranges. For breakfast, he eats 2 apples and 1 orange. How many fruits does he have left?

John started with a certain amount of money in his bank account. He had to pay 10% of his savings for rent in January, 30% for food in February, and $2000 for a car in March. If he now has $5000 left, how much did he initially have in his savings account?
Linda borrowed 20 books from the library. She returned 5 books after a week, then 7 books after another week. How many books does Linda still have?
Michael has a bookshelf with 8 shelves. Each shelf has space for 15 books. If he already has 40 books, how many more books can he fit on his bookshelf?

Every week Julie bakes 3 cupcakes for each of her friends on Friday. She baked 21 cupcakes last Friday. This Friday, 2 of Julie's friends will not be coming. How many cupcakes will Julie bake this Friday?

Sarah spent 2 hours studying Math in the morning and 3 hours in the afternoon. On the following day, she studied twice as many hours as she did the previous day. How many hours did Sarah study in total?
David has a summer job mowing lawns. He earns $20 for each lawn he mows and mows 5 lawns per day. How much does David earn in a week if he mows lawns 6 days per week?
Jack sold 15 soccer jerseys and Tom sold 12 jerseys. If Steve sold thrice as many jerseys as Jack and Tom's combined sales, how many more jerseys than Jack did Steve sell?
At school today, Charlize was 20 minutes late. Four of her classmates were each ten minutes late than she was. What's the total time for which the five students were late?
Bob's car consumes 1 gallon of fuel for every 20 miles. If Bob has to travel 200 miles, how many gallons of fuel will he need?
A group of students are going on a field trip. Each bus can carry 42 students. If there are 168 students going on the field trip, how many buses are needed?
Liam has 3 books, each with 120 pages. If he reads 2 pages per day from each book, how many days will it take him to finish reading all the books?
Emily has been running a marathon training program where she runs an extra mile each week. On her first week she ran 5 miles. How many total miles will she have run at the end of week 12?

Karen plans to repaint her house. She needs 3 gallons of paint for the living room, 2 gallons for the kitchen and 5 gallons for the bedrooms. If a gallon of paint costs $30, how much will Karen spend on paint in total?

A television show has 5 seasons, and each season has 22 episodes. Each episode of the show is 45 minutes long. If you were to binge watch the entire show, with no breaks, how many hours would it take?
Jenny baked 24 cookies. She ate 3 and gave 5 to her friend Sarah. How many cookies does Jenny have left?

Paul invites people for a party. He invites 8 family members and twice as many friends. Paul invites also as many neighbours as the sum of family and friends. How many guests can potentially come to the party Paul is organizing?
Angela spent $80 for groceries. She spent 20% on meat and the rest on other groceries. How much did Angela spend on other groceries?
Steve owns a flower shop. Each bouquet costs $30. If a customer orders bouquets for delivery every 3 weeks, how many weeks will it take for Steve to earn $360 from that customer?
Mary is making cupcakes for her birthday party. She made 4 batches of cupcakes with 12 cupcakes in each batch. She gave 10 cupcakes to her neighbors. How many cupcakes does she have left?
Luke works at a grocery store stocking shelves. He stocks 135 cans an hour for 8 hours a day. How many cans does Luke stock in a week if he works 5 days a week?

Katie and her two friends decided to start a weekly reading club. In the first week, Katie read 5 books, her first friend read 7 books, and her second friend read 4 books. In the second week, they each read twice the number of books they read in the first week. How many books in total did they read in two weeks?
Charlie saved $600 in his bank account. He spent 20% of it on a new camera and 25% on a train set. How much is left?
In his basket, Justin has 15 apples. He has three times as many oranges as apples. How many pieces of fruit does Justin have overall?
A salesman has four vans to transport his goods. Each van has a capacity of 180 kg. How much weight in total can he transport in his vans?

Cecilia started with 200 beads for her arts and crafts project. She used up 30 for a jewelry creation and added 15 more beads. She then decided to use up another 25 beads but eventually decided to add 10 more beads. How many beads does Cecilia have left?
Chelsea's cat eats half a cup of cat food in the morning and half a cup of cat food in the evening. If a bag has 16 cups of cat food, how many days can she feed her cat with the bag?

Cynthia plans on giving away her collection of baseball cards to her 8 classmates. She has 200 baseball cards in total but accidentally dropped and lost 25 cards on her way to school. How many baseball cards will each of her classmates receive?

Emma bought pencils for her two students. Each student received 23 yellow pencils and 30 green pencils. At the end of the school year, they had taken a total of 26 pencils and returned the rest. If Emma decided to split the remaining pencils in half, how many pencils will each student get now?
A library has 500 books. 60% of the books are non-fiction and the rest are fiction. How many fiction books are there in the library?
Luke, Jack and Amy ran a combined total of 220 kilometers last week. If Luke ran 65 kilometers and Jack and Amy ran equal distances, how many kilometers did Amy run?

Leo is filling up his fish tank. The tank normally takes 20 hours to fill. He knows his pump runs at 50 gallons per hour. Water costs 5 cents for 25 gallons. How many dollars does it cost to fill the tank?
Alexa buys a jacket for $85 and a pair of shoes for twice the cost of the jacket. How much did Alexa spend in total?
A music concert sells 150 tickets for their first show. For the next show, twice as many people attend. If each ticket costs $30, how much total money did the concert make from the two shows?

Danny runs a lemonade stand and sells each cup for $2. During a really hot week, he sells 75% more cups than he does during a normal week, where he usually sells 40 cups. How much money does Danny make during this hot week?
In Simon's school, there are 15 classrooms and each classroom has 4 windows. How many windows are in Simon's school totally?

A factory produces 150 items in one week. Each item contains 30 parts. The factory decides to increase production by 50% for the next week. If each item still requires the same number of parts, how many parts will the factory need for the second week's production?

George went on a 4-day fishing trip. He brought 50 pieces of bait. He wants to use the bait at a rate of 2 for morning, 1 for afternoon, and 3 for evening fishing each day. After the trip, he wants to hand over a third of the remaining bait to his friend. How many pieces of bait will he have left after doing so?

Western Collegiate School had their cultural day last week. There were 210 students who came. If 5 students attended on their own, how many groups of friends, each consisting of 5, came to the cultural day?
Penny has 1000 dollars in her account. She spends one-third of her money on furniture and then half of the remaining amount on groceries. How much money does Penny have left?
Tina bought a pack of pencils, one sharpener and three erasers. Each pencil cost $1, the sharpener cost $2 and each eraser cost $1.50. She gave the shopkeeper $20. How much change would she get back?
A bakery sold 15 packs of cookies at $3 each, 10 cakes at $5 each, and 25 breads at $2 each. How much money did the bakery make for the day?
In a grocery store, 60% of the items are food-related and the rest are non-food related. If there are 7200 total items in the store, how many items are non-food related?
John planted 8 trees in his backyard. Each tree needs 25 liters of water each week. How much water will he need to water all his trees in a week?
Jessie is making beaded jewelry. She needs 50 beads for each necklace. A pack of beads contains approximately 200 beads. She has 10 packs of beads. How many necklaces can she make?
Out of 1200 college students, 30% failed their exams. The rest passed. How many passed their exams?
A bicycle store started with 120 bicycles. They sold 40% of them in the morning and half of what’s left in the afternoon. How many bicycles are left in the store?
A theater has 15 rows. Each row contains 25 seats. If the theater is completely full, how many people are at the theater?
George is preparing lunch for his friends. He needs 2 onions for 1 dish. If he is preparing 4 dishes, how many onions does he need?
Fifty years ago, Bella was 3 times older than Edward is now. If Edward is currently 22, how old is Bella now?

Jennifer is filling up her new pool that has a volume of 12,000 gallons. The hoses she is using provide 4 gallons of water per minute. Two hoses are used at a time. How long will it take to completely fill up the pool in hours?
Annie earned $1200 from her summer job. She used 25% of it to buy new clothes and 50% of the remaining for paying school fees. How much money did she have left?

In a family of four, the father spends 3/2 times as much as the mother on lunch every day. The father and the mother spend a total of $30 per day for lunch. How many dollars does the mother spend for lunch per day?
A tennis player has 100 tennis balls. If 20% of them are damaged and not usable, how many tennis balls does he have left?
A pizza shop makes 20 pizzas every hour. Each pizza has 8 slices. If the shop is open for 16 hours, how many slices can they make in a day?
A bracelet shop sells bead bracelets in sets of 4. Each bead set costs $20. Jessica wants to buy bracelets for her 5 friends, and each friend should get 2 sets. How much will Jessica spend in total?
Brian ran for 15 miles on Monday and 20 miles on Wednesday. On Friday he ran 50% more than he did both on Monday and Wednesday combined. How many miles did Brian run on Friday?

Kevin is tiling his bathroom. The room measures 10 feet by 15 feet, and he already has 100 square feet of tile. If a box of tiles covers 15 square feet, how many more boxes does Kevin need to complete the job?
In a parking lot, initially, there were 100 cars parked. Over time, 35 cars left the parking lot and 50 new cars entered. What is the current number of cars in the parking lot?
Cole uses 5 gallons of gas to mow a lawn. If he mows 4 lawns per day and plans to work 6 days a week, how many gallons of gas will he need per week?

In a soda factory, a machine can fill 50 cans per minute. If the machine operates for 8 hours a day, with each hour consisting of 60 minutes, how many cans can the machine fill in one day of operation?
Alice spends 30 minutes reading a book and twice that time playing her guitar. How much time does she spend on these activities in total?
A book costs $8 and is on sale for 25% off. How much does the book cost after the discount?
Marie went to buy groceries from the store. She bought 3 apples and 2 bananas. If each apple costs $2 and each banana costs $3, how much did she pay in total?
In a relay race, a team of 4 friends each run the same distance. If the total distance of the race is 2 miles, how far does each friend run?
If a park charges $10 for an adult ticket and $6 for a child ticket, how much will it cost for 3 adults and 2 children to enter?
Lea opens a bakery and sells cupcakes for $3 and cookies for $2. She sells 7 cupcakes and 5 cookies daily. What is Lea's earning per day?
Jane baked 35 cakes for a charity event. At the event, 5 cakes were sold every hour. How many hours did it take to sell all the cakes?
Sarah crafts 5-page scrapbooks for 3 of her children every month. How many pages does she create in 2 years?
John has 5 bottles of juice, and each bottle contains 2 litres of juice. He drinks 3 glasses of juice every day, and each glass contains 200 ml of juice. How many days will the juice last?
Samantha is a great actress with 3 cats. If each cat has 20 toys and she bought 15 more toys for each cat, how many toys do the cats have in total?

A gas station sells 3 brands of fuel. The low-grade fuel costs $3 per litre, the mid-grade fuel costs $3.5 per litre, and high-grade fuel costs $4 per litre. If a customer purchases 10 litres of each type of fuel, how much will it cost in total?
Julia has a coin jar that can hold 75 quarters and 50 nickels. How much, in dollars, does Julia have if she has filled her two coin jars with those coins?

In a kindergarten, there are 5 tables and each table can seat 4 children. If 3 tables are already fully occupied and two children are seated on the fourth table, how many more children can the kindergarten accommodate?
Peter wants to paint his bedroom wall. The wall area is 30 square meters. He needs 3 litres of paint per square meter. The cost of one litre paint is $15. How much will he spend on purchasing paint?
Peter turned 18 on April 30th. He started dating Lisa when he was 24. Now, they have been together for 8 years and Lisa is 2 times as old as Peter was when they started dating. How old is Lisa now?
Ryan burns 200 calories per hour while running. If he runs for 2.5 hours a day, how many calories does he burn a week, assuming he runs 7 days a week?

Ryan has $45 and his brother Ray has double the amount Ryan has. Their parents gave each of them an equal amount of money so they end up having a total of $120. How much money did their parents give each of them?

During their holiday, Mary, John, and Peter decided to learn cooking. Mary cooked 7 dishes, John cooked four dishes more than thrice what Mary cooked, Peter cooked 5 dishes less than half of what John cooked. How many dishes did they cook in total?

George works as a pizza maker. He uses 4 mozzarella cheese blocks in the morning shift and uses double the morning quantity for the evening shift. Over the weekend,he also works for afternoon shift where he uses triple the quantity of the evening shift. How many mozzarella cheese blocks does he use in a week?
Peter spends 45 minutes thrice a day on yoga. How many hours a week does he spend on yoga?
Mandy baked three boxes of cupcakes with 12 cupcakes in each box. She then divided the cupcakes equally into six packages. How many cupcakes did Mandy place in each package?

James opens up a flower shop.  He needs to pay rent of $1200 a week with an additional 20% of rent to pay for utilities and he has 2 employees per shift with the store open 16 hours a day for 5 days a week.  If he pays each employee $12.50 an hour, what are his weekly expenses to run the store?
Mike walked 30 kilometers each day for 5 days. Then he rode his bike twice the total distance he walked for the next 2 days. How many kilometers did Mike travel in all in 7 days?
Mary is 3 times older than her son. In 5 years, she will be twice as old as her son. How old is Mary now?
Macy has 27 rabbits. 7 more than half the rabbits are brown. How many rabbits are not brown?
Bob flew a kite 100 feet high. He then added 25% more string to the kite. If he used all the string, how high did the kite fly?
John worships 4 gods. Every day, he offers 5 fruits to each god. After making his offerings for 7 days, how many fruits has John given away?
A store had 400 shirts in stock. During a sale, they sold 1/4 of the shirts, and later restocked 1/2 of the sold amount. How many shirts do they have in stock now?

Jordan writes a novel in 5 months more than it took Salma to finish hers. Salma finished her book in 2 years. If one year equals to 12 months, how many months in total did Jordan and Salma spend writing their books?
Jo has twice as many stickers as Jill. Jill has 3 more than Jack, who has 17 stickers. How many stickers do Jo and Jack have combined?
Patricia has 40 books on her bookshelf. If she reads 2 books every month, how many books will be left on her bookshelf after a year?
Tom borrowed $500 from his friend. He repaid $100 every month. How many months will it take for Tom to fully pay his friend back?
A box has 8 red balls, 15 blue balls, and 12 green balls. How many balls are in the box?
Tom sells antique toy cars. For every toy car he sells, he makes $15 and for every toy truck he sells, he makes $30. How many dollars would Tom make if he sold 10 toy cars and 2 toy trucks?
Donna planned to make cupcakes for a party. She wanted to make 6 dozen cupcakes. If one batch makes 15 cupcakes, how many batches does she need to make?
Jake wants to buy a video game that is priced at $50. Jake’s mom agreed to pay 60% of the price. How much will Jake have to pay?
Josie is planning a dinner party. She bought 4 bottles of wine which cost $10 each and 3 hams which cost twice the price of a bottle of wine. How much did Josie spend in total?

Paul wants to go to the cinema with 2 friends next week. Tickets are $15 for adults and a popcorn costs $3. How much will it cost Paul and his 2 friends to go to the cinema and buy each of them popcorn?
A school has 7 classrooms. Each classroom has 30 chairs. If 5 chairs in each classroom are broken and need to be replaced, how many new chairs does the school need to buy?
John receives $50 for his birthday. He buys 2 board games and 3 action figures. If each board game costs $10 and each action figure costs $6, how much money does John have left?
Jane used a 100-watt light bulb for 5 hours each day. How many kWh of electrical energy does she use in a week (7 days)?
A plumber has 30 meters of pipe. He cuts it into 4 equal parts. He then uses 2 parts of the pipe. How many meters of pipe is left?

John has a cross-country trip coming up. The distance he needs to cover is 800 miles. If his car's average speed is 70 mph and he plans to drive for 8 hours each day, how many days will the trip take?

In Billy's room, he has bins for all his different toys. He has 5 bins of legos, 3 bins of action figures, 2 less bins of cars than legos, and the same number of dinosaur bins as cars. What is the total number of bins in Billy's room?
Mr. Johnson has 1/3 as many apple slices as Billy has. If Billy has 3 apples and each apple has 4 slices, how many slices does Mr. Johnson have?
Max's cat knocked over 12 vases in his house. Max was able to save two-thirds of the vases. How many vases were broken?

Vera is making a necklace with glass beads. She's using 25 rows of amber beads with 15 beads per row, 30 rows of frosted beads with 12 beads per row, and a pendant with 48 small diamonds. If each diamond represents 10 beads and beads cost $1 per 8 beads, how much did the necklace cost?
A bookstore buys books at $5 each then marks them up by 200% to sell to customers. If the bookstore bought and sold 100 books this month, what was its profit?

John is a fruit seller. He had a total of 100 fruits to sell at the market. Before he left his house, he gave his brother 15 apples. On the way to the market, he sold a customer 30 oranges quickly. How many fruits does John have left to sell at the market?

A bakery produces 50 more types of pastries in the morning than in the afternoon, and 30 less in the afternoon than at night. If the number of pastry varieties made at night is double compared to the morning, and the varieties at night are 180, what's the average amount of pastry varieties made daily?
An auditorium has 150 chairs. 60% of the chairs are red, the rest are blue. If 15 chairs are added and they are all blue, what percentage of the chairs are blue now?
A restaurant buys 10 crates of oranges every week. Each crate contains 20 oranges. If an orange costs 30 cents, how much does the restaurant spend on oranges in a week?
A fruit orchard has 30 more apple trees than peach trees. The orchard owner plants 50 more peach trees. If the orchard started with 100 peach trees, how many apple trees are there now?

A juice bar sells smoothies for $6.50 per bottle and salads for $7.20 per bowl. Anna wants to buy 6 bottles of smoothies, and with the remaining of the money she has, buy as many bowls of salad as she can. How many bowls of salads could Anna buy if she has $68.70 in total?
Martin had $500 in his savings account. He added $100 to his savings each month for a year. At the end of the year, how much money did Martin have in his savings account?
Emily has 15 books in her collection. She decides to buy twice as many books as she already has. How many books will Emily have after her purchase?

A family with four daughters is planning a picnic. The parents want 4 sandwiches each and each daughter wants 2 sandwiches. If each pack of bread has 6 slices and a sandwich requires 2 slices, how many bread packs should they buy?

Mike has a green apple tree in his backyard. The tree produced 13 apples on Monday, 20 apples on Tuesday, and doubled its production on Wednesday. How many apples has his tree produced in these three days?
Dan has a bag of 100 candy. He gave half to his sister. Then he ate 10. How many candy does he have left?

In a theater, regular show tickets are sold for $8, weekend show tickets for $15, and special show tickets for $25. If they sell 150 regular traffic tickets, 250 weekend tickets and 75 special tickets, what is their total earnings?
A cricket club sells 40 tickets for a match. Each ticket cost $5. Besides this, they also received a donation of $50. How much money did they make altogether?
Annie's mom baked 5 batches of cookies, with each batch containing 14 cookies. Annie ate 4 cookies and gave away 2 batches of cookies to her friends. How many cookies does she have left?
Katie's car gets 50 mpg. She drives 15 miles to school each way 5 days a week. She also drives another 50 miles a week for leisure travel. How many gallons of gas does she use a week?

Suzie only tipped 8% for her $40 bill. A tip of at least 18% is required at this restaurant. How many more dollars than normal would Suzie have to tip to meet the restaurant's minimum tipping requirement?
A farm has 80 cows. If 5 cows are sold every day, how many cows are left after 12 days
In a school, 70% of the students are boys. If there are 75 girls in the school. How many boys are there in the school?
John can run 5 km in 30 minutes. If he keeps the same pace, how long will it take him to run a marathon (42.195 km)?
Mike's son is 5 years older than Linda's son. Mike's daughter is half the age of Linda's son. If Linda's son is 10 years old, how many years younger is Mike's daughter than Mike's son?
Laura buys 12 comic books for $36. If she sells each one for $4, how much profit in dollars will she make on each comic book?
Linda bought 12 identical picture frames for $5 each and one special picture frame for $15. She also bought a $25 gift card. How much total money did Linda spend?

Lily's book has 150 pages. She reads 20% of the book on the first day, 25% of the book on the second day, and 30% of the remaining pages on the third day. How many pages are left to read after the third day?
Tom and Jerry made 20 cheese burgers which they will be selling for $5 each, and 15 pizzas which they will be selling for $10 each. How much will they earn if they sell everything?
A fisherman catches 5 fish every hour. If he fishes for 8 hours a day, how many fish does he catch in a week?

A room is measured to be 144 square feet in size with a width of 12 feet. If the homeowner decides to add an additional 3 feet on all sides, what will be the new total area of the room in square feet?
Layout artists at a publishing company can create 3 pages of a textbook every day. If a textbook requires 270 pages, how many days would it take for the layout artists to finish the textbook?

Every year, Tabitha adds a new color to her hair.  She started this tradition when she was 15 years old, which was the year she added her second hair color.  In three years, Tabitha will have 8 different colors in the hair.  Currently, how old is Tabitha?
Michael printed 150 pages of his thesis. If he can put 5 pages on each binder, how many binders does he need in total?
Drake spends $25 each month on reading books. If he decides to increase his spending by 40% starting next month, how much will he spend on books next month?
Anna is 7 years old. She is 1/5 as old as her uncle and her uncle is 4/7 as old as her grandfather. How old is her grandfather?

Peter is constructing a model castle. He wants to add three towers: a square tower that measures 3 inches by 3 inches, and two cylindrical towers, one that's 2 inches in diameter and 3 inches tall and one that's 3 inches in diameter and 2 inches tall. (The area of a cylinder is pi*r^2*h). How many cubic inches of material does he need in total?
John weighs 200 pounds and can lift twice his body weight. James can lift only 30% of the amount John can. Michael can lift 75 pounds less than James. How much can Michael lift?

Jill and Alex set up a lemonade stand to earn some summer money. Jill estimates they can make $50 per day. However, they have to subtract $15 for the cost of ingredients and supplies each day. If they both share their profits evenly at the end of each week, how much would each kid earn?
In a dog show, each participant is required to bring 5 different breeds. If there are 8 participants in the dog show, how many dog breeds are there in total?

Mike has a bag of candies. Every time he picks 8 candies from the bag, he eats 3 candies. If he continues doing this until he finishes all the candies in the bag which is initially 152, how many candies did Mike eat?

Steve and Scott want to wallpaper their room and agreed to split the cost of the wallpaper. A roll of wallpaper costs $35 and can cover up to 300 square feet. How much will each of them contribute to the wallpaper cost if their room has a total area of 900 square feet?

There are 2/3 times as many people living in City A as the number living in City B. There are 5000 more people in City C than the population in City B. If City C's population is 25000, calculate the total population of the three cities.
Lisa's book has 500 pages. She has already read 200 pages. If she reads 25 pages a day, how many days will it take her to finish the book?
Joey can solve 6 math problems in 4 minutes. How many math problems can he solve in 24 minutes?

Alice has a seven-bedroom apartment with 6 walls in each room. She wallpapers 2/7 of the rooms in the apartment and paints the rest of the rooms with bright color. How many walls in the apartment did she paint bright?

Tom drives to work every day. He spends 20 minutes traveling to work and 30 minutes from work back to his house. If he does this for 5 days in a week, how many hours does he spend on the road in a week?
The school cafeteria serves 4 pizzas each with 8 slices for lunch. If 32 students want to eat pizza, how many slices does each student get?

A pizza shop sells 4 types of pizza: cheese, pepperoni, sausage, and Margherita. The cheese pizza is $12, the pepperoni and sausage pizzas are each $15, and the Margherita is $20. If a customer orders 2 cheese pizzas, 3 pepperoni pizzas, 1 sausage pizza, and 4 Margherita pizzas, how much will the customer pay in total?

A train travels 75 miles due East, then it turns North and travels 40 miles more than it did East. If the train then goes West for half the distance it went North, how far in total has the train traveled?
A class of 60 students is planning a trip. If the bus has 35 seats and each car has 5 seats, how many cars does the class need?
Uncle Bob has 98 chocolate bars and wants to distribute them equally among his 2 daughters and 4 sons. How many bars will each child receive?

Alex sells 120 ice creams on average each day at the beach. On Saturdays, he sells about 30% more ice cream than the daily average. How many ice creams does he sell during a week if the ice cream stand is open seven days a week?
If a ball weighs 250 grams and the box it is packaged in weighs 350 grams, how much is the total weight in kilograms of 6 balls with their boxes?
There are 250 students in a school. If 60% of the students have school lunch and the rest bring lunch from home, how many students bring lunch from home?

Jane has three different types of coins: quarters, dimes, and nickels. She has 15 quarters, twice as many dimes as quarters, and thrice the nickels as dimes. If one quarter is 25 cents, a dime is 10 cents, and a nickel is 5 cents, how much money does she have in total?
A mango tree produced 200 mangoes. If 1/4 of the mangoes were damaged by pesky squirrels, how many mangoes were not damaged?

Sophia bought 30 books for her class. Half of them are math books and the rest are language books. She gave away 40% of the math books and 2/3 of the language books. How many books does she have left?
Mrs. Rodriguez had 36 boxes of crayons in her stock drawer. Every box has 24 crayons inside. She divided these equally among her 18 students. How many crayons did each student receive?

Sonia had 15 cupcakes to sell at her bake sale. She sold 5 cupcakes to her neighbor, 4 cupcakes to her friend, and the rest to the children in her neighborhood. How many cupcakes did she sell to the children in her neighborhood?

Emily is making a caramel treat. First she needs to raise the temperature of the caramel mixture from 40 degrees to 200 degrees. Then she needs to cool it down to 150 degrees. If the caramel heats at 4 degrees/minute and cools at 6 degrees/minute, how long does it take for the caramel to be done (in minutes)?

Lisa is planning a tea party at her house. She sends out invitations to 8 friends but decides to include herself in the count. If each person will have 3 cups of tea, how many cups of tea will Lisa need to prepare?
A school library has 3 shelves of books. Each shelve carries 20 books. If the librarian removes 5 books from each shelve, how many books does the library have?
Paul owns a bakery and wants to make 50 croissants. Each croissant requires 30 grams of butter. If one slab of butter weighs 500 grams, how many slabs of butter does Paul need?
John bakes bread. For every 3 loaves he bakes, he takes 1 home for himself. If John needs to supply 12 loaves of bread for a party, how many total loaves does he need to bake?

A school bus has a carrying capacity of 60 students. At the first pickup point, the number of students who entered the bus was 2/3 of its capacity. If there were 30 students at the next pickup point, how many students could not take the bus because it was full?

Kennedy Middle School is going on a field trip to the museum. There are 87 sixth graders, 100 seventh graders, and 95 eighth graders. There are 3 teachers and 4 parents from each grade accompanying them. There are 80 seats in each bus. How many buses will they need for the field trip?
A fruit shop sells oranges at $3 per kilo and apples at $2 per kilo. If Sam buys 4 kilos of oranges and 5 kilos of apples, how much will the total be?
Peter has 1200 toy cars. He sold 1/4 of his cars on the first day, and 1/6 of his cars on the second day. How many toy cars does he have left after the second day?

Jeff started with a collection of 15 comic books. He bought another 10 at a convention and his friends gave him 25 more. If it takes him 3 days to read each comic book, how long will it take him to read his entire collection, in days?
A bookstore sells novels for $10 each and magazines for $5 each. If Maria buys 3 novels and 2 magazines, how much will she pay in total?
A construction company builds 15 houses per week. How many houses will the company build in 10 weeks?
Hannah has a 250 page book. She reads 25 pages on the first day. On the second day, she reads twice as many pages as the first day. How many more pages she needs to finish the book?

James can run at a speed of 10 meters per second. His dog, Rambo, can run 5 times faster than James. If both of them started running at the same time and same direction with their respective speeds, how much far ahead will Rambo be than James after 15 seconds?
A charity event raised a total of $2000. The organizer of the event uses 15% of the total raised to pay for the event costs. How much money was used for the event costs?

Amanda is baking cookies for her family reunion. Each batch of cookies needs 3 cups of flour. She plans to bake 6 batches. However, she only has 2 bags of flour, each containing 5 cups. How many more cups of flour does she need?
If an apple tree yields 40 pounds of apples each fall, and each apple weighs about 0.5 pounds, how many apples does the tree yield each fall?

Mike drove 30 miles north, then turned west and traveled for 10 more miles than the distance he drove to the north. If he then drove south twice the distance he had traveled to the west, calculate his entire journey in miles.
At the zoo, there were 4 monkeys, 6 lions, 5 elephants and 2 cats. How many legs were there in total?

A library has 400 books. Each reader checks out three times as many books as another reader. If each reader checks out an average of 2 books per day, calculate the number of days it would take for all books in the library to be checked out.

Sarah is an interior designer who will redecorate a client's home. The house has four bedrooms, and three times as many other rooms as bedrooms. Every room requires 3 gallons of paint. All the rooms, except the bedrooms, will be painted beige. Each gallon of colored paint comes in 2-gallon cans, and beige paint comes in 4-gallon cans. How many cans of paint will Sarah need for the entire house?
Sarah works a part-time job at a library. She works 5 days a week, and each shift lasts 4 hours. If she is paid $10 an hour, how much does she earn in a week?
In a race, Martha runs 10 kilometers less than her friend Jenna, who runs a total of 35 kilometers. Calculate the total number of kilometers the two friends run together.
Annie needs 3 cups of milk to make a batch of pancakes. If she wants to make 5 batches of pancakes, how many cups of milk will she need?
Sarah runs a bakery selling cookies and brownies. Brownies cost $3 more than the cookies. She sells 15 cookies and 7 brownies and earns $80. How much does a brownie cost?

A bakery made 1800 muffins for a charity event. In the first two hours, they gave away 1/3 of the total made. In the third hour, they gave away 600 muffins. How many muffins were left at the end of the third hour?
A pizza place sells 20 pizzas per day. One eighth of them are vegetarian pizzas. How many vegetarian pizzas are sold each day?

John started a collection of baseball cards. He bought 20 cards on the first day and decided to increase the number of cards he bought daily by 50%. How many cards did John buy on the second and third days?
An office building has 15 floors. If there are 5 office rooms on each floor, how many office rooms are there in total?
John read 9 books during summer vacation. He read 2 times as many books in fall. In winter, he read three times the number he read in summer. How many books did John read over these three seasons?

Tom can use a pen for 6 hours before it runs out. He has fifteen pens and needs to write for 120 hours. A new pen costs $1. How much does he need to spend on more pens to be able to write for 120 hours?
Sarah found 40 seashells at the beach. She gave 15 seashells to her friend Kate and then lost half of what was left. How many seashells did Sarah have left?
Adrian has thirty flowers. He gave seven roses to his girlfriend, eleven flowers to his mother, and kept the rest for himself. How many flowers did Adrian keep?

Laura is a member of the relay swimming team. There are five other members on the team who swim 2 km each to complete their part of the race. Laura swims thrice as much as any member on her team to complete her part of the race. What is the total distance of the race?
Sara raises chickens and sells eggs. She collects 12 eggs per chicken each month. If she has 15 chickens, how many eggs does she collect in a year?

Billy goes to a musical instrument store to buy a guitar. He finds out that the guitar he wants is on sale. The original price of the guitar is $400 but it's currently discounted by 25%. How much will Billy pay for his guitar?
Sally likes to cook cookies. She bakes two dozens each week for six months, but one day she lost half of them. How many cookies does Sally have left?
There are 8 squirrels in the park. Every day for a week 3 new squirrels come into the park. How many squirrels will be in the park at the end of the week?

John runs a pub and sells 5 kinds of beer. Each type of beer is sold in cans of 16 ounces. People consume 200 cans of the first beer every day, 150 of the second, 180 of the third, 140 of the fourth and 280 of the fifth beer. How many ounces of beer are consumed in John's pub in a day?

Sophia has 15 books that she does not need. She gives 5 books to her friend, Emma. Then she decides to donate the rest of her books to a local community center. Each community center donation costs Sophia $3, how much does she pay in total?

Laura and her sister went to pick flowers for their grandmother. They picked all the flowers they needed on the first day for a total of $45. The day after, they picked 10 flowers. On the last day, they picked triple the flowers they picked on the second day. The price per flower is $1.50, what is the total number of flowers they picked?
The ratio of dogs to cats in a shelter is 3:7. If there are 400 animals in the shelter, how many cats are in the shelter?
Gary has 60 apples. He gave half of them to his friend and ate 10 of the remaining. Then he divided the rest equally among his 5 siblings. How many apples did each sibling get?
Mr. Smith received thank you notes from 40% of his students. Half of these notes included a $5 gift card. If he received $60 in gift cards, how many students are in his class?
One tourist at the national park noticed 10 deer. Another noticed 50% more deer than the first tourist. How many deer did both tourists notice combined?
Jennifer studied for the math test for 2.8 hours. Mary studied for 1.8 hours and Cody studied for 105 minutes. How many minutes more did Jennifer and Mary study than Cody?
Liam walked to school at a speed of 3 kilometers per hour for 2 hours. If he returned home at a speed of 6 kilometers per hour. How long does he take in total?
The zoo has a total of 47 animals. It includes 5 kangaroos, 12 monkeys, and 9 pandas. How many animals are other than kangaroos, monkeys, and pandas?

In a classroom, there are 80 desks arranged in 8 rows. In the morning, 60 students are present. 5 students arrive late and 10 students leave early before the class ends. How many empty desks are there at the end of the day?
A shop sells 10 bottles of 200 grams of honey every day. How many kilograms of honey does it sell every month?

Anthony is planning a road trip. His car has a fuel efficiency of 25 miles per gallon and the total distance of his journey is 750 miles. If one gallon of gas is priced at $3.50, how much would his entire fuel costs be for the trip?
Samantha can bake 4 batches of cookies per hour. How many cookies can she bake in 6 hours?
Joanna baked 8 dozen cookies for a bake sale. How many individual cookies did Joanna bake?
Tom read 200 pages of a book in the first week and 350 pages in the second week. The book has a total of 750 pages. How many pages does Tom still need to read to finish the book?

Ella is planning to organise an event at the community centre. In the community, Mike loves to play ping pong and would like to dedicate 12 hours playing, Ken would like to play for 15 hours and Joe would love to play for half the time Ken would play. How many hours should Ella schedule for ping pong playing at the event?
For every 16 bottles you recycle, you receive $1.00, and for every 6 kilograms of papers, you receive $2.00. If you collected 128 bottles and 18 kilograms of papers, how much money would you receive?
A library has total of 650 books. Over the weekend, 25% of the books were checked out. How many books are there in the library now?
Maria earns $15 per hour at her job and works 25 hours per week. If Maria worked for 1 extra hour each day for 5 days in one week, how much more money would she earn that week?

Tabitha adds 1 serving of honey per cup of tea in the evening.  She usually has 2 cups of tea before bed.  She buys her honey in a 16-ounce container.  If there are 6 servings of honey per ounce, how many nights will she be able to enjoy honey in her tea before bed?
Bill eats 3 pieces of fruit each day, Monday through Saturday. How many pieces of fruit does he eat in four weeks?
George feeds his pet rabbit 3 times a day. If each time he gives his rabbit two carrots, how many carrots does he need for 5 days?
The school basketball team has 12 players and the school's dance team has 18 members. If 3 basketball players and 2 dance team members move to a different school, how many members are left in total?
Jennifer recently received a 15% discount on her gym membership, which was already 10% lower than her initial fee. If Jennifer's initial gym membership cost was $120, what is her current fee?
A warehouse has 180 boxes. Three-fifths of the boxes are filled with books. The rest of the boxes are empty. How many boxes in the warehouse are empty?
Jerry is moving and has packed 14 boxes of kitchen items and 8 boxes of living room items. Each box has a weight of 10 pounds. What is the total weight of the boxes Jerry has packed?

Jack and Jill went up the hill to fetch water to fill a 600 gallon tank. Each bucket is five gallons. Jack is stronger, and is able to carry two buckets of water at a time, but Jill can only manage one. Also, Jack can complete three trips to the well and back in the time it takes Jill to make two trips. How many trips will Jill make before the tank is filled?
A tree grows 3 feet every year. If the tree is currently 15 feet tall, how tall will the tree be in 7 years?
An orange tree bears 60 oranges on Monday. On Tuesday, it bears twice as many as on Monday. On Wednesday, it bears 30 less than it did on Monday. How many oranges did the tree bear in three days?
Steve has to cycle 80 kilometers for a charity event. The track is 10 kilometers around. He has already cycled 4 times around it. How many more complete laps does he have to make?

John has a collection of rocks. He currently has 32 rocks in his collection and wants to increase this number to 100. In the shop, rocks are sold in bundles of 7. How many bundles of rocks does John need to buy to reach his desired number of rocks?
A shirt originally costs $65, but it's on sale for 40% off. If you have to pay 6% sales tax, what is the final cost of the shirt?
A bicycle store sells mountain bikes for $350 each and road bikes for twice that amount. If the combined cost of one mountain bike and one road bike is $1050, how much does the road bike cost?
Billy has 25 fish in his aquarium, and he decides to buy 3 more each week. After 6 weeks, how many fish will he have?
Harper has 100 action figures. She has half as many action figures as Lucas. If 1/4 of Lucas's action figures are superhero editions, how many superhero edition action figures does Lucas have?

A basketball team has two centers and eight forwards. The team also has twice as many guards as forwards, and the rest of the players are shooting guards. If the team has 36 players, how many shooting guards are in the team?
Kim puts 10 marbles in each box. If she has 50 marbles, how many more marbles does she need to make 25 boxes?

A basketball coach has set up a new team. He divided the 20 players into four groups. Each group needs one set of 5 basketballs for training. If each set costs $50, how much will the coach have to spend?
Linda buys 10 books for her library. Each book cost $15. Later, she also buys 5 more books which each cost $20. How much did she spend in total for all the books?
Emily has $42 for shopping. She bought a dress for $15, a pair of shoes for $10, and earrings for $7. How much money does Emily have left?

Anna and her sister, Lily, have a total budget of $15 for their weekend trip. Anna buys a $6 book, then spends $3 on snacks. Lily then buys a bracelet, spending $1 on a drink. They have both used their entire budget. How much did Lily spend on her bracelet?
Lisa runs a blog that gets 15000 visits a week. She earns $.02 per visit. How much does she make in a month (assume a month has 4 weeks)?

Hannah baked a chocolate cake and a vanilla cake for her party. She cut the chocolate cake into 12 pieces and the vanilla cake into 10 pieces. If 70 guests opted to have a slice of the chocolate cake and 50 guests wanted a piece of the vanilla cake, how many cakes in total did Hannah serve at her party?

Tim found $20 on the street. Excited, Tim decided to spend the money on comic books at $3 each and action figures at $5 each. If he bought half as many action figures as comic books, and bought 4 comic books, how much money did Tim have left?
Roger had 52 books. He placed them in 4 shelves equally. Three of the shelves contain books about science. The rest contain books about art. How many books about art does Roger have?

A farmer started the year with 50 chickens. During the year, he lost 20 chickens to illness and sold another 15. He then bought 30 more and was given 10 as a gift. How many chickens does the farmer have now?

In a 100m race, runner A is twice as fast as runner B, and runner C is half as fast as runner B. If runner B completes the 100m race in 20 seconds, how long does it take for runner C to complete the race?
If a ball is bouncing 4 times every 2 seconds, calculate the number of times 8 balls will have bounced in 5 minutes.
A bottle of wine at a restaurant costs $20. If the restaurant is offering a 25% discount on all wines, how much will you pay for the bottle after the discount?
Mark is reading a 27-chapter book, but he skips all chapters that are divisible by 4. If it takes him 15 minutes to read each chapter, how long in total did he spend reading?
A train has 15 rows of seats, with 10 seats in each row. If only 7/10 of the seats in each row can be occupied, how many seats will remain empty?
Albert earns $30 for mowing his neighbor's lawn. He also received a $10 tip. If Albert mows the lawn twice a week and there are 4 weeks in a month, how much will he earn in a month?
In a classroom, there are twice as many girls as boys. If there are 15 boys, and 2 boys and 2 girls leave the classroom, how many students are left in the classroom?
Zara has 2000 dollars in her bank. She withdraws 500 dollars and then deposits another 1300 dollars. How much does she have in her bank now?
A restaurant has 6 tables each with 4 chairs. Someone stole 1 chair from each table. How many chairs are left in the restaurant?
In a school, the number of boys is 3 times the number of girls and the total number of students is 400. How many girls are in the school?
