Sara buys 3 books. The first book costs $4 more than the second book. The second book cost $2 less than the third book that costs $14. How much, in dollars, did the books cost in total?

In her garden, Maria planted 30 rose bushes. Each bush typically yields 50 roses, but due to a drought the yield was only two-thirds of the usual amount. Meanwhile, she also planted 60 chrysanthemums with a usual yield of 40 flowers per plant. Due to the better drought tolerance, each chrysanthemum's yield was reduced to three-fourths of the original. How many flowers did Maria's garden yield?

A bookshop sells 3 types of books: fantasy books for $7 each, mystery books for $10 each, and comic books for $5 each. If a reader buys 4 fantasy books, 2 mystery books and 3 comic books, how much does the reader spend?
Rose can drink 8 glasses of water, 5 times a day, 7 days each week. How many glasses of water can she drink in 2 weeks?

Tom has opened a bakery. He is selling cupcakes for $2 each and cookies for $1 each. After a week, he decides to increase the price of each cupcake by $1 and the price of each cookie by 50%. How much would it cost for a customer to buy 3 cupcakes and 4 cookies?
Jeremy buys a car for $20000. He pays 1/4 of the price upfront as a down payment. Each month afterward, he pays off 1/15 of the remaining cost. How much does he have to pay on the second month?
Each box of toys contains 12 small cars. If Freddy bought 5 boxes, how many small cars did he buy?
John built a 15-foot wall. He used bricks that were each 0.5 feet tall. How many bricks did he need to build the wall?
Megan walks 5 miles to school every day. If there are 20 school days in a month, how far does she walk to school in a month?
A taxi driver charges $10 for a short ride, $15 for a medium ride, and $20 for a long ride. If he does 4 short rides, 2 medium rides, and 1 long ride per day, how much money does he earn per day?

During his first year playing basketball, John's mom attended 80% of the games he played.  In his second year playing basketball, John's mom attended 3 fewer games than she did in the previous year.  If John played 25 games each year, how many games did John's mom attend in his second year playing basketball?
Andrew has a library of 1500 books. Half as many of the books are fiction as are non-fiction. If Andrew donates one-fourth of his collection, how many books will remain in his library?
A group of friends went to a restaurant. The bill was $320 and they decided to leave a 15% tip.  How much was the total cost including the tip?

A recipe for a three-course meal for two people requires 1 loaf of bread and 2 cans of beans. George wants to use this recipe to make a meal for five people. If George has 2 loafs in the pantry, how many more loafs does George need to buy?
Maggie is a beekeeper and she noticed that one of her beehives produced 24 pounds of honey. She decides to sell the honey in jars which can each hold 3 pounds. How many jars can Maggie fill?
Lucas wants to finish reading a 500 page book in a week. If he has already read 230 pages in 3 days, how many pages must he read per day for the remaining days?
There are 120 biscuits in a box. Jamie eats 20% of them and then Tom eats 25% of what remained. How many biscuits are left in the box?
A school sold 50 tickets to a play. If 60% of the tickets sold were to students and the rest were sold to adults, how many tickets were sold to adults?
Liam is planning a party and needs to buy balloons. Each pack of balloons has 20 balloons and he wants to get 400. How many packs does he need to buy?
A dress on sale costs $120 after a 20% discount. What was the original price of the dress?
John has twice as much money as Mary. Mary has 3 times as much money as Jack. Jack has a third as much money as Jill. If Jill has $300, how much money does John have?
Amy buys 10 cupcakes. Each cupcake costs $3. She then gets a 20% discount because she is a student. How much does she pay for the cupcakes?
Anna spends $20 a week for her music lessons. If she received $400 on her birthday and spent it only on music lessons, how many weeks of lessons did Anna pay for?

Peter started a new fitness routine when he was 25 years old. It took 4 years for him to achieve his desired physique. After maintaining this for 15 years, he then retired from his routine at the age of 60. For how many years did he stop his routine?
Mike sells ice cream cones for $3 each. If he sells 15 ice cream cones in one day, how much money does Mike make?
A book club reads 4 different books every month. If the club decides to double the number of books they read, how many books will they read in a year?

John bakes cupcakes on Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays. On Tuesdays and Thursdays, he bakes brownies. If he bakes 15 cupcakes per day and 10 brownies per day, how many more cupcakes does he bake than brownies in one week?
Susie has 50% of a pie left. If she eats 2 slices of pie and there are 8 slices in a whole pie, how much of the pie is left?
Jim worked 40 hours last week and 55 hours this week. His manager promised to give him extra 5 hours of pay for those who work more than 50 hours. How many hours of pay will Jim receive?

A printer printed 30 pages in the first fifteen minutes. For the next 15 minutes, it printed twice that number of pages. It then printed 5 pages for the next 15 minutes. What was the total number of printed pages at the end of 45 minutes?

A coffee can on the family's counter contains coins they've been saving for a carnival. There are 73 pennies, 100 nickels, 70 dimes, and a certain number of quarters. The total amount spent at the carnival was $30. After the trip, they have 39 cents left over. How many quarters were in the coffee can?

A pizza place offers a special deal for school events. The deal includes 5 pizzas, 2 gallons of soda, and 25 plates for a price of $90. If a school's budget is $270 for such an event, how many pizzas can they buy with this deal?
Lucy has a collection of 15 seashells. She found 8 more seashells during her beach vacation. She gave 5 seashells to her friend. How many seashells does Lucy have now?
Jim's toy car shop sold 35 toy cars and 25 toy robots. Each toy car costs $3 and each toy robot costs $6. How much money did Jim's shop make?
A teacher divides the students into 4 equal groups. If there are 12 children in one group, how many children are there in total?
Samantha babysits her two nieces 3 times a week. Each babysitting session lasts 4 hours. If she gets paid $7 per hour, how much does she earn in 5 weeks?
Daisy collects $2 coins. She stored her coins in 10 containers and each container can accommodate 150 coins. If all containers are full, how much money, in dollars, does she have in total?
Sarah has 12 more books than Paul. Mark has half the number of books as Paul. If Sarah has 36 books, how many more books does Sarah have than Mark?
A grocery store has 450 gallons of milk in stock. If 60% of the stock will expire within the week, how many gallons are still good for more than a week?
John drives 45 miles every day to work and back. If the gas price is $2.5 per gallon and his card consumes 1 gallon every 15 miles, how much will he spend on gas in a week?

Jason purchased a brand new laptop and printer for his home office. The cost of the laptop was four times greater than the cost of the printer. If the printer cost $30, how much did Jason spent for his new equipments?
Mike has 10 pairs of shoes. Each of his shoes was put into a separate box. How many boxes were used?
There are 2570 white pebbles and there are 3850 more black pebbles than white pebbles. If the pebbles will be equally divided among 15 children, how many pebbles will one child receive?

A local bakery sold 120 cupcakes at $2.50 each in the morning. In the afternoon, they reduced the price by 20% and sold 85 more cupcakes. How much money did the bakery earn in total from selling cupcakes that day?

A bike shop sells two types of bikes - road and mountain. The cost of a road bike increases by 15% due to higher production costs. After a few months, the price drops by 10% from the increased price. What is the current price of the road bike if it was $600 before the increase?

You are trying to fill your pitcher using two taps. The first tap can fill the pitcher in 10 minutes, the second tap in 15 minutes. How long would it take to fill the pitcher using both taps simultaneously?
A bicycle factory produced 1000 bicycles. Because of a machinery problem, 5% of them weren't functional. How many functional bicycles remained?
Lucy is 15 years old. Her brother is three times her age. When she is 45 years old, how old will her brother be?
A farmer has 20 chickens, 15 ducks and 10 geese. If the chickens weigh 2kg each, the ducks weigh 1kg each, and the geese weigh 3kg each, what is the total weight of all his birds?

A group of 14 friends decide to take a road trip. They decide to rent 2 vans, 7 in each van. Each van requires $30 to fill up the gas tank. If they have to fill up the tanks 4 times during their trip, how much will they spend on gas in total?
A movie theater has 15 rows of seats. Each row has 12 seats. How many seats are in the movie theater?

Jane needs 50 apples to make one apple pie. She goes to the orchard and picks 500 apples, but later finds out that 30% of the apples are rotten and cannot be used. How many apple pies will Jane make with the remaining apples?
Tom owns a tea shop that opens from 9 am to 12 pm and then again from 1 pm to 4 pm, Monday to Saturday. How many hours is the tea shop open in a week?

Bakery owner Pete bakes in batches of 30 bagels, and for his store, he made 20 batches of bagels. He then gave out 2 bagels from each batch to his employees. If Pete had 11 customers and they shared the remaining bagels equally among them, calculate the number of bagels that each customer got.

A group of 150 students went on a field trip. The teacher divided them into groups of 5 for a team-based activity. If each group wins 6 points for their team, how many points in total did the students win?

A teacher purchased some school supplies that cost $13 and $24. She had the remaining $6 budget from last year and for this year, she was given a $50 budget. How much money remains in her budget after purchasing the school supplies?
A local basketball team won 45 games in the previous season. In the current season, they won 5 more games than in the previous season. How many games did the team win in total in both seasons?
Amy plans to read 5 books in a week. She reads one book after every day. After how many days will she be able to finish reading a total of 5 books?

Eve's dog needs a pricey treatment. She has had pet insurance for 36 months that cost $22 per month. The treatment cost $7000 but the insurance covers all but 30% of this. How much money did she save by having insurance?

Harry is a professional dog-walker.  He is paid to go on long walks with dogs while their families are away from home.  On Monday, Wednesday, and Friday, Harry walks 7 dogs.  On Tuesday, he walks 12 dogs.  And on Thursday he walks 9 dogs.  He is paid $5 for each dog that he walks.  How many dollars does Harry earn in a week?

Mark decides to go to a baseball game with 4 friends. Each ticket costs $75 and they all get nachos for $15 each, while half of them also get beer for $25. They later receive a 75% refund due to rain cancellation. How much is not refunded?
Sam spent $800 on a new phone which cost 20% of the original price. What was the original price of the phone?
Deborah has 20 apples in a basket. If she eats 2 apples each day for a week, how many apples will be left in the basket?
A construction company completed 60% of a bridge in 30 months. How many more months will it take them to finish the rest of the bridge at the same pace?
Natalie is playing a basketball game. She scores 16 three-point shots and 12 two-point shots. How many points did Natalie score in total?
There are 5 wagons each carrying 8 boxes.  Each box is filled with 20 oranges.  How many oranges are there in all?

Nina and Anna are planning a joint birthday party. They have decided to each contribute $50 towards the party every month for 1 year. How much money will they have by the end of the year for the party?
A trail mix recipe calls for 2 cups of peanuts, 1.5 cups of raisins, and 1 cup of almonds. Lisa wants to make this recipe 3 times. How many cups of ingredients does she need?

A coach is preparing for a basketball game. The team consists of 12 players. Each player needs 2 bottles of water, and each bottle of water costs $1.20. How much will the coach spend in total for the bottles of water?

John studied for his final exams for 8 days. Each day he studied for 2 hours more than thrice the number of hours he studied the day before. If he studied for one hour on the first day, how many hours in total did he study?

Rico and his roommates ordered a cake and asked to have it cut into 12 slices. During snack time, they only ate one-third of it. The next day, Rico ate one-third of the remaining cake. Then his three roommates ate 1 slice each. How many slices of cake were left?

John bought a box with 2000 pieces of marbles. He shared them between Bob, Tina and Mike in the ratio 1:2:3 respectively. If Mike gave Tina one-third of his share, how many marbles does Tina now have?
Anna runs a cookie baking business. She bought 10 bags of flour. Each bag of flour can make 15 dozen cookies. If Anna sells a dozen cookies for $4, how much money will she make?
A bag of marshmallows contains 50 pieces. If each marshmallow weighs 10 grams, how many kg is a bag of marshmallows?

Lewis sells 4 types of fruit at his road stand. He sells three times as many apples as he does oranges, four times as many bananas as apples, and half as many peaches as oranges. If he sells 50 oranges, how many pieces of fruit does he sell in total?
Samuel is a comic book collector. He buys 7 comic books each month and gives 2 to his younger brother. He does this for 12 months straight. How many comic books does Samuel have in a year?

Samantha is a librarian. On average, she checks out 30 books per day. The number of borrowed books increases by 50% on every Wednesday. How many books does she check out in a week if the library is open from Monday to Saturday?

Eric is eating in a new fancy restaurant that has the habit of tipping the waiter 10% of the cost of the meal. If the meal cost $45 and Eric has $10 to get back home, how much money does he have to have before eating in the restaurant?

Sarah baked 22 cupcakes for a school sale. 4 were eaten by her brother before the sale. 2/11 of the remaining cupcakes didn't sell. If Sarah gave 1/4 of the unsold cupcakes to her friends, how many cupcakes did she giveaway?
Lee is pumping up tires in a bicycle shop. It takes him 4 minutes to pump up one bicycle tire. How long will it take him to pump up 15 bicycles, if each bike has 2 tires?
A manufacturer uses four machines to produce his products. Each machine can produce 1000 units per hour. How many units can the manufacturer produce in 5 hours?
In a classroom, there are 28 students. 40% of them are girls. How many boys are in the classroom?
Peter has 6 boxes of pencils. Each box contains 10 pencils. He gives away 2 pencils each day. How many days do the pencils last?

Amy is making cookies for her school fair. Each batch makes 14 cookies and she needs to make 756 cookies. If the ingredients for each batch cost $2.50, how much will Amy spend on baking all her cookies?
John is 5 years old today. In 10 years, he will be half the age of his brother. How old is his brother today?
Bryan has 12 pigs and 6 cows. Each pig needs 2 gallons of water and each cow needs 4 gallons of water. How many gallons of water does Bryan need to cater to all the animals?
Shawn has 64 pieces of candy. He gave 4 to each of his friends. If he has 8 pieces left, how many friends did he give candy to?

Jasmine is selling her old comic book collection. Each time she posts a new comic online, the price jumps by $3. The bidding starts at $5 and ends at $35. How many times was the comic bid on before it was sold?
There are 24 bottles of water that each contain 500 ml. There are 16 cans that each hold 355 ml of soda. How many liters of beverages are in the bottles and cans in total?
John needs to walk 4 miles to get to the library. If it takes him 30 minutes to walk one mile and he has already walked 2 miles, how many more minutes does he have to walk to reach the library?
A tank has a capacity of 500 gallons. It is currently filled at 80%. How many more gallons does it need to be full?
A carpenter has 80 planks of wood. He needs 5 planks to make a table, 4 planks to make a chair, and he has just completed making 12 chairs. How many tables can the carpenter still make?
Kelly and Megan are having a pizza party. They ordered 3 pizzas which each have 8 slices. If they decided to share each slice of pizza among 4 people, how many slices will each person get?
Amy spends 30 minutes walking her cat and one third as long playing with her. Then she takes her for a grooming appointment that lasts 45 minutes. How much time does Amy spend with her cat total?
10 years ago, the combined age of Robert and his brother was half of their father's age. If Robert is now 50 years old and 5 years older than his brother. How old is their father currently?
Eva and Mia are both painting. Eva can paint 80 square feet in an hour. Mia can paint 30 square feet in an hour. How many more square feet does Eva paint in 50 minutes compared to Mia?
A grocery store sold 6 oranges for one dollar. Maggie bought 3 oranges and two bags of apple slices, which cost two times as much as the three oranges. How much did Maggie pay for her purchase?
In a school, there are 150 students. 2/5 of them participate in drama club and 1/3 of them join the science club. How many students join neither club?

The cable car carries passengers from the foot of the hill to the peak, and then lets them off so they can trek back down. If it takes a passenger 20 minutes to ride the cable car from foot to the top of the hill, and then it takes 10 minutes to trek back down the hill, what is the maximum number of trips up and down the hill a passenger can make in 3 hours?
Cindy runs a cafe and sells 4 types of pastries each day. One day, she sells 20 croissants, 30 danishes, 15 bagels and 50 donuts. What percentage of total pastries sold were donuts?
Grace reads a 300-page book in 10 days by reading the same number of pages each day. How many pages does she read in per day?

At a school of 30 students, a survey was conducted on their ice cream preferences between chocolate and vanilla. 22 students said they like chocolate and 14 said they like vanilla, including 8 students who said they like both chocolate and vanilla. How many students do not like either chocolate or vanilla?
Rachel baked 36 muffins for a bake sale. She sold three-fourths of them in the morning and the rest in the afternoon. How many muffins did she sell in the afternoon?

Lee runs an online clothing store. He sold 10 dresses for $35 each and 5 jumpers for $45 each. He also sold 2 coats at the same price. If his total sales for these items were $820, how much did each coat cost?
Katy wants to buy pens for her classmates.  There are 20 other students in the class and everyone needs 3 pens. A box of 5 pens costs $2. How much does she spend on pens for her classmates?
Benny's bakery baked 28 loaves of bread every day. During the weekend, Benny baked an additional 15 loaves. How many loaves of bread did Benny's bakery bake in a week?

Lucy is playing a game where she needs to score an average of 4 points per round to win. So far she has scored 5, 3, 4, 3, 4, 5, 3 and 4 in the first eight rounds. How many points does she need to score on the next round to win the game?

An auditorium was hosting a concert by a popular country music band. Before the concert started, an announcer mentioned that there are 50 rows of seats in the auditorium, each row consisting of 35 seats. During the performance, all but 115 seats were occupied. How many people were there in the auditorium?

In a school, 1/5 of students participate in choir. Of these students, 40% also participate in a drama club. If the school consists of 500 students, how many students are in both the choir and the drama club?

Emily has just started a garden in her backyard. She has a grid of 4 by 8 where she can plant flowers. Each grid can carry one flower. In her first attempt, she was able to plant 25 flowers. Next month, she decided to fill the rest of the grids. How many flowers does Emily need?
A football field is 120 yards long.  If a player runs the length of the field 3 times in practice, how many yards does the player run?
John ran 5 kilometers a day for a week. If one week is equal to 7 days, how many kilometers did John run in one week?
A baker baked 120 cupcakes for a party. 95 cupcakes had blue icing while the rest had red icing. If each pack can contain 5 cupcakes, how many packs does he need to pack all red iced cupcakes?
Michelle sleeps 5 hours a day and his doctor advised her to sleep 7 hours a day. How many hours of sleep does Michelle lack in a week?

There is a caterpillar infestation in a forest. A group of biologists are monitoring 5 areas of the forest. Each area has an average of 20 caterpillars. On average, around 10% are expected to turn into butterflies. How many caterpillars are expected to turn into butterflies?
There are 12 cupcakes in a box, and each box costs $15. Emily is going to buy enough boxes to serve two cupcakes per guest, and there are 10 guests. How much will Emily spend?
Julia bakes cookies and sells each for $2.00. She spends $8.00 on ingredients. If she sells 15 cookies, how much profit does she make?
At a zoo, there are 4 times as many monkeys as lions. If there are 12 lions, how many animals are there in total?

Sam had 15 liters of fuel in his boat's tank. He used 5 liters of fuel for a short trip and then 3 liters for another trip. The tank can hold up to 20 liters. How many liters of fuel will Sam need to refill the tank?
Maddie and her friends arrange a book club meeting every third Saturday of the month. If the month started on Tuesday, on which date will they meet for the book club?
Samantha spends $150 on lunch every month. She decides to save more money and reduces her lunch expenses by 20%. How much does Samantha spend on lunch after the reduction?
A group of friends decide to split the bill at a restaurant equally. If their bill in total is $250 and there are 5 people in the group, how much does each person have to pay?
Joe went to a bakery and bought 8 pies for a party. Each pie costs $15. After purchasing so many pies, he was given a discount of 30%. How much in total did Joe spend on pies?

A typical tournament of tennis has 4 rounds. There are 8 games in the first round, 4 in the second round, 2 in the third round and 1 during the finals. If each game requires new tennis balls, and on average each game uses 5 cans of tennis balls, how many tennis balls in total are used at the end of the tournament if each can has 3 tennis balls?
A tanker has a capacity of 6000 gallons. If it already contains 2200 gallons of water, how much water can it hold?
A car normally costs $15,000. During a sales event, the price of the car will decrease by 15%. How much will 3 cars cost during the sales event?

On Sunday Luke has 20 comic books. On Monday Luke loses a quarter of them. On Tuesday Luke buys 5 comic books. On Wednesday he buys twice the number he had on Monday. How many comic books does he have on Wednesday?
Eva gets paid $50 per day plus $1.50 for each book she sells. If she earns $80 on Monday, how many books did she sell?

Alice, Jason, and Irma are friends. Alice drinks 7 cups of tea every day. Jason drinks 5 cups of tea. Irma drinks 6 cups of tea every day. In one week, how many cups of tea do the friends drink together?
If one paint tin covers 2 walls in a room, and Chris has 9 rooms in his house he wants to paint, all of which have 4 walls, how many paint tins will Chris need to buy?
Danny has 3 more stuffed toys than Lisa. Lisa has 2 times as many stuffed toys as Kevin. If Kevin has 4 stuffed toys, how many does Danny have?
Lucy bought 4 pizzas and 2 drinks. Each pizza cost 10$ and each drink cost 3$. How much money did Lucy spend in total?
Stella has 2 cats. They eat an average of 10 pounds of food a week. One cat eats 7 pounds a week. The second eats triple this. How much does the third cat eat a week?

Jenny's garden has 12 rows of carrots and 4 rows of tomatoes. Each row of carrots yields 9 pounds of carrots and each row of tomatoes yields 6 pounds of tomatoes. How many total pounds of vegetables does Jenny's garden yield?
Sam reads 30 pages in an hour, and Mike reads 20 pages in an hour. How many more pages does Sam read than Mike in 4 hours?
Albert and Jane decide to split a large pizza. Albert has 35% and Jane eats the rest. If Albert's portion is worth $6, how much does the pizza cost?

A school choir has 25 girls and 15 boys. The girls are wearing red scarves that cost $4.5 each and the boys are wearing blue scarves that cost $3.5 each. All students are also wearing white shirts that cost $10 each. How much did the school spend on the outfits?
Marco is collecting coins. He has 12 quarters, 15 dimes, and 18 pennies. How much money does he have in total?
$300 was divided between Amy and Chris. Chris received 2/3 of the money. From his share, Chris then spent 1/4 of the original $300 on a new jacket. How much does Chris have left?

Tom baked a pie and invited his friends over. He cuts the pie into 4 equal pieces. Two of his friends took 1 piece each and one of his friends took 2 pieces. How many pieces of pie does Tom have left?
Jenny saves $20 every week. How much money will she have after 2 years (assume 1 year = 52 weeks)?

A group of friends decides to go hiking in the woods. Each person manages to collect 7 unique leaves from different trees. At the end of their hike, they realized that 3 of them lost 3 leaves each and another friend managed to lose all of his leaves. If they had a total of 26 leaves left by the time they got back, how many friends were in the group?
Tom had 4 less toys than thrice as many toys as Jonas. If Jonas had 18 toys, how many toys does Tom have?
Liam and Owen went to the park. Liam found 7 acorns and Owen found 3 times as many. How many acorns do they have in total?

Lisa's school is raising funds for their field trip. Each student is required to sell 20 pieces of cookies that cost $1 each. Lisa's class has 30 students. How much will they raise from selling all the cookies?
Alan read a 120 pages book in 6 days reading 10 pages each morning. How many pages did he read each afternoon?
Dave makes a profit of $20 on each book he sells. If he sold 30 books in January, 25 in February, and 35 in March, calculate his total profit.
Danny needs to buy 4 bags of cement for a building project. He already has 1 bag. Each bag costs $50. How much will he spend to buy the remaining bags?
There are 75 students in a school. One-fifth of them are in Grade 5, the rest are in Grade 6. If 7 students moved from Grade 5 to Grade 6, how many students are there in Grade 6 now?
A bag shop retails a bag for $350 but discounts it for 20% off. If John bought the bag and was charged 5% sales tax, how much did he spend in total?
Lila spent 1.5 hours studying math and then 2 hours studying English. Josiah spent 115 minutes studying those subjects. How many more minutes did Lila study than Josiah?

Emily plays violin for 15 minutes every day. She spends 25 minutes practicing piano. She practices guitar for 20 minutes, 3 days this week and 40 minutes the rest of the days. How many minutes does Emily spend on music this week?
Two people are building a puzzle. One person builds 3 pieces for every 2 pieces the other person builds. If they have together built 50 pieces, how many pieces has the second person built?
Riley uses 3 apples to make 1 jar of apple jam. She wants to make 15 jars of jam. How many apples does she need?
Samuel bought cupcakes at $20 per dozen. He sold them at a school fair for $10 for half a dozen. How much profit did Samuel make if he sold 40 dozens?

John's lemonade stand sells each glass of lemonade for $3. He sells 40 glasses in the morning. In the afternoon, he decides to lower the price to $2 and sells 70 glasses. How much money does he make in total?

A father buys a pack of candies. He kept 1/2 of the candies and divided the rest between his 2 children. The older got 10 candies while the younger got half as much. If there are 36 candies in the pack, how many candies did the second child gets?
A man bought a dozen oranges for $3. He wants to sell them for a 50% profit. How much should he sell each orange for?
Crystal Lake has three times as many fish as the two other lakes combined. If the other two lakes have 120 fish each, how many fish are in the three lakes altogether?
Laura is planning her wedding. She expects 220 people to attend the wedding, but she has been told that approximately 5% typically don't show. How many people will go to her wedding?
A dog’s leash is tied to a pole so it can roam in a circular area. The leash is 50 feet long. What is the total area, in square feet, that the dog can roam in?
Linda bought 5 servings of pasta for $12 each and 3 glasses of wine for $8 each. How much money did she spend in total?
A fisherman catches 35 fish every day. He sells each fish for $15. After a week, how much money will the fisherman have made?
A farmer collected 230 eggs from his chickens and sold 70% of them at a market. How many eggs did he sell at the market?

Jane was learning to play guitar. On the first day, she practiced for 1 hour, the second day she practiced for 2 hours, the third day she practiced for 3 hours, and so on. How many hours did she practice in total over the first 7 days?
Liam bought a pair of sneakers for $60 and sold them for $100. If he continues to do this with 15 pairs, how much profit is he making?
Dave bought a bottle of wine at a 20% discount. If the original price was $50, how much did Dave pay for the wine?
There are 25 students in a classroom. If 20% of them are wearing glasses and 30% are wearing braces, how many students are not wearing either glasses or braces?
In a school, there are 5 times as many students as teachers. If there are 18 teachers then how many students are there?

Kristy baked cookies because her friends are coming over to her house. She ate 2 of them and gave her brother 1 cookie. Her first friend arrived and took 3 cookies. The second and third friends to arrive took 5 cookies each. If there are 6 cookies left, how many cookies did Kristy bake?
Jim bought a box of chocolates and found that 30% of the chocolates were dark chocolate, and the rest were milk chocolates. If there were 42 chocolates in the box, how many were milk chocolates?
Frank spends $5 every day on lunch. If he decides to pack his own lunch for 4 days, and saves $30 as a result, how much did his homemade lunches cost him each day?

A pharmacy sells a box of face masks for $15 each and hand sanitizers for $5 each. Anna buys three boxes of face masks and five hand sanitizers for her family. How much does she pay for these hygiene items?

Grace ran a marathon that is 42 kilometers long. When she reached the 70% mark of the distance, she suffered a muscle cramp. If it took her another 30% more time to finish the race due to the injury, and it initially took her 3 hours to reach the 70% mark of the race, how long did it take her total?

Sally, Emily and Jane are training for a triathlon. Each leg of the race is of different length. Sally begins the race, cycling at a speed of 10 miles per hour for 3 hours. Emily swims at a rate of 2 miles per hour for an hour and a half. Jane completes the race, running at a speed of 6 miles per hour. If the total time taken to finish the race is 6 hours, what is the total distance of the race?
At a bakery, the number of cookies baked each day is twice the number of cupcakes baked. If 250 cupcakes are baked each day, how many cookies are baked in a week?
An SUV consumes 1.5 liters of fuel every kilometer. If the price of one liter of fuel is $2, how much will it cost to travel 230 km in the SUV?

In an art competition, Sara painted 3 times as many pictures as Robert. Robert accidentally spilled water on some of his artworks, resulting in the loss of 12 paintings. If Robert originally had 10 paintings, how many more paintings does he need to create to beat Sarah by just 1 painting?

A wooden desk requires 2 planks of wood. If you are making a furniture set which includes 1 desk, 2 chairs, and 1 table, and each chair requires 1 plank and each table requires 4 planks, how many planks of wood are needed?
Liza baked a large batch of cupcakes for her niece's birthday party. She made 72 cupcakes in total. She placed the cupcakes into boxes that hold 6 cupcakes each. How many boxes did she need?
Lucy has 18 cupcakes. She ate 5 and then baked twice as many as she had left. How many cupcakes does Lucy have now?
Bruce has a goldfish that eats 2 fish flakes per day. After 15 days, the fish has eaten 10 flakes and has 20 flakes left. How many flakes did Bruce originally have?
Lena and Kyle are saving for a car which costs $24,000. They plan to save this amount in 2 years. If they share the monthly savings equally, how much does each person save per month?
John can paint 5 walls in an hour. He can also wallpaper 2 walls in an hour. If he spends 4 hours painting walls and 3 hours wallpapering walls, how many walls has he worked on?

Sandy takes 1 hour to drive to school and 2 hours to walk home from school. Sandy drives to school once a day and walks home from school every day. How many hours in total does she take to get to and from school a week?
A bookshelf has 50 books. 1/5 of the books are fiction and the rest are non-fiction. How many non-fiction books are there on the bookshelf?

Jenny is saving for a new bicycle that costs $300. She saves $20 from her allowance each time she does chores. One day, her parents gave her an extra $50 as a reward for good behavior. After altogether, they saw that she already saved 75% of the amount. How much did Jenny initially save from doing her chores?
Anna goes to the grocery store and buys 4 packs of chicken each costing $6.50 and 5 packs of beef each costing $8. How much in total did she spend?

Jack is a fitness trainer and he advises his clients to do pushups daily. He has 2 clients doing 80 pushups each daily. Jack adds 25% more clients this week who do the same amount of pushups. How many pushups are his clients doing daily in total?
Jack had a gallon of apple juice, and he wants to distribute it equally into 8 bottles. How many mL of apple juice will be in each bottle, considering that 1 gallon is approximately 3785 mL?
Peter has been saving money. He saves $15 each week. How much money will he have after a year (52 weeks)?
Peter ran 5 miles each day for 6 days. How far has he run in total?
In a barbecue party, Ross grilled 5 burgers each costing $3 and 3 hotdogs each costing $2. What is the total cost of all the food he grilled?
John spends 45 minutes each day practicing piano. In addition, he spends 30 minutes each day practicing guitar. How many hours a week does he spend practicing music?
Nancy works 8 hours a day. She sleeps 7 hours each day. How much free time does she have in a week, if she spends 2 hours each day for cooking and cleaning?
A writer used 3 sheets of paper for every 2 pages of her book. If the entire book is 120 pages, how many sheets of paper did the writer use?

The school choir is selling cupcakes to raise money for their concert. A local bakery donated 50 boxes of cupcakes, with each box containing 20 cupcakes. If the choir sells the cupcakes for $1 each, how much money will they make?

Tom’s school has a collection week where each student is asked to collect used books for a fair. Tom and his friends in total collected 28 books. Tom collected 5 books and each of his friends collected 4 books. How many friends participated?
Ben bakes 5 dozen cookies for a charity event and sells them for $6 a dozen. How much money does he make from selling the cookies?
A toy company produces toy cars that cost $3 to manufacture each. The company sells the toy cars for $7 each. If they sold 100 toy cars, how much profit did they make?
Lisa has 12 candies. She gave half to her friend. How many candies does she have left?

To make a necklace, Emily needs 12 star beads and 8 heart beads. If a pack of star beads contains 4 beads and a pack of heart beads contains 2 beads, how many packs does Emily need to make the necklace?

Claire collects seashells and places them in a jar. She can fit 70 seashells in the jar, unless they are larger ones, which are three times as big as the rest. She has already put in 10 large seashells and 20 regular seashells. How many more regular seashells can she fit into the jar?
Doris weighs twice as much as Jane's weight. If Doris weighs 160 pounds, what is Jane's weight?
Lucy bought 3 dresses at $50 each and 5 pairs of shoes at $30 each. She also bought 2 handbags for $40. Calculate the total cost of her shopping.
Tom has a collection of 35 comic books. He has 9 Batman books, 11 Spiderman books, and the rest are Superman books. How many Superman books does Tom have?

Nathan is working on a large painting for his art class. The amount of time he spent painting it is equal to twice the amount of time he spent sketching it plus 10 minutes. If he spent 50 minutes on the painting total, how many minutes did he spend painting it?
Timmy has $20. He wants to buy a toy car that costs $4 and a bag of candies that costs $2. How much money will Timmy have left after the purchase?

Stephen Curry is currently averaging 34 points per game. His team has played 10 games this season. He has a goal of averaging 40 points per game for the entire 25 game season. How many points does he need to average per game to reach his goal?
Aaron catches three times the amount of fish as Jacob who caught 5.  Benjamin catches twice the amount of fish as Aaron.  How many fish did Benjamin catch?
If Rebecca receives $20 a week as allowance and saves all of it, how much money will she have saved after 52 weeks?

Rebecca has a vegetable garden. When it is full, the area holds 100 plants. She plants vegetables at a rate of 10 per hour. Unfortunately, there are some pests that eat her vegetables and they eat about 2 plants per hour. How long will it take for her to fill her vegetable garden, in hours?

A box of chocolate has 50 milk chocolates, 60 dark chocolates, and 70 white chocolates. Frank eats a quarter of each of the types and gives 10 milk chocolates to his mother. How many chocolates are still in the box?

Lucy started with 200 jelly beans in her candy jar. She ate some jelly beans every day for a week (7 days). At the end of the week, she only had 85 jelly beans left. How many jelly beans did she eat per day?
A mathematics conference had 120 participants. Each participant received a bag that contained 4 notebooks, 3 pens, and 2 pencils. How many notebooks, pens, and pencils were there altogether?
John had 12 baseball caps, he sold 3 of them and someone stole 2. Then, John's brother gave him 10 more. How many baseball caps does John have right now?

In a school of 800 students, 35% of the students participate in the soccer club. Out of the soccer club students, 80% also participate in the maths club. How many soccer club students do not participate in the maths club?

Billy had $600 in savings. He wanted to buy a new skateboard that cost $150. After he bought the skateboard he also decided to buy helmets and knee pads which cost him half the price of the skateboard. How much money does he have left?
In a basket, there are 3 apples and 5 oranges. If Mary ate 1 apple and 2 oranges, how many fruits are left in the basket?
A bus has 40 seats. If half of the seats are filled, how many seats are still available?
John has bought 30 balloons for his birthday. He filled them all with helium, but 20% of them popped. How many balloons does John have left?
There are 4 trees in a park. Each tree has 7 birds on it. If half of the birds fly away how many birds are left on the trees?
Joey's Pizzeria includes 8 pieces of pizza. Fifteen sixth-graders each bought 25 pizzas from Joey's Pizzeria and put them in their boxes. How many pieces of pizza are the children carrying in total?
Bill baked 60 chocolate cookies and 30 peanut butter cookies. If Bill gave away 10 peanut butter cookies and three times that number of chocolate cookies, how many cookies does he have left?

Martin dropped 3 of his 10 coins in a fountain. These were given to him by his dad. In one month, the value of the coins doubled. So, he deposited all the money he got from the coins to his bank account. If Martin deposited $60, how much money, in dollars, did Martin initially have?
A small bookstore sells 5 books per day. A larger bookstore in the same town sells 8 books per day. How many days would it take for them to sell a combined total of 100 books?
Liam loves to collect stamps. He has a collection of 180 stamps. Every month, he adds 15 stamps to his collection. In how many months will he reach a collection of 225 stamps?

Meenu started a tutoring job where she was paid $15 per hour. After three weeks, her hourly wage was increased by $3. If she worked for four hours each day for five days both before and after the pay raise, how much money did she earn after three weeks?

In a quiz, Tina answered 30 questions correctly and 5 incorrectly. Her competitor, Sarah, answered 5 less correctly and twice as many incorrectly as Tina. If every participant got a different question, how many questions were there in total?
Emma has $150 in her wallet. If Emma buys a novel for $20 and a dictionary for $30. How much money does Emma have left?

A school is taking a trip to the zoo. They will bring all 40 students in the class, plus 3 teachers. The entrance fee for students is $7 and $10 for adults. What is the total amount the school will pay for the zoo entrance?
John works 40 hours a week. He is paid $10 an hour. How much does he make in a week?

Susan mostly cooks her meals at home. One day she decides to calculate her weekly food costs. She spends $8 on breakfast, $10 on lunch, and $15 on dinner daily. How much does she spend on food in a week?
Sam is painting a mural and needs to mix blue and white paint. He needs a total of 78 litres of paint. If he wants to use 2/3 of the paint to be blue, how much blue paint will he need?
Emily bought five books and three pencils for a total of $60.  If all the books were the same price, and each pencil was twice as expensive as one book, then how much did a single book cost?

A box of crayons that normally costs $4 is now sold at $3.50. A pack of construction paper was sold at a discount of $1.50. How much will you save via discounts if you buy 5 boxes of crayons and 3 packs of construction paper?
A classroom has 24 students. Each student is given a box of candies. If each box has 20 candies, how many candies are given to the students altogether?
A movie ticket at a theater is half the cost of a concert ticket. If a concert ticket is $30, how much will Jack pay for 5 movie tickets?
Tommy collects stamps. He has 40 German stamps, 20 American stamps, and 3 times as many British stamps as American stamps. How many stamps does Tommy have in total?
In a soccer tournament, each team plays every other team once. If there are 7 teams, how many games are played in total?
Oliver can finish a project in 5 hours. His co-worker Ben can finish the same project in 7 hours. If they decide to work together, how long would it take to complete the project?
Ella has 3 dogs and she feeds them 2 bowls of food each, twice a day. How many bowls of food does she need in one week?
In a garden, each flower needs 2 liters of water per week. If there are 20 flowers in the garden, and if 1 liter of water costs $0.50, how much will it cost to water all the flowers every week?
A toy store has a total of 80 toys. The number of teddy bears is 2 times the number of toy cars. If there are 30 toy cars, how many teddy bears are in the store?

The school is planning a school field trip and the bus they hired can hold 46 people per ride. There are 5 teachers and 212 students who will be attending the field trip. How many bus rides required to transport all teachers and students?
Shelley has 15 apples and 8 oranges. She gives 3 apples and 2 oranges to her friend. How many fruits, in total, does she have left?

There is a rectangular garden that measures 20 meters by 15 meters. There is a porch on the garden that measures 5 meters by 3 meters. How many square meters of the garden is not covered by the porch?
Brenda runs 2 miles every day for a week. How many miles does Brenda run in a week?
Emily has 7 dolls. She bought 5 more, but then lost 2. How many dolls does Emily have now?

John has 1500 comic books in his collection. 70% are Marvel comics and 30% are DC comics. Paul has half as many DC comics as John and he has only 1000 comics in total. What percentage of Paul's comic collection is Marvel?
Laura purchased 5 novels and 8 textbooks. Each book costs $20. How much money did Laura spend in total?
John is 4 years older than Maria. Maria is 18 years younger than Joseph. Joseph is 3 times older than Sam. If Sam is 15, how old is John?

Lisa is a hairdresser. Her clients pay her a basic rate of $50 per haircut. She receives an additional $20 for every hour spent on colouring hair. If she cuts the hair of 10 clients and spends 15 hours on hair colouring in a week, how much does she earn in a week?
Daniel said he would do 4 more pushups per minute than Elliot did. Elliot then did 50 pushups at a rate of 5 pushups per minute. Compute the total number of pushups they did together.
In a school, there are 20 boys and 24 girls in a classroom. If 4 boys and 3 girls are absent, how many students are there in the classroom?
Anna wants to buy a laptop worth $1350. She saved 2/3 of the amount. How much more should she save to buy the laptop?
A flower shop carries 3 times as many roses as tulips. If there are 25 tulips, how many flowers are in the store if it also carries 45 lilies?
A container held 250 liters of water. Peter drank 3 liters per day and his dog drank 2 liters per day. After 25 days, how much water was left in the container?

Katrina buys 250 gallons of paint to paint a mural. After using up all the paint, she decides she needs 30 gallons more. If she ends up only using 75% of the new paint, how much paint does she have left over?
Alex has a terrible toothache. He takes 3 ibuprofen tablets of 200 mg each and he does this every 6 hours for 24 hours. How many grams of ibuprofen does he end up taking?

If a building has 15 floors and each floor has 50 apartments. Due to some damages from an earthquake, 150 apartments had to be evacuated and repaired. After the repair, 40 apartments were still inhabitable. How many apartments are currently being used in the building?
Anna has 5 blue marbles, 3 green marbles and 8 red marbles. She gives 6 red marbles to her friend. How many marbles does Anna have left?
A teacher had 50 papers to grade over the weekend. She graded 25% of them on Friday, 40% on Saturday, and the remaining papers on Sunday. How many papers did she grade on Sunday?

Jared will visit two friends' houses today. He needs to travel 5 miles to get to Tom's house and then 8 miles to get to Jerry's house. If Jared has already walked 4 miles, how many more miles must he walk before arriving at Jerry's house?

Emma has 20 books and plans to buy 3 new books for each of her friends. She has 4 friends. After buying the books for her friends she wants to buy 1 extra book for herself. How many books does she have now?
Lisa spends 25% of her salary on rent. If she spends $750 on rent, how much is her entire salary?

There are 50 flowers in a garden. 6 are sunflowers. One fourth of the remaining flowers are roses. 8 of the rest are lilies. One third of the remaining are daisies. The rest are all different types of flowers. How many types of flowers make up only one type in the garden?

There are 30 trees in Anna's orchard, each with 15 apples. In Ben's orchard, there are four times as many trees as in Anna's orchard, each with 10 apples. What is the combined total number of trees and apples in Anna's and Ben's orchards?
Jennifer runs at a speed of 6 miles per hour. If she trains for a marathon by running for 3 hours a day for 2 weeks, how many miles in total will Jennifer have run?

David's students are about to do a mathematics assessment. David gets a $700 bonus if their average score is above 85, plus an extra $20 bonus for every additional point the average score increases above 85. So far, David has graded 10 tests, and the average is 80. Given that each student can have a maximum score of 200, what combined score do the last two tests need to have for David to earn a $900 bonus?

During a school trip, four friends decided to evenly split the cost of a $50 gift for their teacher. However, one friend forgot his wallet and didn’t pay his part. How much does each of the other friends have to pay to cover his part?

Judy baked 20 cookies. She gave 8 cookies to her neighbour for $3 each, while 4 other cookies were sold to her co-workers for $2.50 each, and she kept the rest for her family. How much did she earn after selling 12 cookies?

There are 5 people waiting in line for the restroom and three times as many people waiting for the buffet. If each person waits 5 minutes for the restroom and 2 minutes for the buffet, how many minutes shorter is the shorter wait?
John uses 3 litres of paint for every 5 square metres he paints. If his room is 20 square metres, how many litres of paint does he need?
James bought 4 packs of soda each containing 12 cans. How many cans of soda did James buy?

Abigail decides to sell her homemade lemonade at the park. She sells each cup for $1.2, and on her first day, she is able to sell 25 cups. She uses the earnings to buy ingredients that cost $15. How much money does Abigail have left?

Lisa goes to painting classes 4 times a month.  The course lasts for half of the year. She paints 3 canvases each time she goes to class and she takes all of them home.  How many paintings does she finish in a year?
A zoo has 15 birds and triple the number of reptiles compared to birds. The zoo also has twice as many mammals as the total number of birds and reptiles. How many animals are there in the zoo?
Bob has a bookstore. He bought 50 copies of a new book for $10 each. He decided to sell each copy for $20. After selling all the books, how much profit did he make?
Jeremy participates in a running event where he gets a medal for every 3 miles he runs. If Jeremy has run a total of 72 miles this year, how many medals has he earned?
Jenny's garden has 8 rows of carrots, and each row has 12 plants. If Jenny wants to sell 2/3 of her carrots, how many carrot plants does she sell?
Alexa is looking at a mountain. At the same time, the mountain and Alexa are casting shadows - the mountain of 20 meters and Alexa of 1 meter. Alexa is 2 meters tall. How tall is the mountain?

Edward is making chocolate bars. He needs 6 pounds of chocolates. He knows that 1 ounce of cacao make 1 bar and each bar weighs 1/12th of a pound. It takes him 1 hour to make 5 bars. Every hour his chocolate maker runs costs $2.00. Every ounce of cacao costs $0.50. How much will it cost to make all the chocolates?
Melanie's car burns 7 liters per 100 kilometers. If she has to make a journey of 350 kilometers, how many liters of fuel will the car consume?
In a math competition, Mindy answers three more problems than James. Luca answers twice as many problems as James. If Luca answered 18 problems, how many problems were answered in total?
Dave bought a new TV for $600 and a new computer for $1200. He also bought a new washing machine that cost twice as much as the computer. What is the total cost of all the items Dave bought?

Eva plans to spend 2/7 of a day watching movies. She will watch documentaries, animations, and horror movies. She watches 25 pages an hour when she watches documentaries, 40 pages an hour when she watches animations, and 30 pages an hour when she watches horror movies. If she watches each for equal amounts of time, how many pages does she watch?
Jack has 56 apples. He gives 8 apples to each of his 6 friends. How many apples will Jack have left?
A birthday party is to be held at a local park. The host orders 15 pizzas, each cut into 8 slices. If every attendee is expected to eat 3 slices on average, how many attendees can the host feed?
Hazel bought 5 notebooks at $3 each, 7 pens at $1 each and 3 rulers at $2 each. How much did Hazel spend in total?
In a class of 80 students, one third are girls. How many boys are there?

A school library has 5000 books. Due to budget cuts, the library had to sell off 15% of their books. After the sale, an anonymous donor provided the library with 400 books. How many books does the library have now?

Nadia, Ramon, and Lila decide to order pizza for dinner. The prices for their pizzas cost $15, $16, and $20, respectively. If the group gives the delivery driver a 15% tip, how much should the delivery driver receive in dollars?
A tree grows 3 feet each year. If the tree is currently 30 feet tall, how long will it take for the tree to reach 60 feet tall?
A pack of markers that normally costs $4 is now sold at $3. A sketchbook was sold at a discount of $2. How much will you save via discounts if you buy 4 packs of markers and 6 sketchbooks?

Lisa decides to spend 10 days on a road trip. She drives 235 miles on the first day and 158 miles on the second day. On the third day, she drives half as many miles as the second day. How many miles does Lisa have left to drive if her total planned distance for the road trip is 2000 miles?

A company employs 50 more people in the first quarter of the year than in the second quarter, and 20 fewer people in the second quarter than in the third quarter. If the number of employees in the fourth quarter was twice the number in the third quarter,  and the number of employees in the fourth quarter was 200, what's the average number of employees per quarter?

Trevor is collecting rocks. He collects 6 rocks per week in the first year, and 8 rocks per week after the first year. Each birthday, he also receives 50 rocks from friends and family. He estimates that each year, he loses 20 of the rocks he has collected. If Trevor has been collecting for 7 years, how many rocks has he collected so far?
Lucy bought 5 dresses on a sale with a total saving of $45. If she saved the same amount on each dress, what is the original price of each dress, if sale price was $80 each?
A bag contains 20 marbles, half of them are green and the rest are divided into red and blue equally. How many red marbles are there in the bag?
Samantha had 40 pens. She gave 5 pens to each of her 4 friends, and she misplaced 5 pens at school. The rest were eaten by her dog. How many pens did her dog eat?
In a bake sale, Rio was able to sell cookies for $3 each and cupcakes for $5 each. By the end of the day, she sold a total of 50 cookies and 30 cupcakes. How much money did Rio make in total?
Jake has an apple tree in his garden that produces 500 apples each year. He gives half of them to his neighbor. How many apples does he have left each year?
David loves to collect stamps. He has 50 stamps from the USA, 30 from Australia and 20 from China. He displays 20% of his stamp collection. How many of his stamps does he display?
A library has 5 shelves with 20 books each. If 10 books are borrowed every day, how many days will it take for all the books to be checked out?

Thin and crispy pizzas are cut into 10 slices. Four friends, Trish, Benny, Anna and Robert each orders a pizza. Both Benny and Anna eat half of their pizzas and Trish and Robert eat three quarters of their pizza, how many slices are left uneaten?

Lisa ordered 70 boxes of chocolates from a store for a party. However, when she received the order, she found that 30% of the boxes were crushed. She decided to return those boxes. If each box cost $15, how much was Lisa refunded for the crushed boxes?
Leo charges $7 for each lawn he mows and an extra $10 for trimming the bushes. A client asked him to mow a yard with 15 lawns and trim 2 bushes. How much will Leo charge?

Sarah is a fan of a certain Korean Drama. The drama has 4 seasons with each season having 25 episodes. If Sarah watches 5 episodes everyday, how many days will it take for Sarah to watch all the episodes?
A group of friends decided to split the bill of $100 after a meal. If each person contributed $20, how many friends were there?

Tim's cocktail glass was dripping slowly at a rate of 1 ounce per hour. He planned to go to the store later that day to get a new glass, but it may take him as long as 4 hours before he can return home with a new one. Thus, in order to collect the dripping liquid over that time, he placed a bowl underneath the glass that could hold 3 times the amount of liquid that would drip over 4 hours. How big in ounces is the bowl he used?
If a man buys a house for $250,000 and wants to make a 15% profit when he sells it, how much should he sell it for to make this profit?
Kevin loves reading books. He reads 215 pages every day. How many pages does Kevin read in 1 month, if the month has 30 days?
John has saved up $1500. He spent 20% of his savings on a guitar and another 30% on a concert ticket. How much money does he have left?

In a high school, 30 students participate in the music club, which is twice as many as in the drama club. If the chess club has 10 fewer students than the drama club, how many students participate in all three clubs?

Lucy had 502 marbles in her collection. She sold 35% of them in a yard sale for $0.25 each, and she gave 50% of the remaining marbles to her friend. She sold the rest of her marbles for $0.50 each. How much money did Lucy make from selling her marbles?

Ruby needs to drive 10 miles to pick her first friend, then half the distance to pick the second friend, and finally 4 times the total distance driven to reach the mall. How many miles does Ruby drive between the second friend's house and the mall?

Alex and Belinda are planning their wedding. They decided to invite 300 people. If 2/3rd of the guests invited are Alex's friends and family, and 3/4th of Alex's guests attend the wedding, how many of Alex's guests did not attend?

Chris, Elaine, Nick, and Lisa play various computer games. Chris's favorite game takes 2 hours longer to complete than Elaine's game. Nick's game is five times as long as Elaine's game, and Lisa's favorite game is 3/4 times as long as Nick's favorite game. If Nick's favorite game takes 25 hours to complete, calculate the total number of hours to complete all of their favorite games together.
A container ship carries 1000 containers. Due to a rough sea, the ship loses 10 containers per day. How many days will pass until the ship carries only half of its initial number of containers?
A fruit seller has 5 bags of oranges. Each bag contains 20 oranges. A customer bought 2 bags and a half bag. How many oranges does the fruit seller have left?

A small sandwich can serve 2 people, and a large sandwich serves 4 people.  Lisa purchased 4 small and 3 large sandwiches.  Lisa ate one sandwich.  Her friend Pete ate half a sandwich more than Lisa.  Ben ate half as much as Pete.  Sam, Tom and Joe each ate one sandwich.  How many sandwiches will be left over?
Amy has a music playlist with 50 songs. She decides to add 5 new songs every month. How many songs will she have in her playlist after 6 months?
Jason has 10 grapevines. Each grapevine grows 50 bunches of grapes. If each bunch has 75 grapes, how many grapes does Jason get from all of his grapevines?

Billy wants to bake cookies. He has 4 kinds of nuts, 3 types of chocolate, and 5 different grains. He takes only a sort of each category (one nut, one type of chocolate, and one type of grain) for each cookie. If he can't make a cookie with almonds and dark chocolate together since they don't match well, how many cookie recipes can he come up with?
Sophie is picking apples. On Monday she picked 25 apples, on Tuesday 2 times as many as on Monday and on Wednesday 5 less than on Tuesday. How many apples did she pick on Wednesday?
Jack finds a job that pays $30 per day. He has already worked for 15 days and earned $450. How many more days does he need to work to double his current earnings?
Jim runs a bookstore. Every day, he sells 20 books at $15 each. If his monthly rent is $1000 and he only opens 5 days a week, how much profit does he make in a month of 4 weeks?

Every Christmas, Angela receives 50 books from her friends. She also buys 5 books every month for herself. However, she estimates that she loses around 10 books each year due to misplacement. If Angela has been collecting books for 4 years, how many books does she have now?
4 parrots in the living room eat 7 seeds each. 2 finches eat thrice as much as the parrots.  How many total seeds were eaten?
Sarah baked 100 cookies for a party. 25% of the cookies were chocolate chip, and the rest were oatmeal. How many oatmeal cookies did Sarah bake?

Lucy has $32 and needs to buy groceries. The store offers small packs of vegetables for $3, medium packs for $5, and large packs for $8. If the small packs contains 20 vegetables, the medium packs contains 35 vegetables and the large packs contain 60 vegetables, what is the maximum number of vegetables she can buy?
Alex and Ben decide to do a puzzle challenge. If Alex finishes 12 puzzles in 45 minutes and Ben finishes 16 puzzles in the same time, how many puzzles did they complete altogether?

Emma bought 5 bags each of apples and oranges. The apple bags weigh 10 kg less than the oranges, which weigh 20 kg each. If, during transport, 2 bags of apples fall and break with 1/5 of the weight lost, then how much is the total weight of remaining goods?

Kimberly has a YouTube playlist with 50 videos, each video is 5 minutes long. If she adds 20 more videos with the same duration to the playlist, how many minutes will it take to watch all the videos in the playlist?
Pauline has 20 fish in her home aquarium. Her friend gifted her 8 more fish. The next day, 5 fish swam to the other tank leaving only the rest. How many fishes does Pauline have in her aquarium now?
Sydney can typeset 10 pages in an hour. She gets paid $40 for each page she typesets. How much will she earn in 8 hours?

Tyler bought some candies. The number of lemon candies is thrice the number of apple candies. The number of orange candies is 10 less than the number of apple candies. If the total number of candies Tyler bought is 60, how many apple candies did he buy?

Sarah and her friends went out for ice cream. They each bought an ice cream cone and a lemonade. They also shared a tray of 12 donuts. An ice cream cone is $5. A lemonade is $3. A donut is $2. They had 85% of their combined money left over. If Sarah brought $30 how much did her friend bring?
Robert is trying to compile his report and has 500 pages. If 30% of those did not meet his standards resulting in them being discarded, how many pages are considered valid?

A bathtub is being drained and filled at the same time. The drain can empty the tub in 5 hours and the tap can fill it in 7 hours. The tub holds 200 liters of water. If the tub starts filled with water, how much water will be left after 4 hours?
A school bus travels at a speed of 30 km/hour. The school installed a new engine which allowed the bus to drive twenty percent faster. How far will the bus be able to travel in two hours?

Billy has a game collection. He has 6 board games, each cost him $8. He has 3 card games, each cost him $5. He also has 4 video games, all at the same price. If the total cost of his game collection is $90, how much did each video game cost?
Sally works 12 hours a day and sleeps 8 hours a day. If she spends 2 hours on cooking and eating, how much free time does Sally have left?
Daniel decides to bake cookies for a party. He can bake 24 cookies from 2 cups of flour. Daniel has 5 cups of flour. How many cookies can he bake?
It takes 30 minutes for Sara to cook pasta 10 times.  How long would it take to cook 7 times?

A charity baseball game is being organized, with donations promised by spectators. The promised amount is $500, but only $350 has been collected so far. Tim, Joanna, Paul, and Megan still owe donates. Tim and Joanna owe the same amount, while Paul owes $20 and Megan owes double of what Paul does. How much, in dollars, do Tim and Joanna each owe?

Ada baked a huge birthday cake for her friend Ella having 300 pieces. After the birthday cheer, they ate 70% of the cake's pieces. Ella then divided the rest among her four brothers. How many pieces each brother got?
A baker bakes 3 times as many loaves of bread as croissants. If he bakes 15 croissants, how many loaves of bread does he bake?

Emily collects recyclable paper and cardboard. Each stack of paper weighs 5 pounds, and each box of cardboard weighs 3 pounds. Emily can carry a total of 80 pounds. She collects 15 boxes of cardboard and as many stacks of paper as she can carry. If she gets paid 5 cents per pound for the paper and 2 cents per pound for the cardboard, how much money does she make (in cents)?
Samantha earned a weekly salary of $15 for walking the neighbor's dog. If she also babysits for $2 per hour and ends up with a total of $185 after 5 weeks, how many hours did she babysit every week?
Sarah, Dexter and Karen had lunch together. Sarah paid $15, Dexter paid $25, and Karen paid $20 for the food. If they want to leave a 15% tip, how much should each contribute?
Mike ran 5 miles per day for 30 days. If each mile burnt 150 calories, how many calories did Mike burn in total?
Sam's laptop was initially empty and was then charged for 30 minutes and has reached a 15% charge. How much longer must the laptop be charged to reach 100% charge?
A pizza store sells a pizza for $12. If the store gives a discount of one third on each pizza, how much does the pizza cost after the discount?

Due to the restrictions of Coronavirus, Mr. Trent, the local store owner, increased the prices of goods at his store by 15% each to make the same income he was making before. If buying an orange was $40 and the price of purchasing a mango was $50, calculate how much it costs in total now to buy ten oranges and ten mangoes at the new prices.
At the zoo, half of the animals are birds and the rest are mammals. If there are 80 animals at the zoo, how many animal ears (assuming the animals are intact) are there at the zoo?
Paul bought six shirts and a pair of shoes in a store. He pays $30 for each shirt and $100 for the shoe. He also has a coupon for $50 off his total purchase. How much does he pay in total?
In a box, there are some candies. Two thirds of the candies are green, and one third of the green candies are mint flavored. If 800 candies are mint flavored, how many candies are there in the box?
Nancy drinks two glasses of water at lunch and then drinks three times as many in the afternoon. How many glasses of water did she drink?

In his backyard, Liam has 20 flower pots. He plants 5 flowers in red pots and 7 flowers in blue pots. He then planted equal amounts of flowers in green and purple pots. How many more flowers were planted in the blue pots than in the purple pots?
A farmer has 6 acres of land. He plants corn on 2 acres and uses twice as many acres for wheat as for corn. How many acres of land does he have left unplanted?
Amy has 12 cards. On Sunday she bought another 5 cards and on Monday, she bought another 3. How many cards does Amy have?
Tom reads 20 pages of a book each day. The book has 800 pages. How many days will it take for Tom to finish the book?
A novel has 450 pages. Sophie can read 30 pages per hour. On Wednesday, she reads for 4 hours. On Thursday, she reads for 7.5 hours. How many hours more does she need to finish reading the novel?

John decides to fix a racecar.  It cost $20,000 to fix but he gets a 20% discount.  He wins his first race but only keeps 90% of the money.  The prize is $70,000.  How much money did he make from the car?

Paul's father brought home 5 crates of juice boxes. Each crate has 24 juice boxes. During a family picnic, 6 juice boxes were damaged and unfit to consume. During the event, 80 juice boxes were served. How many juice boxes were remaining?

Jennifer has 3 different boxes of cookies. Each of her boxes has 9 cookies. Her sister, Lisa, also has 3 boxes of cookies. One of her boxes has 15 cookies, another one has 5 and the other has 8. How much more cookies did Lisa have?
A farmer has 20 pigs and chicken in his farm. If he has 48 legs in total, how many chickens does he have?

There are 7 categories in a competition and each category has a medal to be won. If Jack won twice as many medals as Ryan and Jason combined and the three of them won all the medals, how many medals did Ryan and Jason win together?
A car travelled with a speed of 60mph for 2hrs and then drove at a speed of 80mph for 3hrs. What is the total distance the car travelled?
A pizza shop sells two types of pizzas - large pizzas for $20 and small pizzas for $15. If I bought 3 large pizzas and 2 small pizzas, how much did I spend?

Alice has a box of 240 crayons. At school, she gives away 4 crayons every day for 10 days. After these 10 days, she decides to give away 8 crayons every day for the next 5 days. How many crayons are left in the box?

In a zoo, there are 2 times as many monkeys as there are lions. If there are 25 lions and each lion enclosure costs 3 times as much as a monkey enclosure. If a monkey enclosure costs $2000, how much is the cost of all the enclosures?

Jack can throw a baseball at a speed of 70 miles per hour. Due to the wind blowing in the same direction, the baseball gets an extra speed of 10 miles per hour. If the wind changes direction and starts blowing opposite to the direction in which the baseball was thrown, how much slower does the baseball go?
Sarah bought a purse for $100 and a coat for twice the price of the purse. If she also bought shoes and a dress which cost $150 in total, how much did Sarah pay for all the items?
I bought a shirt for $30. The shirt was already marked 50% off. What is the original price of the shirt?
Alex goes to a coffee shop every weekday and buys a latte for $4.75. At the end of the month, how much does he spend on lattes?

In a bookstore, there are 6 rows of shelves and each row contains 8 books. If the bookstore owner decides to add 2 new rows of shelves with the same number of books, how many books will there be in the bookstore?
A restaurant sold 120 pasta dishes in one day. Each pasta dish costs $15. How much money did the restaurant make from selling pasta dishes?
Beth was given 18 books on her birthday. She lent 4 books to her friend and bought twice as many books as she lent out. How many books does Beth have now?
A basketball subscription costs $20/month. If you purchase an annual subscription you obtain a 10% discount on your total bill. How much do you end up paying if you go with the annual subscription?
Anna has 50 books in her collection. She gives away half of her books to her friend. Then, she buys 10 more books. How many books does Anna have now?

A mythical beast hides in a cave only to come out once every 50 years to launch an attack. It has attacked 255 villages in 300 years. Every new attack destroys three times as many villages as the previous one. How many villages did the beast attack in the first 50 years?
Michael buys his suit for $430 and shoes for $190. So, if he gets a $100 discount, what was the amount that he paid to the seller?

Caleb helps his mom to bake cookies for a family gathering. They decided to bake 5 batches of cookies, and each batch contains 8 cookies. If his aunt also brings 6 cookies, how many cookies will they have for the family gathering?
Rina spent $100 on groceries. If she made 50 meals and sells each for $5, how much was her profit?

In a shop, 3 boxes of red pens and 5 times that number of blue pens were delivered. Each box of red pens holds 6 pens and each box of blue pens holds 8 pens. How many pens have been delivered in total?
In a school, half of the students are boys and a quarter are girls. The remaining 45 students are school staff members. How many total people are at the school?
Simon decided to buy lunch at a food truck. He ordered a 6-dollar taco and a 2-dollar drink. His friend Greg ordered a 5-dollar hot dog. How much will Simon and Greg pay in total for their lunch?
A 130-pound person burns about 472 calories per hour cycling at a moderate speed. How many hours will it take for this person to burn 2832 calories?
Tina is preparing a meal for 120 people, and she plans to give each person two slices of cake. If a cake has 10 slices, how many cakes does Tina need?
Linda has a garden of 8 roses. She plants 5 more roses and then gives away 2. How many roses does Linda have now?
Cory has 4 times as many trading cards as Brian. If Brian has 15 trading cards, how many trading cards does Cory have?
A construction firm purchases 5 truckloads of bricks, each containing 2,500 bricks and each brick costs $0.75. How much is the total amount spent by the construction firm?
Jeremy buys 3 bags of chips for a party. Stacy and Emily also buy chips for the party. If they need 10 bags of chips total, and Stacy buys 4 bags, how many bags of chips should Emily buy?

John received $70 as a birthday gift. He decided to buy a video game that cost him $12. Later, he went to a fast-food restaurant and spent the same amount as on the video game. How much money does John have left?

Sally wants to buy a set of art supplies for her art class. The art set costs $80 and Sally decides to pay for expedited shipping, which costs an additional 25% of the price of the item. How much will she pay for the art set and the shipping?
A college football team travels 650 miles to their away game. The bus can go 50 miles an hour. At the halfway point, the team stops for a 2 hour break. How long does the trip take?
A shopkeeper bought 30 phone covers and 40 phone chargers for a total of $1600. If a phone cover costs $10, how much does a phone charger cost?
James runs 5 miles a day. How many miles does he run in a week?

Tom has a 800 page handbook. The first quarter of the handbook Tom reads at a pace of 20 pages per day, and the latter three-quarters he reads at a pace of 10 pages a day. How many days does Tom spend reading this handbook?
A group of friends decides to bake cookies for a charity event. They need to bake 500 cookies. Each batch they bake makes about 25 cookies. How many batches do they need to bake to meet their goal?

A farm produces 5 kinds of apples - 12 of each kind, 3 kinds of peaches - 8 of each kind, and 25 plums. The farm has 8 rows with 20 trees each for the different species. How many more fruits can be harvested in the farm?
Joan is twice as old as her sister Mary. If Joan is 32 years old, how old is Mary?
Daniel plans to make 15 wooden tables for a school. He needs 30 wooden planks for each table. How many wooden planks does Daniel need for all the tables?

Daniel was contracted to paint 3 rooms, with the second room being twice the size of the first room and the third room being three times the size of the first room. If the first room was 200 square feet, what is the total area of the rooms Daniel is painting?
A cinema sells a ticket for $9 per adult and $6 per child. If a family of 2 adults and 3 children visits the cinema, how much will they pay for tickets?
A confectionery shop sells cupcakes for $3 each and cookies for $1.50 each. If Amy buys 5 cupcakes and 10 cookies, how much does she spend in total?

Ten students each brought 5 bottled waters for a field trip. Halfway through the trip, half of the students finished all their bottled waters. The remaining students only drank half of their bottled waters. How many bottled waters were left?
In a town, 25% of the population are teenagers, and 60% of these teenagers have cellphones. If the town has a population of 5000, how many teenagers in the town do not have cellphones?
Ethan bought 3 cans of soda at $2 each, and 2 chocolate bars at $1.25 each. He handed the cashier a $20 bill. How much change did he receive?

Amy has a cherry orchard that is 200 feet by 200 feet. 1 square foot can produce 30 cherries. If a jar of cherry jam requires 50 cherries and 1 jar of jam sells for $5, how much can she earn from this batch of cherries?

Lucy plans to purchase potato chips for a party. Ten people will be at the party, including Lucy. The potato chips cost 25 cents per pound. How much will Lucy pay (in dollars) for the potato chips if she wants each person to get 1.2 pounds?
Lucy has 5 dolls. Her grandmother gave her 2 more dolls for her birthday. Later that day, she lost one doll. How many dolls, in number, does Lucy have left?
Susan made a purchase at a fashion store and received a 15% discount. If the discount she received was $30, what was the original price of what she purchased?
Cynthia saves $10 every week. After 15 weeks, she decides to buy a dress that costs $70. How much money will Cynthia have left after buying the dress?

Luke's office is 12 miles away from his home. He travels to office by car moving at a uniform speed. If his usual speed was reduced by 5 miles per hour due to traffic, and it takes him an extra hour to reach the office, find his usual speed.
In a football game, Mark scored twice as many goals as John. John scored 3 fewer goals than Samuel. If Samuel scored 6 goals, how many goals did Mark score?
Ben runs a bakery and uses 2 cups of flour to make a loaf of bread. If he decides to make 12 loaves of bread for the weekend, how many cups of flour will he use?
A basketball player attempts 40 free throws in a practice session. He makes three times as many shots as he misses. How many free throws did he make?
A scientist has recorded that a certain bacterial colony can double its size every 24 hours. If the colony starts with 500 bacteria, how many bacteria will there be in the colony after 3 days?
10 years ago, Rob's age was triple the age of his son Ted. Now, after 10 years, if Ted is 30 years old, how old is Rob?
A school soccer team has a mix of 120 boys and girls. The coach decides to add 20 more girls to the team. If there were 40 girls initially, how many boys are in the team now?
If Melissa buys a book costing $30 and gets a 20% discount, then decides to buy another book but this time gets a 30% discount, how much is the total she paid?
John has 50 marbles and he gives away 2/5 of his marbles to his friends. How many marbles does John have left?
In a school bus, there are 50 kids where the number of boys and girls is equal. The rest of the kids are teachers. How many teachers are on the bus if there are 20 boys?
Michael goes for a run every day. He runs 3 miles per day from Monday to Friday and 5 miles per day on Saturday and Sunday. How many miles does he run in a week?
John bought 50 packs of pens for his classroom. Each pack has 15 pens. If he distributed packs among his 10 students, how many pens did each student receive?
