Kate gathered 33 eggs from her chicken produce and bought an additional 25 eggs from the market for baking. If she uses 5 eggs every time she bakes how many times can she bake?

A restaurant has orange, apple and grape juice. Each bottle of orange juice costs $8 and each bottle of apple and grape juice costs $6. The restaurant sold 50 bottles of orange juice, 40 bottles of apple juice and 10 bottles of grape juice. What is the total revenue from selling these juices?
A pet store has 10 kittens and 15 puppies. They sell 6 kittens and 7 puppies. How many pets does the store have left?

In a town carnival, Kali and her group went picking candies. Kali picked twice as many candies as Joy did. Joy started late and only collected half of what Sophia did. If Sophia collected 68 candies how many did Kali collect?
Lilly is bringing cupcakes for her class of 22 students. She already has 8 cupcakes at home. If she buys packs of cupcakes with 6 cupcakes in each, how many packs does she need to buy?

Danny has a variety of pets. He has five more goldfish than he has chameleons. He has three less parrots than chameleons. All his pets are fourteen. He has 3 cats. How many pets does he have in total?

There are three rabbits in the forest. They like carrots, beets, and radishes. The first rabbit, which likes carrots, eats 4 times as many carrots as the number of beets eaten by the second rabbit that likes beets. The rabbit that likes beets eats 2/3 times as many beets as the number of radishes eaten by the third rabbit. If the rabbit that likes radishes ate 90 of them, calculate the total number of vegetables eaten by the three rabbits?
A pair of sneakers costs $80. If a store has a sale and all sneakers are 30% off. How much will the sneakers cost now?
Oliver runs 3 miles every day. If each mile burns 100 calories, how many calories does he burn in a week?
John had $1500 in his bank account. He used 2/5 of this money to buy a new bike. He then spent 3/4 of the remaining money on bicycle accessories. How much money is left in his account?
A library has 15,000 books. It has 3 branches and each branch has the same number of books. How many books does each branch library have?

Anna enjoys knitting. She once knitted a 5-letter word with a double word score to get forty points. If before multiplying the score, the first, middle, and last letters were all worth three points, how much were the second and fourth letters worth before the word score was doubled?
Greg has 35 stickers and his brother Ben has 22 stickers. If Greg gives 7 stickers to Ben and Ben gives 15 stickers to Greg, then how many stickers does Greg have?
A marathon is 26.2 miles long. If John ran half of the marathon, how many miles did he run?

Owen bought 12 boxes of face masks that cost $9 per box. Each box has 50 pieces of masks. He repacked 6 of these boxes and sold them for $5 per 25 pieces. He sold the remaining 300 face masks in baggies at the rate of 10 pieces of mask for $3. How much profit did he make?
A pizza parlor serves 5 different types of pizza. Each type of pizza has 8 slices. During the lunch rush, they sold 3 slices of each type of pizza. How many slices are left?
Jerry has two more than three times the amount of stickers that Bob has. If Jerry has 32 stickers, how many stickers does Bob have?
Jane has 5 pieces of candy. She gave her friend Alice half of what she had. If Alice ate one of the candies she received from Jane, how many candies does Alice now have?
A bee farm collected twice more honey from their yellow bees than their black bees. If the black bees collected 540 jars of honey, how many jars of honey did the bee farm collect in total?
Jake has 60 grams of chocolate, of which he eats 1/3 everyday. After eating 2/3 of it, how much chocolate is left?
Nancy has 40 apples. Mark has 10 fewer apples than Nancy. Jane has thrice as many apples as Mark. How many apples does Jane have?

John went to the grocery store. He bought 7 apples and 3 packs of flour. He also got 5 bottles of juice. Each apple cost $1, each pack of flour cost $2, and each juice cost $4. How much money did John spend in total?
In a farm, there are 50 orange trees that each yield 100 kg of oranges and 30 lemon trees that each yield 85 kg of lemons. What is the total mass of fruits harvested in this farm?
From a bakery, Mark buys 3 croissants for $18. How much will he pay for a dozen croissants?
A car uses 5 liters of gasoline for every 100 kilometers. If the car was run for 600 kilometers, how many liters of gasoline were consumed?

A caterer is preparing food for a banquet. He plans to prepare 25 chicken, 50 vegetables and 35 fish dishes. He could cook a chicken dish in 15 minutes, a vegetable dish in 10 minutes and a fish dish in 20 minutes. What is the total duration for him to prepare all of these dishes?
Sarah and Tom are saving money. Sarah saves $15 every week and Tom saves double the amount Sarah saves. If they save for a year (52 weeks), how much money will they have saved together?
Henry is 3 years older than Charles who is half as old as Paul. If the total of their ages is 45, how old is Henry?
A car travels 60 miles per hour. How many miles will it travel in 7 hours?

Abby and Bella are picking strawberries. Abby can pick 50 strawberries in 10 minutes. Bella can pick 60 strawberries in 20 minutes. If they both pick strawberries together, how many strawberries can they pick in one hour?
Rich is reading a 372-page book. He has already read 125 pages of the book. If he skipped the 16 pages of maps, how many more pages does he have left to read to finish the book?
Lisa has 5 rows of strawberries in her garden. If each row has 12 strawberry plants, how many strawberry plants does Lisa have?

Sam made 7 cakes for his mother's birthday. Each cake needs 28 ounces of flour. Sam realized that he needs to triple the amount of cakes, so he made 21 more cakes. How many ounces of flour will Sam use in total?

Eva offered to cook dinner for a family gathering. She decided to prepare half as many salads as main dishes. If Eva made 3 salads more than side dishes and a total of 21 dishes, how many main dishes did Eva make?
Daniel needs 60 bricks to build a barbecue pit. Each trip to the store, he can carry 8 bricks. How many trips does he need to make to have all the bricks he needs?
John goes to school 4 days a week and spends 3 hours on his homework each day. If he misses one day of school within a week, how many hours is he supposed to spend on his homework that week?
Peter sold 300 apple pies at his shop. Each apple pie costs $5. Anna sold 400 cherry pies at her shop. Each cherry pie costs $4.50. Who made more money and by how much?
Elina has 3 dozen pencils in her pencil box. She gave 5 pencils each to 6 of her friends. How many pencils does she have left?
Jenny has a coin collection. She has 9 more nickels than pennies. She has thrice as many dimes as nickels. She has 5 quarters. Penny is equal to 5 quarters. How many dimes does she have?
A baker uses 5 cups of flour for each loaf of bread. If he bakes 10 loaves of bread every day for 6 days, how much flour will he use?
John is planning a trip to Europe. He will spend 5 days in Spain, 4 days in France, 3 days in Italy, and 2 days in Greece. If he spends $200 per day, how much money will he spend in total?
Joy baked 15 cakes. Carl baked 5 more cakes than Joy. Lianne baked 30 more cakes than Carl. How many cakes did they bake altogether?

A glass of soda has 25 grams of sugar. If Steve drinks 3 glasses daily, then decides to reduce his sugar intake by 20% by drinking less soda. How many grams of sugar does Steve consume daily after the reduction?
Tonya has won $500 on a game show. She plans to use the money to buy new smartphones for her family. If each smartphone costs $120, how many smartphones can she buy?
John drinks 3 liters of water each day. How many bottles of 1.5-liter water should Robert buy for his 5 days consumption?
A classroom has 4 rows of chairs with 5 chairs in each row. If two chairs are taken away, how many are there left in the classroom?
Jack catches a fish that's 2/5 as big as Tom's. If the combined weight of their fish is 75 pounds, how heavy is Jack's fish?
Julia is a bicycle mechanic, charges $45 for each bicycle she fixes. If she fixed 10 bicycles and 4 customers each gave her a $12 tip, how much in total did Julia make in US dollars?
A school has 25 students in each class. If there are 16 classes, how many students does the school have in total?
Maria has 5 kids. She wants to distribute equal amount of candy to her kids. If she has 25 pieces of candy, how many pieces of candy will each child get?

Sam saves 30% of his total income. This month, he made $500 from his part-time job and received $200 as a gift. He also earned $100 from selling handmade bracelets. How much money will he save this month?

Max has a sticker collection. He had one third as many animal stickers as food stickers last week. This week, he obtained 9 more food stickers. If he has 36 food stickers now, how many animal stickers does he have?
There were 215 people at a concert. There were 45 more men than women. How many women were at the concert?
Gary and his friends had a bake sale where they sold cookies for 55 cents each and brownies for 85 cents each. They sold 180 cookies and 100 brownies. How much money did they make in total?
Alex is learning multiplication and creates flash cards. If he writes 8 problems on each card and he creates 10 cards, how many multiplication problems did he write in total?

Lily and Sue are baking cookies. Lily bakes chocolate chip cookies and Sue bakes sugar cookies. Lily baked 20 chocolate chip cookies and Sue baked 40 sugar cookies. If chocolate chip cookies are sold for $1 each and sugar cookies are sold for 50 cents each, what is the total amount of money they make?
Tom can paint a house in 3 days. Based on this rate, how many days will it take for him to paint a building that is four times as large as the house?
In a school, there are 50 girls with long hair and 30 girls with short hair. The teacher added 20 more girls with short hair to the school. How many girls are there in the school now?

Lola made 15 tuna sandwiches and 10 ham sandwiches for the potluck.  Each tuna sandwich was cut into 6 halves.  Each ham sandwich was cut into 3 triangles.  If her guests ate 30 halves and 18 triangles, how many slices of bread did they eat?

Caitlyn is baking a cake. She adds 3 cups of flour and twice as much sugar to 2 cups of cocoa powder. Then she divides the cake into servings that can hold 3 cups each. How many servings does she get?

8 people are planning a picnic, each bringing 2 sandwiches. However, 2 people are unable to attend, so they each make an extra sandwich to cover for their absence. How many sandwiches will there be at the picnic?
In a library, there are 20 fiction books and 14 non-fiction books on a shelf. If one-third of the fiction books and half of the non-fiction books are borrowed, how many books remain on the shelf?
Greg has $200. He spent 3/4 of his money on a speaker. How much money does Greg have left?
Kate is selling her handmade jewelry. She sells her earrings for $10 per pair. If she sells 8 pairs of earrings, how much total money can she expect to earn?
In the Math Olympics, the Blue Team finished with twice as many points as the Red Team. If the Red Team made four hundred sixty points, how many points do the teams have combined?
A theatre sells regular tickets for $20 and VIP tickets for $50. If they sell 100 regular tickets and 50 VIP tickets, how much total money do they make?
Max is a freelance writer. He is charging $15 per hour for his work and spends 8 hours a day writing articles. If he works 5 days a week, how much does he earn in one week?
Jack had a bag of 120 marbles. He lost 15 marbles and gave 25 marbles to his friend. How many marbles does Jack have left?

Joan is answering English and Math exams with 30 and 15 questions, respectively. The English exam is to be completed in 1 hour and the Math exam in 1.5 hours. If Joan wants to spend an equal amount of time on each question, how many more minutes does she have in answering each question on the Math exam than the English exam?

A bicycle factory produces 7 bicycles every hour. Another factory produces 5 bicycles every hour. If the second factory had to shut down for a three-hour maintenance, how many more bicycles did the first factory produce after 8 hours?
Paul wants to paint his room which has 4 walls. Each wall needs 6 liters of paint. How many liters of paint does Paul need in total?
Thomas gets a $20 allowance every week. He spends $3 on arcade games, $5 on comics, and saves the rest. How much will he have saved after 4 weeks?

Anna is a painter. She painted 20 landscapes that made it into a gallery; fifteen more portraits than the landscapes that sold privately; 10 fewer abstract paintings than the landscapes that were not for sale. How many paintings did Anna paint in all?
Jenny is making pancake batter. She can make 5 pancakes with 2 cups of flour. She spends $8 to buy flour and each bag costs $2. How many pancakes can she make if each bag of flour contains 4 cups?
Emma bought a toy for $25 and a game for $30. Later, she realized she had spent 1/5 of her savings. How much savings did Emma have originally?
Sam had 300 stickers. He received an additional 250 stickers from his friend and gave his sibling 125 stickers. How many stickers does Sam have now?
Raj bought 7 chocolate bars for $35 total. Later he bought 5 more bars of the same type. How much money did he spend in total?
A watermelon weighs 7 kilograms. If Jack eats half of it, how much does the watermelon weigh now?
Anton is preparing French toast for a party of 12 people. A recipe serves 4 people and calls for 3 eggs. How many eggs will Anton need for his party?

Ella is a ceramic artist. She makes vases and sells them at her pottery studio. She charges $70 for a large vase and $35 for a small vase. Last week she sold five large vases and ten small vases. If she sold three times as much this week, how much are her sales for this week?
Lisa bought 15 books at $20 each and she sold one-third of them for $25 each. How much total profit did she make?

Emily needs to pick one apple for each of her 20 classmates. It takes her on average 8 minutes to pick an apple. She has been picking for 1 hour and 30 minutes. Assuming she dropped 2 of the apples she initially picked, how many more minutes does she need to pick the remaining apples?
In a class, there are 9 more girls than boys. If the number of boys is 16, what is the total number of students in the class?
Linda has 2 dozen eggs. She wants to cook scrambled eggs for breakfast, which requires 3 eggs per serving. How many servings can she make?
Samantha bought a car for $15,000. After 3 years, the value of the car depreciated by 20 percent. How much is the car worth after 3 years?
Hannah needs to download a 1000 MB movie to her laptop. After downloading 340 MB, her wifi connection drops to 4 MB/minute. How many more minutes will it take Hannah to download the movie?
A company orders 3 boxes of pens every month. Each box contains 20 pens. If they need to order pens for the next 6 months, how many pens should they order?
Samantha saves $10 every week. After saving for six months, how much will she have saved?
Samuel spent $45 on comics and $20 on a book. He also saved $17 of his pocket money. If Samuel had $100 at the start, how much money does Samuel have left?
John bought a new laptop which cost him 20% more than his old $600 laptop. How much did the new laptop cost him?

In a school, there are 20 students in 1st grade and 15 students in 2nd grade. If the number of students in the 3rd grade is twice the total number of students in 1st and 2nd grade, how many students are there in the 3rd grade?
A warehouse has 1500 boxes. If 3/5 of these boxes contain shoes and 2/5 contain hats, how many boxes contain shoes?
Fannie ate 2.5 pounds of apple pie last week. Greg ate three-quarters of a pound of cherry pie, while Joyce ate 1.5 pounds of pumpkin pie. How many ounces of pie did the 3 friends eat in total?

In the dessert contest, twice as many people voted for the chocolate cake compared to the vanilla cake, and the number of votes for the strawberry cake was 20 more than the number of votes for the vanilla cake. If 10 people voted for the vanilla cake, how many total votes were cast?

Daniel bought a car that cost $15000 and an additional vehicle that cost $10000. He initially owned 500 square meters of land. If the vehicles he bought cost $25 per square meter, how big is his land after buying the new vehicles?
Sara bakes and sells cupcakes. A box of 12 cupcakes costs her $4 to bake. She sells each box for $15. If she sells 50 boxes of cupcakes in a weekend, how much profit does she make?
The quadripedarian has four legs. In a group of quadripedarians, there is a total of 24 heads and legs. How many quadripedarians are in the group?
A printer prints 75 pages in 15 minutes. How long, in minutes, would it take the printer to print 200 pages?
Matthew needs to repair his bike. The repair takes 3 hours and the mechanic charges $60 an hour. The parts cost $180. How much did Matthew pay for the repair?
Matthew is 6 years old. His mother is four times as old as Matthew, and his father is 9 years older than his mother. How old is Matthew's father?

Lucy makes $25 an hour on her job. She works 8 hours a day, 5 days a week. She plans on saving 30% of her weekly income to buy a new laptop that costs $500. How many weeks does she need to save to buy a laptop?
Rachel went to the grocery store to buy apples for an apple pie recipe. She needs 5 apples per pie and she plans to make 4 pies. If apples cost $1.20 each, how much should she pay for all the apples?

A salon offers haircuts for $30. They are running a Valentine's day special and offering 30% off their regular price. David decides to treat his mother, wife, and 3 sisters to haircuts. How much will David spend on 5 haircuts?
Jenny has 200 candies. She gives 5 friends 10 candies each. What's five times the number of candies she has left?

A bakery sells small pies for $8 and large pies for $12 each. If you buy more than 5 pies, you get a 20% discount. Jennifer wants to buy 4 small pies and 3 large pies. How much will she save if she buys 1 more small pie?

While planning to finish reading a 210-page novel in one week, Kyle intends to read 4 times daily, 8 pages each time, every day of the week. How many more pages should he read per day to actually achieve his target?
Tom has 5 red cars and three times more blue cars than red. How many more blue than red cars does Tom have?

Peter collected 3 seashells on Monday. On Tuesday, he collected triple the seashells he collected on Monday. On Wednesday, he collected the same number of seashells as he did on Monday. How many seashells has Peter collected by the end of these three days?

In a particular neighborhood there are 400 houses. The city council is planning the tree plantation drive in which they decided to plant 10 trees per house. Amidst this, the local school volunteered in the drive and decided to plant additional 5 trees per house. Calculate how many trees will be planted in total?

Abby, Bella, and Cyrus were collecting seashells on the beach. Abby collected 15 more seashells than Bella. Cyrus has half as many seashells as Abby. If Bella collected 25 seashells, how many seashells do the three have altogether?

An art teacher orders boxes of colored pencils for his classes. Each box contains 100 pencils. If he needs 2400 pencils each week for six classes and the school week is five days, how many boxes does he need to order each week?

John and Susan want to buy a game that costs $100. They decide to share the cost among them. John already has $35, while Susan has $25. They plan to win the rest of the money from a contest where the prize is $100. If they split the prize equally, how much money will Susan have left after they buy the game?

A class consists of boys, girls, and teachers. The number of boys is twice the number of girls, and the number of teachers is 5 times the number of girls. If the number of girls is 20, how many people are in the class?
Jack's restaurant has 30 chairs. He needs 2 for each table. How many tables can Jack set in his restaurant?
A zoo has 240 animals. A third are birds, a quarter are mammals, and the rest are reptiles. How many reptiles are there in the zoo?
Susie baked 6 trays of cookies, each having 15 cookies. If she gave each of her 4 friends 2 cookies each, how many cookies did she have left?
Mark wants to read 400 pages of a book during his 5-day vacation. He has already read 60% of it. How many more pages does he have to read?

Maria plans to renovate her dining room. She liked Tamara's dining room which consist of 10 chairs, 5 more than two times as many tables as the chairs, and half as many lamps as the tables. Maria wants to buy the same amount of chairs as Tamara, 50% less tables than Tamara, and 10 more lamps than Tamara. What is the total number of items Maria wants to buy?
George is willing to spend $1000 on books. If each book costs $75, how many books can George buy?

A fundraiser sells magazines for $3 each and T-shirts for $10 each. George buys 5 magazines and 3 t-shirts while Emily bought half the amount of magazines that George did and twice the amount of t-shirts. How much did George and Emily spend together?
A garden has 3 times as many roses as tulips. If there are 20 tulips and 10 of the roses are picked, how many flowers remain in the garden?

Tom found some seashells on the beach. Initially, he had found 8 shells. Later, he found more shells, which was triple the number of shells he initially found. If he gave 5 shells to his sister, how many shells does he have left?

There are seven times as many ducks as swans in the park. Each duck consumes 3lbs of food per week and each swan consumes 12lbs per week. If there are 105 ducks in the park, calculate the total amount of food consumed by all the birds in a week.

In a basketball tournament, Team A scored 64 points in the first match, which is 8 more than Team B. In the next match, Team A scored six less than twice as many points as Team B. How many points did Team A score in the second match?
A candy factory creates 10 types of candies. 4 of each candy are placed in every pack. If the factory produces 20 packs in a day, how many candies does the factory produce in 3 days?

Eiffel tower was the tallest man-made structure on Earth for 41 years. It is 140 feet taller than 700 feet, and 150 feet narrower than it is tall. What is the sum of the height and width of the Eiffel tower in feet?

Nina is a teacher planning homework for her students. Each math problem takes 2 minutes to solve, each grammar task 5 minutes to complete and each essay 30 minutes to write. If Nina assigns 5 math problems and 2 grammar tasks, how many essays should she assign if she wants to assign 1 hour of homework?
Melissa sold 15 cupcakes on Monday at her bakery. Each day after that, she sold 5 more cupcakes than the previous day. How many cupcakes did Melissa sell from Monday to Friday?

Jake is a pattern artist and he needs to create a specific pattern for a client. Each day, he draws three times as many designs as he drew the day before. If he draws 3 designs on the first day, how many designs will he have drawn in total in 4 days?

John is planning his monthly budget, and needs to calculate how much he has left to spend. His rent is $500, and he has already paid this. His electricity bill was $80, and he has already paid two-thirds of this. He makes another payment of $15 towards his electricity bill while planning his budget. His internet bill is $50, which he has paid half of. Overall, how many dollars does John still need to spend to finish paying his bills?
A school library has 120 books. If 30% of those books are science books and the rest are literature books, how many literature books are in the library?
A park has 200 mango trees and each tree produces an average of 50 mangoes. Due to a storm, 20% of the mangoes get destroyed. Find the total number of mangoes available after the storm.
Alice wants to buy a skirt for $45 and a T-shirt for $20. She has saved $50. How much more does she need to save?
Peter wants to buy a new video game console which costs $300. He saves $25 every week. How many weeks does Peter need to save for the new gaming console?
Martha's nursery specializes in roses, and each rose bush costs $12. If she sells 35 rose bushes per week, how much would she make in a year?
David buys 5 boxes of cereal. Each box has 15 servings. He pays for the 5 boxes with 3 $10 bills. He gets $5 change. How much does each serving cost, in cents?
Sandra goes to a local market and buys 8 apples for $2 each and 3 watermelons for $5 each. If she also pays a 10% tax on the total cost, how much did she spend in total?
Greg and his 3 friends decide to share equally the cost of a ski trip that is $400 in total. How much will each person have to contribute?
Susan has a garden in her backyard. Her garden currently has 15 trees. Susan plans to plant 6 trees every year for the next 10 years. How many trees will Susan's garden have after these 10 years?

For a science experiment, Danielle doubled the amount of liquid every day. After 5 days, she had 32 ounces of liquid. If the experiment started with a certain amount, how much liquid did Danielle have on the first day?
John can read 20 pages of a book every hour. If the book has 300 pages, how many hours will John take to read the entire book?
Cyrus collects 3 butterflies on Wednesday and 4 butterflies on Thursday. If each butterfly has 4 wings, how many wings are there in total for all the butterflies?
Lara has 12 books. She gave 1/3 of her books to Mark, and another 1/4 of her books to Grace. How many books does Lara have left?
Andy and Mike are planning to make a toy car. Andy can make 3 toy cars in an hour and Mike can make 2 toy cars in an hour. If they both work together, How many toy cars will they make in 5 hours?

Maureen makes cake pops for school fundraisers. Each cake pop requires 4 ounces of chocolate for coating. This fundraiser, she is making cake pops for her class of 25 students, the school board of 5, and the local volunteer team of 10. How many ounces of chocolate does Maureen need for the cake pops?
Billy has 20 apples. He gives 5 apples to his friend, and then his sister gives him twice as many apples as he had left. How many apples does Billy have now?
There is an art competition where each participant needs to make 3 paintings. If there are 20 participants, how many paintings will be made in total?
In one week, Marc cranks out 6 workouts at the gym, while his friend John does double. How many workouts does John do in a week?

Jennifer goes shopping for clothes. She already has 15 pieces worth $50 each in her cart. She adds 10 more dresses to her cart. If the $1200 she has brought is exactly enough to pay for all the clothes, how much does each dress cost?

Cindy manages a small bakery. One box of cupcakes sells for $15, each cookie sells for $1.50, and a loaf of bread is priced at $7. If Cindy sold 10 boxes of cupcakes, 30 cookies, and 20 loaves of bread in a day, how much did she earn for the day?
Jane wants to buy a bike that costs $200. She can save $25 per week from her allowance. If she already has $45, how many weeks will it take Jane to save enough to buy the bike?
Laura and Sam had lunch at a restaurant. Tacos cost $3 each, and sodas cost $2 each. Laura had 3 tacos and 1 soda. Sam had 2 tacos and 2 sodas. How much did their lunch cost?
A five-layer snowman is being built with each layer having two times as many snowballs as the layer above it. The top layer is a single snowball. How many snowballs are in the snowman?
Jake has a stamp collection. He collects 3 stamps every day. After 30 days, he decides to sell half of his collection. How many stamps does he have left?
Peter and his sister played a board game. Peter won 15 rounds and his sister won 20 rounds. What is the percentage of rounds that Peter won?
Jade is 15 years old and her brother is twice as old as she is. How old will her brother be 5 years from now?

Ryan has $1000 saved. Each week of the month, he spends $80. At the end of the month, he decides to purchase as many $20 books as his savings can cover. He leaves the rest in the account. How many dollars remain in the account?
A painter uses 3 tubes of paint to paint a picture. If each tube costs $6, how much money does the painter spend on the tubes of paint?
A baker baked 5 loaves of bread every day. If each loaf sold for $4 and he worked 6 days a week, how much did he earn in a month?
Aiden has a bag of marbles. If the ratio of red marbles to blue marbles is 5:4 and he has 27 red marbles, how many blue marbles does he have?
Alex decides to buy school supplies. They come in 20 packs with an equal number of pencils, sharpeners, and erasers. How many packs does he need to buy if he wants 35 erasers?
Susan earns $20 for every hour she works. If she works for 5 hours a day, how much would she earn in 6 days?
The cost of shipping 50 boxes, each weighing 20 kgs, is $500. What's the cost of shipping twice as many boxes, each weighing 2/3 times as many kgs?
A school has 7 classrooms and each classroom can hold 30 students. The school currently has 180 students. How many more students can the school take?
In a class, there are 30 students who needs 2 pencils each. If a box of pencil costs $5 and contains 10 pencils, how much total money is needed to purchase enough pencils for all students?
In a pet store, there are 35 animals. 15 of them are fishes, 6 are birds, and the rest are dogs. One fifth of the dogs are puppies, and the rest are adult dogs. How many of them are adult dogs?

Sara bought 4 lamps for her house, all at the same price. She also bought a table that costs $70 and three picture frames at $25 for each frame. After giving the cashier $200, Sara got a $5 change. How much did each of the lamps cost?

Martha is selling her book collection to Peter for some of Peter's comic books. Peter charges Martha 3 comics for a book. Martha has 90 books. If she gives half of her books and spends 60% of the remaining books on comics, how many total comics does she get?
Every week Michael saves $80. Every fortnight he spends $30 of his savings on a dinner outing. How long will it take him to save $240?

Ann baked 16 cupcakes for a birthday party. She used two types of frosting for the cupcakes: chocolate and vanilla. If 1/4 of the cupcakes were covered with chocolate frosting, and the rest were covered with vanilla, how many cupcakes were covered with vanilla frosting?
Mike drinks 2 cups of coffee every day. How many cups of coffee will he drink in 20 weeks?

Aitana and Jayda went to Silverlake Flea market to buy some handmade crafts. Aitana spent 2/5 times more money than Jayda at the market. If Jayda spent $400, calculate the total amount of money they spent together.
At her favorite restaurant, Mary usually leaves a 10% tip. If her meal costs 55 dollars, how much will she leave as a tip?

Before Sarah sprained her ankle, she was able to run 12 miles per hour. After she sprained her ankle, she could only jog at 8 miles per hour. What is the difference between the distance she could cover in 1 hour before and after she sprained her ankle?
For a fundraising event, a school sold $7 tickets. In three days the school sold 750 tickets. How much money, in dollars, did the school earn?
Amy made 2 less than three times as many origami birds as Sarah. Emily made a quarter as many as Amy. Sarah made 5 origami birds. How many did Emily make?
Jack buys 5 pizza boxes. Each box contains 8 slices. He shares them among his 4 friends evenly. How many slices does each friend receive?

The length of a country is 7654 miles. Jack started from one end and rode his bike for 345 miles on Monday, 200 miles on Tuesday, and 300 miles on Wednesday. How many miles does Jack still have to cover to reach the other end of the country?
Nathan practiced the piano for a total of 5 hours last week. He practiced for 45 minutes on each of 3 days. How many minutes in total did he practice on the other days?
There are 5 champions in a game, one champion can defeat 4 enemies in a minute. If all champions fight at the same time, how many enemies can they defeat in 3 minutes?
Mom spent 4 more than twice as long to clean the kitchen than to clean the living room. She cleaned for a total of 56 minutes. How many minutes did she clean the kitchen?

It takes a swan 30 days to swim to the North during summer, three times as much time to swim to the South during autumn, and 45 days to swim to the West during spring. How many days is the swan swimming during these seasons?

In a zoo, there are 5 lions, 6 tigers and 4 bears. After a month, the zoo brought in 2 more lions and 4 more tigers but one bear was transferred to another zoo. How many animals are there in the zoo now?
In a school play, 20% of the audience were parents, 30% were students, and the rest were teachers. If the total number of audience members was 500, how many teachers attended the play?
Sarah is three times as old as Jenny and twice as old as Alex. If Alex is 20, what is their average age?
In a pond, there were initially 5 water lilies. It grew an additional 3 water lilies every 2 days. How many days passed before there were 23 water lilies in the pond?

In 1998, there were 90 students in a summer camp. In 1999, the number of students that came to the summer camp was reduced by a half as compared to 1998. In 2000, 2/3 as many students came to the summer camp as in 1999. How many students came to the summer camp in 2000?

A family of three adults and one child go to a restaurant. The food cost them $85, and the rest of their budget of $110 was spent on drinks. If the child's meal was $10, how much, in dollars, did each adult's meal cost?
In a pizza eating competition, Mike ate 10 slices of pizza. Each slice has 250 calories. How many calories did Mike consume?

Pam wants to figure out the average price of her paintings. She has 5 of them. The first one cost $250, the second one cost $300, the third one cost $180, the fourth one cost $320 and the fifth one cost $200. What is their average price?
On Anna's birthday, she got $500. She spent 30% of it on clothes and the rest on books. How much did she spend on books?

Lee has a comic book collection. After getting new comics from a convention, he realizes that he now has 3 left over after completely filling his comic box. Before the convention, Lee had 150 comic books. If his comic box fits 100 comics, how many comic books did Lee get at the convention?

Sam has a collection of 60 stamps. 25 of these are British stamps, 15 are American stamps, and the rest are either French or German. If there are twice as many French as German stamps, how many German stamps are there?
Martin bakes 5 loaves of bread and each takes 30 minutes to bake and 15 minutes to cool down. How long will it take to bake and cool down all the loaves?
Carlos has a garden. He planted 30 apple trees and 20 orange trees. If Carlos gives 3/5 of his orange trees to his neighbor, how many orange trees does he have left?

Jack has $200 in his wallet. He spent $20 on a gift. He then paid half of his remaining money for his meal. After that, he spent a quarter of the money left on clothes. How much money does Jack have now?

A class is divided into four groups for a project. The first group handles the research for two hours, the last group handles the report for one hour, and the second and third group split the remaining hours of work. If the total work takes up 7 hours, how many hours will each of the second and third group take?
In 7 years time, John will be the same age as his father is now. If John is 25 years old now, how old was John when his father was his age now?

Tom wants to buy two books. The first book costs $12, the second book is twice the price of the first book. He also has a discount coupon for $5 off his purchase. If he currently has $25, how much money will he have left after buying the books?

There are 15 grapes in each cluster. Ten clusters of grapes were delivered to a store. The store owner ate 3 grapes. The remaining grapes are packed into smaller boxes of 5 each. How many boxes of grapes are there?
Beth goes shopping and buys two pairs of shoes for $50 each, three shirts for $20 each, and one pants for $40. She then receives a 10% discount on her total purchase. How much did she end up paying?

Due to economic inflation, Mrs Green, a florist, increased the prices of her flowers by 20% each to keep up with her expenses. If a rose was initially $5 and a daisy was $3, how much will Mrs Green charge now for seven roses and five daisies?
A library has 300 books. 40 percent of books are fiction, the rest are non-fiction. If 15 percent of the non-fiction books are on history, how many history books are there?

Lucy needs $2000 to be able to travel for her studies. Her parents agreed to pay a quarter of the amount. She also qualifies for a scholarship of $500. She plans to work part-time and meet the balance. How much does she need to earn to be able to fulfill her goal?

In a digital agency, 64 projects were completed in 8 days by working 8 hours per day. If the agency decided to work 6 hours per day instead, how many extra days would be necessary to complete the same number of projects?
Emma earned $50 but spent some on stationery. Her dad gave her $100 more. If now Emma has $20 more than three times the amount she originally had, how much money did she spend on stationery?
A science lab ordered 6 boxes of Petri dishes. Each box contains 20 dishes and a dish costs $2. If the lab paid with 5 $100 bills, how much change will they receive?

A class has 8 rows with 5 desks each. The class starts with 25 students, during the first break, 10 new students join and 5 leaves the class. During lunch break, another 12 students join the class and 8 students leave. How many empty desks are there at the end of the day?

There are 500 fish in a tank. On the first day 1/5 of them are caught by a fisherman. On the second day 1/5 of the remaining fish get sick and die, and on the third day 40 percent are sold. How many fish remain in the tank after three days?

Martha is creating a montage video about her school's drama club. She has 110 seconds of the lead actor, 145 seconds of the supporting actor, 90 seconds of the extra, 75 seconds of the director, and 180 seconds of the backstage crew. How many minutes does each participant get on average?
If Mr. Smith drives at an average speed of 60 miles per hour and he has to travel a distance of 420 miles, how long will he take to reach his destination?
If a loaf of bread costs $2 and Emily bought 3 loaves of bread and a pack of butter for $5. How much does Emily have left if she initially had $20?
Sam has $75. He spent $25 on ice cream and $35 on a game. Then, his grandma gave him $50 since Sam helped her. How much money does Sam have now?

Lisa is making a pizza. She uses 30 pieces of pepperoni for a pizza, twice as many pieces of ham, and 12 more pieces of sausage than pepperoni. If there are 6 slices of pizza, and everything was distributed evenly, how many pieces of meat altogether are on each slice?
Rachel bakes 3 batches of cookies. Each batch makes 24 cookies. She gives half of the cookies to her friend and eats 12 of them. How many cookies does she have left?
Paul packed 35 books in his bag. He added 15 more books in it, and then gave away half of his books. How many books does Paul have left?
Lucas is 18 years older than his son. In 5 years, the sum of their ages will be 50. What is the current age of Lucas's son?

In a basketball game, Peter makes 12 field goals. Each field goal is scored as 2 points. He also makes 3 three-point shots and 4 free throws which are scored as 1 point each. What is his total points?
Tommy collected 45 buttons and gave half of them to his sister. His father then gave him 20 more buttons. How many buttons does Tommy have now?
In a zoo, there are 45 birds. The number of zebras in the zoo is 5 less than thrice the number of birds. How many zebras are there in the zoo?

Lisa is preparing for a charity bake sale. She made 24 cupcakes, 30 cookies, and 16 brownies. It took her 40 minutes to make cupcakes, 50 minutes to make cookies, and 35 minutes to make brownies. How much time did Lisa spend baking?
Jason collected 500 splitter screws to build a treehouse. If each step of the treehouse needs 25 screws, how many steps can Jason build?

A farmer has chickens and cows on his farm. If each chicken lays 10 eggs a week and each cow gives 5 liters of milk a week and the total number of eggs and milk liters per week collected by the farmer is 85, how many chickens does the farmer have if he has 10 cows?
A rock concert is attended by 1900 people. Each ticket cost $45 and there is an additional convenience fee of $5 per ticket. What's the total income from the concert?
Steve has a candy shop. He started with 100 candies. He bought 50 more candies and then sold 75 candies. How many candies are left in his shop?

Amy starts a day with $30, spends $10 every day and saves the rest. After a few days, her aunt doubled her total savings and her uncle gave her an extra $20. If she now has $400, How many days have passed?
Mr. Jacobs drives to work 5 days a week. If his workplace is 25 miles from his home, how many miles does Mr. Jacobs drive in two weeks time?
Albert has a rectangular room with four walls. He wants to place a painting on each wall. Each painting costs $120. How much will it cost to buy all paintings?
There are 25 students in a drama club. If 3/5 of the students are boys, how many are girls?
Bob bought 5 packs of candies. Each pack contains 20 candies. He ate 15 candies today. How many candies does he have left?

Karen's mother put on 3 kilos during a recent trip that she wants to lose so her clothes will fit comfortably. She asks Karen to help her manage her figure. Karen's mother can burn 2,200 calories from fat per day by moderate exercise. There are 7,700 calories in one kilograms of body fat. How many days will it take for Karen's mother to lose those 3 kilos if she does moderate exercise and adheres to consuming 1,800 calories a day?

A colony of ants collected food from a picnic. There were 6 ants in total. The first ant gathered 5 crumbs, the second ant gathered 2 less than the first, the third ant gathered double the amount of the second, the fourth ant gathered 3 crumbs, and the final two ants each gathered the same number of crumbs as the fourth. How many crumbs did the ants collect in total?
On a road trip, Susan's car gets 40 miles to the gallon. She drives 100 miles per day. If gas is $2 per gallon how much does she spend on gas in 5 days?
Liam bought 2 sets of soccer balls. Each set has 5 soccer balls. Each soccer ball costs $15. What is the total cost Liam paid for the soccer balls?
A factory makes toy cars. Each car uses 5 screws. One day, the factory produces 100 cars and then an additional 50 cars. How many screws were used?

Emma wants to buy 10 pieces of nail polish to avail the store's discount. The regular price of a nail polish is $15 and it's currently on a 30% discount. How much will Emma pay for all the nail polish?
Laura's water bottle can hold 1 liter of water. She drank half of it in the morning and the remainder in the afternoon. How much water did Laura drink in the afternoon?

Edward's gardening company was hired to plant trees in a park. On the first day, they planted 25 trees. Each consecutive day they planted 5 more trees than the previous day. They finished planting after 4 days. How many trees in total did Edward's gardening company plant?
A painter needs to paint a room that is 15 meters long and 10 meters wide. If one can of paint covers 20 square meters, how many cans does he need?
A beekeeper has 60 beehives. Each hive produces 2000 oz of honey per month. Calculate the amount of honey produced per year by the hives.

Lisa has a ribbon that is 50 inches long. She cuts 10 inches off to wrap a present and another 15 inches to make a bow. The remainder is equal in length to Alyssa's ribbon. If Heather's ribbon is three feet longer than Alyssa's ribbon, how many inches long is Heather's ribbon?
Lucy has a box of chocolates. She eats 30% of the chocolates and gives her brother half of what's left. If there were 100 chocolates to start, how many does Lucy have left?
Felicia has 12 cupcakes that she wants to divide evenly among her 5 friends at a party. How many cupcakes will each friend receive?
Sarah is preparing lunch for a large gathering. She orders 700 pounds of vegetables at $5 per pound. She also orders thrice the amount of meat at $4 per pound. How much did she spend in total?

Jake can wash his car in 15 minutes. Daniel takes 5 minutes less than Jake to wash his car while Lisa can wash her car 6 minutes more quickly than Daniel. If they have to wash their cars once a week, how many minutes do all three spend washing their cars each week?
Alice buys 8 packs of pencils. Each pack contains 12 pencils. She then gives out 25 pencils to her classmates. How many pencils does Alice have left?
A bank gives a free money box to every new child account opened. If the bank has 200 new child accounts in a month, and each money box costs the bank $10. How much does the bank spend on money boxes?
Alan got an 89% on his science essay and a 75% on his biology lab. If he wants an average grade of at least 85% in his science class, what does he need to get on his final exam?

Emily is supervising a parking lot. 60% of the cars have valid parking permits, and 1/4 of that number have disabled parking permits. If there are 500 cars in the parking lot, how many cars are illegally parked?
A factory produces 20 cars every day. Due to increased demand, the factory is going to increase its production by 50%. How many cars will the factory produce in a day after the increase?

Joanna is going to bake 200 cookies for a charity event. She already baked 20 cookies, and today, she baked four times as many cookies. How many cookies should she bake tomorrow if she wants to bake half of the remaining number of cookies needed?
Daniel runs 10 laps of a circular track in 50 minutes. Each lap takes him 5 minutes. He takes a 5-minute break every 10 minutes. How many breaks does he have during his run?
A classroom had 35 students. 7 students were absent, and the rest were present. If 3 students did not submit their homework, how many students submitted their homework?
In a race of 100 meters, Alex can run 5 meters per second, while John can run 4 meters per second. How many seconds ahead will Alex finish the race, compared to John?
Amy and Robert go shopping for groceries. Amy buys 4 packs of pasta each costing $5. Robert buys 6 loaves of bread each costing $3. How much money do they spend in total?
There are 320 books in a school library. On Monday, 305 books are borrowed. On Wednesday, 75 books are returned and 45 books are borrowed again on Friday. How many books are left in the library?
Paul is 7 inches taller than Pete. Pete is five inches shorter than Phil. If Phil is 65 inches tall and the three of them stand on top of each other, what is their total height?
In a city, there are 45 people living in each house. The city's north side has 65 houses, east side has 70 houses, and its west side has 60 houses. How many people live in the city?

A reptile exhibit hosts an equal number of snakes and lizards. 30 of the lizards stay in their own enclosure, and the remaining lizards move into a communal enclosure. An equal number of snakes join the lizards in the communal enclosure, but then a quarter of these snakes slither back into their own enclosure. If the exhibit holds a combined total of 200 reptiles, how many snakes are now in the communal enclosure?
A dryer can dry 2 kilograms of clothes per hour. If the dryer works for 3 hours each day, calculate the amount of clothes it can dry in one week.
Jane is baking cookies and uses 2 cups of sugar for each batch. She plans to bake 3 batches today. If each cup of sugar contains about 200g, how many grams of sugar does Jane use?

In a fruit shop, an apple costs 30 cents and a pear costs 20 cents. Kate wants to buy 5 apples and 2 pears, while her friend Max wants to buy 3 apples and 4 pears. How much (in dollars) will they spend altogether?
In an office, there are 24 chairs but 1/4 of them are broken. How many chairs are stiff functional?

In preparation for a cookout, Susan bought 15 packs of beef patties. Each pack has 8 patties and costs $4. She later decides to invite more friends and purchases an additional 10 packs. How many dollars did Susan spend on beef patties?
In 3 rounds of a basketball tournament, James scored 12 points each round, Mike scored 15 points each round and Jack scored 10 points each round. How many points did they score in total?
Karly has 180 pages of reading for the weekend. 70 percent is History, and half of the History is Roman Empire. How many pages about Roman Empire does Karly have to read?
There are 120 students at a school concert. Half of them are in the choir, one third play an instrument, and the rest are spectators. How many spectators are there?

Karen needs to go to the airport. A limo ride to the airport costs $6 more than a Uber ride. An Uber ride costs $2 more than a taxi ride. The limo ride costs $14. If Karen takes a taxi to the airport and tips the taxi driver 15% of the original cost of the ride, what is the total cost of the ride to the airport?
Danny has a box of 24 chocolates. He divides them equally among his 6 friends. How many chocolates does each friend get?

John wants to purchase a bicycle. He has saved $100, his older brother gave him $20 and his aunt twice the amount that his brother gave him. The bicycle costs $140. After the purchase, how much money will he have left?
Jenny has a recipe that requires 9 cups of fruits. She has 6 cups of strawberries and the rest are apples. How many cups of apples does Jenny need?

Stella is designing a jacket using denim patches. She needs 30 patches of yellow denim with 10 patches per row, 25 patches of blue denim with 5 patches per row, and 15 green denim patches. If each patch costs $0.50, how much will Stella spend on patches?
John painted 5 rooms in a house. Each room took 6 hours to paint and he charges $25 per hour. He also spent $50 on paint. How much did John earn on this job?

An ice skating rink has 35 skaters. Half of them are using blue skates, and the rest are using red skates. One third of the skaters using red skates are wearing pink jackets. How many skaters are wearing pink jackets?
In afruit basket, there are 25 oranges. One forth of the oranges are rotten. How many oranges are fine to eat?
In the forest, 15 hunters have caught a total of 10,000 pounds of wild game. If 14 of them caught 600 pounds each, calculate the amount of wild game in pounds the fifteenth hunter caught.

At a family reunion, Emma's family decides to take a group photo. The photographer from the event wants to place them in rows, 8 people in each row. He counts that Emma's family has 56 members. How many rows will the photographer need to accommodate the whole family?
Sophie has 100 pens. One third of the pens are blue. If Sophie wants to divide the blue pens into 5 equal groups, how many blue pens will be in each group?
A train travels at a speed of 60 miles per hour for 2 hours and then at a speed of 80 miles per hour for 4 hours. What is the total distance travelled by the train?

Tom noticed a swarm of bees flying together in a round formation. One quarter of the bees broke off from the formation, flew toward the earth, and landed in some flowers. Then, 2 bees flew up, out of the flowers, and joined those already flying in the air to make a new round formation in the sky. If the final number of bees flying in the round formation was 10, how many bees were in the first formation Tom noticed in the sky?
Elizabeth runs a clothing store. She bought 50 dresses at $35 each. She sold all the dresses at $55 each. How much profit did she make from selling all the dresses?
Jenny bought 30 candies. She gave 1/3 of them to her little brother and of the remaining candies, she gave 1/4 to her best friend, Alice. How many candies did Jenny have left?
Laura goes shopping and buys a dress for $60, a pair of shoes for $45 and a purse for $25. If she pays with a $200 bill, how much change does she get?
April studies 3 hours a day for 5 days. Each day of the weekend, she studies twice as long as she does during a weekday. How many hours does she study in a week?
In his garden, Bob has planted 45 tomato plants in 5 equal rows. If Bob harvests 8 tomatoes from each plant, how many tomatoes will he have?
If an apple costs $0.95 and George buys 6 of them, but he can only pay with a $10 bill, how much change should he receive?
Evelyn saves $20 every week. After 15 weeks, how much money will she have saved?
If Emma feeds three stray cats with one can of cat food per cat per day and a can of cat food costs $1.15, how much would she pay in a month of 30 days?
A bus can carry 50 passengers. If the ticket price is $10, how much will the bus earn if it's full?

Lisa buys four packs of pencils. The first pack included 15 pencils, and the second pack included 18 pencils. If the average number of pencils in the packs is 17, how many pencils were in the other two packs combined?
A zoo has 20 tigers, each of which eats 10 pounds of meat a day. The lions at the zoo eat twice as much, and there are 15 lions. How many pounds of meat does the zoo need in a day?

A kindergarten teacher ordered 4 packets of sweets for her class. Each packet contains 25 pieces of sweets. However, in a turn of events, 3 sweets were lost from each packet. How many sweets does the teacher have now?
Vicky baked 16 cookies and ate 4 of them. She divided the remaining cookies equally among her 3 friends. How many cookies did each friend receive?

Sam, Ella, and Mary were playing a card game. There are 52 cards in the deck. Sam drew 7 cards, Ella drew 5 cards, and Mary drew 4. Then, Ella gave 2 of her cards to Mary. How many cards are left in the deck?
Ann sold 124 cupcakes last week. She sold three times that many the previous week. How many cupcakes has Ann sold over the past two weeks?
Clara borrows $150 from a friend who asks for a 4% interest rate per week. If Clara plans to pay the friend back after 3 weeks, how much would she end up owing including the interest?

Two children are riding bikes in a park. One rides at an average speed of 6 miles per hour, and the other at an average of 5 miles per hour. After 3 hours, the faster child decides to rest. How long, in minutes, before the slower child catches up?
In a football match, Henry was 15 minutes late. Three of his teammates were each five minutes later than he was. What's the total time the four players were late?

Dan has a collection of 100 comic books. Because of lack of space, he decided to sell 40% of his collection. If each comic book is sold for $8, how much money will Dan make from selling his comic books?
Jacob operates a milkshake parlor. Each milkshake uses 3 scoops of ice cream. If Jacob made 200 milkshakes on Monday, how many scoops of ice cream did he use?
A bakery used 4 packets of yeast for each bread they baked. They baked 25 breads a day. How many packets of yeast did they use in a week?

Maya used ribbons to decorate a cake. The ribbon around the base of the cake measures 20 inches. She then used twice that length to make a bow. Finally, Maya used three times the base length to wrap around the box. How many inches of ribbon did Maya use in total?
A bottle of juice contains 2 liters. If each cup has a capacity of 250 milliliters, how many cups can be filled from the bottle?

In a classroom, there are 35 folders, each containing 20 pages of notes. The professor uses one folder every day to help prepare his lectures. If he does not work on Sundays, how many pages of notes does he use in a week?

Lucy's garden produces 10 pounds of tomatoes each week. She needs 5 pounds of tomatoes to prepare her pasta sauce. If she plans on having pasta twice a week, how many weeks' worth of tomatoes does she need to gather from her garden?
Tracy reads a book every week. Each book is about 300 pages long. How many pages does she read after three months?
The library has 500 books. Each student is allowed to check out 5 books at a time. If all the books are checked out, how many students checked out books?
On a school trip, each bus can carry 45 students. If the total number of students going on the trip is 900, how many buses are required for the trip?
Bob fills up his car's 16-gallon tank with gas and then drives 280 miles before the tank is empty. How many miles per gallon does his car get?
A store sold 16 chairs in the first month, 50% more in the second month, and 20% less in the third month. How many total chairs they sold?
Peter has a box with 200 pens. He gave 4 dozens to his classmates. After that, he sold 58 pens at a school fair. Then, he found out that 18 pens were not working. How many pens does he have left?

A bus can carry 20 students at a time. A school plans a picnic for all the students of grade 4. There are 80 students in grade 4. How many round trips will the bus make to take all the students to the picnic spot?
If Andrew does 10 push-ups every morning,  from Monday to Friday. How many push-ups will he do over the entire year?

Mia's nutritionist tells her that she should consume no more than 150 grams of carbohydrates each day. If she consumes 25 grams of carbohydrates with each meal and eats three times a day for the first four days of the week, how many grams of carbohydrates can she consume in the last three days of the week?
Every customer eating in Mary's Cafe gets a stamp. Once you reach 5 stamps, you get a free coffee. This morning, 8 customers have eaten at the cafe. How many coffees does Mary owe her customers?

A school started a charitable event aiming to raise $800. Students can donate $20, $40 or $80. 10 students donated $20 each, 5 students donated $40 each, and 2 students donated $80 each. How much more do they need to reach their goal?
Hannah is preparing for a 10-kilometer race. She trains for 4 days in a week. If she runs 6 kilometers each day she trains, how many kilometers will she run in 3 weeks?
The temperature was -2 degrees Celsius in the morning. By noon, it had risen by 7 degrees. What was the temperature at noon?

Billy has a box of apples and oranges. He has twice as many oranges as apples. If there are a total of 36 fruits in the box and each fruit weighs 5 ounces, how much does the box of fruits weigh in pounds?

Stephanie bakes bread at home. For each loaf, she uses 3 cups of flour, twice as many tablespoons of sugar as cups of flour, and half as many eggs as tablespoons of sugar. If Stephanie wants to bake 3 loaves of bread, how many eggs will she need?

Diane is baking pies for a charity bake sale. The pie crust costs $2, the filling costs $4, and the whipped cream costs $1 per pie. If Diane is planning to bake 12 pies, how much will she spend in total?

Mark loves planting roses and tulips. He planted 15 rose seeds and 30 tulip seeds in his garden. If 70% of the rose seeds and 85% of the tulip seeds germinate, and 75% of the resulting plants bloom, how many plants will bloom in his garden?
Emily has 10 books in her bookcase. She gets 4 more books for her birthday, but later lends 2 books to her friend Sally. How many books does Emily have now?
John bought 20 balloons for his birthday party. He gave 8 balloons to his friends and popped 3 balloons by accident. How many balloons does John have left?
Rachel received 84% in a spelling test. She got 5 words wrong. How many words were in the spelling test?
Ted bought three books for $15 each and two pens for $2 each. He then pays a 5% sales tax on the total amount. How much did Ted pay in total?
Melinda is 5 inches taller than Lisa. Lisa is 7 inches shorter than Carlos. If Carlos is 70 inches tall, what is the average height for the three of them?
Lorena plans to buy 3 dozen of mugs for her upcoming party. The regular price of a mug is $20 and is now on a 30% discount. How much will Lorena pay for the mugs?
A boy weighs 65 kilograms. His weight is three times his younger brother's weight. If 1 kilogram equals 2.20462 pounds, what is the weight of the boy's brother in pounds?

Hortense loves daisies and sunflowers.  She planted 25 daisy seeds and 25 sunflower seeds in her flower bed.  If 60% of the daisy seeds germinate, and 80% of the sunflower seeds germinate, and 80% of the resulting plants produce flowers, how many plants will she grow that produce flowers?
A printer prints 200 pages in 15 minutes. How many pages will it print in 2 hours?
In a 10 mile marathon, Sam covered 4 miles in the first hour and 2 miles in the second hour. How many more miles does Sam have to run to complete the marathon?
Each day for a week, Maria practices piano for 3 hours. How many minutes does Maria practice piano in a week?
A bookstore offers a discount of 35% on all best-selling novels. Judy wants to buy a novel that normally costs $30. How much will she pay after the discount?
James has 9 orange trees in his orchard. He wants to buy more trees and plans to have 3 rows of trees, with 8 trees in each row. How many more orange trees does he need to buy?
Jennifer bought four apples. Each apple cost her two dollars. When she arrived home, she realized she had given her mom two of the apples. How many apples Jennifer have left?
Sally baked five trays with 20 cupcakes in each tray. She then equally divided the cupcakes into ten packs. How many cupcakes did Sally place in each pack?
In a class of 50 students, 60% are girls. Each girl brings 2 notebooks and each boy brings 3 notebooks. How many notebooks did all the students in the class bring in total?

Emily and John are preparing for a tree planting activity in their school. Emily was able to gather 250 tree saplings while John gathered 120 saplings. During the activity, 3% of the saplings gathered by Emily did not survive while 4% gathered by John also did not survive. How many tree saplings were left?
Logan distributed candies during his birthday party. He cut the cake into 10 equal pieces and gave each of his 4 friends two slices. How many slices of cake are left for Logan?
Jesse went to the bakery and bought bread and cupcakes. He bought 2 loaves of bread which cost $5 each and 5 cupcakes which cost $1.50 each. How much did Jesse pay in total?
Anna has a jar of candies, there are 5 candies of each flavor: chocolate, strawberry, vanilla, apple, and lemon. How many candies are there in total in the jar?
Emily owns a flower shop. She buys roses for $0.50 each and sells them for $2.50 each. If she buys and sells 200 roses, how much is her net profit?

In a school, there are 4 grades: first, second, third, and fourth. There is a total of 200 students in the school. The first graders make up 25% of all students, and second graders make up 30%. The third graders are as numerous as the first and second graders put together. How many fourth graders are there in the school?
Maria has 35 dollars, 10 euros, and 2500 yen. If there are 1.5 dollars per euro and 110 yen per dollar, how much does Maria have in yen?
Lisa bought a doll for $14.25 and a teddy bear for $13.40. For lunch, Lisa spent $8.50 on a pizza and $2.60 on a bottle of Coca-Cola. How much more money did Lisa spend on toys than on her lunch?
Ben uses 65 gallons of water per day for his garden. He buys a new automatic sprinkler that uses 55 gallons per day. How much water does he save in one week with the new sprinkler?
Mrs. White owns two identical 3-story buildings. The first floor has 2 offices while the rest of the floors have 4 offices each. How many offices does Mrs. White have in both buildings?
Martin buys 5 comic books at $3 each and 3 action figures at $7 each. How much does he spend in total?
Jenny has 5 times as many books as Maria. Sarah has 2 times fewer books than Maria. If Maria has 20 books, how many more books does Jenny have than Sarah?

Paul has a stamp collection. He started with 85 stamps. Each month, he bought 3 new ones. By the end of the year, he sold 15 of his stamps to a friend and gave his brother 5 stamps. How many stamps did he has now?
Nathan plants 120 trees. 25% of these trees are apple trees, 30% are orange trees, and the rest are banana trees. How many banana trees did he plant?
Jodi's pumpkin plant produces 3 pumpkins every month. After 8 months, how many pumpkins will the plant have produced?
Emily planted 6 rows of sunflowers in her garden. Each row has 9 plants. Unfortunately, pests ate 15 of her plants. How many sunflower plants does Emily have left?
There are 125 blue marbles and thrice as many green marbles. If the blue and green marbles were added to 200 red marbles, how many marbles are there in total?
Michael donates $10 to his favorite charity every month. How much money does he donate in a year?
Sophia has 6 times as many stickers as Ava. If Ava has 5 stickers, how many stickers do Sophia and Ava have altogether?
A basketball team won 8 games out of 20 games they played. If each win got the team 5 points and each loss got the team 1 point, how many points does the team have?
A lamp costs 1/5 the price of a table. If the table costs $500, how much does it cost to buy a table and 2 lamps?
James bought 2 boxes of donuts and 5 boxes of bagels for a breakfast meeting. A box of donuts costs $4 and a box of bagels costs $3. He paid with a $50 note. How much change did James get?
There are 60 students in a music class. 35% of them play the piano. How many students play the piano?
Jane bought 5 dresses and 2 pairs of shoes. Each dress costs $50 and each pair of shoe costs $25. She also has a coupon for a $20 discount. How much does Jane need to pay?
A candy store sells 600 candies a week. 20 percent gets melted, and 50 percent are sold. 1/3 of the remaining candies are used to prepare candy floss, how many candies are used to make candy floss?
Karen reads twice as many books on the weekends as she does during the weekdays. If she reads 4 books on weekdays, how many books does she read in a week?

Harry is collecting comic books. He already has 35 comic books. His grandmother gave him 5 comic books, his friend gave him 4, and he bought 11 more. After reading, he gave 10 comic books to his cousin. How many comic books does Harry have now?
For a pizza party, John orders 8 pizzas worth $12 each, 5 sodas worth $2 each and 2 packs of crackers worth $4 each. How much did John spend for the pizza party?

Mary has a garden where she grows vegetables. She has twice as many tomatoes as cucumbers. She has five fewer peppers than cucumbers. If she has 10 cucumbers, how many vegetables does she have in total?
Ricardo is a software engineer who earns $60 per hour. If he works 8 hours a day, 5 days a week, how much does he earn in a week?
Mel has 5 times as many pencils as her brother, John. John has 3 times as many pencils as their friend, Lisa. If Mel has 60 pencils, how many pencils does Lisa have?

A jeep travels 300 kilometers with a speed of 60 kilometers per hour. A motorbike starts the same route one hour later with a speed of 90 kilometers per hour. How many hours will it take for the motorbike to catch up the jeep?
A pizza place charges a flat fee of $5 for delivery. If a pizza costs $10 and Jessie buys 2 pizzas for delivery, how much does she pay in total?

Jessica spent $30 on fruits. She bought 10 apples and each apple costed her the same. She also bought some oranges that costed her $2 less per orange than the apples. If she bought the same number of apples and oranges, how much did one orange cost her?

Lily is deciding which route to take for her run. One route is 15 miles and flat, so Lily can cover it at 4 miles per hour. The other route is 9 miles, but it's hilly, so Lily will only be able to cover 2 miles per hour and will have to take a half hour break halfway through. How many hours faster is the fastest route?
Maddy has twice as many dolls as Bella. Bella has 30 more dolls than Emma. If Emma has 250 dolls, how many dolls do they have in total?
In a board game, Rick gains three times as many points as Ethan. If Ethan earns 6 points, how many points does Rick earn?
A store has 90 shirts. 50% of the shirts are white, 25% are blue, and the rest are red. How many red shirts are in the store?

A year of a cat's life equals 15 human years for the first year, 9 human years for the second year, and 4 human years for every year after that. According to this scale, how many human years has my 7-year-old cat lived?
A teacher uses 2 chalks a day for his classes. He has 50 pieces of chalk left in his drawer. How many days can he continue teaching with the remaining chalk?

Lena makes her own lemonade.  She makes it in batches of 1 gallon.  She drinks 48 ounces of lemonade every 2 days.  It takes 10 hours to make lemonade.  How long does she spend making lemonade over 12 days?

Oscar is on an escalator that is slowly moving downwards. The escalator needs to descend 15 levels to reach the bottom. It takes 10 minutes for the escalator to travel half the levels. The escalator then takes 4 minutes per level to travel the next 5 levels. It finally takes 14 minutes per level for the escalator to finish traveling the last 5 levels. How many minutes did it take for the escalator to reach the bottom?

Emma reads a 300-page novel at a rate of 40 pages per hour. After she reads for 3 hours, she decided to take a break. After her break, she reads the rest of the book at a faster pace of 60 pages per hour. How long in total did she spend reading the book?
Liam's old fan consumes 60W electricity and his new fan consumes 40W less than the old fan. How much electricity will be saved if Liam uses the new fan for 20 hours?
Steve bought 15 chocolates each for 2 dollars. He found out that he got a discount of 10% on the total bill. How much total discount did he get in terms of dollars?

A group of teachers agreed to a schedule where each teacher would take an equal number of hours to supervise the playground. The schedule runs for 6 hours and four teachers are there. How many hours will each teacher take?
In 4 years, Jake will be 3 times as old as Sam. If Sam is currently 8 years old, how old is Jake?
Linda wants to buy a dress that costs $120. She saves $15 per week. How many weeks does she need to save in order to buy the dress?
John is lifting weights. He bench presses 15 pounds for 10 reps and does 3 sets. How much total weight does he move?
A soccer team won 70% of their 20 games. How many games did the soccer team win?
A group of friends decides to share equally the cost of a $120 gift for their teacher. If there are 8 friends in the group, how much does each friend need to contribute?
Jennifer walks 2 km every day. How much distance will she cover in a week? And what about in a month of 30 days?
Samantha bought a dress for $50 and a pair of shoes for $30. If she paid using a $100 bill, how much change did she get?
A foot is equals to 12 inches. Mike finds out that his room is 12 feet in length and width. What is the area of his room in square inches?
Peter has 36 marbles. He gives 1/3 of his marbles to his friend Jim. He then loses 2/9 of his remaining marbles. How many marbles does Peter have left?
A farmers market has 180 established fruit trees. If 5% of the trees are apple trees and 10% of the remaining trees are pear trees, how many pear trees does the farmers market have?

A farmer harvested 150 apples from his farm. He sold each apple for $3. He used the profit to pay his worker $100 and to buy a new apple press for $200. How much money was left from the farmer's earnings?
James writes from 1 PM to 4 PM every night.  He can write 5 pages per hour.  How many weeks will it take to finish his 735-page book?
Emma has a stack of books that she read. Each book is 2 inches thick. The entire stack is 18 inches high. How many books are in the stack?

Anna wants to plant red and yellow tulips in the shape of a smiley face. She needs 8 red tulips for each eye and 18 red tulips for the smile. If she needs 9 times the number of tulips in the smile to make the yellow background of the face, how many tulips does she need total?

In a park, there are 150 trees divided into different varieties. There are twice as many oak trees as pine trees. There are ten more birch trees than pine trees. If there are 20 pine trees, how many maple and willow trees are there if they make up the rest of the trees in the park and they have an equal number of trees?
A school has 120 kindergarten students, 135 first grade students, and 130 second grade students. If the school bought pencils at the rate of 3 pencils per student, how many total pencils were bought?
Alex has twice the number of books Will has. Will has 3 times the number of books Jane has. If Jane has 15 books, how many books do they have in total?
Emma is teaching her kids to save money. For every $5 they do not spend, she will double their pocket money. If her daughter saves $15, how much will she have in total?

A farm is organizing its animal pens. There are 3 cow pens in a row and behind each of these are 2 sheep pens. There are twice as many chicken coops as sheep pens scattered around the farm. The cow pens hold 5 cows, the sheep pens hold 8 sheep and the chicken coops hold 6 chickens. If these are the only animals on the farm, how many animals are on the farm?
Paul bought a pizza with 16 slices. He and his three friends equally shared the pizza. How many slices did each get?
A bookshop was having a 25% discount on all books. Lucy purchased a book that originally cost $60. How much did she save from the discount?
John has a garden where he grows tomatoes. Each plant produces 20 tomatoes. This season, he planted 30 plants and sold each tomato for $0.5. How much money did he made by selling the tomatoes?

Frankie bakes 3 loaves of bread daily. He donates half of it to the local shelter. Last week he also did a charity event where he baked an extra 5 loaves. How many loaves did Frankie bake and keep last week?

Philip has started a vegetable garden in his backyard and has planted 3 rows of carrots with 12 carrots in each row. He also planted 4 rows of tomatoes with 15 tomatoes in each row. How many vegetables has Philip planted in total?

Mike goes for a run every morning. He runs for an hour at the speed of 6 mph. Sometimes he also runs in the evening for half an hour at the speed of 8 mph. If he runs both in the morning and evening for 5 days, how many miles will he cover in total?

Lisa wants to prepare 12 blueberry muffins for a bake sale. Each muffin requires 5 blueberries and she already picked 40 blueberries from her garden. How many more blueberries does she need to buy to make all 12 muffins?

Mike is currently leasing a motorcycle that costs $40 per month. He is planning to buy a brand new motorcycle that costs $60 per month. If Mike will have to pay for the new motorcycle for a year, how much will be the difference if he gets the brand new motorcycle instead of leasing a motorcycle?

A group of friends went on a picnic. They picked 5 baskets of strawberries. Each basket held 7 pounds of strawberries. They decided to make strawberry jam later, which requires 4 pounds of strawberries for each jar. How many jars of jam can they make?

Stanley is making bead necklaces to sell at a local craft fair. He used 15 beads for a short necklace and twice as many beads for a long necklace. If he made 9 short necklaces and 7 long necklaces, how many beads did he use in total?

Jennifer is preparing goodie bags for her daughter's birthday party. She has bought 5 packs of assorted candy to put in 20 bags. If it takes 1 pack to fill 4 bags and 1 pack to fill 6 bags, if there is no candy leftover after Jennifer fills the rest of the bags, how many bags did she fill with the third pack of candy?
Anna is baking cakes for a party. She buys 3 kg of flour and 2 kg of sugar. The flour cost $4 per kilogram and the sugar is twice the price of flour. How much does Anna spend for the flour and sugar?
In a classroom, there are twelve boys and fifteen girls. If five more boys join, the number of boys becomes three times the number of girls. How much more are the girls than the boys initially?
A box contains four times as many green marbles as blue marbles, and there are 6 blue marbles. How many marbles are there in total in the box?
A mall has 3 stories. On the first floor, there are 6 open shops. Each upper floor has 7 more open shops than the floor below it. How many open shops are there in the mall?
Erica and Courtney have $50 each. They want to buy shoes which cost $60. How much more money do they need?

Jack is sorting his comic book collection. He loses 20% of them in a move. Of the remaining ones, he loses another 10% when one of his boxes gets damaged in water. If he has 360 left, how many did he start with?
Ben runs a fruit stall. He bought 18 kilograms of apples and kept ⅔ in his stall. He sold the rest to a supermarket. How many kilograms of apples did Ben keep more than what he sold?

Rachel prefers to drink lemonade daily. She consumes 90 ounces of lemonade per day. In order to reduce the use of plastic, she buys a glass bottle. If the bottle's capacity is 100 ounces, how many times does she need to fill it in a week?

Emma's jogging route consists of running North 30 miles, then turning East and running for 10 more miles than the distance she ran to the north. If she then turns South and runs twice the distance she had ran to the East, how far did she run overall?
John earns $25 an hour. His job lasts 5 hours a day. If he worked for 9 consecutive days, how much did he earn in total?
A pharmacy sold 30 boxes of medicine on Monday and 45 boxes on Tuesday. How many boxes of medicine did the pharmacy sell in total?
A bicycle shop originally sold a bike for $200. Then the price was marked up by 25%. After that, the price was discounted by 20%. What is the final price of the bike?

Ahmad sells homemade candles for $15 each on his online store. He decides to hold a sale and offer a 20% discount on each candle. If Jared buys 3 candles from Ahmad after the price drop, how much will Jared pay?
A teacher has to mark 250 History assignments and 300 English assignments. If he marks the same number of assignments every day for 10 days, how many assignments should he aim to mark each day?
A school class has 20 children. For a field trip, 3 chaperones join them. Later, 2 students leave early. How many people are left on the field trip?
A charity organized a fun run event and are selling 100 entry tickets each for $5.00. They also received 4 $25 donations and a $50 donation. How much money did they raise in total?
There are 20 students in Jeff's painting class. If the students used up 4 painting tubes today and each student takes 2 tubes, how many more tubes must Jeff buy to replace the used ones?
Joseph works 6 hours a day and earns $20 an hour. He then spends 1/5 of his earnings on lunch. How much money does Joseph have left each day?
There are 30 students in class A, 32 students in class B, and 35 students in class C. Each student needs 2 notebooks. How many notebooks in all are needed?
