The Ames family is going on a two-week vacation trip. They would need $150 per day for expenses. They already have $1300. How much more money do they need for the trip?
Laura is planning a birthday party for his brother. She bought 4 packs of soda. There are 24 cans of soda in each pack. During the party, 18 cans were drunk. How many cans of soda were left?

Ella is a catering manager. She provides food for 30 clients a day. 40% of them order a $20 lunch meal and the rest order a $15 dinner meal. How much does she make in a week when she works 6 days a week?
Billy trains for a marathon every day. He runs 3 miles in the morning and another 5 miles in the evening. If he maintains this training regime for a week, how many miles will he run in total?

Samantha is planning to buy a new desktop computer. The computer costs $1500, and she plans to make a down payment of $300. The rest of the amount she'll pay in 10 equal monthly installments. Each month, she also has to pay interest equal to 3% of that month's payment. How much will Samantha's monthly payments be?

A bookstore has 200 mystery novels on a shelf. 3 customers borrow 4 books each from the shelf, and another 30 books are borrowed the next day. How many books are left on the shelf after the second day?
Susan is out riding her two horses in the morning, and her daughter joins her for the ride. How many legs' worth of organisms are riding along?

Samantha wants to feed the neighborhood squirrels. She has 5 pounds of peanuts and each squirrel eats 1/2 pound of peanuts. If Samantha wants to save 1 pound of peanuts for herself, how many squirrels can she feed?
Lara has 40 books on her bookshelf. She has three times as many fiction books as non-fiction books. How many fiction books does Lara have?

A quiz has three categories: easy, medium, and hard, with scoring values of 1, 2, and 3 respectively per correct answer. If Maria answered 10 questions correctly in the easy category, 4 in the medium category, and 6 in the hard category, what is her total score?

A shop received 8 blue bracelets, 4 yellow bracelets, 2 green bracelets and 1 red bracelet. However, 1 blue bracelet and half the yellow bracelets had to be returned due to defects. How many bracelets does the shop have left?
Michael and his two sisters decided to evenly split the amount of the bakeware set their mom wants. The set costs $60. How much will each of them need to pay?

Jessie bakes cookies to sell at a fair. She makes 6 cookies on Wednesday and three times as many on Thursday. By the end of the day on Thursday, all of the cookies she made are sold. How many cookies did Jessie make?
David has saved $440 to buy a new game console. If the price of the console is $550, how much more money does he need to save?
Sarah wants to bake a cake and needs 3 cups of flour. If she only has cups measuring 1/4 cups of flour each, how many of these does she need?

A mother and a daughter start walking towards each other at the same time from opposite ends of a street that is 40m long. If the mother is walking twice as fast as the daughter, at what distance from the mother's end of the street will they meet?

Tom has a savings account with $250 more than his sister's savings account. Tom's account earns him simple interest at the rate of 5% per annum. If his sister has $300, how much money will Tom have in his savings account after three years?
A bus can fit 50 passengers. If 3 buses are filled and 4 more people need a seat, how many buses are needed in total?
Mary goes to the gym 3 days a week and spends 2 hours each day there. If Mary pays $15 for each hour at the gym, how much does she spend on the gym in a month?
In a basketball game, George scores twice as many points as John. Together, they score a total of 27 points. How many points does George score?
Mike bought 3 shirts for $25 each and 2 pairs of shoes for $50 each. Mike also has a coupon that discounts $20 on his total purchase. What is the final amount Mike needs to pay?
Judy has saved 20% more than Lucy. Lucy has 15% more savings than Alice. If Alice has $200, how much has Judy saved?
Lisa has 60 candies in a jar. She gives her brother a third of the candies, then eats half of the remaining candies. How many candies does Lisa have left?

It's Yvette's turn to treat herself and her three best friends to a round of ice cream sundaes.  Alicia orders the peanut butter sundae for $7.50.  Brant orders the Royal banana split sundae for $10.00.  Josh orders the death by chocolate sundae for $8.50 and Yvette orders the cherry jubilee sundae for $9.00.  She leaves her waiter a 20% tip.  How much will her final bill be?

Diego collects rare coins. He has 4 coin albums, and each album fits 50 coins. If each coin costs an average of $15 and he fills up all his albums, how much money will he have spent on his coin collection?
A book store sells hardcover books for $12 each and softcover books for $7 each. If John buys 4 hardcover books and 3 softcover books, what is the total cost?
Samantha bought 15 chickens. Each chicken lays 5 eggs per day. How many eggs will Samantha have at the end of the week?

Jenny has a garden with 40 roses. She decides to add more roses, getting a fifth of her current roses from her neighbor. If each rose has 5 petals, how many petals does she have after receiving the roses from her neighbor?
John has 10 red balls and 12 green balls. Each red ball weighs 6 grams, while each green ball weighs 8 grams. What is the total weight of all the balls in John's possession?
Lisa bought a gown on sale, which was originally $200, for 30% off. She then has to pay a 5% sales tax on the new price. How much did Lisa end up paying for the gown?
John plans to travel 250 miles. He wants to cover the first `x` miles by car, and the rest by bike. If he travels three-fourths of the trip by car, how many miles does he travel by bike?

Janette went camping for 5 days. She brought along 40 pieces of beef jerky. She wants to ration it out so that she can eat 1 for breakfast, 1 for lunch, and 2 for dinner each day. When she returns from the trip, she plans on giving half of the remaining pieces to her brother. How many pieces of beef jerky will she have left once she does this?

Jennifer is making a smoothie that requires 2 ounces of spinach and double the amount of fruit. If she makes this smoothie every morning for 3 weeks, how many ounces of ingredients, in total, will Jennifer use?
Henry has $200 to spend on a new guitar. He spends $50 on the body, and $100 on the strings. What does he have left, in dollars, to spend on a case and picks?
George has some quarters and dollars in his wallet. He has 3 fewer dollars than quarters. He has a total of 21 dollars in his wallet. How many quarters does he have?
John has a stamp collection. He started with 500 stamps. He bought 100 new stamps but later sold 300 stamps. How many stamps does John have now?

John has 50 pieces of candy and decides to share them with his 4 friends equally. However, his little brother eats 2 pieces before he could share them out. How many pieces of candy does each person get?
Sam spent $50 buying a new pair of shoes, 2 pairs of socks and 3 t-shirts. If the shoes cost $30 and both pairs of socks cost $2.50 each, how much did each t-shirt cost?
Sandy baked 120 cookies. Her children ate 1/5 of the batch, and Sandy packaged 1/2 of the cookies for a friend. How many cookies does Sandy have left?
Anna and Peter are collecting stamps. Anna collected 200 stamps, and Peter collected twice as many as Anna. How many stamps did they collect all together?

Hannah has 10 hamsters, and Lloyd has 3 more hamsters than Hannah. After a week, Hannah has half as many hamsters as before and Lloyd gains twice as many hamsters. How many hamsters do they have in total?
Jane, Jack and Judy all own apple trees. Jane has 9 apple trees. Jack has 3 less than twice as many apple trees as Jane. Judy has 4 more apple trees than Jack. How many apple trees does Judy have?
A bakery wants to make 8 pies for a holiday giveaway. Each pie requires 5 cups of flour. If a bag of flour contains 20 cups, how many bags does the bakery need to buy?
Caroline bought 15 apples. She gave 4 to her friend John and ate 6 herself. How many apples does Caroline have left?

At a party, there are 5 slices of pizza per pizza box and 3 pizzas boxes. Each person at the party eats 2 slices of pizza. If there are 2 slices of pizza left at the end of the party, how many people attended the party?
Amara bought 3 novels for $15 each and a dictionary for $30. She also had to pay 5% tax on her entire purchase. How much did she have to pay in total?
Luke used 5 pounds of apples for a pie. Max needs 3 more pounds of apples to make apple jam. How many pounds of apples do they need in total?

Naomi is baking cookies. She needs a bag of chocolate chips for $3.85 and a bag of sugar for $2.25. She would also need 2.5 pounds of flour. The flour comes in a 5 lbs bag for $5. Given that she already has butter and eggs at home, what is the total cost for making the cookies?

Lisa picked 100 apples from her tree. Half the apples were ripe while the other half were not yet ripe. Lisa kept 30 unripe apples and gave the remainder to her neighbor who made them into pie. If it takes 5 apples to make a pie, how many pies can Lisa's neighbor make?
Liam withdraws $5000 from his savings. He spends $600 on groceries and gives 1/2 of the remaining amount to his sister. How much money does Liam have left?
In a school, 2/5 of the students are girls, and the rest are boys. If there are 300 students in total, how many boys are there?
Susie wants to purchase a pair of boots that cost $125. The store is having a sale where all items are 20% off. How much will Susie have to pay for the boots?
Tony is selling toys. He bought 120 toys for $1500 and wants to make a profit of 20% per toy. How much will he sell each toy for?
Jacky has four times more toys than Billy and six times more than Teddy. If Jacky has 24 toys, how many toys do Billy and Teddy have together?
Samantha has 24 pencils. She wants to share them evenly with 6 of her friends. How many pencils will each friend receive?

Kelly begins to write her book and on the first day writes one chapter. From the second day, she writes twice the number of chapters she wrote the previous day. If Kelly continues in this manner for 5 days, how many chapters would she have written?

Marie is swimming in a relay race. The pool is 50 meters long, but each swimmer must swim back and forth twice for each turn. If Marie and her three teammates each take turns, how many meters does the team swim in total?

Victor's school is holding a fundraiser. They made $340 from selling cupcakes, $400 from selling brownies, and the amount from selling cookies is half as much as the total of cupcakes and brownies. How much did they make from the fundraiser?
Lucy, Liz, and Pat are collecting stickers. Pat has 15 stickers more than Liz. Liz has 70 stickers fewer than total which is 200. How many photos does Lucy have?
A group of 60 students went on a field trip. 1/3 of the students bought souvenir magnets at $3 each. How much was spent on magnets in total?

Anna is planning a birthday party and needs to buy balloons. One store sells packages of 25 balloons for $5 each. Another store sells individual balloons for 25 cents each. If Anna needs 150 balloons for her party and decides to buy them from the first store, how much money does she save compared to buying them from the second store?

Ella and her friends went for a bike ride. Ella rode 15km in the morning and another 12km in the afternoon. Her friend Sophia rode twice as many kilometers as Ella did. What is the total distance that Ella and Sophia covered?
Lucy and her friends collected some bugs for a school project. They found 52 caterpillars, 14 beetles, and 29 butterflies. How many bugs did they collect?
John sold some comics to a vintage collector. John sold the comics for 200$. The collector then sells them for 50 more than 2 times their price. What is the collector's profit?

Dan is planning to build a wooden table. Each table requires 8 long planks and 6 short planks. If long planks are sold in a bundle of 4 and short planks in a bundle of 3, how many bundles does Dan need to buy to build the table?
Mr. Herbert's farm has 10 acres of corn. An acre yields 150 bushels of corn. If he sells the corn for $6 per bushel, how much will Mr. Herbert earn after selling all his corn?
John can complete 20 tasks in an hour. If he has 100 tasks to complete and he works for 4 hours, how many tasks does he still need to complete?
A fruit shop had 1200 fruits in total. 50 were apples and the rest were oranges. If 45 oranges were not sold, how many fruits were sold?
In 8 months Anna and Alex will be celebrating their 6th anniversary.  How many months ago did they celebrate their 4th anniversary?
In a room, there are 25 men and 35 women. If 30% of the women leave the room, how many people remain in the room?
Heather had 58 stickers in her collection. She bought 2 sheets of stickers with 14 stickers on each sheet. How many stickers does she have now?
Kevin invested $3000 in his savings account with an annual interest of 2.5%. How much money does he have in the account after 3 years?
A train carries 400 passengers per trip. Due to an event, the number of passengers triples. How many passengers does the train carry in 5 trips?
A library has 30 history books, 25 science books, and 15 math books. If a student checks out 2 history books, 3 science books, and 1 math book, how many books are left in the library?

Liam has a truckload of 75 large boxes to deliver. Each stop he makes allows him to unload 5 boxes. It takes him 10 minutes to unload the boxes at each stop and 15 minutes to drive to the next stop. If there are 15 stops on his route, how much time will he spend delivering all the boxes?

Larry has a garden where he grows flowers. He wants to order some new bulbs for his garden. The daffodils cost $3 per bulb, the tulips cost $2 per bulb and the lilies cost $4 each. He wants to order 5 daffodils, 3 tulips and 2 lilies. How much will Larry spend on new flower bulbs?
A school has 500 students. Twenty percent of the students play basketball, forty percent of them play football, and the rest do not play any sports. How many students do not play any sports?
A bookstore has 120 books on a shelf. If 4/5 of the books were sold, how many books are left?

Jane has a bicycle shop where she rents out bicycles for $10 per hour. On the weekend, she rented out 15 bicycles for 4 hours each. She also sold 10 helmets for $25 each. How much total money did Jane earn?

In a day, Mike catches 7 fish. Jane catches half as many fish as Mike. Chad catches 1/4 of the total of what Jane and Mike catch. If Harry catches 4 times as many fish as Chad does, how many fish do they all catch in a day?
Emma has $200 in her savings account. She wants to buy a laptop that costs $350. How much more money does she need to have in order to buy the laptop?
Sarah cuts down 40 bamboos. From each bamboo, she can make 20 sticks. A chair takes 10 sticks to make and sells for $200. She paid $4000 for all the labor. How much profit did she make?

Amy is building a fence around her garden. Each side of the garden is 25 feet long and she has 150 feet of fencing. How many sides will she be able to fence if one foot of fencing is wasted in the corners?

A bakery uses 4 cups of flour and 2 cups of sugar to make a loaf of bread. If the bakery baked 10 loaves yesterday, and flour costs $0.75 per cup while sugar costs $1.2 per cup, how much did the bakery spend on ingredients?

Liam bought a bike and asked everyone to get him biking accessories as gifts which he already owned half of. On his birthday, he is given 10 biking accessories. On Christmas he is given 6 more. How many biking accessories does he have now?
A library has 10 times the amount of fiction books as non-fiction books. If the library has 90 non-fiction books, how many books in total does the library have?
If Maria baked 100 cookies and selling each for $2, and she gave away 10 cookies to her friends, how much did Maria earn from selling her cookies?

Hayley is calculating her expenses for her freshman year in university. She will take 16 subjects, with each subject costing $500. She needs to buy 6 textbooks for $100 each plus a $150 registration fee. How much will Hayley's expenses be in total?
Liam has a lemonade stand. He sells each cup of lemonade for 5 cents. If he sold 100 cups of lemonade one sunny afternoon, how much money did he make?
Lisa bought a necklace for $100 and earrings for twice the price of the necklace. She also bought a bracelet which cost three times as much as the earrings. How much money did Lisa spend in total?
In his sophomore college class, Felix learned that there were 150 students, 40% of them were male. If 25% of the males are wearing glasses, how many male students were wearing glasses?
The count of strawberries that John has to what Nancy has is 12:36. If the total number of strawberries they have is 288, how much strawberries does Nancy have?
John eats 4 apples a day. His brother, Mike, eats twice as many apples as John does. If their mother buys a week's worth of apples per shopping trip, how many apples does she need to buy?
A truck used 60 liters of diesel fuel to travel 600 kilometers. If the price of diesel is $1.20 per liter, how much will it cost for the truck to travel 1800 kilometers?
The population of Smallville rose from 12000 in 1990 to 15000 in 2000. What was the average annual increase in the population of Smallville during this period?

Diana decided to cycle 10 miles a day to keep herself fit. She cycled for 15 days straight. But then, she felt a bit tired and reduced her cycling to 7 miles a day for the next 8 days. How many miles did Diana cycle in total?

Michael and three of his friends decided to jog in the local park. Michael jogged 5 miles and his friends jogged an average of 3 miles each. Calculate the total number of miles Michael and his friends jogged together.
Jessica has 100 apples. She gives half of them to her classmates and then gives a quarter of the remaining apples to her teacher. How many apples does Jessica have left?

Peter is building block towers. He makes 7 towers and each tower consist of 8 blocks. Later he decides to disassemble three towers and create 5 new ones from the disassembled blocks. How many blocks are there in total at the end?
Samuel has 200 stickers. He puts 5 stickers on each page of his sticker book. He fills up 18 pages of the book. How many stickers does Samuel have left?
If a football team scores 4 touchdowns (6 points each) in a game and an additional 2 extra points for each touchdown, how many points has the team scored in the game?

Emily has a flower garden that is 8 meters wide and 12 meters long. She decides to increase the width by 3 meters and the length by 2 meters. What is the new area (in square meters) of Emily's flower garden?
Riley bought a new Lego set with 500 pieces. After playing with the set, he lost 7% of the pieces. When Riley counted, how many pieces did he have left?
In a marathon, the first runner runs 18 miles, the second one runs twice as much, and the third one runs half as much as the second runner. What is the total distance they have run?
A roller coaster can accommodate 90 people in 15 minutes. If the roller coaster is open from 2:00 pm until 8:00 pm, how many people will get to ride?

Emily is organizing a trip and have some seats in the bus and in her van. If she has 6 seats in her van and the number of seats in the bus is three times as many as in the van. How many seats in total does Emily have?
A farmer has 2000 kg of rice harvested. He packs equal amounts into 50 bags. How much rice is in each bag?

Jenna plans to keep fit by cycling. On Monday, she cycles 7 miles; Tuesday, 6.5 miles; Wednesday, 7.2 miles; and on Thursday, 8 miles. Determine the average distance Jenna cycles on each of the days she cycles.
An auditorium has 40 rows of seats. Each row has 25 seats. During a performance, 35 rows are filled. How many seats remain unoccupied?
It takes 2 robots 4 hours to assemble a car.  If you add 2 more robots who work at the same rate, how much time will they take to assemble the same car?
John earned $2000 from his job in July. He plans to save for a car that costs $25000. If he can save the same amount each month, how many months will it take John to save for the car?

Alex runs a coffee shop that sells coffee for $4 a cup. On Monday, he sold 50 cups of coffee, and on Tuesday, he sold 60 cups of coffee. How much did Alex make in total from selling coffee on Monday and Tuesday?

Jake's house is twice as far from Mike's house as Mia's house along a certain road, and Jake's house is 8 miles away from Mike's house along this same road, how much distance (in miles) will Jake cover if he goes from his house to Mike's house, then Mia's, then back to Mike's before going back to his own house?

Sophie has a 3 hour movie that she finds rather lengthy; she watches 45 minutes and then takes a 10 minute break. This break adds to her total viewing time. She watches the movie for another hour, then takes another 20 minute break. Completing the last 75 minutes in one go. If she added up the duration of the movie plus all the breaks she took, how long in minutes did it take her to watch the movie?
Mark bought a car for $20000 and sold it for a 25% profit. How much did he sell the car for?
A TV network has a 60-minute slot for a movie. However, the movie they want to air is 90 minutes long. They want to cut the movie by 33.33% to fit the slot. How long will the movie be after it's cut?

David and his friends decide to play a game of poker. His friend brings 100 chips. Each round, they bet five chips and whoever wins gets to keep all the chips. After 17 rounds, David has 85 chips. How many rounds has he lost?
Anna buys 6 packs of cookies. Each pack contains 12 cookies. She invites 9 friends over and wants to ensure each friend gets an equal amount of cookies. How many cookies will each friend eat?
Mark bought 15 pounds of mangoes. He used 1/3 of it to make mango shake and used 2 pounds to make pies. How many pounds of mangoes are left?

In a tournament, a tennis player can earn 5 points for a win, 3 points for a draw and 2 points for a lost match. If player won 10 matches, drawn 6 matches and lost 5 matches. How many points did they earn?
A group of children is selling lemonade for a charity. They made 3 batches of lemonade with each batch providing 10 cups. If they sold all cups for $2 each, how much money did they make?

Tiffany is going to the beach and wants to make sure she has enough sunscreen. She knows she needs to re-apply sunscreen after 2 hours. She also knows she needs 3 ounces of sunscreen each application and a bottle contain 12 ounces and costs $3.5. If she is there for 16 hours, how much will the sunscreen cost?
A bike costs $600. Richard has saved up $250. How much more does he need to save to be able to buy the bike?

A bakery sold 20 loaves of bread on Friday. On Saturday, the bakery sold twice as many loaves as on Friday. On Sunday, they sold half the number of loaves as on Saturday. How many loaves did the bakery sell by Sunday?

Ali is a builder constructing a fence around a yard. He needs to build 14 sections that are 2 metres long and 8 sections that are 3 metres long. It requires 4 metres of wood to build 1 metre of fencing. If wood costs $30 per metre, how much is the total cost of wood for the fence?

There are three groups of swimmers. The number of swimmers in the first group is triple the number of swimmers in the second group. There are 10 swimmers in the second group. The third group has half the number of swimmers in the first group. How many swimmers are there in total?
Mrs. Johnson baked 60 cupcakes for a school fair. If she sold 70% of the cupcakes, how many cupcakes are left over?
Jake is a woodworker. He can carve 15 wooden spoons in an hour and works for 7 hours a day, 6 days a week. How many wooden spoons does he make a week?
An apple tree produces 25 apples every year. If the production increases by fifteen percent every year, how many apples will the tree produce in the first and second year?
A pizza place sells each pizza slice for $3. If Mark has $27, how many slices of pizza can he buy?
A garden has 100 roses. 20% of them are red. George added 30 more red roses to the garden. What percent of the roses are red now?

Lisa invites 2 friends and 1 cousin to her house.  They each drink 2 cups of coffee a day.  How many cups do Lisa and her guests drink while they are there for the 3 days if each person drinks 1 cup of coffee per meal?

Adam wants to buy a video game console that costs $500. He has saved $375 already. His grandfather gave him a gift of $50. How much more money does Adam need to save in order to buy the video game console?
In eight years, Alex will be five times as old as his younger brother, Charlie. If Charlie is currently seven years old, how old is Alex?
Sam has a sticker collection. He gets 10 new stickers every week for 7 weeks. His sister gives him twice as many more stickers as Sam collected in a week. How many stickers does Sam have now?

Harper has a box of chocolates. There are 500 chocolates in the box. Harper ate 1/5 of the chocolates on Monday and 3/10 of the remaining chocolates on Tuesday. How many chocolates does Harper have left after Tuesday?
A restaurant sells burgers in packs of 4 for $16. How much does each burger cost?

Emma owns a small floral shop. On regular days, she sells 15 bouquets of roses and 10 bouquets of tulips. One day, she sold 18 bouquets of roses and 23 bouquets of tulips. If bouquets of roses cost $7 and bouquets of tulips cost $5, what is the difference, in dollars, of Emma's sales for that day and her daily average?

John is part of the hiking club. He took 20 photos of birds to show to the club. He took twice as many photos of trees as birds, and 15 fewer photos of mountains than trees. If John took 25 fewer photos last week, how many photos did he take then?

Sophie and her sister want to fill a big water tank with bottles. Sophie can carry four bottles each round, and her sister can carry five bottles each round. If it takes 1800 bottles to fill the tank, how many rounds will it take to fill the tank?
Johnny shares a third of a large pizza with his friend every day. A large pizza costs about $18. How much will he spend for them to have enough pizza to last for a week?

Jacob has 5 apples. He ate 2 apples for breakfast and gave 1 apple to his friend Ethan. If he wants to divide the rest of the apples equally between his remaining 3 friends, how many apples will each of his friends receive?
Mark has a YouTube channel where he teaches math. On the first day, he gained 200 subscribers. His subscribers increased by 50% on the second day. How many subscribers did Mark have after two days?
Jane did yoga for two hours at a rate of 200 calories per hour. Later she went running for 3 hours at a rate of 600 calories per hour. How many calories did Jane burn in total?
Monica baked cookies to sell at a bake sale. She baked 125 cookies on Saturday and twice as many cookies on Sunday. How many cookies did she bake in total?

In a tropical aquarium, John started with twice as many angel fish as clown fish. After buying 8 more angel fish, he now has a total of 36 angel fish. How many clown fish were in the aquarium initially?

Jenny is arranging her comic books collection. She has 10 racks, and each rack can store 20 comic books. Unfortunately, two of the racks broke, and she had to use the remaining ones. How many comic books was Jenny able to store?

John is printing out flyers for his upcoming concert. Each band flyer uses 30 pages, each ticket flyer uses 15 pages, and each sponsor flyer uses 50 pages. If John prints out 6 band flyers, 4 ticket flyers, and 5 sponsor flyers, how many pages does he use?
A bakery made some chocolate cakes and red velvet cakes. There are 24 chocolate cakes. The bakery made 3 times as many red velvet cakes. How many cakes does the bakery have in total?

Bob is a coffee seller. He sold 25 cups of coffee yesterday. Each cup was sold for $4. Today, he wants to make $120. If he sells coffee for the same price as yesterday, how many cups of coffee does he need to sell today?

In Mr. Garner's class, some students are absent on each of the three days. On the first day, 40 students were present. The number of students present on the second day was 20 more than the day prior. On the third day, the number of absent students was the same as the first day. If there are 100 students in the class, calculate the number of absent students over the three days.

Kate loves painting. She decided to go to an Art-museum in her city. The entrance fee is $5. Kate loved the visit so much, that she decided to go there once a month. After 1 year, the ticket price increased to $7 and Kate decided she is going to visit the museum only 4 times a year. How much did Kate pay for all visits to the Art-museum after 3 years of visiting?

Allen is tracking how many kilometers he runs in a month. He runs to the park and home six days a week, runs on the treadmill every day, and runs to the supermarket once a week. The park is five kilometers away, treadmill runs are three kilometers, and the supermarket is two kilometers. How many kilometers does Allen run in a month?
Charlie is buying more fish food. He buys a 5kg box then another 3kg box, and he now has 12kg of fish food. How many kilograms of fish food did Charlie already have?
In a class of 35 students, 8 brought an apple for lunch, 5 brought a banana, and 10 brought a sandwich. How many children didn't bring any food?
Mike currently has double the age of his sister, Jane. In 6 years, the sum of their ages will be 46. How old is Jane currently?
In a fruit shop, an apple costs $2 and a banana costs $1. Lucy bought 4 apples and 3 bananas. How much did she need to pay?
If a bag of apples weighs 4 lbs and there are 3 apples in each bag, how much does each apple weigh?
Alex sold 4 crates of apples at $20 per crate. Later that day he sold another crate of apples at a discount of 50%. How much did Alex earn that day?
A bookstore sells a notebook for $5 each. If Lucy bought 7 of them and got a 10% discount on the total price, how much did she pay?
Lisa loves reading books. She reads 2 books a week and each book is 80 pages long. How many pages does she read in a year?

Tony has a cleaning service. He spends 10 minutes vacuuming, 15 minutes dusting, 25 minutes mopping, and 20 minutes doing laundry per house. This afternoon, he cleaned 3 houses. How many minutes did Tony spend cleaning this afternoon?
Andy purchased a car worth $15,000. After a year, the car's value depreciated by 20%. How much is the car worth after a year?
Emily baked 120 cupcakes for a party. Each guest eats 3 cupcakes, and there were 25 guests at the party. How many cupcakes were left?

A marathon training club has 150 members who each pay $5 per week. To cover the cost of high demand, the club recruits an extra 75 members. How much does the club receive in total per week from its members after the recruitment?

Joshua, Jose, and Albert are collecting rocks. Joshua collected 80 rocks while Jose collected 14 fewer rocks. Albert has collected 20 more rocks than Jose. How many more rocks did Albert collect than Joshua?
Tom reads a page of a book every 2 minutes. If the book has 340 pages, how many hours will it take for Tom to read the book?
Kim rents a car for $20 a day. She pays $200 as the deposit. If she rents the car for 5 days, how much does she have to pay in total?
Mark and Brian were splitting a bag of candy. The ratio of candy Mark got to Brian's was 2:5. If the entire bag had 210 pieces of candy and Mark ate half of his, how much candy does Mark have left?
A factory produces 300 gadgets each day. It operates 6 days a week. How many gadgets does it produce in a week?

Paul cooks 4 meals per day. Each meal requires 2 ounces of olive oil. A bottle of olive oil costs $10 and contains 20 ounces. He uses 1/2 a gallon of milk per week for his desserts. A gallon of milk costs $5. He doesn’t use anything else. How much does he spend on his cooking per week?
A train travels at a speed of 85 mph and needs to travel a total of 510 miles. How many hours will the train need to reach its destination?

Sara runs a lemonade stand. She charges $1.50 for each cup of lemonade, and her daily expenses for making the lemonade are $20. If Sara sells 100 cups in a day, how much money does she make after taking into account her expenses?

Harry owns 3 geckos, 2 iguanas, and 4 snakes. If he spends $10 to feed each snake, $5 to feed each iguana, and $15 to feed each gecko every month, how much does he spend every year to feed all his pets?
There are 13 boys in a class. There are three fewer girls than boys. How many students are there in total in the class?

A local gift shop sells custom snow globes. The materials for each snow globe costs $8, and the shopkeeper charges $2 for a special gift box for each globe. The selling price of each snow globe is $20. How much profit does the shopkeeper make on each snow globe?
Sam discovered a new star. It is 3 light years closer to Earth than Alpha Centauri, which is 4.37 light years away. How far is Sam's star from Earth?
In a week, Emily runs 3 miles each day while Alan runs 2.5 miles each day. How many more miles does Emily run than Alan in the span of 10 weeks?

John had initially budgeted $200 for his new pair of shoes but the pair he wanted turned out to be 30% more expensive. If he decided to buy a cheaper pair at half the price of his initial budget, how much money does he have left?
Grace baked 3 batches of cookies. Each batch of cookies contained 12 cookies. Grace gave 4 cookies to her brother and 6 to her sister. How many cookies does Grace have left?

Maddie, a florist, is arranging a bouquet of flowers. Each bouquet consists of 10 roses. She currently has 50 roses. One day, her supplier delivers 20% more roses than she currently has. How many bouquets can Maddie arrange with all the roses?

A bakery has 500 cookies to divide evenly between 25 boxes. Each box normally costs $10. The bakery decides to increase the price of each box by 10%. How much will all the boxes cost if they are sold at the new price?
Sarah has 15 apples. She shared four-fifths of her apples to her friends. How many apples does she have left?
Molly is preparing a fruit basket. She has 8 apples, which she slices into quarters, and 9 pears, which she halves. How many pieces of fruit will be in Molly's fruit basket?

The area of a forest is three times the area of the square-shaped street. If the street has a length of 100 meters on one side and the number of trees per square meter in the forest is 4, calculate the total number of trees in the forest.
Susan has $60 and 500 dimes. If she gives her brother $10 and 300 dimes, how much money in total is she left with?
Helen's age is 3 times that of her son Samuel's. If Samuel turns 15 next year, how old will Helen be by then?
A laptop uses 4.5 watts of power per hour. How many watts would the laptop use if it was used for 10 hours a day for 3 days?
Jane wants to buy a computer that costs $900. She already managed to save $250. If she can save $50 per week, how many weeks does she still need to save up to buy the computer?
Paul rides his bike at a speed of 20 mph. He rides for 4 hours going to a place and took a longer route going back that is twice the distance of the original route. How long did he spend biking?
Elsa is a teacher. She has 24 students in her class. She wants to divide them into equal groups for a science project. If she places 6 students in each group, how many groups will there be?
Samantha buys 3 novels for 30% off. They each cost $15. How much did she pay?
Jim has $20. He buys a toy for $6 and a book for $3. How much money does he have left?

Leo aimed to lose weight. At first, he lost 10 pounds. Then, he gained back three times the weight that he initially lost. Then, he lost four times more weight than he initially had lost. But finally, he gained back 5 pounds. If he weighed 150 pounds at the start of his fitness journey, what was his final weight, in pounds?
There are 4 blue candies and 6 red candies in a jar. If Lisa takes 2 red candies and two times as many blue candies as red candies from the jar, how many candies does the jar have now?
Mark wants to rent out his house for $1200 per month. If the real estate agency charges a fee of 10% of the rent, how much will be the fee?
Jane’s garden grows 200 strawberries every month. Jane eats 5 strawberries a day. How many strawberries are left after a month?
Mary has 16 cookies. She eats 4 and gives 5 to her friend. How many cookies does she have left?

Scott has 240 marbles. He wants to distribute them equally amongst his 8 friends. After distributing the marbles, he finds 2 more marbles under his bed. How many marbles does each friend get if he gives the two more marbles to his friend who likes marbles the most?

The population of Gotham city this year is triple what it was five years ago. If the Gotham City Census Bureau predicts that there will be 2000 more people in the city next year, and the population five years ago was 10000, calculate the total population of Gotham city next year if the predictions are accurate.

A local gym started a campaign for new members. The first day they get 25 members and then, each following day, they get 5 more members than the previous day. How many members will they get by the end of a 5 day campaign?
If Jane has 15 oranges, John has 45 oranges, and Jenny has 30 oranges, how many times can Jane's number of oranges fit into the average amount of oranges for a person in the group?
There are 10 girls and 8 boys in a class. How many more girls than boys are in the class?
Sofia found a cute dress on sale for $50. However, there was an additional 30% discount on the sale price. How much will Sofia pay for the dress?
Sophia slept 7.5 hours every night in April. She decided she needed more sleep and started sleeping 9.5 hours a night in May. How many more hours of sleep did Sophia get in May?

A school conducts a fundraising event where students can buy cupcakes for $2 each. If 18 students bought cupcakes and the organizers contributed a quarter of the proceeds back to the school, how much did the school earn?
Billy has 4 times as many baseball cards as Mark. Mark has one third the baseball cards David has. How many baseball cards are there altogether if David has 27?
A pizza place offers a large pizza for $14. If you buy 4 large pizzas, you get a discount of $10. How much would it cost to buy 5 large pizzas with the discount?

In a school, there are 40 students who play soccer, 25 students who play basketball, and 10 students who play both sports. How many students in the school do not play either of the two sports if the total number of students in the school is 100?
Lewis found 4 more items on the scavenger hunt than Samantha.  Samantha found four times the amount of items than Tanya who was only able to find 4 items.  How many items did Lewis find?
Larry starts at an elevation of 800 feet. He climbs upward at a rate of 15 feet per minute for 6 minutes. What is his elevation now?

In a bookstore, there are three kinds of magazines. A fashion magazine costs $5, a science magazine for $3.5, and a cooking magazine for $2 per copy. One day the store was able to sell 10 copies of fashion magazines, 7 copies of cooking magazines, and 16 copies of science magazines. How much did the bookstore earn?
Samantha has 15 roses in her garden, and each rose has 10 buds. If she makes a bouquet for her mother with 3 roses, how many buds are remaining in Samantha's garden?
Sonia earns $15 per hour at her part-time job. If she works 5 hours a day, how much will she earn in a 30-day month?
Dante has 450 marbles. He gave an equal number of marbles to 5 of his friends. How many marbles does he have left?
For a movie night, there are 50 seats available. Ben reserved 3/5 of the seats on the first day, and then 2/3 of the remaining seats on the second day. How many seats are still unreserved?
Mike is saving money for a new video game that costs $50. He earns $10 per hour of lawn mowing. If Mike mows lawns for 2 hours every day for a week, how much money will he earn?
George has 6 pairs of socks. His brother has 2 fewer. How many pairs of socks do they have in all?
A pack of 24 pens costs $12. Tom needs to buy pens for his 30 students. If every student needs 2 pens, how much will Tom spend on pens?

Ian trains for hockey every day. On Monday, he spent 45 minutes on skating, on Tuesday, he spent 40 minutes, and on Wednesday, he spent 50 minutes. How many minutes in total did Ian spend on skating in these three days?
In a football match, Tom scored 12 goals, Jerry scored half as many as Tom, and Anderson scored 3 less than Jerry. How many goals did they score in total?

Mary is making cupcakes for her daughter's birthday party. If she has 24 pieces of cupcake liners and 6 trays that can each hold 10 cupcakes, how many cupcakes can Mary make if all the trays and liners are used?

There are seven apples in a fruit basket. There are three less pears than apples.  There are 2 times as many bananas as pears, and there are three fourth as many grapes as bananas.  How many fruit are in the basket?
Mike owns a grocery store. He sells apples for $1.50 each, and bananas for $0.50 each. If he sells 100 apples and 200 bananas in one day, how much money does he make?
In a small town, 4 out of every 9 residents work in the local grocery shop. If the town has a population of 450 people, how many work in the grocery shop?
A farmer has 4 times as many chicken as pigs. If the farmer has 15 pigs, how many animals does the farmer have?
Tom has 102 pennies, 95 nickels, 60 dimes and 200 quarters. If he uses a coin-counting machine to convert all his coins into dollars for a 20% fee. How much will he receive after the 20% fee?

Alice went shopping and bought a skirt, a shirt, and a pair of shoes. She paid a total of $60. The shoes were double the price of the shirt and the skirt cost $10 less than the shirt. How much did the pair of shoes cost?
A grocery store sells pineapples for $4 each. If Meg buys 6 pineapples, how much does she pay in total?
$500 was divided between Alex and Jackie. Alex received 2/3 of the money. From her share, Alex then spent 1/8 of the original $500 on makeup. How much does Alex have left?
Sandy is making bracelets. She needs 15 beads for each bracelet. She has 200 beads. How many bracelets could she make?

In a school of 800 students, 65% of the students participate in at least one after-school activity. Among those who participate, 30% are in the drama club. How many students in the school are in the drama club?
Sarah has a pizza shop. She sold 5 pizzas on Saturday, 3 pizzas on Sunday, and on Monday, she sold triple the number of pizzas she sold on Saturday. How many pizzas does Sarah sell?
Sam received three more puzzles than Daniel did. Alex received twice as many puzzles as Sam did. If Daniel has 5 puzzles, how many puzzles does Alex have?
A farmer has a field of 160 watermelon plants. He sold 100 of them in the morning and 10 more in the afternoon. How many watermelon plants are remaining in the field?
Jenny bought groceries worth $200 and used a coupon that saved her 25% of her total bill. How much did she pay for her groceries?
A farm has 7 apple trees. Each tree produces 320 apples. The farmer sells the apples for $0.50 each. How much does he earn from selling all the apples?

Jacob is planning a dinner party where there will be a total of 18 people. He will need 3 bottles of wine per person for the dinner. Bottles of wine are sold for $4.00 each. How much will he spend on wine?

Kurt's old refrigerator cost $0.85 a day in electricity.  He recently bought a new energy-efficient refrigerator that only cost $0.45 a day in electricity.  How much money does Kurt save in a 30-day month with his new refrigerator?
Megan is training for a marathon. She runs 6 miles every day. How many miles does she run in a month?
A toy shop sells small teddies for $5 and large teddies for $15. They sold $70 worth of teddies in total. If they sold 6 small teddies, how many large teddies did they sell?
Emma has 100 collections of books. She gave her sister 15 books and her brother 10 books. How many books does she have left?

John decided to go on a diet. He was consuming 3500 calories a day, but he decided to cut down 500 calories from his diet. He also decided to burn 200 calories through exercise daily. How many calories would he consume in a week?
A candy bag contains 8 red candies, 12 green candies and 5 blue candies. How many candies are there in the bag?
Betsy has 50 lemons. She makes lemonade using 3 lemons per glass. She sells each glass for $2.50. If she sells out all her lemonade, how much money does she make?
Adam has 45 blue marbles and 30 green marbles. He wants to give equally his marbles to his three friends. How many marbles will each friend receive?

Susan is mixing carrots into her children's mashed sweet potatoes. They didn't notice when she mixed 3 cups of carrots with 6 cups of sweet potatoes. If she has 24 cups of sweet potatoes, how many cups of carrots can she add?
A train travels at a speed of 60 miles per hour. How many miles does the train travel in 25 hours?
If a worker is paid $15 an hour and works 8 hours a day, how much will 3 workers earn in 5 days?
Jackie is baking cookies for a charity event. If one batch of cookies requires 2 cups of flour and she plans to make 7 batches, how many cups of flour does she need?
A math tutor charges $40 per hour. If Calvin studied for 2 hours a day, 3 days a week, how much will he need to pay his tutor at the end of the week?
An advertising agency produces jingles for TV commercials at $5,000 each. If they produce 15 jingles in one week, how much money do they make in a single week?
John is four times as old as Mark. Sue is two times as old as Mark. If Mark is 7 years old, what is the sum of their ages?
In a sale, a store is offering a 25% discount on a book that costs $80. How much will you save after the discount?
In an orchard, there are 80 apples, bananas, and oranges in total. If one quarter of all the fruits were apples, and a fifth of the fruits were bananas, how many of them were oranges?
Amy wants to buy a handbag. The store buys the handbag for $50, but charges 20% for profit. However, the handbag is on sale for 60% off the selling price. What is the price now?
Sam's Motorcycle can travel 180 miles with one full tank of gas. If his motorcycle's tank holds 5 gallons of gas, how many miles per gallon does his motorcycle get?

A car rental company charges $45 per day for a basic car. An additional $10 per day is charged if a navigation system is added. John plans to rent a car for 5 days with a navigation system. How much will the rental cost him?

Jane had a bag of candies. She gave 1/3 of her candies to her brother. After that, she gave half of her remaining candies to her friend. If Jane had 24 candies left, how many candies did she have at first?

James buys books for his classroom. He buys 2 physics books that costs $28 each, 3 math books that cost $15 each, and 4 novels that cost $12 each. James gives the cashier a hundred-dollar bill. How much does the cashier give James as change?

Maria can read 20 pages of a book per hour. She started reading a new novel yesterday and kept reading for 3 hours. Today she continued reading for 2 hours, took a break, and read for another 1 hour. By the end of today, she had read 130 pages of the book. How many pages had she read when she started today?
Chris bought 3 bags of apples and each bag has 12 apples. Bill has 8 apples. How many apples do they have altogether?
A supermarket sells oranges for $3 per kg. If Jane buys 5kg of oranges, how much will she pay?
A painting contest has a $500 prize for the winner. If the prize is equally divided among the 5 winners, how much does each person get?
Tom and Jerry decided to have a race. The track is 500 meters long. Tom managed to finish it in 2 minutes while Jerry took 3 minutes. How much faster was Tom compared to Jerry?
If all twenty 18-wheel trucks in the parking lot have their spare tire with them, how many tires are there in total in the parking lot?
Jody has 6 shelves. Each shelf has a space for 20 books. However, she already has 75 books on the shelves. If Jody wants every spot on her shelves filled, how many more books does she need to buy?
Jake buys a watermelon. The watermelon weighs 10 pounds. He eats 2 pounds of the watermelon every day. How many days will it take for him to finish the watermelon?

In a small town, it costs $2 to recycle a pound of plastic. Each person produces 1/3 pound of plastic waste a day. If there are 3000 people in the town, how much will it cost to recycle all the plastic waste in one day?

Hannah wants to know how much hot chocolate her thermos can hold. So she takes a cup that holds 8 ounces and starts filling the thermos. When it reaches the top, she has filled it 10 times. Then she drinks 2 cups of hot chocolate. How much hot chocolate does she drink if she does this every day for a week?
Christopher wants to build a rectangular pond in his yard. He bought 34 feet of fencing to surround it. If the width of the pond is going to be 7 feet, what will be the length of the pond?
A pack of pencils comes with 24 pencils. Billy uses a pack of pencils for drawing at school every fortnight. He doesn't draw on weekends. How many pencils does he use per day at school?
Tom gets 10 jellybeans from his father and 6 from his mother. He already had 8 jellybeans. How many jellybeans does Tom have in total?
Brinley finds a great deal on bracelets and buys 3 for $15 each. She then sells them at her boutique for $25 each. How much profit did she gain from selling these bracelets?
John can paint a room in 5 hours. Mike is a less experienced painter, and it takes him 7 hours to paint the same room. How long will it take them if they work together?
In a class of 35 students, 15 are boys and the rest are girls. After 5 girls transferred to another school, how many girls are left?

Five friends and three parents are going to a football game. The cost for a kid's ticket is only three fourths of the adult ticket's cost. The total cost for the tickets is $84. How much is one kid's ticket?

Gary decided to harvest his apple trees from his farm. From the first tree he collected 78 apples. From the second tree he collected 140 apples and from the third tree he collected 90 apples. He then found that 5 apples weighted one pound. How many pounds of apples did Gary collect?

Ani bought 26 novels and 15 historical books for her library. During a house move, 3 novels and 2 historical books were damaged and had to be thrown away. Three months later, Ani bought thrice as many novels and four times as many historical books as the number that were damaged. How many books does Ani's library currently have?

Emma and her colleagues participated in a wildlife count in a national park. Emma saw 15 moose, while her colleagues saw an average of 10 moose each. If Emma had three colleagues, calculate the total number of moose they saw.
Karen wants to make wreaths for Christmas. It will take 4 dozen pine cones to make 1 wreath. If she wants to make 3 wreaths, how many pine cones does she need to gather?
In a soccer match, there were 11 players from each team on the field. At halftime, each team made 3 substitutions. After the changes, how many players in total did play during the match?
Sofia made 9 pies for a fundraiser. Each pie was sold for $12. If she donated 1/3 of her earnings, how much did she donate?
Martin collected 6 conch shells with 2 spirals each and 2 nautilus shells with 4 spirals each. How many spirals on the shells did he collect in total?

Amy and Josh buy tickets to ride the roller coaster at the local fair. Amy rides the coaster five times and Josh rides it seven times. If a ticket for the roller coaster costs $3, how much money did they spend?
Samantha usually reads 6 pages of a book per day. But if she reads an additional 2 pages per day, she can finish the book in 10 days. How many pages does the book have?
A chicken farmer sells 4 dozen eggs per day. How many eggs does the farmer sell in a week?
During a piano class, Alexa was 15 minutes late. Three of her classmates were each five minutes later than she was. How late were all four students combined?

David has a tank of gas for his car. The tank can hold 50 liters of gas. He starts a trip with his tank 80% full. During the trip, he uses 60% of the gas in his tank. How many liters of gas does he have left?
A bakery makes 200 cookies each day. 15% of these cookies are chocolate chip and the remaining are sugar cookies. How many sugar cookies does the bakery make daily?

Jenny went on a shopping spree and bought 15 items of clothing. When she got home, she realized that she only liked 60% of what she had bought. If she returned the rest to the store, how many items of clothing did she return?
If the library has 240 books and 4/5 of them are fiction books, how many non-fiction books does the library have?
Lucy needs 8 hours to complete a task. But when Mike helps, they finish the task in 5 hours. If Mike can only keep this pace for 2 hours, how much time will Lucy need to finish the task on her own?

Liz baked 4 dozen cupcakes for her daughter's birthday. On the way to the party, 5 cupcakes fell and were ruined. During the party, each kid ate 2 cupcakes and there were 15 kids there. How many cupcakes were left after the party?
Amy decides to enroll in a gym that charges 20% more than her previous gym.  If the cost of her old gym was $300 a month, how much is the new gym charging?
Ann and Leah are running a book-stand at a fair. Leah sold thrice as many books as Ann. In total, they sold 200 books. How many books did Leah sell?
Fisher has 12 apples. She wants to divide them equally into baskets. If she has 4 baskets, how many apples will be in each basket?

John gets an allowance of 50 dollars per week. He decides to save 60% of it. From the remaining money, he spends 12 dollars on snacks and the rest on movie tickets which cost 8 dollars each. How many movie tickets can he buy?
On his farm, Fred has twice as many chickens as ducks. If the total number of birds (chickens and ducks) is 105, how many more chickens than ducks does Fred have?
Mason is training for a marathon and ran at 8 mph for 15 minutes today. After taking a rest, he then walked for an additional 30 minutes at a pace of 3 mph. How many total miles did he run and walk?
Emma goes shopping and buys 5 bags of apples. Each bag contains 24 apples. If she eats 2 apples a day, how many days will the apples last?

Paula has 5 butterflies and 8 beetles in her insect collection. Each butterfly needs a 3-inch by 3-inch space, and each beetle needs a 2-inch by 2-inch space. How many square inches total space does Paula need?
A car uses 4 liters of fuel for every 100 kilometers. How much fuel will the car use for a 1500 km trip?
If a movie theater has 8 rows with 15 seats in each row and for one movie, tickets were sold for all but 20 seats, how many tickets were sold?
Liam paints 5 paintings a week and sells each for $100. How much money does Liam earn in 4 weeks?
A bag contains 4 red balls, 2 black balls and 3 green balls. If a ball is drawn out of the bag at random, what is the probability that the drawn ball will be not be a black ball?
A room has 6 tables. Each table can accommodate 5 students. If 4 students leave, how many students can the room still accommodate?

An up-market coffee shop offers takeaway coffee with 3 types of coffee beans. Each cup contains an equal mixture of each type of bean. If a customer orders 27 cups of coffee, how many cups of each type of coffee bean are required?
A laptop costs $400 more than a smartphone. The smartphone costs $200. How much is the total cost of both the laptop and the smartphone?
A pizza place sells 3 sizes of pizzas. The medium pizza is 10 inches in diameter and costs $12. The large pizza is 14 inches in diameter and costs 25% more. How much does the large pizza cost?
Tim sells handmade notebooks to fund his school's art club. He buys them at $6 each and sells them at a 20% higher cost. How much will he give back to the art club by selling 150 notebooks?
A rectangle has an area of 36 square meters and a length of 9 meters. How long is the width of the rectangle?
Sam scored 15 points higher on his chemistry test than Peter. Peter scored thrice the score of Jenny who scored a 30 on her test. What was Sam's test score?

Juan is saving money for a new bicycle that costs $150. He already has $45 and plans to save $15 each week until he reaches his goal. How many weeks will it take him to have enough money to buy the bicycle?

Andrew bought 750 stickers. He shared some of his stickers with his friends, Daniel and Fred.  Daniel received 250 stickers, while Fred received 120 more stickers than Daniel.   He kept the remaining stickers. How many stickers did Andrew keep?
A train travels 200 miles at 50 miles per hour. How long will it take for the train to complete its journey?

In a bakery, Donna used 5 grams of sugar for every cookie she bakes. She baked 100 cookies for a party and still had 250 grams of sugar left. If she started with 1 kg of sugar, how many additional cookies could she bake?
Tim has a garden with 20 apple trees. Each tree produces an average of 15 apples. Tim sells the apples for $0.50 each. How much money will Tim make if he sells all the apples?

A local library receives donations of books every month. The library received 200 books in January and plans to increase the number of books it receives by 25 each month. How many total books will the library have received by the end of March if they had an initial collection of 1500 books?
Jenna baked 130 cookies. She packed them into 10 boxes, each box containing the same number of cookies. How many cookies are in each box?
A bakery makes 100 cupcakes each day. If it uses 5 chocolate chips in each cupcake and twice as many blueberries in each cupcake, how many toppings in total are used in a day?
A family has planned a road trip that takes 3 hours to complete. Halfway through the trip, they take a 20-minute break. How many minutes do they have left to complete the trip after the break?

Charlie planted carrots in his garden. He has 6 rows of carrots, and each row has 90 carrot plants. Approximately each 9 carrot plants will produce a bucket of carrots. How many buckets of carrots does Charlie get?
If one python can eat one 70-cm rabbit per month, how many pythons would it take to eat twenty 70-centimeter rabbits in four weeks?
Nick collects twice as many stamps as Lisa. Emma collects three times as many stamps as Nick. If they collected 70 stamps in total, how many stamps did Nick collect?
A store had 500 shirts in stock. 30% of the shirts were sold in the morning, and 50% of the remaining shirts were sold in the afternoon. How many shirts are left in the store?
Emma had $200. She spent one-third of her money on some books and the remaining on a handbag. How much did the handbag cost, in dollars?

Robert has a fruit farm. He packs apples in crates, each crate can hold up to 10 apples. Each harvest, half of the crates are full, and the remaining crates are three-quarters full. He gets an average of 40 crates each harvest. How many apples does Robert get in an average harvest?
John and Mary are selling lemonade. They sell one glass for $2. If they sold 30 glasses in the morning and 50 glasses in the afternoon, how much money did they make in a day?
Melanie is hosting a dinner party and needs to make spaghetti. She has 45 strands of spaghetti and serves 5 strands per portion. How many portions can she make?

Jane is making a necklace with a pattern that includes 4 blue beads, 6 yellow beads, and 3 times as many orange beads as blue beads. If the pattern repeats twice per necklace and 7 times per bracelet, how many beads does she need to make 2 necklaces and 4 bracelets?

Reggie is playing marbles with his friend. His friend arrives with 100 marbles. Each game, they bet ten marbles and whoever wins gets to keep all the marbles. After 9 games, Reggie has 90 marbles. How many games has he lost?

Raj runs every day. On weekdays he runs 5 miles, but on weekends he runs 7 miles. He has now decided to also run an extra 3 miles every Wednesday for his half marathon training. How many miles does he run in total in a week?
A shop sells 2 kinds of toys: teddy bears and dolls. Each teddy bear costs $12 and each doll costs $8. If a customer buys 6 teddy bears and 4 dolls, how much does the customer spend?

David is building a fence. Each section costs $200 and he needs 14 sections. He also needs a gate that costs $100. How much does the fence cost?
Alex has 18 comic books. He has 3 times as many comic books as Jonathan. 1/2 of Jonathan's comic books are Batman edition. How many Batman edition comic books does Jonathan have?

Emily exercises every day by jogging 6 miles carrying a 15-pound backpack, then she cycles another 15 miles without the backpack. If she jogs at a speed of 4 miles per hour and cycles at a speed of 10 miles per hour, how many hours each week does she spend exercising?

A bus travels at an average speed of 60 miles per hour. The bus is scheduled to arrive at its destination in 5 hours. How far is the destination from the departure point?
Karen plants 25 sunflowers, 34 daffodils and 23 tulips in her garden. What percentage of the flowers are sunflowers?

A library has 150 books on science. This is 10 fewer than 3 times the number of history books in the same library. How many history books are there in the library?

Tom and his mom are baking cookies for a school event. Tom made 5 batches and his mom made twice as many. How many more batches did his mom make compared to Tom?

Sara is saving for a $300 bicycle. She saves $20 each week. How many weeks will it take for Sara to save enough money for the bicycle?

The soccer team is practicing their goal kicks, kicking as many as they can in one minute. John made 16 goal kicks, and Annie made 25% more than John. If Betty made 3 fewer than Annie, how many goal kick did Betty make?

Kenny has twice as many dollars as Andy. If Andy has 10 dollars, how much money does Kenny have?

A bakery has 8 trays of cupcakes. Each tray can hold 15 cupcakes. If they sold 50 cupcakes, how many are left?
A school is filling four shuttle buses with students for a field trip. Each bus can hold 50 students. If the school has 175 students, how many more students can fit in the buses?
A movie theater has 15 rows of seats, each row has 12 seats. How many seats are there in the theater?

The price of 1 large chocolate bar is equal to the price of 20 candies. How much total money can be made from selling all the candies and chocolate bars Emma made during these 10 days?

John needs to collect 100 stamps for a project. He already has 20 dame nells, 30 thorns, and 15 roses. How many more stamps does he need to collect?

A charity event collected $150 and decided to split it equally among 5 charities. How much did each charity receive?

A toy manufacturing company facilitates the production of 4500 toys each day. One day, due to machine malfunction, they were able to produce only 3/5 of the total number. How many more toys does the company need to manufacture to meet the target?

Johnny's mom baked 3 dozen cookies. She gave Johnny's sister a dozen, and Johnny ate 4 cookies. How many cookies are left?

If a dress originally costs $80 and it's on sale for a 15% discount, how much will Emma need to buy it?

If a bus can transport 50 passengers at a time. How many round trips does it need to make to transport 350 passengers?

Emily has a garden where she plants 15 tomatoes, 9 cucumbers, and 12 carrots. Each tomato costs $1.20, each cucumber costs $0.75, and each carrot costs $0.50. If Emily spent $18 on seeds, and sells all of her vegetables, how much profit will she make?

John starts his shift at the office at 7:00 a.m. He works until 12:00 p.m., takes a lunch break until 1:00 p.m., then continues working until 6:00 p.m. How many hours does John work per day?

A train takes 2 hours to cover a distance of 120 miles. After traveling for half an hour the train stop for a 30 minute break before continuing. If the whole journey took 4 hours, how much time was spent traveling?

The capacity of Maria's fuel tank is half the size of John's fuel tank. If John's fuel tank has a capacity of 60 liters and Maria's fuel tank is full, how many liters will John's tank have if it's also only half full?
Florence has 12 trees in her orchard. Each tree produced 5 baskets of apples. How many baskets of apples did Florence collect?
Emma made 15 friendship bracelets. Kaylee made twice as many as Emma. If they want to give each of their friends 3 bracelets each, how many friends can they give bracelets to?
Jake is four years older than Lily.  When Lily is three dozen years old, how old will Jake be in years?
Bella had 48 apples. She bought 15 more from the grocery store. Her daughter ate 10 of these apples. How many apples does Bella have left?

A farm has 15 chickens. The number of cows is 3 times the number of chickens, and the number of sheep is 2 times the number of cows. How many animals are there on the farm in total?
Bill is installing a fence around his square garden. Each side of the garden is 15 feet. How many feet of fencing does he need?

Rita bought 75 packs of gum from a store. Her friend Mia also comes to the same store to buy gum. Rita added 4 more packs for every 3 packs Mia bought. If Mia purchased 30 packs, how many packs of gum did Rita have?

Emily has a goal to read 8 books in a year. She finished across 3 books in the first quarter and another 2 books in the second quarter. How many books does she still need to read in the third and fourth quarters to achieve her goal?
Linda needs to prepare for a test. The test contains 150 questions, and Linda already studied 60 of them. Each day, she plans to study 15 questions. How many days does she need to finish studying?

Lola has 36 chocolate bars. She decides to give 6 of them to her brother. She then gives four times as many to her friend. How many chocolate bars does she have left?
There were 18 biscuits in a box. Liam ate 5 biscuits. His sister Anna ate 1/3 of the remaining biscuits. How many biscuits are left in the box?

John is a painter, and he has a 100 foot ladder.  He first climbs up 50 feet to paint a window. Then he climbs down 30 feet to paint a section of the wall. After that, he climbs up 70 feet to paint the roof. At the end of the day, he climbs down 90 feet to reach the ground.  Over the course of the day, how far, in feet, had John moved up and down the ladder?
Sarah had 48 cupcakes for her birthday party. After the party, she had 14 cupcakes left. If she gave the same number of cupcakes to each of her 8 friends, how many cupcakes did each friend receive?
Liam has been delivering newspapers for 6 days. So far, he's earned $60. How many more days must Liam work before he earns three times this amount, assuming he earns the same amount every day?
There are 150 people at a business conference. If 5 people leave every hour, how many people will be left after 4 hours?
Tristan is packing books into boxes. Each box can hold 10 books. If Tristan has 95 books, how many boxes does he need?
Macy baked 12 cookies and her mom baked twice as much. If they decided to sell all the cookies and made $2 from each cookie, how much total did they make?
John can finish a project in six days, while Peter can finish the same project in 9 days. How long will it take them to finish the project if they work together?
Jenna makes three batches of muffins. Each batch has four trays, and each tray has five muffins. How many muffins does Jenna make?
A library has 360 books, and 2/3 are fiction. The library purchased another 90 books, 3/4 of which are fiction. How many books are non-fiction in total?
James sells toy cars at a price of $12 each. If he sells 18 toy cars in one day, how much total money does he make in four days?
Sarah and her friends decided to buy a gift for their teacher. The gift costs $60 and they plan to share the cost equally. If there are 12 people in total, how much does each person need to pay?
A train has 320 empty seats. 50% of the seats are filled in the first station and 30% of the remaining seats are filled in the second station. How many empty seats are left in the train?

Sammy, Mabel and Steve are collecting seashells on the beach. Sammy collected 50 seashells, Mabel collected 7 more seashells than Sammy, and Steve collected 12 fewer seashells than Mabel. How many more seashells did Mabel collect than Steve?

Ruth buys candies for her friends for Valentine's Day. She wants to buy 1 candy for each letter of her friends' names. Her friends are Sam, Lisa, and Maria. If each candy costs $1, how much will she spend in total?

In a historical reenactment, Ben plays a knight who must defeat a dragon. The dragon's health is represented by 1500 hit points. Ben's sword does 50 hit points of damage each hit. If Ben has already hit the dragon 20 times, how many more times does he need to hit the dragon to defeat it?
A bookstore received 8 boxes of books. Each box contained 12 books. After selling 30 books, how many books are left in the bookstore?
A train with 16 carriages each having 9 rows of seats with 6 seats in each row runs 3 times a day. How many passengers can the train service in a day?
Jenny bought a box of chocolates. The box contained 16 chocolates. She ate 5 chocolates and gave 3 chocolates to her friend. How many chocolates does Jenny have left?

Sadie currently is twice as old as Bella. Bella is currently 2 years less than three times as old as Jenna is now. Jenna is currently 1 year younger than Jane is now. If Jane is currently 3 years old, how old is Sadie now?
Sophia and Ethan are going to a movie. The price for each ticket is $15. Sophia is bringing 3 friends and Ethan is bringing 2 friends. How much will they spend on tickets altogether?
If Sarah reads 7 pages of her book on day one, 14 pages on day two and doubles the number of pages every following day for a week, how many pages will she have read in a week?
Wendy prepares 5 dozen cupcakes for her office party. There are 36 colleagues in her office, but only 75% like cupcakes. How many cupcakes does each colleague who likes cupcakes get to eat?

The bakery shop sells two types of muffins. Blueberry muffins are at $1.5 per muffin and Cranberry muffins are at $2 per muffin. If the shop sold 200 muffins in total and the revenue from Cranberry muffins is $150 more than from Blueberry muffins, then how many Blueberry muffins did the shop sell?
Leo bought a skateboard for $60 and sold it for 25% less. How much did Leo sell the skateboard for?

Adam needed to complete a few errands. He spent $35 at the grocery store, $45 at the pet store, and $30 at the book store. He also had to pay $15 for parking. When he finished, he had $40 left. How much money did he have to start with?

Emily sees 3 paintings in an art gallery. She knows that the painting in the middle is 120 cm wide. The painting on the left is 75% of the width of the middle painting. The painting on the right is 10 cm wider than if the paintings on the left and middle were placed side by side. How wide does Emily estimate their total width to be?
A library receives 80 books and 25 magazines per day. How many items does it receive in a year if every month has 30 days?
Dean had 5 boxes of chocolate which he divided among his 4 friends equally. If each box of chocolate contained 60 pieces, how many pieces of chocolate does each friend receive?

Jacob has a comic book collection. He has twice as many comic books as his brother. His sister has 3 times as many comic books as Jacob. How many comic books do these three siblings have if Jacob has 45 comic books?
A car journey takes triple the time by bus. If the bus journey takes 4 hours, how long does the car journey take?
Rebecca and her friends are baking cookies. It takes 2 hours to bake a batch of 20 cookies. If they need to bake three batches, how long will it take?

Ben's mom receives 30 emails a day. At the start of May, she registered for a cooking course that sent 10 more emails per day to her inbox. What's the total number of emails Ben's mom had at the end of the month?
Caroline has 180 emails in her primary inbox. She deletes 30% of them and moves 60% of the remaining emails to her social folder. How many emails are still in the primary inbox?

Clara the fairy collects 80 ruby gems, 50 emerald gems, and 40 diamond gems. If each emerald gem is valued at 10 ruby gems, and each diamond gem is valued at 2 emerald gems, what is the total value of Clara's collection when converted to ruby gems?
Jenna has 50 pencils. Each week, she loses 3 pencils. If Jenna's mom buys her 12 more pencils after 4 weeks, how many pencils will Jenna have at the end of those 4 weeks?

Marcus works at a museum and every day, he checks the number of visitors. On the first day, there were 20 visitors. The following day, there were 45 visitors. On the third and fourth day, there were 70 and 90 visitors respectively. But he had to subtract 10 visitors from the fourth day because they were his friends who came for a visit. How many visitors did he record the presence of after the fourth day?

John has 12 apples. Mike has 7 apples. They both find an apple tree and each pick 8 apples. After that, an old man takes 3 apples from each of them for helping him. How many apples do they have in total?
Emily bought 12 boxes of cookies and 3 boxes of chips. Each box of cookies cost $2 and each box of chips cost $3. How much more did Emily spend on cookies than on chips?
In a competition, Michael scored 15 points higher than Jessica. Thomas scored three times as many points as Jessica. If Thomas scored 72 points, how many less points did Jessica score than Michael?
In a forest, there are 50 animals. 10 are rabbits, twice as many are deer and the remaining animals are wolves. What is the percentage chance that a randomly selected animal will be a wolf?

In Rose's collection, there are twelve more purple beads than green beads and thrice as many green beads as orange beads. There are also six orange beads and three less red beads than orange beads. How many beads in all are there in Rose's collection?
A bird can fly at a speed of 10 kilometers per hour. If it flies constantly at that speed for 90 minutes, what distance would it cover?

Emma has an assignment to read a 890-page book over the summer. If her summer vacation is 70 days and she read 10 pages per day for the first 30 days, how many pages does she need to read per day for the remaining 40 days?
The class average on the last history test was 85%. Billy received 15% less than the class average and Bobby scored 5 points more than Billy. What score did Bobby receive on his test?

Angela is baking cookies for an event. She can bake 100 cookies in an hour. The event lasts for 3 hours and she wants to have twice the amount of cookies needed for the event for backup. How many cookies does she need to bake?
Emma knits 4 sweaters for each of her 4 grandkids. She sews 5 buttons in each sweater. How many buttons must Emma use for all the sweaters?
There are 30 fish in an aquarium. 40 percent of the fish are goldfish and 25 percent of the goldfish are females. How many female goldfish are in the aquarium?
A box contains 500 pieces of assorted candies. If 100 of the candies are chocolate and 50 of the candies are strawberry, how many candies are neither chocolate nor strawberry?
Toothbrushes cost $2.00 each at the local store, or $3.50 per pack of four online. How much would Jason save by buying 16 toothbrushes online instead of at the local store?
Sofia walked 8,000 steps on Monday and doubled the number of steps she took the next day. If she continued this pattern for a week, how many steps did she take in 7 days?

Jake works at a coffee shop. He makes $3 in tips per customer. On Monday he served 30 customers, triple of that on Tuesday, and twice as much on Wednesday. How much did Jake make in tips over those 3 days?

Farah runs a bakery. She can bake 20 cakes in a day, and each cake requires 1 cup of sugar. She buys her sugar in 10-pound bags, each of which has about 20 cups of sugar. How many 10-pound bags of sugar will she need to buy in one week to have enough for her cakes?
Bill loves to read books. He read 25% of a 400-page book in the first day, 35% in the second day, and the remaining pages in the third day. How many pages did he read on the third day?
Charlie works as a tutor. He charges $20 per hour and works 5 days in a week. If he works 4 hours each day, how much does he earn in a week?

Billy bought 75 baseball cards at the sports shop. He gave 18 cards to his brother and gave 5 more cards than he gave to his brother to his best friend. He kept the rest. How many baseball cards did Billy keep?
Lucy is saving for a new game console that costs $450. She already saved up $190 and her birthday gift was $50. How much more does she need to save to buy the game console?
In a classroom, there are 35 students. 40% of them are girls. How many boys are in the classroom?
Sammy the squirrel collects 15 acorns each day. If Sammy collects the same amount of acorns each day, how many acorns has Sammy collected after 20 days?

Kev drives at 60 mph to pick up a pizza from a restaurant that is 90 miles away. It takes 20 minutes for the pizza to be prepared while he waits, and then he drives back at the same speed. How long is the total round trip time, including waiting, in hours?
There are 8 red apples, 6 green apples and 4 yellow apples in a basket. If Sally took 2 red apples, 2 green apples and 3 yellow apples from the basket, how many apples are left in the basket?
Liam has 5 times as many baseball cards as Noah. If Noah has 30 baseball cards, how many baseball cards does Liam have?

There are 132 international students coming to study in a university. The university has chartered busses that each seat 33 students. How many busses does the university need to transport all the students?

Amy is in a cycling group with 3 other people.  The riders all bike 10 miles together.  On top of that, Amy bikes an additional 5 miles per day for 5 days while her friend Ben bikes a third of that amount in 5 days.  If they bike this same route 5 days a week, how many miles do they bike in total?
A classroom contains 25 chairs. If the number of chairs is reduced by 20% after renovations, how many chairs are left in the classroom?
Liam has decided to start jogging to stay fit. He jogged 8 miles on the first day and planned to jog twice as much the next day. How many miles did he jog in total in two days?

A 150-page novel has 10 pages of dedication and acknowledgment at the beginning. Half of the remaining pages are the actual story and the rest are sketches and character profiles. How many pages of sketches and character profiles are there?
