Maria has a bag full of candy. She gives half of her candy to her sister and 30% of the remaining candy to her friend. If there are 28 pieces of candy left in her bag, how many pieces did she originally have?
A bakery sold 100 cupcakes in the morning. In the afternoon, they sold 25% less than in the morning. How many cupcakes did they sell in a day?

A small pond has a rectangular shape measuring 5 meters wide. Its length is 3 meters more than its width. Park management wants to surround the pond with metal fence. How many meters of fence are they going to need?

It's the winter season in Boston, and it snowed 3 inches every hour for 5 hours on Tuesday. On Wednesday, it snowed 2 inches every hour for 4 hours, and on Thursday, it snowed for 3 hours at half the rate of the previous day. How much snowfall, in inches, was recorded for these three days?
In the school cafeteria, there are 32 tables and each table can sit 8 students. If only 3/4 of the total seats are occupied, how many students are sitting down?
If a train travels at a speed of 50 miles per hour for 4 hours, how many miles does the train cover?
Jackie and her friends plan to pool in money to buy a gift for their teacher. If they need $80 for the gift and Jackie contributes 1/4 of the total amount, how much does Jackie contribute?

In a school, the rate of students attending music classes is 1 out of 5, while the rate of students attending sports classes is 1 out of 3. If there are a total of 375 students in the school, how many more students attend sports classes than music classes?
A tray can accommodate 9 muffins. If a baker has baked 234 muffins, how many trays does he need?
Two schools X and Y merge. School X gets 75% of the total funds and school Y gets the remaining 25%. If school Y gets $15000, how much is school X getting?
Jessie made 5 cakes each containing 4 slices. Jason and his friends ate 10 slices, how many slices did Jessie have left?
Mary spends 2 hours a day walking. She spends half of that time jogging. How many hours a week does she spend jogging?
A snake's head is one-tenth its length. If a snake is 10 feet long, calculate the length of the rest of its body minus the head.

Mr. Miller makes toys for children in the hospital. Every day from Monday to Friday he makes 5 toys. On Saturday and Sunday, he makes double the amount he makes on weekdays. He later distributes them equally among 7 hospitals. How many toys did Mr. Miller deliver to each hospital?

12 friends decided to buy a boat together. The boat costs $24,000. If 4 of the friends can only afford to pay 2/3 of what the others are paying, how much will each of the remaining friends need to pay?

A local hardware store stocks 15 different types of tools. Each set of tools has 30 hammers, 40 screwdrivers, and 20 wrenches. The hammers cost $15 each. The screwdrivers cost twice as much as the hammers and the wrenches are half as expensive as the screwdrivers. How much does it cost for the store to restock all the tools?
Karen's father gave her $100 for her birthday. She bought 5 novels and 3 cd's. Each novel costs $6 and each cd costs $10. How much money does Karen have left?
Megan has a bee farm where she has 3 kinds of bees. Each hive has 5 bees of each kind. If she loses 10 bees, how many bees does she have left?

Anna and Bella are saving money for a trip. They both save $30 each week. By the end of the month, their parents also give them $100 each. How much money do they have together at the end of the month?
Tony’s orchard yielded 12 pounds of apples per tree. He harvested apples from a total of 25 trees on Monday and 40% more than that on Tuesday. How many pounds of apples did Tony harvest?
Emmy and Lena are best friends. If Emmy's age is 4 years less than twice Lena's age, and the sum of their ages is 26, how old is Lena?

Lily spent 1/3 of her money and an additional $15 on some clothes. She then spent 1/4 of the remaining money and an additional $5 on some accessories. If she was left with $100, how much money did she have at first?
Lisa baked 11 dozen cupcakes for a bake sale. Over the course of the sale, half of the cupcakes were sold, and 7 were dropped on the ground. How many cupcakes remain?
Jack's garden is 8 feet wide. It's 3 times longer than it is wide. How long is Jack's garden?
Sam collects stamps. He has 200 stamps from America, twice as many from Europe, and half as many from Asia. How many stamps does he have in total?

There are two types of butterflies in a field. One is black and the other is yellow. For every 4 black butterflies, there is one yellow butterfly. If there are 20 butterflies in the field, how many yellow butterflies are there?
Emily is packing a box of old books to donate. She places 12 books in the box and realizes she still has 3 times the number of books she packed left to box. How many books did Emily have altogether?

Julia is making lasagna. It takes her 15 minutes to boil the pasta and half of that time to prepare the meat. It takes her 25 minutes to mix the sauce, three times as long to bake the lasagna, and 1/5th the combined boiling, preparation, mixing, and baking time to serve the lasagna. How many minutes does Julia spend on the lasagna in total?

Leona threw a birthday party and invited 35 friends. She ordered 5 pieces of fried chicken per person. After the party, there were exactly 3 pieces of chicken left over. How many pieces of chicken did Leona's friends eat?
At Jerod's Grocery Store, oranges cost 30 dollars for a dozen, and berries cost 60 dollars for a dozen. If Dawn bought 20 dozen of each kind of fruit, how many dollars did she spend?
Mike has 4 dozen eggs. He uses 8 eggs to make a cake and 12 eggs to make an omelet. How many eggs does Mike have left?
Becky paid $1200 for a drawer, a bed, and a mattress. The bed cost $200 more than the drawer, and the mattress cost $400 less than the drawer. How much did the drawer cost?
In a class of 60 students, 12% are left-handed. How many students are right-handed?
Eva is planning to buy 4 boxes of pencils and each box costs $5. She also wants to buy 3 notebooks where each costs $7. How much will Eve spend in total?
In a room, there are 4 green chairs, 6 red chairs and 3 blue chairs. How many chairs are there in total in the room?
Emma had 90 marbles her grandmother gave her. She gave a third of them to her brother and another quarter of them to her sister. How many marbles does Emma have left?
Emma is planning a road trip. If she drives at an average speed of 60 miles per hour and her destination is 480 miles away, how long will she take to reach her destination?

Jill owns a cafe where she sells 2 types of pies sliced into 6 pieces: apple and cherry. Apple pie slices are $4 each while cherry pie slices are $5 each. If Jill sells all slices of 3 apple pies and 4 cherry pies, how much money does she earn in total?

Jon needs 4 glasses of wood to repair his house. Window frames make 5 glasses of wood, doors make 7 glasses of wood, and cabinets make 3 glasses of wood. If Jon has 10 window frames, 5 doors, and 3 cabinets, how many glasses of wood does he have?
A pizza parlor has 8 slices per pizza. If a family of 4 people each eat 2 slices of pizza, how many pizzas did they completely eat?
A bookstore owner has 450 books. He sold 35 books to Jenny and 20 to Charles. How many books does the bookstore owner have left?
Lydia's flower shop sold 15 bouquets at $25 each, 10 bouquets at $20 each, and 5 bouquets at $30 each. In total, how much money did her flower shop make?

Alex has a storage capacity of 120 GB on her computer. She already filled 45 GB of the storage. If each movie she wants to download takes up approximately 2 GB of storage, how many movies can Alex download onto her computer?

Kevin is a vegetable distributor. He purchases cucumbers from farmers for $15 per crate, and sells them to markets for $0.50 each. If each crate contains 30 cucumbers, how much profit, in dollars, does he make after selling 120 cucumbers?
Sandra has twice the number of cookie jars as Melissa. If Sandra has 12 cookie jars, how many cookie jars do they have in total?

To make a chocolate cake, Henry needs 2 kg of chocolate. There are 10 chocolate bars in one kg of chocolate. It takes 5 minutes to melt 1 chocolate bar. How many hours will it take Henry to melt all the chocolate?

Adam and Bryan went to the mall to buy video games and comic books. Adam bought twice the number of video games and comic books Bryan bought. If Adam bought 16 video games and 24 comic books, how many video games and comic books did Bryan buy in total?
Julie earns $20 per hour. She gets a raise of $3 per hour. If she works 35 hours per week, how much does she earn in a week after the raise?
Jack goes up three flights of stairs, then down six flights of stairs. If each flight of stairs has 12 steps that each each 8 inches high, how many feet further down is Jack than when he started?
A classroom has 18 desks. If each desk seats 2 students, how many students can the classroom accommodate if 2 desks are removed for a handicapped student?
Mary goes to a supermarket to buy oranges. She notices that 1 orange costs $0.8. If she buys 15 oranges and pays with a $20 bill, how much change does she get back?
Due to a bug infestation, 35% of the apple trees in an orchard died. The 252 trees that are left make up the remaining 65% of the trees. How many apple trees were in the orchard originally?
John babysits for $10 an hour. One week, he works 25 hours; the following week, he works for 15 hours. How much does he make in total?
Enrico owns a rooster farm, he sells each rooster for $0.50 per kilogram. He was able to sell a 30-kilogram rooster and a 40-kilogram rooster, how much money was he able to earn?
Nancy's mother has ten apple trees in her backyard. Each tree produces 150 apples and Nancy picks 1/3 of the apples from each tree. How many apples are left on the trees?

An ancient knight wants to bury his treasure of 5000 rubies and equally distribute it in 4 locations. He also decides to add 250 sapphires and as many emeralds as twice the number of sapphires in each location equally. How many gems will there be in each location?
Greg's garden has twice as many tomato plants as pepper plants. If he has 8 pepper plants, and each plant yields 5 vegetables, how many vegetables can Greg expect to harvest from his garden?
Thomas is ordering pizza for a party. He orders 10 pepperoni pizzas that cost $12 each and 5 cheese pizzas that cost $10 each. How much does Thomas spend on pizza?
A car rental agency charges $40 per day for a small car and $60 per day for a large car. If the agency rented out 5 small cars and 3 large cars for a day, how much did they earn?

Sarah, Ava, and Lily have decided to make cookies for a charity event. Sarah made 56 cookies, Ava made twice as many as Sarah, and Lily made half as many as Ava. How many cookies did the girls make in total?

Kim received 10 candies from her mom and an additional 15 candies from her dad, while her brother William received 3 times as many candies as Kim. If Kim and William ate 40% of all their candies, how many candies are left?
A basketball player scores 12 points in each game. After 7 games, he has scored a total of 84 points. How many more games does the player need to play to reach 120 points?
Lucy spent $12 on lunch and $18 on a shirt. The cost of her dinner is two times the amount she spent on lunch. How much money does Lucy need to pay for her lunch, shirt, and dinner?
Jessica and Lily were arranging books on a bookshelf. Jessica arranged 70 books and Lily arranged twice as many as Jessica. How many books did they arrange in total?
Ellis loves jogging. If he runs 5 miles every morning and 4 miles every evening, how many miles does he run in a week?
Billy has a goldfish aquarium with 8 orange goldfish and 6 black goldfish. If he adds 5 more goldfish of undetermined color, what percentage of the aquarium's goldfish are black?
Bella is planning a big dinner. She buys 4 pounds of potatoes, 3 pounds of chicken, 2 pounds of pasta, and already had 1 pound of green beans at home. How many pounds of food does she now have?
Ben's hockey team is 60% boys and the rest are girls. Half the girls are juniors and the other half are seniors. If the team has 50 players, how many junior girls are there?

Lucy buys a pair of shoes for $75 and a purse for $45. She hands the cashier $150. If the cashier has only 2 five-dollar bills and a bunch of one-dollar bills, how many one-dollar bills does Lucy get back as her change?

Margo and Steve enjoy bird watching. Every time they go bird watching, Margo spots 3 fewer than seven birds, and Steve spots two fewer birds than Margo. If Margo and Steve go bird watching 10 times, how many birds do they spot in total?

Sandra had 2 different bags of candy.  Each of her bags had 6 pieces of candy left.  Her brother, Roger, also had 2 bags of candy.  One of his bags of candy had 11 pieces left and the other had 3 pieces left.  How much more candy did Roger have?

Two trains leave a station with 70% and 80% of capacity full, respectively. If the people in the trains are all students of a high school, and the trains have a capacity of 250, calculate the total number of students in the two trains combined?
Jessica needs 7 yards of fabric to make one curtain. If she plans to make 4 curtains, how many yards of fabric does she need in total?
A restaurant made 120 pizzas. 25% of them have pepperoni and 35% of them have mushroom. The rest of the pizzas are margherita. How many pizzas are margherita?
Anna reads 5 books each month. For each book, she writes two pages of book reports. How many pages of book reports does Anna write in a year?
Rachel plants 20 trees on her farm and plans on planting 5 trees every day for the next week. How many trees will she have after a week?

Richard has only gone snorkeling 3 times in his life. Carmen has gone on 5 times as many snorkeling trips as Richard, and Valeria has gone on 10 more snorkeling trips than Carmen. If Richard wants to go snorkeling as many times as Valeria and plans to go on 2 snorkeling trips a month, how many months would it take Richard to achieve his goal?

The price of a book is $12 for a copy. During one day, the book store sells 30 books. The cost of maintaining the bookstore each day is $50. Supposing the bookstore sold all their books during the day, how much money did it earn?
Emily runs a dog walking business. She charges $10 for each dog walk. If she walks 5 dogs every day for a week, how much does she earn in a week?

Mike buys 60-pound bags of chicken feed for $15 each. His chicken coop holds 20 pounds of feed, and he refills the coop every week. How much will it cost, in dollars, for him to buy enough chicken feed to last 300 days?
Robert has $30. He spends $4.50 on a sandwich and another $1.75 on a drink. How much money, in dollars, does Robert have left?
Jim prepares 4 tuna sandwiches for a picnic. If each sandwich needs 2 slices of bread, how many slices does Jim need?
If Charlotte drinks 8 glasses of water a day and each glass is 250 ml, how much water will she drink in a week?

Sarah is planning to host a dinner party for 15 people. Each person will eat 2 bread rolls. A package of bread rolls contains 10 pieces and costs $3.50. How much will Sarah spend on bread rolls for the dinner party?

To build a model plane, Tom needs 10 medium parts and 8 small parts. Each medium part is constructed from 3 large pieces and each small part from 2 large pieces. If Tom wants to build 5 model planes, how many large pieces does he need in total?
Kyle bought 10 books that cost $15 each. He also bought a pricey hardcover novel that cost twice as much as the total cost of the 10 books. If he paid with a $200 bill, how much change will he get?
Amy has 5 dollars more than twice as much money as Bob. Helen has 2 times as much money as Amy. If Bob has 10 dollars, how much money do Helen and Amy have?
Jacob went shopping for clothes. His total shopping was worth $250. He used a discount coupon of 20%. How much discount did he get and what was his final bill?
A theater has 20 rows of seats. Each row has 15 seats. The theater sells tickets for each show for $10. How much will the theater earn if all seats are occupied in one show?
Fred had 50 apples. He gave twice as many apples to Sarah as he gave to Tom, who received 10 apples. How many apples does Fred have left?
Timmy cut a 2-foot long rope into 6 equal lengths. How long is each piece?
If the price of 5 kilograms of apples is $15, how much would 12 kilograms of apples cost at the same rate?

Gilbert's cat got himself dirty playing in the garden. He spent 12 minutes washing him off with the hose, then gave him a bath twice, which took 20 minutes each. What was the total time he spent cleaning his cat in minutes?
A school canteen sell 5 cupcakes for $2 each and 3 cookies for $1.5 each. If the school sells out all cupcakes and cookies, how much do they earn?
Emily is buying books for her new bookstore. Each book costs $15. If Emily has $450 available to spend, how many books can she buy?

A school nurse has to provide vitamin syrup to students and the recommended dosage is 10 ml per student. If the school has 50 students and the nurse has to give the dosage in 2 equal parts. How many ml will each part of the dosage be?
Chris bought 4 comic books and 2 action figures. Each comic book cost twice as much as an action figure. If Chris spent $36 in total, how much did a single action figure cost in dollars?
Lucy has 5850 pages to read this week, and she wants to distribute them evenly over 6 days. How many pages will she read per day?

Lara wants to buy a bike. The cost of the bike is $1200, but it is advertised with a 30% off discount. After buying the bike, Lara decided to buy some accessories which cost her $100. How much did Lara spend in total?
Cathy bought 6 packets of cookies. Each packet contained 18 cookies. If Julie bought two packets less than thrice as many packets of cookies as Cathy, how many cookies did they buy altogether?
Michael's salary is 3/4 of what it used to be and Chris's salary is now three times what Michael used to earn. How much do they earn combined if Michael used to earn $8000?
Gianna buys a dress for $80 and shoes for $50. She gets a discount of 15% off the total price. How much does she have to pay?
Samuel has $100. He spends $20 on a book and $30 on a shirt. How much money in dollars does Samuel have left?
If Emily reads 2 books a week, how many books does she read in a year?

Anna is planning to read a book of 120 pages. She decides to read 1/4 of the book on the first day and 1/3 of the remaining pages on the second day. If she reads the remaining pages equally over the next three days, how many pages does she need to read each day?
A factory produces 500 smartphones per day. After increasing production by 20%, it is estimated that sales will triple. How many smartphones are expected to be sold after the increase in production?
James bought 4 packs of cookies. Each pack has 10 cookies. He ate 2 cookies and gave half of what is left to his brother. How many cookies does James have left?
Tommy sneezes 3 times every 5 minutes. His sister, Susie, also has a cold, but she sneezes twice as much as him. After 30 minutes, how many times have they sneezed?
Rose plants 15 flowers every week in her garden. If the gardening season is 12 weeks, how many flowers has Rose planted by the end of the season?

Ellen has 200 inches of wire.  She cuts the wire in half, and then takes one of the halves and cuts it in half again.  She grabs one of the remaining pieces and cuts it into fourths.  She's holding one length of the wire she's most recently cut.  How long is it?

A box of candies contains 300 candies. Paul and his 5 friends take 1/3 of the candies and divide it among themselves. One of Paul's friends doesn't like candies and returns 10 of his candies to the box. Later, his brother Jack takes 10 fewer candies than those taken by Paul and his friends. How many candies are left in the box?
A soldier is marching straight from his tent to his training ground. If he has to take 8 steps to cover 6 meters, how many steps will he need to take to cover 540 meters?
John mows lawns for $20 each. If he mows 12 lawns per week, how much will he have in 3 weeks?

Steve wants to buy a motorbike. The motorbike costs $20,000, and he can pay a down payment of $5,000. He'll have to take a loan for the remaining amount and make 36 equal monthly payments. Each month, he also has to pay interest equal to 3% of that month's payment. How much will Steve's monthly payments be?

An office requires 18 liters of water every day. If the office orders water in packs where each pack contains 36 liters of water, how many days will a pack of water last? Later, the number of people in the office increases by 50% and now the office requires 50% more water. How many days will a pack of water last now?
Samantha can complete a piece of artwork in 10 hours. If she worked continuously for 8 days at the same rate, how many pieces of artwork did she complete?

Vicky decides to start painting. She paints and frames 15 pictures. It takes her 3 hours to paint each picture and 25% less time to frame each picture. How much time does she spend on all the pictures?
Jane lent Tom one-third of her fountain pens. If she originally had 96 pens, how many pens does Tom have now?
Andre has read 72, 80 and 95 pages of a book on Sunday, Monday, and Tuesday, respectively. How many pages does Andre need to read on Wednesday to finish the book if it has 400 pages?

A company decided to give a special bonus to all its employees who are fathers. This bonus will be 10% of the company's profit for the year, which was $1,000,000. It is known that the company has 5000 employees in total; one-half of them are women, and of the men, 1500 are not fathers. How much was the bonus that each male father employee received, if the bonus was divided equally among them?
An exam has a total score of 100. If a student scores 25, what percentage did the student achieve?

There are 3 times as many chairs as tables at a restaurant. If there are currently 270 chairs at the restaurant, and the management decides to purchase 20 more tables and 60 more chairs, how many pieces of furniture are present at the restaurant?

In a basketball tournament, Team A scored 75 points and Team B scored 68 points. Team C scored double what Team A scored while Team D scored 20 points less than the total of Team A and C. What's the total score of all teams?

Jack is selling fruit. For every 5 apples he sells, he gives 1 orange for free. And for every 4 bananas he sells, he gives 1 pear for free. If Jack sells 15 apples and 16 bananas, how many pieces of fruit will the buyers take home?
If Riley reads 90 pages a day and Jason reads 40 pages a day, how many more pages does Riley read in 5 weeks?
A garden has 30 apple trees and 25 orange trees. If each apple tree yields 12 apples and each orange tree yields 8 oranges, how many fruits in total were produced?
Peter received $500 for his birthday. He spent 1/5 of his money on books and then gave 2/3 of the remaining amount to his sister. How much money does Peter have left?
Michael bought 3 sets of pencils. Each set contained 12 pencils. If Katie bought twice as many sets of pencils as Michael, how many pencils did they buy altogether?
Mike is cooking for a dinner party. If he wants to serve each of his 12 guests 3 jumbo shrimp and each jumbo shrimp costs $2.50, how much will Mike spend on jumbo shrimp?
Justin read 12 books this month and Aidan read 15 books. If Stacey read three times as many as Justin and Aidan's combined reads, how many more books than Justin did Stacey read?
Emily is saving money for a pair of shoes that costs $50. She saves $5 every week from her allowance. How many weeks does she need to save for to buy the shoes?

A runner has to complete a marathon of 42 kilometers within 6 hours. He has already run for 3 hours at a pace of 6 kilometers per hour. What should be his pace for the rest of the marathon to meet his target?
A movie theater can seat 120 people. The first showing of a new movie sells out, and the second showing sells 75% of the seats. How many tickets were sold in total?
Ana biked 45 miles in 3 hours. At the same speed, how far can she bike in 5 hours?
Janet spent 45 minutes doing math homework and another 15 minutes doing reading homework. If Janet does this every day of the week, how many minutes does she spend on homework per week?

Rachel breeds rabbits, and her adult rabbits recently had four litters of 6 each. She gave a fifth of the baby rabbits to her neighbor Jessica to keep as pets. She sold twice the number of babies she gave Jessica to a pet store. Three-quarters of the remaining rabbits were sold to a local farm. How many baby rabbits did Rachel have left?
Rachel purchased pens for her class of 24 students. Each pen cost $3. She had a coupon for $10 off her total. How much did Rachel pay for the pens?

Melissa sold triple the amount of candles on Monday as she did on Sunday, and she sold double the amount on Sunday as she did on Saturday. If she sold 900 candles on Saturday, how many candles did she sell on Monday?
Daniel runs a laundry business. He charges $4.00 to wash a shirt and $2.50 to wash a pair of socks. One day, he washed 10 shirts and 8 pairs of socks. How much money did he make?
Mary bakes 4 batches of cookies every day. Each batch contains 8 cookies. How many cookies does she bake in two weeks?

Lucy is filling her swimming pool during a water shortage. The pool can hold 1000 gallons of water. Lucy's hose can normally pump 10 gallons/minute, but due to water restrictions, it can only pump half as fast right now. How many minutes will it take Lucy to fill her pool?

Kayla and Michael decided to race each other to see who could finish a 2-mile jog first. Kayla jogs the first mile in 8 minutes, and the next half mile in 3 minutes. Michael finishes the jog in 15 minutes. Kayla finishes the jog 2 minutes before Michael. How long, in minutes, did it take for Kayla to jog the remaining half mile?
Anna empties her purse and finds three $1 bills, a $10 bill, 10 nickels, and 30 pennies. How much money does Anna have?

Jenny has a collection of gemstones. She has 5 diamonds, 7 rubies and 4 sapphires. If each diamond has 50 facets, each ruby has 60 facets and each sapphire has 45 facets, how many facets are there in total?
Alexis bought a set of books for $70.00, a binder for $10.00 and a notebook for $5.00. She also decided to buy a pack of 5 pens for $1.00 each. How much did Alexis spend in total?
In a company, Sally works for 25 hours a week, Matt for 35 hours, and Linda for 30 hours. If the employees are paid $15 per hour, how much money does the company spend on salaries every month?
Federico runs four kilometers every day for a week. In total, how far does Federico run in a week?
Julia saves $10 a week. She wants to buy a pair of boots for $120. How many weeks does she need to save for the boots?
Mrs. Lee has 10 more cakes than Mrs. Kim. Mrs. Kim has 5 less cakes than Mrs. Park. If Mrs. Park has 15 cakes, how many cakes do the three have in total?
The total cost of a pair of shoes and a tie is $240. If the cost of the shoes is $180 more than the tie, how much does the tie cost?
Hannah sells books in packs and singles. Each pack contains 5 books. Each single is just 1 book. How many more books are in 6 packs than there are in 4 singles?
Paula buys 4 pencils and 3 erasers. Each pencil cost $0.75 and eraser cost $1. If she gives a $10 bill, how much change will she get back?
A family planted 3 types of trees. They planted 15 apple trees, 12 orange trees, and 8 pear trees. How many trees did they plant in total?
John's shirt cost 60% more than his pants.  His pants cost $50.  How much was John's outfit?
Peter mows lawn for $20 per hour and cleans pool for $30 per hour. If Peter mows for 4 hours and cleans 2 pools, how much will he earn in total?
Emily visited the school library sale where books were sold for $4. She bought 6 books and also a pencil box for $10. If she had $50 initially, how much money does she have left after her purchase?
A construction worker needs to lay 150 bricks to build a small wall. If he lays down 5 bricks every 10 minutes, how long will it take him in hours to finish the wall?
A bookstore has a sale with a discount of 20% applicable to all books. If a book originally costs $80, how much is the book after the discount?
Mike has 4 times as many stickers as Mark. Mark has twice as many stickers as their friend, Martha. If Martha has 64 stickers, how many stickers does Mike have?
In a chess club, the number of boys is twice the number of girls. If there are 18 girls, how many children are there in total in the club?
John had saved $500. He spent 15% of this amount on a video game, and then 35% of what was left on a new book. How much money does John have left now?
Bob is three times as old as Alice. Alice is 15 years old. Bob's father is 35 years older than Bob. How old is Bob's father?

Lucy finds a bag of marbles.  She finds 6 blue marbles, 4 red marbles, and 1 white marble. Her brother gives her another 5 marbles, doubling the amount she originally had. How many marbles does Lucy have in total?
If one pizza provides 8 slices and you're throwing a party for 20 people where each person will eat 2 slices of pizza, how many pizzas do you need to order?
Dave’s pizza place offers 2 slices of pizza for $3. If he buys 6 slices, how much will he spend?
A theme park parking lot can hold up to 500 cars. At noon, the parking lot is 60% full. By 3 p.m., another 120 cars arrived. How many more cars can the parking lot accommodate?
Steve can run 20 km per hour and Rose can run 3 km less than Steve. If they both run for 5 hours, how many kilometers can they run in total?
In a book, there are averagely 250 words in a page. If the book has 30 pages, how many words does the book have?

Joshua enjoys volunteering at the pet shelter. He takes care of 150 cats and dogs. By lunchtime, a certain number of animals had been adopted. He received 70 more animals after lunch. By evening, 50 more animals were adopted. If the shelter had 90 animals remaining by the evening, how many animals had been adopted by lunchtime?
Ramon is saving money for a video game. He saves $5 per week. If the video game costs $50, how many weeks will it take for Ramon to save enough money?

Jack charges $17.00 per person when catering a lunch event. He offers a 15% discount for all senior citizens. Anna, a senior citizen, is hosting a lunch for 15 friends. How much will Anna have to pay?

A baker baked 16 trays of muffins, with each tray holding 13 muffins. After selling each muffin for $2, the baker spent 15% of the earnings on new baking equipment.  How much did he spend on the equipment?
Peter drives his car at a constant speed of 70km/h. He drives for 2 hours every day. How much distance will he have travelled after one week?
Rose plants 30 roses in her garden. Each day she waters 5 roses. If she waters her garden every day, how long will it take her to water all the roses?

Ryan, Maria and Tim decided to donate coins to a local charity. Ryan donated 250 coins, Maria donated half the amount Ryan did and Tim donated one-fourth the amount Maria did. What was the total number of coins donated by all three?
A trucking company has 25 employees. Each truck requires 2 employees to operate. If the company wants to operate at full capacity, how many trucks can they operate?
Benjamin reads 5 pages of a book per day. The book he is currently reading has 250 pages. How many days will it take to finish reading this book?
Lucy spends 30% of her after-tax income on her mortgage. If her mortgage payment is $500 and she pays 1/4 of her gross income in taxes, how much does she make per month before taxes?
There are 5 times more roses than daisies in a garden, and there are twice as many tulips as daisies. If there are 16 daisies, how many flowers are there in total?

Amy opened a lemonade stand for a charity event. Every gallon of lemonade landed her 20 glasses. It cost her $2.00 to make every gallon of lemonade. She made 3 gallons to sell and decided to sell each glass for $1.50. She gave away 3 glasses for free.  She ended with 8 glasses of lemonade. How much net profit did Amy earn?
At the beginning of the month, Sarah had $700 in her bank account. After spending 25% of her money on rent, how much money does she have left?
There are 25 more puppies than three times the number of kittens at the pet shop. If there are 15 kittens at the shop, how many puppies are there?

Zara goes through jogging shoes every 60 days. Each pair of her favorite brand of jogging shoes costs $45. If she wants to continue jogging every day for the next 2 years, how much will she need to invest in shoes?

John and Peter are building a wall from bricks. John can lay four bricks per round, and Peter can lay two bricks per round. If they need 60 bricks to build the wall, how many rounds will it take to build the wall?

In a game, James scored 12 points in the first round, 15 points in the second round, and in the third round he scored half as many points as he did in the second round. How many points did he score in total?

In a typing competition, George typed 75 words per minute. The competition lasted for 2 hours. If each participant has to pause for 15 seconds every 5 minutes, how many words did George type in the competition?
Emma sells 40 handmade soaps for $5 each. The materials cost her $50. How much does she make in profit?

For the entire academic year, Peter's mom attended 80% of his basketball games. On the following academic year, she attended 5 less games than what she had attended on the previous year. If Peter had 25 basketball games each academic year, how many games did his mom attend on Peter's second academic year?
Jessica has a bison and a sloth. The bison weighs 2 tons (a ton is 2000 pounds). The sloth weighs 80% less. What is their combined weight in pounds?

Cory's school is hosting a festival with 15 more female than male teachers, and the number of students is triple the total number of teachers. If there are 25 male teachers, how many people are in the festival?
Chris has a stamp collection with 400 stamps. Mary has twice as many stamps as Chris. Sarah has half as many stamps as Mary. How many stamps do they have altogether?
A movie theater sells tickets at $8 each. If 75 students bought tickets for a school trip, and their teacher got a ticket for free, how much are the total earnings of the movie theater?
Oliver, Hector, and Molly have a collection of 230 baseball cards. Oliver has twice as many cards as Hector. If Hector has 50 cards, how many baseball cards does Molly have?
Samantha goes shopping for fruits. She buys 5 apples and 7 bananas. Each apple costs $2 and each banana cost $1. How much did Samantha spend?
John's car has a fuel capacity of 15 gallons. If he has already filled up 8 gallons and he also added 4 gallons more, how many more gallons does he need to fill it up completely?
Colby is throwing a party and wants to buy pizza. He has $90 and each pizza costs $15. He also wants to buy soda which costs $10. How many pizzas can he buy after buying soda?
Sonia bought 4 packets of stickers. Each packet included 15 stickers. She gave some stickers to her friend and still had 42 stickers left. How many stickers did Sonia give to her friend?

Fred sells oranges and apples on a stall. He sells oranges for $1 each and apples for $2. He started the day with 50 oranges and 40 apples. He accidentally squeezed 5 oranges and 4 apples were over-ripe. At the end of the day he had 15 oranges and 10 apples left. How much money did he make?
Julie was having breakfast. She had 2 slices of bread, 6 eggs, and later on she ate 3 apples. How much food did Julie eat?
If Carl, who is 3 times the height of Andrew, is 180 cm tall, how tall is Andrew?
Tom and Jerry were playing a card game. Tom won 3 times and Jerry won two times more than Tom. How many games did they play in total?
Anna, Lisa, and Tina run a bakery. The bakery sells cupcakes for $2 each. If they sold 150 cupcakes in one day, how much money did they make?

Jessica is baking cookies for a school bake sale. Each batch of cookies uses 2 cups of flour. If Jessica only has 10 cups of flour left and wants to use all of it, how many batches of cookies can she make?

Beetles are Tommy's tarantula's favorite food. The tarantula eats 3 beetles every day. In the garden, Tommy collects beetles. In the morning he finds 7 beetles, and in the afternoon he finds 5 more, but some fly away so there are 11 left. He wants to collect a whole week's food. How many beetles does he need?

Pablo has a music collection. He has 10 CDs of rock music, 15 CDs of classical music and 25 CDs of pop music. He has 5 more jazz CDs than rock CDs and 10 less country CDs than pop CDs. How many CDs does Pablo have in his whole collection?

Sarah and James are starting a business together. Sarah invested $7000 and James invested $3000 more than Sarah. If they made a profit of 30 percent of the total amount and split the profits equally, how much did each receive?
Erin has 12 boxes of pens. Each box has 5 pens. She gives 3 boxes to her friend, and the rest of the boxes she donates to school. How many pens does she donate to school?

Liam has a budget of $15000 to furnish his new apartment. He bought a sofa set for half his budget. He further spent $2000 on a dining set. He decided to keep 1/5 of his remaining budget for miscellaneous expenses. How much does Liam have left for miscellaneous expenses?
Alex has 15 stamps, while Isaac has twice as many stamps as Alex. How many fewer stamps does Alex have than Isaac?
Lola is 8 years old. Her older sister, Hanna, is 10 years older than her and 3 years younger than their older brother, Andy. How old will Andy be by the time Lola fills his present age?

A bakery prepares 15 loaves of bread every morning. Out of each loaf, 1/3 is sold as whole loaves, 2/5 is sold as half loaves, and the rest is used for sandwiches. How many loaves are used for sandwiches?

Anna, Bob, Carl, and Diego are on a movie marathon. Anna's favorite movie lasts 2 hours longer than Bob's. Carl's movie is twice as long as Bob's movie, and Diego's movie is 3/4 times as long as Carl's. If Carl's movie lasts 24 hours, how many total hours do all their movies last put together?
Anna and her three friends decided to rent a cabin for the weekend. The cabin rental costs $400. If they decide to split the cost evenly, how much does each person have to pay?
James observed the birds in the park. 50% are sparrows, 30% are pigeons and the rest are crows. If there are 30 sparrows, how many crows are there in the park?
Liam has a farm where he grows corn. He grew 2000 pounds of corn and he sold 75% of it. How many pounds of corn does he have left?

There are three statues in the city square. The first statue has twice as many pigeons as the second statue. If there are 40 pigeons on the first statue, how many pigeons are there on all three statues combined?

There are 80 people at a concert. The band starts playing and 30 people get up to dance. After 10 minutes, 20 more people join in the dancing, and 10 people stop dancing. How many people are dancing after 10 minutes?
The library has 200 books. During a book drive, they received additional 150 books. After a week, 50 books were borrowed. At the end of the month, how many books are there in the library?
Jeff runs 4 miles every day from Monday to Friday and 2 miles on each day of the weekend. How many miles does he run in a week?
Samantha is participating in a readathon. She reads for 2 hours a day from Monday to Friday and 3 hours a day on Saturday and Sunday. How many hours does Samantha spend reading in one week?
In a school's athletic meet, each student from a team needs to run 200 meters. If the team is made up of 10 students, how many meters in total does the team need to run?

Austin wants to build a swimming pool that holds 80 gallons of water. Each bag of cement can build 1 square foot. (There are 6 square feet per gallon). If each bag of cement costs $20, but Austin gets a 15% volume discount, how much does he spend on cement?
Emma baked 42 cupcakes for a bake sale. She sold 75% of them. On the second day, she baked another 28 cupcakes and sold 60% of them. How many cupcakes did Emma sell in total?
Based on a survey, children 7-12 years old spend 60 minutes each day playing video games. How many hours do these children play video games in a week if they are allowed to play 5 days a week?
Susan is training for a marathon. She runs 10 miles each day. How many miles does she run in two weeks (consider a week as 7 days)?

In a marathon, Maya ran a 42-kilometer track. For the first 10 kilometers, her speed was 15 kilometers per hour. For the next 20 kilometers, her speed was 10 kilometers per hour. For the remaining 12 kilometers, her speed was half as fast as her speed on the first 10 kilometers. What was Maya's average speed for the entire marathon?
A movie theater has 10 rows of seats. Each row has 12 seats. How many seats does the theater have in total?
Emily picked 35 apples on the first day and 65 apples on the second day. If she wants to divide them evenly into baskets of 5 apples each, how many baskets will she need?

Jenny prepared two types of cupcakes for her bake sale. She made five trays with 30 chocolate cupcakes on each tray and four trays with 25 vanilla cupcakes on each tray. How many cupcakes did Jenny bake in total?

Each student needs to bring 3 notebooks to school. There are 25 students in class A, 31 students in class B, and 28 students in class C. How many notebooks do the students in these three classes need in total?
Lisa has 15 barbie dolls and each one has 3 dresses. If she has washed 60% of the total dresses, how many dresses does she still need to wash?
Melissa bought 15 boxes of candies, each cost $2 and she also bought 5 chocolate bars, each cost $5. How much did she spend in total?

Jack is packing gift boxes. Each gift box contains 4 chocolate bars, 5 candy canes, and 3 small toys. If Jack packs 8 gift boxes and 1 box lacks the small toys because they ran out, how many items in total did Jack pack?
Every week, for her ballet classes, Emily covers a total of 15 miles. Calculate the total distance she covers in a semester of 15 weeks from home to class and back.

Taylor wants to knit scarfs for her friends. She made 15 scarfs and painted 3 different designs on each scarf. She decides to give 2 scarfs from each design to her dogs. If Taylor has 10 friends and she divides the remaining scarfs among them, calculate how many scarfs each friend received.

Mark has an ant farm with 10 worker ants, each of which can carry 5 grains of sand. If 3 ants are carrying a full load and the rest are carrying half of their maximum capacity, how many grains of sand in total are the ants carrying?

Kaye's babysitter charges $20/hour. Kaye is thinking about hiring a new babysitter who charges $4/hour less, but charges an additional $5 for each time the kids move furniture. If Kaye usually needs the babysitter for 5 hours, and her children usually move furniture four times, how much less will the new babysitter cost?
Taylor has 15 boxes of chocolate. Each of her boxes has as many chocolates as 4 of Morrie's boxes. If Morrie's boxes have 30 chocolates each, how many chocolates does Taylor have?
In a bookstore, there are 50 books on a shelf. If 18 books were sold, how many books are left on the shelf?
Jason bought a car for $15,000, and he sold it after 2 years for $12,000. If the car has depreciated linearly over the time he owns it, what is the rate of depreciation per year?
Linda spent 45% of her $800 monthly salary on rent. She saves 15% of her salary and spends the rest on her daily expenses. How much does Linda spend on her daily expenses?

In a library, there are fiction books, non-fiction books and science books. There are 100 fiction books. The number of non-fiction books is double the number of fiction books. There are 50 less science books than the sum of fiction and non-fiction books. How many books are there in the library?

Max, the baker, made doughnuts by mixing 8 pounds of dough, dividing it into 50 dough balls, and then frying them. Later in the day, his cat, Ginger, sneakily ate 10 doughnuts. After Ginger's mischievous feat, how many ounces of dough were in the remaining doughnuts?

A shelter trying to save endangered species of dogs has taken three times as many german shepherds as the number of dalmatians into the shelter. If the number of dalmatians is 300, calculate the total number of dogs that have been taken into the shelter.
In an online gaming contest, Mark scores 200 points in 5 rounds. His gaming partner, Jack scores three times as much as Mark in the same rounds. How many total points did they score together?
Henry can mow 4 lawns in a day. He gets two days off each week. How many lawns can he mow in two weeks?

In a school, there are three times as many girls as boys. The total number of students in the school is 800 and all the 450 girls were present that day. If all the absent students were boys, how many boys were absent?

A high school is hiring school bus services for a school field trip. The school bus can accommodate a maximum of 60 students. There are 180 students going on this trip. How many buses does the principal need to hire?

Susan sells handmade candles at a local market. The cost of materials to make each candle is $3. She sells each candle for $8. Out of 100 candles she made this month, she was able to sell 75% of them. How much profit did Susan make this month?
John runs a bakery and he wants to make 500 muffins each week. Each muffins needs 60 grams of flour. How many kilograms of flour does he need in a month?

Sarah reads a book for 3 hours every day. On the days she does her homework, she spends twice as long as she reads. If she does homework 4 days a week how much time does she spend reading and doing her homework?

Tom wants to bake a cake for 15 people. The recipe he is making serves 3 people. Each batch of the recipe calls for 5 eggs and 200g of sugar. An egg costs $0.2 and a bag of sugar costs $5 and has 1000g. How much money will Tom spend on ingredients?
Bob bought 3 packs of tea bags. Each pack contains 25 tea bags. After drinking tea every day for two weeks, he only used a single tea bag per day. How many tea bags are left?
Every day, for commute to work, Mary cycles a total of 7 miles. Calculate the total distance she cycles in a 90 day work period from home to work and back.
Amelia has 17 dolls and Frank has 5 more dolls than Amelia. If Amelia's cousin Lazy gifts her 22 dolls, how many more dolls will Amelia have than Frank?

Scott has a fish tank that can hold 145 gallons of water. It's currently half full, and Scott starts filling it with a hose that pumps water at a rate of 5 gallons per minute. How many minutes will it take to fill the rest of the tank?
Maria is a teacher who has 30 students in her class. She wants to give each of her student 2 pieces of chocolate. If a box of chocolate has 15 pieces, how many boxes does she need to buy?
Jack wants to buy a car, the cost of which is $25,000. He has 60% of the needed amount. How much more does he need to save to buy the car?
Rachel practiced playing violin for 45 minutes each day for 15 days. Maya also practiced violin. Together they practiced for 1280 minutes. How many minutes did Maya practice violin?
Anna reads 50 pages of a book each day. If the book has 350 pages, how many days will it take Anna to finish the book?
Alex wants to complete a 200-page book in 5 days. He already read 50 pages on the first day and 40 pages on the second day. How many pages should he read during the remaining days to finish the book?
Ella bought 15 packets of chips and 6 bottles of soda. Each packet of chips cost $2 and each bottle of soda cost $3. How much more did Ella spend on chips than on soda?

Bill is planning to grow a flower garden. The garden is 200 feet long by 100 feet wide. Half of the garden will have roses. A fourth of the remaining part will have sunflowers, and the rest will have daisies. How big will the daisy section of the garden be?

Peter is shopping for books. The first store offers a book for $30 with a 10% discount. The second store sells the same book for $35 with a 8% discount. What is the difference in price between the two stores?

Johnny is a guitar player and he has three colors of guitar picks: red, blue, and yellow. Half of his picks are red, one-third of the picks are blue, and the rest are yellow.  If he has 12 blue picks, what is the total number of yellow picks in his collection?
Jane baked 8 cupcakes and her friend Paula made 2 pies. Each pie is equivalent to 4 cupcakes. How many cupcakes did they make in total?
A class has 30 students. The girls are 2/3 of the total students and the remaining are boys. One third of the boys are left-handed. How many boys are right-handed?
Lisa's garden is 3 meters longer than twice the length of Mike's garden. If Lisa's garden is 16 meters long, how long is Mike's garden?
A grocery store has 15 cashiers and 10 stalkers. If 5 cashiers and 2 stalkers quit their job, how many cashiers and stalkers are left?
A wildfire destroyed 5 acres of forest. Each subsequent fire destroyed double the acres of the previous one. After 4 more fires how many acres had been destroyed including those from the first fire?
Samantha started a journey at 6 AM and drove at an average speed of 60 mph. She arrived at her destination at 2 PM. How many miles did she travel?
Ian wants to put a fence around his rectangular garden which is 15 feet long and 10 feet wide. How much fencing will he need to buy?

Matthew has 16 cows on his farm. Each cow produces 4 gallons of milk. He wants to fill up milk containers with the milk. If each milk container can hold 2 gallons, how many milk containers can Matthew fill?

In a bookstore, there are 12 hardcover books, 3 more paperback books than hardcover books, twice as many ebooks as hardcover books, and the rest are audio books. If there are 40 books in total, how many books are audio books?

George and Arnold are planting trees in their backyard. They plant 5 trees from their own nursery and 3 trees from a local nursery. Each nursery delivers 4 trees. George takes an extra 2 trees when Arnold is not checking. How many trees is Arnold left with?
Samantha babysits for $15 per hour. If she babysat for 7 hours and had to pay her brother $10 for helping her with one of the children, how much money did Samantha earn?
Jenny goes to school 5 days a week. Each day she spends 8 hours at school. How many hours does she spend at school in 2 weeks?
Nicole received 4/15 of the total project funds. Out of this, she used 2/5 to clear some pending bills and is now left with $1200. What was the total project fund?
Mark bought some picture frames for his office. He bought 5 large ones and 10 small ones, for $80 total. If the large frames cost $7, how much do the small ones cost?

Jessie has gone cycling only 5 times. Daniel has done 4 times as many cycles as Jessie, and Anthony has done 10 more cycles than Daniel. If Jessie plans to equal Anthony's cycles with a pace of 3 cycles per week, how many weeks would it take Jessie to achieve this?
A bakery sold 300 cupcakes in one day. They sold 75 in the morning, and 125 in the afternoon. How many cupcakes did they sell in the evening?

A flea market sells vintage books at $14 each and LP records at $7 each. One Sunday, they manage to sell 17 vintage books and 32 LP records, making a total of $401. How many vintage books did they sell?

Terry planted 6 rows of carrots and for each row of carrots, he planted 3 rows of bell peppers. He has enough space for 24 rows of plants in total. Each row of carrot plants can produce 7 carrots. If each carrot plant produces 2 carrots, how many carrots will Terry have?
In a school, there are 600 students. 60% of them are boys. How many girls are there in the school?
In a school, there are exactly 12 students per teacher. If there are 96 students in the school, how many teachers are there?
John bought 5 more comic books than three times the number of action figures he bought. If he bought 23 comic books, how many action figures did he buy?

Mark works out for 12 hours in a week. His sister, Sandy, works out for three-quarters of the number of hours Mark works out. Their brother, George, works out for half the combined total number of hours both Mark and Sandy take for their workouts. Calculate the number of hours George takes to workout in a week.

Liam has a vegetable garden where he plants 10 tomatoes, 8 beans, and 4 zucchinis. If each tomato plant yields 15 tomatoes, each bean plant yields 25 beans, and each zucchini plant yields 10 zucchinis, how many vegetables does he harvest?
Jane has a bag of marbles. There are blue and red marbles. If the bag contains 40% more blue marbles than red and she has a total of 120 marbles, how many blue marbles are there?
There are blue and yellow boxes in a container. The total number of boxes is 50. If there are 10 more blue boxes than yellow boxes. How many boxes, in total, are yellow or blue?

A bicycle rider rides his bicycle at a speed of 2 miles per hour. After buying a more efficient bicycle, his speed was increased by 50 percent. How many miles can he travel in 5 hours with the new bicycle?
If a gallon of paint covers 400 square feet, and Olivia plans to paint two rooms that are each 12 feet by 15 feet, how much paint will she need?

Alan's car can drive 24 miles per gallon of gas. He was able to drive 14 miles to work, 5 miles to the gym, 3 miles to a grocery store, 6 miles to his son's school and 12 miles home before he ran out of gas. How many gallons of gas did he start with?

For every 10 bottles you recycle, you receive $0.20, and for every 10 kilograms of newspapers, you receive $0.80. If your family collected 200 bottles and 50 kilograms of newspapers, how much money would you receive?

A basketball team made a total of 45 shots during a game. They made 3-point shots, 2-point shots, and free throws (1-point shots). They made twice as many 3-point shots than 2-point shots and eight more free throws than 3-point shots. How many 3-point shots did they make?
Lily started at an elevation of 200 meters. She started climbing at a rate of 5 meters up per minute for 10 minutes. What is her elevation now?
An electronics factory produced 100 laptops last month. This month, they produced three times the number of laptops they did last month. How many laptops did the factory produce in total?

David baked donuts for all of his co-workers and put them in the break room. He made 200 donuts so his 20 co-workers could get 10 donuts each. However, the last co-worker told David there were only 5 donuts left. David suspects it was Emma who took too many donuts. If all the other co-workers took the correct amount of donuts, how many donuts did Emma take?
Mason bought 10 more oranges than Alyssa. If Alyssa bought 20 oranges, how many oranges do they have altogether?

A restaurant charges $25 for a main course and $10 for a dessert. A group of 8 people orders main courses and desserts; while a group of 3 people only orders main courses. How much does the restaurant earn?
In a class there are 15 boys and 20 girls. If half of the girls and two-thirds of the boys have brown eyes, how many students in total have brown eyes?

David decides to build a model ship for his hobby. The time he spent building the model is 2 times the time he spent for painting it plus 10 minutes. If David spent a total of 90 minutes for the ship model, how many minutes did he use for painting?

Patrick decides to take 2 buses to go to the zoo. The first bus ride takes 45 minutes and the second bus ride takes 30 minutes. How long does it take Patrick to get to the zoo if he waits for 15 minutes at the bus stop?

David drives his car daily to work. Each trip to work and back to home is a total distance of 25 miles and his car can run 20 miles per gallon of gas. If gas costs $3.00 per gallon, how much does David spend on gas weekly?
Amy is baking cookies for her school fair. She uses 1/4 cup of flour for each cookie and plans to bake 60 cookies. How many cups of flour does she need?

Jackson has 20 cherry pies. If every cherry pie can be quickly divided into thirds and every third of a cherry pie can be split into 4 bite-size samples, how many people can taste Jackson's cherry pies if he divides them all into bite-size samples?
Laura has 8 rectangular photo frames. Each frame has 4 sides. She buys 3 more frames which have 6 sides each. How many sides are there on all photo frames in total?
Joe purchases a grill for $500. It is on sale and is 15% off the original price. How much did the grille originally cost?
Simon bought 10 soccer balls for his team. Each soccer ball originally costs $20, but the store was having a sale where each ball is discounted by 30%. How much did Simon save on his purchase?
Sarah went to the store to buy some apples. If the cost of each apple is 60 cents and she bought 30 apples, but she had a coupon for 20% off. How much money did she spend?
There are 15 girls and 18 boys in one dance class and three times that number in another class. If each person only attends one class, how many dancers total are there in both classes?
Sarah has been saving $20 every week for one year. She then spends $200 on a new phone. How much money does she have left?

To earn money for her new computer, Tina sells handmade postcards. In a day, she can make 30 such postcards. For each postcard sold, Tina gets $5. How much money did Tina earn if she managed to sell all the postcards she made every day for 6 days?

Abigail baked 54 cupcakes for a bake sale. She sold half in the morning and then her friend Caspar ate 4 in the afternoon. That evening she sold 1/5 of the remaining cupcakes. How many cupcakes did she sell in the evening?
Brian raised $120 for a charity. He then raised an additional $45 and then followed by a $10 withdrawal. What is the total amount raised for the charity?
In a classroom of 30 students, 10 students have iPhones, and the rest have Androids. If one student can only have one phone, how many students have Androids?
Logan plays video games, for 3 hours every day. He decided to reduce his video game time by 5 hours per week. How much time does Logan want to spend playing video games during one week?
Jacob bought 70 cookies for his party. However, 2 out of 5 people didn't show up. How many cookies did each guest get if everyone got the same amount and all cookies were eaten?
Sophie wants to buy a book that costs $20. This week, the bookstore is offering a 15% discount on all books. How much will the book cost after the discount?
Ben spends $5 on coffee every day. How much does he spend on coffee in a year?
Karen has 30 flowers. She wants to put 5 flowers in each vase. If Karen has 4 vases, how many more vases does she need?
Emily is 65 inches tall. Her younger brother is 2/3 her height, and her younger sister is 8 inches taller than her brother. How tall is Emily's sister?
Jenny read 5 books in January. In February, she read 20% fewer books compared to January. How many books did she read in total during both months?
A fruit shop has four times as many apples as a nearby grocery store. If the grocery store has 240 apples, how many apples do they have combined in total?
Hank bought 2 boxes of pencils. Each box consisted of 30 pencils each. If Hank gave away half of his pencils to his friend, how many pencils is he left with?
Kelly has $375 in her savings account. Every month, she deposits 150% of her previous balance. After 2 months, how much money does Kelly have in her savings account?

A group of 7 friends planned a 3-day trip to the beach. The beach house rental rate for weekdays is $300 per day and for weekends it is $350 per day. If they stayed from Friday to Sunday and evenly split the cost, how much does each person have to pay?
Liam has a car with a fuel tank of 10 gallons. After making a 25 miles round trip to his office, he finds he has 1/2 of the tank left. How many miles per gallon does his car give?
A pizza shop has a deal where if you buy 2 pizzas, you get 1 free. If Alice plans to buy 8 pizzas, how many pizzas does she get for free?
It's Halloween and Mark and his friends are bobbing for apples. The bucket can hold 30 apples. After Mark's turn, 9 apples are left in the bucket. How many apples did Mark and his friends bob?

Shane prepares sandwiches for the Boy Scouts. He buys 2 packages of sliced bread containing 20 slices each, and he also buys 2 packages of sliced ham containing 8 slices each. Shane will make as many sandwiches as he can according to the ham he has. How many slices of bread will he have leftover?

Sophia bought two lamps for her home, each at the same cost. She also bought a coffee table that costs $30 and three cushions at $10 each. Sophia gave the cashier $100 and got back $20 change. How much did each lamp cost?
One batch of cookies requires 4 cups of flour and 1.5 cups of sugar. How many cups of flour and sugar combined would be needed for 8 batches?
Andrew spends $6.50 charging his electric car daily. If he charged his car five times in a week, and the budget for his weekly charging was $45, how much money was left at the end of the week?
Peter has to mow a lawn that is 100 square meters. His lawnmower can mow 20 square meters in one hour. How many hours will it take Peter to mow the entire lawn?
Roger has a blue box full of yellow and red balls. There are 25 yellow balls, which represents 1/3 of the total number of balls in the box. How many red balls are in the box?
Danny has a collection of coins. He collected 6 coins in the first month, 8 coins in the second month, and 10 more coins in the third month. How many coins does Danny have in total?

Chris goes to the orchard and picks 20 apples, 15 peaches, and 10 pears. If the apples weigh 0.5 pounds each, the peaches weigh 1 pound each, and the pears weigh 1.5 pounds each, how many total pounds of fruit did Chris pick?
A mailman delivers 15 packages in three hours. If each package weighs 2 pounds and he works for eight hours in a day, how many pounds does the mailman carry in a day?
Kaleb went to a grocery store to buy fruits. He bought 7 oranges and 5 apples. If each orange costs $2 and each apple costs $1, how much did Kaleb spend on fruits?
In Tim's town, there's a new cinema that charges $5 for each movie. Tim wants to see three movies and buy a popcorn for $2. How much will he pay in total?
The toy store sells teddy bears for $12 each. If the shop owner size down on restocks and only orders $500 worth of teddy bears each month, how many teddy bears does she order?
Shawn has a savings account and withdraws $500. He then deposits $1500. If he starts with $5000, how much money does he have after these transactions?

For his science project, Jayden has two containers, 60 shapes and 900cm³ of water. One shape displaces 20cm³ of water, and he can fill any remaining shape with ordinary air. If he used all of the water and filled every shape, how many more shapes did he submerge than not?
Lucy bought 5 shirts that each cost $15 and 3 pants that each cost $20. How much did Lucy spend in total?
With every 15 minutes of exercise, you burn 100 calories. If you plan to exercise for 2.5 hours today, how many calories will you burn?

Sandra's monthly salary increased by 30% to $5200 this year. David's salary from last year was equal to two times Sandra's salary this year. If David's current salary is 15% more than his salary last year, what is his current salary?

A school has 30 first-grade students, 32 second-grade students, 28 third-grade students, and 34 fourth-grade students. If the first and second graders are in one class and the third and fourth graders are in another class, what is the average class size?
Mr. Johnson buys apples and oranges for his fruit market. He buys apples for $2 each and oranges for $3 each. If he buys 15 apples and 20 oranges, how much will he spend?
A class of 25 students are asked to collect plastic bottles for a recycling project. Each student collects 8 bottles. How many plastic bottles did the class collect all together?
In a game of basketball, Rachel scored 2 points less than twice the number of points that her teammate Monica scored. If Monica scored 10 points, how many points did Rachel score?
Jimmy has 200 posters. Nick has three times as many as Jimmy. Bella has 50 fewer than Nick. How many posters do the three of them have in total?

Emma wants to improve her swimming skills. Her goal is to start by swimming 2 laps a day the first week, then spend the next three weeks swimming two additional laps/day each week. How many laps is Emma swimming each day at the end of this exercise program?
Carol danced for 15 minutes in the morning. If she danced 5 minutes more than twice as long in the evening, how many total minutes did she dance in the day?
Anna buys 7 boxes of pencils at $2 each and four erasers at $1 each. If she has $30, how many notebooks can she buy for $3 each with the remaining money?
A grocery store has 18 shelves and can fit 50 packs of flour per shelf. How many packs of flour can the grocery store fit altogether?
Daniel earns $25 per hour at his job. However, for overtime, he earns 1.5 times his regular pay. If Daniel worked 45 hours in a week, with 5 hours being overtime, how much did he earn that week?
Martin has 25 marbles. He gives 5 marbles to his friend John and then he loses 3. How many marbles does Martin have left?
George is planning to build a robot. If each robot needs 125 bolts, and George has already bought 3 times that number. How many more bolts will he need if he decides to build 10 robots?

Linda made a large batch of cookies and divided it into 36 pieces. She had two with breakfast and dinner every day for 4 days. Her children each took one per day for 4 days to school, and she has 3 children. They shared 6 with neighbors. How many cookies were left?
Mark purchased five packets of balloons. Each packet contains 20 balloons. During the party, 18 balloons were popped. How many balloons were left?
Lucy has a garden with 16 tomatoes plants. Each plant gave her about 12 tomatoes. She decided to give 3/4 of the tomatoes to her neighbor. How many tomatoes did Lucy's neighbor recieve?
Tim, Max and Grace together own 100 apples. Tim has 10 less than the total of apples Max and Grace have. Grace has double the number of apples than Max. How many apples does Max own?

Laura buys 1 cappuccino for $5.00 every morning, 6 days a week.  4 days a week, she buys a smoothie for $3.00. She set a goal to cut her coffee and smoothie spending by 30% for the next year. How much will she save?
Alex and three of his friends are sharing a bag of 32 candies. If they decide that everyone gets an equal number of candies, how many candies will each of them get?
James needs 8 gallons of water to fill his fish tank. If he fills it using a bucket that holds 2 gallons, how many times will he have to fill the bucket to completely fill his tank?
Samuel is double the age of his brother who is 10 years old. If Samuel's age is increased by 5, how old is Samuel?
Amy has 5 apples and Ethan gives her twice as many as she already had plus 1 extra apple. How many apples does Amy have now?
Noel bought 9 textbooks for each of his 3 college classes this semester. Each textbook cost $75. How much total did Noel pay for all of his textbooks?

Laura, Michael, and Dawn each bought a packet of cookies. Laura's bag has three more cookies than Michael's. Dawn's bag has five more cookies than Laura's. If Michael's bag contained 15 cookies, what is the total number of cookies in all three packets?
Julia goes to the fruit market and buys 6 apples, 4 oranges, and 5 bananas. If one apple costs $1.25, one orange costs $0.75, and one banana costs $0.50, how much does she spend in total?
A salad bar charges $6 per pound for salad. If Alice prepared a salad which weighs 2.5 pounds, how much would she have to pay?

A mother cat has 6 kittens. The first 2 kittens catch 3 mice each. Another 2 kittens catch 5 mice each, and the remaining kittens each catch half the number of mice that the mother cat catches. If the mother cat catches four times the total number of mice as the first two groups of kittens, how many mice do the cat family gets collectively?
Kelly bought a designer bag for $450 and sold it for 20% more. How much did she sell the bag for?

Two friends, Tom and Jerry, decide to pool their money to buy a video game console. Tom contributes $200 and Jerry contributes $150. If the cost of the console is $315, how much money will they have left after buying the console?
Mia has 6 roses and 8 daisies in her garden. Each rose has 5 petals and each daisy has 4 petals. How many total flower petals are in Mia's garden?
Alicia is a jewelry maker and she sells handmade bracelets for $15 each and earrings for $10 per pair. If she sells 6 bracelets and 8 pairs of earrings, how much money does she earn?
Paul owns a bakery.  He has 15 hens that lay 8 eggs each per week. He sells the eggs for $3/dozen.  How much money will he make in 4 weeks if he sells all his eggs?

Mary planted a seedling tree, and it began to grow. The tree grew at a rate of 2 inches per week for the first 6 weeks, then it grew at a faster rate of 3 inches per week for the next 4 weeks. How tall, in inches, is the tree now?
Bob ordered 3 pizzas for a party. Each pizza has 8 slices. Half of the slices were eaten during the party. How many slices were left?

While Bryan was on vacation for 10 days, it snowed for 5 days. He found that the amount of snowfall was 2 cm, 3 cm, 1 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm on the five days. During the same period, it snowed 40 cm at his home. How much less snow did Bryan experience while on vacation?
Olivia sells plants. A cactus costs $15 and a succulent costs $10. If she sold 10 cacti and 20 succulents, how much did she earn?
Rebecca bought 2 packages of green beads and 7 packages of yellow beads. If there are 20 beads in each package, how many beads does Rebecca have in total?
A cake recipe calls for 6 eggs. Nancy wants to make 3 cakes but she only has 14 eggs. How many more eggs does she need?
Sarah jogged 3 miles each day while Jane jogged 2.5 miles each day. How many more miles, in all, does Sarah jog than Jane jog in 10 days?
A school has 600 students. One-fourth of the students are in fourth grade, half are in fifth grade and the rest are in sixth grade. How many students are in the sixth grade?
Albert has twice as many books as Alex, and Alex has 18 books less than Amy who has 42 books. How many books do Albert and Alex have altogether?
A sports club has 4 teams with 15 players each. If it plans to buy 3 pairs of shoes for each player, how many pairs of shoes will the sports club need to buy?

A scarf manufacturer produces 400 scarves per day at a cost of $2 each. To boost sales, the manufacturer offers a 25% discount on each scarf's price. How much revenue does the manufacturer earn from selling all the scarves for seven days, given that each scarf is sold?

Peter built a tower using blocks. The first layer had 5 blocks, the second layer had 6 blocks more than the first, and the third layer had twice as many blocks as the second. How many blocks did Peter use in total?
Velma and Daphne run a podcast. Velma gets an average of 30 listeners per episode while Daphne gets three times as many listeners. If they make $2 per listen, how much does Daphne make per episode?
A company earns $1000 in a day. If the company works 25 days a month, how much does the company make in a month?

Katie is planning a birthday party for her son. She bought 5 packets of balloons, each with 12 balloons, and 8 party hats. If each party hat cost $2 and she spent $64 in total, how much did she spend for each packet of balloons?
A soccer team needs 15 soccer balls for a season. Each soccer ball costs $10. If a local company sponsored half of the team's soccer balls, how much did the team need to pay for the soccer balls?
A stack of $20 bills is $3000. How many bills are in the stack?
A teacher gives 5 apples to each of her 20 students. Each apple has 8 seeds. What is the total number of apple seeds?

Julia, Sam and Charles decide to have a popcorn eating contest. They decide to judge the winner based on the weight of popcorn eaten. Julia eats cheese popcorn that weighs 2 ounces per serving. Sam eats caramel popcorn that weighs 3 ounces per serving. Charles likes plain popcorn which is just 1 ounce per serving. Sam eats one less serving than Julia and Charles eats twice as many servings as Sam. If Julia eats 5 servings, how much popcorn does Charles consume in total?

Robert bought a paperback book for $20 and a hardcover book for $40. He used a coupon that gave him 50% off on the paperback book and a 10% discount on the hardcover book. How much did he pay for both books?

A toy store sold three types of toys: legos, dolls, and cars. The store sold 36 legos. The number of dolls sold was three times the number of legos sold. The number of cars sold was half the number of dolls sold. How many toys did the store sell in total?
A family uses 5 cartons of milk each week. If the milk costs $3 a carton, how much will the family spend on milk in a month?

Brandon has 16 green marbles and 14 red marbles. He gives his cousin 1/4 of his green marbles and 1/2 of the red marbles. If his cousin had 8 green marbles and 9 red marbles to begin with, how many marbles does his cousin now have?
An airplane flies 500 miles. Then, it flies 25% farther. How far in total did the airplane fly?
Liam bought 12 dozen eggs for $4 per dozen, and sold them for $1 per egg. How much profit did Liam make?

Connor read 20 books last month. Matthew read half the number of books that Connor read. Amelia read 10 more than twice the number of books Matthew read. How many books in total did Connor, Matthew, and Amelia read?

Jacob and his sister Sarah went to a book fair. Sarah bought 2 more books than Jacob, but Jacob bought three times as many pens as Sarah. If Sarah bought 10 books and 5 pens, how many items did Jacob buy all together?
Simon needs to be 16 years old to get his driver's license. His brother, who is 4 years older than him, just got his license. How many more years does Simon need to wait to get his driver's license?
A toy train has 50 cars, each car is 10 cm long. When all the cars are connected together, what is the total length of the train?
A baker has made 70 cupcakes, 15 less muffins than the number of cupcakes, and three times as many pies as muffins. How many baked goods has the baker made in total?
Ricardo is training for an upcoming race. He is planning to run 5 kilometres per day. The race is in one month. How many kilometres will he run in total?

For his morning routine, Sam makes a smoothie using 1 banana, 2 oranges and twice as many strawberries as oranges. If he wants to continue this routine for a week, how many pieces of fruit does he need altogether?
A batch of handmade soap costs $10 to make and sells for $25. If Amy made and sold 15 batches, how much profit did she make?

A Thai restaurant received 5 different shipments of rice. The first three shipments of 10, 20 and 25 pounds arrived on the same day. The next day two shipments were of 30 and 35 pounds each of rice. If it takes 10 pounds of rice to make a dish, how many dishes did the restaurant make?

On Monday, Stephanie baked 5 cupcakes. On Tuesday, Stephanie baked twice as many cupcakes as she did on Monday. On Wednesday, Stephanie baked half as many cupcakes as she did on Monday. How many cupcakes did Stephanie bake in total?
A bookstore has 200 books in stock. 30% of the books are fiction, 50% are non-fiction, and the rest are children's books. How many children's books does the bookstore have?
A box contains 5 red balls and 7 blue balls. If Maria randomly picks one ball from the box, what is the probability that it is blue?

Alice spends 10 hours each week practicing piano. Each hour, she drinks a full glass of water that holds 3 cups. At the end of the week, she also waters her plants with an additional 6 full glasses. How many cups of water did Alice drink/use in a week?

Ben drives 15 miles to pick up his friend from work. Then, he then drives a quarter of that distance to take his friend to his home. After dropping off his friend, Ben drives thrice that total distance he drove so far to get back to his own home. How many miles did Ben drive in total?
Sarah bought a box of 24 candy bars. She gave 4 candy bars each to her 3 friends and ate 2 herself. How many candy bars does she have left?

John buys 4 24-packs of beer for a BBQ.  Including John, 8 people are at the BBQ.  Half of the people at the BBQ have 4 beers each, 3 of the people have 5 and 1 person has 6.  How many beers are left over when the BBQ is over?
James has 6 comic books. His brother gave him 2 more, and then his friend gave him twice as many as his brother did. How many comic books does James have now?

Jacob's yearly salary is $50,000. He needs to pay 25% tax and 10% for insurance. After these deductions, Jacob uses half of the remaining money for his living expenses. How much money does Jacob have left after these deductions and expenses?
A baker made 150 muffins for a large event. He gave away 20% of the muffins to a local charity and sold the rest. How many muffins did he sell?

Cindy is arranging 30 flowers for a wedding decoration, but she needs more ribbons. She currently has a total of 40 ribbons. She wants to decorate half of the flowers with 1 ribbon each and the other half of the flowers with 2 ribbons each. How many additional ribbons does Cindy need to complete the flower arrangement?
Andy goes shopping and buys 3 shirts for $30 each and 2 pairs of jeans for $50 each. What is the total cost of, Andy's shopping?
Bill baked a batch of 24 brownies. He ate 3, shared 5 with his sister, and 2 with his friend. How many brownies are left?
Mia has 60 math books and 30 percent of her books are algebra books. Mika has 120 books and 40 percent of them are algebra books. In total how many algebra books do Mia and Mika have?

Jake travels 60 miles to visit his grandmother every weekend. If gas costs $3 per gallon and his car gets 20 miles per gallon, how much money does Jake spend on gas each weekend to visit his grandmother?
Amy bought a $1200 laptop at a 15% discount. How much money did she save?
Mary's car completely runs out of gas after driving 400 miles. If the gas tank can hold 10 gallons, how many miles per gallon does her car get?

A local ice cream shop was offering a "Create Your Own Sundae" deal.  The sundae bowl cost $1.50 and each scoop of ice cream costs $0.75.  Any topping costs $0.25 and a cherry on top costs $0.50.  If Alex orders the sundae with 3 scoops of ice cream, 4 toppings, and a cherry on top, how much will his dessert cost?

Sarah is baking a cake for a party. She has been given a recipe that makes one cake but she needs to make 5 cakes. The recipe calls for 2 cups of flour, 3 eggs, and 4 cups of sugar. How many cups of sugar does she need?
For his school project, Terry needs to buy a sketch pad that costs $15 and six color pencils that cost $3 each. How much does he spend for these items?
