In a bakery, a cake costs $10 to make. The bakery sells the cake for 150% of its cost. How much profit will the bakery make for each cake sold?
Samantha went apple picking and collected 3 bags full of apples. Each bag can fit 15 apples. She decided to make apple pies and each pie requires 4 apples. How many apple pies can she make?
A school canteen serves 100 pieces of bread every day for breakfast. If a mold infection destroys every fifth piece of bread, how many good pieces of bread are served in two days?
Samuel has 24 toy cars. If he decides to give half of his toy cars to his little brother and then his friend gives him 10 more, how many toy cars does Samuel have now?
Benjamin earns $400 per week at his part-time job. He decided he will save up for a new laptop which costs $2,000. How many weeks will it take Benjamin to save up for this laptop?
A roller coaster ride lasts 3 minutes. How many times do you get to ride it within an hour?

Jared has a farm that has 30 plots with 5 trees in each plot. He plants mango trees in the first 4 plots and avocado trees in the last three plots. The rest of the plots will be planted with orange trees. How many orange trees will Jared plant?
Sam purchased 3 CDs, each priced at $12, and 4 books, each priced at $15. How much did Sam spend in total?
Lily's mom baked 20 cupcakes for a party. Lily ate 3 cupcakes and her sister ate 2 cupcakes. Her mom gave 5 cupcakes to their neighbors. How many cupcakes are left?
Mark and Peter dug ponds in their backyards. Mark’s pond is 4 feet deeper than 3 times Peter’s pond. If Mark’s pond is 19 feet deep, what is the depth of Peter’s pond?
If you take the maximum speed of a cheetah, double it, add 6 to that then double it again you get 246. How fast is the cheetah?

Lisa is making a blanket that is composed of 300 square pieces of fabric. Each piece of fabric costs $0.75. If she wants to add a decorative trim around the blanket and trim costs $2.00 per yard and she needs 12 yards, how much will Lisa spend on making the blanket?
Jackson collected 100 baseball cards and 50 football cards. He sold 60 baseball cards and 20 football cards. How many cards does he have now?
Mary's average score on the first 4 tests was 85. After the fifth test, her average dropped to 80. What was her score on the fifth test?
Sophie, Grace and Olivia plan a road trip. Sophie drives for 2 hours at 80 mph, Grace drives for 3 hours at 75 mph and Olivia drives for 4 hours at 60 mph. What is the total distance they covered?
Dave buys a box of cookies every week. Each box contains 12 cookies. After 8 weeks, he gives away 3 boxes of cookies to his friends. How many cookies does he have left?
Jake studies 3 times per week for 4 hours at a time. How many hours will Jake study if he keeps this routine for a whole year?

Sarah is a fitness instructor. She spends 2 hours a day, from Monday to Friday, preparing for her classes. She then teaches for 3 hours a day, from Monday to Friday. On the weekend, she rests. How many hours does Sarah spend working in a week?
Katie has a collection of comic books. She has 150 marvel comic books and has twice as many DC comic books. How many comic books does she have in total?
A library has 15 shelves and each shelf can store up to 30 books. If the library is filled to capacity and then 45 books are borrowed, how many books are left in the library?
Sarah went to a game shop and bought 4 games each at $30, 2 controllers each at $50, and 1 gaming console at $300. How much did she spend in total?
Jake was jogging in the park. He jogs 5 laps around the park each day. Each lap is 0.8 miles. How many miles would Jake have jogged after 10 days?
John has twice as many books as Mary. If Mary has 25 books, how many books do they have in total?
John bought a jacket for $800 and then sold it for 20% less than he bought it for. How much money did John lose?
Sarah has 5 different shades of lipstick. Each shade has 3 similar shades at Macy’s. How many more shades does Macy’s have than Sarah?
Leah read 12 pages in 30 minutes while David read 24 pages in the same amount of time. How many more pages did David read than Leah in one hour?
Jenna has a collection of 200 comic books. She gives away 10% of her collection to her friend. Of the remaining comic books, she sells 1/5. How many comic books does Jenna have left?
A book store has some books on sale. 60% of the books are detective novels and 240 are romance novels. How many books are on sale?
Cambridge Middle School has 30 teachers, 2 vice principals, 1 principal and 10 classes with each having 25 students. How many people are there in total in Cambridge Middle School?

Oliver works at a burger joint and earns a base salary of $9 per hour plus a 10% commission on everything he sells. This month, he worked 170 hours and sold $30000 worth of burgers. How much did he earn?
Maggie purchased 12 chocolate bars and 8 packs of cookies. Each chocolate bar costs $2 and each pack of cookies costs $3. How much more did Maggie spend on cookies than on chocolates, in dollars?
A factory produces 200 chairs a day. After 15 days, the factory closes down for maintenance for 5 days, after which it resumes its usual production. How many chairs are produced in a month?

In a marathon race, Lyra reached the first checkpoint in 2 hours. She took a 15 minutes break before continuing to the second checkpoint which she reached in another 2 hours. What's the total time, in minutes, that Lyra took to reach the second checkpoint from the start?
There are 500 seats in a theater, and each movie showing costs $10 per seat. If the theater is showing 3 movies in a day and 70% of the seats were purchased, how much was generated in ticket sales?

Lily decides to share her box of chocolates with her friends. She has a box of 38 chocolates and she gave 4 chocolates to each of her 7 friends. She also ate 2 chocolates because she was hungry. How many chocolates does she have left?
A box has a length of 10 cm, a width of 4 cm and a height of 5 cm. What is the volume of the box?
Bethany reads 50 pages of a book per day. If the book has a total of 450 pages, how many days does it take for Bethany to finish the book?
Max had 96 marbles. He gave 1/4 of his marbles to his friend Sam, and then he lost half of the remaining marbles. How many marbles does Max have left?
Liam reads twice as many books as William. After buying 7 more books, Liam has a total of 27 books. How many books does William have?
A chef has 50 eggs and each egg weighs 70 grams. If 1/4th of the eggs broke and the remaining were used for cooking, how much total weight did the eggs used for cooking have?
A fruit seller sold 100 kilograms of oranges at $3 per kilogram and 50 kilograms of apples at $4 per kilogram. If he paid $200 for the delivery of the fruits, what was his total profit?
Sophia goes to the gym 3 times a week. Each session lasts 1.5 hours. How many hours does Sophia spend at the gym in 2 weeks?
In Dana's garden, she has twice as many rose plants as tulips. If she has 12 tulips, how many rose plants does she have?
Frankie has 756 baseball cards. He wants to sell them in packs, if each pack contains 6 cards, how many packs can Frankie make?

Aunt Ava baked 324 brownies. She gave 20 brownies to Sara, 18 brownies to Max, kept some in the pantry and gave the rest to Bob. How many brownies did she put in the pantry if she gave thrice as many brownies as she gave Sara to Bob?

Mina is comparing two jogging routes. In the first route, she jogs for 10 minutes uphill, then takes a road for half of the uphill jogging time, then finishes the jog in twice the time it took to jog the first two parts. In the second route, she jogs for 15 minutes on a flat path, then finishes the jog in three times this amount of time. In minutes, how much shorter was the first route compared with the second?

Kylie picks apples for 3 hours. The first hour she picks 66 apples. The second hour she doubles her apple picking rate, and the third hour she picks a third of the apples picked in the first hour. How many apples did Kylie pick total?
Amy can read 10 pages of a book in an hour, and she can only read for 2 hours a day. If Amy has a 300-page book, how many days will she need to read the entire book?
Out of Rachel's 60 toys, 1/4 of them are dolls, 2/5 are teddy bears, and the rest are Lego sets. How many Lego sets does Rachel have?
Susie is 36 years old. Her daughter is currently 15 years younger than she is. How old was Susie when she gave birth to her daughter?
Jack collected 75 coins in total. He got 20 quarter dollar coins, 15 nickels, and 30 pennies. The remaining are dime coins. How many dime coins did Jack collect?
There is a school of 500 students. 200 students joined the football team, 150 students joined the chess club, and the rest joined the music club. What percentage of students joined the music club?
Danny decides to buy himself a new telescope.  It cost 50% more than his old $500 telescope.  How much did it cost?
Diane had a basket of 12 apples, Jeff had a basket of 9 oranges and Ken had 15 bananas. If they combined and distribute the fruits equally, how much will each person get?
Adam collects comic books. He has 7 Marvel comics and twice that number of DC comics. If one of his Marvel comics was ruined and had to be thrown away, how many comic books does Adam have left?

Two bicycles are travelling to their destination along different paths. The first path is 80 km long and the bicycle travels at 20 km/h. The second path is 60 km long and the bicycle travels at 30 km/h. How much time did they take on average? (Round to the nearest integer.)
If a swimmer completes a 50 meter freestyle race in 25 seconds, what is their speed in meters per second?
A farmer sells two types of fruits, apples and oranges. He sells an apple for $2 and an orange for $3. He sold 150 apples and 250 oranges in a day. What is the total amount he earned in a day?
A movie starts at 8:15 pm and ends at 10:45 pm. If Emily misses the first 15 minutes of the movie, how long does she watch the movie?
In a classroom, there are 60 seats and seats are arranged into 4 rows, if seats are evenly distributed among the rows, how many students can seat in each row?
John spent $50 on groceries. Vegetables cost him a third of the total amount. He spent the rest evenly on grains and dairy. How much did he spend on dairy?
Mike takes 2 hours to finish a painting. He spends 3/4 of his time sketching and the rest coloring. How much time does he spend coloring?
A manufacturer produces 25 toy robots each month. The number doubles every three months. How many toy robots will the manufacturer produce in 9 months?
A factory produces 30 cars per day. Over the course of 5 days, how many cars are produced?

An amusement park charges $20 admission and $10 for a ride. A group of 20 people goes to the amusement park and takes the ride; while a group of 10 people only goes to the amusement park. How much does the amusement park earn?
Emma is planning a road trip which is 450 miles long. Her car can cover 30 miles with 1 gallon of gas. How many gallons of gas will she need for her trip?

An author wrote a series of 6 books. Each book was $13.95 and every book was also available in e-book format for $7.99. A fan decided to buy 3 paper books and the rest in e-book format. What was the total cost?

In a flower shop, there are 68 red roses and 52 white roses. The florist decides to create bouquets using 6 roses each. If she will mix red and white roses in one bouquet, how many bouquets can she create?
Janie needs to take 3 pills of her 500 mg medication every 8 hours. How many mg does she take in a day?
Robin agreed to paint a fence for $200. She has to buy her own paint, which costs $15 per can. If she needs 4 cans of paint, how much will Robin earn after expenses?

Ben is preparing 4 pizzas for dinner. Each pizza takes 8 minutes to prepare and requires 10 more minutes to bake than to prepare. Then it takes 4 more minutes to cool than to bake. How many hours will it take to prepare all 4 pizzas?
Susanna started a small business with an initial investment of $5000. After a month, the value of her business grew by 50%. How much is her business worth after 1 month?

A train travels at a speed of 80 kilometers per hour. However, due to a rail maintenance, the train's speed slows down by 20 kilometers per hour. If the train needs to travel a distance of 720 kilometers, how long will it take?
Linda baked 24 cookies. She gave half to her friends and saved the rest for her family. How many cookies did Linda save?

In a baseball tournament, team A scored 40 runs and team B scored 12 more than team A. In the second round, team A scored double the runs scored by team B in the first round while team B scored 5 less than team A. How many more runs in total did team A score than team B?

Paula decides to bake cupcakes for her friend's birthday party. The recipe that she has yields 48 cupcakes and she decides to make 3 batches. However, during the process, she accidentally drops and ruins one batch of 48 cupcakes. To her dismay, her dog eats 6 cupcakes from the remaining cupcakes while she isn't looking. How many cupcakes does Paula have in the end?
Sara has 60 calligraphy pens. She gives half of them to her friend, then buys 20 more. Later on, she drops 10 pens and they get damaged. How many pens does Sara have now?

A group of 50 tourists visit a local museum.
30 of them purchase the general ticket that costs $20 each.
the rest purchase the VIP ticket that costs $35 each.
How much money did the museum earn from selling tickets?
Megan bought twice as much candy as Susan. Kathy bought 5 less candy than Megan. If Susan bought 10 candies, how many candies did they buy altogether?
Each day, Gina reads 5 pages of one book and 7 pages of another book. If she does this for 10 days, how many pages total did she read?

A school choir has 10 sopranos, 8 altos, and 15 tenors. Each soprano has 2 sheet music pages, each alto has 3 sheet music pages, and each tenor has 1 sheet music page. How many sheet music pages are there in total for the choir?
Martha has been a nurse for 35 years. Her colleague worked for 5 years less. What is their combined work experience?

The public library has a book drive where people can donate their books. Charlie has 10 mystery novels, 15 adventure novels, and 20 drama novels that he wants to donate. He then persuaded his 2 friends to each donate the same amount of books as him. If Charlie changed his mind and decided to keep half of his books, how many total books are being donated?
There are 18 people in a yoga class. Half the class are women and the rest are men. If of the men, only a third have a yoga mat, how many men have a yoga mat?
Sally sells lemonade for $2 per cup. If she sold 15 cups in the morning and twice as many in the afternoon, how much did Sally make for the day?
Alex has 7 apples and eats 2. His friend gives him 3 more. How many apples does Alex have now?
Kian has 150 marbles. He gave 30% of them to his best friend and lost another 50% of the remaining marbles. How many marbles does he have left?
In a painting workshop, the evening batch has 4 more than half as many students as in the morning batch. There are 12 students in the evening batch. How many are in the morning batch?
Derek has a quarter as many comic books as Danny. Danny has five times as many comic books as Sam. If Sam has 96 comic books, how many comic books does Derek have?

Juan has four less sugar candies than Maria, while Maria has 1/2 as many caramel candies as Juan. If Juan has 16 caramel candies and Maria 20 sugar candies, calculate the total number of candies they have together.

In her garden, Nancy planted 3 apple trees and 4 orange trees. Each tree gives exactly 30 fruits. If Nancy distributes the fruits among 5 of her neighbors, each receiving an equal amount, how many fruits will each neighbor receive?

Liam has an old motorbike that requires some maintenance. He needs 5ml of lubricant for each wheel and another 10ml of lube for the rest of the bike. How much lubricant does he require in total to service the bike?
Five athletes jogged a total of 300 miles last month. Michael jogged 80 miles. Roger, Scott, Kelly, and Sara all jogged the same distance. How many miles did Sara jog?
For a company event, they set up 25 tents with 8 chairs in each tent. If only 13 chairs were left unoccupied, how many chairs were used?

A restaurant offers a buffet. The restaurant spent $50 on vegetables, $75 on meats, and $25 on desserts. The restaurant charges customers 300% of what it spends on food for the buffet. How much do customers pay for the buffet?

There are 15 students in a class. Each student has 5 textbooks. A school year is 180 days and each day the students bring all their textbooks. How many textbooks are brought to school during a school year?
Barbara, Jenna and Rico are to share a bag of 300 candies. If Jenna takes 20%, and Barbara and Rico are to share the remainder in the ratio 5:3 respectively, how many candies will Rico get?
Alice is responsible for creating 3 copies of a report for 6 people attending a conference. The report is 30 pages long. How many pages will Alice copy?

Tina got a 7% raise at her job. Before the raise, she earned 30 dollars per hour. She works 9 hours per day for 5 days per week. Her old bills used to be 500 dollars a week but she decided to add a hundred and fifty dollar a week yoga trainer. How much does she have leftover a week?
Emma started with $5 in her savings account and received $20 each week for her allowance. How much money does Emma have in her savings account after 10 weeks?
Peter spends 45 minutes a day, 5 days a week practicing the guitar and 30 minutes a day, 3 days a week practicing the piano. How much time in total does he spend practicing his instruments each week?

Martin manages eight employees. Five of them are office clerks who make $12/hour, and the other three are supervisors who make $18/hour. Martin has to pay 12% of his workers' salaries in FICA taxes. If everyone works 20 days a month and 7 hours a day, how much does Martin owe total for their wages and taxes for one month?
John is twice as old as Henry. In four years John will be only one year older than Henry. How old is John now?
Madison reads 20 pages per day. If she reads for 5 days, how many pages does she read altogether?
Timmy has 12 apples. He gives 2 apples to Sally, and then he gives twice as many apples as he gave Sally to Johnny. How many apples does Timmy have now?
Tom and Jerry collected some seashells from the beach. Tom found 15 seashells, twice as many as Jerry. In total, how many seashells did they collect?
Abigail sells homemade bracelets. She sold 15 bracelets at $6 each and 20 bracelets at $4 each. How much money did she earn?

Linda collects tiny figurines. She had 12 crystal foxes and three times as many clay bears. She later sold 4 of the crystal foxes, and replaced them with some glass birds. How many glass birds did she acquire if her total collection after this now stands at 67 figurines?
Jenny loves to read books. She reads 10 books each month. After reading books for 5 months, Jenny decides to sell half of her books for $4 each. How much money did she make by selling her books?
Cindy has 5 cats. Each cat consumes an average of 7 pounds of food a month. One cat eats 10 pounds a month. The second eats half of what the first one eats. How much does the third cat eat a month?

Vinny weighed 300 pounds then lost 20 pounds in the first month of his diet. He continued his diet but each month, he lost half as much weight as he had lost in the previous month. At the start of the fifth month of his diet, he worked harder to lose extra weight then decided to end his diet. If Vinny weighed 250.5 pounds at the end of his diet, how many pounds did he lose throughout the fifth month?
Jacob can complete a 500m race in 8 minutes. He runs the first 100m in 2 minutes. For the rest of the race, his friend, Lucas, runs 3 m/min faster. How long does it take for Lucas to finish the race?
Ally made 60 candles in 4 hours. If she keeps making candles at the same rate, how many candles will Ally make in 7 hours?
In his greenhouse, Mark grows tomatoes. He has 10 boxes with 8 tomatoes each. He sells one third of them to grocery. How many tomatoes does he have left?
Peter has 7 coins of 1 dollar, 12 coins of 50 cents and 20 coins of 25 cents. How much money does Peter have in total, in dollars?
Jane's bakery made 100 cookies. She sold 3/4 of the cookies on Monday and 1/2 of the remaining cookies on Tuesday. How many cookies does she have left?
Olivia is restocking her bookshop. She adds 50 mystery novels at $6.00 each, 40 fantasy novels at $7.50 each, and 30 thriller novels at $5.00 each. How much is all the novels she stocked worth?

Tom bakes cookies for a local bake sale. He bakes 20 sugar cookies, 35 chocolate chip cookies and 45 oatmeal raisin cookies. At the end of the day, he sold 60% of the sugar cookies, 80% of the chocolate chip cookies, and 50% of the oatmeal raisin cookies. How many cookies did Tom sell?
Fred's garden has 5 types of vegetables and each type has 20 plants. If 1/4 of the plants have been harvested, how many plants are left?

George bakes Christmas cookies. He places 4 of them with green frosting, 7 of them with red frosting, and 3 of them with both green and red frosting. What percentage of the cookies have green frosting?
Mary had 48 cupcakes. She gave 1/4 of the cupcakes to her friend and ate 1/3 of the remaining cupcakes. How many cupcakes are left?
Farmer Joe has a chicken that lays 6 eggs every day. He collects eggs for 10 days. How many eggs will he have?
Sarah purchases one magazine every week for a year, but then losses a third of them due to water damage. If one magazine cost $5, how much did she spend on undamaged magazines?
John buys 8 books, each costing $15. His younger brother needed 4 books, so John gave him those. His friend gave him $20 for 2 books. How much did it cost John for the books he kept?
A cafe uses 2 cups of coffee beans for each pot. They make 25 pots in a morning and 35 pots in the afternoon. How many cups of coffee beans do they use in a day?
Mark's GA3 algorithm obtained 85% of the possible reward on the Procgen environment. Procgen's maximum reward is double the maximum PPO reward of 100. How much reward did Mark's GA3 algorithm get?

Jackie went to the market and bought bananas, mangoes, and apples. The bananas cost half the price of the apples, while the cost of the mangoes was 80% of the price of the apples. If the apples cost $24, how much did Jackie spend in total?
Anne bought some groceries. She paid $20 for fruits, $15 for vegetables, and $5 for milk. If she had $10 left, how much money did Anne have at the start?
Alice lives three floors above Bob. Bob lives on a floor whose number is 1/3 Tim's floor number. Tim lives on the 18th floor. What floor does Alice live on?
Hannah spends 45 minutes three times a week on practicing guitar. How many hours a month (assume 4 weeks) does she spend on practicing guitar?
Mary is a baker. She uses 2 cups of sugar for each cake and bakes 10 cakes each day. How many cups of sugar will she use after baking cakes for 5 days?
Emma can read 10 pages per hour. In the last two days, she spent 4 hours each day reading. On the third day, she only read 30 pages. How many pages did Emma read over the three days?
Sam is decorating his skateboard with stickers. He sticks 7 rows of 9 red stickers each, 6 rows of 11 black stickers each, and 8 rows of 8 green stickers each. How many stickers does he add total?
Mia has a garden where she grows carrots. One carrot weighs around 150 grams. If Mia has just harvested 40 carrots, how many kilograms of carrots does she have?

A rectangular yard has a length of 10 meters and a width of 8 meters. There is a doghouse made with a height of 1 meter, a length of 2 meters and a width of 2 meters. What is the total area of the yard not occupied by the doghouse?
Sarah baked 36 cookies to sell at a bake sale. Each cookie is sold for $1. She gave away 6 cookies to her friends. How much did she earn from selling the rest of the cookies?

Brian wants to buy a new car. The bank approved him for a loan amount of $20,000 at 6.8% annual interest rate. The loan is for 5 years. If each year has 12 months, how much will he be paying each month?
Katrina buys 3 books a month. Each book costs her $12. After 6 months, how much money has she spent on books?
There are 25 classrooms in ABC Elementary School, each with 30 students. If they open 5 new classrooms, how many students will the school have in total?
Bill saved up $200 to buy a new skateboard. He spent half of his savings on the board, and then used a tenth of what was left on accessories. How much money does he still have?
A printer uses a cartridge of ink every 3 days. Each cartridge of ink cost $10. How much does the printer spend on ink cartridges for a 30 day month?
The school library has a novel priced at $25. If the same novel is sold at the local bookstore for 10% less, how much would three novels cost at the local bookstore?
Diane has 16 hair clips, 4 hats, 7 scarfs and 5 purses. How many hair clips does she need to purchase to double the number of individual items in her collection?

Sam’s school is planning a field trip to a museum. The entrance fee per student is $15, and the school bus rental is $200. If the total allocated budget is $800, how many students can Sam's school afford to send to the museum?
John and Sarah are readers. John reads twice the amount of books as Sarah. If Sarah reads 30 books in a year, and John reads 50% more books the following year, how many books does John read in total?
Edgar orders 45 kg of cherries, 55 kg of plums and 35 kg of peaches for his fruit store. By the end of the day, 95 kg of fruit were sold. How much fruit does he have left?

A team of 5 workers are making tables in a carpentry workshop. Each of them produces 3 tables an hour. As a group, they produce an additional table every 4 hours. In total, how many tables have been produced after 8 hours?

John runs a lemonade stand. He sells 30 cups of lemonade each day at $0.75 a cup. If he takes a day off and comes back the next day to find that he's made $45 in sales, how much more did he make on his day off than an average day?
A carpenter makes 3 chairs in an hour. How many chairs does the carpenter make in a week assuming a 40-hour work week?
Lisa ordered four burgers, each burger costs $6. She then gave a tip that equals 1/6 of the total cost of her order. If she paid with a $50 bill, how much change did she receive?

William and his friends started a small company. There are 8 people in this company and every month each person earns $1500. If they have decided to divide the profits equally, how much does each person get every month?

The difference in the number of sheets in the notebooks of Amy and Lily matches their height difference in inches. Amy has 100 sheets and Lily has 1/4 times as many sheets as Amy. If Amy's height is 64 inches and Lily is shorter than Amy, calculate Lily's height.
Susie bought 5 times as many candies as Mike did. Mike bought twice the number of candies as Tim did. How many candies did Susie and Mike buy altogether if Tim spent $15 on $0.25 candies?

Amy plans to start a vegetable garden. She allotted an area of 6 square meters for cabbages which costs $2 per square meter and 4 square meters for carrots which costs $3 per square meter. How much will she spend for the seeds?

Daniel hired a consultant for his company. He paid $5000 upfront. The consultant charges $150 per hour. The consultant has to work 60 hours in meetings. The prep time before meetings takes 3 times that long. His business partner pays half of the total fee. How much did Daniel pay?

Maria is planning a birthday party for her daughter. She invited 15 kids and she wants the party to last 5 days. Everyone loves eating cupcakes and Maria calculated that each kid will eat 2 cupcakes per day. If her oven can bake 30 cupcakes in just one turn, how many turns will she have to make to have enough cupcakes for everyone?

Lucy plans to read a 400-page book in 4 days. She reads 60 pages the first day, and 70 pages the second day. If she reads the same number of pages the last two days, how many pages does she read each of these days?

Alice sold 200 pears at her fruit vendor in the farmer's market today. She has three types of pears: green pears, red pears and yellow pears. The number of green pears sold was thrice the number of yellow pears and twice the number of red pears. How many red pears did Alice sell?

Kyle has a long drive ahead of him. His destination is 240 miles away, and he drives at a speed of 60 miles per hour. His car uses 1 gallon of gas for every 30 miles. How many gallons of gas will Kyle use for this trip?
One weekend, a park sold 3 more times the number of adult tickets as it did children tickets. If 40 children tickets were sold, how many adult tickets did the park sell?
A perfume shop is selling small perfume bottles for $25 each and large perfume bottles for $38 each. If a customer bought 1 large perfume bottle and 2 small bottles, how much did the customer pay?

Marty downloaded a game on his PC. It took him 15 minutes to download the game, but later he had to install updates for the game which took four times as long. After starting the game, it crashed twice, each crash taking 5 minutes to fix. He spent three times as much time playing the game without it crashing. How much time did he spend in total on the game?

Jake hit a baseball and it traveled 120 feet toward the fence. On his second turn, he hit the ball again straight toward the fence and it traveled two-thirds as far as it did on his first turn, but the ball ended up 30 feet beyond the fence. What's the distance from where Jake was to the fence?
If you buy 4 t-shirts at $25 each and get a fifth one for free, how much do you pay in total?
Jason is mixing red, blue and yellow paint in a 3 : 2 : 6 ratio. If he uses 18 gallons of yellow paint, how many gallons of paint does he use total?
Andy runs a car rental business which has a fleet of 12 cars. He had 3 cars rented out today, and 2 got returned. How many cars does he have available for rent now?

A bakery sold a dozen doughnuts for $6.25. Bob wanted to buy 3 dozen doughnuts. The bakery also had a special promotion going on where anyone who buys at least 2 dozen doughnuts gets a 10% discount. How much did Bob have to pay for the doughnuts?

A train travels at a speed of 80 km/hour. It has to reach a city which is 240 km away. If the train speed is reduced by half because of heavy rain, how much time will it take the train to reach the city?

Julia has an e-commerce business. She spends $10 on marketing per product and sells each product for $50. If she wants to sell 200 products per month, how much does she have to spend on marketing expenses?
A vase can hold 4 glasses of water. How many glasses of water are needed to fill 7 vases and 8 half-capacity vases?

A library has 60 books, 25% of which are fiction and the remaining are non-fiction. If one-third of the fiction books are thrillers and half of the non-fiction books are history, how many books are thrillers and history?
Anna sells lemonade at $5 per glass. She sold 12 glasses of lemonade today and her mother gave her an additional $10. She then spends $15 on new lemons and sugar. How much money does Anna have left?
A carpet of 5 by 7 feet needs to be installed in a room. If the carpet cost $15 per square foot, how much will the cost total?
A baker makes 15 doughnuts every hour. He starts baking at 8 AM and finishes at 5 PM. How many doughnuts does the baker make in one day?
An apple shop had 85 red apples and 120 green apples. They sold 55 red apples and 83 green apples in a day. How many apples are remaining in the shop?
Lily has two brothers. The first brother is thrice as old as Lily. The first brother is 2 years younger than Lily. Their average age is 3. How old is Lily?
Laura had ten books, Clara had eight books, and Julie had six books. If they share the books equally, how many will each person get?
Emma works at a flower shop. They sell roses for $3 each and tulips for $2 each. Emma sold 15 roses and 10 tulips today. How much money did she make?
Jason has a car that gets 20 miles per gallon of gas. He drives his car 5 hours a day at a speed of 60 miles per hour. If gas costs $2.50 per gallon, how much will Jason spend on gas in a week?
Sarah has 80 roses and John has triple the number of roses as Sarah plus another 20. How many roses must Sarah buy to have 34 fewer roses than John?

James decides to try and collect rare artifacts in the field.  He spends 6 months researching before going on a 2-year-long expedition for his first find.  The second artifact takes 3 times as long for the research and discovery.  How long did it take him to find both?
Lisa goes shopping for a discount sale. The sale says buy 2 get 2 free. The price is $25 per dress and she buys 16 dresses. How much did she pay for the dresses?

An employee earns $35 an hour for the first 35 hours in a week and gets a 60% increase for every hour worked above 35 in the week. If he works 7 hours for the first 4 days and 9 hours per day for the remaining 3 days, how much does he earn in a week?
A tree had 50 apples. Over a week, 30% of the apples fell off. How many apples are remaining on the tree?
Daniel drives a truck for a delivery company. He can deliver 8 packages in an hour. Each package earns him $5. If he works 6 hours a day, how much money does he make in a day?
Brad wants to buy a limited edition of a book, that costs $75.00. He has saved $20.00 so far. If Brad earns $6.50 on his weekend job, how many weekends must he work to buy the book?

Jill and Jack went to pick blueberries at a local farm. Jill picked 260 blueberries, while Jack picked half as many as Jill. After, they sold the blueberries to earn some money. Jill sold each of her blueberries for 10 cents while Jack sold each of his blueberries for twice as much as Jill. How much money did Jill and Jack earn in total?
A tree grows 15 inches per year. Assuming each year there is 10% growth, how tall will the tree be after 3 years?
Peter has 4 more than 3 times the number of books Paul has. If there are 31 books total, how many does Paul have?

Jake is a professional artist who is currently engaged in a big project. He is painting a mural that measures 20 feet by 30 feet and is using three colors for the project, each with the same area coverage. One can of paint can cover an area of 100 square feet. How many cans of each color of paint will he need?
James wants to buy a jacket that costs $100. He saves $5 every week. How many weeks will it take him to buy the jacket?
Michael has 7 pets. He spends $50 a month on each pet for food. How much does he spend on pet food in a year?
Paco gets 5 new toys at the start of the week. After 3 weeks, he has 3 toys in his basket and has hidden the others. How many toys has he hidden?
A school conducted a spelling contest. There were 30 participants in total. Of these, 1/6 were 7th graders and the rest were 6th graders. How many sixth graders participated in the contest?

A lemon tree has 150 lemons. Bob picks 1/3 of that number for use in their home. After a few days, he goes back and picks 30 more lemons, and the next day picks double that number. What's the total number of fruits remaining on the lemon tree?

Linda wanted to sell all her handcrafted jewelry before closing for the night. She reduced her $10.00 necklaces and her $5.00 earrings by half. How much money did she make if she sold the last 20 necklaces and 15 earrings?
A rectangular garden has double the width compared to its length. If the total area of the garden is 600 square meters, what is the length of the garden?

A designer is preparing for two fashion shows. He has to create 210 designs for the first show and 230 designs for the second show. If he works on the same amount every day for 30 days, how many designs should he aim to finish per day?

Alice plans to buy a camera which costs $500. She has only $300 and decides to borrow the rest of the money from her friend with a 5% interest rate. How much will she have to pay in total for the camera?

Emily's cat had a litter of 6 kittens. She decided to give away three-fourths of them. Then, she adopted one of the remaining kittens. Lastly, she sold each remaining kitten for $500. She also gifted half the amount to a pet shelter. How much money did she retain?

Kevin is injured at his work and files a lawsuit for loss of a $60,000 annual salary for 40 years, $300,000 in medical bills, and punitive damages equal to double the medical and salary damages. If he gets 75% of what he's asking for, how much money does he get?
Sally's car has a fuel tank of 15 gallons. After driving to and from the grocery store, which is 12 miles away, she has half of a tank left. How many miles per gallon does her car get?
Josh has 4 cars, 3 motorcycles, and 2 bicycles in his garage. Each car has 4 wheels, each motorcycle has 2 wheels and each bicycle has 2 wheels as well. How many wheels are in total?
Emily wants to buy a new smartphone which costs $510. She already has saved $180. How much more does she need to save to buy the smartphone?
Emily sold 30 cupcakes; James sold 23 more than her. Angelina baked half of the cupcakes James sold. What is the total number of cupcakes they sold?
Emma gets rewarded $2 for each book she reads in a week. If she read 3 books this week and promises to read 2 more books next week, how much will she earn in total?
At a zoo, there are 4 parrots and 3 peacocks. Each parrot can say 8 words and each peacock can say 12 words. How many words in total can all the birds say?

A garden has 6 apple trees. Each apple tree produced 32 apples this year. If all the apples can be gathered into bags, and each bag can hold 8 apples, how many bags can be filled using all the apples from the garden?
Carol baked 6 cupcakes. She baked twice as many cookies as cupcakes, and three times as many muffins as cookies. How many baked goods in total did Carol bake?
A bicycle travels at a speed of 10 miles per hour. How long, in hours, will it take to travel a distance of 70 miles?

Lucy has a cubic block of gold that measures 4 inches on each side. A cubic inch of gold weighs 8 ounces. Each ounce of gold sells for $50. She sells it for 120% of its gold value. How much does she sell it for?
A company has 150 employees. Every year, 20 employees leave the company and 30 new employees are hired. How many employees will the company have in 5 years?
Dick drills three holes on a block of wood each day. If he drilled 18 holes by Wednesday, skips Thursday and drills 9 more holes on Friday, How many holes did he drill over the week?
Lucy and her two sisters want to buy a gift for their mother. The gift costs $150. If each one of them pays an equal amount, how much does each one have to pay?

Emma is looking to purchase some school supplies for the new semester. She requires six yellow highlighters and 12 blue highlighters. If a yellow highlighter costs twenty cents and a blue highlighter costs three times as much as the yellow highlighter, how much money does Emma require for the highlighters?

Tim decides to run a campaign for governor.  He gets the maximum $1200 donation from 500 people, and three times as many people donated half this sum of money to his campaign.  These donations together accounted for 40% of the total money that Tim raised.  How much total money did he raise?

A toy factory produces 500 toy cars. During the first day, 2/5 of the toys are sold, and 1/5 of the remaining toys are sold the next day. If half of the remaining toys are sold on the third day, calculate how many toys are left.

A theater recorded a total of 400 moviegoers in the past three weekends. The first weekend, there were 120 moviegoers. The second weekend had 30 more moviegoers than the first weekend. How many moviegoers were there in the third weekend?
John planted 30 more trees than Adam in the city park. If Adam planted 10 trees, how many trees did they plant in total?
Billy has a weekly allowance of $10. He saves 25% of his weekly allowance and spends the rest on comic books. How much does he spend on comic books over a year?
At a birthday party, Molly received 5 stuffed animals. Her friend Lisa gave her twice as many stuffed animals as she already had. How many stuffed animals does Molly have in total?
At a grocery store, for every 5 oranges you buy, you get 2 free. If Emily bought 20 oranges, how many oranges did she get for free?

On national superhero day, one third of the students who attended class came wearing superhero t-shirts. Of those students who wore superhero t-shirts, one half wore Superman shirts. There were one-fourth as many students wearing Batman shirts as were wearing Superman shirts. If 240 students attended school that day, how many wore Batman shirts?

A shoe factory produced 200 pairs in the first month, 250 in the second month, and 350 in the third month. If the company produced their average number of shoes from the first 3 months during the fourth month, how many total pairs will they produce?

David has a rectangular warehouse that measures 25 meters by 30 meters. He decides to paint 5 meters of the ceiling and 6 meters of the floor of the warehouse red. Furthermore, he paints 3 meters of the ceiling and 4 meters of the floor blue. The rest he decides to color yellow. How many square meters of the warehouse does David color yellow?
In a party, each person drank 3 cups of soda. If the party had 8 people, and each cup of soda costs $2, how much was spent in total on soda?
Peter has 5 apples. He eats 2 apples and gives 1 to his friend. How many apples does he have left?

Jessica has 5 dogs and she just adopted 3 more. Her best friend, Reina, has double the number of dogs Jessica had before she adopted the new ones. If they take all of their dogs for a walk, how many dogs will be walking?
Tara has a dozen cats. Each cat consumes 1/4 pound of cat food three times a day. Tara initially bought 25 pounds of cat food, how many pounds of cat food are left after a week?

For a summer vacation, Megan needs a hat, sunglasses, and a new bathing suit. The hat costs $5.50, the sunglasses cost $6.75, and the bathingsuit costs $19.95. Megan only has $25. How much more money does Megan need?
Greg and Marc go out for lunch. They order two burgers and one pizza. The burgers have 4 slices each and the pizza has 12 slices. They each eat 7 slices. How many slices are left?
Jenna has a box of candies that contains 24 candies. She gives 6 candies to her brother and 4 candies to her sister. How many candies does Jenna have left in her box?
A school has 500 students, 40% of which are girls. How many are boys?
Jessica has twice as many books as Emily does. Emily has 12 books. Jessica's books cost an average of $10 each. How much is Jessica's book collection worth?
John ran at a speed of 7 mph for 15 minutes and then walked at a speed of 3 mph for 30 minutes. How far did he travel in total?

A group of friends went to the movies. They bought 5 tickets for adults and 3 tickets for children. An adult ticket costs $15 and a child ticket is half the price. How much did all the tickets cost in total?
Andrew picks 3 apples from his apple tree every day. After 2 weeks, how many apples will Andrew have picked?

Mike can swim 5 miles an hour. His friend Bob can swim 7 miles an hour. If they start from the same place and swim in straight lines in opposite directions for 2 hours, how many miles apart do they end up?

A tennis player plans to practice by serving 150 shots 3 days a week. He is able to recover 25% of his tennis balls. The tennis balls he uses cost $3.5 per ball. His team agrees to pay for 75% of the cost of his tennis balls. How much does he spend for tennis balls a week?
Helen made 8 bracelets. Each bracelet used 15 beads. How many beads did Helen use?
Bob is stacking boxes. He starts with 15 boxes. He then removes 4 boxes, and adds 5 more boxes. Then, he gives 3 boxes to his friend, Joe. How many boxes, in total, does Bob have left?
David spent $800 on 8 pieces of clothing. One piece was $50 and another piece was $100. If the other pieces were all the same price, how many dollars was one of the other pieces?

Jim witnessed a car accident on his walk home less than an hour ago. The accident occurred 25 minutes from his house and it took him 15 more minutes to get home. How long ago was the accident from now?
Paul has $90. He spends $30 on a book, $15 on lunch, and the rest on a video game. How much did the video game cost?

Linda is knitting scarves for a charity event. On Sunday, she knits 1 scarf. Every day for the next week, she knits two more scarves than she did the day before. If she has 15 scarves left to donate by Saturday, how many did she originally knit?
Jamie got a bottle of 100 capsules. He takes 4 capsules a day for 5 days, then reduces it to half for another 7 days. How many capsules would be left in the bottle?
Lana reads 15 pages of a novel on weekdays and 25 pages on weekends. If it takes her 3 weeks to complete the novel, how many pages does the novel have?
A school spent 60% of its $5000 monthly budget on teachers' salaries. If the remaining budget was equally split between IT and cleaning services, how much did the school spend on cleaning services?
May reads 10 pages in her book every day. After 10 days, how many pages should she have read?

A car travels at a speed of 15 MPH. It takes 3 hours to reach a city across the length of the highway, then travels the width of the city at the same speed which takes 45 minutes. What is the area of the city in square miles?

Mrs. Denton bought cloth to make her tablecloth. She requires 15 yards of cloth. If the price is $10 per yard and the seller of the yardage gives a discount of 5% for every yard purchased, how much money does Mrs. Denton save?
Susan baked 5 pans of brownies for a school fair. Each pan is cut into 8 pieces. Each piece of brownie is sold for $2. How much money will Susan make if all pieces are sold?
Jenny is 4 years older than her brother Tim. If Tim is 7 years old, how old is Jenny?
A mall has 100 parking spaces. If 55 cars have parked and 2/5 of the remaining spaces are filled, how many parking spaces are available in the mall?
A forest had 5000 trees before a forest fire which burnt 1/3 of the trees. After the fire, a recovery project planted 25% more trees than what were burnt. How many trees are there in the forest now?
The average biking speed for a 2 hour ride is 12 miles per hour. If Allison wanted to bike thrice as fast for 3 hours, how many miles will she cover?

A craft store sells three types of beads: small, medium, and large. Small beads cost $2 per pack, medium beads cost $3 per pack, and large beads cost $4 per pack. Mary went shopping at the store and bought 5 packs of small beads, 3 packs of medium beads, and 2 packs of large beads. How much did Mary spend?
Miles wants to cook pancakes for his friends. The recipe he wants to use needs 4 eggs for every 5 pancakes. If Miles has 16 eggs, how many pancakes can he make?
Thomas has a construction company and he used 4/5 of his budget for labor cost which amounts to $25000. How much is his total budget?
One entrance ticket to the zoo costs $5. In one hour, the zoo sells an average of 20 such tickets. What will be the revenue from selling tickets in 3 hours?

Beth ordered 10 yards of fabric for her clothing business from a supplier. Each yard costs $5. She used 2 yards to make a dress which she sold for $30. How much profit did she make from selling the dress?

Ethan and Olivia are saving money to buy a new game console. They already saved $45 together. The game console costs $200. If Ethan can save $25 per month and Olivia can save $15 per month, how many months will it take for them to buy the game console?
Kimberly had 27 books on her shelf. She went to the bookstore and bought 13 more books. Then, she decided to donate 5 books to a local library. How many books does Kimberly have now?
Sophia earned $40,000 from her startup. After spending $12,000 on operational costs and $5,000 on marketing, how much was her net earnings?
The ratio of red to green marbles in a box is 2:3. If there are 40 red marbles, how many green marbles are there?
Sara earns $15 washing cars and $30 mowing lawns each week. If she saves 1/3 of her total earnings each week, how long would it take her to save up $50?
George collects stamps. He has 25 stamps from Europe and 35 from Asia. If each stamp costs $3, how much did he spent on his entire stamp collection?
A candy factory makes boxes of candy. Each box contains 10 bags of candy, and there are 5 candies in each bag. Each crate contains 8 boxes of candy. How many candies are there in 7 crates?
In a class of 25 students, the teacher distributed chocolates between them in the ratio 2:3 boys to girls respectively. If there are 150 chocolates, how many did the girls receive?

John has a piggy bank. He saves $1 every day for the first week. For the next week, he doubled his savings and saved $2 each day. And for the following week he tripled his initial saving, saving $3 each day. How much money did John save in the piggy bank over the three weeks?

Jake decided to bake cookies for a charity event. He buys 7 pounds of chocolate chips at $3.00 per pound. He also buys 4 pounds of sugar at $1.50 a pound, 5 pounds of flour at $2.00 a pound, and 2 pounds of butter at $4.00 a pound. If all ingredients yield 8 batches of cookies, how much does it cost to make each batch?
Liam is 2 inches taller than Noah. Noah is 5 inches taller than Ethan. If Ethan is 60 inches tall, how tall is Liam in inches?
Sarah bought 2 pairs of shoes a month at $30 each.  She sells them back at the end of the year for $400.  How much money did she lose?
Alex likes to swim for sport and he does 8 laps per session. If Alex wants to swim a minimum of 200 laps in a month, how many sessions should he attend?

Lola loves to send letters. She bought twenty-five post stamps. She used five stamps to send postcards to her friends, eight stamps to send letters to her relatives, and dies to keep the rest. How many post stamps did Lola keep?
In order to stay fit, I run a mile every day. I plan to run an additional half mile more than the previous day. If I continue this for four consecutive days, how far will I run on the fourth day?

The basketball team has played 15 games and won 9 of them. To make the playoffs, they need to win 60% of their games over the season. If there are 5 games left, how many do they have to win to make the playoffs?
Bob earns $10 per hour for the first 40 hours in a week. For each hour above 40, he is paid 1.5 times his regular rate. If Bob worked 55 hours in a week, how much money did he earn?
A train travels at a rate of 90 miles per hour for 6 hours. How many miles does the train travel?

A wall is being built with 5 rows of bricks, with each row containing 7 bricks. Each brick has a length of 10 inches and a width of 5 inches. What is the total area of the bricks used in square inches?

In Dana's senior high school class there were 200 students.  60% of the students were female, and 50% of the females were brunettes.  If 50% of the female brunettes were under 5 feet tall, then how many female brunettes in Dana's high school senior class were under 5 feet tall?
Sarah has a vegetable garden where she grows tomatoes and cabbages. If she has 25 tomatoes and 4 times as many cabbages, how many cabbages does she have?
Christine is three times as old as her daughter. Her husband is 5 years older than her. Her son is half the age of her daughter. If Christine is 36 years old, how old is her son?

Paul plays games on his phone for 3 hours every day. On the days he chats with his friends online, he chats for twice as long as he plays games. If he chats online 2 days a week how long does he spend on playing games and chatting?

Ria works at a bakery and she had 48 bread loaves to deliver this morning. she managed to deliver two-thirds of her bread loaves by noon. She still has another 11 bread loaves to deliver in the evening and a few went missing. How many bread loaves are missing?
A printer prints at a rate of 40 pages per minute. If Jack has to print a 120-page document, 2 copies of a 90-page file, and 3 duplicates of a 60-page statement, how many minutes will it take?
A book club has 35 members and each member recommends 3 books for the club to read. Each book costs $15. How much will it cost for the club to purchase all recommended books?
A baker needs to bake cookies for a party. He has to bake 10 cookies for each of the 20 guests and each cookie takes 3 minutes to bake. How long will it take the baker to make all the cookies?
Lucy is a beekeeper. Each honey jar she sells costs $3.75. If she wants to earn $100 by selling jars, how many jars does she need to sell?
Kate has 2 bird cages. 1 of the cages has 15 birds in it and the other one has thrice as many birds as the first. How many total birds does she have?
If a beehive produces 20 pounds of honey in a month and the beekeeper sells 75% of the total production each month. How many pounds of honey does he have after three months?
Lisa loves planting roses in her garden. She has 5 pink roses, 6 red roses and 2 white roses. Lisa then added 3 more red roses in her garden. How many roses does Lisa now have in her garden?

A recipe for banana muffins requires 3 bananas to make 12 muffins. If Emily has 15 bananas, how many muffins can she make?
Laura visits a toy store where she buy 5 toy cars each costing $6. She also buys 4 teddy bears each priced at $8 and a robot toy costing $15. What is the total amount Laura spent on toys?
Jacob works at a bakery. He bakes loaves of bread and sells them for $3 each. In three days, he makes $315. How many loaves on average did he sell each day?

Paul worked part-time for a library. Each day he could sort 37 books in the adult section and 54 books in the children's section. After five days of work, he decided to donate half of his earnings to a charity. If each book sorted earned him $1, how much did he donate?
Peter has a 14-meter rope and cuts it into two equal parts. If each linear meter of the rope weighs 120 kilograms, how much does each cut piece weigh?
A bus leaves the station every 10 minutes starting from 7 am. If a passenger arrives at the bus station at 7:40 am, how many buses has he missed?
Jack used all of his stickers today. He used half of them on his notebook. He used 20 stickers on his backpack and another 30 on his lunchbox. How many stickers did Jack use today?
Samantha has 10 cakes. Each cake can be sliced into 8 pieces. How many pieces does Samantha have if she slices all the cakes?

A shoe store has 200 pairs of shoes. When the shoes go on sale, 50 pairs are sold in the first hour, and in the second hour twice as many pairs are sold as in the first hour. How many pairs of shoes are left in the store after the second hour?

Kelly has saved $600 for her vacation. She plans on spending $75 per day. She ends up spending an extra $150 on a scuba diving trip. How much money does she have left after her vacation if her vacation was 7 days long?
Tom has a job mowing lawns. He earns $15 per lawn and mows 4 lawns per week. How much does he earn after 3 weeks?
Jake borrowed $150 from his friend. His friend charges an interest of 3% every month. If Jake plans to pay back after 3 months, how much in total including the interest will he have to pay?
Hannah found a 50-percent discount sale on bags. She bought 3 bags, and each originally cost $60. How much did she end up spending?

Liam can read a book in 4 days, while Noah takes 3 days longer than Liam to finish the same book. If Liam reads a book and Noah starts reading the same book the next day, how many days will it take for both to finish the book?
In a school, there are 60 students in a class. If 30% of the students are girls, how many boys are there in the class?
Sarah is asking for donations for a charity. If the average person donates $20, how many people will she need to ask to raise $10,000 if she already has $2,000?
A restaurant sold 180 bottles of red wine this month, the number of which made up 45% of the total wine sold. How many bottles of wine did the restaurant sell this month?
A city recycles 75% of its waste. If the city produced 1200 tons of waste, how much was not recycled?

Lucy paints and sells her artworks for a living. She can create 25 paintings in a day and she sells each painting for $10. If she managed to sell every painting she made for 4 days, how much did she earn?

A construction company has four trucks to transport sand. Each truck carries five containers where each container can hold 200 kilograms of sand. How many kilograms of sand in total can the company transport in its trucks?

Graham has $350. He wants to buy a skateboard that costs $150 and a helmet that costs $50. If he decides to also buy knee pads for $25 and elbow pads for $20, how much money will he have left after his purchases?
Ralph decided to work his yard today. He planted 5 trees every hour for 4 hours. After that, he planted 3 bushes every 15 minutes for an hour. How many trees and bushes did Ralph plant in total?
Lorenzo bought a jacket and a pair of shoes. The price of the jacket was two times the price of the shoes. If the shoes cost $25, how much did Lorenzo spend on his shopping?

Tony goes on 5 rollercoasters while he is at the park. The first went 50 miles per hour. The second went 62 miles per hour. The third when 73 miles per hour. The fourth when 70 miles per hour. His average speed during the day was 59 miles per hour. How fast was the fifth coaster?
The Stallions played 100 games this season. They had 30 losses and three times as many wins. How many games did they draw?
Billy baked 30 brown cookies and 20 white cookies. He ate 10 white cookies and twice as many brown cookies. How many cookies does he still have?

Julia earns $10 per hour working as a part-time employee in a restaurant. She works 4 hours a day, 6 days a week. If she needs to save $240 to buy a new laptop, how many weeks does she need to work to afford the laptop?
Each glass of smoothie requires 3 strawberries. If you have 24 strawberries, how many glasses of smoothie can you make?
Susan read 75 pages of a book on Monday, 90 pages on Tuesday, and 120 pages on Wednesday. If the book has 400 pages, how many pages does she still have to read?

Jill and Jack are reading for their school's classic literature club. Jill reads paperbacks and Jack reads hardcovers, so the page thickness differs. They find that 1 inch equals 4 pages for Jill and 6 pages for Jack. If Jill's pile of books is 560 inches tall and Jack's is 430 inches tall, who has more pages and how many?

A city experiences a sudden heavy snowstorm lasting for 3 days. An area that was supposed to collect snow to avoid disturbance to the city couldn't function the 3rd day. The area can accumulate the equivalent of 10 inches of snow. It can also melt away the equivalent of 4 inches of snow per day to the drains. The first day, it snowed 7 inches. The second day, it snowed thrice as much as the first day. The area fails to melt any snow on the third day. What is the minimum amount it snowed on the third day?
Sean has 200 pages to read for his English class and 120 pages for his History class. He reads 30 pages a day. How long will it take Sean to finish his readings?

Liam and his friend went out for dinner. They each ordered a burger for $4.5. He ordered a soda for $1.5 and his friend ordered a juice for $2. They split a large pizza that cost $10. His friend also ordered a dessert that cost $3. The tax was $1.5. If they agreed to split the bill, and Liam had $25, how much change is he bringing home?
The total population of a village is 1500. If 60% of the population are males, how many females are there in the village?
A train journey costs $15 per hour. If John wants to travel a distance that will take 5 hours, how much will he have to pay?

There is a marathon race of 4 miles from the starting point towards the finishing line. Every 30 minutes, a runner finishes the marathon. How many hours have passed once 45 runners have finished the marathon?

Emma has $500 in her bank account. She wants to buy a new laptop which costs $1500. Her grandma gifts her $200 and her parents give her $300. How much more money does Emma need to buy her new laptop?
Linda is 40 years old. Three years from now, her son will be half her age. How old is Linda's son now?
A book club has 30 members. Each member reads 5 books per month. If each member keeps up their reading rate, how many books will have been read collectively by the club members in one year?
Anna planted 7 apple trees and each tree can produce 30 apples. However, 2 trees died and she had to give 3 apples from each remaining tree to her friend. How many apples does Anna have now?
Lisa bought 5 bags of candies for a party. Each bag contains 25 candies. During the party, Lisa's younger brother sneaked in and ate 10 candies. How many candies are left?

Bill has twice as many oranges as Jim. If Jim has 12 oranges, how many oranges does Bill have?

Sammy has a lemonade stand. She sells her lemonade for $0.50 a cup and she also offers cookies for $1.00 each. On her first day, she sold 20 cups of lemonade and 10 cookies. How much did she make?

A school has 1820 students and plans to move 30% to a new branch. Each grade level is divided into 5 classes at the new school. Each class will have 25 students and rest will form an additional special class per grade. How many students will be there in each special class at the new branch?

Kristin plants 8 flowers in each of her 5 flower beds. How many flowers has she planted in total?

Oliver's rectangular garden has a length of 20 feet and a width of 10 feet. If Oliver decides to make the garden twice as long and the width to remain the same, what would be the new area of the rectangular garden?
Eva runs at a speed of 5 miles an hour. If she runs for 2 hours every day from Monday to Friday, how many miles does she run in total?
Thomas bought 3 comic books each costing $5 and 2 novels each costing $12. He also paid $10 for a service charge. How much did he spend in total?

Elliot has written 3 novels and each novel contains around 600 pages. If each novel sells 12,000 copies worldwide, how many pages are read worldwide?

A basketball slam dunk contest comprised of four competitors. The first competitor dunked the ball at a height of 9 feet. The second competitor dunked one foot higher than the first, the third competitor dunked a foot lower than the second, and the fourth dunked two feet higher than the third. How high, in feet, did the fourth competitor dunk the ball?
John is 6'2".  He grows 4 inches.  How tall is he now in inches?
Dawn arranged her books in 6 shelves. In each shelf, there are 9 books. How many books did she arrange?

Hunter needs to make a square patch of land whose perimeter is twice as large as a rectangular patch of land. If the rectangular patch of land has a length of 400 feet and a width of 300 feet, calculate the length of one side of the square patch of land.
Katherine works at a bookstore and earns a 15% commission from selling each book. If she sells $85 worth of books in a day, how much does she earn that day?

In a week, Harry collected 120 stamps for his collection. On Monday, he collected 10, and on Tuesday he collected twice as many as Monday. On Wednesday, he collected 3 less than twice he collected on Tuesday. On Thursday, he collected the same amount as Wednesday. For the remaining days of the week, how many stamps did he collect each day if it the amount is the same every day?
Aaliyah is a singer. She was the lead singer in 75% of her performances. In total, Aaliyah performed in 200 concerts. How many times was Aaliyah not the lead singer?

Amelia and Noah have a bakery. Amelia bakes 4 loaves of bread, and Noah makes 2 cakes per day. If each loaf uses 3 cups of flour and a cake uses 6 cups of flour, how many cups of flour do they need every 5 days?
Jeremy has 9 boxes of cookies. Each box has 8 cookies, and each cookie costs $2. How much did he spend on cookies in total?
Lucia has 5 bags and each bag contains 30 candies. She gave 2 bags to her friends. How many candies does Lucia have left?
Peter has $200 in his savings account. He adds $50 every month. After 6 months, he withdraws $100 to buy a new gadget. How much money does he have in his account now?
A box contains 12 red apples and 18 green apples. If Alan takes two red apples and four green apples from the box, what percentage of the remaining apples in the box are green?
Mia and her friend Alice went shopping for dresses. Mia bought 2 dresses, each costing $30, and Alice bought 3 dresses, each costing $45. How much money did they spend together?

Mia is a beekeeper. She has two types of beehives. 40% are type A and 60% are type B. The A type gives her 15 pounds of honey and 50% is good. Type B gives her 20 pounds and 1/2 is good. If she gets 130 pounds of good honey per month, how many total beehives does she have?
In Lisa's school, there are 8 classes and in each class there are 25 students. If each student brings 2 family members to the school ceremony, how many people will be at the event?

A group of friends ordered a large pizza. The pizza was divided into 10 equal parts. Mark ate 3 parts, John ate 2 parts, and the rest of the pizza was shared equally among the remaining 3 friends. How much did each of these three friends eat?
Jack runs a car rental shop. He rents out cars for $45 per day. In the last month, he was able to rent out his cars for a total of 87 days. How much did he earn in that month?

Jessica earns $20 per hour. If she works for 5 hours per day, 5 days a week for a month, how much does Jessica earn in the month?
A jar contains 10 red marbles, 15 blue marbles and 5 green marbles. If a marble is taken out at random, what is the probability of picking a red marble?

James is packing boxes of candles. Each box can contain up to 20 pounds. He has both 2 pound and 5 pound candles. An order requires equal quantities of both sizes. How many boxes can James prepare if he has three boxes?

Mason purchases a puzzle that has 500 pieces. He completes 1/5 of the puzzle on the first day. On the second day, he completes 3 times as many pieces as he did on the first day. How many pieces does Mason have left to finish?

Billy has a vegetable garden where he plants tomatoes and cucumbers. Each tomato plant yields 20 tomatoes and each cucumber plant yields 15 cucumbers. If Billy has 5 tomato plants and 4 cucumber plants, how many vegetables will Billy have after the harvest?

A shoe store sold 18 pairs of shoes on Monday and twice as many on Tuesday. How many pairs of shoes did the store sell in total?
Sally wants to bake muffins for her friends. She has a recipe for 12 muffins but needs to make triple the amount. If one recipe requires 2 eggs, how many eggs will she need?
A rectangular playground is 20 meters long and 15 meters wide. What is the area of the playground?
Lucy fills a 3-gallon watering can with water to use in her garden. The can is 80% full. If water has a weight of 8 pounds per gallon, how many pounds does the watering can weigh?
Rick can bake 3 pies in an hour, while his sister, Anna, can bake 2 pies in the same amount of time. How many pies can Rick and Anna bake together in 4 hours?

George watches five TV shows. The first show is 4 minutes long, the second show is 5 minutes and 30 seconds, and the remaining three shows are of equal length. If he spends a total of 890 seconds watching TV, how many seconds long was each of the last three shows?

Bryan's family decided to go on a camping trip. They drove 150 miles to the campground and then 150 miles back home. During the trip, they drove an additional 60 miles to visit a nearby tourist site. How many total miles did they drive?
Martin is making cupcakes. He needs 5 eggs for a batch of 12 cupcakes. If he wants to make 84 cupcakes, how many eggs does he need?
Each tree at a Christmas tree farm produces 8 cones. There are 15 trees on the farm. If every cone is sold for $3, how much money will the farm make by selling all the cones?

John has a fruit orchard with 60 apple trees and 45 peach trees. He planted 15 new apricot trees this summer. However, a disease killed 7 of his apple trees. How many trees does John currently have in his orchard?

Edward has read 40 books this year.  Stella has read 1/2 the amount of books that Edward has read.  Nancy has read 11 more than three times the number of books that Stella has read.  How many books total have Edward, Stella, and Nancy read?
In a quiz game, a correct answer gets 5 points and a wrong answer loses 2 points. If Peter answers 20 questions correctly and 3 questions wrong, what is his final score?
Sam looks at his wall clock and notes that the time is 2:45 pm. He continues to play video games until 10:30 pm. How many minutes did Sam spend playing video games?
Sharon is cooking a big batch of her special spaghetti sauce. If she uses 3 liters of tomato sauce and 1 liter of water, how many 250 ml servings can she make?
Samson buys an antique for $300. He marks up the price by 60% before reselling it. How much does he sell the antique for?
Tommy has a vending machine business. He pricing each bottle of soft drinks at $1.50 or 10 bottles for $10. If Robert wants to purchase a total of 30 bottles, how much will Robert have to pay?

Anna started a monthly book club with friends. On the first month they read 2 books, on the second month they read 4 books, on the third month they read 6 books. If the pattern continues, how many books will they have read in total after the 8th month?

A coach has to order equipment for a game. Each player needs a ball, a helmet and a pair of gloves. Balls are $10 each, a helmet is $20 and a pair of gloves is $5. The coach has 15 players in the team. How much will the equipment cost?
A bus can carry 40 passengers. A plane can carry 250 passengers. How many more passengers can 3 buses carry than one airplane?

John is trying a new way to finish his dinner quickly. He keeps a record of his time for the week and on Monday it takes him 3 minutes. On Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday, it takes him 4 minutes. Before Friday arrives, he looks at his old way of dining and sees that it was taking him 4 minutes on average to dine. How quickly does he have to finish his dinner on Friday so his weekly average matches his old method?
A bookshop sells hardcover and paperback books. A hardcover book costs $20 and a paperback book costs $10. If Mary buys 3 hardcover books and 5 paperback books, how much does she spend?
Lila spends 3 hours every day painting. She sells her paintings at $100 each, and she paints one painting per hour. How much would Lila make if she sold all her paintings for 2 weeks?
Raymond has a stash of 120 candies. He gives half of it to his sister. If he plans to divide the remaining candies equally between his four best friends, how many candies will each friend get?
Susan worked 7 hours a day from the 1st to the 6th, including the 1st and not including the 6th. How many hours did she work?
Tim is 15 pounds lighter than Bob. Bob is 12 pounds heavier than Mike. If Sam weighs 130 pounds and is 32 pounds lighter than Tim, find the weight of Mike.
Each box contains 10 oranges and each orange costs $2. If Jake buys 5 boxes, how much money does he spend?
Jeremy harvested 4 baskets of apples. Each basket contains 8 apples. He sold each apple for $2. How much will he earn in total?
Tom decided to cycle to his friend's house and back. He cycled 20 miles to get there and it took him 4 hours. How many miles did Tom cycle in total?
Jake has $45. He spends $8 on a movie ticket and another $12 on popcorn and a drink. How much does Jake have left?
A store keeper had some apples. He sold 30% of the apples one day and sold another 25% of the remaining apples on the next day. If he then had 60 apples left, how many apples did he originally have?
Sally bought a skirt that costs $50 and got a 25% discount. She also bought a top that costs $20 and paid it full price. How much did she spend in total?
Bella has 120 books in her library. 30 books are about history and 90 books are novels. If 60% of the history books and 40% of the novels are hardcovers, how many paperback books does she have?

A toy store is selling a limited edition toy car for $75. Each car comes with 5 cars free if it is bought at its original price. John decides to buy 3 cars. He gets 5 free cars with each car. How much is each car actually costing John?
A soda drink costs $5 and the fries cost $8. A student gets a 20% discount on this total if they buy them together. What is the final price?
Dana is writing a book. The maximum word limit is 50,000 words. She wrote 28,000 words during the first two months and 24,000 words in the next two months. By how many words did she exceed the limit?
In a school, there are 4 classes each having 20 students. If two pens are distributed to each student, how many pens are distributed in total?

Mark has a garden, in his garden he has 3 apple trees and 2 lemon trees that need 1.5 gallons of water a day. He also has 6 tomato plants that need 2 gallons of water a day. How many gallons of water does he need to water the plants for a day?
Sarah and John took a taxi to their hotel. The base fare was $3, and it cost $1 per mile. If their hotel was 23 miles away, how much was their total taxi fare?

Alan has twice as many postcards as Jeff. Jeff has three times the number of postcards as Stacy. Stacy has 10 fewer postcards than Linda. How many postcards do they have together if Linda has 50 postcards?
A jar contains 65 marbles. If 3/5 of the marbles are red, how many marbles are not red?
A baseball team played 75 home games this season. They had 15 losses and three times as many wins. How many games ended in a tie?
A group of 45 people are going on a trip. They will travel in cars that can carry 6 people each. The trip organizer wants to know how many cars they will need. Help him figure out!

Josh has 600 pages to read for his English literature assignment. After reading for 2 hours, he has read one fifth of the book. If he reads at a constant pace, how many pages can Josh read in one hour?
Peter repairs 7 bicycles.  4 of them take 30 minutes each to repair. The remaining ones take 30% longer each to repair. He makes $15 per hour repairing the bicycles. How much money does he make?

Emma and her friends are going camping. Emma brings 16 marshmallows, Mia brings twice as many, and Lily brings half the number of marshmallows Emma brings. How many marshmallows do they have in total?
John has a collection of 50 comic books. His friend Mike gave him 10 more comic books and their mutual friend Sarah gave him twice as much as Mike. How many comic books does John have now?
In a class, there are seven girls and boys combined. Girls are twice as many as boys. How many boys are in the class?

Samantha has a flower shop where she sells roses. She sold 10 roses in the morning and then sold 2 times more roses in the afternoon than in the morning. If she had 100 roses at the beginning of the day, how many roses does she have left?
Maria bakes a cake that weighs 7 kilograms in total. The cake has 2 kilograms of flour, 1 kilogram of sugar, and 2 kilograms of butter. How many kilograms are the remaining ingredients of the cake?
Tom has 2 tarantulas and 40 beetles as pets. Each tarantula has 8 eyes and each beetle has 2 eyes. How many eyes are there in total among Tom’s pet insects?
A basketball team won 75% of their games this season. If they played 80 games, how many games did they lose?

Mrs. Gable’s third grade class is on a field trip to the beach. For lunch, Mrs. Gable brought 20 lunches for the 20 students in her class. She included a pack of animal crackers in each lunch bag for dessert. Each pack of animal crackers contained 10 animal crackers. If 2 students did not eat their animal crackers, how many animal crackers were eaten in total among Mrs. Gable’s students?

Thomas goes to the gym 4 days a week and spends 2 hours there each day. During weekends, he goes hiking, spending 5 hours each day. If Thomas maintains this schedule for 8 weeks, how many hours will Thomas spend exercising?

A printer can hold a maximum of 500 sheets of paper. Currently, there are 200 sheets of regular paper and 100 sheets of colored paper in it. If the user wants to add a pack of 48-sheet photo paper and a pack of 120-sheet card stock, how many more regular paper can the user add?
Jake's train leaves at 1:45 PM and it takes 4 hours to arrive at his destination. At what time does he arrive?
Emily paid $45 for 5 shirts and 3 jeans. If each jeans cost $6, how much does each shirt cost?
Laura can read 2 pages of a book in a minute. If she reads for 40 minutes every day, how many pages can she read in a week?

Mike began to train to play basketball every day for a tournament. One day he plays a maximum of 2 hours. After a week of training, he increased the maximum time to 3 hours. How many hours did Mike train during the first two weeks?
A fruit stall contains 60 lesser apples than oranges. There are 5 times as many oranges as bananas. If there are 100 bananas in the stall, how many fruits are there altogether?

Jennifer, Lucy, Ben, and Max are cousins. Lucy is 3 years older than Jennifer, and Ben is 4 years older than Max. If Max is 13 years old, and Jennifer is 17 years old, how old are all four family members in total?
Lucas has 18 books. He gives away 3 books to each of his friends. If he has 6 books left, how many friends does he have?
Bobby bought a video game for $50 and a controller that costs $25. He used four $20 bills to pay for the items. How much change should he receive?
Megan has 30 books. Alex has 15 books and Lily has half the number of books Alex has. How many books do they have in total?
Amelia and her friends went to a restaurant. The bill was $120, and they decided to give a tip of 20%. How much will the total cost be, including the tip?

Kim is averaging about 6 hours of sleep each weekday and 8 hours each night on the weekend. If Kim would ideally like to get 9 hours of sleep each night on both weekdays and weekends, how many hours of sleep is Kim behind on from the last week?
A yard has 40 plants, some of which are trees others are flowers. A fifth of the plants are trees. How many plants on the yard are flowers?
Linda travelled 60 miles to visit her grandmother. It took her 2 hours to get there by car. If she travels at the same speed, how many miles would she cover in 1 hour?

At a picnic, Mary picked up 7 blueberries, Julia picked up twice as many as Mary, John picked up 5 less than Mary, and Bob picked up three times as many as John. How many blueberries did they pick up in total?
Molly had 52 stamps. She used 4 large stamps for the front page of her scrapbook and 6 stamps each to 5 other pages of her scrapbook. How many stamps does Molly have left?

A pizza place offers small, medium and large pizzas. A small pizza has 8 slices, a medium pizza has 12 slices, and a large pizza has 16 slices. If a party ordered 4 small pizzas, 3 medium pizzas and 2 large pizzas, how many slices of pizza are there in total?
