BlueBox Corp sold half of its inventory in the first week of sales, and the remaining inventory was sold after two more weeks. How many weeks did it take to sell all the inventory?
Samuel has 5 boxes of cookies. Each box contains 8 cookies. After eating two cookies, how many cookies does he have left?
Oscar has a jar of 50 marbles. He gave 1/5 of his marbles to his friend Abby and 1/4 of the remaining marbles to his friend Ben. How many marbles did Oscar give to his friends?

In a bananas farm, there are four types of bananas. The first type of bananas is twice as many as the second type. The third type is half as many as the second type. If the fourth type had 80 bananas, and was twice as many as first type, calculate the total number of bananas?

In a school library, there are 64 fiction books and 48 non-fiction books. The librarian receives a new shipment of 32 new non-fiction books and 16 new fiction books. How many books are in the library now?

In a farm, there are an equal number of cows and goats. 15 of the goats stay in their area, and the remaining goats mix with the cows. An equal number of cows join the goats, but then a quarter of these cows return to their area. If the farm has a combined total of 80 animals, how many cows are now mixed with the goats?
A librarian can shelve 30 books per hour. If 2500 books must be shelved in a 9-hour day, how many librarians must you hire for that day?
Peter is currently 28 years old. 8 years ago, Mark was 2 years younger than half Peter's age. In 4 years, how old will Mark be?

On International Hat Day, three-quarters of the students in school wore hats. Of those students wearing hats, one-third wore baseball caps. There were twice as many students wearing fedoras as baseball caps. If 180 students attended school that day, how many wore fedoras?

Alex is a computer programmer. In one hour, he can code around 60 lines. If he's working on a project that requires around 900 lines, on average how many hours would it take him to finish the project?
Emily has $200 for buying clothes for her vacation. She buys three shirts at $15 each and two more dresses than shirts at $50 each. How much money does Emily have left?

A vet needs to administer a vaccine to a dog, and she knows that the vaccine amount depends on the weight of the dog. The instructions say that for every kilogram of weight, the dog should receive 4 ml of vaccine. The dog weighs 20 kilograms and the vaccine is given in 4 equal parts. How many milligrams will each part of the vaccine be?
James is a vet and he needs to vaccinate on all the dogs that came in for the day. He vaccinated 112 dogs, but two of the dogs needed to get two more shots. How many shots did James give?

Carlos wants to bake 30 cupcakes for a party. Each portion of batter makes 6 cupcakes, and the batter costs $5 per portion. He also needs to buy cupcake liners, which are sold in packs of 20, each pack costs $3. How much will Carlos spend on baking the cupcakes?
Amy and Steven are saving money for a trip. Amy saves $10 every week and Steven saves $15 every week. How much money will they have saved together in 30 weeks?

Ricky has a garden where he grows strawberries. He collects a total of 45 strawberries every day and sells them in the market for $2 per strawberry. He met a new customer who wanted to buy strawberries in bulk. The customer asked for a discount and Ricky agreed to sell the strawberries at 90%. How much will he earn from selling to the new customer?
Linda had 100 candies. She gave 20 candies to her best friend and 15 candies to her brother. How many candies does Linda have left?
Every day Mike walks 8 kilometers to the office and 16 kilometers when he goes trekking during weekends. How many kilometers does Mike walk in a month?
In a factory, a machine can produce 50 tennis balls every hour. How many tennis balls will the machine produce in a week, assuming it works 8 hours a day and 5 days a week?
Luca is the top scorer on the soccer team. He averages 15 goals per season and his teammates average 35. How many total goals will their team have after 4 seasons?
Emma buys toys for 5 foster homes. Each home has 20 children and she decides to buy 4 toys per child. How many toys does Emma buy?
David went to the park and fed 5 pigeons. Each pigeon ate 3 breadcrumbs. How many breadcrumbs did David feed the pigeons in total?
Samantha has to drive 70 miles to a party and 70 miles to get back home. If her car uses 1 gallon of fuel for 20 miles, how many gallons of fuel will she use for the entire trip?

Olivia has read thrice the number of books that Ethan read. Ethan read four times the number of books that Sam read. Sam read a quarter the number of books that Hannah read. If Hannah read 12 books, how many books did Olivia read?

Lily has collected 15 seashells from the beach. Alex collected four times as many shells as Lily, while Sarah collected three times as many as Alex. If James collected 5 seashells, how many seashells did they collect in total?
David bought 5 dozens of cupcakes for his birthday party in addition to his 20 cupcakes. If 25 cupcakes are left over, how many cupcakes were eaten?
Joshua walked 5 miles. Emily walked 820 yards and Dominic walked 1360 feet. How many combined feet did the friends walk?

In a zoo, there are 50 animals. Half of these animals are elephants, and the remaining number are evenly divided between lions and zebras. If an elephant has 20 teeth, a lion has 4 less teeth than an elephant, and a zebra has 20 less teeth than an elephant, how many teeth are there in total at the zoo?
Jackie has a garden and she has grown 80 tomatoes. 65% of them are ripe and ready to be eaten. How many tomatoes are ready to be eaten?
William has a beautiful rose garden. He has 12 red roses and 8 pink roses. This spring, each red rose produced 3 new buds and each pink rose produced 2. How many roses does William have now?
There are 600 grains of sugar in one tablespoon. If one tablespoon is equivalent to 5 teaspoons, how many grains of sugar are in one teaspoon?
John bought 7 cups of coffee for his office. Each cup cost $3. If he tipped the barista $5, how much did John spend in total?
Lisa bakes 5 batches of cookies every week, with each batch containing 28 cookies. Over a span of 20 weeks, how many cookies does Lisa bake?

Ali threw a coin 30 times, and 16 times it came up heads. If he throws it another 20 times, how many times does he need it to come up heads if he wants the number of heads to make up 60% of all the throws?
Liam is preparing for a marathon. The first day he ran 5 miles. Each day after, he ran 2 miles more than the previous day. After running for 3 days, how many total miles did Liam run?
Laura’s monthly internet charge is normally $40. If the company increases the bill by 15%, how much will she pay for the entire year?
Julia bought fruits at the grocery store. She got 5 oranges for $1 each, 3 mangoes for $1.50 each and 2 bunches of grapes for $2.25 each. How much money did she spend in total for the fruits?
Emily has 2 pots of flowers. Each pot contains 6 flowers. After 3 weeks, each flower doubled. How many flowers does Emily have?

Paul is at the third floor of a shopping mall. He then moves up 3 floors and goes down 1 floor. He again goes up 2 floors and finds he is 5 floors away from the top of the shopping mall. How many floors does the shopping mall have?
Allison plays piano for 30 minutes each morning and 40 minutes each afternoon. How long does she spend playing the piano in one week?

Joshua owns a collection of silver dimes. If he uses them at the grocery store, they are worth the normal amount. But if he sells them to a collector, he can get $50 per ounce. Each dime weighs 1/10 of an ounce. How much more money can he make by selling them to the collector rather than spending them at the store?

In her garden, Amy plants 4 rows of carrots and each row has 6 carrot plants. If her neighbor also follows the same planting pattern but he has 7 rows of carrots in his garden, how many more carrot plants does the neighbor have?
Andy drives a 18-wheeler that can carry 20 tons. If he needs to transport 250 tons of material, how many trips will he have to make?

A group of students decided to plant trees for Arbor Day. Each student can plant 5 trees in a day. If they spent 4 days planting trees, and there were 20 students in total, how many trees did they plant?

A train left a station at 9 am with 500 pounds of fruits. After 4 hours of traveling, 3/10 of the fruits had been delivered to various spots. After another 5 hours of traveling, 2/5 of the remaining fruits were delivered. How many pounds of fruits were left on the train?

Timothy is at the grocery store and buys 3 loaves of bread for $2.5 each. He then buys 5 cartons of milk that cost $1.75 each. He spent 1/8 of the money he earned from mowing lawns. If he charges $3 per lawn, how many lawns did he mow?
A library has a budget of $1200. It has already spent $350 on books, $150 on furniture, and $200 on supplies. How much money is left in the library's budget?

Bailey wants to buy 4 new cell phones for her family. The first company, C-Mobile, offers a family package that costs $70 per month for two lines and $10 monthly for each additional line. The deal is cheaper at B-Mobile, with $60 monthly for two lines and $8 extra per line. How much cheaper is B-Mobile than C-Mobile?
Sarah earns $5000 a month. She spends 30% of her salary on rent, 20% on food, and 10% on transportation. How much money does she have left after these expenses?
Lisa paid $23 for a dress, $15 for a shirt, and $7 for a pair of socks. How much did she spend in total?
Ryan has saved $300. He spends 25% of his savings on shoes. How much money does Ryan have left?
In a home science experiment, Lily has 6 bottles of distilled water, each containing 500 mL. She pours an equal quantity into 4 empty bottles. How much water is there in each of the 4 bottles?
A truck container can hold 8 tons of sand. If a 50-pound bag of sand costs $6, how much would it cost to fill the truck with sand?
Sarah bought 5 kg of apples and 3 kg of oranges. If apples cost $4 per kg and oranges cost $6 per kg, how much did she spend in total?
Tim cries 3 tears for every four onions he peels. He needs to peel 5 onions per pot of stew. If he's making 8 pots of stew, how many tears does he cry?

A restaurant uses a total of 40 pounds of potatoes every day to make their famous fries. If one bag of potatoes cost $20 and can provide 10 pounds of fries, how much does the restaurant spend on potatoes per month (30 days)?
Joseph has 120 candies. He gives away 30% of his candies to his friends. How many candies is he left with?
Jenna reads 3 books more than twice the number of books Kurt reads. If Kurt reads 4 books a month, how many books does Jenna read in 4 months?

Leyla went to the supermarket. She saw that the frozen pizza is $4 per package, the ice cream is $3 per carton, and the apple juice is $1 per bottle. If she bought 10 packages of pizza, 5 cartons of ice cream, and 15 bottles of apple juice, how much is the total amount she paid?
Maria has 4 packs of stickers. Each pack has 25 stickers. If she gave 5 stickers to each of her 20 friends, how many stickers does she have left?
A bakery sells bread for $3 each. On Monday they sold 15 pieces, on Tuesday they sold 22 pieces, and on Wednesday they sold 18 pieces. How much money did they make in total?

A bakery makes 500 cupcakes in a day. The bakery sells packs of cupcakes, with each pack consisting of 12 cupcakes. During a special sale, all packs left at the end of the day are sold at half price. On a particular day, they sold 18 packs in the morning and 15 packs in the evening, and the remaining packs were sold during the special sale. How many packs of cupcakes were sold during the special sale?
A healthy fig tree produces 300 figs every day. Before selling the figs, the tree keeper removes 10% of them as they are not market quality. How many figs can the tree keeper sell a day?
A movie theater has 10 rows of seats. Each row has 18 seats. During a certain show, 75% of the seats were occupied. How many seats were occupied?

Tom and Jerry decide to sell their comic books at a garage sale. Tom has 100 comic books and Jerry has 80. At the end of the day they count how many comic books they have left. Tom has sold half of his comic books and Jerry has sold three-quarters of his. How many comic books do they have left in total?
Ariel had 12 horses. She bought 3 times as many horses as she already had. How many horses does Ariel have now?
Billy is 10 years old. If his brother is 6 years older than him, how old will Billy be when his brother is 30?
A bakery made 80 loaves of bread. One-fourth of them are whole grain bread while one-third of them are white bread. The rest are sourdough bread. How many sourdough bread loaves are there?
George is building a fence around his garden. Each side of his garden is 20 feet long, and he plans to put a fence post every 5 feet. How many fence posts does he need to buy?
Mina can read 30 pages in an hour and Brad can read 20 pages in an hour. How many more pages can Mina read than Brad in two hours?
At a school fair, 70 percent of the 400 attendees are parents. 30 percent of the parents bought snacks. How many parents bought snacks at the fair?

Matt drives 45 miles from his home to his office. After office hours, he goes to the grocery store, which is a 20-minute travel at 30 miles per hour from his office. How many miles in total does Matt travel?
A car can travel 500 km on a full tank of gas. If the gas tank contains 20 litres, how far can the car travel with 5 litres of gas?
A total of 70 baby ducklings are born on a farm, but half of them wandered off into the woods.  How many are still on the farm?
Sara started working at a company 10 years ago with a salary of $30,000 per year. After 2 years, her salary increased 20% and remained the same since then. What is her current salary?

Jared is on the 30th floor of a building and needs to get to the ground floor. The elevator he's in takes 10 minutes to travel down the first half of the floors. For the next 8 floors, the elevator takes 4 minutes per floor then, for the remaining 7 floors, the elevator takes 15 minutes per floor. How long in minutes did it take for Jared to reach the ground floor?

Santa Claus is packing gifts for children. He has 40 toy cars, 60 dolls, and 30 teddy bears. He plans to pack each gift into individual gift boxes. If each box costs $2, how much will Santa Claus spend on gift boxes?
A factory produces 100 cars every day. Due to technical issues, it could only produce half of its usual production for 2 days. How many cars are produced in a week?
David has 15 boxes of pencils. He wants to distribute these to class of 45 students such that each student will receive the same number of boxes. How many boxes will each student receive?
Suppose I host a party at my house. I invite 6 relatives, twice as many friends as relatives, and three times as many coworkers as friends. How many guests are at the party in total?
Brooklyn collected basketball cards. She has 100 cards of players 7 feet tall and above, which is 25% of her collection. How many cards does she have in total?
Ann was 45 inches tall on her 7th birthday. If she grows 1/3 a foot a year, how many inches tall will she be on her 11th birthday?
A car factory produces 15 cars in a day. Each car requires 8 hours to manufacture. If the factory operates for 10 hours per day, how many days does it take to produce 200 cars?
In a class, the ratio of boys to girls is 3:4. If there are 42 students in total, how many girls are in the class?

John clocked 5948 miles on his truck before going on a long drive. He filled up his tank four times during the trip, each fill-up holds 12 gallons of gas. If his truck gets 15 miles per gallon, what is the current mileage on his truck?
Jacob was 15 years old 10 years ago. How old will he be 5 years from now?

Ava went out for a bike ride. In 10 seconds, she rode a distance equal to five times the length of a basketball court. She rested for a bit, then rode 200 more meters. If the court's length is 28 meters, how far did Ava ride?
Mitchell raises chickens and goats. He has five times as many chickens as goats. If Mitchell has 42 animals in total, how many goats does he have?
Mr. and Mrs. Johnson are inviting people over for a dinner party. They have invited 5 families. Each family has 4 members. How many people are the Johnsons expecting at their dinner party?
In a school play, the cost of the costumes for 10 actors is $150, and the cost for props rented is $80. If the admission cost is $4 per person, how many people have to attend to cover the costs?
In five years, John will be twice as old as Sally. If John's age is currently three quarters of his mother's age, who is 64, how old is Sally now?

A warehouse received 50 crates of different products. One third of the crates were vegetables, one quarter were fruits, and a fifth were dairy products. The rest were grains. How many crates of grains did the warehouse receive?
Alex signed up for a painting class. The classes are 1.5 hours long and the course runs for 8 weeks, with lessons once a week. How many hours of painting will Alex have done by the end of the course?
Hazel had to buy gifts for her three friends. Each gift cost $15. She then had lunch costing $12. Later, she took a taxi home for $20. How much did Hazel spend in total?

Emilia wants to build a fence around her rectangular garden that is 10 feet long and 8 feet wide. If it costs $5 per foot to build the fence, how much will it cost Emilia to build the fence around her garden?
In an art competition, there are 15 paintings displayed. If there are 3 times as many sculptures as paintings, how many pieces of artwork are there in total?

At Noah's school, there are 80 students and 20 teachers. They decided to have a cookie party. If 3/4 of the students and 1/2 of the teachers ate cookies, how many non-cookie eaters are at Noah's school?
Samantha has a garden with 50 flowers. 20% of them are roses and the rest are daisies. How many daisies are in Samantha’s garden?
Ben spends 20% of his after-tax income on his car. If his car payment is $400 and he pays 1/3 of his gross income in taxes, how much does he make per month before taxes?
Becky initially had $150. She decided to spend 35% of her money on novels and the rest on comic books. If each comic book costs $3, how many comic books can she buy?
Mary and John are racing. The race is a 1 mile long. Mary runs 1/4 of the distance and John runs 1/6. How much of the race have they run altogether?

Max has 5 dogs. He and his sister needed to buy dog food for each of their dogs. Max bought 2 kg of dog food for each of his dogs. Max's sister bought 3 kg of dog food for each of them. How much dog food did they buy in total?

Michele decides to remodel her room. She already has 2 shelves in her room and wants to install 3 more. She also wants to install twice as many art prints on 2 different walls each. She currently has 1 art print already. How many total items does she have in her room after the remodeling?

A skincare company uses natural ingredients in their products. Each soap uses 10ml of rose water, and each cream also needs rose water. In one day, the company makes 50 soaps and 10 creams. They do not use rose water in anything else. The company has used 700ml of rose water by the end of the day. How much rose water, in millilitres, does each cream need?
Lisa purchases a house for $250,000. She makes a down payment of $50,000. Her monthly mortgage payment is $1,500. How many monthly payments does she need to make to fully own her house?
Peaches cost $1.8 per pound. The price increased by 20%. How much does it cost to buy 5 pounds of peaches for a 3-member family?
John went to a bookstore and bought 6 books for $15 each, then he went to the supermarket and bought 12 apples at $1.5 each. How much money did John spend?

In a class of 20 students, 13 students have a dog and 9 students have a cat. If every student has at least one pet and 5 students have both a dog and a cat, how many students have only a dog or only a cat?
Anna has 10 apples. She gave 3 to her friend and ate 2. How many apples does Anna have left?
Ben buys a pair of shoes for $75. If the sales tax is 7%, how much does he have to pay in total?
A hiker walks 3 miles each day during weekdays and 5 miles each day on the weekend. How many miles does the hiker walk in a week?
A bakery sells 150 muffins a day. Each muffin is sold for $2. If the bakery is open 5 days a week, how much does the bakery make from selling muffins a week?

Mark and Oliver are playing basketball. Each shot is worth 2 points. If Mark shot 10 times and scored 8 times while Oliver shot 15 times and scored 12 times, who scored more points and by how many points?
Jenny has a large collection of stuffed animals. She currently has 24 stuffed animals, and each year she adds 5% to her collection. How many stuffed animals will Jenny have in two years?

Joe plans to swim a total of 300 meters every week. He wants to gradually increase his weekly swimming distance by 10% until he is swimming 30% more than his initial goal. How many meters does he need to add per week to reach his ultimate goal?
Robin baked 7 more cookies than Tom, and Tom baked 10 cookies less than Jerry. If Jerry baked 25 cookies, how many cookies did Robin bake?
Anthony has a 100 page book. He read 15 pages per day for the first 3 days. Then, he read twice as many pages per day for the next 2 days. How many pages has he read so far?
Lisa went to a bakery to buy donuts for her office meeting. Each donut cost $1.5. If she bought 12 donuts for her team, how much did she spend in total?

A software engineer has written 6000 lines of code. Every 200 lines of code, he checks the program. If each check only finds two issues, and the engineer solves the issues each time before proceeding, how many issues has he solved so far?
A school planned a trip for its 60 students to the zoo. The bus they hired costs $500 and the entry fee per student to the zoo is $10. How much will the school pay in total?
Sam needs to bake a cake but realizes he's only got 1/4 of the flour the recipe calls for. If the recipe requires 2 cups of flour, how much flour does Sam have?
A classroom has 4 baskets. Each basket contains 5 apples and 3 oranges. One student took 2 apples and 1 orange from each basket. How many fruits are left in all the baskets?

At the beach, Arman saw couples riding bicycles and families riding quadricycles. Bicycles have two wheels and quadricycles have four wheels.  7 couples were riding bicycles and 10 families were riding quadricycles.   How many wheels did Arman see at the beach?
David has 75 toy cars. He buys 5 more then gives 1/4 of his collection to his cousin. How many toy cars does David have left?
For the next week, John wants to run three times the distance he ran last week. If he ran 5 miles last week, what will be the total distance that he will run in two weeks?
A football club has a total of 340 members. If the number of members decreases by 5% each month, how many members did the club have four months ago?

In the city park, the city council is planning to plant new trees around a circular pathway. If the diameter of the pathway is 56 meters and they are going to plant a tree every 7 meters, how many trees will they need?
Liam owns an apple orchard. Each apple tree yields 15 apples. If he has 200 apple trees, how many apples does his orchard yield?
Sophie saves 30% of her earnings. This month, she earned $500 from her part-time job, got $200 as a gift, and made $150 from selling homemade bracelets. How much will she save?
In a bookstore, there are 5 shelves. Each shelf has 20 books. If one of the shelves is half empty, how many books are there in total in the bookstore?

Linda goes to the supermarket and buys 3 apples for $.50 each. She then buys 5 lemons which cost $.30 each. She spent 1/5 of the money she earned from babysitting. If she charges $2 per hour, and babysat for 5 hours, how many more hours does she need to babysit to earn the money back?
John has 7 times as much money as Peter. If Peter has 15 dollars, how much money does John have?
If Mike runs 2 miles a day and wants to run a total of 200 miles, how many days will he need to achieve his goal?
Christina practices piano 1 hour per day 6 days a week.  On Sundays, she practices 2 hours a day.  If her piano recital is 4 weeks away, how much time will Christina spend practicing?
A reading group is planning to read a book of 440 pages over the next 4 weeks. If they plan to read the same number of pages each day, how many pages per day do they need to read each day?
Sam read a book every 70 minutes. How many books does he read during a 3-hour literature marathon?
A company needs $20,000 for a project, and it already has $7,000. If the company can profit an average of $1,000 each week, how many weeks does it need to reach the target?
Lizzy is reading a book of 600 pages. She can read 50 pages per day. However, on the second day, she read twice as many pages as on the first day. How many pages does she have left after two days?
Harry runs 4 miles every day for his morning exercise. If he runs 6 days a week, how many miles will he run in 3 weeks?
A library has 123 books in English, 87 in Spanish and 45 in French. If 35 English books, 29 Spanish books and 15 French books are borrowed, how many books are still available in the library?

At the school carnival, Kuldeep makes $80 from selling cotton candies. He made 10 boxes of cotton candies which were all sold. Each box contained 4 cotton candies. How much did each cotton candy cost?
Daniel is a book lover and reads an average of 100 pages per day. He just started a new book which has 500 pages. How many days will it take for Daniel to finish the book?

In a museum, there are 6 different types of artifacts. Viewing each artifact type takes approximately 10 minutes. How much time would it take to see each artifact type if the museum decided to showcase 3 new types?

Greg has had a busy week and not getting enough sleep. He was only able to sleep for 2 hours on 3 of the days, and on the rest of the days he only got 75% of the recommended 8 hours of sleep. How many total hours of sleep did he get this week?
A watermelon is cut into 8 slices. Four kids bought 2 melons. If they share all 2 melons equally, how many slices of melon did each of them get?
Lucy went strawberry picking and picked 50 strawberries. If each strawberry weighs approximately 12 grams, how much in kilograms did Lucy's strawberries weigh?
A farmer wants to evenly distribute his 2000 apples and 800 oranges into 10 baskets. How many fruits will be in each basket?
A pair of shoes costs $25. If Sarah wants to buy 6 pairs of shoes, how much will it cost?
Every weekend Anna saves $5.5 from her pocket money. What's the total amount she will save in 12 weekends?
Brian worked 6 hours daily from the 4th to the 10th, including the 4th but not including the 10th. How many hours did Brian work?
At the Jackson's farm, there are 3250 red apples, 4500 green guavas, and 2030 yellow mangoes. How many fruits are at the Jackson's farm in total?
Tricia wants to place an exotic plant at each four corners of her square-shaped patio. Each plant costs $20.00. How much will it cost to fill all the corners?

Marie wants to lose 20 pounds in a month. She aims to lose 5 pounds on the first week. On each subsequent week, she plans to lose 1 pound more than the previous week. If she sticks to her plan, how many pounds will she lose on the 4th week?
John is 5 years older than Mark. Mark is 3 years younger than Lisa. Lisa is 25 years old. How old is John?

Ella has a shoe shop. If she dedicates 30% of the shop to sneakers, 20% to high heels and the rest to flats. If the total area of her shop is 500 square meters, how many square meters are dedicated to flats?
Bella baked a batch of 300 brownies for a charity bakesale. During the bakesale, Bella sold 40% of the brownies. How many brownies does Bella have left?
Samuel has to read a book containing 300 pages for his homework. He read 50 pages on Monday, 75 pages on Tuesday, and 40 pages on Wednesday. How many pages does he still have to read?
Peter catches 7 fish every day, how many fish does he have after a week?
Maria is organizing a book fair. She needs 15 boxes of books. Each box contains 50 books. If 25% of the total books are about science, how many books about science are there?

Mary buys boxes of chocolates for her friends. Each box costs $5 and she bought 6 boxes. If she also wants to buy flowers that cost $10 per bouquet and she wants to buy 2 bouquets, how much will she spend in total?

A school is planning to plant 25 trees in its garden. The cost of each tree is $18. If the school already spent $150 on gardening tools, how much will the school spend in total on the entire garden project?
Lisa fills 8 boxes with books each day during weekdays. On the weekends, she fills twice the number of boxes she fills on weekdays. How many boxes does Lisa fill in a week?
Mike works as a social media influencer. He earns $0.5 per like on his posts. If it takes him 30 minutes to shoot and edit a post, how much does he earn per hour?

A cyclist wants to cycle 100 km at a speed of 20 km/hour, but after cycling for 2 hours, he decides to decrease his speed to 15 km/hour for the rest of the journey. How long will it take him to finish his journey?
Jason has a rectangular garden that is 10 feet long and 6 feet wide. He decided to double the length and triple the width of his garden. What is the new area of the garden?

Ralph’s school hockey team requires 4 complete kits, each consisting of a jersey, pants, helmet, and stick. The jersey costs $15, the pants cost the same as the jersey, the helmet costs half as much as the jersey and the stick costs $5. How much will Ralph need to spend?
Joe buys a computer for $800. After a year, he decides to sell it and he manages to get 70% of the original price. How much money did he sell the computer for?

Sara buys 5 pounds of chicken and 8 pounds of vegetables for her weekend party. The chicken costs $5 per pound and the vegetables cost 2 times less than the chicken. What is the total price of the chicken and vegetables?

The balance of Steve's bank account doubled during the year. At the end of the year, Steve withdrew $300. If his current balance is $700, how much did he have in the bank at the beginning of the year?

Martha gets a headache for every 5 chocolate candies she eats. She gets 3 candies from her mom and 2 candies each from 3 of her friends. Then, she buys 1/6 as many as she was given. How many headaches does she get from eating all her candies?
A factory produces 100 chairs every day. If there are 20 working days in a month, how many chairs does the factory produce in one month?

Jerry works five days a week as a waiter and earns a variable amount of tips every night. The past four nights, he earned $20, $60, $15, and $40. How much does he need to earn tonight if he wants to make an average of $50 in tips per night?
A printer can print 30 pages in 2 minutes. How many pages can it print in 12 minutes?
Jenna's garden produced 150 cucumbers and 200 tomatoes. If she sells cucumbers for $1.50 each and tomatoes for $1 each, how much money can Jenna make?
Lucy is taller than Emma by 4 inches. Emma is shorter than Bella by 3 inches. If Bella is 38 inches tall, find the height of Lucy.

A family went on a road trip. On the first day, they traveled 240 miles. On the second day, they traveled 3/5 as far. On the third day, they traveled 2/3 as many miles as the first two days combined. How many miles did they travel for 3 days?
A theater group needs 75 costumes for a play. Each costume costs $85 to make. If the group has already made 40 costumes, how much more will it cost to make the remaining costumes?
On April 1st the sun rises at 6 AM. Every day after the sun rises 1.5 minutes earlier. It is 5:30 AM and 30 days after April 1st. How many minutes until the sun rises?
Joe invested a sum of money in a business that returned twice the amount he invested after one year. If after two years, his investment was worth $180, how much did Joe initially invest?
Tom has an apple orchard where he planted 80 trees in the first row and 70 trees in the second row. Each tree gives 60 apples. How many apples did Tom get in total?
Paul has twice as many marbles as Jack. Jack has 5 less marbles than Linda. If Linda has 30 marbles, how many marbles do they have in total?

In 2010, a number of people visited a zoo. In 2011, twice the number of people visited the zoo compared to 2010. In 2012, half as many people visited the zoo as in 2011. If there were 30 people visiting the zoo in 2012, how many people visited the zoo in 2010?

In one day, a city experiences several hours of heavy snowfall. From 5am to 8am, it snowed at a rate of 5 centimeters per hour. From 8am to 1pm, it snowed at a rate of 3 centimeters per hour. If the streets started the day completely clear of snow, how many centimeters of snow will the city have by 1pm?
Elina has a lemonade stand. A cup of lemonade costs $2. One day she sold lemonade and made $56. If she also gave away 13 free cups, how many cups of lemonade did she sell that day?

In the university's bookstore, there are 5000 different textbooks. 70% of all the textbooks are in science, but only 50% of these textbooks are published by a local publisher. How many science textbooks have been published by a foreign publisher?
Thomas, a builder wants to buy plywood for a construction project. Each sheet of plywood costs $20. If he needs 30 sheets, how much will he spend?

Dave is the head chef at a restaurant. He prepared 375 meals in a week. He needed to make 25 breakfasts per day, 35 lunches and 40 dinners daily. How many special request meals did he prepare that week?

Lynn works on a farm and is responsible for feeding the pigs every day. There are 15 pigs and each pig needs 6 pounds of feed daily. The bag of feed that Lynn bought contains 360 pounds. How many days will the feed last?

A store sold 25 shirts on Monday. On Tuesday they sold double the amount of shirts they did on Monday. Then on Wednesday, they sold triple the amount they did on Monday. How many shirts did they sell in total from Monday to Wednesday?
A group of 5 friends went to a restaurant. Each ordered a meal that costs $12 and a drink that costs $3. If the service charge is $10, how much is the total bill?
A sports car can go from 0 to 60 miles per hour in 5 seconds. How long would it take to go from 0 to 120 miles per hour at this rate?
In a school, there are 100 students. 60% of the students play basketball, 30% play football. How many students do not play either basketball or football?
Vivian types at a constant speed and can type 5 pages in 20 minutes. How long will it take for her to type a 30-page document?
A cake shop had cakes on display. Bella entered and bought half of all the cakes and 2 more pieces. Then Zac bought the last 5 remaining cakes. How many cakes were on display at first?
A classroom has 30 chairs. 40% of them are blue, the rest are yellow. How many yellow chairs are there in the classroom?

Quinn's library was hosting a summer reading challenge.  For every 5 books you read, you a coupon for a free donut at a nearby bakery.  Quinn decided he could read 2 books a week for 10 weeks total.  How many free donuts would Quinn be eligible for?
Benjamin has $30 and wants to buy candy bars that cost $2 each. How many candy bars can Benjamin buy?

On Grandparent's Day, grandparents get a 15% discount in the bookstore. Grandparents who have 5 grandchildren or more can get an additional 5% off the discounted price. If Mr. Wilson has 6 grandchildren and wants to buy a book that costs $75, how much will he pay?

James gifts books to his friends. On Sunday, he gifts 2 books. Each day for the rest of the week he gifts one more book than he did the day before. If on Saturday he counts that there are 10 books left, how many did he originally have?
There are 500 people at a concert. Out of these, 1/4 are teenagers and half are adults. The others are children. How many children are at the concert?
John has to save $3000 by the end of the year. By the end of first 6 months, he saved 30% of the total amount he plans to save. How much money does he still need to save?

Samantha is hosting a dinner party. She is making 3 pans of lasagna, each with 10 servings. She bought bread for $0.50 per serving and plans to have 2 pieces of bread per serving of lasagna. If she spent $60 on the lasagna ingredients, how much did she spend in total?

Susan's school makes $120 from selling tickets for a show. There are twice as many tickets sold for adults as kids. The price of each kid's ticket is half the price of an adult ticket which costs $10. How many adult tickets were sold?
Amanda has 25 apples. She gives 4 apples to each of her 5 friends. How many apples does Amanda have left?
Annie has 4 times as many marbles as Ted. If Ted has 5 marbles, how many marbles does Annie have?
Sara baked 15 cupcakes, and then she ate 3. How many cupcakes are left?

Every day, Bryan puts 100 candies in his lunchbox. Unfortunately, his friend Mark took some of Bryan's candies, and 35 candies were lost. So at lunchtime, Bryan only had 20 candies remaining. How many candies did Mark take from Bryan?
George has $150 and Anne has $120. If together they buy four concert tickets for $35 per ticket, how much money do they have left?
Lucy’s age is thrice Sarah’s age. If Sarah is 7 years old, how old will Lucy be in 5 years?

Linda plans to travel for a weekend and decides to take a bus. If the bus ticket costs $20 and she's planning to bring a suitcase which costs half of the ticket price, how much will Linda pay if she eats a $12 meal on the bus?
John bought 7 packs of soda for a party. Each pack has 12 cans. After the party, they are left with 15 cans. How many cans of soda were consumed during the party?
There are 12 boys and 14 girls in the fifth grade and half that number in the fourth grade. How many students are in grades 4 and 5?
A store offers a 20% discount on all winter coats. The original price of the coat Amanda wants is $150. How much will Amanda pay if she decides to buy the coat?
Michael bought a video game at a price of $50. After a month, the price of the video game increased by 30%. What is the new price of the video game?
In a week, Jenna studies for 3 hours each day from Monday to Friday, 5 hours on Saturday, and doesn't study at all on Sunday. How many hours does Jenna study in a week?
Alice bakes a cake with 10 slices. She eats 1/3 of the cake which has 200 calories per slice. How many calories did she eat?
Lisa spent 45% of her salary on rent, and 30% of the remainder on groceries. After spending $100 on clothes, she is left with $200. How much salary did Lisa earn?
A lumberjack cuts down 3 types of trees in the forest. He cuts down 15 pines, twice as many oaks as pines, and half as many maples as pines. How many trees did the lumberjack cut down in total?
A rose plant cost $10. If Claire bought 5 rose plants, 4 ferns, and a bouquet that cost the same as 6 rose plants, and each fern costs the same as a rose plant, calculate how much she paid.
Stacy spent $20 on buying a novel and she has $100 left. How much money did she have before buying the novel?
Peter is 34 years old. His father is twice his age. How old was his father when Peter was born?
Michelle baked 3 times as many cookies as Laura and 2 times as many as Anna. If Laura baked 5 cookies, how many cookies did Anna bake?
Peter is creating a mural for his school library's wall. It will be 12 feet tall and 18 feet long. Each tile he uses is 2 inch square. How many tiles will he need?

Mr. Flynn owns a fruit orchard. In a day, he collects 50 apples from his apple trees and 35 oranges from his orange trees. One afternoon, he sold 75% of the apples and 90% of the oranges he collected. If each apple is sold for $1.5 and each orange for $1, how much did he earn in total?
John can complete a task in 15 days and Jim can complete the same task in 10 days. How many days will it take them to finish the task if they work together?
Jack buys a secondhand bike for $60 and manages to sell it for $80. What is the profit Jack made on this sale?
A bookstore has 500 books. 300 of them are children's books, the rest are adult books. The store orders 200 more books, and 60% of those new books are adult books. How many adult books are there now?

A farmer has 2 types of animals: cows and chickens. Each cow provides 5 liters of milk per day, and the farmer has 10 cows. Each chicken lays 2 eggs per day, and the farmer has 15 chickens. How many products (milk liters and eggs) does the farmer get each day?

A class has 85 students in total. Fifteen of them take the school train to school, 3/5 of the remaining take their own car to school, and the rest whose homes are near the school walk to school. How many students are walking to school?
A parking lot charges $15 for the first three hours and $2 for each additional hour. If Greg parked his car there for 7 hours, how much did he have to pay?
Every week Harper buys 5 kilos of oranges and 7 kilos of apples. If he continues to buy the same amount of fruits for the next 3 weeks, how many kilos of fruits will he have?
Angel likes to play a mobile game in his free time.  He buys a pack of 500 gems every month for a year, but then loses half of them in a game update.  How many gems does Angel have left?
Amy wants to read a 500-page book within 10 days. How many pages does she need to read per day?
Adam bought a baseball cap for $25, a football jersey for $50 and a pair of shoes for $100. If he bought 4 caps, 2 jerseys and 5 pairs of shoes, what is the total money Adam spent?

In his backyard, James had noticed 45 birds before going on a business trip. When he came back from the business trip, he noticed 5 times more birds. How many birds came to his backyard during his absence?
John has 14 chickens. He sells half of them at the market in the morning, and then buys 7 more in the evening. How many chickens does John now have?
In a school, there were 200 students. The number of boys was 30 more than the number of girls. How many girls were there in the school?

John is aiming to mow 60 lawns this summer. He has already mowed 5 at his house, 7 at his neighbor's house, and 3 at his grandparents' house. How many more lawns does he need to mow to reach his goal?

Ali and Zara own a baking business together. On a good day, Zara bakes 5 less than double the amount Ali bakes, while Ali bakes 4 more than triple the amount of days in a week. How many does Zara bake in a week?
A car traveled 60 miles per hour for 4 hours. If the car needs to travel a total distance of 300 miles, how many more hours does the car need to travel at the same speed?
A book store sells 40% of all its books to three libraries evenly. If the bookstore sold 120 books to the libraries, how many books does it have in total?

Emily is working on a drawing project. She has to draw all the birds in a zoo, and she has taken pictures of 500 birds. It will take Emily 3 minutes to draw each bird. How many hours will it take to draw all of the birds?
Ava spends 45 minutes/day four times a week doing yoga and 30 minutes/day 3 days a week doing Pilates. How many minutes does she spend exercising each week?
Liam had 200 marbles. He lost 1/4 of his marbles, gave 3/5 of the remaining to his friend, and kept the remaining marbles. How many marbles did he keep?
Jonathan bought 5 comic books that each cost the same amount. He paid 20 dollars for all 5 books. How much did one comic book cost?

Chuck's bracelet is made entirely of gold, silver, or copper beads. If Chuck has 10 gold beads, two and a half times as many silver beads as gold beads, and 20 more copper beads than silver beads, how many beads are on the bracelet?
Nick plays 15 games of Chess.  He wins an average of 3 games out of 5.  What is his total win percentage?
A fruit garden has 6 apple trees and 3 orange trees. Each apple tree yields 15 apples and each orange tree gives 25 oranges. How many fruits in total are in the garden?
A grocery store received 500 apples. They distributed 2/5 of the apples on the first day and the remaining half on the second day. How many apples were left after two days?

Alex and Sam are cycling down a road that is 60 meters wide at one end. If the road's width decreases from this end uniformly by 1 meter every 5 meters along, and they cycle along the road at a rate of 6 m/s, how long (in seconds) will it take to get to the point where the road is 30 meters wide?
Kimberly is making pancakes. The recipe calls for 2 cups of flour for one batch of pancakes. Each packet of flour contains 8 cups. To make 21 batches, how many packets of flour will Kimberly need?
Paula is preparing for a marathon. She runs 10 miles every day for a month to train. If a month has 30 days, how many miles will she run in a year?

It takes 40 minutes to prepare a tray of cupcakes and another 20 minutes to bake. If one tray carries 5 cupcakes and the oven can only fit 2 trays at once, how many hours will it take to finish baking 30 cupcakes?

Jasmine has some money. She spent $20 buying a dress. She spent half of the money she had left on a pair of shoes. If the pair of shoes cost $30, what is the amount of money, in dollars, Jasmine had at first?
There are 15% more boys than girls in a school. If there are 230 boys, how many girls are there?
Cindy baked 24 cupcakes for a party. She ate 2 of them and her brother ate 4. If the remaining cupcakes were shared between her 5 friends, how many cupcakes did each friend get?

For a school project, Adam bought 120 colored pencils. He gave 20 pencils to his classmate Brian and 15 pencils to his other classmate Chris. He gave the remaining pencils equally to 5 classmates. How many pencils did each classmate get?
Sarah has a monthly budget of $500 for groceries. If she already spent $325, how much more money she can spend on groceries?
Amanda can read 30 pages in an hour, but she reads 50% faster when she's drinking coffee. If she spends 3 hours reading without coffee and 2 hours reading with coffee, how many pages can Amanda read?

Bob wants to split up his collection of 100 baseball cards equally between his 4 friends. After he has split up his collection, he discovers he has received 10 new cards as a gift. How many cards does Bob now have?
A bakery makes 10 loaves of bread per batch, with each loaf containing 12 slices. Each slice of bread contains 10 raisins. How many raisins are in a batch of bread?
John currently has $500 in his saving account and plans to deposit $200 into his account every month. How much money will he have in his saving account after one year?
A hardware store is offering a 15% discount on power tools. If the regular price of a power drill is $50, how much does the power drill cost after the discount?
Karen saved $350. She wants to buy a dress that is $50 more expensive than the amount she saved. If she asked her mom to lend her 1/5 of the dress’s price, how much more does she need to have?
Carlos earns $15 per hour and he has worked 45 hours this week. How much will he earn before tax?

Sally ran a lemonade stand over the summer. For every cup of lemonade she sold, she earned $0.50. She sold 250 cups before the end of July and 200 cups after. She used half of her earnings to buy a new bike. How much did the bike cost?
A car travels 150 miles on 9 gallons of gasoline. How many miles per gallon does the car get?

There are 4 scoops of ice cream on each cone. If John and his 2 brothers each eat two-thirds of the ice cream on their respective cones, how many scoops of ice cream are still left on their cones altogether?
A rabbit eats 3 carrots a day. How many carrots will it eat in a week?
Scott is painting his house. He needs 3 cans of paint for the living room, 4 for the bedroom, and 2 for the kitchen. If each can of paint costs $15, what is his total cost for the paint?
Lucy gets 2 GB of cell phone data each month. If she uses 500 MB on Instagram and 3/5 of the remainder on video calls, how many MB of data does Lucy have left?

Jake works for $10 an hour and 40 hours a week at his job. It’s been two months since he started working there. He has saved up all of his money because he wants to buy a car for $1700. How much money will he have left over after he buys his car?
Carrie has 8 candles and her mother gives her 5 more for her birthday. She then gives 3 to her best friend. How many candles does Carrie have now?
Ben watched 5 movies last week and 3 movies the week before. If he plans on watching 6 movies next week, what is his average number of movies watched per week?

Lisa's bus ride to school is 30 minutes long. She spends twice this time studying before school and three times this time playing after school. How many hours does she spend either going to school, studying, or playing every day?

John spends $600.00 a year to have unlimited movie tickets. Amy buys movie tickets individually. Everyday, she spends $3.50 and on Sundays, she spends $7.00. How much more money does Amy spend buying movie tickets in a year than John?
Jack has 25 candy bars that he wants to share with his 4 friends. How many candy bars will each person get?
Tom's garden is 8 feet longer than 3 times the length of Jerry's garden. If Tom's garden is 32 feet long, how long is Jerry's garden?

Lisa went to the mall to buy a book and 3 magazines. A magazine costs $6 while a book is $2 less than the magazine. If she decides to buy a hat which costs half the total price of the 3 magazines and a book, how much will she pay for all those items?

A group of friends are planning to have a pizza party where each person expects to eat 3 slices of pizza. If a pizza is divided into 8 slices, how many pizzas they need to buy if they are expecting 20 people?

A school buys 48 boxes of markers. Each box has 12 markers in it. The school also buys 20 large drawing pads, and each pad can be used by 4 students. How many more markers than students can use the drawing pads does the school have?
In a football club, 4 members left the club each week. Given that the club initially had 80 members, how many members will the club have after 5 weeks?
A bakery makes 12 dozens of cookies each day. If each dozen is made up of 12 cookies, how many cookies does the bakery produce in a week?
Marlon is planning a long trip that requires him to travel 1000 miles. If his car runs at 20 miles per gallon, and gas currently costs $2.5 per gallon, how much will he spend on gas for this trip?

Emily is grocery shopping. She sees that tomatoes are sold in a pack of 3 for $1.50 and oranges are sold in a pack of 4 for $2. If Emily wants to buy 15 of the least expensive items, how much will she have to pay, in cents?
A farmer planted 4 times as many tomato plants as pepper plants. If 80 pepper plants were planted, how many plants were planted in total?
James runs a laundry service. He charges $5 for a normal wash and $7 for a delicates wash. If he delivers ten normal washes and five delicates wash per day, how much money will he earn in a week?

Mike counted 10% more birds than his friend Paul while they were bird watching, and Paul counted 20 fewer birds than their friend Susan. If Mike counted 330 birds, how many birds did all of them count?
Karen was 60 inches tall three years ago. If she grew 10 inches more than her sister who grew 2 inches in the last three years, how tall is Karen now?
Alex, Sam, Eva, and Mary each order large pizzas cut into 6 pieces. If Sam and Eva eat 60% of their pizzas and Alex and Mary eat 40% of the pizzas, how many pizza pieces are left uneaten?
Tom is a farmer and he has 60 cows and 40 sheep. Each cow gives 30 liters of milk and each sheep gives 15 liters of milk. How many liters of milk does Tom get?
John has 30 stickers. He gave away a quarter of the stickers, and he still had thrice as many stickers as Peter. How many stickers does Peter have?

Darren has a garden that has a width of 5 yards and a length of 7 yards. He decided to expand his garden by increasing its length by 3 yards. After increasing its length, how many square yards is Darren's garden?
A store sells pens for $3 each. Mark wants to buy pens for his entire class of 20 students. How much will Mark have to pay in total?
A painter has a fixed supply of 20 liters of paint. If each coat of paint uses 2 liters, how many coats can the painter apply before running out of paint?

Andrew wants to buy a designer watch worth $12,000. He has saved $5,000 and plans to get a loan for the remaining amount to be paid in 60 equal monthly installments. If the interest rate on the loan is 10%, how much will Andrew's monthly payment be?

Jack is training for a marathon. He can run 7 miles in an hour. He decides to run for 1 hour a day for 9 days but on the 10th day, he tries to double the distance. How many miles will Jack run in 10 days?
George has a monthly budget of $1000 for necessary expenses. If he spends one-third of his budget for groceries and gets a discount of 10% there, how much money does he save?
Sarah has 45 roses and 60 sunflowers. Using all the flowers, how many floral arrangements containing 15 flowers each can she make?

A marker box has 30 markers total.  10 markers are red, 7 markers are blue, there are half the number of green markers as blue markers, and the rest of the markers are black.  How many markers are black?

Kim baked a batch of cookies using 2 cups of sugar. She spilled half of the sugar when she was adding it to her mixture. If Kim wants to keep 3 cups of sugar available, how many cups of sugar does she need to buy?
There are 4 bowls, each containing the same number of apples. If the total number of apples is 68, how many apples are there in each bowl?
Rose grows 15 lilies in her garden while Daisy grows twice as many roses. If Daisy then loses a fifth of her roses to a storm, how many roses does she have left?

A school is planning a trip to the zoo. This school has 4 classes, with 30 students in each class. Each student needs to pay $15 for the ticket. How much will all of the students have to pay altogether?
Emily and her sister went berry picking. Emily picked 12 strawberries in the morning and 7 in the afternoon. Her sister picked 15 strawberries. How many strawberries did they pick in total?

Jane planned a vacation to Europe. She spent 14 hours on a flight and 8 hours waiting at the airport. In addition, she had to quarantine for 10 days upon arrival due to COVID-19 regulations. How many total hours did Jane have to wait?
Herman has 50 nickels and 40 dimes. How much money does Herman have in total?
A soccer team is selling team t-shirts for $15 each to raise money for their upcoming trip. If the team wants to raise $900, how many team t-shirts do they need to sell?

Maria and Eli are reading two different novels. In Monday, Tuesday and Wednesday, Maria reads 50 pages each day and Eli reads 45 pages each day. On Thursday and Friday, Maria reads 55 pages each day and Eli reads 60 pages each day. On the weekend, both decided to rest. How many more pages did Maria read than Eli by the end of the week?
Each chicken lays 5 eggs per week. William bought 250 chickens to start an egg production business. If 20% of eggs are not suitable for sale, how many eggs can he sell every week?
Kurt's monthly salary is $3000. He spends 1/5 of his salary on rent. How much does he spend for rent every month?

Tom has a collection of marbles, and each jar holds 120 marbles. He has 5 full jars and one additional jar that is only half full. If he gives his sister 2 full jars, how many marbles will he have left?
Alice takes up knitting and knits 15 scarfs per month. She sells each scarf for $20. How much will she earn if she sells all the scarfs she knits in 6 months?
A gym has an initial fee of $50 and a monthly fee of $20. After how many months would the total cost to be a member of the gym be $210?
John spent 5 hours cleaning houses at a rate of $10 per hour. He then spent 2 hours cleaning a car at a rate of $15 an hour. How much did John make in total?

On the first day of my yoga training, I did 20 poses. Each day, I plan to increase my number of poses by 7 more than the previous day. If I continue my training for five consecutive days, how many poses will I perform on the fifth day?
Oliver plants 4 apple trees every year in his garden. If each tree produces 60 apples, how many apples will Oliver have after 3 years?
A farmer has both chickens and cows on his farm. There are 25 chickens and 18 cows. Each chicken has 2 legs and each cow has 4 legs. How many legs are there in total on the farm?
Emma studies for her exams 3 hours a day. If she studies for 2 weeks straight, how many hours does she spend studying?

Bob is learning to sculpt statues to sell at the art exhibition, he makes statues, over the first hour, at the rate of one every 15 minutes. Then, in the second hour, he makes one at the rate of every 7 minutes. How many statues does he make over the course of those two hours?
A bag contains 15 apples and 10 oranges. Judy takes 5 apples and 4 oranges from the bag. How many fruits does the bag contain now?
James wants to fence his rectangular garden that measures 15 meters by 10 meters. If the cost of the fence is $20 per meter, how much will he pay for the fence?
Ben went to the store and bought 10 apples. He already had 7 apples at home. Then, he ate 5 apples. How many apples does Ben have now?
Liam saved $10 every week. After saving for 23 weeks, he spent $125 on a new bike. How much money does Liam have left?
A coffee shop sold 10 coffees and 5 teas in the morning. Each coffee cost $3.50 and each tea cost $2.25. How much did the coffee shop make in the morning?
Billy got 12 candy bars on Halloween. His sister, Betty, got 3 times as many as Billy. They both gave 2 candy bars to their neighbor. How many candy bars do they have now?
Karen is baking cookies. Each batch requires 3 cups of flour and she has 10 cups of flour. If each batch makes 12 cookies, how many cookies can she make?

Tom walked a distance of 5 kilometers from his house to the park and later walked 3 kilometers from the park to the library. After that, he walked another 2 kilometers to the supermarket. How many kilometers did Tom walk in total?
Emma had $15, she spent $5 on a book and another $4 in a cafe. On her way home, she found $2 on the sidewalk but then realized she only had $1 left in her purse. How much money did she lose?
Emily has 6 times as many books as Alex. Alex has 7 fewer books than Joy, who has 12 books. How many books does Emily have?

A biologist is studying the population of a certain species of bird. In the first year, the population triples. In the second year, the population doubles, and in the third year, the species increases by 150 birds. If there are now 1150 birds, how many birds were there in the beginning?

Each month, Lisa spends $400 on rent, 1/4 as much on groceries as she does on rent and her car payment is 5 times the amount she spends on groceries. If she saves $800 and also pays the same of her savings in utilities, calculate her total monthly expenses.
Paul bought a box of 16 candy bars for $20. He ate four and sold the rest. If he sold each candy bar for $1.50, how much money did Paul make in total after selling the remaining candy bars?
Ellen wants to make 8 identical bracelets for her friends. Each bracelet needs 20 beads. However, she only has 73 beads. How many more beads does she need to make the bracelets?

Jenny bakes 80 cupcakes and 50 donuts. Jenny eats 3 cupcakes and 2 donuts. Jenny's dad eats 2 cupcakes and 4 donuts. If Jenny sells a cupcake for $2 and a donut for $1.75, how much money will Jenny make if she sells all of her baked goods?
Anna is organizing her library. She has 300 books and 60% are romance novels. 50 are science-fiction books. The rest are mystery novels. What percentage of mystery novels does she own?

A pizza parlor offers a family meal deal that includes 1 large pizza and 4 soft drinks for $24. If John wants to host a pizza party for 20 of his friends, how much will it cost him to order enough family meal deals to feed his friends?
In a beach cleanup event, John collected 4 times as many plastic bottles as Peter, who collected twice as many as Emily. If Emily collected 35 bottles, how many bottles did John collect?
Ashley is 10 years older than her sister Bethany, who is three times as old as their brother Carlos. If Carlos is 5 years old, how old is Ashley?

There are 60 students in class. 25% of them bring their own lunches, and the rest eat school lunches. If 10% of the students who eat school lunches decided to start bringing their own lunches, how many students in total would be bringing their own lunches?
Anna loves reading. She reads 2 chapters a day on weekdays and 4 chapters a day on the weekend. How many chapters does Anna read a week?
A bus can carry a maximum of 50 passengers. If 70% of seats were occupied, how many people were on the bus?
Ava paints pictures and sells them at a local craft fair. Each painting costs Ava $12 to make and she sells them for $35 each. If Ava sold 20 paintings, how much profit did Ava make?

In a toy shop, Thomas saw kids buying toy cars and toy trains. Toy cars have 4 wheels and toy trains have 6 wheels. 5 kids were buying toy cars and 7 kids were buying toy trains. How many wheels in total did Thomas see at the toy shop?
Daniel has 5 liters of orange juice. He poured an equal amount of juice in 5 glasses. Then, he drank 1 glass. How much orange juice does he have left?

After the massive exams in Michigan, the total number of Covid-19 positive cases was recorded as 3000 on a certain day. The number of cases increased by 600 on the next day, with 60 recoveries. On the third day, the total number of fresh cases rose to 1800 with 300 recoveries. What's the total number of positive cases after the third day?
Peter started playing a game at level 1. Every day, he advances 3 levels. After 10 days, he buys 15 extra levels. What level is Peter at?
Tom rents his motorcycle for $30 an hour. He rents it for 4 hours a day, 5 days a week. How much does he earn in a week?

Tom planted 2 apple trees, 3 orange trees and 4 pear trees in his garden. Each apple tree yields 20 apples, each orange tree yields 30 oranges and each pear tree yields 25 pears. How many fruits in total does Tom get?
Shane found a treasure chest and divided the gold coins equally among his 5 friends. Each friend received 30 coins. How many total coins did the treasure chest contain?
Jane is making bracelets to sell at a fair. She has 100 beads and each bracelet requires 10 beads. Each bracelet is sold for $5. How much will she make if she sells all the bracelets?
A pizza restaurant gives 6 free breadsticks with every order. If a school orders 30 pizzas for an event, how many breadsticks does the school get for free?
Each yoyo costs $8. Zack has $40 in his pocket. How many yoyos can Zack buy?

Joanne runs 10 miles from her home to the gym every day. After finishing her workouts at the gym, she decides to take a jog for an extra 30 minutes at a speed of 6 miles per hour. How many miles does Joanne run in total each day?

Emily wants to buy a new bicycle that costs $950. She saved $280 from her birthday gift and the rest were her earnings from her part-time job. If she works 5 days a week, how many weeks did Emily work to afford the bicycle?
Jim needs to save $300 for a gift. He saves $20 every week. How long will it take him to save enough money?
Sophie is 75 years old. Her granddaughter Karen is currently five times younger than she is. The mother of Karen - Patricia, is three times her age. How old is Patricia?
A contractor is building a playground and needs 70 loads of sand. If one dump truck can transport five loads, how many dump truck trips are necessary?

In medieval times, knights stationed in castles along the border would light fires to signal danger. Since the castles were located at 10 kilometer intervals, they could send a signal across the entire border. If the border was 800 kilometers long, and each castle had four knights, what was the total number of knights in the castles along the border?

Alden wants to build a rectangular garden that is 9 feet long and 7 feet wide. If he plans to build a fence around the garden with each section of the fence being 3 feet, how many sections will he need?
A restaurant has four tables that can each seat 6 people. Two tables can be put together to seat 10 people. If the restaurant is packed, how many people can it seat altogether?

Mary purchased some sea shells by the beach. She saw that from the first seller, 5% of the sea shells were damaged. From the second seller, 10% of the sea shells were damaged. The first seller sold her 150 sea shells. The second seller sold her 400. How many sea shells in total were damaged?
Alice sells handmade earrings for $8 a pair. She wants to earn at least $200 to donate to a charity. How many pairs of earrings does she need to sell to reach her goal?

Anya washes 32 hairs down the drain when she washes her hair and brushes out half that amount when she brushes it. How many hairs does Anya have to grow back to always have one more hair than she started with after washing, brushing, and growing it?

A writer is crowdfunding a new book project. He has three different book prices, each ten times higher than the last. He needs to get at least $10000 to publish his book. He accomplishes this by selling 3 books at the highest price, 2 books at the second highest price, and 5 books at the cheapest price. What was the price of the most expensive book?
A class of 24 students is planning a BBQ. If each student eats 2 hot dogs and each pack comes with 12 hot dogs, how many packs should they order?
A city has two football stadiums. One stadium can seat 20,000 people and the other can seat 15,000 more people than the first one. What is the total capacity of both stadiums?
In a school, there are 500 students. 40% of the students are boys, and the rest are girls. If 30% of the girls are members of the chess club, how many girl chess club members are there?

Leon had twenty rulers, and he wanted to share some of them with his two brothers, Zane and Flynn. Leon gave five rulers to Zane and five more rulers to Flynn than he gave to Zane. He kept the rest of the rulers. How many rulers did Leon keep?

The daily temperatures of Mountainpeak, Norway were recorded for 3 consecutive days. The temperatures were: -12 degrees Celsius, -10 degrees Celsius, and -5 degrees Celsius. What was the average temperature in Mountainpeak, Norway on these 3 days?

Emma is baking cookies for a party. She spends 15 minutes preparing the dough, twice as long baking it, and triple the combined preparation and baking time decorating. How long does it take her to finish making all the cookies?
Mark spends 2 hours doing his laundry and 4 times as much time studying. How much time does he spend on these activities in total?
Jenny buys a pair of jeans for $70, a shirt for $30, and a handbag for $200. How much money did she spend in total?

Emily wants 200 candies for her party. Emily has already made 56 cherry candies, and 74 berry candies. Emily wants to make an even number of chocolate and vanilla candies for the rest. How much of each should she make?
Jessica is 2 inches taller than Laura. Laura is 4 inches taller than Emma. If Emma is 55 inches tall, how many inches tall is Jessica?
Susan bakes 10 loaves of bread. Then she sells 3 loaves. If each remaining loaf of bread were to be sliced into 8 slices, how many slices of bread would Susan have in total?

Sid has a bug collection that contained 20 butterflies. His friend Larry gave him twice that amount of beetles. Then his cousin Johnny borrowed half of Sid’s beetles. How many bugs did Sid end up with?
An arcade offers packages where each game token costs $0.25. If Michael spends $10, how many tokens will he get from the arcade?

A restaurant uses 8 potatoes for every dish of potato salad it prepares. If a sack of potatoes with 80 potatoes costs $40, how much will the restaurant spend on potatoes to prepare 50 dishes of potato salad?

John and his friends were playing a card game. John initially had 5 cards, then he drew 4 times the number of cards he initially had. After a few rounds, he ended up giving away twice the initial number of cards he had. How many cards does John have now?
Jacob has a bag of 25 marbles. He gives 2 marbles to each of his 7 friends.  How many marbles does Jacob have left?
Anna and Bob went apple picking. Anna picked 3 times as many apples as Bob. Together, they picked 80 apples. How many apples did Bob pick?

Each month Jenny gets 6 pairs of socks as a reward for being the best employee of the month. If she initially had 80 socks, calculate the number of socks she'll have in 7 months assuming a pair contains 2 socks.
Billy earned twice as much money as Chris. Together they earned $100. How many dollars did Billy earn? Use C to represent Chris's earnings.

An airplane travelled for 12 hours on the first day, stopped for 2 hours, then travelled for 15 hours on the second day, stopping for 3 hours afterwards. If it consistently took 1 hour break after every 10 hours of travelling from the third day, how long, in hours, was the airplane not moving if the entire journey took 5 days?
Jane goes to a café. She buys a cup of coffee for $5 and a slice of cake for $3. She also leaves a tip of 15% of the total bill. How much does she pay in total?

In a factory, there are three types of machines: A, B, and C. The factory has twice as many machines A as B, and three times as many machines B as C. If there are 90 machines in total, how many machines C are in the factory?
A basketball team won 70% of their 50 games. How many games did they win?
A garden has 60 roses. In spring, each rose produces 5 new rosebuds. In summer, half of the rosebuds bloom into roses. How many roses are in the garden by the end of summer?
Sarah's sister is twice as old as Sarah's cat. If in 4 years, Sarah's sister will be 24 years, how old will Sarah's cat be in four years?
Jake runs 5 miles every day for a week. He also runs 10 miles on weekends. How many miles does he run in total in a week?
A factory produces 400 toys per hour. If the factory operates for 8 hours in one day, how many toys does it produce in one week assuming it operates 5 days a week?
A chef has 60 ounces of beef, 30 ounces of chicken and 10 ounces of fish in his fridge. If there are 16 ounces in a pound, how much meat does the chef has in pounds?

Emma bought 90 cupcakes for her birthday party. She expects to have 10 guests and wants to give each guest equal number of cupcakes. However, she suddenly remembers that she also needs to keep some cupcakes for her family of 4. How many cupcakes can each guest have if Emma saves 10 cupcakes for her family?
Lucy is 5 inches shorter than her sister Kate. Kate is 2 inches shorter than their friend Reese. If Reese is 48 inches tall, how tall is Lucy?
Lucy bought 5 kilograms of rice and 3 kilograms of potatoes at a store. One kilogram of rice costs $10 and one kilogram of potato costs $6. How much did her purchases cost?

John's dad wants to buy water bottles for John's football team. There are 25 players on the team and John's dad wants to give 2 bottles to each player. Each pack of water bottles has 6 bottles in it. How many packs should John's dad buy?

Daniel decides to equally distribute his collection of comic books to his 2 brothers. He has a total of 104 DC comics, 162 Marvel comics and 240 Dark Horse comics. How many comic books will each brother get?
Samantha baked 6 cakes. One cake had 12 slices and the other cakes each had 5 more slices than the first cake. How many slices of cake did Samantha bake?
Susie has a savings account and she wants to save exactly $480 in 8 months, how much should she save every month to reach her goal?
A pizza place sells cheese pizza for $12 and pepperoni pizza for $18. If a customer ordered 4 cheese pizzas and 3 pepperoni pizzas, how much is the total bill?

Cynthia harvested 67 potatoes from her garden. After washing them, she cut 13 of them into wedges. She then halved the remaining potatoes and made french fries with one half, and potato chips with the other half. If one potato can be cut into 8 wedges or make 20 potato chips, how many more potato chips than wedges did Cynthia make?
Andy has 50 boxes of chocolates. Each box has 18 chocolates. He gave half of the boxes to his friends. How many chocolates does Andy have left?
In a soccer tournament, a team won 15 matches, lost 10 matches and drew 5 matches. If a win gives 3 points, a draw 1 point and a loss 0 points, how many points did the team have at the end?
Lucy had $30 to spend at the toy shop. She bought a toy car for $6 and another toy doll for $9. She decides to spend the remaining money on $3 stickers. How many stickers can she buy?
Samantha has to read a 450-page book for her literature class. She plans to read the book in 15 days. How many pages does she need to read each day to finish the book in that time?

Jim's garden has 20 apple trees, 25 orange trees, and 30 pear trees. For making a fruit basket, Jim picks 40% of apples, 30% of oranges, and 50% of pears. If each tree gives 10 fruits, how many fruits will Jim have in his fruit basket?
Carl has 4 grandchildren. He sends each one a gift every month, and each gift costs $50. How much does he spend on gifts for his grandchildren in a year?
A grocery store sells 92 apples at 2 dollars each and 105 bananas at 1 dollar each. If the whole sale of the day consists of only these apples and bananas, how much did the store earn?
Malcolm earns $60,000 a year which he receives in 12 equal monthly payments. Every month, he saves 15% of his earnings. How much does he save each month?
John goes to the gym every other day and does 30 sit-ups each time. If he also does 20 push-ups each time he does sit-ups, how many sit-ups and push-ups would John do in a month of 30 days?
Eddie has saved $15. He wants to buy a video game that costs $30. How much more money does he need?
Ben has 11 apples in his bag. He distributes the apples to his 4 friends. How many apples does each friend get?
Luke is selling lemonade at a stand for a school project. Each cup of lemonade requires 300mL. If he has 6 liters of lemonade, how many cups can he make?

John, Sarah, and Carl decide to run every day to improve their endurance. John runs 10km per day, Sarah runs twice the distance John runs, and Carl runs half the distance Sarah runs. What's the combined distance they run in a week?

In a concert, a famous band sells merchandise for $20 each. A group of 10 fans bought some for themselves and also bought three times as many for their friends. Since they bought a total of 40 items, they were given a 10% discount for each item. How much did the group of fans pay in all?

Sam went to the farmers market and bought 3 kilograms of tomatoes that cost $2 each, 2 kilograms of lettuce that cost $3 each, and one basket of strawberries cost $4 each. How much did he spend in all?

Suzy bought four books for her English class. Each book cost $25. Her dad gave her $100 to cover the cost of the books. She also has to buy a notebook that costs $10, and a pack of pens that cost $5. How much money will Suzy have left?
If a pantry has 10 shelves and each shelf can hold 16 cans of food. How many cans of food can be stored in the pantry?
In a bakery, Mr. Baker made 8 batches of cookies and 5 batches of muffins. Each cookie batch contained 12 cookies, and each muffin batch had 10 muffins. How many baked items did Mr. Baker produce?
Lucas bought candy bars to share with his friends. He tasted one and shared the rest equally among 5 of his friends. Each friend received 4 candy bars. How many candy bars did Lucas buy?

Jack is selling cookies at a fundraiser. He sells chocolate chip cookies for $2 each and oatmeal cookies for $1.50 each. If he sold 20 chocolate chip cookies and 30 oatmeal cookies, how much money did he raise in total?
Tom is counting the birds in the park, but he gets confused and counts 5 pigeons twice and misses to count 2 sparrows. If Tom thinks there are 78 birds, how many are there actually?
Jennifer bought a car for $20,000. She made a down payment of $5,000 and will pay the remainder in 60 monthly installments. How much is each installment?
Alex can hit 3,000 tennis ball shots when he practices the whole day on a tennis court. If he only practiced half the time during the day, how many shots could he hit?
In a garden, there are 5 apple trees. Each tree has 40 apples. If a gardener picked 60 apples on the first day and 70 on the second day, how many apples will be left on the trees?
John bought 3 comic books for $2.50 each and a poster for $3.65. He has $10 left. How much money did John have originally?
Lance has 10 boxes of candies. Each box contains 15 milk candies, 20 fruit candies and 25 chocolate candies. If he sells each candy for $0.75, how much will he earn if he sells all?

A school is planning a field trip for the students. The budget for the trip is $500 and each student needs to pay a fee of $25. However, 5 students cannot pay the fee, and the school will cover their expenses. How many students can go on the trip?
In a year, Sam saves 20% of his $1000 monthly salary. Additionally, his mom gives him $500 every year. After 3 years, how much money will Sam have saved in total?

Lisa was at a buffet where you serve yourself and pay based on the weight. She gets 20 pieces of sushi. Then she gets an amount of shrimps, and the ratio of sushi to shrimps is 5:6. Then she gets an amount of crab legs, and they make up 30% of the food in her plate. How much food does she have in total?
A pizza place sells their large pizzas for $18 each and makes 60 large pizzas every day. If they decide to give a 20% discount on their pizzas for a day, how much total revenue do they lose?
In a classroom there are 30 students. 1/3 of them are boys. Among the girls, 40% own dogs, 20% own a cat, and the rest have no pets. How many girls in the class have no pet?
In the drawer, Emma has 45 pairs of socks and she fills the washing machine with 15 pairs. How many pairs of socks are left in her drawer?
A rectangle has a length of 5 inches and a width of 10 inches. A square has a width of 7 inches. What is the difference in area between the two shapes?
Liam runs a pizza parlor. He sells each pizza for $15. He also charges $5 for a delivery fee. Clare orders 6 pizzas from Liam and also tips him 15%. How much total, in dollars, did Liam receive?
A movie theater has 12 rows of seats with 10 seats per row. If each ticket is sold for $5 and all seats are full, how much money will the theater earn?
Fiona has a collection of 68 buttons that she wants to distribute equally among her four friends. If one of the buttons is too special to give away, how many buttons will each friend get?
A theater has 200 seats. In the morning show, half the seats are filled. The afternoon show has double the attendees of the morning show. How many people attended the shows in total?
In a basketball game, a three-point shot scores 3 points and a two-point shot scores 2 points. Matthew scores 10 three-point shots and 5 two-point shots. How many points did Matthew score in total?
