Joseph can paint a house in 2 days while Tom can do it in 3 days. How many houses can they paint together in a week?

In a basketball match, player A scored 20 points, player B scored twice as many points as player A, and player C scored 10 points fewer than player B. How many points were scored in all by these three players?

Michelle has 30 origami papers and she uses a fifth of her papers for a school project. Nichole has 50 origami papers and she uses a fourth of her papers for her project. After using their origami papers, how many origami papers do Michelle and Nichole have together?
Julia saved 300 coins in her piggy bank. William has a mason jar filled with 200 quarters. Steve has 500 pennies stashed inside a giant box. How much money did they save altogether?
A high-end watch costs $5000. A reseller wants to make a 20% profit. How much should he sell the watch?

A train can carry 300 passengers. One day, 35% of the total capacity was filled in the morning, and an additional 120 passengers boarded in the afternoon. How many more passengers can the train carry?
A fruit seller bought 14 watermelons. If each watermelon weighs 12 kg and the seller sold half of them, what was the total weight of the watermelons he sold?
A French teacher teaches 5 classes a day, with each class lasting for 40 minutes. If she works five days a week, how much time does she spend teaching in a week?
A high school baseball team plays 30 games in a season. If the team wins 60% of their games, how many games does the team win in a season?

Tom has a collection of toy cars. He has 100 red cars, 200 blue cars, and 150 green cars. If he decides to sell 50% of his red cars, 40% of his blue cars, and 30% of his green cars, how many cars will he have left in his collection?
A zoo has 25 monkeys and 3 times as many tigers. How many animals are there in total in the zoo?

A restaurant has a discount for lunch meals. If you eat between 12-2 pm, you get a 25% off discount. James chooses a meal that costs $30.00. If James ate at 1 pm, how much did he have to pay for his meal?

Tom, Bob and Mike are participating in an 800-meter relay race. Tom runs the first 300 meters, Bob runs the next 300 meters and Mike runs the final 200 meters. Tom takes 36 seconds for his part, Bob takes 38 seconds and Mike completes his part in 25 seconds. How long does it take for them to complete the relay?
Jeremy borrowed money from his brother. He promised to pay it back in 7 months including an additional 15% of the money he borrowed. If he pays $20 per month, how much money did he borrow?
A baker baked 50 loaves of bread a day for 6 days and then decided to donate 10% of all the bread to a charity. How many loaves of bread were donated?

A car travelling at a speed of 60 miles per hour travels a certain distance. How long would it take for the car to travel that same distance if it was travelling at a speed of 75 miles per hour instead?
Harper collects stickers. She has 30 stickers in her collection. If she adds 5 new stickers to her collection each week, how many stickers will Harper have in her collection after 8 weeks?

Max is in a running group that also includes 6 other people. The group runs 5 miles together every day for 4 days a week. In addition to this, Max runs an extra 3 miles alone each day for 4 days. If the group maintains this routine for a week, how many miles do they run in total?

Beth baked 15 loaves of bread, with 12 slices in each loaf. She kept 2/3 of the loaf for her bakery, stored 1/2 of the remaining slices in the freezer, and sold the rest. How many slices of bread were sold?
Lucy and her three friends decide to buy a pizza to share. The pizza costs $12. How much will each person need to pay?

There are 70 people waiting in line to ride a ferris wheel at a county fair.  The ferris wheel has 10 cars, and each car seats 5 people.  How many times will the ride operator have to run the ferris wheel to give everyone in line a turn?
Luke scored twice as many points in the second basketball game as he did in the first. If the total points he scored in both games is 36, how many points did he score in the first game?
In a class of 25 students, 10 students are on the volleyball team, 8 are on the basketball team, and the rest do not play on a sports team. How many students are not on a sports team?
A school has 4 classrooms. Each classroom has 6 tables, and each table can fit 4 students. If half of the tables are full, how many students are in the classrooms?
A movie theater has 300 seats and normally sells tickets for $8 each. They are having a promotion where tickets are 25% off. How much income does the theater make if all tickets are sold?
James has 4 toy cars. He bought 3 times as many cars as he originally had, then he was gifted 7 more cars. How many toy cars does James have now?
In a school, there are 150 students. In a random survey, it was found out that 60% like mathematics. How many students like mathematics?
A car depreciates in value by 15% each year. If the car was purchased for $20,000, how much will it be worth after 2 years?
There are 5 teachers in a school, each teacher has 18 students. If each of the students presents a gift box to their teacher on Christmas, how many gift boxes will the teachers receive collectively?
Rita baked 36 cupcakes for her son's class.  The class has 24 students and each student gets 1 cupcake. How many cupcakes does Rita have left?

Allison is an event organizer and she just finished setting up 5 events. In each event, there are 3 different rooms to set up. She took 2 hours to set each one up. How many hours total did she spend setting up all the events?
Jerry bought 8 packs of bubble gum and each pack contains 6 pieces of gum. If Jerry gives 3 pieces of bubble gum to each of his 12 friends, how many pieces of bubble gum will Jerry have left?

Firefighter Max needs to fill his truck with water. His hose can fill the truck at a rate of 50 gallons per minute. His associate, Amy, has a smaller hose that can fill 10 gallons per minute. How long, in minutes, would it take for them working together to fill the truck with 1800 gallons of water?
Lucy builds puzzles at a warehouse. She can assemble 25 puzzles an hour and works for 7 hours a day 6 days a week. How many puzzles does she make a week?

A supermarket sells 500 kilos of bananas every week. If the owner decides to sell them 20% cheaper one week, and sells 650 kilos as result, how many more kilos of bananas did the supermarket sell that week?
In four years, Alice will be twice as old as Ben is now. If Ben is 22, what is 5 years less than their average age?
A group of friends decided to have a pizza party. They ordered 7 pizzas and each pizza was sliced into 8 pieces. If each friend ate 4 slices, how many friends are in the group?
Martin runs a bakery. He makes 4 batches of cookies each day, with each batch containing 24 cookies. He sells each cookie for $2.5. How much does he make in a week?
Peter is reading a 360 pages book. He has already read 50 pages on the first day and 30 pages on the second day. How many pages does he still have to read?

Ben packed his backpack with 15 litres of water, 10 litres of juice, and 12 kg of food for his camping trip. During the trip, he drinks 1 litre of water per hour and 0.5 litre of juice. How much weight is he left with after four hours into the trip?
An apple tree produces 100 apples in a season. Mr. Wilson has 8 apple trees. How many apples do all of his trees produce in one season?

Emily has a garden filled with roses. She waters each rose with 2 liters of water. It takes her 5 minutes to pour each liter. If it takes Emily 3 hours to water all of her roses, how many roses does Emily have in her garden?
Paul has a 32 pack of chocolate bars. He ate 1/4 of them on Monday, and 1/3 of what was left on Tuesday. How many chocolate bars does Paul have left after Tuesday?
Richard and Jessica are collecting stamps. Richard has 50 stamps and Jessica has 80. What percentage of the total stamp collection does Richard have?
In a marathon, Emily completed the first 5 miles in 50 minutes. Assuming she maintained the same pace, how long would it take her to complete a full marathon of 26 miles?

Lindsey saved $50 in September, she saved $37 in October, and $11 in November. Lindsey's mom said that since Lindsey had saved more than $75,  she would give Lindsey $25.  Then Lindsey spent $87 on a video game. How much money did Lindsey have left?
Sam's bakery sells 36 muffins, and each muffin costs $1.5. If Sam gives a 20% discount on each muffin, how much will Sam make from selling all the muffins?

Jim's new gaming console cost $1200. He needs to get a controller and games that cost 1/6 that much. He also wants to upgrade the $150 headset in the base console with one that costs thrice as much. How much did he spend?
On a farm there are 5 times as many chickens as cows. If the total number of cows and chickens is 72, how many chickens are there on the farm?

A video game console costs $250 in a local store. If online stores sell it 25% cheaper, how much can Sam save by buying from an online store instead of the local store if he plans to purchase 2 units?
Miguel has a budget of $70 for groceries this week. If he bought a dozen eggs for $4 and three pounds of bread for $2 per pound. How much money remains in his budget?

Jesse is participating in a cycling competition. On the first day, he cycled 30 kilometers and on the second day he cycled 35 kilometers. If 1 kilometer is equal to 0.62 miles, what is the total distance Jesse cycled in miles for these two days?
Billy works two jobs. The first job pays $8 per hour and he works there for 6 hours. The second job pays him $11 per hour and he works there for 5 hours. How much does Billy make in total in a day?

In a sports festival, the tennis club has 3 times as many players as the badminton club. If the badminton club has 20 players and the basketball club has 10 more players than the tennis club, how many players joined the sports festival?

A group of hikers is taking a trail that usually takes 15 days to complete. They walk 8 hours each day. If they decide to extend their hiking hours to 10 hours a day, how many days will it take them to complete the trail?
John has 24 songs on his playlist. He wants to listen to all of them, but can only listen to 6 songs per hour. How many hours does it take to listen to all his songs?
Luke and Leia ordered 3 slices of pizza for $2 each, and 2 cans of soda for $1 each. The service charge is $1. Later, Luke decides to order 2 more soda cans. How much change should they get from $20?
Arthur has 8 books on the shelf. He bought twice as many books at the bookstore and then gave away a third of his total book collection to a liberary. How many books does Arthur have now?

Lila is making sweaters for her family. She needs 3 balls of yarn for each sweater. She has enough yarn to make 4 sweaters but decided to take some out to make 3 scarves. It takes 2 balls of yarn for each scarf. How many balls of yarn does Lila have?
In a basketball game, Lilly scored 2 fewer points than her teammate, who scored 7 points. How many points did Lilly and her teammate score together?

A travel agency has 200 bookings for a tour. However, 30% of the tourists cancelled their trip, and 15% of the remaining tourists chose to postpone their bookings. How many tourists actually went on the tour?
Lena runs 4 km every day. After running for 10 days, the total distance she covers will be?
Lucas sold 120 comic books last month. He sold thrice that number this month. How many comic books has Lucas sold in total?
A train travels at a speed of 45 mph and it needs to travel a distance of 360 miles. How long will it take for the train to arrive at the destination?
George has saved up $3200 to spend on his vacation. If he spends $400 per day during his vacation, how many days can George stay on vacation without exceeding his budget?
A baker makes 10 batches of cookies each day. Each batch contains 12 cookies. If she sells each cookie for $3 and all the cookies are sold out, how much will she earn?
Liam has three blocks of clay. Each block weighs 15 grams less than the 45-gram block he used yesterday. If he combines all three, how much grams does he have in total?

Yesterday, Peter went to a book store and bought a $20 worth of a book, a $15 worth of magazine, and a $10 notebook. He bought 5 books, 3 magazines, and 8 notebooks. How much did Peter spend in total?
A family decides to go to a theme park. The ticket for an adult costs $35 and a child’s ticket costs $20. If they have 2 adults and 3 children in their family, how much would it cost them in total?

A game center sells toy cars by the packs. Each pack costs $10 for 2 packs. Tom buys 4 packs, Jerry buys 3 packs, and Peter buys 6 toy cars. How many toy cars do they purchase in total, assuming 1 pack has 3 toy cars?
An airplane flies at a speed of 500 miles per hour. The distance from New York to Los Angeles is 2451 miles. How many hours does the airplane need to travel from New York to Los Angeles?
A toy manufacturing company produces 500 toys per day. Due to increasing demand, they decided to increase their production by 20%. How many toys will they produce in a week after this increase?
A city decides to plant 360 trees in a park. If 1/4 of the trees are oaks, 1/3 are pines, and the rest are maples, how many maples are there?
Harry did 30 push-ups every day for one week. Bob did 25 sit-ups on each of five days. How many exercises did Harry and Bob do in total?

Ada needs to gather apples for a big cookout. Ada needs 25 pounds of red apples, 30 pounds of green apples, and needs 1/4 as many pounds of yellow apples as green. How many total pounds of apples does she need?
In a basketball match, there were 500 spectators seated and 50 standing. If 25 people left before the game ended, how many people total watched the match?
An apple weighs 150 grams. An orange is twice as heavy as the apple and a watermelon is 3 times as heavy as the orange. How much does the watermelon weigh?
John is twice as tall as his son Peter. If Peter is 4 feet tall, how tall is John?
A pack of 12 pencils costs $3. If they are sold individually for $0.30 each, how much will be the total savings from buying 5 packs of pencils instead of buying them individually?

A journey from Toronto to Vancouver takes 5 hours by plane. The train takes 3 times as long for the same journey. If Tim spends 2 hours layover in Vancouver before taking the train back to Toronto, how long will his round trip take in total?
A kinder garden class operates from 10 am to 2 pm every Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. How many hours does the class run in a week?
Lisa designs a balloon that can float 200 ft above the ground. She designs a second balloon that can float three times as high. What is the combined height of the two balloons?
Bob has built a rectangular dog pen with length 10 feet and width 7 feet. If he decided to add 3 feet onto width side. What will be the new area of the rectangular pen?
In a marathon, John ran 10 kilometers every day for a week. How many kilometers did he run at the end of the week?
Thomas is filling his backpack with books. Each book weighs 2 lbs. He already put 10 books in his backpack. If he adds 3 more books, what will the total weight of his backpack be?

David is planning to visit the zoo in the city with 7 of his family members. The entrance fee for children is $3 and for adults is $7. If there are 4 adults and 4 children, including David, what is the total cost for them to enter the zoo?

Justin found a buried treasure chest. He spent one third of the treasure reward on a car. He then gives away half of the remaining reward to his best friend. If he has $163 left, how much money, in dollars, did he find in the treasure chest at first?

A concert hall sold 30 more than four times the number of tickets to the rock concert as it did to the classical music concert. If the hall sold 60 tickets to the classical music concert, how many tickets did it sell to the rock concert?
Lisa saves $4,500 for her vacation to Japan. If she spends $1,500 on the flight, $600 on a hotel, and $2,000 on food and souvenirs, how much money, in dollars, does she have left?
A store sells candies in packets. Each packet contains 20 candies. James bought 5 packets and gave 2 packets to his brother. How many candies does James have now?
On Anna's farm, she has 15 cows, 10 pigs, and 25 chickens. She sold 5 cows and bought 10 more chickens. How many animals does Anna now have on her farm?

David adds 4 tablespoons of coffee and 20 tablespoons of water to his french press. After brewing, he ends up drinking 5 tablespoons of the coffee. He then doubles the amount of water in the press and drinks it. What percentage of the final liquid David drank was coffee?
Samantha has a garden with 15 rose bushes and 10 sunflower plants. She plans to add 5 more sunflower plants to her garden. How many plants will Samantha have in total?
A bakery makes 15 dozens of cookies each day. Each dozen contains 12 cookies. If the bakery operates for 7 days a week, how many cookies does it make in a single week?
Jane's chameleon eats one insect every 3 days. How many insects will it eat in a quarter of a year?

Mr. Johnson is a teacher in a school. He started his day by grading 30 answer sheets in the first hour, 25 in the second hour, and 35 in the third hour. If he continues grading at the same pace, how many answer sheets will he grade in 8 hours?

An art gallery sells paintings for $200 each. A certain individual bought 3 paintings for his office and also bought three times as many for his home. There was a 10% discount for the purchase since the individual bought more than 5 paintings. How much did he pay in all?
A bag contains 100 marbles, 70 of which are blue. If 2/5 of the blue marbles and 1/4 of the marbles of the other colors are taken out, how many marbles are left?

Andy has a calm cat and an energetic dog. He has to play with the energetic dog three times for every one time he plays with the calm cat. If he plays with the calm cat 10 times, how many times does he play with both pets combined?

Liam drives a truck for a living. He works 5 days a week, and each day he drives 8 hours. For half that time, he is carrying a load, and the rest of the time the truck is empty. If the time carrying a load is y, then the time driving empty is also y. If he rests for one-third of the time he is carrying a load, how much time in hours does he spend resting each day?
John uses 5 cups of flour to bake 20 cookies. If he plans on baking 60 cookies for a party, how many cups of flour will he need?
Pete's garden is 8 meters wide and 15 meters long. He wants to build a fence around it. The fence costs $12 per meter. How much will he spend on the fence?
Billy is 4 times older than his younger brother Bob. If Bob is 7 years old now, how old was Billy 5 years ago?
Richard is 12 years old. His brother is 4 years older than him. How old will Richard be when his brother is 50?

Molly is selling lemonade for a charity. She ends up raising a total of $500. Molly keeps $200 to pay for the cost of lemons and sugar. She gives away half of the remaining total to the children's hospital, and the other half to an orphanage. Molly also decides to contribute $30 from her savings to the children's hospital. How much money in total does Molly donate to the hospital?
David has $80. He wants to surprise his dad on Father's day by buying a shirt for $45, a cap for $25, and a pair of socks for $8. How much more money does David need to buy all those?
Marcie bought 6 t-shirts at $4 each. She also bought pairs of socks at $3.00 each. If she paid a total of $34 for the t-shirts and socks, how many pairs of socks did Marcie buy?

Benjamin sold some loaves of bread at his bakery. Each loaf costs $3. He sold 75 loaves on Monday, 80 loaves on Tuesday and 100 loaves on Wednesday. How much money did he make from selling bread during these three days?

Tom has 50 energy drinks he has to distribute equally among the team members of a basketball team. If the basketball team consists of 12 members, how many energy drinks does Tom serve each player? And how many does he have left after each player received an equal number?
Jill can run up a hill at 9 feet/second and down a hill at 12 feet/second. How long does it take her to run up and down a 900 foot hill?

John has 4 aquariums to take care of. 1 aquarium contains 6 goldfish and 7 guppies. The second aquarium contains three times as many fish as the first aquarium and the third aquarium has half the number of fish in the second aquarium. How many fish are in the third aquarium?

John needs to travel 300 miles to visit his friend. If he has already traveled 120 miles and his car has an average speed of 50 miles per hour, how many more hours will he need to reach his friend's house?
Jessy is collecting coins. She found 5 silver coins each week for a month of 4 weeks. If she already had 12 silver coins, how many does she have now?

A headphone store is offering a discount of 10% off for customers who buy at least 4 headphones at once. Frank, Anne and Sergio want to buy a Beats headphone each. If a Beats headphone costs $200, how much can they save by pooling their money together and buying four headphones at once from this store rather than buying individually?

Jenny needs to pay for an expensive dental operation. She has had dental insurance for 3 years that cost $50 per month. The operation costs $6000 but her insurance only covers 60% of the cost. How much money did she save by having insurance?
Billy and Jane are collecting stamps. Billy has 120 stamps and Jane has 35. How many more stamps does Billy have than Jane?

Rick has a collection of 35 comic books. He reads 2 comic books the first week, 3 comic books the second week and then 5 comic books every week after that. How many weeks will it take Rick to finish reading his collection?
A fruit seller has 15 apples and 12 oranges. Each apple costs $1.20 and each orange costs $0.90. How much do all the fruits cost?
Rachel is training for a marathon and she runs 5 miles each day. If the marathon is 26.2 miles, how many days does she need to train to run the equivalent of a marathon?

Lisa and Stacy went to the bookstore. Lisa bought 3 novels at $8 each and some pens for $5 total. She spent a total of $35. How many novels did Stacy buy if she bought the same amount of novels as Lisa and spent a total of $27?

Tom is participating in a beach clean-up filling bags with trash. He was able to collect three bags of trash himself. The rest of the people together collected 70 times as much as Tom did by himself. Each bag of trash weighs 5 pounds. How many pounds was all the trash they collected?
Tim had 2000 baseball cards. He gave his two friends 250 cards each. What's two times the number of cards he has remaining?
Tom earns $25 per hour. His workday is usually 8 hours long, but if he works overtime, he will earn 50% more per hour. If Tom works 10 hours, how much will he earn that day?
In a race, Sam takes 4 minutes to complete one lap and Jenny takes 5 minutes. If they both start at the same time and race for an hour, how many more laps does Sam complete than Jenny?
Sara weighs 10 pounds more than her sister Emily. Emily weighs 7 pounds more than their mother. If their mother weighs 130 pounds, how much does Sara weigh?
Harold saved $15 per month from February to June and saved $25 per month from July to October. How much should he save in December so he will have a total savings of $200 in the year?
Jack earns $15 per hour and he works 8 hours every day. How much does he earn in a week?

Belinda is counting birds in the sky. She knows that of the birds that she sees, 30% will be sparrows and 70% will be other birds. In the morning she counted 10 birds. In the afternoon, she counted twice as many. How many sparrows did she count overall?
Kevin finds 4 seashells on the beach each day for 3 years. If there were no leap years, how many seashells did he find in total?
Emily is a writer. She aims to write 500 pages for her new book. She writes 50 pages every week. After 7 weeks how many pages are left to be written?
Lucy bought 75 episodes of the show 'Breaking Bad' online. Each day, Lucy watches 1/5 of the episodes she bought. How many days will Lucy need to finish 75 episodes of the show she bought?
Fred wants to build a patio that is 12 feet by 15 feet. How many 3 feet by 3 feet tiles will he need to cover the entire patio?

At a school's fund-raising event, there are 120 tickets to be sold. Half of them are adults tickets and the rest are children's tickets. If all the adult tickets were sold and 30 children's tickets were unsold, how many tickets were sold in total?

A sunflower was 150 cm tall on March 1 and was growing 10 cm each week. After 4 weeks, the gardener added a 2-meter tall stake to support the plant. How tall was the plant including the stake at that point?
In a garden, there are 8 trees and each tree gives 12 oranges each year. If all the oranges are sold at 2 dollars each, what will be the total revenue in 3 years?
A factory produces 20 cars per day. After a month, the factory increases its production by 15%. How many cars is the factory now producing a day?
A truck travels at an average speed of 40 miles per hour. If the truck keeps this average speed and needs to travel 200 miles, how long will it take to get to its destination?

Chris does 10 pushups every day. Over the month, he increased by 30% the number of pushups he does each day. How many pushups is he doing each day by end of the month, and how many pushups will he do in total over the month?

30% of the computer programmers resign after 6 months and another 20% of the remaining leave before the end of the year. If 50 programmers are employed at the start of the year, how many will be there at the end of the year?
Lucas travels 30 miles in 1 hour by bus. If the bus stops for 30 minutes for a break, how far will Lucas travel in 1.5 hours?
Bill has a box of 60 pencils. He arranges them equally in 5 jars. He then gives away 2 jars. How many pencils does Bill have left?

Abby is a professional tutor. On Monday, Wednesday, and Friday, Abby tutors 4 students. On Tuesday, she tutors 6 students. And on Thursday, she tutors 5 students. She is paid $20 for each student she tutors. How many dollars does Abby earn in a week?
Joseph bought 2 dozens of oranges. He wants to distribute all the oranges equally among 6 friends. How many oranges can each friend get?
Julie has 75 notebooks. She decided to donate 2/5 of her notebooks to a local school and the rest to the library. How many notebooks did Julie donate to the library?
John's farm has 100 acres of land. He wants to plant corn on 60% of his land, and soybean on 25% of the remaining land. How many acres does he have left?
Leo, Lily and Lana have a total of 2400 books. Leo has 5 times as many books as Lily, and Lily has triple the number of books that Lana has. How many books does Leo have?
A bag of apples weighs 2 kilograms. If Tim buys 3 bags of apples and 2 bags of oranges that each weigh 1.5 kilograms, how much do all of the fruits weigh?

Eric bowled 489 points during the league. There were 9 matches during the league. He averaged 3 strikes (worth three points), and 6 spares per match (worth 2 points). How many one-pointers did he average a match?
Larry cuts 5 yards of fabric to make a curtain. He cuts another 8 yards for a tablecloth and 2 more yards for napkins. If fabric costs $10 per yard, how much did he spend for the fabric?
Peter is saving money for a new bicycle. He puts in his piggy bank half of his weekly allowance of $20. If he also finds and puts in his piggy bank $5 each week, how much will he save in 5 weeks?
Lionel walked 4 miles. Esther walked 975 yards and Niklaus walked 1287 feet. How many combined feet did the friends walk?
Anna has 7 chickens. Each chicken lays 2 eggs a day. How many eggs will Anna have after 5 days?
For his birthday, Jordan received 3 kinds of presents: books, video games, and board games. There were 7 books, 4 video games, and 5 board games. How many presents did Jordan receive in total?

Jamie start a campaign for president. He gets $3000 donation from 1200 people, and four times as many people donated a third of this sum of money to his campaign. These donations together accounted for 50% of the total money that Jamie raised. How much total money did Jamie raise?

Jerry has 24 books in his library. He read 25% of his books while on vacation and 5 more books when he comes back. After lending some books to his neighbor, he realizes he has only left 35% of his original number of books. How many books did Jerry lend to his neighbor?
Leah and Mary decided to sell their homemade cookies. In a week, Leah sold 120 cookies, while Mary sold 1/3 less. How many cookies did Mary sell?
A delivery company charges $15 for the first 10 miles, then $2 for each additional mile. What would be the cost for a 25-mile delivery?
There are 5 pens in a box. Lisa has 6 boxes of pens. How many pens does she have in total?
In a parking lot, there are thrice as many blue cars as red cars, and red cars are half as many as white cars. If there are 30 white cars, how many cars are there in the parking lot in total?

Bridget has a honeybee farm. For each inch of honeycomb, she collects 10 ounces of honey. On Tuesday it accumulated 5 inches and on Wednesday it accumulated 6 inches. She sells honey for $1.5 per ounce. How much money did she earn from selling all the honey?
Shirley has a garden where she planted 10 tomato plants. Each plant produces 18 tomatoes. She plans to put 6 tomatoes in each jar for canning. How many jars will she need?
Sarah has 10 lollipops. Thomas gives Sarah three times as much as she already has. Sarah then gives Thomas back half of her new total plus 3. How many lollipops does Sarah have now?
A library has 10 shelves with 35 books on each shelf. Each book has 150 pages. How many pages are there in total in the library?
In a zoo, there are 6 lions, 12 tigers, 18 elephants, and 9 bears. How many animal legs are there in the zoo in total?
In a class of 32 students, the ratio of boys to girls is 3:5. If 4 boys and 6 girls were transferred to another school, how many girls are left in the class?
A vessel contains 40% water and the remaining 60% is oil. If the vessel's capacity is 500 liters, how many liters of oil does the vessel contain?
John takes 5 minutes to peel each potato and 10 additional minutes to peel each onion. If he needs to peel 2 potatoes and 3 onions, how long will it take him?
Eva spends half of her $60 allowance on crafts and saves a fourth of what is left. How much money does Eva save?

Sam's garden has 4 trees. Each tree produces 6 apples each day. If he plants another 2 trees and all the trees continue to produce the same amount of apples every day, how many apples will Sam have after a period of 15 days?

Samuel has two toy boxes. One box has 30 Lego bricks and 40 Lego figures. The other box has double the amount of Lego bricks and half the amount of Lego figures. How many Lego items does Samuel have in total?
A manufacturer produces one toy in 5 minutes. How many toys can it produce in 1 hour?
A movie theater has 15 rows with 20 seats in each row. If the price for each ticket is $8, how much money does the theater make if all the seats are filled for a single show?
Jacob is buying school supplies. He needs notebooks which cost $7 each. If he has coupons that would give him a 20% discount on each notebook, how much would he pay for 5 notebooks?
Grace has four times more pencils than Jenny and Jenny has three times more than Cindy. If Grace has 16 pencils, how many pencils do Cindy and Jenny have together?
Tommy has a garden that is 8 feet wide. The length of the garden is twice its width and 4 times its height. What is the volume of the garden?

A fitness instructor is planning a circuit training program. He will run four stations, each with a different exercise. If the program needs to be completed in a total of 60 minutes, how long in minutes should each exercise station be?
Richard grew 6 apple trees. The first year, 25% of the trees did not bear any fruit. How many apple trees bore fruit in the first year?

Henry bakes 100 loaves of bread in a day. He sells them for $15 each and uses 2/5 of the sales to purchase ingredients for the next day's baking. How much money does Henry keep after buying the ingredients?

Danny is in charge of snacks for his soccer team's party. He decided to bring granola bars that come in packs of 4. Danny has 15 team members, 2 coaches and 5 parent volunteers. How many packs of granola bars does he need to buy?
In a chess tournament, Ben won 15 games, which was two-third the number of games won by Daniel. Sara won half the games that Daniel won. How many total games did they win?
There are 4 shelves in a bookstore. Each shelf accommodates 100 books. Every day 10% of the total books are sold out. How many books are left in the bookstore after one week?
Mandy, Mark and May all have different types of plants. Mandy has 5 plants. Mark has 4 more than three times as many plants as Mandy. May has 5 fewer plants than Mark.  How many plants does May have?

Emma has 200 candies to sell at her candy store. She plans to keep the candies she doesn’t sell by the end of the day. In the morning, she sells 2 dozen candies. During the lunchtime, she sells 45 candies. In the afternoon, she sells 30 more candies. How many candies does she have left to keep?

Ella is baking cupcakes for a party. Each cupcake requires $0.50 worth of ingredients, and she's making 24 cupcakes. She also bought a $5 decoration set. How much did she spend on the cupcakes and decorations?
Leah's car can travel 350 miles on a full tank of fuel. If her car's fuel tank has 10 gallons, how many miles per gallon can Leah's car travel?
Paul owns a dairy farm with cows. 5 cows produce 35 gallons of milk in 7 days. Paul decided to buy 10 more cows. How many gallons will all cows produce in 21 days?
A box contains 50 lego blocks. 15 blocks are red, 10 are blue, and there are 2/5 the number of green blocks as blue blocks. How many blocks have no color?
Oliver has 7 shirts. He bought 3 more shirts every month for 5 months. How many shirts does Oliver have after 5 months?
Samantha has a monthly salary of $1200. She spends 40% of her salary on rent, 30% on groceries and 20% on utilities. How much money does she have left?
In a musical band, the number of girls is three times the number of boys. If there are 4 boys, how many members are in the band in total?

Sarah wants to crochet some scarves for a charity event. She has 80 yards of yarn and she knows each scarf requires 5 yards of yarn and 2 hours to make. How much time does she need to crochet all the scarves?
Amy's mother gives her $100 for her birthday. She spends 1/4 of it on a new book and 1/5 of it on a new shirt. She also wants to buy a pair of shoes that cost $40. How much money will she have left?
Sally bought herself an ice cream for $3 and a soda for $2. Her friend, Betty, bought a soda, but decided not to get an ice cream. How much did Betty pay for her soda?
Liam has a collection of 20 books. He gives away 5 out of the 20 he had to his friend but he still has three times as many as Noah. How many books do Liam and Noah have together?
Lucas, Mike and David decided to go on a 2000 miles long road trip. Lucas drove 10 miles more than twice what Mike drove, and Mike drove 4 times as many miles as David. How many miles did Mike drive?

John wants to build a three-tiered bird house. He needs 3 wooden panels for the smallest tier. If the second tier needs twice as many panels as the first and the third tier needs four times as many as the second, how many total wooden panels will he need for the third tier?
Jack owns a bookstore. He has 500 books in total. 75% of the books are fiction and the rest are non-fiction. How many non-fiction books does he have?

In a Friday night pub quiz, a team of 8 friends decided to participate. The entrance fee of the quiz is $400 and they plan to share the cost equally. They already had a yard sale to help raise funds, which would be subtracted from the total cost. The remaining cost would be split between the 8 friends. At the yard sale, they earn $200. However, 2 of them got sick and could no longer join the quiz. How much more does each friend have to pay now that 2 aren't participating?

Sam and Leo need a total of 18 figurines to complete their collection. Sam has a box of 12 figurines and 50% of them are needed. Leo has a box of 10 figurines and 60% of them are needed. How many figurines will they need to buy to complete their collection?

Mike buys 6 rose bushes at 75 dollars each, 2 of them are for his friend and the rest are for him.  He also buys 2 tiger tooth aloes for 100 dollars each.  How much money did he spend on plants for himself?
There are 60 cookies in the jar.  Mary takes 1/3 of them to the picnic.  John takes away half of the remaining cookies, how many cookies are left?
Susan bought a shirt for $30 and a pair of jeans for $50. She had a coupon for 20% off her total purchase. How much did she save with her coupon?
John has 60 marbles and 4 times as many toy cars. If he gave 5 marbles and a quarter of his toy cars to his friend, how many more toy cars than marbles does he have left?
The ratio of red to blue marbles in a bag is 4:9. If there are 5 more blue marbles than red ones, how many marbles are there in total?
A boat can travel 30 miles in an hour. How far can the boat travel in 8 hours?
Ella planted 50 flower bulbs in her garden. Ten of the bulbs didn't sprout. Half of the remaining bulbs were eaten by pests. How many flowers did Ella end up with?
Cindy walks 20 miles per day for the first 120 days of the year. For the rest of the days of the year she walks 25 miles per day. How many miles does she walk for the year?

Samantha is sewing 225 pillowcases for a charity event. On Monday, she sews 40% of the pillowcases. On Tuesday, she sews 50% of the remaining pillowcases. On Wednesday, how many more pillowcases does she need to sew?
Shelly has $5000 saved up. She wants to spend only 25% of it on a designer label bag. How much would she be spending on the bag?
There are 200 students in a school. Out of these, 30% are boys. How many girls are there in the school?

A delivery van can go 5 miles per gallon of fuel. The delivery guy needs to travel to a destination 150 miles away. He already has 10 gallons of fuel in his van. How much more fuel does he need to reach his destination?
Alexias has 10 boxes of chocolates. Each box contains 50 chocolates and a chocolate is priced at $2. If she managed to sell 2/5 of the chocolates, how much money did she make?

Mr. Smith earns $4,000 a month. His house rental is $500 each month; his monthly food expense is $200; his electric and water bill costs 1/5 of what he makes, and his insurances cost 1/4 of what he makes. How much money does he have left after paying those four bills?

For many years, the number of lions in Savannah has been three times the number of hyenas in Jungle Plain. Today, biologists say one-fourth of the hyenas in Jungle Plain have moved to Savannah Plain. If there are still 30 lions in Savannah, how many hyenas remain in Jungle Plain?
Liam and Niel went to a bookstore. Liam bought 5 books costing $22 each and Niel bought 4 books costing $15 each. How much total money did they spend in the bookstore?
Tommy's bicycle needs a new chain. A chain costs $25. Tommy's mom gives him an allowance and increases it by 50%. The allowance is now $15, so how much did Tommy receive before the increase?
Tina has a lemonade stand. On Monday and Tuesday, she sells 25 cups each day. On Wednesday, she sells twice as many as she did on Monday. How many cups of lemonade did she sell in those three days?
A company has 120 employees. 70% of the employees work from home. Of the employees who don't work from home, one-third walk to work. How many employees walk to work?
Andres earned $3000. Peter earned one third of what Andres earned. How much money did the two men earn together?
Lisa baked 70 cookies and brought them in containers to a picnic. Each container holds 5 cookies. If she decided to keep 6 containers for herself, how many cookies did she bring to the picnic?

Kit runs 5 miles each day for training. After running the same distance for a week, she decides to increase the daily distance by 2 miles. If she maintains the new daily distance for another week, how many miles does she run in total after two weeks?
Bradley has a coin collection with 15 coins each from United States, France, and Canada. How many coins does he have in total?

Bella had an anniversary party and she invited 15 of her coworkers, asking them to each bring a guest. If two-thirds of the invited coworkers each brought a guest, how many people attended the party, including Bella?

Peter has a farm and calculates that each of his 40 sheep needs 2kg of hay a day and additionally his 30 cows need 5kg each. If he wants to prepare for 10 days in advance, how much hay does he need in total?

At a café, a cup of coffee costs $4 and a muffin costs $3. After selling 10 cups of coffee and 5 muffins, they reduced the prices by half for the remaining items. They sold 8 more cups of coffee and 4 more muffins. How much money did the café make?
Alex works in a toy store. He wraps 12 toys in an hour. If he works for 8 hours a day and 5 days a week, how many toys can he wrap in a week?

Lana was on a diet and switched from eating 3 slices of pizza a day to eating salad. Each slice of pizza has 200 calories and a salad has 350 calories. If Lana ate salad for 5 days, how many calories did she save?
Jake has a toy car collection. He has 40 toy cars. 5/8 of them are race cars. How many race cars does he have?
Anita needs 5 pieces of colored paper to make a flipbook. Each pack of colored paper has 20 sheets. If Anita wants to make 50 flipbooks, how many packs of colored paper does she need to buy?
Kiki bought 2 dresses for $80 each, a pair of shoes for $60, and a coat for $120. What percent of the total cost are the dresses?
Mike is a beekeeper who has 5 bee hives. Each hive produces 80pounds of honey annually. If he sells the honey for $10 per pound, how much money does Mike earn in one year?
Jordan saved $1355 during the last year. In January, she saved $400 more than in February. If she saved the same amount each month, how much did she save each month?

Mike is packing for a camping trip. The comforter takes up twice the space as the tent and the backpack takes up half the space of the tent. If the tent takes up 15 L, how many liters in total does Mike need for the trip?
Mary received a box of chocolates for her birthday. The box contained 30 chocolates, 1/3 of which were dark chocolates. How many chocolates were not dark chocolates?
John has 5 packs of stickers. In each pack, there are 8 stickers. If John gives 2 stickers to each of his friends, how many friends can receive stickers from John?

Sam likes to collect stamps. He currently owns 600 stamps, 200 of which are from Asia. The rest are from America and Europe, with America’s counting twice the number from Europe. How many stamps from Europe does Sam have?
There are 14 pieces of sushi in a box. If Lee ate 5 pieces and Kim ate 3 pieces, how many pieces of sushi are left?
At Katie's birthday party, there are 3 pizzas. Each pizza has 8 slices. If each of her 10 friends eats 2 slices, how many slices will be left?

Alex has $65 and he wants to buy notebooks and pens for school. Each notebook costs $5 and each pen costs $1. If he wants to buy 5 times more notebooks than pens, then how many pens can he afford to buy?

Samantha is baking cupcakes for a party. She made 8 batches of cupcakes, and each batch made 12 cupcakes. 3 batches were chocolate, 2 were vanilla, and the rest were strawberry. How many strawberry cupcakes did she make?
Sarah wants to purchase pencils for her class. Each student needs 4 pencils, and there are 25 students in the class. If each pencil costs $0.5, how much will Sarah spend on pencils for her class?
In a classroom, there are 25 students. Each student has 4 pencils. How many pencils are there in total?
Clara spends 40% of her money on clothes and 20% on food. She has $300 left. How much money did she have originally?

Rachel has 180 minutes to spend on her art projects each day. She spend 60 minutes on watercolor paintings, 90 minutes on pencil sketches, and the rest of the time on oil paintings. How many minutes does she spend on oil paintings?

Emma has to read 300 pages for her literature class. She read 70 pages on the first day and has 4 days left. How many pages does she need to read each day for the remaining 4 days to finish her reading?
Peter studies 5 hours a day from Monday to Friday. He then spends half this time studying on Saturday and doubles this on Sunday. How many hours does Peter study in total over the week?
A chocolate factory produces 200 chocolates per day. Each chocolate cost $0.75. What will be the total cost of chocolates produced in 30 days?

Julia bought a book online which cost her three times as much as the one she bought in the store. She also had to pay for shipping charges which were 70% of the price of the book she ordered. If the book in the store cost her $10, how much did Julia pay in total to get the book online?

Each morning Bob spends 10 minutes searching for his glasses and another 5 minutes remembering where he put his phone. If Bob starts keeping his belongings in the same place, how many minutes will he save every week?
At a pet shop, a tropical fish costs $15, a bird costs $25 and a rabbit costs $40. If someone buys 8 tropical fishes, 6 birds, and 2 rabbits, how much would the total cost be?
Jason has 15 comic books. His friend, Derek, has 7 more comic books than Jason, and Mark has 10 fewer comic books than Jason. How many comic books do Mark and Derek have together?

Jennifer bought 2 dresses for $120 each, 3 pairs of shoes for $75 each, and 4 belts for $30 each. She received a $50 discount for being a loyal customer. What is the total cost of the products after the discount?
A company produced 10,000 televisions this year. This is double the amount they produced last year. If a half of this year’s production is sold, how many televisions are remaining?
A baker uses 3 eggs for each loaf of bread and 2 eggs for each batch of cookies. If he makes 10 loaves of bread and 15 batches of cookies, how many eggs does he use?
A box contains 56 toy cars. 5 friends decided to divide the toys evenly. If there are any remaining toys, they will be donated. How many toy cars will be donated?
The number of people in a room is 35. 12 more people enter the room and 17 people leave the room. How many people are there in the room in total at the end?
A mathematician is working on a complex formula. He starts with the number 10, multiplies it by 5, then adds 50. He then divides the result by 2 and subtracts 7. What is the final result?
Robert runs a lemonade stand. He sells each cup for $2 and makes 50 cups a day. If his expenses amount to $30 per day, how much is his profit in a week?
Billy has $85 in his savings box. If Billy buys a toy car for $10 and a board game for $12. How much money does Billy have left?
Linda has $300 in her savings account. She wants to save $50 every month for a year. How much will Linda have in her savings account after a year?
Kimberly made 80 cookies for a bake sale. She sold 65% of them on the first day. How many cookies did Kimberly have left?

Billy bought 6 balloons for his sister's birthday party: 2 red, 2 blue and 2 yellow. The red balloons cost twice as much as the yellow ones, and the blue ones cost half as much as the red. If Billy paid $18 in total, how much, in dollars, did a single yellow balloon cost?
Alice gave one-third of her 18-meter rope to Lisa. She then gave half of the remaining to Mike. How many meters of rope is left with Alice?
Alex spends 2 hours each day on homework. If Alex spends the same amount of time on homework each day, how many hours will he spend on homework in a week?
Joe is making a smoothie with apples, oranges, and bananas using a ratio of 2 : 3 : 4. If he uses 6 apples, how many pieces of fruit does he use total?
Lisa bought a dress for $100. She got a 10% discount on her purchase. After the discount, the sales tax applied was 5%. How much she has to pay at the end?

The basketball club is going on a trip and they have 2 buses and 2 minivans available. Each bus can seat 20 people and each minivan can seat 8. They have a total of 38 people going on the trip. How many more people could have been accommodated?
A school has 100 students, and 30% of them are on the basketball team. Another 20% are on the chess team. How many students are not on either of these teams?
Matthew has to read a book of 500 pages for a school assignment. He can read 50 pages in an hour. If he has already read 100 pages, how many more hours will it take him to finish the book?

Ella has a garden with 3 types of fruits: apple, banana and cherry. She has 5 apple trees, each yielding 10 apples, 10 banana trees, each yielding 20 bananas, and 15 cherry trees, each yielding 30 cherries. How many fruits does Ella harvest in total?

Billy finished reading an entire novel in 7 days, reading an average of 6 hours per day. To complete the book, however, he needs to study each page twice. If he can study an average of 10 pages per hour, how many pages does the novel have?
In a fruit shop, apples cost $3 per kg, bananas cost $2 per kg and cherries cost $5 per kg. If Jack bought 2 kg of apples, 3 kg of bananas and 1 kg of cherries, how much did Jack spend?
A field trip is being planned for all 30 students in a class, and each student is allowed to bring 2 guests. How many people are expected to go on the field trip in total?

Ted has to travel from city A to city B which is 120 miles away. His car consumes 1 gallon of gas for every 20 miles. If the price of gas is $2.5 per gallon, how much will Ted spend on gas for his trip?
Nicholas saved $15 every week for 3 months in order to buy a new guitar. How much did he save in total?
John bought a game online that was 25% off its regular price of $60. How much did John pay for the game?
Samantha runs a food truck that sells burgers for $10 each. She pays $4 to produce each burger and $1 of the price is tax. If she sold 50 burgers on a busy day, how much did she make in profit?
A farmer has a rectangular field that is 200 meters long and 150 meters wide. What is the area of the field in acres, if 1 acre is approximately 4046.86 square meters?
James often buys his lunch at school. This week he bought 3 hamburgers each costing $4, 2 bags of chips each costing $1.5, and 4 sodas each costing $2. How much did James spend on lunch this week?

Madison hosts a baking competition every Sunday, and she buys 15 butter packets for every cake bakes. If one butter packet costs $2 and she bakes 3 cakes every Sunday of the month, how much does she spend on butter in a month?
If Paul runs a distance of 7 kilometers each day for 5 days in a week and chooses to rest for the remaining 2 days, how many meters does he run in a week?
Amy has $50 in her savings account. Each week, she adds $12 to the account. How much money will Amy have in the account after 9 weeks?
Paul has twice as many bananas as Sarah, and Sarah has 3 times as many bananas as Mark. If Paul has 18 bananas, how many bananas does Mark have?

Sarah is working on a community painting project. She paints 4 murals herself. The rest of her community collectively paints 70 times as much as Sarah did by herself. If each mural weighs 6 pounds, how many pounds was all the paint they used?

Todd’s car's speed is 60 miles per hour. Going uphill decreases its speed by thirty percent, and going downhill increases its speed by forty percent. If the distance going uphill is 90 miles and the distance going downhill is 144 miles, how many hours will Todd need to cover the entire distance?
A school has 5 classes each with 20 students. If 4 more students join each class, how many students will be there in total?

Five years ago, Anna's art collection had 150 paintings. Three years ago, she bought 90 new paintings, and last year she bought 60 more than she had bought the previous year. This year, Anna sold 100 paintings from her collection. How many paintings are in Anna's collection now?
Rachel cooked meals for 5 days of the week. Each day she cooked twice as long as the previous day, starting with 1 hour on the first day. How total hours did Rachel cook throughout the week?
A snack bar has 48 packs of chips. Each pack contains 12 chips. If the snack bar sells each chip for $0.25, how much revenue did the snack bar make from selling all the chips?

A school organizes a car wash event to raise funds. During the first hour, they wash 10 cars. In the next hour, they manage to wash 25% more cars than the first hour. How many cars did they wash in total?
A park has 80 trees. One third of the trees are oak trees. James planted 15 new pine trees. After the planting, what is the percentage of pine trees in the park?

For a birthday party, Penny is preparing sandwiches. Each sandwich needs 100 grams of cheese. If Penny has 2 kilogram of cheese and each pack of cheese costs $3, how much money did she spend on cheese?

Sally put 27 decorations on her Christmas tree. She noticed that one-third of the decorations were red baubles. There were three times as many gold stars as red baubles. The rest were green tinsels. How many green tinsels does she have on her tree?

Max works at a bakery, where he earns $11 an hour. Besides this, he gets paid $8 for each cake he decorates, $5 for each dozen cupcakes he bakes, and $3 for each loaf of bread he bakes. How much does he earn (before taxes) during a 6-hour shift if he decorates 5 cakes, bakes 3 dozen cupcakes, and bakes 8 loafs of bread?
Laura is arranging her books on the shelf. She has 15 novel books, 9 comic books, and 12 reference books. If each shelf holds 10 books, how many shelves does she need?

Adam's favorite tank has a capacity of 80 liters and his car has a capacity of 60 liters. His tank is 1/4 full and his car's tank is 1/2 full. How many liters in total, will he add to fill them up completely?
Julie found four times as many seashells as Mike. If Mike found 45 seashells, how many did Julie find?

Emma can write ten pages of her novel in one hour. However, she realises it would be faster to type on her computer, which allows her to write fifteen pages in one hour. If she has a total of five hours to work on her novel today, and she decides to type for the first two hours, how many pages of her novel will she complete?
Taylor is building a fence around his yard. Each fence panel costs $15. If he needs 30 panels to complete the fence. How much will Taylor spend?
Sam bought candies from the store. He spent $20 on gummy bears, $30 on lollipops, and $50 on chocolate bars. If he had $200, how much did he have left?
A team of 8 people worked on a project for 6 days. On each day, each person worked for 5 hours. How many hours in total did the team work on the project?
Abby baked 20 cookies. She shared 1/5 of her cookies with her friend. How many cookies did Abby have left?

John loves chocolate chip cookies. In each pack, there are 10 cookies. He eats 2 packs every day. If each cookie pack costs $5 and John eats cookies for 50 consecutive days, how much money does he spend on cookies?
Emily is making bracelets to sell at a local market. Each bracelet uses 15 beads. If she has a bag of 800 beads, how many bracelets can she make?
A library has 2000 books. 30% of the books are fiction, the rest are non-fiction. How many non-fiction books are there in the library?
Zara paints 3 pictures a day. Leah paints twice as many pictures per day as Zara. If they both paint pictures for 5 days, how many pictures will they have in total?

Mary planted 80 seeds in her garden. 1/5 of them are roses and the rest are sunflowers. Two-thirds of those sunflowers will eventually turn into sunflower seeds. How many of Mary's plants are not going to produce seeds?
Sofia and Maria decided to start a lemonade stand. Sofia sold 10 cups of lemonade for $0.75 each while Maria sold 20 cups for $0.50 each. How much money did they make altogether?
The price of a movie ticket has been increased by 20%. It now costs $12. How much original price, in dollars, for the movie ticket?

Mia bought 2 barrels of grapes that each weighed 40 kilograms. Lily bought 3 barrels of grapes that each weighed 30 kilograms. They combined their grapes and then packed them into 10-kilogram boxes. How many boxes did they use?

Emily had a dinner party for 12 friends and to keep things fun, she decided to make a punch that is one part rum, two parts pineapple juice, and three parts carbonated water. If every part consists of 75 milliliters, how much punch did Emily make in total?
Fred went to a dinner party where he and his friends ate 3 pizzas. Each pizza had 8 slices. If Fred ate 3 slices, how many slices were left for his friends?
Moon Beach has twice the number of turtles as Star Beach. If Star Beach has 35 turtles, how many turtles are there in total on the two beaches?
John bought 3 comic books for $5 each and a few action figures that cost the same amount of money each. He spent a total of $35. How much did John pay for each of the action figures?
Peter has 8 apples. He eats 2 and gives away half of the rest to his friend. How many apples does he have left?
Harry sold 35% of his comic book collection and gave 50% of it to his brother. Then he gave the rest of his comic books, 30 in total, to his cousin. How many comic books did Harry have to start with?

A restaurant sold 100 hotdogs and each hotdog costs $2. They also sold hamburgers, which are twice as expensive as the hotdogs. If the restaurant made $500 from selling hotdogs and hamburgers, how many hamburgers did they sell?
Jack is selling concert tickets. Each ticket costs $75. He sold 20 tickets in total. Half of the buyers purchased the VIP upgrade for $25 extra. How much money did Jack earn from selling the tickets?
John has both $100 and $20 bills in his wallet which total to $440. He has two $100 bills and eight $20 bills. How much money does he have in $100 bills?
Jenna has a collection of 40 books. 25% of them are fiction and the rest are non-fiction. How many more non-fiction books does Jenna have than fiction books?
A toy store has 250 action figures and 350 dolls. The store sold 100 action figures and 200 dolls. How many toys are left in the store?
Liam buys 3 pencils and 7 pens for a total of $21. If each pen costs $2, what is the cost of each pencil?
Grant started to read a 600-page novel and read 50 pages per day. After 10 days how many more pages does he have to read to finish the novel?
Carly collected 15 seashells and Alexa collected twice the amount Carly did. Anthony collected 5 fewer than half the amount that Alexa collected. How many seashells were collected all together?
A car can travel 400 miles on a full tank of gas. If gas costs $3 per gallon and the car gets 20 miles per gallon, how much does it cost to fill up the tank?
Larry bought some groceries from the supermarket for $50. He then bought some clothes for 3 times what the groceries cost. How much did Larry spend in total?
John has 7 cups of flour. He uses 2 cups of flour for a cake and another 3 for cookies. Later, he gained 4 cups of flour from his neighbor. How many cups of flour does John have in total now?
Ryan has 60 stickers and wants to distribute them equally among his 5 friends. If Ryan also wants to keep some stickers for himself, how many stickers will he have left if he keeps 10 for himself?
A car factory has 200 employees working on a production line. Each employee needs to make 2 tires every hour. If the factory operates for 8 hours a day, how many tires are produced in a day?
A pet shop has 35 dogs and 18 cats. Each cat costs $200 and each dog costs $400. If a customer wants to buy 3 cats and 2 dogs, how much will the customer pay?
The depth of a lake is 200m below sea level. If this depth is a third of the vertical distance between the mountaintop and the bottom of the lake, how high is the mountain?
A group of friends must split a bill of 120 dollars. If they each pay 20 dollars, how many friends are there?

Jan is making candy necklaces for herself and a few friends. Everyone receives a candy necklace each and each candy necklace is made up of 10 pieces of candies. The pieces of candies come from blocks of candy, which each produce 30 pieces of candy. If Jan breaks down 3 blocks of candy and every single piece of candy from this is used in the candy necklaces, how many friends receive a candy necklace?

Victoria had two glasses of orange juice and a bottle of water before the exam. She knows that if she consumes more than 40 ounces of liquid, she has to use the bathroom, and she cannot leave the exam hall once the test started. If she drinks from her flask during the test, how many ounces can she drink before she needs to go to the bathroom? (Each glass of orange juice is 8 ounces and the bottle of water is 20 ounces)

Martha runs a small bakery. One day, she made 15 loaves of bread and 10 pies. By the end of the day, she had sold 8 loaves of bread. Martha then realized that 4 of the remaining bread loaves had gotten squashed and were unsellable. She also observed that she also had 6 pies left and that 1 of these pies had a burnt crust and wasn’t sellable. How many total items did Martha sell that day?

Mitchell is a chef who runs a catering service. He has been assigned to cater for a wedding with 200 guests. Every guest will be served a single serving of roast beef that costs $15 each for ingredients and preparation. In addition, Mitchell will be paid 1/5 of the total cost of the roast beef as his service charge. How much will the total cost for the wedding catering be?

Fred needs to travel 350 miles across the state. His car can average 25 miles per gallon of gasoline, and gasoline costs $2.50 per gallon. How much will Fred have to pay for the gasoline for his trip?
Rachel wants to buy a new laptop. The laptop costs $900. She has already saved $300. If she saves $50 per week, how many weeks will it take Rachel to save enough money to buy the laptop?
A library fines you $2 for every overdue book per week. If you had 3 books overdue for 2 weeks, how much do you owe the library?
The heaviest elephant weighs five times as much as the lightest elephant. The heaviest elephant weighs 5000 kilograms. How many more kilograms does the heaviest elephant weigh than the lightest one?

Jack and Jill are baking cookies for a fundraiser. They each bake 5 dozen cookies. Jack sells his for $2 a dozen and Jill sells hers for $3 a dozen. How much will they have raised if they both sell all their cookies?

Roseline and her friend each saved up money to buy two tickets to a concert. Each ticket cost $45 and Roseline managed to save $80. If her friend makes up the rest of the amount, how much did Roseline's friend spend?

Jane owns a bakery shop. She sells one third of their goods as cinnamon buns, a quarter as bagels, and the rest as bread. If the bakery was able to sell a total of 120 goods, how many of them were bread?
A library is updating its book collection. They start with 523 books. Each day for a week, they remove 28 outdated books and add 45 new books. How many books do they have after a week?

Maria has 4 kids and each kid has a piggy bank. At the end of the year, each kid's piggy bank is full. The older kid's piggy bank has $75, younger one has $50, the youngest has $30 and the remaining kid has $45. How much money do the kids have in total at the end of the year?
Barbara is currently 50 years old. 10 years ago, she was three times older than Laura would be in 5 years. If Laura's sister is 3 years older than Laura, how old is Laura's sister?
Thomas has 50 oranges and sells them at $2 each. Then he buys 25 apples at $3 each. How much money is left from the orange sales after he has bought the apples?
Linda runs for 30 minutes every morning for 5 days. Frank runs for 45 minutes on each of four days. How many minutes did Linda and Frank run in total?
On a farm, there are 10 cows and each cow gives 8 litres of milk a day. If the farmer sells the milk at $2 per litre, how much does he earn in a week?
A sapphire pendant is worth 60 dollars and a ruby pendant is worth 30 dollars. If you have 4 sapphire pendants, 6 ruby pendants, and 50 dollars cash, how much money do you have in total?
A movie theatre has 8 screening rooms, each able to hold 120 attendees. During a special event, each room only managed to sell 85 tickets. How many people attended the event overall?
Tina ordered 30 pounds of chicken. She cuts that into 8-ounce portions. How many portions did she get?
For a school project, Julie needs 4 beads to make a bracelet. If she intends to make 15 bracelets, how many beads does she need in total?

Samantha received a monthly salary of $3500. She decided to rent a new house which costs $1000 per month, and she has to pay for her car loan that costs $500 a month. How much money does she save in a year after rent and car loan payment?

Oscar has a fruit stall and sells apples and peaches. On Mondays, Wednesdays and Saturdays, he sells peaches and each day he sells 15 peaches. On Tuesdays, Thursdays and Fridays, he sells apples and each day he sells 12 apples. How many more peaches than apples does he sell in a week?

Daniel has 5 jars of juice containing 2 liters each. He wants to serve a full glass of juice to each person at a party. He knows that each glass can hold up to 250 milliliters of juice. How many full glasses can he give?
Emma is on a cycling trip. Each day, she travels 50 miles. If she continues this pace, how long will she take to travel 250 miles?
John can type 60 words per minute. If he types for 2 hours a day for a week, how many words did he type in total?

Mr. Brown buys his coffee beans in a bulk from a coffee supplier. He pays $6 for a pound of coffee beans. He has a coffee business and charges $3 for a cup of coffee. If he can get 50 cups from a pound of coffee beans, how much does he earn from a pound of beans?
Joe has a collection of 500 trading cards. He trades 1/5 of his cards to his friend. How many cards does Joe have left?
A toy train runs for 2 hours on 3 batteries. If there are 9 batteries, how long will the train run?
Frank spent $8 each month from February to May for his gym membership. From June to October, he spent $10 per month. How much did he spend in total for his gym membership?
In a park there are 15 trees. Each tree has 4 times the number of birds as the number of nests. If each tree has an average of 5 nests, calculate the total number of birds in the park.

Sandra's friends hire her to clean their house and rearrange some furniture. Cleaning the house takes 2 hours and pays $25. If Sandra wants to make $30/hour working for her friends, and rearranging the furniture takes 3 hours, how much should Sandra charge (in total, not per hour) for rearranging the furniture?
Mike's bakery supplies bread to various stores. He delivers 100 loaves every day, but on Sundays he delivers twice that amount. How many loaves does he deliver in one week?

Lucy booked a holiday trip for her and 7 of her friends. Each person pays $500 for the plane fare and $150 for their accommodation, and half of them also buy travel insurance for $50/person. The holiday trip is called off because of weather conditions, and each person gets an 80% refund. How much money did they all lose together?
Jerry shared his pizza with three friends. If he kept 2 slices for himself and each of his friends got 3 slices each, how many slices were there in the pizza?
In the school talent show, each act lasts for 7 minutes. There are 15 acts, and a 5-minute break after every 5 acts. How long will the talent show last?

Marvin and Harry were participating in a reading challenge where they had to read 30 fiction novels each in one month. Marvin can read four times as fast as Harry. If Marvin takes 12 hours to read one book, how long will it take Harry to read three quarters of his books?

Mandy is driving to a town 200 miles away. Her car uses 1 gallon of gas every 20 miles. Mandy stops to fill up her car when she has used 80% of her gas. If a gallon of gas costs $3, how much will Mandy spend on gas for the trip?
In a music class, 3/4 of the students play guitar but only 2/5 play piano. What percent of the class does not play an instrument if the class has 100 students?

Joey has a collection of seashells. He has 15 conch shells and 80 scallop shells. Each conch shell has 5 ridges and each scallop shell has 8 ridges. How many ridges are there in total on all Joey’s seashells?
Dan has 140 pages to read in a book. If he reads 20 pages every day, how many days will it take him to finish the book?
A small cake baking business made a total of 125 cupcakes. If they sold 100 cupcakes and 12 were damaged, how many cupcakes do they have left?
Lindsey bought 5 pencil boxes and then she gave away 3 pencil boxes. Lindsey wants to put 8 pencils in each of remaining pencil boxes. How many pencils will she use in total?
Sebastian wants to build a model airplane. He has already spent $20 on supplies, and every additional part he needs costs $4. He still needs 7 parts. How much will all the parts cost?

Jacob is arranging his toy cars in a pyramid. Each level of the pyramid has 70% as many cars as the number of cars in the previous level. If he makes four levels and the top level has 56 cars, how many cars are in the pyramid in total?

Anna, Ben, and Charlie go hiking. Anna hikes 15 more kilometers a day than Ben, who hikes three times the distance Charlie hikes. They hike for an entire week, covering the same distances each day. If Charlie hiked 20 kilometers, what's the combined distance for all three of them for the week?
Emma baked 10 dozen cookies to sell at a fundraiser. Each dozen contains 12 cookies. If she sold 75% of the cookies, how many cookies does Emma have left?

Suzanne needs to prepare sandwiches for a picnic. She is making sandwiches for 2 soccer teams, both of which have 15 players and a basketball team with 20 players. How many sandwiches does Suzanne need to prepare?

Sarah visits the gym for 4 hours every week. She goes on Monday, Wednesday and Friday. On Monday and Friday, she spends 1 hour each. If she spends the same amount of time at the gym on Wednesday as she does on Monday and Friday, how many hours does she spend at the gym on Wednesday?
Eric is planting apple trees in his garden. Each tree needs 3 square meters of space. If his garden is 30 square meters, how many trees can he plant?

Brian has 8 comic books in his collection. His dad buys him 3 more for his birthday. If his friend then gives him twice as many comic books than he already has, how many comic books does Brian have in total?
Gasoline costs $3.25 per gallon. Sarah has a discount card that subtracts $1.50 from the total price. She bought 4 gallons of gasoline. How much did she pay?

Ian, Isaac and Irene entered their dogs in the Super Dog Race. Ian's dog finished the race in 7 minutes. Isaac's dog finished 3 minutes faster than Ian's dog. Irene's dog took twice as long as Isaac's dog. How long did it take Irene's dog to finish the race?
Two years ago, Jake was triple the age of his younger brother. In 4 years, his younger brother will be 18. How old is Jake now?

Ms. Angela has 100 sheep on her farm. Thirty percent of the sheep on her farm have a black wool, 20 percent of the sheep without a black wool have a white wool and the remaining sheep do not have any wool. How many sheep on the farm do not have any wool?
A bookstore buys a popular novel for $10 and sells it for $20. If they sell 120 novels in a month, how much profit does the bookstore make?
There are 3 chairs with four legs each and two tables with three legs each in a room. How many legs are in total in the room?
Jason had 62 stickers. He gave 8 of them to his brother and now he has 6 times more stickers than his brother. How many stickers did Jason's brother have in the first place?
Beth baked 6 cakes using 2 boxes of cake mix. How many more boxes of cake mix does Beth need to bake a total of 15 cakes?

Bill has two cars. With the first car, he drove 85 kilometers on 5 liters of fuel. With the second car, he drove 90 kilometers on 6 liters of fuel. How many more kilometers per liter did the first car make compared to the second car?
Charles took a loan of $8000 to buy a car. He has to repay this loan in 2 years. If he repays an equal amount of money every month, how much does he pay per month?
Larry and his friends came back from a trip and noticed that every 3 apples from the orchard weighed approximately 1 pound. Larry bought 21 pounds of apples. How many apples did Larry buy?
Max sells cell phone chargers for $15 each. He bought them in bulk, at a cost of $8 per charger. If Max sells 60 chargers, how much profit does he make?
At a fruit stall, apples cost $2 each and oranges cost $3 each. If Lily spent $20 on apples and $18 on oranges, how many pieces of fruit did she buy?
Jenny wants to bake cupcakes for her birthday. The recipe she follows makes 24 cupcakes and she used 4 cups of sugar. If she wants to make 72 cupcakes, how many cups of sugar will she need?
Bella received $200 for her birthday. She spends $50 on a new dress, $40 to eat at her favorite restaurant and $20 on some novels. How much money does Bella have left?
Sophia is baking cookies. She bakes 15 cookies in one batch. If she wants to bake 100 cookies, how many batches should Sophia bake?

David is a runner training for a marathon. He runs 16 kilometers a day for 5 days every week. If a marathon is 42 kilometers long, how many weeks would it take for David to run the equivalent of a marathon?

Sarah sells homemade jam at a local market. The first shop needs 3 jars, the second shop needs 5 jars, and the third shop needs 10 jars of jam per week. How many jars of jam does she need to supply all the shops in 6 weeks?
You find five small gemstones that weigh 35 grams each, and three larger stones that weigh 125 grams each. What is the total weight of all eight gemstones?
Lola is three years younger than Al. If Lola is 5 years old, how old will Al be in two years?
Jake and Sarah are jointly having a birthday party. Jake invites 20 friends and Sarah invites 15. If only 75% of their friends show up, how many friends will come to the party?
A zoo had 500 visitors on Monday and 200 more visitors on Tuesday compared to Monday. Calculate the total number of people in the zoo over these two days.

Donna was handed a $30 bill and told to shop for a couple of items. The grocery list included a cereal for $5.50, a bottle of milk for $2.50, a packet of washing powder for $7.75 and 6 pounds of apples at $1.00 per pound. She also had a coupon that gave her $2.00 off the washing powder. When Donna checked out, she discovered that the cereal was discounted by 50%. How much money did Donna have left after her shopping?

James has three cars. On average, they consume a total of 140 liters of fuel a week. The first car consumes an average of 3 liters a day. How many liters a day does the second car consume if the third car consumes an average of 5 liters a day?
In a marathon, Tom ran 45 kilometers. He first ran East for 25 kilometers then North for 15 kilometers. How many kilometers does he still need to cover to complete the marathon?

Caitlin makes bracelets to sell at the farmer’s market every weekend. Each bracelet takes twice as many small beads as it does large beads. If each bracelet uses 12 large beads, and Caitlin has 528 beads with equal amounts of large and small beads, how many bracelets can she make for this weekend?

John and Matt are practicing for a marathon. On the first day, John ran 6 miles and Matt ran 9 miles. If every day they increase their distance by 25%, how many miles will they run on the second day combined?
Katie baked 45 cupcakes for a fundraiser. After selling half of them, each of her two children wanted to take 5 home. How many cupcakes does she have left to sell?

A pizza restaurant charges a flat rate delivery fee of $3.00 for orders below $40. If the order total exceeds $40, they add 10% of the total bill. Adam orders two large pizzas for $25.50 each. How much is his total bill including delivery?
Benjamin drives to the beach at 50 mph. It's a distance of 250 miles. He makes a 1 hour stop along the way to have lunch. How long does Benjamin take to get to the beach?

Susan and Carlo play a dart game together, earning 10 points for every bullseye. After playing 15 rounds, Susan scored 6 points more than 1/2 of the total points scored. How many total points did Carlo score?
A pizza place can make 9 pizzas in an hour. On a Saturday, they are open for 10 hours. If they didn't work for 2 hours due to power outage, how many pizzas did they make that day?
A baker has 6 recipes, each of which requires 9 eggs. If he needs to double one of the recipes and the grocery store only sells eggs by the dozen, how many dozen eggs does he need to buy?
Mary has a savings account that earns 5% interest every year. She puts $500 in it, and then $50 each year. How much money is in it after three years?
A baker needs 5 eggs to make a cake. If he has a tray of 30 eggs, how many cakes can he make?

A shipping company is transporting packages with a weight of 25 pounds each. They can accommodate 8 packages in one truck. The company has to deliver 6 trucks of packages in a day, then two more trucks delivery than triple that on the second day. How many packages are left to delivered on the third day if they have 1000 packages at the beginning?
A class of 50 students goes on a field trip. Three students forget their permission slips, then 1/5 of the remaining students forget their lunch. How many students have everything they need?

Andy and Ben are at a fair. They each decide to play a shooting game. Each game costs $2. Andy won a large teddy bear worth $14 and Ben won a medium teddy bear worth $9. If Andy started with $20 and Ben started with $18, how much money did they have left altogether?
Jane is making sandwiches for a picnic.  The bread costs $2.00 per loaf, a pack of ham is $3.00 and a pack of cheese is $2.50.  She wants to make 10 sandwiches.  How much does each sandwich cost?
Mike runs a grocery store and he adds 20 kg of potatoes, 30 kg of onions and 15 kg of tomatoes on the shelves. By evening, 40 kg were sold. What's the remaining mass of vegetables that he has left?
If an engine consumes 5 liters of oil for every 100 kilometers, how much oil will it consume for a distance of 350 kilometers?

Jenny ordered a custom-made rug for her living room that is 15 feet by 25 feet. The cost of the rug was $5 per square foot and an additional $2 per square foot for the fabric protection. How much did Jenny pay for the rug?
Rachel spent $35 on books and $25 on stationery items. If she had $100 originally, how much money does she have left now?
There are 35 children in a party. Each child was given 3 cookies. However, 5 children gave their cookies to their friends. How many cookies are left with the children?
