David bought 18 bananas at the store, which cost him $3.00. Later, his friends gave him 12 more bananas. He also ate 4 bananas. How many bananas does David have left?
Martha has 18 crayons. She lost half of them, so she bought a new set of 20 crayons. How many crayons in total does Martha have after the purchase?
Alice is a beautician and she charges $50 for each haircut. If Alice had 35 customers in one week, how much money will Alice make in that week?
Liam has two more than thrice as many stickers as Noah has.  If Noah has three stickers on each page on his book and six stickers on each of his posters, how many stickers does Liam have?
A company manufactures balls and for each ball they make a profit of $20. If they made a profit of $12000 this month, how many balls did they sell this month?
A football field is three times longer than it is wide. If the width is 50 meters, what is the area of the field?

Tim is filling up his swimming pool. He fills it halfway and then takes a break. While he's gone, half the water in the pool evaporates. When Tim comes back, he doubles the amount of water in the pool. If the pool is 6 feet long, 5 feet wide, and 4 feet high, how many cubic feet of water are in the pool?
Leila paints the 16 miles of road at a pace of 2 miles per day, and then repaints it at a pace of 4 miles per day. How long did her painting take, in days?

Neil runs a bakery and wants to invest in buying bread making machines. He plans to buy 2 machines and 15 bags of flour. Each bag of flour costs $10. Neil’s budget for the machines and flour is $250. How much, in dollars, can Neil afford to spend on each machine?

A sink's drain is blocked so that only 3 liters of water can run through it per minute. After twenty minutes, the blockage is cleared enough for 4 liters of water to run through per minute. Ten minutes later, the drain is cleared enough for 5 liters to run through per minute. How many liters of water ran through after 40 minutes?
Alice read 210 pages of a book yesterday. Bob read 15 pages more than half of what Alice read. How many pages did Alice and Bob read in total?
Jeremy bought 30 more books than Ryan. Samuel bought five times as many books as Ryan. If Samuel bought 60 books, how many more books did Samuel buy than Jeremy?
A bottle of juice contains 3.5 liters. A cup of juice is 0.25 liters. If Jeff wants to fill 4 cups with juice, how many liters will be left in the bottle?
Peter has 25 orange marbles and twice as many green marbles. He has half as many blue marbles compared to orange and green combined. How many total marbles does he have in total?
Samantha bought 15 packs of candy. Each pack has 24 pieces of candy. She shared her candies equally with her 4 friends. How many pieces did each person get?

Robert has an art supply store. He ordered 100 paints, 50 brushes, and 200 sketchbooks. He sold 3/4 of the paints, 1/2 of the brushes and 1/5 of the sketchbooks. How many art supplies does he have left in his store?
A computer was bought for $1200 and sold for 75% of its purchase price. How much money was made from the sale?

Sarah is planning a road trip and needs to budget her spending. She saved up $500 and already spent $120 on a new tent, $80 on camping cookware and $60 on a sleeping bag. She also bought a camping chair, but she lost the receipt so she doesn't remember how much she paid for it. After all her purchases, she has $220 left.  How much did Sarah pay for the camping chair?
Jake and Mary are having a pizza party. They order 5 pizzas, each with 8 slices. How many total slices of pizza do Jake and Mary have for their party?
A rectangular field has a length of 120 yards and a width of 75 yards. If the owner wants to plant corn on 60% of the field and soybeans on 30% of the field, how many square yards will remain unused?
Susana is preparing onigiri (rice balls) for a party. Each person will eat 3 onigiri. There will be 15 guests at the party. If there were originally 60 onigiri, how many will be left?
A rectangular playground is 20 meters long and 15 meters wide. What is the total area of the playground in square meters?
A toy store sells 5 dozen dolls every day. Each doll costs $8. How much does the toy store earn after 7 days, if all dolls are sold?
Sarah earned $5000 from her business. She used $1500 to pay off her business expenses. She donated 10% of what was left to charity. How much money did Sarah have left?
Every winter, Cindy uses 2 meters of wool to knit scarves. If she knits twice as many scarves every Fall as every winter, how many meters of wool does she use in a year?
A library had 2000 books. However, due to budget cuts, the library had to reduce its collection by 25%. How many books did the library have left?
Chris spends 25 hours a week at work. He spends 2 hours per day working out at the gym. He sleeps 7 hours per day. He spends 15 hours per week doing chores. How many hours left over does Chris have?
An athlete is working out. He completes 10 sit-ups every minute for 15 minutes. How many sit-ups does he complete?
A shop offers a 15% discount for the total purchases above $200. Leo bought 3 books for $50 each; 4 notebooks that cost $10 each; and a laptop that costs $150. How much will Leo pay for these items?
John's father gave him two $100 bills, three $20 bills, and four $1 bills. His mother gave him one $100 bill, two $20 bills and five $1 bills. How much money does John have?
Samantha brought 4 packets of candies and each packet consists of 15 candies. She decided to share half of the total candies with her brother. How many candies will each get?
A farmer has 120 animals on his farm. He has 4 times as many chickens as cows. If each cow costs $200 and each chicken costs $20, what is the total value of all the animals on the farm?

You cycle three times faster than Miss Smith, and Miss Smith took 3 hours to cycle to the park. If your destination is 4 times further away than the park Miss Smith cycled to, how many hours will it take you to get there?

Ben owns a bookstore and he decided to invest in new stock. He buys 5 copies of a new bestseller, then finds out the book has been selling so well that he orders an additional 7 dozen copies. A week later, he gets a small shipment of 15 more books. How many copies does he have in total?
Sally spends 45 minutes every morning and evening exercising. She also spends 15 minutes every day cooking healthy meals. How many hours does Sally spend keeping herself healthy each day?
Lucas wants to buy a guitar which costs $300. He saves $15 every week. How many weeks will it take for Lucas to buy the guitar?

Laura and Sara are twins. Laura owns 120 books and Sara has 3/5 times as many books as Laura. If both are 25 years old, how many books will Laura need to give away to Sara so as to match their book collection with their age difference?
John's garden has 28 roses. He plants an additional 7 roses each week. After 4 weeks, how many roses will be in his garden?
George grows oranges in his garden. He harvests 400 oranges in a year and decides to sell each for $2. How much will he earn if he successfully sells all his oranges?
Victoria exercises for 3 hours a day and burns 120 calories per hour. How many more calories would she have burned if she exercised for 6 hours?

Sam decided to save his money in a savings account with an interest rate of 5% per year. He deposited an initial amount of $1,500 and then added $50 each month for a year. How much money will he have in his savings account after one year?

Jay drives 70 kilometers in two hours to visit his grandmother. From his grandma's house, he drives an additional 30 kilometers to his friend's house in an hour. How fast is Jay's average speed in kilometers per hour for the entire trip?

Lena, Rebecca, and James made bracelets to sell at a craft fair. Lena made 15 bracelets, Rebecca made 6 more than Lena, and James made half as many as Lena and Rebecca together. If they sold each bracelet for $5 and divided the revenue equally, how much money did each of them make?
Dan wants to build a fence around his garden. His garden is a rectangle that's 7 meters long and 5 meters wide. He already has 10 meters of fencing. How much more does he need?
Ron and Harry are playing a video game. Ron defeated 10 zombies per hour while Harry defeated 8 zombies per hour. How many zombies did they defeat together in 5 hours of playing?
Benjamin and his 3 friends rented a car for a week. The total cost is $840. If they share the cost equally, how much does each person need to pay?
Lisa bought 2 kilograms of oranges and 3 kilograms of apples. One kilogram of oranges costs $4 and one kilogram of apples costs $5. How much did Lisa pay for the fruits?
Timothy has 3 birds, 2 dogs, and 1 cat as pets. His friend gave him 1 bird, 2 dogs, and 3 cats. How many pets does Timothy have altogether?
A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. If I eat 2 slices and give 2 slices to a friend, how many slices are left?

Billy can spin on the carousel 3 times longer than Steve, who can only endure it for 5 minutes. Linda can outlast Billy on the carousel by 20% longer. How long can Linda endure the ride on the carousel?
Carla runs a cupcake shop where she sells 150 cupcakes everyday. One third of her cupcakes are chocolate flavor, how many chocolate cupcakes does she sell in a week?

Jemma and her two sisters decided to order cupcakes for a party. Each of them can eat 5 cupcakes. The mini cupcakes come in packs of 6 and the classic cupcakes in packs of 12. If they order 1 pack of classic cupcakes, how many packs of mini cupcakes do they need to order?

Luke attempts to break his record of 450 push-ups completed in a week. As of Friday, he has done 320 push-ups. Over the weekend, on Saturday, he performs 70 push-ups and on Sunday he does 25. How many more push-ups does he have to do on Monday to surpass his previous record?
Louise is making bracelets for a charity. It takes her 15 minutes to make one bracelet. If she spends 5 hours a day making bracelets, how many bracelets can she make in 10 days?

During a party, the guests ate 50 slices of pizza, 10 less than twice as many slices of pizza as bowls of salad consumed, and half as many bowls of salad as slices of cake. How many slices of cake remained if the guests ate all the pizza and salad?
Melanie sold 120 bracelets at a school fair in June. The next month, she sold half as many bracelets. How many bracelets did Melanie sell altogether in June and July?
Sam walked 3 miles. Monica walked 1140 yards and Candice walked 1540 feet. How many combined feet did they walk in total?
A ferry has space for 120 cars. Today, 1/5 of the spaces are unused. How many cars are currently on the ferry?
Mark has two times more books than Julia. If Mark has 32 books, how many books does Julia have?

Peter is a musician and he has 15 guitar strings. He breaks 3 of his strings. After a week, Peter buys new guitar strings which are twice as much as he initially had. How many guitar strings does Peter have now?
An art class has 30 students. If 60% of them are girls, how many boys are there in the class?
The ratio of notebooks to pencils that Bella has to buy for his school supplies is 5:1. If she bought a total of 300 stationery from the store, calculate the number of notebooks she bought.
In a parking lot there were 30 black cars, 20 red cars and 50 blue cars. If 10 black cars and 5 blue cars left the parking lot, how many cars remained?

Jane is planning a party for her 10 friends. She wants to have 15 pieces of cake and donuts for each of her friends. She went to a bakery store that offers both for $12 per packet which contains 5 pieces. How many packets of cake and donuts should she buy?
Tom paints 4 pieces of fence per day. If he needs to paint a total of 28 pieces of fence, how many days will it take for Tom to finish?
Paul plans to stay in Spain for 15 days, he will need an international call plan costing $4.00 per day. His regular bill per month is $200. How much will Paul's charge be for the said month?

Lisa buys 4 pairs of shoes, one for herself and three for her sisters. When she sells the shoes to her sisters, she charges them $10 more than she paid for each pair. If she collected a total of $240, how much money did each pair of shoes cost?
A bookstore is selling notebooks for $2 each and pens for $1.50 each. If Susan bought 3 notebooks and 4 pens, how much did she spend in total?
Rachel went to the hello stationery store to buy 15 pencils and 10 pens for her school. If each pencil costs $0.5 and each pen cost $0.75, how much did she spend in total?
A bag of coffee can make 40 cups of coffee. If an office uses up 2 bags a week, how many cups can be made from the coffee consumed in a quarter (12 weeks)?
Jay scored 50 goals in the first soccer season. In the next two seasons, he scored 60 and then double the first season. How many total goals did Jay score?

Julie started the day with 35 cookies to sell at her bake sale. Throughout the day, she gave away 1 cookie as a free sample, sold 5 more, and baked an additional 8. How many cookies does she have left?

A gym club had 150 members in January. The number of members in February increased by 20%. There were 10 more members in March than in February. What is the total number of members for these three months?

Bennett sells window screens. He sold twice as many window screens in February as he sold last month. In February, Bennett sold a fourth of what he sold in March. If Bennet sold 8800 window screens in March, how many screens did Bennett sell from January to March?
Melissa baked 35 chocolate chip cookies and 4 times that number of oatmeal cookies. How many cookies does she have in total?
A train carries 140 passengers in the morning and twice as many in the evening. If the train runs 5 days a week for 4 weeks, how many passengers does it carry for a month?
Susan bought 3 beings bags each for $80 and a lamp for half the price of a bean bag. She also purchased a small table for $75 more than the lamp. How much did she spend in total?
Priscilla and Andrew arranged a party. They ordered 5 bottles of drinks for $12 each and 3 pizzas for $15 each. How much did they spend in total for the party preparations?
Mrs. Smith paints for 2 hours every afternoon. How many hours will she have painted after 3 weeks?
Lucas and his three friends decided to buy a video game that costs $240. They decided to share the cost equally. How much does each of them have to pay?

Anna is making desserts for a party. She spends 20 minutes preparing each cake and needs to make 5 cakes. If the cooking time is 1 hour per cake and the cooling time is 30 minutes per cake, how long does the whole process take?
Suzan bought packs of pencils for her class. Each pack contains 12 pencils. If she bought 15 packs, how many pencils did she buy in total?
A baker used half of a bag of flour on Monday, and 1/4 of the bag on Tuesday. What fraction of the bag of flour did the baker use?

A charity decided to raise money through a charity run. Participants can run for 2 miles, 5 miles, or 10 miles. If runners running 2 miles donate $20, runners running 5 miles donate $50, and runners running 10 miles donate $100, and at the end of the event there were three times as many 2-mile runners as 10-mile runners, and twice as many 5 mile runners as 2-mile runners, and a total of $11700 was collected, how many 2-mile runners were there?
Pears used to cost $2.4 per pound. The price increased by 30%. How much does it cost to buy 3 pounds of pears for each person in a 5 member family?

David, Rachel, Susan, and Mike collected seashells at the beach. David found 23 seashells, Rachel collected 15, Susan discovered 9, and Mike picked up 33 seashells. They wanted to distribute the seashells evenly between them. So, how many seashells did each of them have?
A train is moving at a speed of 60 km/h. How far would it go in 4 hours?
A forest has 60 oak trees and 80 pine trees. A lumberjack cut down 10 oak trees and 20 pine trees. How many trees are left in the forest?

Clark has a vegetable garden where he grows tomatoes and bell peppers. He has three rows of tomato plants that produce 13 tomatoes each and four rows of bell peppers that produce 8 peppers each. How many vegetables does Clark have in total?

Simon is making a music playlist that lasts 1 hour. Each song in his playlist lasts 5 minutes. However, he decides to add 3 more songs to his playlist, each of which are 7 minutes long. How many total minutes is Simon's playlist?
75% of people prefer reading to movies. If there are 5000 people in a town, how many people do not prefer reading?
Lisa has 3 cats. She wants to give each cat 2 tins of cat food per day for 7 days. If each tin costs $1.5, how much will she spend in total?
Mike wants to buy a new guitar that costs $845. He has already saved $515. How much more does he need to save to buy the guitar?
A fruit vendor sells apples at $0.50 each, bananas at $0.30 each, and oranges at $0.60 each. In a day, he sold 100 apples, 150 bananas, and 60 oranges. How much money did the vendor make in a day?
A park has 7 drinking fountains. Each fountain has 3 spouts. How many spouts are there in the park?
George invested $200 in stocks. In the first week, his stocks increased by 10%, and in the second week, they increased by 20%. How much are his stocks worth at the end of the second week?

Martina is an orange wholesaler.  She buys oranges from growers and then delivers them to the supermarkets and sells them for profit.  She buys individual oranges from growers at a cost of $15 per crate, and sells them to the supermarkets at a price of $0.60 per orange.  If each crate contains 60 oranges, then how much profit, in dollars, does she make after selling 200 oranges?

A group of friends went out to watch a movie. The ticket for each person was $8. If 5 of them couldn't go at the last moment and only 7 people went to the movies, how much less did they spend all together on tickets?

Grace went to a thrift store. She wanted to buy a pair of jeans, a satchel, and some used books. The satchel costs twice as much as the jeans. The jeans are $15 cheaper than the books, which cost $30. How much did Grace spend that day at the thrift store?
Hannah spends 20 minutes every day practicing the piano. How many hours does she spend practicing the piano in a month?
Linda is 30 years now. Her sister Ava is twice Linda's age. In 5 years, how old will Ava be?

Sophia only trained yoga 8 times in her life. Maria trained yoga 7 times as many as Sophia, and Jessie trained yoga 12 more times than Maria. If Sophia wants to say that she trained yoga as many times as Jessie and plans to train 3 times a week, how many weeks would it take Sophia to achieve her goal?
Jacob has five cats and three dogs in his house. Each pet eats 2 cans of pet food weekly. If he wants to stock up pet food for the next 4 weeks, how many cans does he need to buy?
Jenny worked in a restaurant where she cleaned 80 small plates and 15 more large plates than small plates. How many plates did she clean?

A school is organizing a trip to a museum. There are 160 fourth graders, 178 fifth graders, and 185 sixth graders. 5 teachers and 3 parents from each grade are joining the trip. A bus has a capacity of 60 seats. How many buses will be needed for the trip?
There are 50 seats in a bus. If the bus is full, how many people can go on a trip if 10 buses go?

A restaurant uses 8 liters of vegetable oil a day to deep-fry their french fries. If the restaurant is open 6 days in a week, how many liters of vegetable oil does the restaurant use by the end of the week?
Marco wants to buy a doghouse which cost $200. He saved $15 each week for several weeks. How many weeks will Marco save for the doghouse?
A bakery makes 60 loaves of bread every hour. If the bakery is open for 9 hours per day and half of the bread is sold every day, how much bread is left at the end of the day?
Freya goes to the gym 4 times a week and each gym session lasts 1.5 hours. How many hours does she spend training in the gym in two weeks?
Henry has 50 CDs. He sells 30% of them to pay for bills and then loses half of the remainder in a fire. How many CDs does Henry have left?
In a tree farm, there are 12 apple trees, 18 orange trees, and 25 pear trees. If the number of cherry trees is three times the number of apple trees, how many total trees are there on the farm?
Jane wants to bake 8 pecan pies for a family gathering. Each pie needs 5 pecans and she already gathered 30 pecans. How many more pecans does she need to buy to make all 8 pies?
When Hannah was 6 years old, her age was double the age of her sister July. Now, 20 years later, if July's husband is 2 years older than her, how old is he?

Joseph is a furniture maker who specializes in wooden chairs. Each chair uses 8 metres of wood. He also needs to make a table that uses 4 square metres of material. If the wood he uses costs $6 per meter, how much does he spend on the wood in total?
Kim and Lily went to collect strawberries. Kim collected 15 more strawberries than Lily. If Lily collected 20 strawberries, what's the total number of strawberries they collected together?
Jason earns $15 an hour. He worked 8 hours every day for 5 days. How much did he earn?

Emma works as a foreign language tutor. She tutors Spanish for 5 hours every day from Monday to Friday and tutors French for 3 hours a day during the weekend. If she charges $40 per hour for her services, how much does she earn in a week?
Sue bought 3 meal kits, each costing $20, and 2 bottles of wine, each costing $15. She received a discount of 10% on her total purchase. How much did she pay for her shopping?
In a school, there are 250 students. If 3/5 of them are boys, and 1/4 of the boys are on the soccer team, how many boys are not on the soccer team?
Becky, Bill, and Bob are splitting a prize money of $210 equally. How much will each receive?
Stella is 5/7th the age of her mother. Her mother is three times the age of Stella's brother. If Stella's brother is 14 years old, how old is Stella?
Lisa bought 2 boxes of colored pens to share with her friends. Each box contains 10 pens. If Lisa wants to place an equal amount of pens in 4 gift bags, how many pens will be in each bag?
Lana and her friends ordered 16 pizzas for a party. If each pizza has 8 slices and 30% of the pizza slices were left uneaten, how many slices were left after the party?

Mike is planning to build a wooden desk which will require 28 pieces of wood. Mike already has 8 wooden pieces. He can buy boxes of wood where each box contains 4 pieces of wood. How many boxes of wood does Mike need to buy to build his desk?
Sarah has 8 bottles of juice. Each bottle contains 5 cups of juice. How many cups of juice she has in total?
Jacob wants to buy 7 pencils, each at a price of $3. He also wants to buy 5 notebooks, each costing $2 more than a pencil. How much will Jacob pay in total?
Brianna's age was 5 years more than thrice her younger sister's age when her sister was 3. If her younger sister is now 12 years old, what's the sum of their ages?

An art gallery showcased a total of 200 paintings over the past four weeks. Three weeks ago, there were 50 paintings which was 20 more than the number of paintings four weeks ago. How many paintings were there in the gallery last week?
Mrs. Brown harvested 80 oranges from her orange tree and 4 times as many mangoes from her mango tree. What is the difference between the amount of the two fruits?
A soccer game lasts for 90 minutes. Coast City's football team trained for nine times the duration of a match. How many hours did the football team train?

It takes Dean's bike ten fewer minutes to complete the same route to the park than the bus. If the bus takes 50 minutes to go 100 miles to the park, how many minutes will it take the bike to make a round trip?
A shop had 850 cupcakes on Saturday morning. By the end of the day, they had sold 75% of the cupcakes. How many cupcakes were left unsold?
Dina loves baking cookies. If she uses 300 g of flour and 100 g of sugar for one batch of cookies and she intends to make 8 batches, how much of ingredients will she use in total?

Carla reads 2 books per week. In the third week, she was able to read thrice the number of books per week. On the fourth week, she only reads half the number of books she reads per week. How many books in total Carla reads in a month?

Peter decided to sell his gardening tools as he was moving to an apartment. He sold his garden seeds for $15. He got $10 for his trowel and agreed to sell his $20 gardening gloves at 25% off. He also sold both pairs of his gardening boots for $5 each. How much money did Peter make?
A plumber charges $50 for a visit plus an additional $25 per hour. If a job takes 3 hours to complete, how much will the total cost be?

Jonas loves books. His current collection consists of 90 mystery novels, 120 romance novels, and 150 science fiction novels. Jonas has decided to donate 20% of his mystery novels, 30% of his romance novels, and 25% of his science fiction novels. How many novels will Jonas donate in total?
Mary has 7 more cookies than Sam, who has 3 times as many cookies as Jack. If Sam has 21 cookies, how many cookies does Mary have more than Jack?
Bella has to read a 300-page book for her literature class. She has already read 1/3 of it during the weekend. How many pages does she still need to read?
Mary has 24 red apples, 16 green apples, and 20 yellow apples. If she divides the apples equally among her 12 friends, how many apples will each friend get?
The city pool charges a $10 entry fee. If 400 people visit in a single day, how much money is made in a day?
Chris works in a factory and produces 25 pieces of a certain part every day. After every five days he has a day off. How many pieces does he produce after a month (considering a month has 30 days)?

Jennifer's fridge uses 135 watts per hour. Her microwave uses twice the fridge's watts and her oven uses three times the microwave's watts. If Jennifer uses all the appliances for 3 hours, how many watts has she used?
On her first day of opening, a florist sells 3 roses for $5 each. The next day, she sells 5 tulips for $4 each. Later, she sells 10 daises for $2 each. How much does she earn at the end?

In a sports competition, there were 5 volleyball teams participating, with 12 players in each team. If the players get paid $15 per game and each team played 2 games, calculate the total sum that all players in all teams earned.

Liam uses 1/6 of a bottle of shaving cream every week. He has 1/2 of a bottle of regular shaving cream and 1/3 of a bottle of aloe vera shaving cream left. How many weeks will his shaving creams last?
A car rental company rents out 5 cars every day. After one week, every car has been rented 3 times and there are still 20 cars available. How many cars does the company originally have?
Cheryl needs 4 cups of basil to make 1 cup of pesto.  She can harvest 16 cups of basil from her farm every week for 8 weeks.  How many cups of pesto will she be able to make?
Sophia has 10 marbles. She gave 1/5th of her marbles to her younger brother, then lost 2 of the remaining marbles. How many marbles does Sophia have left?

Sara's savings account had $2000. She spent $150 each week for 10 weeks. At the end of the 10 weeks, she deposited half of her remaining money into her checking account. How much money is left in her savings account?
An aquarium holds 300 gallons of water. Every week, 5% of the water is removed for cleaning and replaced with fresh water. How many gallons of water are replaced in a year?
In a football match, John scored 7 goals and Mike scored twice as many as John. How many goals were scored in total?
Charlie bought 10 pencils, each costing 0.50 dollars. He gave the cashier a $10 bill. How much change should he get?

Carmen travelled to Spain. She paid $500 for the flight and three quarters of that for accommodation. If she had $2000 initially, how much money does she have left after paying for the flight and accommodation?
Of the 1000 stickers that Jenny had, she gave her five friends 120 stickers each. What's three times the number of stickers she has remaining?
A construction company has 3 excavators that each weigh 15 tonnes. There are also 10 trucks on the site that each weigh 5 tonnes. How many tonnes heavier are the excavators than the trucks?
Nick runs 4 miles each morning to keep fit. If there are 30 days in a month, how many miles will he have run by the end of the month if he doesn’t skip his morning run?

To cook soup, the restaurant needs meat and vegetables. Meats $5 per pound and vegetables at $3 per kilogram. To cook one pot of soup, the restaurant needs 2 pounds of meat and 4 kilograms of vegetables. How much does it cost the restaurant to cook four pots of soup?

Robert opened a new jar of peanut butter which had 500 grams. He used some for his sandwiches for 5 days, using 20 grams each day. On the sixth day, he decided to make a peanut butter cake and used twice the amount that he did for the sandwiches. If Robert now has 300 grams left, how many grams of peanut butter did he use for the cake?

Amy and her friends are hosting a charity event. Amy baked 10 cupcakes and her friend Julia baked 15 cupcakes. Later, they realized they only needed 20 cupcakes for the event. How many cupcakes did they have left?
There are 5 oranges for each 2 apples in a basket. If there are 20 apples, how many oranges are there?
A hardcover book costs $30. A paperback book and a hardcover book together cost $40. How much would you pay for 1 hardcover and 2 paperback books?

A pizza parlor sells 20 large pizzas at $15 each, and 30 medium pizzas at $10 each. The losses it incurs are the cost of ingredients worth $200 and the salary of their workers worth $300. How much profit does the pizza parlor make?
Thomas baked 45 loaves of bread in the morning and another 80 loaves in the afternoon. If he sold half of his loaves, how many were left?
In a week, Chris earns 3 times more money than Dana. If Dana earns $150 per week, how much money does Chris make in a month?
A train is traveling at a speed of 50 miles per hour and it needs to cover a distance of 250 miles. How long will it take the train to reach its destination?
A school is organizing a field trip for its 500 students. If each bus can accommodate 50 students, how many buses does the school need to arrange for the trip?
Jeremy has 7 balloons. If 3 balloons are blue, 1 balloon is red, and the rest are yellow, what percentage of balloons are yellow?
A rectangular room is 16 feet long and 12 feet wide. Bob wants to carpet the entire room. Each carpet square is 1 foot by 1 foot. How many carpet squares will Bob need?

Jaime needs to buy 5 shirts, 3 pants and 7 ties for his new job. A clothing store has a sale on pants, buy one get one 1/2 off. If the shirts cost $30 each, the ties cost $10 and each pair of pants is $25. How much will Jaime spend on his clothes?
Tim owns a small apple orchard. He picked 60 apples in the first hour, 90 apples in the second hour, and 150 apples in the third hour. How many apples in total did Tim pick?

A rectangular field measures 15 m in length and 10 m in width. A round swimming pool with a diameter of 6 m is installed in the field. What is the area in square meters of the field that is not covered by the pool?
Teddy and Freddy are playing a game. The player who collects the most marbles wins. Teddy collects 15 marbles and Freddy collects double that amount. How many marbles does the winner have?
Jacob bought a pair of shoes and a hat online. The hat is cheaper by 10% than the $100 shoes. Since he is a returning customer, he got a 15% discount for the total cost. How much did Jacob pay?

At a family gathering there are 8 adults and 15 children. Each adult will eat 3 slices of pizza and each child will eat 2 slices. If each pizza is divided into 8 slices, how many pizzas should they order?

Sophie's aunt gave her $260 to spend on clothes at the mall. She bought 2 shirts that cost $18.50 each and a pair of trousers that cost $63. She then decides to purchase 4 more articles of clothing with her remaining budget. How much money would each item cost if she split the cost of each item evenly?
A bus can carry 50 passengers. How many buses are needed to carry 300 people?

George, the gorilla, Grace, the graceful grey goose, and Gabby, the galloping gila monster, perform at the County Zoo. In the performance, George swings from one side to another for a total of 15 minutes. Grace flies overhead three times longer than George. And finally, Gabby runs for half the time that Grace was flying. What is the combined length of time that the three performers performed?
A group of friends splits the check equally at a restaurant. If the check was $200 and included a 10% tip, how much was the original bill before tipping?
A farmer sells 5 kg of apples for $20. If a basket can accommodate 10 kg of apples, how much does a full basket cost?
Bob has a bag of marbles. It contains 4 red, 3 blue and 2 green marbles. If Bob picks a marble at random, what is the probability he will pick a green marble?
Grace's income is 3/4 of what it used to be and John's income is now thrice what Grace used to make. How much do they earn combined if Grace used to make $8000?

Andrew is drifting on a river current that constantly moves downstream. The current needs to move 25 miles to reach the end of the river. It takes 20 minutes for the current to travel along the first half of the river. The current then takes 4 minutes per mile to move along the next 5 miles. It then takes 10 minutes per mile for the current to finish moving along the final 5 miles. In hours, how long did it take for the current to reach the end?
Amy buys a pair of jeans for $65 and a shirt for 50% less than the price of the jeans. How much did she spend altogether?
Jane bought 4 books at $7 each. She also bought a notebook. Jane paid $35 in total. How much did the notebook cost?

Daniel took part in a marathon. He ran 20 km without taking a break. After the break, he ran half of the initial distance. Finally, he completed the marathon by running the last 5 km. What is the total distance of the marathon?
Liam and Olivia are artists. Liam painted three-fifth as many paintings as Olivia. If Olivia painted 50 paintings, how many paintings did Liam paint?
Michael goes jogging every morning. He runs 2 miles every day on weekdays and 5 miles every day on weekends. How many total miles does he run in a week?

Alex saves $200 each month. After some months, he buys a computer for $1200. He continues to save $200 each month and buys a phone that costs $600 after a few months. How many months in total is he saving money?

Mrs. Clarke baked 4 blueberry pies and 6 apple pies for a bake sale. At the end of the bake sale all the pies were sold for a total of $90. If each blueberry pie costs $7, how much was each apple pie?

In a basketball game, The Giants scored 3 points in the first quarter and twice that amount in the second quarter.  The opponents, The Eagles, scored half the amount that The Giants scored in the first quarter and three times the amount that The Giants scored in their second quarter.  How many points did both teams score in total?
A dog barks 5 times per minute. Count the total number of barks 15 dogs at the same pace will have made in 4 hours.

Brandon is writing a research paper. The introduction and conclusion are each 500 words, and each of the three body paragraphs is the same length. If his paper has to be 3500 words total, how many words does each body paragraph have to be?
At an arts and crafts sale, one-fifth of the 100 artwork pieces are paintings. Six among the paintings will be donated to a local museum. How many paintings will still be for sale?
In a competition, Alex hit 200 targets. 40 percent of his hits were bullseyes, and 30 percent of the bullseyes were with a single shot. How many bullseyes did Alex hit with a single shot?

Emily wants to buy a pair of sneakers she saw online, which cost $100, but she only has $30 saved. She earns $40 every week from her part-time job, but spends $20 every weekend on hanging out with her friends. How many weeks will it take for her to save enough money to buy the sneakers?
A wine shop has bottles of wine at $10 each. If a customer buys 12 bottles, he gets a discount of 20%. Find the total amount the customer has to pay.
Billy is buying peanuts for his party. He has $15 to spend and needs 150 peanuts. A bag of 50 peanuts costs $3.50. How much change will Billy have when he buys what he needs?
On a 400 meter race, John can run 10 meters per second while Eric can run 8 meters per second. How much faster does John finish the race than Eric in seconds?
Jenny collects stickers. She has 45 animal stickers, 38 flag stickers and she just bought 27 new cartoon stickers. How many stickers does Jenny have in total?
A factory produces 1700 goods in a week. It plans to increase its production by 15% next week. How many goods will the factory produce next week?

Roger's small farm features a chicken coop with 4 chickens each having 2 legs, a pig pen which is home to 3 pigs each with 4 legs and a small barn housing 2 cows each having 4 legs. How many legs are there in total on the farm?

Annie the dog eats 10 pounds of food per day, which is 5 times more than the cat eats per day. The mouse eats 1/50 as much as the cat per day, but Hank the horse eats 2000 times more than the mouse does per day. How much, in pounds, does the Hank the horse eat than Annie?
Paul has 200 stamps in his collection. He gives 1/2 of his collection to his brother and then buys 75 more stamps. How many stamps does Paul have now?
If a loaf of bread costs $2 and a gallon of milk costs $3, how much would Robert spend if he wanted to buy 15 loaves of bread and 10 gallons of milk without any discounts?

Alice sold 45 cupcakes at the school fair. The next day she sold twice as much as the first day. A week later, she made more cupcakes and sold three times the number she sold on the first day. How many cupcakes did Alice sell in total?
Peter watches movies for 4 hours. He spends $7.50 for every 30 minutes. How much money did he spend, in dollars?
Sophia trained for a marathon. She ran 8 miles on the first day, 9 miles on the second day and on the third day, she ran 10 miles. What was her average miles per day?
A box contains 150 candies; 30 are red, 50 are blue and the rest are green. How many green candies are in the box?
70% of people prefer cats over dogs. If there are 1200 people in a certain town, how many people prefer dogs over cats?
Jenna bought 4 packs of printer paper. Each pack contains 5 reams of paper. Each ream of paper has 500 sheets. How many sheets of paper did Jenna buy?

A farm has 12 cows, 27 chickens, 35 pigs, and 15 horses. The number of sheep on the farm is half of the sum of cows and horses, and the number of goats is two-thirds the number of the sum of pigs and chickens. How many more goats are there than sheep?

A school cafeteria serves lunch to 60 kids on Monday, and expects to serve double that amount on Tuesday. If they need to cut apples into halves for dessert and each child is expected to get 2 apple halves, how many apples do they need to prepare for Tuesday?
Lisa borrows $5000 from a bank at a 5% simple annual interest rate. How much total money will she have to pay back if she clears the loan after 4 years?

A delivery service uses vans to deliver packages. Each van cannot load more than 1200 kilograms. Each package weighs 10 kilograms. If there are 80 packages, how much more weight can still be placed into the van?

Fred has a stamp collection of 150 stamps. These are either Star Wars stamps, Pokemon stamps, or Classic Vehicle stamps. 40% of them are Pokemon stamps. 10% are Star Wars stamps. How many Classic Vehicle stamps does he own?
Andrew runs 5 times around the park every day. Each circuit is 800 meters long. How many kilometers does Andrew run in a week?
Grace is reading a 250-page novel. She has already read 30% of the novel. How many more pages does she need to read to finish the novel?
Sara decided to gift her students 3 boxes of chocolates every week for a month. If each box contains 30 chocolates, how many chocolates did she gift to her students in the entire month?
If Amy bought 500 candies for ten times the price John paid, and John paid $100 for his candies, how much did Amy pay for each candy?
A train travels at a speed of 90 km/hr for 2 hours, then slows to a speed of 60 km/hr for another 3 hours. What is the total distance that the train travels?
The average score for 7th graders is 92, the 8th graders is 3 more than the 7th graders and the 9th graders average score is 89.  What is the average score for the school?
Joe works part-time and earns $25 for each shift worked. If he saved his money for 10 shifts, how much money did he save in total?
Peter plays a video game for 6 hours each day. How many hours would he play the video game in a month of 30 days?
The class must read a book that has 120 pages. If each student reads 4 pages a day, how many days will it take them to finish the book?
Dec's bakery bakes 550 cupcakes a day. Each cupcake uses 100 grams of flour. In a week, how many kilograms of flour does the bakery use?
Lucy is knitting a blanket. The blanket's width is 6 feet long. Each knot Lucy makes is 1/2 inch long. If Lucy makes 20 knots per minute, how many minutes does it take Lucy to make the blanket?
Linda has twice as many books as Laura. If Laura has 3 more books than Sarah and Sarah has 2 books, how many books does Linda have?
Samantha is planning a road trip that spans 1500 miles. Her car can travel 30 miles per gallon. If gas costs $3 per gallon, how much will Samantha have to spend on gas for the entire trip?

Melissa is baking a cake. The recipe requires 4 eggs, 500 grams of flour, 2 teaspoons of baking powder and 5 tablespoons of sugar. However, Melissa wants to bake only half a cake. How many eggs will she need?
Jane spends $5 on coffee each day. If she buys coffee for 30 days straight, how much does she spend in total?
Rakesh and his friends decided to set a gym membership. The membership fee is $30 per person. Rakesh paid for his own membership and for 3 of his friends. How much did Rakesh pay in total?
85 students participate in a science fair. 15 fewer students participate in a math competition. How many students participate in both the science fair and the math competition?
If Kevin said that his age is 2 times his sister's age when his sister was 5 years old and currently his sister is 15 years old, what's the total of their age now?

A racing event has a limit on the weight of each car. Each car can be no more than 3 times the minimum weight and no less than 6 pounds heavier than the standard car. The standard car weighs 150 pounds. What is the maximum weight of a car in the event?
Anna, Brian, and Charlie share 20 chocolates. Anna eats 1/4 of all the chocolates, Brian eats 1/5, and Charlie eats the rest. How many chocolates did Charlie eat?

A factory received 50 crates of merchandise. A third of the crates were toys, a fifth were kitchen utensils, and a quarter were books. The rest were storage bins. How many crates of storage bins did the factory receive?
A local pizzeria can bake 14 pizzas in an hour. If they stay open for 10 hours a day, how many pizzas can they bake in a week (considering a week has 7 days)?
If Amelia can paint a house in 3 hours and Jackson can do it in 2 hours. How long will it take both to paint the house together?
In a bakery, Sarah and Jake used 2/5 of a bag of sugar and 3/5 of a bag of sugar respectively for making pastries. If a bag of sugar is equal to 5 kilograms, how many kilograms of sugar were left?
Julia has 60 candies. She gave half of it to her brother. How many candies does Julia have left?
Rachel has 40 candies. She gave 3 candies to each of her friends. If she has 8 candies left after giving candies to her friends, how many friends does Rachel have?

Bill can run at a speed of 6 miles per hour on a track and 4 miles per hour on a trail. If he ran for a total of 5 hours, 3 hours on a track and 2 hours on a trail, how many miles did he run in total?
Each horse eats 5 pounds of hay each day. If there are 8 horses on a farm, how much hay is needed to feed all the horses for one week (7 days)?

Alexa has 2 dozen goldfish in her aquarium. Frederick has quarter as many goldfish as Alexa in his tank. Megan has one-fourth of what Frederick has in his tank. Zara has 5 times as many goldfish as Megan in her pond. How many goldfish do all four friends have together?
Hazel received 19, 20, and 21 in her weekly quizzes. What is her average grade?
Tom had 72 marbles. He divided them into 8 even groups; 3 groups were blue marbles, 2 were red and the rest were green. How many green marbles were there?
There are 90 passengers on a bus. If the bus has 45 seats and each seat can hold up to 2 people. How many people are standing?

It takes Sarah 15 seconds to run 150 meters. She only goes 10 meters in the first second. She then maintains the same speed for the rest of the race. Jenny is 3 m/s faster at top speed and she can run the first 15 meters in 3 seconds. How long does it take Jenny to run 150 meters?
In a marathon race, 1 water station is placed every 3 km. If Richard has already passed 6 water stations, how many kilometers has he already run?
A bakery has 150 loaves of bread. They sell 50 loaves a day. How many loaves of bread will be left after 2 days?
Adam has 120 apples. He gives 20% of his apples to his friend, George. How many apples does Adam have left?

Lee has a strawberry farm. She harvested 60 kilograms of strawberries. If she sold 1/5 of them to a local market and 2 kilograms of them to her neighbors, how many kilograms of strawberries does she have left?

Liam wants to save for a new computer that costs $1,500. Liam's parents can cover a third of the cost, and Liam has saved $200 already. If Liam can work 50 hours a month, how many dollars does Liam have to make per hour to buy the computer in 3 months?

Benjamin jumps into a helicopter and takes off. The helicopter rises at a rate of 60 feet per minute but slowly descends at a rate of 15 feet per minute when not ascending. During his flight, he ascended for 20 minutes, then descended for 5 minutes, ascended again for 20 minutes, and finally landed. At what altitude did the helicopter attain during the flight?
Greg has 35 apples. He gave 9 apples to his friend. He then went to the market and bought twice as many apples as he gave to his friend. How many apples does Greg have now?

Lisa wants to buy a new game console which costs $500. So far, she saved up 60% of the cost. Her father decided to give her $75 to help out, and her mother gave her $25 more than her father. How much more money does Lisa need to buy the console?
A bakery bakes 80 cinnamon buns and 120 muffins every day. If the bakery operates for 6 days a week, how many total pastries does the bakery make in a week?
There are thrice as many swimmers at Lake Tahoe as in Lake Berryessa. If there are 30 swimmers in Lake Berryessa, compute the total number of swimmers at the two lakes.
A bookshop sold 18 copies of a new novel on the first day. On the second day, they sold twice the number of copies sold on the first day. How many books did they sell in two days?
Abby read 45 pages of a book the first day, 35 pages the second day and 60 pages on the third day. If the book has 300 pages in total, how many pages does she still have to read?

Greg wants to start a coffee shop. Based on his calculations, it will cost $7 per square foot of the location and he will need 10 square feet for every table in his coffee shop. He wants a 80 table coffee shop. For decorations and other setup, he will need to spend three times as much as the location cost. Fortunately, his friend decides to fund 50% of total cost. How much will Greg need to invest?
For his birthday, Graham received twice as many shirts as he did belts. If he received 10 belts, how many pieces of clothing did Graham receive?
John's car can travel 300 miles on 15 gallons of gas. He plans to go on a 1200 mile trip. How many gallons of gas will John need for the trip.
An orange tree produced 150 oranges this year, which is twice as many as it produced last year. How many oranges did the tree produce last year?
Every Sunday, Joe buys 3 comic books for $5 each and 2 magazines for $7 each. How much money will Joe spend on comic books and magazines in one month?

Lucy wants to install a rectangular swimming pool in her yard which measures 25 ft by 18 ft. She has a tree in the yard that occupies a space of 5 ft by 3 ft, which she doesn't want to remove. How many square feet of her yard will the pool occupy?
A car travels 600 kilometers in 5 hours. If Jesse wants to reach his destination in 4 hours, at what speed should he travel?

Alice plants some carrots in her backyard. In the first week, each carrot grows 3 inches. In the second week, they each grow twice as much as the first week. In the third week, each carrot grows 3 times as much as it did in the second week. How tall are the carrots now?

John sells oranges in a fruit shop. On Monday, a shipment of 500 kg of oranges arrived at the shop. On Tuesday, John sold a total of 200 kg of oranges to customers. On Wednesday, the shop was closed, causing 100 kg of oranges to rot and to be thrown away. On Thursday morning another shipment arrived, three times the size of the first one. How many kilograms of oranges did John have ready for sale on Friday?
At a birthday party, there are 5 pieces of cake. Twenty kids each get 15 pieces of cake. How many pieces of cake are the kids eating in total?
Each year, the local Little League orders special jerseys for an annual event. This year, they placed an order for 15 teams. Each team has 18 players. How many jerseys did the Little League order?

For an art project, Ella created a mural which was a grid of colorful tiles, 12 tiles wide and 9 tiles high. A set of tiles contained 15 tiles, and she bought four sets. Later she realized she needed more, so she bought a half number of sets she initially bought. How many total tiles did she have?

Sam's garden, in the shape of a triangle, has sides measuring 10, 12 and 14 units. If Pete has a rectangular garden that is 1.5 times bigger than Sam's garden and Pete decides to extend his garden by 25%, how big will Pete's garden be?
Jen baked 20 loaves of bread at $3 each and 15 pies at $5 each. How much money did she make?

Sally has a farm. She has 50 chickens, each of which lays 6 eggs every week. If Sally throws away half of the eggs because they are too small and then sells the rest of the eggs in dozens for $3 each, how much money does she make in a week?
A factory produces 200 units of product each day. The packaging department can package 5 units per minute. How many days will it take to package all the products produced in a 30-day month?
A basketball team lost 8 games and won twice as many as they lost. How many games did they win?

In an office, the total number of employees is 120. Among them, 2/5 are female and the remaining are male. If 3/4 of the female employees are working in the sales team and the remaining are in the HR team, how many female employees are in the HR team?
Ms. Liu drives to work at a speed of 40 miles per hour for 30 minutes. After work, she drives home at a slower speed of 30 miles per hour for 1 hour. What is the total distance she covered?
There are 45 chocolates in a box. If Tony eats 1/3 of them and Mary eats 1/5 of what's left, how many chocolates are remaining?
Joel is a taxi driver. He works 5 days a week, driving 3 hours each day. If he earns $20 per hour, how much does he earn in a week?

Steve is driving 800 miles to visit his grandparents. His average speed for this trip is 60 miles per hour. Every three hours of driving, he makes a rest stop for 20 minutes. His car gets 20 miles per gallon of gas, and his gas indicator comes on when he's used 16 gallons, at which point he stops to refill, which takes 15 minutes. How long, in minutes, does he take to get to his grandparents' house, if he starts out with a full tank of gas?
Sam has 20 comic books and decided to give half of them to his cousin. After realizing he had nothing to read, he buys another 12 comic books. How many comic books does Sam have now?

A programmer earns $50 for every issue he fixes. On Monday he fixed 9 issues, on Tuesday 7 issues, on Wednesday 5 issues and on Thursday 11 issues. How much the programmer earned during these four days?
There are 4 roses in a bouquet. How many roses are there in 7 bouquets?
A school has four times as many students as teachers. If the school has 200 students, how many teachers does the school have?
Beth has a box of candies. She gave half of it to her friend. If she had 20 candies left, how many candies did she first have in her box?

A new movie gets released and makes $120 million in the box office for its opening weekend.  It ends up making 3.5 times that much during its entire run.  If the production company gets to keep 60%, how much profit did they make if the movie cost $60 million to produce?
There are 35 students in a classroom. Four-fifths of them participated in the school play. How many students did not participate?
Rachel purchases 4 cords of wood that are 18 feet each. It takes 6 feet to build a small cabin. How many cabins can she build?

Tom is packaging three holiday gifts. One gift requires three square feet of wrapping paper. The second gift requires half that amount. The third gift requires the same amount as both other gifts combined. How many square feet of wrapping paper does Tom need for all three gifts?
Frank starts mid-year with a balance of $300 in his savings account. Each month, he deposited $50 into the account. How much money is in his savings account at the end of the year?
A group of 50 people are going on a bus, but 5 forgot their tickets and another 10 missed the bus. How many people actually got on the bus?
In a music band, there are twice as many guitarists as there are drummers and there are 5 times as many singers as there are drummers. If there are 3 drummers, how many members are there in the band?
Jimmy has 100 apple seeds. He discovered that 30% of the seeds are not viable. He wants to plant the rest in his garden. How many apple trees can Jimmy plant?
Lucy paints a room that has 4 walls.  Each wall is 3 meters by 4 meters.  She can paint 1 square meter every 15 minutes.  She has 8 hours to paint everything.  How many hours does she have to spare?
Emma gets paid 50 cents for each picture she draws. She also gets a 5 dollar bonus each time she draws 50 pictures. If she draws 100 pictures how much will she earn including bonuses?
A math teacher distributed 3 pencils per student. If there are 20 students in a class, how many pencils did the math teacher distribute?
A library has 30 shelves, and each shelf has 20 books. Every week, the librarian removes 5 books from each shelf for cleaning. How many books would be removed from the shelves after 3 weeks?

Angela sold watermelons at her farmer's market stand for $7 each. One day, she sold 17 watermelons. The following day, she increased the price by $2 and sold 12 watermelons. What was her total sales in those two days?

Sandra had 80 pairs of shoes but started giving some to friends. She gave away 3 pairs to one friend, doubled that amount to another friend, and then decided to throw out 10 old pairs. How many pairs of shoes does she have left?
A garden has 320 apple trees and 170 pear trees. Each apple tree produces 24 apples, and each pear tree produces 28 pears. How many pieces of fruit does the garden produce?
Emily runs a bakery and makes 8 cakes a day during weekdays and 12 cakes each day over the weekend. If she works 6 days a week, how many cakes does she make in a week?

Lucy joined a book club where members read an average of 15 books per month. The club manager deletes older books from the list at the rate of 3 books per month per person. If the club has 12 people and the total number of books in the list is 500, how many books would be in the list after Lucy had been a member for 3 months?
Sally buys 3 books for $30 each and 2 books for $50 each. How much did she spend in total?
Lisa has $300 in her bank account. She spends 3 dollars every time she goes to the grocery store, and makes 5 trips to the store every month. How much money will Lisa have after 6 months?

Aiden owns a convenience store and he decides to take inventory. He has 50 packs of chips, with each pack containing 10 bags. He also has 40 cans of soda, and 15 candy bars. If a bag of chips costs $1.00, a can of soda costs $2.00, and a candy bar costs $1.50 each, What is the total worth of Aiden's inventory?

Chris ran for 8 kilometers. He realized that he had run half the distance he intended to run. He made a short stop and rested for 15 minutes. If he runs at a speed of 12 kilometers per hour, how much time in total will Chris take to finish his run (including his rest)?
A local farm sells apples at a rate of 3 apples for $2. Amy went to the farm and bought 15 apples. How much did Amy spend on apples?
A pizza shop sells 200 pizzas each day. Each pizza is sold for $12. If the cost to make each pizza is $4, how much profit does the pizza shop make in a week?
Joe is planning to paint 3 rooms in his house. Each room needs 2 gallons of paint and one gallon costs $15. How much will Joe spend on paint?

If Tony normally has a temperature of 95 degrees, and he comes down with a sickness that raises his temperature by 10 degrees, how many degrees above the threshold of fever is his temperature if a fever is anything over 100 degrees?

In a classroom, during the music period, each student needs a set of 5 different music instruments. If there are 25 students in a class and they bought another 10 sets for spare parts, how many total music instrument items did they buy?

Jake baked 6 whole apple pies and gave away 1 whole pie to his neighbor. He then sold 3 whole pies. He cut the remaining whole pies into 4 pieces each. His family ate 2/3 of the pieces. How many pieces of apple pie do they still have?
A pond has 50 fish and each year the number of fish doubles. How many fish will be in the pond in five years?
A classroom has 25 students. Each student needs 3 notebooks for the school year. Each notebook costs $2. How much will all the notebooks cost?
In a race, Timmy beat Bobby by 5 meters. Bobby was able to run 35 meters. How many meters did Timmy run?

A workshop is offered to a company for their management team. The regular workshop fee is $200 but they offer a 10% discount if they register a week before the scheduled workshop. The company registered 15 managers for the workshop three weeks before the schedule and it also released a $15 lunch allowance for each of the managers. How much did the company spend in all?
It's 123 miles from San Francisco to Monterey. If Anna drives at an average speed of 60 mph, how long will it take her to get to Monterey?
Tom works out 4 days a week lifting weights for 30 minutes each day. If he continues this routine for 5 weeks, how many minutes has he spent lifting weights?
Sandy bakes 24 cupcakes. Each cupcake contains 2 tablespoons of sugar. If she has a cup of sugar that contains 16 tablespoons, how many cupcakes can she make from a cup of sugar?

The bookstore has two types of books. The first one costs $2.5 each. The second one costs $3.5 each. A girl then walks in and buys 8 books from each type. What was the total cost, in dollars, of the books she bought?
The pet store has 8 parrots, 10 rabbits, and 4 turtles. How many total legs do all the animals have?
There are 920 deer in a park. 10% of the deer have 8 antlers, and a quarter of that number also have albino fur. How many albino 8-antlered deer are there?
Lacy wears a size 5. Her friend Julie wears a size three times smaller than Lacy's. Hannah wears a size three times Julie's size plus 2. What size does Hannah wear?
Beth baked 5 dozen cookies for a school bake sale. If each dozen contains 12 cookies, and half of them were sold in the morning, how many cookies does Beth still have to sell in the afternoon?
Jack plants a tree that is 2 years old and 10 feet tall. It gains 4 feet per year. How old is it when it is 42 feet tall?
Tracy has a beautiful garden with 60 roses. She gives away 5 roses to her sister. Then she divides the remaining roses equally into 5 flower vases. How many roses are in each vase?
Garry received one box of chocolates every week from his grandmother for the past year. This Christmas, she gave him an extra 7 boxes. How many boxes did Garry receive in total this year?
Jake is watering his plants. If one plant needs 2 liters of water and there are 15 plants in his garden, how much water does Jake need?
Audrey can play 7 songs on the piano. She practices 2 new songs every week. How many songs will she be able to play after 4 weeks?
Angelica is a cashier who gets 2% commission off total sales. If she sold items worth $4500 in a week, how much commission did she earn?

Assuming the first year of a cat's life equals 15 human years. The second year of a cat's life equals 9 human years. After that, every year of a cat's life equals 4 human years. According to this, how many human years has my 11-year-old cat lived?
A landscaper needs 4 times as much fertilizer as pesticide for a yard. If he uses 150 pounds combined of pesticide and fertilizer, how many pounds of fertilizer does he use?

A carpenter had 350 nails in his toolbox. In the morning, he used 3/5 of the nails for making furniture. By the afternoon, he used half of the remaining nails. How many nails are left in the toolbox by the end of the day?
Mike and Emily went to the water park. There are 4 slides in the park and each slide has 3 lanes. If Mike and Emily each go down all the lanes, how many lanes did they slide down in total?
Alan and Jane both start biking at the same time. Alan travels at a speed of 4 mph and Jane at 5 mph. If they both bike for 2.5 hours, how much further did Jane travel than Alan?
A sports stadium can seat 20,000 people. If the stadium is filled to half of its capacity, how many people are in the stadium?
There are 500 marbles in a box. Max takes out 75 marbles, Laura takes out another 225. If Laura then puts back in 45 marbles, how many marbles are left in the box?
Ray has 38 small boxes that he needs to pack into large shipping crates. Each large crate can hold 7 small boxes. How many large crates does he need?

James and Steven are collecting old newspapers for recycling. James collected 3 bags with 20 newspapers in each bag. Steven’s collection was 2 bags with 35 newspapers in each bag. How many more newspapers did Steven collect?
A box of chocolates costs $6, while a box of biscuits is 50% more expensive. Amy went and bought three boxes of biscuits and two boxes of chocolates. How much did she pay for her purchases?
Sam has 40 stamps in his collection and he bought new stamps, which increased his collection by 20%. How many new stamps did Sam buy?

In a poll about a new coffee blend, 25% of the respondents rated it as Excellent; 55% rated it as Good; 90% of the remaining respondents rated it as Fair and the rest rated it as Poor. If 200 people answered the poll, how many of them rated the coffee blend as Poor?
Matt can buy key chains in packs of 10 for $20 or in packs of 4 for $12. How much money can he save if he chooses to buy 20 key chains at a lower price?

Jackie started a diet plan to recommence her daily jogging routine. On her first day, she jogged for 35 minutes. On her second day, she jogged for 15 minutes more than her first day. On her third day, she jogged for half the time she jogged on her first day. How many minutes in total did Jackie jog in three days?

Petra has 5 dogs; Max, Spot, Furry, Cookie, and Puff. Each dog has a toy bone. Spot has twice as many toy bones as Max but Puff has one less bone than Max. If Max has 2 toy bones, how many toy bones does Spot have?
A bakery has 20 dozens of cupcakes. Each box can hold 2 dozen cupcakes. How many boxes are needed to pack all the cupcakes?
Danielle reads 4 books a month. Each book has an average of 300 pages. How many pages does Danielle read in a year?
Sarah makes homemade jams and sells them in 12-ounce jars. She usually makes 5 jars from 3 pounds of fruits. However, this time she has 7 pounds of fruits. How many jars can she make?
A bakery sells donuts in a pack of 12 for $7.50. If John buys 3 packs of donuts and a bag of coffee for $4.25, how much will he spend in total?
A toy factory produces 4 teddy bears every minute. It operates for 8 hours on weekdays and 3 hours on weekends. How many teddy bears can the factory produce in a week?
Ajay purchased a 1.5-kilogram bag of rice. He uses 350 grams of rice for each dish he cooks. How many dishes can Ajay cook before he runs out of rice?

Lisa wants to preorder a new book with her saved money. The book costs 20 dollars and Lisa heard there is a 15 percent shipping fee. She saves 2 dollars a week from her lunch money, and believes she can save all of it. How many weeks will it take for Lisa to be able to preorder the new book with her savings?

Nathan had a nasty cough and bought a bottle of 30 syrups. Each day he drank 1 syrup three times a day for the first 3 days. For the next 2 days, he reduced this amount to two times a day. On the sixth day, he drinks 1 syrup and felt better. How many syrups are left in the bottle?

Lilia is preparing for a long road trip. She fills her vehicle with 12 gallons of gas in the morning. Along the way, she refuels twice, adding 8 and 10 gallons respectively. If her car consumes 1 gallon for every 20 miles, how many miles can she cover?
In a school, there are 20 classrooms. Each classroom can seat 30 students. However, only 80% of the seats are filled. How many students are there in the school?

A group of friends agreed to share their vacation expenses equally, including for their tour guide's fee. However, the tour guide had unforeseen travel costs amounting to $200 and the friends agreed to pay half. The other half will be deducted from his $250 fee. If one friend paid the entire half of the travel costs, how much does he still need to contribute to meet his equal share?
Gina is making homemade candles. Each candle requires 0.3 pound of wax. If she has 120 pounds of wax, how many candles can she make?
Ava and Bella plan to share the cost of a present for their friend. If the present costs $80 and Bella contributes $30, how much does Ava need to contribute?
There are 12 cookies on a plate. Ken ate 1/4 of them, and Dan ate 1/3 of the rest. How many cookies are left on the plate?

In a park, there are ducks of three different types: white, black, and yellow. In total there are 112 ducks, with 12 being white and two times as many black ducks as white ducks. Yellow ducks make up to the rest. How many yellow ducks are there?
In a chess match, Emily won 60% of her games. If she played 30 games, how many games did she win?
Tina is hosting a barbecue party and her recipe recommends 1/4 kilogram of meat per guest. If she's inviting 12 people and the beef is $8.00 per kilogram, how much will the meat cost her?

Bill has a rectangular garden with a length of 15 meters and a width of 10 meters. He wants to plant potatoes in the whole garden. If one packet of potato seeds can plant 5 square meters, how many packets of potato seeds does he need to buy?
Harry has 5 books and decides to buy another 3 books at his favorite bookstore. If each book costs $15, how much does Harry spend on new books?
At Lulu's Lunchbox, Colin ordered 2 sandwiches each costing $7 and 3 bottles of water, each costing $1. He also decided to leave a tip of 15%. What is the total cost of Colin's lunch with tip?
Cathy has 7 necklaces. Katie gives Cathy a third of her 18 necklaces. Then Cathy gives half of her necklaces to charity. How many necklaces does Cathy have remaining?

John, Laura, and Sarah planned to meet at a restaurant for lunch at 1:00 p.m. John arrived at 1:15. Laura arrived 20 minutes later than John, and Sarah was twice as late as Laura. How late, in minutes, was Sarah for the lunch?
Sara planted 15 rose bushes in her garden. Each rose bush had 6 roses when planted. By the end of summer, each rose bush had 3 more roses. How many roses in Sara's garden by the end of summer?

Frank wants to build a fence around his backyard, which is rectangular in shape, having a length of 30 feet and a width of 20 feet. If he needs 1.5 foot of fencing for every foot of his yard's perimeter,how much fencing does he need, in feet?

Anna takes $50 to a flea market. She spent some of her money on vintage jewelry and $10 on handmade candles. After shopping, she has $5 more than half of her starting money left. How much did she spend on vintage jewelry?

A boy received 65 red marbles and 35 blue marbles as gift. He distributes the total collection equally among his 5 friends. If he then takes back a few marbles from each friend to have 16 marbles left with each, how many marbles did he take back from each friend?

Bob has $450 in his savings account. He decides to divide this money into three parts: one third for his day-to-day expenses, half for his tuition fees, and the remaining amount for his weekend trip. How much money does he have for his weekend trip?
A book costs $30. During a sale, the book's price was reduced by $15. What is the new price of the book?
Peter is planning a road trip. His car's tank can hold 16 gallons of gas, and he knows he gets 30 miles to the gallon. If his trip is 1800 miles, how many times will Peter have to stop for gas?
Mary rented 4 books from the library that charges $0.30 per day for each book. She returned all books after 15 days. How much did she pay for the books?
Michael needs to pack 100 books into boxes. Each box can contain 8 books. How many boxes does Michael need?

Kenneth went shopping for some furniture for his new flat. He spent $800 on a sofa, $400 on a table and 6 chairs which cost $75 each. After buying the items, Kenneth had $115 left. How much money did Kenneth have when he went shopping?
Sam has 60 dollars, 50 pounds, and 6000 yen. If there are 2 dollars per pound and 120 yen per dollar, how much does Sam have in yen?
A class has 20 students, 10 boys and 10 girls. If 2 more boys join the class, what is the percentage of boys in the class now?
In Jane's garden, there are 8 rows of apple trees and 6 apple trees in each row. Jane decides to cut 2 rows of trees completely. How many apple trees remain in Jane's garden?
Joe decided to lose weight and opted for jogging daily. Day 1, he jogged 3 miles. Each day he decided to jog 1 mile more than the previous day. How many miles would he have jogged by Day 5?

For a fundraising event, the school gymnasium has 60 available chairs and 20 available tables. The school can seat 8 people per table. How many more chairs does the school need to ensure that each person sitting at a table has a chair?
Beth is making necklaces to sell at a craft fair. Each necklace requires 15 beads and she has 250 beads. How many necklaces can she make?
A car tires can run for about 8 km on 1 liter of fuel. If the car's tank capacity is 10 liters, how much distance can it travel on in 3 full tanks?
A rectangular garden has dimensions of 20 by 15 feet. There are 5 equal-sized beds in the garden, and a larger bed that is the same size as 2 of the regular beds. What is the area of the larger bed?
If Jacob runs 6 miles a day and his twin brother Luke runs twice as much, how many miles would they have run together in a week?
Ashley is turning 12 and wants to have five cakes with her age in candles on each of them. Candles come in packs of 10 and cost $3 a pack. If the candles cost $18, how old is Ashley?

Marcus is the head architect of a housing construction project that will be used to construct a new neighborhood. He uses Chicago as a reference city, which has 1000 houses, 50 schools, and 10 hospitals. The new neighborhood must have 700 houses, 40 fewer schools, and twice as many hospitals. How many buildings will Marcus need for the project?

A baker has 15 sacks of flour. Each sack contains 8 bags of flour. Each bag of flour can produce 120 bread. If the baker uses the flour sacks to bake bread for one week and divides all the bread evenly among 7 days, how many breads will be baked each day?
An amusement park offers a 15% discount on tickets for students. If the original price of the ticket is $30, how much will a student pay?
Kathleen purchased a dress for 50 dollars and shoes for 30 dollars. She then decided to return the dress and buy a cheaper one for only 20 dollars. How much did Kathleen spend in total?

Emily, Ethan, and Evan are studying courses for their college. Emily takes two more courses than Ethan, who takes 3 courses more than Evan. If Evan took 5 courses, how many courses do they all take in total?
At a farm, there are chickens and rabbits. Each chicken has 2 feet and each rabbit has 4 feet. If there are a total of 30 feet, and there are 3 more chickens than rabbits, how many rabbits are there?
Jenny baked 7 cakes each day for a week. Mike then ate 32 of her cakes. How many cakes remain?
During a science experiment, Chris found that 75% of the substance evaporated in 5 hours. If the initial amount of the substance was 4 kilograms, how many kilograms remained after 5 hours?
Andrew has 30 marbles. He gave 1/5 of his marbles to his friend Peter. How many marbles does Andrew have left?
Lucy buys 10 pairs of socks with a 30% off discount. Each pair originally costs $8. How much did Lucy pay altogether?
Mark can paint a fence in 45 minutes, and his younger brother Jake paints at half his speed. If both they simultaneously start painting a fence, how long will it take them to finish painting it?
Nick has 5 red balls, 6 green balls and 4 yellow balls. He wants to put them into boxes. Each box can only contain 2 balls. How many boxes does he need?

A fruit vendor sold 50 pounds of oranges and 30 pounds of apples one day. The next day, he sold twice the amount of oranges and three times the amount of apples than he did the previous day. How many pounds of fruit did he sell altogether?

Peter carried $500 to the market.  He bought 6 kilos of potatoes for $2 per kilo, 9 kilos of tomato for $3 per kilo, 5 kilos of cucumbers for $4 per kilo, and 3 kilos of bananas for $5 per kilo.  How much is Peter’s remaining money?
A group of 12 friends are planning a bike trip. Each friend can carry 3 water bottles. If they consume 2 water bottles per day, how many days will they last without replenishing their water stock?
Zoe was 6 years old 8 years ago. How old will she be after 9 years?
Alex bought 5 books from the bookstore. Each book cost $12. However, he got a discount of 20% on his total purchase. How much did he spend at the bookstore?
Ben needs 4 eggs to bake a cake. If he bought a tray of 24 eggs, how many cakes can he bake?
Ella is purchasing a shirt online. The shirt cost $50 and there is a 20% discount on the item. Additionally, there is a shipping fee of $5. How much does Ella pay in total?
In a school, there are 7 times as many students as teachers. If there are 28 teachers, how many students are there in the school?
Mia has 8 apples and 4 oranges. She gives 2 apples and 1 orange to her friend Bella. How many pieces of fruit does Mia have left?
Jim has to read a book for a report. The first day he reads 20 pages, and he increases the number of pages he reads by 5 every day. How many pages will he have read after four days?
Jenny baked 120 cupcakes for a party. If one-third of the cupcakes were chocolate flavored and one-half were vanilla flavored, how many cupcakes were other flavors?

Sarah needs 150 calories a day to maintain her weight. If she eats this amount for 5 days but decides to eat an extra 100 calories for the next 2 days. How many total calories will she consume in a week?

During a school trip, Lily collected 15 leaf specimens, 5 beetle specimens, and 2 bird feather specimens. If the number of beetle specimens she collected is three times the number of bird feather specimens, and the leaf specimens are five times beetle specimens collected, how many specimens did Lily collect in total?
Emma has 5 bags, each with 8 small toys inside. She takes two toys away from each bag and adds one more toy to each. How many toys in all of Emma's bags now?
Sarah sold 70% of her cupcakes at a fair. If she had 150 cupcakes at the start, how many cupcakes did Sarah sell?
A factory produces 20 computers per day. Each computer costs $500 to produce. What is the total cost of producing computers in a week if the factory operates for 5 days?
Maria has a box of chocolates of which 50% is dark chocolate and the rest are milk chocolates. If there are a total of 30 dark chocolates in the box, how many milk chocolates does she have?
Melissa is tutoring 6 students for 2 hours each. If Melissa charges $15 per hour and spends $10 on transportation costs weekly, how much will she make in 3 weeks?

Lucas wants to build a table for his workroom. The table is a rectangle 3 feet by 4 feet. He also wants to build a square shelf that is 2 feet on each side. How many total square feet of wood does Lucas need?
Samantha bought 5 books for $7 each and 3 notebooks for $3 each. How much did she spend in total?
David spent $25 on a shirt and $45 on a pair of pants. He used a coupon that was worth $15 off his total purchase. How much did he end up spending?
A bakery makes 130 cupcakes every hour. It also makes 50 more muffins than cupcakes every hour. How many products did the bakery make in a week if it works 8 hours a day, 6 days a week?
Carter scored 20 points higher on his English test than Jessica. Jessica received thrice as many points as Mike who scored a 30 on his English test.  What was Carter's test score?

Amy wants to paint her house. Each gallon of paint covers 400 square feet and one can of paint costs $30. Her house is 2000 square feet. She also needs to spend $50 on painting tools. How much will Amy spend on painting her house?
Bobby has a collection of 100 marbles. Every time a friend visits, he gives them 2 marbles. If he had 10 visitors, how many marbles does Bobby have left?

A construction company plans to build a wall that requires 200 bricks. Each brick costs $2.5. The company also needs 10 bags of cement costing $8 each. How much will the materials cost to build the wall?
In a basket of 18 fruits, 1/2 are apples, and 1/4 are oranges. The rest are bananas. How many bananas are there?

Paul has decided to start a cake baking business. He made 25 chocolate cakes and each cake can be sold for $15. However, he had to throw away 5 cakes due to them being burnt. How much money can Paul make from selling the non-burnt cakes?

Mia is on a strict diet and needs to consume 1500 calories daily. She plans to eat 3 meals and a snack. If breakfast is 400 calories, lunch is 450 calories and snack is 100 calories, how many calories should she consume for dinner?

There are 10 people in a competition. In the first round, 2 people were eliminated, in the second round, 20% of the remaining were eliminated, in the third round, 25% of the remaining were eliminated. How many people were left after the third round?
A bus can carry up to 60 passengers. If a group of 243 people want to go on a trip, how many buses do they need?
