A train travels at a speed of 60 kilometers per hour. It travels from city A to city B, then back to city A, and then again to city B. The distance between city A and city B is 120 kilometers. How many hours does it take for the train to travel?
Fifteen percent of Nathaniel's savings are for emergency. If his savings is $3000, how much is not for emergency?
Sammy bought 5 hot dogs for $3 each. He also bought 4 sodas for $1.50 each. How much did he spend in total?
Jenny ran 15 miles a day for 7 days. How many miles did she not run if she planned to run 150 miles in a week?
In the school library there are 987 books. The librarian decided to remove 231 old books and added 345 new books. How many books are there in the library now?

John bought 12 pairs of socks for $8 each. Later, he found a similar set of socks sold at a discounted price. He bought 8 more pairs which cost $6 each. How much money did John spend buying all the socks?
If John cycles at an average speed of 15 miles per hour and he cycles for 2 hours, how far does he cycle?
In a book club, there are three times as many female than male members. How many female members are there if there are 12 members in the club?
Mark and Susan have a collection of 180 stickers. Mark has 30 stickers more than Susan. How many stickers does Mark have?
Sarah goes shopping with $50. She spends $12 on a pair of jeans, $15 on a shirt, and $6 on a hat. Her friend later gives her $10 for her birthday. How much money does Sarah have now?
A pub has 20 chairs and each chair seats 3 people. If all the chairs are occupied, how many people are there in the pub?
The price of a laptop was reduced by 20% in a sale. The sale price is now $600. What was the original price of the laptop?
A gardener planted 50 rows of cucumber with 15 plants in each row. Each cucumber plant yields 25 cucumbers. How many cucumbers can the gardener get from all his planted cucumbers?
A school library has 74 books on math, 85 books on literature, and 90 books on science. If students checked out 17 math and 23 literature books, how many books are still in the library?
Ian had twenty roses. He gave six roses to his mother,  nine roses to his grandmother, four roses to his sister, and he kept the rest. How many roses did Ian keep?
Leo is collecting trading cards, which costs $10 each. If his allowance every week is $50, how many trading cards can he buy in four weeks?
If a restaurant meal costs $60 and the tax is 8%, how much will be the total bill?
Kelly walked 4 miles every day for two weeks. During weekends, however, she walked twice as much as she did on weekdays. How many miles did Kelly walk in those two weeks?
A toy shop received 200 new teddy bears to sell. After a week, 35 percent of the teddy bears had been sold. How many teddy bears were left to be sold?
During the holidays, Lance works as a merchandiser. He works 35 hours a week, spread equally over 5 workdays. If Lance earns $9 an hour, how much does he make on each workday?
A 5 gallon bucket of paint can cover 100 square feet. How many square feet can 15 gallons of paint cover?
Four bags of candies together cost $60. Of the 4 bags, there are 2 bags that cost $10 each. If the remaining bags are each equal in value, what is the cost, in dollars, of one of the remaining bags?

Donna wants to be able to do 100 consecutive push-ups so she starts a routine each day. On the first day, she does 10 push-ups. On the second day, she does 20 push-ups. On the third day, she does 30 push-ups. After realizing that she can add ten more push-ups each day, she calculates how many days it will take until she reaches 100 if she keeps up the same pace. So how many days until she can do 100 push-ups in a row?
Bill read 52 pages of his book on Saturday and 38 more pages on Sunday than on Saturday. How many pages of the book did Bill read in total over the weekend?
In a 120-item exam, Max solved 40% of the questions correctly. James solved 30% more correct answers than Max and William got twice James's score. What is William’s score?
Maya baked 3 trays of cupcakes. Each tray had 12 cupcakes. She sold the cupcakes at $5 each at a bake sale. How much money did she make from selling all the cupcakes?

Emma planted 5 potato plants. The first potato plant yielded 9 potatoes and the next one yielded 6 more potatoes than the first. The rest of the plants each yielded the same number of potatoes as the first two combined. How many potatoes does Emma have?
A bookstore sells novels for $20 each and magazines for $5 each. If Peter buys 3 novels and 4 magazines, how much money does he spend?
Phillip is going for a jog. Every 30 minutes of jogging burns 200 calories. If Phillip's goal is to burn off 800 calories, how long, in minutes, must he jog?
A company has 200 hires, and 35% of the workers are from overseas. Out of the local employees, 50% received promotions. How many local employees got promoted?
Ella bought a car for $25,000. Each year the car's value decreases by 15%. What will its value be at the end of two years?
Peter is building a toy car track for his son and needs 200 pieces of track. Each set comes with 10 pieces and costs $15. How much will Peter spend on the toy car track sets?

Jessie decides to participate in a reading competition and wants to match last year's winner's book count. Last year, readers were reading 2 books a week. This year readers read 1.5 books a week. If last year's winner read 24 books, how many more weeks will Jessie read than last year's winner to read the same quantity of books?
Mary has 7 times as many marbles as John. John has triple the number of marbles as Amy did. How many marbles does Mary have if Amy has 4 marbles?
Alex is planning to make a rectangular garden in his backyard. The length of the garden is 6 meters and the width is 4 meters less than the length. What will be the area of the garden?
A square playground has a side length of 20 meters. To make the playground 30% bigger, how many additional square meters are needed?

Tom got a 10% raise at his job. Before the raise, he earned 30 dollars per hour. He works 8 hours per day for 5 days per week. His old bills used to be 400 dollars a week but he decided to add a fifty dollar a week gym membership. How much does he have leftover a week?

Lisa collected 80 seashells at the beach. She kept some of them and gave the rest to her friends. She gave Sarah 30 seashells and Mary, who is her best friend, received 10 more seashells than Sarah. How many seashells did Lisa keep?

In a school science fair, students need to prepare 3 models each costing $15 and an additional project report costing $10. How much will it cost a group of 4 students to prepare for the science fair if they divide the cost equally?
Jasper bought a pack of 4 chocolates for $16. He enjoyed it so much that he decided to buy 3 more packs. After these purchases, how much did each chocolate cost him?
Sara has 30 dogs and Jack has 5 dogs. If Sara gives Jack 6 of her dogs, how many more dogs does Sara have than Jack?
In a basketball game, Ian scored triple the points of what Alex scored. If Alex scored 15 points, how many points did they both score in total?
Anna attends a 2-hour dance class thrice a week. She also spends half as much time for her self-practice at home. How many hours, does Anna spend dancing in a week?
Sally goes to a grocery store and buys 7 cartons of milk, each costing $2.50, and 5 packets of cookies each costing $1.90. How much will she spend in total?
There are five teams in a Soccer league and each team has 11 members. One of the players got injured in all teams during the play. How many players are available to play the match?
Mary, Tim and Alex are gardening. Mary plants 12 flowers, Tim plants 15 flowers, and Alex plants 18 flowers. How many flowers do they plant on average?

A painter was asked to paint 80 doors for a building. He was able to paint 25% of the total doors in the first day, 30% in the second day, and 35% in the third day. How many more doors does the painter need to paint?

A baker needs to bake cookies. He can get 12 cookies out of 1 batch of dough. He has made 10 batches of dough but he is only expected to deliver 100 cookies. How many batches of dough will he have leftover?
A baker used 6 kilograms of flour to bake bread in the morning and double that amount in the afternoon. How many kilograms of flour did the baker use in one day?
In a football match, a player scored 3 goals in the first half of the game. He scored twice as many goals in the second half as he did in the first half. How many goals did he score in total?
A library fines you $0.25 per day for overdue books. If you return a book 16 days late, how much will you have to pay?
Amy is planning to buy a pair of sneakers that costs $55. She already has $30. How much more does Amy need to buy the sneakers?
In a classroom, there are five times as many girls as boys. If there are 12 boys, how many students are there in total?
George is a landscaper who plants 30 flowers in an hour. If he works for 5 hours, how many flowers will he have planted?

Oliver saved up money for 14 days, putting aside $7 each day. On top of that, he saved $5 for every day he didn't use his cellphone. If he didn't use his cellphone for a total of 8 days, how much money did he save in total?
There are 30 trees in a park. 8 trees are oak trees and the rest are pine. If each pine tree has an average of 15 pinecones, how many pinecones are there in the park?
Max drives 55 miles per hour. If Max drives for 5 hours straight, take a 25-minute break, and then drives for another 7 hours straight, how many miles will he travel?
A company gives $100 bonus to each of its employees for holiday season. If the company has 400 employees, how much did they give away?
Little Suzie has 7 apples. Each day she eats 2 apples. After how many days will she run out of apples?
If Bob painted 60 doors and his apprentice painted 240 doors, what percentage of the total doors did Bob paint?

Emma travels 7 miles to the library from her house. From there, she goes 20 miles to her aunt’s house. From her aunt's house, she goes back 30 miles to her own house. How many additional miles did Emma drive with the library stop, compared to a round trip without the library stop?
There are 500 pages in a book. Each day, Emily reads 25 pages. How long will it take her to finish the book?
The first stall sells bananas at $2 for 5 pieces, while the second stall sells at $3 for 8 pieces. How much can you save per banana if you buy from the second stall instead of the first?
A class of 130 students is planning a field trip. The school bus can carry 60 students at once. After the first two loads, how many students still need a ride to the field trip location?

Bob has a toy car collection. He has 15 red cars, twice as many blue cars, and half as many yellow cars as red cars. Bob decided to buy green toy cars, which is 5% of the total toy cars he owns. How many toy cars does Bob own after his latest purchase?
Gordon has 12 toy cars. He gives 3 of them to his brother and buys 2 more. How many toy cars does Gordon have now?

Oliver cycles 60 kilometers to reach his school from home. After school, he cycles back to home and then goes to the park. If he cycled 200 kilometers in total, how many kilometers is the distance between his home and the park?
A party store sold 300 balloons. 120 of them were yellow, 90 were red and the rest were blue. If the owner gave 50% of each color to a charity, how many balloons does he have left?
Alice bought 5 kilograms of pasta. She cooked 1/5 kilograms for lunch and 1/3 of the remaining for dinner. How many grams of pasta did she have left?

Jenna agrees to give her friends 2 candy bars for every task they do for her. Each friend has 3 tasks to do per day. Jenna has $20 to buy candy bars. Each pack of candy bars contains 10 bars and costs $2. How many days can Jenna incentivize her friends with candy bars?
A garden has 120 apple trees. Each tree produced 15 apples this season. Unfortunately, 10% of the apples were not good and had to be thrown away. How many good apples does the garden have?

Bob runs a betting business. He has two games, one with a 40% chance of winning $3,500 and the other with a 50% chance of winning $1,000. How much more likely is it for Bob to win the first game compared to the second game?
Dan worked for 9 hours on Monday. Terri worked twice as long and Ron worked half as long. How many hours did the 3 people work in total on Monday?
Nina bought five books costing $15 each, and she gave a $10 tip. How much change should she get if she hands over a hundred-dollar bill?
Angel owns a bakery. He makes 100 bread loaves a day and sells them for $3 each. If he wants to increase his sales by 20%, how much money will he make a day after the increase?
James runs a small store selling notebooks. He bought 120 notebooks for $500. He decides to mark up the price by 25% to make a profit. How much will each notebook cost?

Billy has a piggy bank with breakfast, lunch, dinner, snack and fun money. The amount of money in his piggy bank for each meal is $17. It costs Billy $5 everyday for snacks. If Billy has $87 in total, how much fun money does Billy have?
Evelyn ran 400 meters yesterday. Alex ran 15 more than half that distance. What is the combined distance that Evelyn and Alex ran?

The Foodies Cafe sells a grilled sandwich for $30, a coffee for $10, and a chocolate dessert for $20. If they sell 20 grilled sandwiches, 10 coffees, and 15 chocolate desserts in a day, what is their total revenue for that day?
Abby saved $25 each month for 8 months. She then spent 1/4 of her savings to buy a new phone. How much did Abby's new phone cost?
Amy plants 5 trees in each of her 8 gardens. Each tree produces 15 fruits every year. If Amy sells each fruit for $2, how much money will she make in a year from selling fruits?
In a school, there are 120 students. 40% of the students like football, 35% like basketball and the rest like volleyball. How many students like volleyball?
Olivia painted 20 pictures in a month. Her friend Vince painted two-fifths of her paintings in the same month. How many paintings did Vince paint that month?

Elisa had 1200 roses before Valentine's Day. On Valentine's Day, she sold all her roses. In the first hour, she sold 1/5 of her roses. The following hour she sold 30% of the remaining roses, and in the third hour she sold half of what remained after the second hour. Calculate the number of roses Elisa had not sold by the third hour.

Five days ago, Cindy and I painted 150 pictures for a charity drive. Today, Cindy and I will paint 30% fewer pictures for the same charity drive. If our goal is to paint 500 pictures for the charity drive, how many pictures still need to be painted?
A flower shop sold roses at $2 each, and daises at $1 each. If Jane bought 14 roses and 22 daises, how much money did Jane spend?
If a car rental service charges $30 for a day's rent and $0.12 per mile driven. How much would Jay have to pay if he rented the car for 3 days and drove it for 200 miles?
A cycling track is 20 miles long. Jerry decides to bike this track every day for a month. If a month has 30 days, how many miles does Jerry bike in a month?
In a park, there were 56 squirrels last year. The population has since doubled. How many squirrels are there this year?
Jack drove 100 miles to visit his grandmother. The trip took him 2 hours. How fast was Jack driving?

Mary bought a new laptop for $1200. After 2 years, she sold it for $600. She was also able to earn a total of $3000 using the laptop for freelance work. What was her net profit from purchasing and using the laptop?
Tina weighs 60 pounds, 10 pounds less than Sara and 20 pounds more than Bob. What is the average weight of the three people, in pounds?
Cafe Specials offers a Tuesday breakfast meals at 40% off. Alice ordered a pancake for $12 and Emma ordered a bacon omelette for $15. If they ate breakfast on Tuesday, how much was their total bill?
Emma runs a bakery where she sells cupcakes for $3 each and donuts for $2 each. If a customer bought 4 cupcakes and 6 donuts, how much did the customer pay?
Christopher scored 60 points on his Science test, 75 on his French test, and 58 on his Art test. If his Music score is the average of these 3 scores, what was his total score across all 4 subjects?
A farmer has a rectangular field whose length is three times its width. If the width is 30 feet, how many feet is the perimeter of the field?
Emily's pond is 3 feet shallower than 2 times the depth of Hannah's pond. If Emily's pond is 11 feet deep, what is the depth of Hannah's pond?
In a school, there are 15 boys for every 10 girls. If there are 300 students in total, how many boys are there?
In a city park, there are 12 oak trees. Each oak tree has 23 branches, and each branch contains 40 leaves. How many leaves are there in total on all the oak trees?

Jacob is a librarian. He organizes books into three sections. He has 500 books in total. 1/5th of the books are in Kids section. 2/5th of the books are in Adult section and the rest are in the Reference section. How many books in the Reference section?
George's cat is 2/3 the length of his dog. The dog is 1/5 the length of George. If George is 70 inches tall, how long is the cat in inches?
Alice takes 7 minutes to read each page of a book. If the book has 120 pages, how many hours does it take her to read the book?
A group of friends decided to share the expenses for a taxi ride. The fare was $30. If five friends shared the price, how much money does each friend have to pay?
Alexandria is making bracelets. She has 75 beads and each bracelet takes 5 beads. How many bracelets can Alexandria make?

Amy drinks 3 cups of coffee daily. Each coffee has 95 grams of caffeine. She takes a caffeine pill that has the same amount of caffeine as her three cups of coffee. How much caffeine does she consume in a week?

In a concert, Peter counted 120 cars in the space A parking lot. He saw that there were three times more cars in Space B than in Space A. If the total number of cars by the end of the concert was 800, how many more cars parked during the concert?

Martin is getting ready to bake a cake. He has 2 pounds of sugar, 3 pounds of cake flour, and 1 pound of butter. If he plans to use 2 more pounds of sugar and 1 pound of baking powder, how many pounds of baking materials does he now have?

Logan is selling lemonade to raise funds for his scout troop. He wants to raise $200. He buys lemonade mix at $4 per gallon and sells each glass of lemonade for $2. If he can get 10 glasses of lemonade from a gallon, how many gallons of lemonade does he need to sell to reach his goal?

John works as a freelance software developer and charges $50 per hour for his work. He worked on a project for 3 weeks with 40 hours per week. After completing the project, he worked on another project for 2 weeks with 30 hours per week. How much money did John make from these two projects?
There are 3 blue candies for every 5 red candies in a bag. If there are 45 candies in total, how many blue candies are there?
James wants to build a square fence for his new puppy. Each side of the square fence needs 9 planks. If James needs 3 nails per plank and he has only 75 nails, how many more nails does he need?

Peter is driving to the mountain resort. The resort is 200 miles away from the city. He drove the first half of the path in 2 hours and then, due to a heavy rain, he had to slow down and drove the second half of the way in 3 hours. What was the overall average speed of the travel in miles per hour?
Alice has a bag of 100 apples. She gives 15 apples to each of her 5 friends. How many apples does Alice have left in her bag?

Mandy went to a book store and bought some books. She then went to a pet store to buy some stuff for her pet that costs $80, $30 more than twice the amount she spent on books. How much did she spend in total?

Liam's dog needs a regular medication.  He has had pet insurance for 18 months that cost $25 per month.  The medication costs $2000 but the insurance covers all but 10% of this.  How much money did he save by having insurance?
It takes Sarah 2 hours to finish a painting. If she needs to make 12 paintings for an Art exhibit, but only paints for 4 hours a day, how many days will it take her to finish all the paintings?

In a art class of 30 students, students were asked if they like painting or sculpturing or neither. 15 students said they like painting and 12 said they like sculpturing, with these statistics including the responses of 5 students who said they like both painting and sculpturing. How many students do not like either?
Emily baked 18 cookies. She gave away 1/3 of her cookies to her friends. How many cookies does Emily have left?
Mike made 120 calls on Monday, twice as many calls on Tuesday, and 30 calls each day Wednesday through Friday. How many calls did he make on average during those five days?

A store transports fresh vegetables in crates and cartons using its delivery truck. One crate of vegetables weighs 4 kilograms and one carton 3 kilograms. How much will a load of 12 crates, and 16 cartons of vegetables weigh?
Maria has 5 shirts and 9 pants. If Maria wants all her clothes to be unique combinations of shirts and pants, what is the maximum number of outfits she can make?
In a school, there are 7 classes with equal number of students. If there are 245 students in total, how many students are in each class?

Molly has 220 pencils. She decides to only keep 35 pencils and gives Jenny some pencils. Then, she sees 10 friends and gives them 15 pencils each. The remaining pencils were equally distributed among her 4 cousins. If each of Molly's cousins got 4 pencils, how many pencils did Molly give to Jenny?
A conference room is rented out at a rate of $100 per hour. If a company rents the room for an 8-hour meeting, how much will they pay?

In a cookie baking competition, Mary used 70% of the flour for her first batch. The remaining flour was used to bake smaller cookies, 50% of which got burnt. If initially there were 150 grams of flour, how many grams of flour were successfully used to make the smaller cookies?
Mark planted 4 apple trees and twice as many orange trees in his orchard. How many trees did he plant in total?

Robert was writing a research paper for a project. He wrote 10 pages in the first hour. Robert then increased his productivity by 50% for the second hour, and by 100% for the third hour compared to the first hour. How many pages did Robert write in the three hours?
In a soccer match, Messi scored 3 goals and the rest of his team scored 5 times more than him. How many goals did his team score in total?
David is reading a book of 280 pages. On the first day, he reads 35 pages. Everyday after, he reads 10 pages more than the day before. How many pages will he have read after 5 days?

Mike works for a moving company. During one day, he can move maximum 45 boxes. One week, Mike moved the maximum number of boxes, only twice. On two other days, Mike moved a total of 60 boxes, and on one day he moved only one-sixth of the daily maximum. On the last two days, the sum of the boxes moved was only three-fifth of the maximum daily performance. How many more boxes could Mike move during that week, if he would work at maximum performance?
Mark has $25 in his pocket. He spends $1.5 daily on bus fare to and from work. How much money will he have left after a week?
Sarah needs to buy ingredients for her bakery. She spends $30 on a 10lb bag of flour. How much would 25lb of flour cost?

Sandy has a vegetable garden where she grows carrots and tomatoes. She picked 40 carrots and 25 tomatoes. If each carrot weighs 0.2 pounds and each tomato weighs 0.5 pounds, what is the total weight in pounds of vegetables Sandy picked from her garden?
If a designer chair costs $1,000 and during a sale, its price is reduced by 25%, what is the sale price of the chair?

Alice and her friends prepared a buffet for their class year end party. Alice baked 15 apple pies. Her friend, Betty made twice as many blueberry pies as Alice's apple pies. Cindy, another friend, made 20 fewer chocolate cakes than Betty's blueberry pies. How many pastries did Alice and her friends prepare for their class year end party?
A class buys two boxes of pens for a project. Each box contains 15 pens. If the class has 27 students and each student gets one pen, how many pens are left?

James loves reading mystery novels. He has a collection of 50 novels. Each day he reads 2 novels. At the end of the week, he buys 5 more mystery novels. How many novels will he have left at the end of the week?
Billy spent 4 hours studying Math, 3 hours for English and 2 hours for Science. If there is 24 hours in a day, how many hours in a day does Billy have left for other activities?
Mike bought a comic book for $12. He then sells it to his friend, John, for 20% more than he bought it. How much did Mike sell the comic book to John?
Taylor had three cats that gave birth to 8 kittens each. Taylor, later on, sold 2/3 of the kittens each at $150. Calculate the total amount of money Taylor received from the sale of the kittens.

Amy and Matt got a combined total of 18 past due bills. Matt has twice the number of bills as Amy, and they each have an equal number of medical bills. If Matt has 5 medical bills, how many remaining bills does Matt have?

A library is organizing books into shelves. Each shelf can hold 25 books, and currently, there are 10 empty shelves. If the library has 200 books, how many remaining empty shelves will there be after all the books are organized?

Sam runs a marathon that is 42.2 kilometres long. He runs at a steady speed of 12 kilometres per hour. If he takes a 15-minute break every hour, how many hours does it take for Sam to complete the marathon?
A bag has 12 apples and 8 oranges. If Holly takes 1 apple and 2 oranges, what fraction of the fruits did she take?
George has 500 comic books. He buys more comic books that cost $5 each and his total expenses are $1000. How many comic books does he now have?
Melinda has 2 cats and each cat eats 3 cans of cat food in a week. How many cans of cat food does Melinda need to buy for her cats for the next 5 weeks?

John, a gym instructor, gives out two protein bars to each of his trainees after each session. If John holds five sessions a week and there are 15 trainees in each session, how many weeks would a stock of 600 protein bars last?
Jonathan plays for a football team which plays 20 games in a season. Each game lasts 90 minutes. How many hours does Jonathan play in a football season?
Susan spends 30 minutes every day doing exercises. How much time in total will Susan spend exercising in a year (excluding leap years)?

Aaron has eaten 4 cookies out of a jar. The jar originally had 12 cookies in it. If he then decides to divide the remaining cookies equally between his two friends, how many cookies will each of his friends get?
John has twice as many books as his friend, while his friend has three more books than their classmate. If their classmate has 40 books, how many books do they have altogether?
Samantha has 15 apples. She wants to give 1/3 of them to her friend. But she eats 2 before she can do that. How many apples does she give to her friend?

Jil is a teacher supervising a field trip. 30 of the kids are in grade 5, twice the number of grade 5 kids are in grade 6, and the rest are in grade 4. If she added 15 grade 4 kids to the group, how many grade 4 students will there be in total?

In a school, 15 out of 100 students are wearing glasses. The rest of the students either have contact lenses or don't have any visual aids. The number of students wearing contact lenses is 5 times as many as the students with glasses. How many students don't have any visual aids?
A school supplies shop sells notebooks and pens. A notebook costs $1.50, while a pen costs $0.85. How much do 60 notebooks and 100 pens cost?
Pete is backpacking across Europe. He started with $2000. If he spends $150 a day, how many days can he afford to stay travelling?
Tina baked 4 cookies for herself and each of her 3 children on both Saturday and Sunday. How many cookies would she need to bake for every weekend over the course of 1 year?
Megan runs 2 miles a day. She runs 6 days a week. How many miles does she run in a month?

Rebecca decides to trade in her old phone. The resale value is 75% of the original price, which was $300. She then buys a new phone priced at $500, but gets a 20% off discount. How much does Rebecca have to pay out of her pocket?
A small zoo has 15 monkeys. It also has 5 more than twice the number of monkeys in giraffes and 3 less than the number of giraffes in lions. How many animals are in the zoo all together?
It takes Madison 20 minutes to make one handmade bracelet. If she makes 5 bracelets on Monday and 5 bracelets on Tuesday, how many hours does she spend making bracelets?
Kelly bakes a batch of cookies for her family every weekend. If each batch contains 16 cookies and she bakes for 8 weekends, how many cookies does she make in total?
Samantha practices piano for 1 hour every day. Her brother practices for twice as long as Samantha. Their sister practices for half as long as Samantha. How many hours do they practice in total?

A library bought 600 new books to add to their collection. The library has three sections: fiction, non-fiction, and children's books. The fiction section received 1/3 of the new books, and the non-fiction section received 3/5 of the remaining books. How many new books were added to the children's section?
James is a freelance graphic designer and he charges $20 per hour. For a certain project, he worked for 15 hours. How much would he earn for this project?
A forest has an area of 5200 square miles. It loses 12 square miles each year due to logging. How many square miles will the forest be in 5 years?

Sam finds a shirt he likes that is priced at $30, but it's on sale for 20% off. After deciding to purchase the shirt, he finds that sales tax is an additional 8%. How much will Sam pay for the shirt including the discount and sales tax?

A bookstore is having a clearance sale. Each book sells for $12. The bookstore has 10,000 books to sell. During the first day, it sold 1,000 books, and 2,500 books on the second day. How much revenue does the bookstore need to make on the subsequent days to reach its goal of $120,000?
Peter has $45. He needs to buy some books, which cost $6 each and a calculator priced $15. After his purchase, he has $11 left. How many books did he buy?
A school had 3 gallons of paint to create a mural. Each gallon contains 4 quarts. If each student used 2 quarts of paint, how many students were able to paint?
Alex had 64 books in his collection. He sold 18% of his books at a garage sale. How many books does Alex have left?
Sally owns a bakery and just baked 8 dozen cookies. If she sells 2 dozen to a customer and gives 1 dozen to a friend, how many cookies does she have left?
Peter spent 7 hours on a kayaking trip where he covered a distance of 35 kilometers. What was his average speed per hour?
Allie is 7 years older than Lilly. Lilly is three times as old as Sam. If Sam is 15, how old is Allie?
Four friends decide to split the cost of a pizza. The pizza costs $24. If Lily contributed $6 more than Mike, and Mike contributed the same amount as Jack and Hannah combined, how much did Mike pay?
Peter bought a $5000 TV for his new house, a $1500 music system, and a washing machine three times as expensive as the music system. How much did Peter spend in total?
A library has twice as many fiction books as non-fiction books. If the library has a total of 600 books, how many books are fiction?
A cycling team is training for a race. If each cyclist pedals 20 miles per day, and the team has 15 cyclists, how many miles will the team as a whole have pedaled after 3 days?
Jane has $120, if she buys a dress for $80 and some accessories for 10% of the price of the dress. How much money will Jane have after making the purchases?

Annie is preparing goodie bags for her classmates. She has 3 classes and each class has 20 students. She plans to put 2 pencils, 3 erasers, and a small notebook in each bag. If pencils cost $0.50 each, erasers cost $0.75 each, and notebooks cost $1.50 each, how much will she spend on goodie bags?
A baker bakes 10 loaves of bread every day. He sells each bread for 3 dollars and his daily expenses are 15 dollars. How much does he earn in a week after covering the expenses?
Bob has 675 marbles. He emptied the marbles into bags, each bag containing 25 marbles. How many bags does he need?
Sarah is a florist. She can make 4 bouquets from every bundle of flowers she buys. If Sarah buys 3 bundles every day, how many bouquets can she make after 7 days?

Jamie purchased 100 books for her library. 10 books were thriller novels, 7 books more than thriller novels were biographies, three times the number of thriller novels were fiction, while the rest were self-help books. How many self-help books did Jamie buy?
Maria planned a road trip that is 110 miles long. Her car consumes 1 gallon of gas every 22 miles. If a gallon of gas costs $3, how much is Maria going to spend on gas for the trip?

As gardeners, John, Emma, and Liz have planted a lot of trees over the week. John has planted 30 trees for the week and Emma has planted 2 times more than John. Meanwhile, Liz has planted the same number of trees John was able to. How many trees have they planted for the entire week?
Ricky has 400 marbles. He lost 15 while playing at the park. How many marbles does he have left?

Jared and Tina went to a driving range. Each token lets them hit 20 golf balls. Jared used 14 tokens and Tina used 16. Jared hit the golf ball 40 times. Tina hit the ball 60 times. How many golf balls did Jared and Tina miss altogether?
An ice cream store sells 6 different flavors of ice cream. If a customer can choose 2 flavors to create a double scoop cone, how many different flavor combinations can the customer make?
Rebecca bought 80 marbles for $4.00, and she sold 1/4 of them at $0.10 each. How much money did she make after deducting her initial expenses?
A farmer has 15 cows, 10 horses and 15 sheep. Due to a bad storm, he had to sell 5 cows, 3 horses and 4 sheep. How many animals does he have left?
Amy's laptop needs a new battery. The labor costs $85 an hour for 4 hours. The battery itself cost $250. How much did the repairs cost in total?
There are 40 kids in a school bus. Each row has 5 kids. If the first 3 rows get off at the first stop, how many kids are still in the bus?
A bookstore has thrice the number of fiction books as non-fiction books. If it has 125 non-fiction books, how many books are there in total?
There are 400 students in a school. The school decided to buy a notebook for each student. If each notebook costs $5, how much did the school spend?
A fruit vendor had 400 apples in his cart. He sold 60% of them in the morning. In the afternoon, he sold another 20% of the remaining apples. How many apples did he have left at the end of the day?
Paul has 15 books. He lent 3 books to his friend Mahmoud, and then 2 books to his sister. When his sister returned the books, he lent 1 more book to another friend. How many books does Paul have now?
A school has 4 classrooms. Each classroom has 25 desks. How many desks are there in the school?
Jenny owns a small apple orchard. She picked 250 apples from one tree and 165 apples from another tree. If she can make an apple pie with 15 apples, how many apple pies can she make?
Jamie has a gift shop. She sells bracelets for $10 each. Last week, she sold 35 bracelets. How much total income did she make from the bracelet sale?
Bobby collected stickers. He had 3 times as many stickers as Jimmy. Together, they have 64 stickers. How many stickers does Bobby have?

Jane bought 5 packs of disinfectant wipes that cost $3 per pack. Each pack has 80 pieces. She sold 3 of these packs and sold them for $2 per 40 pieces. She sold the remaining 160 wipes individually at $0.05 each. How much profit did she make?
Ruby has 15 more dolls than Daisy. Wanda has 3 times as many as Daisy. If Wanda has 21 dolls, how many dolls does Ruby have?
Fruits are sold in a market in packs. Each pack of apples has 5 and each pack of oranges has 6. If you want to buy 15 apples and 18 oranges, how many packs do you have to buy?
In a forest there are two types of trees. There are twice as many oak trees as there are maple trees and together they are 1500. How many oak trees are there in the forest?

A grocery store sells 10 kilograms of apples per day. After a new marketing campaign, their daily sales increased to 16 kilograms per day. How much more apples will the grocery store sell in a year due to this campaign?

Mia is writing a book and needs to make sure that she has not exceeded the 50000-word limit. She wrote 34750 words in the first month and 15750 words in the second month. How many words has Mia exceeded the limit by?
Sandra earns $40,000 in a year. After a successful project, her boss decided to increase her salary by 15%. How much is her new annual salary?

Johnny is a professional piano tutor. He teaches 8 students on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays, and 11 students on Tuesdays and Thursdays. He is paid $20 for each student he tutors. How many dollars does Johnny make in a week?
Olivia has 6 roses and 4 sunflowers in her vase. Each rose has 5 petals and each sunflower has 15 petals. How many petals are in her vase in total?
A movie theater sells adult tickets for $10 and child tickets for $5. If they sold 75 adult tickets and 60 child tickets in one day, how much total did they make from selling tickets?
Bill, a school bus driver, drives 120 miles to take children to school and back home. He drives 5 days a week. How many miles does he drive in one school week?

Sherlock eats 2 apples a day for one week. Over the next two weeks, he eats the double number of apples he ate in the first week. Over the next three weeks, he eats 4 apples a day. Over these 6 weeks, what’s the average number of apples he eats in a week?

At a community event, they were selling tulips for a fundraising event. A tulip costs $1. They also had a special where you could buy one dozen tulips for $9. If Sarah bought a dozen tulips for each of her 4 neighbors and bought one tulip for each of her 16 friends, how much money would she spend altogether?
Maddy and her brother are collecting sea shells. Maddy finds 15 shells, and her brother finds twice as many as Maddy. How many shells did they find in total?
A book club reads 4 books a month. If each book is roughly 250 pages long and one member reads 25 pages an hour, how many hours, in total, will the club member spend reading in a month?

Daniel and his family go to the amusement park where adult tickets cost $20 each, and children tickets cost $10 each. If Daniel, his wife, and their 3 kids go to the park, how much do they have to pay in total?
A group of tourists includes 20 men and 30 women are planning to go camping. If the camp site charges $5 per person, how much will the entire group be charged?
A baker baked 12 loaves of bread. Each loaf has 10 slices. How many slices of bread did the baker make?
Harry can pick apples from a tree in 6 minutes. However, one day he was ill and it took him 10 minutes. How much longer would it take him to pick 5 apples when he was ill?

Arlo purchases a laptop for $1500.  He then decides to install extra RAM in it. He sells the old RAM for $400 and buys a new one for $600.  How much did he spend on his laptop, counting the savings from selling the old RAM?
Sandy biked 5 kilometers to the park and then another 10 kilometers to her friend's house. Later, she returned back home the same way. How far did Sandy bike in total?
From her garden, Anna harvests 200 tomatoes. She gives away 30% of her tomatoes to her neighbors, then she sells half of what is left. How many tomatoes does she keep?
There are 250 people at a conference and 1/5 of them are women. Half of the men did not attend the second day of the conference. How many people attended the second day of the conference?
David and Mark have a total of 120 comic books. Mark has 3 times as many comic books as David. How many comic books does David have?
Anna is packing books into boxes. Each box can contain 10 books. If she has 75 books, how many boxes does she need?
Chargers cost 15 cents less than headphones. If chargers cost 18 cents, how much would you pay for 12 headphones?

At a farmers market, mangoes are sold in bundles of 9 and apricots are sold in bundles of 7. If Jane buys 14 bundles of mangoes and 11 bundles of apricots, how many pieces of fruit does she buy in total?
Tina bought a 50-page sketch book and used 3 pages per day. At the end of the week, she bought another sketch book of the same size. How many pages does she have in total after 2 weeks?

Every week, Jimmy earns $15 mowing lawns. He is saving up to buy a new game console that costs $320. If he has saved up 75% of the amount he needs, how many more weeks does he need to keep working to reach his goal?
Mary bought 12 bags of candy. Each bag contains 15 pieces of candy. She wants to share the candies equally among her and her 4 friends. How many pieces of candy will each person get?
An art contest has 5 categories. 3 categories have 40 entrants each while the other 2 categories have 25 entrants each. If each entrant submits 2 pieces, how many pieces of art are there in total?

John is building a model airplane. He spent $25 on paint, $15 on brushes and $35 on the model kit. He also wants to buy a display case that costs twice as much as the painting supplies. How much will John spend in total?
Sam is at the beach and wants to collect some sand for his art project. He collects 5 kg of sand in his bag. If the sand contains 10% seashell, how many kg of seashells will Sam get?
Amy runs a burger shop where every tenth customer gets a free burger. Burgers cost $5 each. If she sold $250 worth of burgers, how many free ones did she give away?
Elizabeth runs 6 miles every day for a week. How many miles will she have run by the end of the third week?
A bakery has one oven which can bake 25 loaves of bread in an hour. If the bakery operates for 10 hours a day, how many loaves of bread can they bake in a day?
Emma decided to run a half marathon, which is 13.1 miles long. If she can run at a speed of 7 miles per hour, how long will it take for her to finish?
Julie buys a pair of leggings for $8, and a jacket for six times the price. How much did Julie pay for both items?

Nine friends decide to go to a pizza place for dinner. Each of them eats 3 slices of pizza, and each pizza has 8 slices. If they also get 2 additional pizzas for take-out, how many pizzas did they order in total?
A train travels at an average speed of 80 miles per hour. If it travels for 2.5 hours, how far does the train travel?
In a gym, there are 18 athletes training, and 6 of them are women. If each athlete does 10 push-ups, how many push-ups will all the athletes do in total?

A bakery sold 15 loafs of bread, 25 croissants, 30 muffins, and 10 cakes. The breads cost $4 each, croissants cost $2 each, muffins cost $1.5 each, and cakes cost $20 each. How much did the bakery earn?

Sam has 15 pink shirts and 20 purple ones. His friend Ben has 1/3 as many pink shirts and 3 times as many purple shirts. If they put all their shirts together and share them equally, how many shirts will each have?

Gary is planning a road trip. The distance he will cover is 300 miles. His car uses 1 gallon of gas for every 25 miles. The price of gas is $3 per gallon. How much will Gary spend on gas for the return trip?

A month ago, Annie had $200 in her savings account. Last week she deposited $25. Then a few days ago, she had to withdraw some money. Today she made a deposit of $30, so her current balance is $10 more than it was a month ago. How much money did Annie withdraw?
Mike's piggy bank has 150 coins consisting of dimes and quarters. If he has two times as many dimes as quarters, how many dimes does Mike have?
Robby has to ship 800 pounds of meat that is packed into 50-pound boxes. If the shipping cost of each box is $2.5, how much will Robby pay for the shipment?
If a car travels at a speed of 60 miles per hour, how far will it go in 5 hours?

A local high school has decided to sell t-shirts for a fundraising event. A youth size t-shirt costs $15 and an adult size t-shirt costs $20. The school managed to sell 150 youth size t-shirts and 100 adult size t-shirts. How much money did they raise from the t-shirt sales?

Jessica is saving to buy a new laptop that costs $1200. She manages to save $100 each month. After 10 months of saving, Jessica got a raise and now saves $150 each month. How long will it take Jessica to buy the laptop?
